Detail: Accha Authentic Indian Cuisine: A Taste of 'Proper' UK Indian in Chiang Rai My wife and I decided to treat ourselves to a takeaway from Accha Authentic Indian Cuisine in Chiang Rai recently, and frankly, our only regret is that we didn't order more! From the moment we placed our order to the second we tucked in, the experience was brilliant. I opted for the Chicken Tikka Madras, which delivered the perfect balance of heat and flavor—rich, deep, and satisfyingly spicy. My wife went for the classic Korma, which was creamy, fragrant, and perfectly balanced, a genuinely comforting dish. The portions were decent, providing good value for the good price we paid. But the real standout point? The taste. If you're a British expat, or just someone who understands that the Indian food perfected in the UK is truly the gold standard (wink, wink), Accha hits the mark precisely. It was exactly like the delicious, high-quality Indian takeaway you'd get back home. Accha is a definite must-try in Chiang Rai. Next time, we're doubling our order! Verdict: Delicious, authentic, and great value. Highly recommended. Read less
Not recommended the restaurant is not "vegetarian friendly" as it advertises in the website they added chicken to a vegetarian dish we would not recommend this to veg people

The restaurant made a “mistake” by adding chicken to a veg paneer dish which one of our veg friends ate and the owner had the audacity not to show up with a bad attitude and tried to cover up for the staff which is not a bad thing buttt his attitude was very bad of his

The paneer Tikka had chicken in it we seperated ourselves a non veg side and a veg side we are very mad that they served us chicken in a paneer dish where one of our religious friends is veg and she ate a piece of the chicken. I wouldn't recommend this place fot you if your a veg.
The place I love to be there with my family. Best Indian food in town. Owner is very kind and generous. Will come back again soon.

The food here is Accha ( Good ). Very nice. There are lots of vegan and vegetarian options. I highly recommend all the chutneys ( Green Chutney, Mango chutney, Tamarind Chutney, Mango pickle, Schezwan Chutney ans Sirke waale pyaaz ( Pickled Onion ) ). Reccomended dishes are Hara bhera kebab, Butter Roti, Chili Paneer, Samosa and Mix Veg curry. But the Channa Masala and Gulab Jamun were not too good. The taste of the other things were authentic.
Visited this restaurant during April 24 with group of friends. Food - 5/5 Service - 4/5 Very glad to get authentic tasty Indian food in a place like Chiang Mai.

Really delicious Indian curries - we had the lamb Rogan Josh, chicken madras and chicken biryani. The garlic nann was also excellent. Was pricier than local Thai food but a nice change and beautifully cooked. Recommended.
Good Indian food at Phuket with good prices too...although it also serves non- veg. food. Good choice....one can keep visiting again n again
Our 3rd visit here and accha deserves a review, great food and service in a convenient location we will visit again when in town next

having traveled ther world on business I've often relied on Indian food as a constant in my life -- palak panner is palak panner no matter what country its served in. Out of all The Indian restaurants in 50 countries ive been too this is #2 -- IT would BE #1 IF I hadnt worked at Taj West End in Bangalore! The extensive menu was great and creative presentations are worth going out of your way for if you love Indian food. YOu cant get much better

Wow! We chose here after walking past and also seeing the reviews. The food was excellent, service brilliant and great value for money. Portions are big so be mindful of that, we felt guilty we had to leave things. The food was so tasty, cooked fresh. Would definitely recommend!
Very good authentic indian food, good quality and taste. The restaurant is in a good location and decent decor too. If you want indian food in Chiang Mai, you will not regret.

We had a fabulous meal. Even dish was spectacular and the condiments were all a special treat. We also loved the nan. The palak paneer was our favorite dish. We enjoyed the succulent tandoori chicken too. It was a total feast. Will return again and again.
One of the best restaurants in Chiang Mai , halal , nice staff and very nice place and great location thanks for the service

Overall a good dining experience in a pleasant setting, with good service. The chicken was plump and moist, and the sauce full of flavour, couldn’t fault any of the food, my only criticism is that they brought the starters and main courses at the same time. It’s a bit more expensive than other Indian restaurants in the city, but offers a different selection.

Amazing food I have travelled a lot in whole world , I m typical vegetarian and I got all dishes whatever I wanted , daal makhani palak paneer paneer tikka masala din Kashmiri Aloo , wow amazing taste . The chilli garlic naan , stuff kulcha , plain kulcha are too tasty and delicious, If you are in chiangmai no other place to go except accha restaurant. The name itself says ACCHA . The owners Mr Purav singh from Dehradun and K. JOY are very caring and had a wonderfull talk with them . It’s a lively place and only foreigners and thai people were having food and relishing there. They serve beer and wine . Thx Wonderfull experience I had Read less

Considering india is my favorite country and I'm a diehard foodie I feel qualified to share on food. Atmosphere nice authentic but because of this food is way over priced for chiangmai and what I was given. I ordered thali ,set meal. Two curries small cups to thick for what's common as dry as well. The dishes on it weren't supposed to be so dry. No yogurt to balance heat. I'd probably not return due to double price of others in chiangmai and slight dry and over spiced not heat spices
Delicious North Indian food that was served very quickly. We requested for the kids food with no chilli to be served first, and they did exactly that. Highly recommend !

Very good taste and quality of starting materials in this place if you look for Indian food in Chiang Mai. Chicken byriani & raita were excellent, other curry dishes very tasty also. Garlic and cheese nans are generous. It is not as cheep as other places, but the quality is worth it. Highly recommended. Fred & family

Four of us ate here tonight, after the other couple suggested it. We ate "family style" sharing several dishes. The "mixed vegetables" were bland, tasteless and should not have been on the menu. All the other dishes were quite good though. Service was good, with a small bell on the table to ring if you wanted someone -- and they came immediately.

Nothing special at all. The food was ok but the portion size was an absolute joke. Especially taking into account the pricing. Honestly the combo I ordered had 2 or 3 small pieces of chicken in the curries. I love Indian food. I’ve eaten Indian all over the world, Michelin stared in London to street food in Goa. There is nothing special about this. There are much better Indian restaurants within walking distance from it. Check my pic. Have you ever seen smaller portions 🤣 Read less

... was excellent. The service was good and friendly, the food tasted great, and I especially loved the six little chutney dishes they put out on the table like condiments. All that stuff tasted great on the garlic naan I ordered. I also had something called the chicken banjara kebabs which was really moist and flavorful with some kind of spicy yogurt marinade on it. It tasted great as well on the naan with some of that chutney on it. What else, oh I had a papadum masala appetizer. It was served rolled-up into a cone, and you put the "salad" on yourself. Usually it comes kind of flat with the stuff piled on it. I think that this is a better way of eating it. Price was like real reasonable as well. When I want to eat Indian food again I don't see a reason to go take a chance with someplace else when I know what a solid place this one is. Read less

We thought the food was quite tasty. Very extensive menu. Almost too much selection, which made deciding what to order difficult! I like that the restaurant is open until 9:30pm, as many restaurants close much earlier. Vegetarian and non-vegetarian options. Highly recommended.

Ate here for the first time as a friend suggested it was popular. Waited ages to place an order. Place had a lot of customers on the night but only 2 or 3 staff on the floor. Food wasn’t cheap but also was nothing special. Have eaten at better Indian restaurants in Chiang Mai, and for less $. Wouldn’t eat here again.

Our friends took us to Accha as they had dined there twice before and thought it has authentic Indian cuisine. We agreed! The selections were those popular in India and vegans, vegetarians, and carnivores have choices of dishes. We shared samosas, mixed meat starters, mango lassis, and four different dishes, and naan breads. The mixed naan was so yummy. The biryani is exceptional, just like in Hyderabad! Our friends did not want dessert but we convinced them to try the kulfti ice cream. The subtle cardamon flavored ice cream sprinkled with pistachios and golden raisins was memorable. The staff were friendly and not angry that we stayed past closing time. The decor is charming. Toilets clean. The tables have Indian bells you ring for service. The evening was total enchantment! Read less

I've been to Accha twice now and I haven't been disappointed. The food is excellent and so is the service, the prices are very reasonable, more or less the same as any other Indian restaurant in Chiang Mai.

Staff forgot my roti despite being only 1 other table of 2 in the restaurant. Bland food, not spicy at all. Staff could not advise what is normally eaten with dahl, strange lack of knowledge of indian food. Did not ask preference of spicy/not spicy. Very expensive, rice 60 baht normally costs 10 or 20 in thai restaurants. Roti also double price. Dahl bucket had a high bottom to give illusion of more inside. Came here on recommendation, dont know why its recommended, will continue to go to the authentic indian on loi kroh road not at this fusion place that completely lacks atmosphere. Read less

Everywhere I travel to I always try and get out to an Indian restaurant. This place was awesome for some well priced eats. The appetizers were tasty as was the main course. Would definitely recommend this place for anyone looking for Indian in Chiang Mai
excellent taste for authentic Indian/veg/jain food, super excellenbt service, reasonably price, must for vega nd indian food.
Everything was excellent , The food was outstanding , The staff and service brilliant , Overall highly recommend .

I loved the service, atmosphere and food at Accha. The service was great. They were checking on you without being intrusive. If you asked for something, they did their best to accommodate. The food was delicious and brought back memories of my childhood in India. The atmosphere was cheerful and bright with beautiful surroundings.
Absolutely amazing place. The food is delicious and i recommended 100 %. We order the specialty of the chef. “ Chicken Tikka” and “ Malai Tikka “

Wonderful selection of vegetarian options, so many that I asked the waiter to choose for me. I was very pleased with his choice! Within moments of seating we had our drinks and a complimentry tasting selections of chutneys.

Aloha! We had the veg samosas (fantastic, wonderful crust) served with 6 types of chutney - yum! The naan was also really great. The paneer tikka masala was a bit different than what I am used to, a little spicier and earthy than normal, but the paneer itself was excellent. Very nice ambience and decor. About 400 baht for the two of us, and definitely worth it.
We loved the ambience food and service.. we sat outside and order their vegetarian platter, chicken curry and panner curry.. there was lot of food..
The food is so flavorful and delicious. Definitely worth it! Large portions, great environment, and stunningly delicious.

My wife and I celebrated our wedding anniversary at Aacha recently, during our visit to Chiang Mai, and it turned out to be an excellent choice. The food was superb, with all the authentic flavours and aromas one would expect from a top-tier Indian restaurant. I found the missi (chickpea flour) roti to be a healthy alternative to normal naans and rotis. Service was brilliant and the place quite comfortable, with ample space between tables. We enjoyed the experience so much that we went there again 2 days later. More visits are definitely planned.

A pricey, but delicious dinner. The butter chicken was very well prepared. Two entree dishes plus naan (no drinks) cost 450 baht, with is a lot to spend for dinner in Thailand. Clean bathrooms behind the restaurant. Cordial staff. We’d go back if we win the lottery.

Believe the only Indian restaurant in Chiang Rai. I watched as my lunch was freshly prepared and very good it was too. Had Paneer Tikka and Aloo Gobi. Need to return to taste more items on their menu.

One of the best restaurants I have been to in my two week stay in Thailand. The food is very authentic and sooo delicious!! The owners are from India so you know you’re getting the real deal. The ambiance is also wonderful! Went last night and going back again tonight :) Can’t get enough.

I absolutely love Indian food! And have had lots of traditional from Indian friends and while in India. This place has the most authentic that I have found in Chiangmai. I especially love their butter chicken, garlic naan, chicken tikka , and veg samosas. Also, don't forget to order a traditional chai tea before leaving!

I was here for an early dinner, as starter onion bhajee, which was a big portion, and for main i had massala tikka with garlic naan. The food tasted great! There was a big group present, but when they left i could enjoy the music and peace ;) Prices are reasonable.
Nice, clean restaurant. Large selection of Indian dishes. Open from 11am-10pm. There are some outdoor seats as well.

For a change for spicy Thai food we went to the Accha for Indian spicy food. All I can say visit - each dish we had was excellent, full of flavour and subtle heat. Would recommend the poppadoms (cornets) to start with, look great and taste even better and the Rogan Josh (should be lamb). Limited parking and would suggest to book in advance (Thursday - Saturday)
We were very pleased with our meal, we tried a number of dishes for the first time and each one was yummy. We'll definitely be back!

Cute and clean restaurant, good stuff, no overpriced! They have a big menu.....the food is decent but no exceptional, the dishes comes fast to our table....but in my opinion it needs more flavour! We ordered tree differents dishes and they are the same! Is really hot inside, only one AC for the whole place!
Came here based on the reviews and would fully endorse all of the good comments made. Exceptional food of a high standard, great wine and huge portions. Do try the onion bhajis.

This restaurant has got good vibes and really nice aroma that sets your mood. Table service is prompt and attentive. I terms of food, I find it okie sort as it has set my expectations really high but never mind, it was a good experience. I think this is one of the better Indian restaurants as compared to others. I have tried almost all of them in CM. Ordered non veg and drinks. Have separate bar too.
Nice food and ambience, have decent options for vegetarians, reasonable price, delivery available,walkable distance from main roads

After going to an Accha in Chiang Rai we had to try the one in Nimman as it was where we lived. It didnt disappoint. The food was stunning so flavour some and the chicken so tender. It was all perfect and the nan so much better than the crap we used to get in Spain. The service could do with a bit of improvement. We sat with our dirty plates for ages and we wanted another drink and the brought them but left all the plates! Weird. Didnt ruin the experience as used to the relaxed nature of the area. Portions are plenty and certainly enough for one person with a side. Recommend the chicken kebabs similar to a shashlik. It comes out a different times so go with the flow it's how they serve. Read less

I loved the food here and the service was attentive, friendly and fast. I came for a late dinner and a glass of wine - the house red was chilled and delicious, I recommend it. The menu is huge, with vegan and vegetarian options clearly marked. The restaurant is lovely and colourful and the venue has both indoor and outdoor dining areas, both with a great atmosphere. They delivered me a range of 6 different chutneys to enjoy with my meal and a little bell to get their attention for ordering or when requesting anything like the bill. My meal didn't take long to get to me after ordering. I would love to return here one day. Read less

Went there with friends a few days ago. Conversation was nice, which was great considering we waited 1h40 to get food on the table. The food was then eaten in less than 20 minutes ; after what we proceeded to pay and leave to get real dinner. The portion size is ridiculous. 320 thb for a lamb combo = 3 nut sized pieces of meat. All of this could be excused or pass as fine dining if the taste was great. It was not. The sauces are bland as a frozen sauce, the lamb recipes didn't taste like lamb. Only thing worth saving is the naans, which were tasty. But I'm not in the market for 320 THB naans Read less
visited 2 times for dinner, both times i left extremely impressed Food was very good and tasty interiors are very nice staff is very courteous

Waited over an hour to receive any food (a tomato soup for my child). Asked twice and was told it's nearly ready and they were especially busy. Third time I asked the manager (I think) explained they'd made a mistake with our order. When the food finally arrived it tasted decent but my overall experience was ruined by the poor service.

... and I lived 4 years in India, the garlic naan could be a little bit thinner nit so puffy, but it still was good. The paneer tikka masala was a bit to dry, could be mire gravy and a little bit more spicy, but again, it was good. Also the Paneer! The vegetable Jalfrezi was also good, almost authentic. The Restaurant itself is really comfy also to sit outside feels very relaxing.The stuff is attentive and nice. All in all, I would come back and try a different dish. Only the Pappadam was missing , but you get some peanuts , also nice 👍😎
The food is of a good quality and it’s easy to get vegan dishes without modification. The food is less hot than I expect with Indian food, but it has a lot of spice and flavor.

We come here once a month because it is very authentic and very rich. Service is usually very good if occasionally slow. If you eat outside, which is nice when the weather is so great, it’s a lot for the servers as they are stationed inside. The little bell provided helps with that! Recommend the chicken and lamb vindaloo, with the rice palau or a fluffy naan. They are so big they are meant for sharing. Chefs are spot on and really care. Try the onion bhaji with mango chutney or other spicy sauce provided. Lovely location and property. Must try! Read less

We booked in here for NYE as we thought it would be a treat. We were quite disappointed. The food tasted OK but there was no spice at all in the curries and they were a little bit bland. We ordered one of the combo sets which came with rice and a naan. The naan was large but the curry and rice portions were around half the size of a typical curry, something we wished we knew before ordering. We ordered an extra curry and rice from the standard menu which were larger but still didn’t feel full after eating. My partner also had to wait a long time for a bottle of beer (only arrived after starters) while my red wine was served freezing cold, something I now expect in Thailand but hoped might be different in a more expensive restaurant. I wouldn’t return here. Read less

Although not quite like we experience in the UK, I would recommend this restaurant if you enjoy Indian food. We all enjoyed our food but especially enjoyed the onion bhajis which were amazing and vegetable jalfrezi. Naans were great too. Loved that you are asked how spicy you want everything and that you have a bell to ring for service. Prices are at least half the price we'd usually pay.
The service was good. The food was not too spicy. It was not pricey (quite affordable). I would recommend a visit. Try the Butter Naan (the Naans are large !) and the Paneer Lababdar.

Excellent food in cozy surroundings. Very clean and inviting restaurant. You can sit outside and enjoy cool nights or inside in air-conditioning. The food is very tasty. Chicken is Juicy and tender, lamb is succulent and tasty. Everything is made from quality ingredients and it shows. Highly recommended.
Food was pretty good but I got food poisoning from the lamb madras. Service was pretty good but I wouldn’t eat her again since I was throwing up all night from the food.

Very delicious Indian food! We ordered one butter chicken, one rice, one garlic naan and one butter naan and couldn’t finish it with the two of us. We especially liked the naan a lot. Friendly employees, good food and relaxing vibes!

Have heard a lot of people talk about this place (mind you, mixed opinions). Some feel it had better food when they were in the old location, (I haven't been there), some feel that the food is great but I prefer to trust my experience. I finally went there, first time with a friend and the second alone. I like the location, the ambience, and the set-up. The staff was welcoming, helpful and generally warm and attentive. The place is kid friendly, quite relaxed, clean and comfortable. Let's get to the food part; Okay, I am Indian and frankly wasn't impressed by the food. I found the Dal to be just about right except for the fact that it was too bland. The Naan had ample Garlic, which I personally love, but it was too white and a bit on the chewy side. Tried the Tandoori Aloo and that I can say was very well done. The Green Chutney was well made. All in all I would say that I can always skip a trip to Accha. They miss the Indian touch. In terms of pricing, I will put them in the expensive category if I have to compare what I generally spend on 1 meal in Chiang Mai. They have pretty much the same prices as some restaurants in Delhi, Bombay. Go there if you want to have a little bit of an Indian experience one of the evenings while I enjoy my Thai meals :D :D Read less
This is one of the best Indian food I ever had outside if India. Ambiance and service is at par. Owner is friendly. Close your eyes and go for it. Try their set menu. Its awesome.
Ordered 2 naans and fried fish. The naans were terrible, the fried fish is okay. The service is below par, no welcome or something they really doesnt say anything.. Like a robot waiter

This is only Indian cuisine we found in this area . Food is really amazing and authentic . We enjoyed the food and service . Everyone should must try , they guys are awesome . We had all three meals at this place .

We live in Chiang Mai and have recently been to this restaurant twice. Once as a couple and another time in a larger group. The food is wonderful with lots of variety. The service is just appalling- wait so long for them to come and take orders and we had to go to the front desk to order drinks. The staff are very slow and only 1 Indian girl is on the ball. You need to either put more staff on or train them in service techniques. It is a shame because the food is so good.

Lovely lovely food. We ordered too much as usual!!! Great place. Looks really clean you can see the chefs working through a glass window. The outside has a beautiful seating area. The price is great. We paid the equivalent of 18 pounds sterling for starters and main course plus beer. All round great experience. Highly recommended :)

This is a first time for my review but I go here several time. Lamb kadhi is the recommend, I order it every time when I go to Accha. At the first time that I ate it, I fell in love with Indian food, lamb was so soft and get along well with soup. Butter chicken and garlic naan is good too.

We ordered chicken , prawn biryani and it was awesome. Haven't tasted such a yummy biryani. It is definetly worth a try. Both the days we had our lunch from this restaurant. I would recommend to anyoneone who would like to have an authentic indian meal. There is also takeaway option.

Delicious, authentic Indian food, reasonably priced, pleasant decor. The waitress didnt speak any English and missed our order for one family member's main course. Although after all else had arrived and time had elapsed, we repeatedly gestured, pointed at the menu and asked, she did not catch on to her mistake and it wasn't until the manager (?) happened to pass by - after everyone else had finished their meal - that the message got across. She promptly ordered the missing course and we all waited as our hungry daughter rushed through her meal. No compensation, even symbolic, was offered. In summary, food was very good. Service, though well intentioned, was less so.

There are only 3 Indian restaurants in Chiang Rai we checked this one out to see it was open for lunch. Set in a lovely court yard with shade the food was excellent. The chicken madras and vindaloo were wonderful. The naan and allo mutter were also enjoyable. Service was great. Would recommend to any curry lover.

I really liked eating here with my study abroad group. The vegetarian selection is wide and has a dish for anyone who wants mild to super spicy. If you aren’t into super spicy, order the chicken tiki Marsala but mention mild because my friends forgot to do that and it was very spicy. The lemon soda sweetened literally tasted like a margarita. It was so good! I also recommend the mango drink.

The restaurant itself is just off the main drag in Nimman area and so very accessible. Wonderfully imagined restaurant space--architect did a great job of fusing glass walls with 19th and 20th century wood embellishments, including the magnificent door. Spacious entry, great seating that guarantees privacy, banquet rooms for larger groups, and a solid wait staff and front end that assures a classy and smooth dining experience. English is widely spoken, and so you'll receive great help in this language from staff and menu. the menu has the classic 30 or 40 Indian dishes. Nans, Roti, all vegetarian and meat dishes are exceptionally flavored, served hot, and exceptional condiments provided. I thought the chicken madras curry was great, but could have been happy with so much else that I saw passing before me. Nans are huge and fluffy; and the house wine at 100 bhat is a solid deal for a Chiang mai restaurant (both red and white are provided). A real international clientele. You can eat in on sultry nights, but the outside seating is totally recommended if it cools a bit by dinner time. Pricing is about average for restaurants in this neighborhood, so it should not be difficult to get out of here with a solid three course dinner for 500 bhat; though you can certainly enjoy a tasty main course for less than half this. What you get for your money here will, however, be hard to match at even the closest dozen restaurants on this street. Highly recommended for those who miss the tastes of Chennai and points north, or for those who just need a change of cuisine. Read less
A great place. Very authentic food. Would definitely come back as the food was really great and the service was lovely. It was always a pleasure being there. I can't wait to go back. Recommended :)

Came here late after we had arrived in Chiang Mai and wasn't let down! They have a large range of classic Indian food. We ordered a few curries, rice and naan breads (the breads are huge btw!!) and it was all really yummy and great! Service is good and reasonable price.

I wanted a paneer dish and I like it spicy. I was recommended to take the paneer tikka masala that was not spicy at all. I asked for some raw chili's (to sprinkle on top), and they bought a small bowl of while green chilies. I had to ask to have them chopped... The dish itself was passable, and not more. Meh.

Gotta say the food was really good although pricy compared to other restaurants in CM. The mutton biryaani was well prepared and the Chai and Gulab jamun were also awesome. Great ambience, good Indian music in background, friendly staff, good seating arrangements(indoor, outdoor and on the floor tables) and fast service. One less star because they charge a 3% per fee for CARD payments even when our bill was over a 1000 Baht and were not informed about this fee when I enquired at the begging of our order. Which didn't make sense but whatever that's their choice I guess. But overall a good Indian restaurant to go to.

Visited this place with 3 friends. I had never had Indian food before but everything we ordered was delicious. About 10 different dishes to share and drinks so a total bill at 1300 baht is absolutely reasonable. The only negative was the non Thai staff who could have been friendlier and more attentive, especially as we were there on a quiet night.

I've been to Accha may times. One of the best indian foods in Chiang Mai. The portion is quite big I recommended to order Combo set it come with two curry basmati rice and choices of Naan. And yes, Malasa chai smell so good. Parking lot available and its located in Nimman soi 5. The staff is very nice and of course very polite.

I have been to Accha a few times since it moved to its new, larger space, and it is my go to place in CM when looking for Indian food. The new building is great, plenty of indoor and outdoor seating. Very comfortable. The menu is pretty standard, with a wide selection of curries, briyanis, both meat and vegetarian choices. The service is friendly and efficient, and the food is served pretty quickly. It's not at the quality you will find in India, or London or Singapore, but in my opinion very good for Chiangmai. Read less
the best indian food in chiang mai, the price is not expensive,the taste is perfect. For me it is better food in India.

Lives up to its name. You can go in and your stomach will be full to your heart's content. Great place to go for a family, food is of great quality and the price is just right. They have moved to Soi 5, Niman and this new place is very spacious and has an outdoor eating area too. The ambience brings us back to the feeling of being in culturally rich India. Really hospitable , couteous and polite staff and we are very happy with the good service too! On the whole the experience was truly Accha.... Waiting to go again soon ! Read less

One can choose whether to stay on the inside or outside terrace of the restaurant nicely decorated in a typical Indian style. We ordered onion bhaji to share and two curries as a main dish with one naan bread and one rice and we were well satisfied. We were given complimentary peanuts and dips too. The price is right for the quality of the food and the service. We only ordered one bottle of water, and spent about 350 Baht per person.

It's exactly in the correct location on my Google map. You see it, all painted in varying primary colours, a delight for the visual senses. Everything is decorative and beautiful, the thick staff friendly. All the dinnerware, chairs were similar, great! Drinks were served at good cold temperature. But what about the food? The food did not quite match the standards of the decoration and the beautiful plates and cups. The meat was tender, but everything seemed to be reheated, nothing had great flavours, it was produced in a rather cheap way, but presented perfectly. Even the curry tasted a little strange. The attractive utensils, plates were confusing i never quite understood which were serving plates and what I can eat from. There didn't seem to be enough plates to actually eat from, all was in the presentation. The pickles, Chutneys, sauces were very good. Portion sizes enough to fill you up. The menu prices don't include VAT, which is always annoying to me. We still haven't found a good restaurant after 5 days in Chiang Mai, despite its reputation! Read less

You have a choice of sitting outside on a veranda, in a courtyard or inside. Staff are busy and attentive. You get a bell to summon them which is weird. The food was bought promptly and was very tasty. It was nicely spicy but not overly so. The roti was too fluffed up for my liking. But that’s not really a deal breaker. Oddly, the same song played the entire time we were there.

The environment is happy and colorful. The food is average as compared to all the Indian food I have had in the past twenty years in India (our production was in India and I had to travel to India 3 times a year for the past 20 years). I love Indian food. I was kind of disappointed with the Papad (a kind of crackers with pepper) because the restaurant presented only 3 pieces in a hipster way as shown in my picture, this is not traditional but modern. It cost us Baht 30 but in all the Indian restaurants I dined in came free as a snack. One more info for the dinners, be prepared with cash if you are not willing to pay the 3% surcharge on a credit card. Read less
New location, great atmosphere, had butter chicken and more, naans were the best, service was ok, could be better, would recommend

We haven’t been here in a while. The location is changed and not reflected on TripAdvisor maps/directions. Now located on Soi 5. Have been to this location but they hadn’t finished the courtyard and it is awesome! Good service, only reason for 4 star was my chicken was tough in a few pieces which was a first. It’s usually perfectly tender. Hubby’s lamb vindaloo was up to standard. I’m sure it was just a glitch, at least I hope so. Such a gorgeous weather day, perfect for dining al fresco.
I love the food and the atmosphere here. The staff are friendly and always there to help. The menu range is good with great choices and delicious Indian flavors. I would say try it!!
Beautiful space and good solid Indian dishes. Friendly service and great atmosphere. We will be sure to return!
Nice, colourfully decorated eatery with parking! The food we ordered was good, the waitresses could be a bit less dozy & attentive otherwise it was good enough.
Excellent food. Service was lacking but hard to find good service In Thailand so over all I recommend it. Garlic naan tasty. And can never go wrong with butter chicken

Only God knows how much I love North Indian cuisine and tonight we were lucky to stumble upon this gem of an Indian restaurant! Plenty of veg dishes and all of them are perfectly cooked and spiced. I had a paneer tikka masala, rice, and a cheese naan. All the food was served fast and friendly. And they were playing Indian music to accentuate the already great ambiance. We left full, happy, and a bit nostalgic:)) and didn’t pay too much for this joy of an evening. Keep up the good work guys!

We ordered veg food and few dishes without onion ND garlic too. Amazing ,delicious food ..ND top of it lovely hospitality by the owner ND the whole staff....food was delicious ,perfect taste ND spiced,like we have in india... Masala tea was superb...definitely recommend for the people searching Indian veg food..... Thank you joy for the gulag jammuns.

What your curry hit then this is a very good Indian with friendly staff and good tasting food. First found them when they were operating out of a small kitchen and serving food on the pavement of a courtyard but they have since moved into a "proper" restaurant and I have visited several times; never been disappointed. I would say this is a more anglicised version of the Indian curries I have enjoyed in the likes of Malaysia but the taste is good and the portions are more than adequate - I have never left feeling empty!! Parking in the Nimman area is always a pain but a visit to Accha is worth it. Read less

I love a good Indian curry and having lived in India, Malaysia and the British Midlands I have had more than a few. I was hoping to enjoy the food at Accha .. but it was only average. We had the veggie combo set plus starters and a main curry. Most of the dishes lacked any depth of flavour apart from an aftertaste of sterilising fluid, which I guess means the hygiene is good, but doesn't make for a satisfying meal. The dhal was really tasteless and the veggie curry too salty. The positives were the quick service and the Nan bread which was good. Maybe we were unlucky that night, but I just can't see how anyone would rave about it. Read less

The young hostess standing out front can be quite rude and short with her words, poor attitude (she goes out of her way and intends to be unfriendly)... this can ruin good food and good environment. (the food is nice, the general manager is nice,)... I am (was) an every day customer, and there is just no excuse for words that talk down a customer with a lot of attitude..... i complained but was not heard or taken seriously, ... so ill leave a review that the restaurant does not understand depth of customer service beyond the surface and does not understand the value or repeat customer.. there is no acknowledgment of the customer if there is an issue. Read less
Cant understand why this isn't rated more highly on TripAdvisor? The food was fabulous, lovely staff and great setting.
It's an above-generic Indian restaurant, a rather close shot to perfect Northern Indian cuisine, it is also very reasonably priced, therefore perfectly ok for a dinner out not too far from the hotel.

I’m from Vancouver, Canada, and I generally wont eat Indian as I travel because it so often disappoints. But oh! SO GOOD. Some of the best Indian I’ve ever had, and considering I used to live 2km from Vancouver’s Punjab Market district, that’s saying a lot. The portions are generous — 160 baht each for Korma and Butter Chicken. Huge man for 50 baht, the pilu rice can serve two, especially if you share a naan. The samosa pastry is so thin and crispy. Fantastic! They give you a little bell to ring for service too. :) Read less

We love this place so much! The onion bahji are. to. die. for!!! Don't miss out on that. I'd eat 3 plates of that only if it was a reasonable decision. Instead, we get a bunch of plates and always leave full, happy and feeling great. Service is great as well.
Food is amazing, good prices, good service, english could be a bit better. It is like being in india for an evening while travelling a thailand. So good, can only recommend
I love this place food is delicious and price is very affordable although located in a very expensive area of chiangmai i would really love to go again
Very quick service. Good homemade style food. Commendable that it's NOT oily as most North Indian restaurants are wont to be. Very economical too.

Visited on a Friday night. There was quite a big crowd but service was fast. We had mutton Rohan Josh, butter chicken, mixed raita and aloo Gobi with plain naan. Everything was great, really enjoyed our dinner.

We arrived at Chiang Mai after a week of a trip in the North and were craving to break the Thai food ritual. Indian food sounded great to us and we were happy to find this restaurant just a few minutes walk from our hotel. We ate a full scale family dinner- including chicken and lamb dishes that were excellent. We enjoyed the food so much that we decided to come back again the day after. Highly recommended!

Went with the Ladies of Chiang Mai. The restaurant provided a buffet of selected dishes. A nice dip into their menu. We all left with a smile. The staff are helpful. It was a rainy day but they do have seating on the outside. Only 3 parking bays.

It was a good walk from where we thought we needed to be but we were guided through some alleys and streets to where we needed to be. Good food and very reasonably priced. We recommend this one. Particularly the chicken saag was full of flavor. I'd recommend that one!!
Loved the food. Very tasty. A small place but good food, nice staff, drinks available. Easy to get to in a popular part of Chiang Mai. Ill go back.

Just finish dinner at Accha. A rainy night got us there as its near our hotel. Upon arrival, we were seated at a low table. My wife request a normal dinning table, the staff pointed us to a table that still undergoing bussing. A group of caucasians enter the restaurant shortly and they were seated immediately at the table thats meant for us. Another couple paid and left and we were seated soon after. We placed our order way earlier than the group of caucasians, our order as follows: 1 x Orcra 1 x Chicken Vindaloo 1 x Fish Tikka Massala 1 x Garlic Naan 1 x Pilau Rice 1 x Masala Tea 1 x Mango Lassi We waited about 15 mins, my wife had a feeling that the restaurant preferred Caucasian guests instead of Asian. I started to feel angry when the group of caucasians got their baskets of naans and curry ahead of us when we placed our order way ahead before them. I delibrately asked for the bill, foreseeing that the staff will realise their mistake, instead a male guy ununiformed came to our table asking why we are leaving before the food is served. He apologise and serve us our food promptly citing the cooking time for the fish dish caused the delay. I am in the Food business for 20 years, my wife is a chef. Do not tell me how long you need to cook my dish. So I did a quick temperature check. Food serve lukewarm on the surface, only hot within, indicating food has been waiting at the pass for a while. Food is average, service is attentive (Tuesday night). But restaurant choose to serve Caucasian better. In the midst of our meal, we withness the host serve another pair of Caucasians warmly, but a group of 3 Asians were made to wait at the door and they walked away shortly. Will never step foot into that restaurant ever again. To all Asians, bring your money elsewhere. Read less
I ate here for the first time yesterday and was so impressed. The flavors are delicious. It was the best Indian food I've ever had. Great service and lovely decor. I will be back!

I am from Bangladesh and so much familiar with Indian food as we use same kinds of ingredients and share very similar food habits and cooking style. A week ago I went there (the new one, nimman soi 5) with few friends and ordered more than 10 different kinds of dishes. Whatever we ordered we got the same kinds of taste. I think they use just a common gravy for all dishes. That's not a problem. I don't expect that they will prepare separate gravy for every dish because Indian foods needs a lot of times to prepare. But the problem is they just put different meats or fishes and served it. At least they should mix some more ingredients for few dishes and should prepare few types of gravy because some dishes should contain thicker gravy and some don't. And most importantly they should cook a bit more after mixing the gravy with meats or fishes to permit the gravy entering into meats or fishes. Nans and rotties were not properly baked. We hadn't try any kind of Biriany. Staffs are not that friendly and have less patience. They spent enough for decoration I guess but it looks average. No Indian environment. As I didn't like the food at all, I won't comment about price. But almost same price of other Indian restaurants in Chiang Mai. BTW a year ago I went to their old branch (near nimman Starbucks) few times. Everything was fine there except the availability of space. Summary: Food (1/5), Staffs (2/5), Decoration (3/5), Space (3/5) My final rating is 2/5 as I don't count anything except the taste of food. Read less

Visited their new premises which was delight to see the nice decor and detail in the building. I did however find the restaurant to have only a few tables and seats for the size of the room. I assume since its a new building the owners are still working on this. The tables were a little low for the chairs so my legs were pinned. However the food was amazing. Which is the core of what makes a restaurant great. You will not be dissapointed. If in the area and you love indian food i recommend you try this place. Read less
Great Indian food that's a nice break from Thai food. Very reasonably priced and portions are good. I loved the potatoes and peas dish. I'm not Indian though so I can't comment on authenticity.

I never visited the old location next to Starbucks on Nimman but heard good reports. The new place in Soi 5 is now my favorite Indian in Chiang Mai. Great location and a lovely building with nice decor and unlike the old place, aircon. The food was excellent, Vegetable Samosas, Chicken Tandoori Kebab and a Plain Naan. With a soda water the bill came to Bht265. I look forward to many return visits.

Tried the veggie combo: rice and naan with a sour dal darka and a bland curry made with frozen peas and carrots. 200 baht is not a lot of money but seems like a lot for such little effort on their part. Not the worst in the world, but the worst Indian I've tried in Chiang Mai so far.

Lovely little indian restaurant with outdoor seating. Food was good - not amazing but good and worth it. Owner is very friendly and staff are attentive. Would recommend to someone looking for indian food in chiang mai

When you need a break from Thai food, Accha is a great option for some authentic Indian food. I love their tandoori chicken, any of their chicken dishes are really good. I also like their biryani, but the flavor is not always consistent. The people are nice, and it's right off the main road of Nimman. Also great that they serve Halal food because some of my friends can only eat Halal meat. Read less

Great selection of North Indian food! Friendly staff with outdoor seating, so avoid during early afternoon. Their homemade achar is a rare find to Indian cuisine in Thailand, so it gives a great surprise!

This little gem is hidden away off nimman rd but worth the visit. Food was delicious, all eight of us tried something different and we were all happy. Staff can be slow, but ringing the bell for attention is quirky but works.

Heard about the restaurant on a blog and had good reviews so decided to give it a try. Thought the seating area was nicely done being outdoors and the staff made sure every table had a fan. The stools and short chairs weren't the most comfortable though. We had rice, garlic naan, chicken korma, chicken masala and lamb rogan josh. The masala was actually a mistake, we didn't mean to order it but turned out to be our favorite. We didn't like the korma, the taste was off and I've never had korma that color before, it was unappealing. In every other Indian we've been to, the rogan josh is thicker but here it was like soup. Overall the food was okay, we've had worse but also had much better. Prices are average 160TB and up for curry. Read less

We were craving for Indian food!! After we went there, we were extremely satisfied with the quality and taste of food. Their food is very nice with a good variety and definately quite fresh!! It's worth the price they charge.. The boys there are very friendly too! Wish they were located near the night market though!

There's not much competition in the Indian food restaurant market in Chiang Mai. This is the best of the bunch. The food here can be excellent or not that great--depends on the dish, day, or the cook, I guess.

This is the first time in my life for eating an india food, My indian friend reccommend me to this place and I have a wonderful moment here! I ordered butter naan and chicken tikki masala, it was really delightful with a signature smell. Cute style of calling staffs by ringing the bell, have a bit of mosquito but staff do pay attention to customers for mosquito spray So dont worry about that(It was late when i was there). Outside looks simply but this place is the treasure. Worth trying 👍 jake🍽
Authentic Indian food. Not bad , nice staff , delicious food and clean environment. A fair and reasonable price.

It's so small it almost looks like a big food stall, but don't be fooled, it's a proper Indian restaurant. I went several time for lunch and had a great time each time. Vegetarian, meat, the menu offers variety and 95% of dishes are cooked to pefection. (The 5% would include byriani and some barbecue meat, a bit bland to my taste). Service is friendly. Price is mid range - again, not street food, but still reasonable for the quality. You will be outside on benches that are barely comfortable - apart from that I cannot find any cons to this place. It's all pros, go get your meal now ! Read less

The staff was able to accommodate our family of 12. This is an outdoor seating eatery. The staff was very friendly and efficient and the food was extremely tasty! We had vegetarians and non-vegetarians in our group and all were very satisfied with with their meals. Some non-vegetarians recommendations are the chicken tikka, fish tikka masala, and chicken biryani. Their garlic naans were very soft and flavorful. We found this restaurant to be very reasonably priced (compared to the US). I would highly recommend this place for a nice Indian meal. Kudos to the owner - Jay and his staff :) Read less

Hidden in a small leafy outdoor nook tucked in front of Starbucks in Nimman area of Chiang Mai, I am surprised I haven't seen this place busier than it is. I eat vegetarian Indian and they have an excellent selection. All food, including naan breads are cooked fresh to order and arrive presented in gorgeous indian copper dishes. No request seems too big and the staff are very helpful. The naan bread is too die for and one curry is a good size to share between two people if you don't have huge appetites. Very reasonable price for an evening meal too.

Food was pretty good. I thought my meal was fairly bland. My friend liked hers though. I thought it was a little over priced for the quality. Very cool atmosphere though. They give each table bells to ring when you need service. I haven't seen this anywhere else in Chiang Mai. The wait staff was attentive and polite.

This place is great. My boyfriend and I have been here multiple times and is our go to spot for Indian in Chiang Mai now. Service is great, food comes out fast and it's so tasty! It's very reasonably priced and it's in a cute outdoor setting. We usually come here for date night and pop next door to the bar for 70 baht large beers! :) We will be back often! Highly recommend. Read less

Food was absolutely delicious, although restaurant was busy for a Thursday evening staff were quick for service and our starters and mains were served within 30 minutes from when we sat down. Lovely setting and great atmosphere. For all us Brits who love Indian food, me and my partner both believe this is the best Indian food we have had! Love this place wish we had time to go back *****
Accha retaurant is located centrally close to main road . caters for freshly cooked Indian meals . service is prompt & outdoor seating is nicely laid out

Ate here this weekend. We had to wait for a table, and whilst this isn't a big restauant, I see this as a recommendation, rather than an inconvenience. Attentive staff, plenty of choice, and wine by the glass is inexpensive. Our choices included a vegetarian combo, chicken tikka masala, samosas, chicken saag and assorted naan breads. Indian is my favourite food, I can honestly say, this was excellent, and I would return in a heartbeat. Read less

Not sure why it's so difficult to find a decent Indian in chiangmai. This place has attentive servers, small seating area, but decent enough, normal waiting times on food, but the food is so bland. Nothing really is spicy, serving sizes small, mid range prices- won't be returning. Forgettable.

I don't eat Indian Food, so when I joined a birthday dinner at Accha I was a little hesitant. Well the food was very good! I enjoyed my meal and the other meals eaten in our group were all well received, and all completely finished, so no wastage here. The prices seemed a little higher than average, but it was quality. The restaurant is outdoors mostly and very busy, but surprisingly quiet for the area it is in. This restaurant has won many local awards from eating groups, so well worth checking out if you like Indian Food.

We came here with a large group of about 10 people. The service was really great, and the food actually came ALL TOGETHER, which is nearly unheard of in Chiang Mai. The menu has a lot of great classics - chana masala, tikka masala, butter chicken, paneer dishes, etc. There was a great vegetarian menu and they offer Jain dishes as well. The food was *delicious* and the servers took the time to see how spicy we wanted our dishes. Even though this is an Indian restaurant, I would recommend erring on the side of requesting the dish be less spicy. The heat levels seemed to hew to Thai levels of heat/spiciness. I had the chana masala, it was lovely! I'm glad I asked for the less spicy version though. The garlic naan was fantastic. At the end of the meal, the servers had already split up the bill for us, without us even asking! We were all very impressed. My meal (1 chana masala, 1 garlic naan, 1 water) came out to 220 Baht (about $8 Canadian or $6 US). I love Chiang Mai prices!! Read less
Excellence fusion Indian food. Nice atmosphere. Warm welcome with good service. Get there early to make sure you have a seat.
Fantastic chicken madras and nan bread All cooked from fresh so dont go if your in a hurry. Enjoy the wait with a cold beer it is sure worth it

There are a couple of really good Indian places in Chiang Mai but this is my personal favorite. Curries and tandoori are all great, as they are at my other two favorite Indian restaurants, but I think what puts this one ahead of the rest are the breads and the raita. Best naan in Chiang Mai and best raita too. All the chicken dishes are excellent and go from not spicy at all all the way to burning hot so everyone will be happy.

A small hut kind of restaurant with Indian food. Service is great and reciprocate to customer needs. It's great to have an Indian food overseas. Ordered a vegetarian combo meal with a bowl of rice,one veg ,one dal and one roti. More than sufficient for an individual.
What a ripoff! Standoffish service and a hefty price tag! Very spicy food and slow service. I had a hankering for Indian food but this place was a total waste of time and money!

I had always wanted to try this place. I was pleasantly surprised. The kitchen is very small. They put out a great variety of tasty food. Nice service. Noticed a lot of takeout as I waited for my dinner. Nice nan bread, good chicken tikka. Tandoori chicken tasted quite authentic. Reasonably priced for Nimman area.
They were kind enough to veganize my aloo gobi! It was delicious and spiced right for my taste! Stuff is friendly and speak good english.
Great Indian food at very reasonable prices. Best Indian food in Chiang Mai. Loved the Daahl. Beef and wine at good prices.
Try the mixed naan - just awesome. Main course was also very good. Maybe a little too much coriander for our taste but overall a recommendation!

I had a take away - so can't comment on the interior or customer service. The food was lovely - I had a butter chicken and my boyfriend had a vindaloo, we also shared a garlic naan. I can't usually manage a whole curry portion but I ate all of this one. Sometimes I find butter chicken too rich but this one was spot on! Would definitely recommend a visit :) Read less

Very impressed with the Indian food, can't vouch for the other Indian restaurants in CM but probably wouldn't go anywhere else after the amazing cuisine we had last night. We eat Indian food regularly back in England (it's pretty much considered the adopted national dish!) so were very happy to find something to satisfy our cravings of home. Succulent chicken in the Biriani, perfectly cooked rice and even poppadoms and lime pickle (very surprised to see that here). Good service and value for money, would highly recommend to anyone seeking authentic Indian cuisine.

The food here is great! I had the chicken masala and the special naan bread. My friend had the chicken saag and plain naan and enjoyed his as well. Everything hit the spot. Service is quick and professional. We sat at a cute outdoor table where the staff put a fan on us to keep us cool on a hot day. Washed everything down with cold beers. Total cost for two entrees, two naans, two orders of rice and two beers came to roughly $18UDS. I would definitely return if in the mood for Indian cuisine.

We really try to eat local when we travel. So a minimum of European food. We did eat here because I just love Indian food since my trip there earlier this year. And I have to say the food was amazing. It took me right back to India. The staff is also very friendly and attentive. Prices are good and they have real wine (not Thai fruit wine) for 90 bath!

Created in a small kitchen, with outdoor seating, this small place serves up very tasty Indian. Have a good deal 200 baht meal deal for a vegetarian plate, which includes rice, bread, dahl and a curry. Nicely presented. Nice balance of spices. Highly recommended.

We ate here last night. The food was delicious! The staff were very attentive and helpful. Nothing was too much for them. The decor was fitting and it is a great, relaxing outdoor setting. We we be back again!
A small place but with very nice food. Try the lamb vindaloo with garlic naan bread. Spicy but good!

Great, easy, fresh Indian food. Not heavy or stodgy. I tried quite a few dishes, as there were a group of us there, and each one was as good as the last. So much choice and really friendly staff. Recommended.
Excellent food with al fresco seating. Everything is good here, so take your pick. Best naan and raita for sure. Ask for spicy and it will kick!

My husband and I ended up here at 9:30 on a Monday night when many other places were closed. Despite arriving when many people were finishing up, the service was efficient and professional, and the food was delicious. I had the chicken madras and my husband had the chicken vindaloo. We love Indian food, and this was some of the better that we have had. The prices were good, the service was great and the food was excellent. Highly recommended.
Me and my wife gone there ..it was yummy food .. I always there when I m in Chiangmai . The owners are lovely host.
We ordered the palaak paneer and the aloo mutter curry, garlic naan and mango lassi, everything was fabulous, and the paneer was green and vivid!

The Accra is basically a small building that houses a kitchen and bar with a few bar stools. The tables are all outdoors in a small yard with umbrellas. The food at this establishment is nothing but excellent, freshly cooked with a great taste. But it does not stop there, the price in my opinion is ridiculously low and great value. Try the spiced cone pampadoms with lime pickle and tamarind chutney. I suggest the Accha lamb or chicken curry both are the best, onion baji are so tasty. This place is defnitely worth a visit for quality Indian food at great value. They also have kingfisher beer and a glass of red is only 90 baht.
Great vegetarian dishes, all the stews where amazing. Me and my girlfriend went there every night and tried something else. You will have a great time

Food is fab, staff are lovely and you get a little bell to ring when you need service. It's not easy to find good Indian food in Thailand and I can highly recommend this place for flavour, presentation and attention to detail. Have been three times and I'm sure will go again!

We went to this restaurant based on the ratings on Tripadvisor and were very disappointed. The service was bad - no other word for it - just bad. Our onion bhajis arrived and they were overcooked, dried out morsels of nothing. The samoosas didn't have much flavour and there was a very strange sauce served with them. The main courses arrived eventually and whilst the flavour was quite good there was very little meat. In addition, the serving of rice was so small that we had to order more. Rice, being a staple of Indian food, should have been served in large bowls. We had to ask several times for the bill before it finally arrived. Not the most expensive place but there a other Indian restaurants in Chiang Mai that are much better. Read less

As always when we are away, we needed to find a curry house! Not for everyone I know but we love our Indian food and that doesn't stop when we are away on holiday! This was a real find!! We found it on trip advisor and gave it a go. It always says something when the restaurant was busy with Indians eating! The food was fantastic, extremely tasty and fresh. Also, the chicken was nice big pieces of breast which is much unlike Thai food. Yes we would eat here again but maybe I would ask for the Vindaloo to be hotter next time! The curry arrives much milder than it would be in the UK so if you like your food spicy, make sure you ask them to make it hotter! My vindaloo was no more spicy than a bhuna but it was very tasty non the less! Price for four starts, four adult meals including rice, nan bread, beers etc was 1400thb. Read less

This restaurant does quite good indian food, at affordable prices. We really enjoyed the dinner we had. It is right on Nimman Rd so you don't have to venture out too far. They also have cosy seating which adds to the experience. Will go back.

My husband and I popped by this food-stand type stop in between appointments for a snack since we planned on a late lunch, and we would highly recommend you do the same. The atmosphere is outside which is warm, but the waitress gave us a little bell to ring for service and the silverware/chutney dish is a gorgeous blend of metals. We ordered the palak paneer and being a purveyor of palak paneer, I was quite surprised by how thick, creamy, and flavorful it was! Do it better than we did and make a whole meal out of it. Read less

This place is just outstanding, the food is simply great & a delight to have, authentic Indian food which can also be customized to your choice just ask for chef Ravi, tell him your needs and you will get it. I have been visiting to this place since last more than 1 year & never ever had any complaints, the service is excellent & the order comes quite fast. This place is value for money, if you are in Chiang Mai and you want to have Indian food then this place will definitely tickle your taste buds.
Quite outstanding food, prepared in a tiny kitchen, in front of your eyes. Portions were large and spiced just right. Recommended.

This entire restaurant is about the size of my condo's bathroom, but they consistently pump out amazingly delicious Indian meals. People-watching outside is great, and they have a small bar. The food is always yummy, and the staff speak a variety of languages.

I ate here for dinner the other night and had a great meal. I ordered the vegetarian combo meal which came with lentils, mixed vegetable curry, rice, and naan. It was only 200 baht which was quite a bargain, especially if you're not sharing with a group. I would definitely eat here again.

I went to the branch in Chiang Rai - although I was a bit dubious about having Indian food in Thailand (only because I wanted embrace Thai food) I found that this food was very flavoursome and the service was good. The staff were also very friendly too. I would go back again if I was in Chiang Rai again.

Great service, friendly wait staff, delicious food! I had chicken korma, butter naan, and basmati rice. Everything was so good that I decided to get an order of chicken biryani to-go so that I can enjoys these flavors again for lunch tomorrow. I'll definitely be back, and will bring friends! 👍

Go here and order the chicken korma. Simple as that. Cute atmosphere hidden in a courtyard type jungle place. Amazing food so delicious I thought I was in India. Why are you still reading this review? Go here ASAP!

I love Indian food. It is, by far, my favorite food type. I am especially fond of a good biryani but It is not always easy to find. This place has got it! The chicken biryani was wonderful and my friends chicken korma was spot-on. The naan is fluffy and soft while the mixed raita was the perfect balance of yogurt and spices! The staff is super friendly and spoke great English. This place is outside seating only which makes it perfect for lovely cool evenings in Chiang Mai!

Accha Indian is a small "whoie in the wall" type Indian Restaurant. It is only very small, and has outside seating only. The best to way find it, it is directly in front of the Starbucks on Nimmanhaemin Soi 5. Have dined at Accha many times and have never been disappointed. Note, they actually use Ghee in their Indian cooking. We went there for lunch today and the food was the usual high standard, great flavours and very reasonably priced. Staff are very nice and speak great English. Highly recommend the Chicken Malai Tikka and the Garlic and Cheese Naan. However all the dishes we had were great. Read less
I enjoyed Accha's Indian food - great curry and roti. It's right next to a Starbucks, so if you were needing caffeine, bot are there. It's outside, so probably not a rainy-day spot.

Was on my way to an Italian place down the street because of all it's rave reviews, then I spotted this hidden gem. I walked to the other place and quickly realized I was at the wrong restaurant and came back here! I am so glad I did!! Made fresh to order and I could tell the chef did not cross contaminate with meat dishes. Many many veggie only dishes I had a hard time choosing, but landed on pakora, aloo gobi and garlic nan (270BHAT). I would have ordered more but I thought my stomach was going to bust! Staff was friendly, kind and dishes were definitely made with affection 👍 Read less
We keep coming back to Accha for the cozy outdoor seating, friendly service, and delicious and spicy food. Easy to find and good for lunch or dinner!

First time visiting this restaurant, a small spot just off busy Nimmenhaemin Rd. on Soi 9. I have generally not been impressed with Indian restaurants in Chiangmai, but was pleasantly surprised by Accha. The service was friendly and efficient. The menu was extensive with many traditional Indian dishes, including a large number of vegetarian choices. My friend and I ordered a few dishes to share, and all were flavorful and good portion size. They also carried an Indian beer, Kingfisher, that I like but often can't get in Thailand.
Good Indian food in a convenient location in Nimman. The staff are very helpful - I have been here many times!
Amazing food, we ve been there with colleagues and it was best indian I ve eaten so far. Rashmi kebab is especially wonderful.
i like to eat chicken tikka masala and Garilc naan is yummy and mango lassi it's very well and service very nice

almost missed this place as we walked past although the smell soon had me hooked and dinner plans were set for the eve. firstly it appeared to only have 4 or 5 tables so it nice and cosy. service was quick all through dinner. food was of a very good standard. we ordered popadoms which were a little on the small side but fine. next came the veg samosa a portion of 3 piping hot and up there with some of the best i have had in terms pf taste. they came with a great little dip which i think was a tamarind based sauce. our mains chicken biriyani and fish masala were both lovely and of good portion size. we also ordered 2 nans which where huge so 1 would have done us. i have tried a few indians in chiang mai and this has been the best upto now. Read less
We went twice in this restaurant as the service is fast and the food is truly awesome! The combos are good value for money! Me and my girlfriend love indian food and this place met our expectations 😄

Love my Indian food and had heard good things about this place so decided to check it out. Service was particularly slow and samosa serving size was tiny (definitely didn't match the stock photo they've used on their menu). Chicken tikka masala took about an hour but came out cold and was too tomatoey and pretty substandard. Not the worst Indian in the world but this place is definitely overrated.

This is a small place just off the main Nimman Road. There are various vegetarian options on the menu and the food was really nice. I've knocked a point off for the service, it wasn't great and they got my order wrong on one occasion. Nevertheless if you fancy a curry when in Chiang Mai this is a good option.

It was a wonderful experience. A great treat for the tastebuds! It was worth every penny. The ambiance and the location was great too. Not too noisy and crowded but not too formal either. A great place to meet interesting people and the owner and manager were really great host. They also host regular parties and gatherings which adds to the excitement.

Accha is a minuscule Indian restaurant capable of turning out impressive dishes including excellent daals, aloo gobi (possibly the best I've had anywhere), and paneer. I would recommend everything other than the vegetable curry (boring).. Pair any of the other curries up with yellow rice and naan and you will be satisfied. The seating is limited and outdoors, essentially in a courtyard, so it's better for groups smaller than 4... *absolutely bring bug spray*. The wait is justifiably long if they are busy or you over-order, so it's good if you are patient and enjoy a mango lassi or samosas first. The food is very nicely presented and I enjoyed walking away feeling stuffed every time.

It's super small, like 4 tables (max 4 skinny people) It's not clean. The waiters don't care. I am a fan of Indian food but here it's just not special. It was bland, it didn't burst of any aromas.. I would have given 'average' but a hair I unexpectedly found in my mouth after a spoonful of lamb vindaloo, made me choose poor. Hygiene is important. Ugh! Chefs please cover your hair!

Their butter naans and meat and vegetable combos are really good - comes at a price, yes, but quite obvious since they're located in that posh Nimmanhaemin Road. I enjoyed the service there - especially if you're a 'regular'!

We had a nice dinner at Accha. The food was good, but we found the price a bit high, considering the many restaurants in the area. A small child came to our table to beg, which made us a little uncomfortable. Nice outside dining area.
The food is very good but the service is not great and the portions are pretty small. This place seems to be a little overrated.

For me it is the second time eating indian food. My first time was 20 years ago and I didn't like it so much that I kept away. I decided to be adventurous and try this lovely rrstaurant. We got help from the man working there and I must say it was delisious. Thank you
I ate vegetarian thali and food was great. My friend ordered couple of different kinds of naan and they were pretty good. I give it a 4 star as the mango lassi that we ordered was a bit sour.
Best samosas in CM. Go here for that! They should open street stands for samosas all over town! Great daal and tandoori. Everything else I've tried here (and I've tried a lot) was good as well.

Needing to keep warm we decided to try this restaurant it was about 15 minute drive from our hotel. The staff are friendly and welcoming. The food was tasty and not to spicy, it was a nice treat to have good Indian food. I will return when next in the area.
Great for a change from all the delicious Thai food available. Quite a small garden restaurant but we were lucky enough to get a table right away. The food was delicious and generous serves too.
Having dinner here reminded me our meals in Dehli. I had a samosas and then Butter Chicken and they were delicious. It is a small place but with a great atmosphere.
Suggest arriving about 6 pm as waiting lines at 7. Open air (not aircon), and delightful in winter, can't speak about summer. Sometimes only open evenings. Good service and tasty food. Large menu.
Had lamb masala, dall, cheese naan and a chicken kebab to share between two. Was about ฿250 each with a beer each included. Great food and reasonable price

The food was okay but because of the price I cannot rate it higher. Small items like rice or a papad were really overpriced. The lassi was disgusting as the yoghurt was off so we didn't even drink the one we ordered. The chicken dish was nice
We stumbled across this little courtyard setting restaurant yesterday. The food was really good and I would definitely recommend this place!
Great little spot. Been here twice and will likely go back. Good value for the price and atmosphere. Pretty unique little place.

Checked out this place as girlfriend was craving Indian food. Wasn't expecting much, but was very surprised. It is small restaurant in front of starbucks on nimman street that is across from MAYA mall. Had biryani and curry and they were both amazing. They also make fresh naan bread with their food so I suggest trying each one. (garlic was my favourite) The owner came out near the end of our meal and offered all the tables a small treat. A classic Indian desert. Unfortuantely I cant remember the name but it is a small white moist ball that is to die for! Suggest having one. Will be back next time I am in Chiang Mai.

After reading reviews here, I have decided to go eat at Accha. What seems to be the owner took my order, he seemed totally unfocused, I clearly specified to have madras lamb medium spicy. I got it mild. What was surprising, it's when I mentioned it to him, he didn't acknowledge that he didn't pay attention to my order, instead of being open and learn from his mistake. Food was nothing special, the sauce was watery not tick as supposed to be in real Indian cuisine. It was not great culinary experience.

I've been here a couple of times and I will return again. The food is good, although I wouldn't say great. I like that they have a lot of vegan options and the Aloo Gobi is really spectacular. However the portions are quite small vs price and for a small bowl of rice is costs 60thb which is ridiculous!! You can get a whole meal elsewhere for that price so because of that I can't give them a higher rating.

I had the chicken vindaloo and yellow rice. I've had vindaloo many times before, even in Goa. Accha's vindaloo was perfectly spiced and I had it medium hot, which was delicious. In addition, the service was attentive and the owners made sure their customers were satisfied with their food and service. Prices were very reasonable for this kind of food in Thailand. The restaurant only has a handful of tables, so you can use some of the common tables near the Starbucks, just make sure Accra knows you want to order their Indian food. All in all, a really nice place to try Indian food in Chiang Mai.

5 stars for the food, but dining outdoors only limited the rating here to 4 stars as it gets hot in CM, and I've had a few meals there where the mosquitoes got to me while I got down to eating great Indian food. Overall, highly recommended, wish I could've given 4.5 stars. They offer excellent Indian cuisine whether you're a vegetarian or meat lover. Raita and naan are both very good, and all chicken dishes, whether curries or kebabs, are excellent. If you order vindaloo spicy its the real deal - serious fire - but it's also available less spicy for those who don't enjoy having their face seared off like I do. Aloo gobi and other veg dishes are great too. Friendly staff and pretty good service even when they're at capacity, which is often, very good service when there are fewer customers. Read less

Lived in Chiang Mai for more than 4 years and tried many Indian restaurants here and consider Accha to be the best. The atmosphere, service, and food are simply fabulous and I'm totally hooked on the place. My favorite dishes are lamb masala and lamb biryani with butter naan. The tandoori chicken is also great. Highly recommended.

After reading all the positive reviews for this place I was excited to try out my very first ever Indian in my favourite city. Living in Thailand for 5 years I can't say that I have had many Indian meals so when the opportunity arrives I go for it! We ordered 2 curries, some naan and a starter. The starter (fried cheese parcels) tasted incredibly bland and may in fact of been deep-fried cardboard. My curry was actually very nice and was complimented by the generous portion of naan, my partner's curry, however, tasted a little off and was in fact inedible. Equally off were the iPhone addicted service staff who were loud, unprofessional and seemed perturbed to be taken away from their beloved phones when we requested anything. After our meal was cooked the chef then decided to join the service staff in the smartphone marathon orgy, which I wouldn't of minded if not for the fact that this was all happening less than a meter from our table. Perhaps this was just an 'off' day but we won't be going back to this one I'm afraid. Read less
Everything on their menu is amazing! They are very prompt with your food and drinks and very friendly.

Living in Chiang Mai offers one lots of different places to eat. My husband and I love Indian food so when we come across a new Indian restaurant, we try it. We have been to all the Indian restaurants in Chiang Mai and there are several that are very good. Accha ranks way up there. We would have given it 5 stars but it's a very small restaurant and only offers outdoor seating, but the food is superb. There is a plus for us. It is close to where we live. If you're out and about in the Nimman area and you like Indian food, do not hesitate to try Accha. You won't be disappointed.

i had been to chiang mai with friends for songkran. luckily we found the restaurant in our soi. a very good environment, affordable price (you can say cheap comparing to other restaurants in BKK). we really had a great food, YUM!! the only concern we had, and we even told the manager was, there was no BIG BOTTLE beer. apart from that everything is great. must try

Each time that I'm in chiang mai i keep returning to this amazing Indian restaurant. The food . The service. The authentic dishes. The serve. Everything is great with a low cost. I already recommended about this amazing place to all my friends and relatives and all of them like it.

I have only order take away from this restaurant and was disappointed, ordered two chicken dishes, rice and a nan the chicken had a strange taste and had to throw the food in the bin, will be going back to my usual Indian restaurant next time.
I lived in India for three years and let me tell you..... This place is totally legit. Amazing food at decent prices. The naan is so fresh!
Delicious food, cozy place, excellent service, beautiful dishes Arrives regularly and never disappointed
Lovely little restaurant with great Indian food. Good value too. No desserts though! Attentive staff too.

The food here was great. Though the place is very small but it is very cozy, neat and clean. Being a vegetarian i was difficult to find a nice indian restaurant in chaing mai but this one really brought smile on our face. Even my kid loved the place.
Found this restaurant while wandering around and after reading the reviews decided to give it a go!! Delicious food quick and prompt service. Nice settings outdoor. And not pricy! Highly recommended.
