Great Indian food with a lot of flavor. The staff is very very friendly although English is not their strength. Environment is nice. It was a little hard to find. Once you see Starbucks on the main road, make a left before Starbucks and it will on the right hand side (next to a smaller and more hidden Starbucks). Very nice plates and utensils which look Indian). I would come here if you're in the area and not if you're not.

Me and my wife got to this place by accident, after a day tour in the north of chaing mai, about 21:00 in the evening. We are both vegetarians, and we wanted to sit in a more quiet place, so this place was the perfect choice for us. The service and the restaurant staff were super friendly. The nan bread with the vegetarian combo was awesome, as well some other plates that we order. The food is a litlle bit hot but great! The indian beer is also recommended after a long tour day. Read less

Located in NIMMAN ROAD Offer GOOD INDIAN FOOD for VEGETARIAN e NON at right price.. Very small OPEN STREET RESTAURANT with few but confortable accomodations.. Usually INDIAN FOOD not my first choice BUT THIS TIME COME BACK 2 TIMES IN 5 DAYS .lunch was served too STAFF IS FRIENDLY AND SPEEK ENGLISH TOO different types of indian food and beverage are available at right price. A Piece OF INDIA IN CHIANG MAI

The best Indian food in Chiang Mai. Great place for a casual dining experience. Service is friendly and prompt. With your menu they give you a small brass bell. When you want service, ring the bell, and someone will come over promptly. There is a good assortment of Indian food, all of which is tasty and well prepared. Because it is an outdoor cafe, it can be uncomfortably hot from April to June. They do have fans and coolers strategically placed throughout the seating area.

This is the best indian food I have experienced in Chiang Mai. Out door sitting only with great tasty food and very inexpensive. The food tastes great, inovative appertisers, decenct size portions and great wine and beer. There is only 4 or 5 tables so be prepared to wait or get there early, you will not be disappointed with Accha

This little Indian outdoor restaurant has very good northern Indian food, very attentative and friendly staff and a beautiful atmosphere. Good Indian food was exactly what we were missing in the Nimmanhaemin area and we are happy that we found it here.

The food we had both times was so good. The samosa sold out fairly early and we know why they were deicious, other entrees we tried were tandoor potatoes, pakoras and tandoori chicken. Mains included paneer mutta, lamb korma and chicken marsala. The garlic naan bread was light and full of flavour. All the food was absolutely delicious and young girl who took our order had so much personality it was worth going back than once! Loved it!

We live in Chiang Mai and this is our regular Indian place! Coming from the UK, it was tough to find a nice clean place with great food. We love this place and so does everyone we recommend it to! My friend has lived in India for 2 years and regularly travels back there... I brought him here and he Loved it and said its some of the best Indian food he's ever had! If you go, you definitely have to order the Accha platter !! And the chicken vindaloo! Or tikka masala! Basically, everything is good here.. Mango Lassi drink here is the best ever!! The owner and the employees are very friendly and make us feel welcome every time we visit. It's awesome so get yourself there! Read less

Like the area, like friendliness of the staff, like the relaxing atmosphere… The food is good but you can’t compare it with the real Indian food. I would call it the Indian-Thai fusion cuisine; and it is not bad at all, rather good. The portion size is just enough for one person. We’ve noticed that majority of restaurants in Chiang Mai do not serve the ridiculously large American portions, and it is perfectly OK. We definitely will return to Accha! Read less

Just moved to Chiang Mai, this restaurant is in my new neighborhood. Nice change from Thai food, which we love. Husband had vindaloo and I had madras curry. Both extremely tasty and satisfying. If you think the portions aren't big enough, you may be eating too much. One our new favorite places!

Got some vegetarian takeaway from there last night. They presented me with some warm peanuts and iced water while I waited, which was nice. Food took a fair while to come. Garlic naan and tumeric rice was fine, alu gohbi wa fair, but the other vegetable dish looked like it was frozen mixed veg from a packet in a bland white sauce. Maybe the meat dishes are better, but I wouldn't go back.

Went to have lunch at Accha yesterday. Ordered yellow dal, fish tikka and buttered chicken. Buttered tikka was too sweet but yellow dal was delicious. Need to look for the best Indian restaurant in Chiang Mai.

This restaurant was a great break from Thai food for our family. The Indian curries were tasty and were made as spicy(or mild) as we asked. The service was fast and efficient. (They even provided us with a bell to call the server! since we were at the far table in the garden.) This is a great find in the Nimman Road area of Chiang Mai for anyone looking for a change of flavors or just craving good Indian food.

This is a regular lunchtime haunt for me. I find that with a naan and a shared bowl of rice the portions are more than big enough to fill me up and at 188cm & 115kgs, I ain't small. You can request extra spicy if you are so inclined (which I am) and the food has always been fresh, tasty and inexpensive. And remember - a Korma ain't curry!

But it's not excellent as so many reviews claim. We got the lamb byrani and a vegetable korma. It functionally filled the belly. The vegetable dish included peas, carrots and corn. The spices were pretty good and the lamb was tender. Overall, I wished for something a bit more substantial for the price.
Small little place right in the middle of Nimman area , great food, plenty of vegetarian and tasty garlic naans.

Me and my girlfriend went here for our first night in Chaing Mai and we're not expecting much considering how cheap it is. We were very pleasantly surprised, the non vegetarian dishes we had were lovely and the naan bread was also particularly good. Staff were also very friendly and very keen to help out where they could. Would definitely recommend!
Lovely food, very reasonably priced. Staff were also very friendly, even better that that spoke very good English. Would recommend to anyone.
Tasty, nicely presented,fast and in a nice setting....just 20 meters from the main strip. Look for Starbuck coffee...Accha is located next door. Best Indian food in Chiangmai...

Well located in Chiang Mai's Niman Hemin quarter, you will be served tasty and varied Indian food (made by Indian cooks). Excellent choice of vegetarian food as well. Open kitchen, which I personally like, as one can't hide things.... The place is a bit tiny, not too comfortable, open air, but you quickly forget about it as everything else is just fine. The service is efficient, friendly, responsive and attentionate, the food is great and prices reasonable. Waiting time for the food, perfect. What to ask for more?
Been there a few times the last couple of months. Nice setting in a little garden. Just a few tables and quick service. Reasonable priced. A nice addition to Niemmans gourmet scene.....

Came across this place while wandering around Chiang Mai and was pleasantly surprised at how good was. The food also came out pretty quickly! I would recommend the mango yogurt drink to start. The only downside is that this restaurant is outdoors and has no indoor seating, but they have a bunch of fans that should be able to keep you cool.
in the local peopel and never try indain food before and im eat here in the frist time here is so amazing and price also good....^^
Food is delicious. With original Indian flavours. Service is good. We had both vegetarian and non. veg. food and everything was good. Not to spicy. Biryani is awesome.

The outdoor garden-style seating area is charming, casual, and conveniently located just off of Nimmanhaemin Road. The service is fast and reflects the hospitality and friendliness that Thais are renowned for. Like many restaurants in Chiang Mai, the prices are reasonable. On our last visit, my partner and I each ordered a beer, water, garlic naan, and a main dish, plus a bowl of yellow rice that we split, for about $10USD per person (including tip). We have been in Chiang Mai for a month and have dinned at Accha at least once a week in that time. As a vegetarian, I am grateful for the wide selection of vegetarian options.

Boring of cafe and bakery pop up in Nimman? This food shop is a good try. Even the shop advertise a fusion india, but I would say it is very authentic. Indian food here is nice and delicious. Try some combo set, it's worth! However, the food is just standard. Nothing surprise or creative menu. And, if you visit during the day, the outdoor ambience is also not much nice either. The staff is very nice and helpful. You can ask some food restriction i.e. No butter, less fat. This means all dishes is freshly cook. The presentation of the dish also nice. Another good thing is the price is very reasonable. Read less
I can't stop eating here, it's just so good and well priced. A must visit while in Chiang Mai. A filling meal for approx. 200 baht

Acchu is a small bar and patio style restaurant in a very upscale neighborhood of Chiang Mai. The food is excellent, authentic, and reasonably priced. A nice innovation: When the wait person seats you at a table, he/she will give you a menu, optionally take your drink order, then place a small brass bell on the table. They will leave you alone until you ring the bell, then they will promptly reappear to take your order.

It all starts from the Naan, you know a place is proper when they have invested in an authentic Naan Oven which Accha has done. Couple this with the curries and the condiments and you have yourself a fantastic meal. I have been here about 5-6 times and have always left satisfied.

Delicious fairly authentic Indian grub comes out of the little box of a kitchen at Accha. Beautifully presented in copper dishes with nice touches like home made pickles. Everything we had was tasty (papads, dal, chicken biryani) and the portions were generous enough to make a lunch the following day as well. Tables are set back from the street in a pretty little courtyard garden. Charming and attentive service too, not always a given in Chiang Mai.

We haven't tried any other Indian food in Chiang Mai but our recent visit to Accha may prove to be the only Indian restaurant we need to visit here. The quality and flavor of the food was excellent, chicken tikka masala had wonderful flavor, cheese naan was also great but next time will probably go with the garlic. A glass of house red wine was only 79 baht, somewhat of a small pour but still a deal, small Chang was 60 baht. Service was great, very attentive and a real care for quality of food. Definitely check them out if you're in the Nimman area.

So a friend and I bought some late night take away from Accha and while the food was tasty my non-vegetarian combo dish didn't contain any meat at all - only a ton of sauce. Did I get what was left in the pot at the end of the night or isn't the non-veg combo suppose to have any meat in it? Anyhow, the sauce was good, the bread that came with the dish was somewhat stale and the veg combo was really good albeit a bit pricey compared to other places.
Been there a couple of times now. Great food. Great vibes. Friendly staff. My favourite dinner place at nimman.

Im never try indian food before but i walk past this restaurant often than i think i should to try because the smell very good. And today i come and have veg samosa , chicken curry , plain naan, white wine.... Its so good of the test , value of money if you never eat indian food munch to try it....Accha

It was our first visit to this establishment and it was worth it. A menu with adequate options both for vegetarians and otherwise. Prices were generally good. The size of the portions were neither big nor small. The quality of the food was of high standard. This is an outdoors establishment so do not expect luxurious sitting arrangements or table sets. Having said that, this was not a show stopper for us as the "casual garden" ambience of the place was welcoming. The staff were very helpful and spoke more English than the average Thai. Also, being located on the side of the main street (Nimmamhaemin) saves the visitor the noise whilst being literally not more than 10 metres off it. On the whole, recommended and we will personally visit it again. Read less

Taken here by my son and his wife who have lived in Chiang Mai 9 years. My wife and I toured extensively in India many years ago and the Accha is up there with the very best! Food is created by a passionate Indian chef who is totally dedicated to providing top class cuisine for the lucky diners who find this place. The owner and staff are all very courteous caring and hardworking - nothing is too much trouble. Open air seating (which we like) - and is probably expensive by Thai standards - but I absolutely enjoyed every last Baht I spent and left the staff a big fat juicy tip which I hope they all enjoyed. Big Up and thank you to the Accha ! Soy 9 (Opposite Tesco Express Nimmahaemin Road) Chiang Mai in front of Starbucks Read less

The food was superb. We liked the biryani, non-veg set, tandoori chicken, cheese nan and roti. All are delicious, even the chutneys. The best part is that you can smoke in this open air restaurant. We met the owner and his wife who were very pleasant and hospitable. The staff were also very friendly. We had brunches, and dinners, and been there at least four times during a week stay. I will go there again whenever I'm in Nimmanhaemen area.

Went there for a curry and some onion bajis this evening after I heard it is the Nr.1 address for homesick indian tourists. That sounded like a promising indicator to me so I decided to give it a try. It wasn't a disappointing experience at all, in fact it was quiet a delight. Atmosphere was pleasant, Service good, the indian food was delicious, in fact so delicious that for a moment while I was eating the curry it felt as if a little indian angle was peeing on my tongue, the prices where absolutely reasonable. Joy, the lady who runs the place also was very charming. This definitely is my favourite Indian Restaurant in town, by far. The wine menu isn't extensive but it is no issue to bring a bottle from outside to compliment the freshly baked Naam bread and a bowl of their rich curry. See you guys again and thanks for a pleasant and delicious sunday evening. Read less

we arrived during songkran holiday, the thai new year. everything was closed so we decided to eat what even is open...we find out that there is a small restaurant with an amazing food, a great biryani,great serving,and the most important very low rates. we can 3 hungry girls,wet from the water splashing... we seat in the garden, enjoying the authentic indian food and also the indian tea (i forgot it's name). it's not a high class restaurant but it's really a great place to enjoy indian food with your friends or your family. we are now in bangkok and missing the great food that we had in accha restaurant, to compare we eat indian food in sukhumvit soi 5 in bangkok and it's not the same as the great food that we eat in accha, also the prices here in bangkok are twice than accha. Read less
Great indian fusion food ever!!!! Type of different curries are recommended. I love this place.. Must try!!

Randomly picked the place. It was still early when we visit. But they're so nice to have us before their opening hour. The authentic indian food and i like the fact they're not pricy. A wide range of variety. vegetarian menu also available. No longer need to travel to india as long as we have such place in chaingmai. Ohh.. Almost forgot to add the fact that they speak fluent english.
Very good taste of Indian food and Chic location. I went there with my friend and we enjoyed with food, Warm welcome from staff and good service. Next time we gonna go back there again and again.

Good food and great service. Spicier food is on request so if you want some more zing make sure to ask for it. Helpings are good and prices are very affordable. All round great location and atmosphere.

Ate here with my boyfriend during our short trip to Chiang Mai. The food was delicious, particularly the fried paneer appetisers. We live in Phuket, Thailand, and have found Indian to be the more expensive option so we were pleasantly surprised when our bill came to less than 900bt. For that we had 2 appetiser, 4 alcoholic drinks, I had the meat combo set and my boyfriend had butter chicken and two naans. The service was great, which is rare in Thailand, and we were given a bowl of warm peanuts as soon as we sat down. Definitely recommend this place and we would be regulars if we didn't live the other end of Thailand! Read less
Very good taste. Good location and not expensive. I will tell my friend to come here and try this food....
Terrific food, even good for the children as broad selection available, quiet corner next to Starbucks, wish it was close to home!

Overlooked but well worth a visit. Situated on Nimman Soi 9 next to Starbucks Accha is serving authentic Indian food. The small restaurant got a great selection of tasty Indian choices - great curries and plenty vegetarian choices. Cozy and friendly and at a very inexpensive piece point Accha is a great addition to Nimman and fast becoming a favorite among locals and expats. Easy going el fresco dining and worth every Satang

Spent a great evening with friends at the Accha. Food was excellent & well presented. price was very good The staff were attentive & friendly . This was my fist visit to the restaurant and I look forward to returning again soon.
First, I love the spices and the moistness of the Chicken tikka... The naan breads were nice, hot and fresh when delivered to my table... The prices are low with average meal about 300 baht.

Hi. I arrived to the restaurant on 14th march with my wife and my children (6,2 ). Usually my children don't eat anything except pizza,but here they ate everything! We chose fish tika+samosa +chicken tika and 2 more mixed recommended by the owner. Everything was so delicious. I recommend this place to come also with children,my kids loved it. By the way the prices are so low. I think it's the lower prices that i ever ate in Indian restaurant. Try also the drink mango lassi-my 6 years daughter enjoyed it. I will come back here in my next trip in Chiang Mai for sure !

AAAHHA i never felt so happy to write a review for such an excellent food and restaurant. believe me i can never imagine that a small restaurant located in center of chiangmai with a very lively atmosphere can serve such an AUTHENTIC Indian food with a finger licking taste and high standard service QUALITY. I have to mention here the hygiene maintained was EXCELLENT. Staff and restaurant owner itself dedicated to make their costumers to feel comfortable and make their visit most memorable. The food is served in authentic indian plates which enhance the enjoying of eating. I am sure in thailand you can never found such a delicious indian food with very reasonable prices.Everything in menu is worth to order. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS RESTAURANT :-) Read less

I've been to Accha several times, and it never fails to deliver. Really tasty and authentic Indian food (my Indian friend was very impressed!) at good prices. I highly recommend the aloo gobi and the "Accha Special Kadhai Paneer." The mango lassis are also delicious!

Hi. It's my 2nd visit here. I came in the 1st time in the beginning since the place was opened and the food was great. I came yesterday with my guest from bangkok. He loved it! I was very happy to see that my guest love the food because he is not easy with food...the food is great. The location is great in a small place near Starbucks.the service is great . The prices are very low food indian food.in bangkok me and my geust paid 3 times more for the same food. As a return customer i highly recommend about this small and lovely restaurant. Everything is clean and the food served with authentic plates,spoon ,nife etc. The chai is great,i loved it. We ordered many kinds of food and everything was great . it's the best fish tikka that i ever ate! This restaurant show to everyone that Indian food can be with reasonable prices and not with the expensive prices that you can see in other restaurants. Read less
Best Indian food I've had in Thailand. The presentation is fantastic, and they really know what they're doing! Everything is home-made and well done. Definitely recommended !
The food is fantastic at Atcha, definitely the best Indian in CM, and you get real value for money. Great location and atmosphere. I'd eat there everyday if I could! 5 stars for sure!

This is one of the best restaurants in Nimman by far, and there are a lot of contenders in the area. I've eaten there a few times and have never been disappointed by my choice, you really can't go wrong. The meat is always tender and delicious, the spices are perfect, and they always make it the ideal degree of spicy which is amazing when you consider you only give them vague words like "a little spicy but not too much". It's a little tucked away and definitely a blink and you'll miss it place, it's only 4 tables and a tiny bar space outside in front of Starbucks but it's soooo worth finding. They're lamb is especially good there, if you like lamb, definitely try the lamb option.
The best ฿500 I have spent in Thailand, great food great value. Authentic Indian food in Chiang Mai. A small and intimate venue just if the Nimmanhaemin rd.

We've lived in Chiang Mai for years and had never been impressed by Indian food until we ate here. Delicious lamb, tender chicken, perfectly cooked nan. Nice atmosphere and staff as well. We'll be going back.
Awesome daal and machurian really place to go very nice naan Drinks they serve beer are chilled Deserts are delicious service is prompt they serve hot food Owner is nice Divyanshu Read less
Lovely food, great ambience, happily served delicious food... What else someone wants!!! It's just awesome.. Thanks for the lovely treat given by all staffs n members. Must go!!!!!!

Hi we just visited yesterday 2-1-2015 and it was awesome , we are vegetarian and jain , they have all food , the best was dal makhani and paneer lawabdar ... The jeera rice was also nice with lots of corridondor, the owner Ms. Joy is very helpful and nice girl . Three cheers for them , they serve all alcoholic drinks too . Thanks MJ Read less

I first checked out this place because it was conveniently located just off of Soi 9 Nimman Road. It seemed small for an Indian Restaurant but after giving it a try my friends and I were hooked. The chicken masala with naan, tandoori chicken, and chicken pakora are my favorites. I organized a group dinner at Accha to celebrate Diwali and since then I often come across friends having dinner there. I've now got their number saved for easy take out when I get cravings. I also recommend this place to friends because the owners are very customer services oriented and will see to it that you have a great experience!

Very nice and lovely place, just like a small oasis. The food is really good and the service of the waitress was nice too. The price is not too expensive, comparing the food quality and taste. Will definitely coming soon :) :) Thank you for the lovely meal.

Look, I've got to be honest here. The above average vegetarian dishes, really deserved a better seating arrangement. Bending over in a contortionist manner, while trying to enjoy a nice meal is not ideal. So, even though it felt like a cramped fast food joint, all is forgiven as the dish was heavenly! Hope you guy's can expand the restaurant soon.

We have been twice to the new charming little e-y Accha Restaurant at the top end of Soi 9 off Nimman Rd. The courtyard ambience delights with fairy lights, and the new menus make it easy to order. The food is the real deal- excellent Indian cuisine cooked in a tandoori oven by an authentic Indian chef! The tandoori oven cooks scrumptious naan and roti breads that mop up the yummy sauces from the tender and tasty meat and veggie curries. Best of all the food tastes freshly prepared and perfectly spiced. Nimman has needed a good Indian restaurant, and Accha certainly fits the bill! Highly recommended.
I had briyani rice and tandoori chicken.. so so good! Definitely one of the place to eat when you are in Chiang Mai and craving Indian food.

Tucked away in Soi 9 is this lovely little Indian restaurant. Initially, I walked straight past it but was very happy that I turned around and gave it a try. It has a lovely ambience and the owner, Joy is a real sweetheart and very hospitable. The food is delicious and great value for money (and has vegetarian options). You can also buy a decent house wine that doesn't break the bank. It has become a regular in my weekly dining routine and I am sure to take visitors from overseas to enjoy the experience too. Very worth the visit.

You might have missed this tiny place, or you might have seen it when it first opened and only had a few bar stools up... but now it's up and running with an actual Indian chef and serving food in addition to running a great little bar. It's small, tucked behind the bar on the corner of Nimmanhaemin and Soi 9 and right at the top of the little walk to Starbucks. Benches with Indian cushions and "coffee tables" have been added, along with dishes like vegetarian biryani and an "Indian burger" -- which is a chicken wrap by another name. TIP: If you've met a new friend at one of the raucous sports bars in the area, this is the perfect romantic spot to seal the deal for the next date. Closes at midnight. Read less

Hi all We spent out last night in chiang Mai at the night market We asked a good recommendation from the concierge at le meridian And he told us that there is a new place that it's worth to try We came about 9pm and order some traditional dishes recommend by the owner and also beer and chai tee We have to say that it was amazing .everything was so good ,so delicious ! Another surprise was the price,comparing to other restaurants the price is a great value for Money . Good service and great food! Read less

I was on Chiang Mai last week and was missing indian food and decided to try this place based on the good reviews , everything was great and I really enjoyed my meal and the prices were fair too The owner was there to check on us and we got great service to go with the great food I would recommend to anyone visiting Chiang Mai
Hello guys , I came with family to chiangmai and had wonderfull delicious food at this Restaurent , they serve hain food also , very nice owner , her name is joy . Will go back again Mohit jain
We had a great night. Food was amazing. Service was also nice.wish to go back to this place when we come back for our next vacations in Chiangmai :)
Fantastic vegetarian food. Great portions and great value for money. Friendly staff and nice general ambiance. Highly recommend this place even if you are not a vegetarian- which I am not.

I love this restaurant so much. I have been coming for years, every time I’m in chiang mai. The food was delicious as usual. Not a lot of fuss—simply really, really good vegetarian food. The Anchan pad Thai and the red curry pumpkin are favorites, but everything is so good! Staff are quiet, helpful, kind. I can’t wait to come back.

Fantastic vegetarian eatery offering a wide variety of traditional dishes, including fried rice, curries, Thai soup, and delectable beverages. I've been a few times already, and I will most likely return since it's always so amazing! The tempeh curry in coconut milk is highly recommended, and as you can see, the amounts are substantial.

Great taste, good portions, and good prices, this is a great value restaurant. We enjoyed four dishes for two people - green curry, pad thai, som tam, and khao pa - but there was too much food, so we left some. It was a shame that there was no alcohol on the menu.
The food amaizingly tasty. The vegetable they use so fresh and cook perfectly. During our stay we eat most of our meal here. Highly recommended.

Anchan is a GREAT restaurant on the 2nd floor (walkup) in the Nimman area. So many things looked good on the menu, but today I opted for the fried tempeh in tamarind sauce, which was so crazy delicious! Their kitchen is so open, which I very much appreciate, but I do Not appreciate being treated rudely by the cook and being told not to take pictures.
Very tasty vegan dishes, food is very fresh and full of flavour. Highly recommend this place. Excellent and delicious food!

We visited this restaurant a few times and enjoyed delicious food a lot. We tried drinks, fried rice, tempeh curry, and a few more items. Everything was superb. Food was not very spicy yet good to taste typical Thai.
This restaurant is fantastic. Very tasty vegan dishes, the food is very fresh and full of flavor, good price, delicious food.

My wife an I have just spent 8 nights in Chiangmai, 6 of those nights we ate in the Anchan restaurant just off the Nimman road. The only reason we didn’t eat there every night was it was closed on a Tuesday and Wednesday. We tried to work our way through the menu but unfortunately failed as there was too much to choose from. Every dish we had was fantastic, so fresh and tasty and good value for money in comparison to the 2 other vegetarian restaurants we ate in when Anchan was closed. Authentic Thai flavours and excellent food. We can not recommend this restaurant highly enough even if you are not like my wife and I who are vegetarians. A must visit when in Chiang Mia. Read less

My wife and I recently had lunch at Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant. She has crispy tofu with tamarind sauce and I had vegetables stir fry. Both dishes tasted delicious and the vegetables were fresh and cooked perfectly. I recommend this small, tucked away restaurant even if you are not a vegetarian.

The food is standard vegetarian and vegan food so no need to rave about it- i last ate here 3 years ago, and you’d expect that they would be grateful for returning custom but no, same old robotic service- they didn’t even say ‘thanks’ as we left. I came back for the nostalgia of the good meals I’d had here with friends but businesses aren’t built on nostalgia- if you want customers to be regulars they need to up their game, and make customers feel valued.

Amazing vegetarian restaurant with many traditional food options (from curries, thai soup, fried rice and delicious drinks). I already went a few times and will definitely be back as it’s always so good ! Don’t miss the tempeh curry in coconut milk and as you can see portions are very generous !

I order food from Anchan a few times each week, and it is always super fresh and scrumptious! I highly recommend that vegetarians, and non vegetarians, try this yummy one- of- a -kind restaurant. My favourite dishes are: Indian yellow curried vegetables, stir fried eggplant with miso and Spanish omelette. Chef A is meticulous about using the freshest and best organic vegetables, and his flavour combinations are the best- while only using healthy oils! I love this food, and I pine for it when I am away from Chiang Mai! Enjoy enjoy enjoy!!

Went there many times during my two weeks stay in Chiang Mai. Tried many different dishes and they were al amazing! Loved the butterfly pad Thai (purple). Loved that it was vegetarian and organic!! And very close to my Airbnb

The pad thai is so so amazing, I loved it :) It is the best one I've had whilst in Thailand. It was so flavourful :) the restaurant itself is nice, but easy to walk past it, as it is on the first floor of another building.

The food in this restaurant is amazing! Probably one of our favourite meals whilst we were in Chiang Mai. It was spicy without being too spicy, whilst still being very flavourful! The menu options were better than most places as well in terms of variety. Definitely add this to your list if you're in Chiang Mai

We discovered this lovely little restaurant through Google reviews,and were delighted each and every time we visited. They have an extensive vegetarian menu and we tried different dishes each of the 5 times we ate there. Each one was exquisitely spiced and full of flavor, made with fresh, perfectly cooked organic veggies. Like some reviews have mentioned, food is made to order, so allergy needs or specific food requirements can all be accommodated. We have an onion allergy in the family, and while many Thai restaurants have onion in their curry paste, Anchan creates their own so could ensure our food was onion free. The fruit smoothies are amazing as well... I think our favorite what's the mint lime combo. Very reasonably priced with most dishes ranging between 95-120 THB. The servings are very generous and always delicious! Read less
Food is just ok! We ordered two salads and except for the chilli peppers, they have no flavour at all. Service is not good.
Simply the best food, hands down in Thailand. We have been 5 times, choosing different dishes and all are amazing. Don't miss this amazing place. Prices super reasonable and great service.

I traveled quite for only for this restaurant. Doesn’t worth it. I was excited when seeing the purplish colored noodle, but it turns out just noodle soaked in salt. I assume it won’t be that healthy with this much amt. consumption of salt. I don’t think I can finish the plate. Probably few more bites.

Very RUDE staff, food is alright, prices are high!! Took friends visiting form abroad who were certainly NOT impressed--damn shame! Asked for NO SUGAR but got it anyway. Used to know the owner-who used to care--but !! Will never go back again. There are many other places that serve great vegetarian food with friendly staff at reasonable prices.

We eat at this little restaurant so many times in the past, so this year we wanted to repeat the past experience and ordered our favorite Massaman curry and Green Curry. Both did not disappoint us. The meal was concluded with the vegan gelato made by Fu-rei-ko, a new addition to the menu.

This was my favorite meal in Chiang Mai. Hands down, the best. We had the salad with crispy fried banana flower, soooo delicious. And, Panaeng curry with rice, which is my favorite curry, also a home run. Also, the iced butterfly pea flowers blue iced tea is so refreshing!

Aloha, we had khao soi, pad thai, massaman curry, and these corn fritter things which I actually liked the most. The curry and khao soi were on the spicy side, but I liked it. The pad thai was meh. A bit on the expensive side but big portions.
We went across town to sample their food based on the stellar write ups on the internet. Food is as good as Reform Kafe, GoodSouls - just a tad pricier. Staff is very attentive and pleasant.

Lovely food but horrible service. Unfortunately we were still waiting to be served any food when, having already waited at least 20 minutes, the table that had come in after us were served two dishes. I then saw a waitress approach that same table with our tempura, and watched as they rejected it. She looked confused and took it back to the counter. When I approached the counter to claim it (and mention that we had been overlooked) I hadn't even said a word when the frazzled chef caught my eye and snapped at me that we have to wait until things have been cooked. I realise he must have anticipated yet another complaint about wait times and thus be frustrated, but he really must learn some customer service skills to tone down the thinly veiled contempt he seems to have for customers - something many others have noted here. It left a sour taste despite the delicious food and I will now take my money to the better vegetarian restaurant off Srimankalajan. Read less
Dropped in by coincidence for lunch and ended up very happy. Especially happy with the Tom Kha, but the red curry fried rice was also really good. Will be coming back to try more from the meny

Just got yelled at by the waiter AND the chef for not having to want sugar in my food. I sent the drink back as I didn't want sugar, still needed to pay for it and staff was very unfriendly about the entire thing, even though I didn't refuse to pay. Very unusual for Thai people to be that rude, never happened before.

Went to Anchan recently at a friends suggestion. I’m not a vegetarian but do try to eat healthy. This is a nice casual place on the 2nd floor on a small Soi off Nimman Soi 8, past the 7-11. They have a large menu of vegetarian specialties, and seems they substitute tofu in most dishes that usually come with chicken or pork. The portions are very generous, and the food very tasty and spicy. The service was a bit slow, but efficient and friendly. The prices are a bit higher than some nearby Thai places, but the portions large and the food very fresh. Read less
Definitely worth a try here. The best vegetarian restaurant I have been visited to. Wouldn’t regret.

Struggled to get a table which is a sign of it's popularity. We shared banana flower salad (delicious apart from too much celery), fried chilli tofu, sweet and sour with cashews, and red curry rice (too heavy an aniseed flavour). Overall nice but a couple of overriding flavours that were too strong for the food in 2 cases as described.

I will keep this simple. The vegan food was delicious. The flavours were amazing - spicy Thai as you imagine. The place is functional. I will definitely return before leaving Chiang Mai and I have no hesitation to wholeheartedly recommend it. Treat yourself.
Had an amazing vegetarian meal at Anchan! Would heartily recommend the massaman curry and pad tai. Service was good.
This is the best restaurant in Chiangmai!!! We love the menu, we would like to stay longer so we can try everything! The service is great and fast and everything is affordable!!! Can’t ask for more

Anchan might not seem the cheapest option at first, but the portions are big and the Pad Thai is the best in Nimman. A lot of options and also dishes you don't find anywhere else. Good for Solo travelers and small groups!

Anchan is a GREAT restaurant on the 2nd floor (walkup) in the Nimman area. So many things looked good on the menu, but I opted for the fried banana flower salad and massaman curry with a butterfly pea flower drink (anchan means butterfly pea flower). It was all so crazy colorful and delicious! I loved being there.
Generous but not oversized portions, and delicious everything. After 3 weeks here this is our absolute favourite.

This is my second time visiting Anchan, and it definitely my favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai, with all the great dishes and very reasonable prices. Here are few recommended dishes, they are all delicious. - Lime Mint Smoothie - Long Eggplant Thai Miso Stir Fry - my favorite - Crispy Fried Banana Flower Salad - Mixed Vegetables Stir Fry No matter you are vegetarian or not, I am pretty sure you will loved it ! Read less
Nice restaurant serving plants. Excellent flower salad. It's upstairs in case you don't see it from the street.

If you like vegetarian food that is not just 90% tofu this is the spot. I had the vegetarian Green Curry and it was the perfect temperature for me and full of veggies. The price was middle of the road for this area and certainly not a concern. The service was great and we were given a piece of double sided A4 paper which was the menu and a pen to write on it what you wanted. I wish I could have tried a lot more of the options as it all sounded so good.

It is a highly recommended restaurant. Again, it was probably better a few years ago, It was nearly at 100% when we entered, so we had to seat on a small table that looks over the street, out of the fan's reach. Fortunately one of the big tables insided got free and we could move in. We found the soups quite tasty; liked the noodles and also the eggplant's recipe. As said, not particularly cheap.

I found this place on Google maps as I was staying nearby and love eating vegan. It had great reviews so I walked over to check it out. They have a great menu with plenty of options and meals are reasonably affordable (you can tick on a paper menu what you wish to order and hand it into them). The food is made to order and was delivered to my table in about 5 minutes. Delicious and nourishing meal; large portions. I ordered the Sweet and Sour Tofu stir fry with Cashew Nuts. I sat on the balcony in the sun which was lovely. The atmosphere was pleasant and the painted feature wall adds a lot of colour along with the indoor plants. I look forward to returning here for a vegan feast the next time I visit Chiang Mai. Read less

Amazing food presented in an honest, authentic, unpretentious way, showcasing the taste of fresh local produce prepared Thai style. Dishes are well seasoned, colorful and very appetising. Anchan has been infused into a few dishes and the pad thai was delicious, done al dente style with balanced flavours using a myriad of unique local ingredients. An achievement considering no fish sauce was used. The fried banana flower salad was outstanding including the aubergines with miso paste and pumpkin red curry. Will absolutely be back again !

We were a party of 6 adult 1 child. When we made booking we were told we had to be split into two tables which we agreed. When we turned up, we saw the two tables could be easily joined together. The waiter refused and just walked away. Never mind, so we ordered out food. The waiter was rude and looked like he really hated his job there, and everybody else. Anyhow, the food was pretty good. The purple phad thai was very yummy, so was the banana flowers tempura. The other dishes were good but not outstanding. We left immediately after we finished our food so we didn’t have to look at the waiter’s sour face. Oh well. So much for a well known restaurant that I had so looked forward to trying. Read less
Affordable food. If you haven't tried Thai Vegetarian cooking before check the food at this place. Each of us ordered a different rice or noodles dish and liked it.

I’m not vegan, but wanted to try this place. Had the pumpkin red curry and it was delicious. Already wanting to come back for lunch tomorrow. Only reason I didn’t give it 5 stars, staff provided adequate service, but not the warmest that I’ve come to live in Thailand. Have to look carefully to find this place. It’s up a set of stairs and overlooks the street. Great setting

Great little place. Some friends of our here suggested it and after reading the reviews we decided to try it out. We normally eat meat, but are fine with veggies only. This place was great. The textures and flavors of the food were perfect. The service was great and the place was on a quiet street. We had the Thai basil fried rice (which had egg in it), the long egg plant and the tempura mushroom dish. That along with a Chang beer and a pineapple smoothie came to 545 THB. We would love to come back and highly recommend this place.

Anchan was my comfort food place to go to in Chiang Mai. For about 150 baht you can get full of vegan goodies, in a calm atmosphere far from the dust of the road, and the sound of motorbikes. Freshly made dishes, large portions, clearly marked vegan options (and many of them!) on a long menu. Taro fritters, cashew pad thai (or any pad thai actually…) are great choices. Mint smoothie was my fav refreshing drink, but I bet all others are as good (tried berry one too).
Great pad Thai. One of the best I’ve had in Thailand. Also one of the few places that catered to my request to make it spicy and did it well 🙌🏽

Hey. I only come here because I am a vegetarian and, as has been said before in a previous visit months ago, the food isn’t bad (but not amazing, and you shouldn’t break a leg to get here) but the service is so unremittingly awful that I am NOT coming back here EVER The previous review was entitled ‘they take the customer for granted’ and tonight two servers came up to me. One gave me the menu and walked away, not allowing me the chance to finish my sentence (I know what I want to order) and the second (second time I saw him wearing a face mask) server was as unfriendly as my previous visit. So, having recently discovered another Vegan place (you can google it) in Chiang Mai I won’t go back here. I’m totally incredulous when I read glowing and fulsome reviews of the service here: were we in the same place? Read less
The food here was delicious. Tucked away from the busy streets in a nice quite setting. The food traditional Thai all vegetarian with vegan options. Highly recommend if you are in Chiang Mai

We ordered the banana flower salad, eggplant, pad thai, probably too much. The food were all delicious, portions is big, but price is very reasonable. Lime soda smoothie was great too. No matter you are vegetarian or not, this is a great diner place to stop by.

Just had a late lunch at Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant. So quirky! Up the stairs to the first floor restaurant from a quiet street off the main strip and there’s only 9 tables so get here early as you may need to wait a while for a table. Another reason is that the restaurant closes by 8.30pm off peak. The kitchen is fully open and you can see the owner cooking your meal. The kids were absolutely fascinated. I have to say we had the best Massaman Curry here compared to anywhere we have eaten in Thailand. The corn cakes are amazing. Had to order a mango smoothie to cool things down. It was well worth it Read less

I am so delighted to have eaten here. My friend and I may have ordered too much food but leftovers are my favorite. I ordered the fried banana blossom salad and pad Thai butterfly pea noodles with mushrooms. She ordered the green curry and the Northeastern spicy Thai soup. Huge portions and absolutely tasty. Everything! We had large beers (Chang & Leo) and enjoyed ourselves fully while relaxing near the open balcony during the rain. I highly recommend anyone (Vegetarian or not to give it a try). Read less
Anchan owner always looking at ways to keep menu fresh makes a big difference when you live here. Same big high quality portions now with full menu everyday.

I was a vegetarian for 22 years, and in that time, I learned that truly delicious and health vegetarian restaurants are uncommon. Anchan is a gem in world of otherwise bland, salty, or deep-fried drivel. True to much of the cuisine in Northern Thailand, it offers a filigree of complex flavors in each dish that really hit the spot. And so many veggies! Plus, the serving sizes are quite large by Thai standards and reasonable for Western standards - a hard thing to find sometimes. The ambiance isn't as chic as some newer places, however the flavors and affordability more than make up for it. This is my new favorite restaurant in Nimman.

The food’s not bad - hence the two stars but 3 stars are withheld for the indifferent service: the waiter we had today looked indescribably bored and unfriendly and what kind of management would hire someone so evidently lacking in any social skills at all? There were 5 in our group and the restaurant was empty - why are restaurants so bad at turning casual customers into lifelong clients? Because they don’t care, probably. I could have made this a regular place to eat but with the rubbish customer service I don’t think I’ll bother until you give the staff proper training or hire a better quality of waiter. Read less

We were in Anchan on our first evening in Chiang Mai and were amazed. After three weeks in Thailand, the eggplant-miso dish convinced us, finally a really different taste than most. Also the mint smoothie is super refreshing and not too sweet. We came back a few days later and tried a curry with palm hearts and the tofu sweet sour. Both very tasty and served with brown rice. The choice is not huge, but everything is fresh. The tables for two on the balcony are sweet, absolutely recommended.

I eat in this restaurant at least once a week and have done for about seven months. The food here is just excellent. The veggies are uniquely flavorful and beautifully prepared so that every dish is an experience. They change the menu every week so I never get bored. They have a few signature dishes like the Anchan pad Thai that are must-try and always on the menu. It's a small thing but I also just love their soda with lime because they give you so much lime! One of the best places in Chiang mai IMHO.

i feel very sorry for foreigners who thought this is a good thai food- perhaps they are inexperienced with the taste standards in Thailand. I ordered 'panaeng pasta' of which I got a very poor quality macaroni and bland curry sauce. We have asked a question about where they sourced their vegetables from and we can dare say that is is not truely organic as they claimed to be.

This is a great and healthy vegetarian restaurant conveniently located in Chiang Mai. We had a nice dinner there. It has a nice dining room with a balcony overlooking the street. Interesting menu with unique veg dishes. We liked the place a lot.

We had planned a dinner at Anchan based on the reviews, it was worth all the time and effort, though a bit outside the old town , I highly recommend this place for vegan and vegetarian food lovers. The food was amazing. We ordered two entrees and 1 soup and the quantity was more than enough for the two of us.

Finally a good address in CM for vegan Thai restaurant ! My mom had a delicious tofu pad Thai and me an egg tofu yellow curry with brown rice and it was so gooood!! For the dessert of the day it was vanilla hazelnut cake and it was superbe! Even the anchan drinks was great! Liked the place and the vibe. Thanks to the Belgian owner ( my compatriat) for his warm welcome and bravo for this great restaurant, keep it up! Read less

I called in for lunch and ordered my favourite dish, massaman curry with brown rice ... great presentation of the food and the quality was really very good and the serving portion was large ... a clean, cool resturant with nice friendly staff ... there are both vego and vegan food options available ... this was my second visit as they were full on my first visit earlier this week

Not a very large selections of food. I ordered the Thai stir fry vegetables and it tasted really good. On the bottom floor they have a digital work coffee shop, and upstairs is the restaurant. I was there on a Monday afternoon and about every five minutes there was a plane boarding

Had a delicious meal at Anchan today! We were greeted very well by the staff and they were very helping, answering all our questions. They have a menu that changes weekly, using local produce that are in season. We tried the coconut palm heart green curry which was excellent - a bit spicy because of the peppers but sweet with the coconut milk. We also tried the pad thai which had blue rice noodles - the color of the butterfly pea flower. This was a very beautiful dish! The crispy tofu with tamarind sauce was exquisite as well! To drink, we had the passion fruit smoothie and the butterfly pea flower lemonade - both very refreshing and came in a pretty mason jar with some garnishes. The desert was to die for.....Fresh bananas cooked in coconut milk!! It's all vegetarian and most things can or are vegan. Greet place for celiacs or gluten intolerant as well. I definitely recommend this place!! Read less

Small and casual veggie/vegan thai place that was probably the best of the vegetarian restaurants we tried during our time in Chiang Mai. We got the pumpkin curry and a cashew stir fry, both quite good, and service was perfectly fine. We ate at several other sub-par veggie places in the old city which shall go unnamed and Anchan was far superior.

We loved our food here! It was one of our favorite meals in Chiang Mai. We got a eggplant stir-fry and pumpkin stir-fry and both were delicious. It was such a refreshing break from typical vegetarian Thai food with tofu. Unfortunately we discovered this place on our last night in Chiang Mai so we were only able to eat here once. I'd definitely recommend checking it out even if you're not vegetarian!
Best Thai food I’ve ever had. Even my non-veg friend said he would come back! Will be recommending this gem to all my friends who visit the area!
Food was far from our expectations after the review we read. Service wasn’t welcoming either. Maybe we came on a bad day? Still, we won’t give it another try.
Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant is sooo good and incredibly priced for the quality of food and amazing taste you are getting. Healthy and nutritious! My fav is their anchan pad thai - it's a must try!

After a week of fried meat (and many fried or oily vegetables) my carnivorous wife and I were ready for a change and craved some veg. Anchan did not disappoint. The menu here changes week to week based upon what’s in season or in the market, though there are a few fixtures on the menu. We had the massaman curry, anchan pad Thai, and our favorite, the miso eggplant (get it, amazing). This place is worth a visit even if not a vegetarian.

Menu changes weekly with some staples that stay the same. Not a vegetarian but my sister is. We came here a few times during the month we lived here and enjoyed it so much each time. Huge portions of delicious fresh food. The veggie pad Thai is amazing - it has blue noodles, not sure what it is but it’s awesome. The eggplant dish is also a must.

We tried several vegetarian, vegan, and just regular Thai restaurants and this was by far the best one! The portions are very big and the quality is always so fresh and delicious. The menu changes so you always get to try something new. I ate here several times during our visit to Chiang Mai and was never disappointed. They're always busy in the evenings so make sure you go early to get a seat! I wish there was an Anchan here in my city!! Thank you Anchan!

After reading rave reviews about this restaurant I went to experience their food. The chef himself came to take my order. I asked about the curries if they were spicy and he said not too spicy and pointed out the one he recommended. I ordered that dish. When it arrived I had such high hopes it looked delicious. First I sampled the broth and whoa spicy city and the after kick was too much for me. I thought perhaps I would just eat the vegetables (potatoes and tomatoes) but they too were way to spicy for me. This was not just a little heat. I told the server it was too spicy and that I wanted to order something else the stir fried vegetables. When I went to pay the bill I was charged for the curry which I found unusual and unexpected as typically if there is a problem with a dish one is not charged. My issue with the charging of the curry was that the chef himself said it was not too spicy, he knew I didn't want spice and I asked. If I had not asked and didn't like it I get charging me or if the chef said it is spicy and I still went ahead and ordered it then my bad. But I went on the chef's recommendation. It's just not great customer service and produces a poor review. You may be thinking okay Esther we get why you are disappointed but what about the vegetable stir fry? The vegetables were fresh and crispy, the broth nice but honestly average for me not living up to some of the reviews I read. Hey if you like spicy this would not have been a problem but keep in mind the customer service part of this experience. Read less

My Tom yum soup with noodles was not very good. It was too sweet or something it just tasted off. My friends pumpkin curry was delicious though. The service was just ok I didn’t feel super welcomed or wanted here.

An interesting vegetarian menu. The service was quick, portions huge. We left feeling very satisfied. Excellent value. Only thing was that it seems they shut at 8.30 pm although that may have just been the night or week we were there because of the mourning period for the king, so we just made it in on time. Nothing amazing about the decor, but it was pleasant and clean. Think they could make it a little more atmospheric - candles, lanterns or fairy lights would help. SO nice to find decent vegetarian food - its hard to come by in Thailand.

We visited this place at 4 pm in the evening for a late lunch. It has a limited item menu which keeps changes on weekly basis. Being vegans who are ok with milk products, we relished good food served here. The service was standard and so was the nice little setup at the place. The only mistake we made was visiting this place right at the start of our stay at Chiang Mai allowing the food to set a benchmark which none of the places we visited could break. Must go especially for vegetarians.

Anchan came recommended highly, so it was the first place I visited on my most recent trip to Chiang Mai. And I was not disappointed! The chef, Aye, knows how to balance flavours and textures, and is a lovely person on top of that. Portions are very generous, so this would be a good place to go with a few people to share a few dishes. Items are also clearly marked on the menu if they are vegan or not. I would love to return throughout the seasons to see what new dishes pop up on their ever-changing menu.
Fast service, super tasty eggplant dish and the banana flower curry delicious. Large portions and clean place.
No air-conditioning Great dishes and they understand requests Good service, great attitude I'll go back.

Love that they are constantly changing up their menu, helps make it easier to keep coming back and not eat the same thing over and over! I always get the banana honey coconut smoothie and if you can get their yellow curry fried rice... doooooo it! Staff is very friendly and accommodating :)

Been twice now! Fantastic that the menu is changed every week to reflect the fresh quality ingredients in the kitchen! Delicious vegetarian and vegan food at reasonable prices! Friendly staff and great selection! We loved it both times! The only negative... it isn’t in England! ❤️

My wife and I have spent a lot of time here in Chiang Mai and eaten at numerous vegetarian restaurants and this place definitely serves up the best Vegan Curries. Always get service with a smile and they are open everyday which is a plus.

I've now been to Anchan more times than I care to count, and one of the things that I continue to love is that the menu changes according to what's in season and available at the local organic market. Anchan's changing menu means there's always something different to try... yes, I have a few favourites that I come back to time and time again (hello banana flower salad!) but it's so good to be able to have a wide variety of dishes in one place without having to traipse around the city, seeking out decent veggie places! Unlike a lot of veggie eateries in Chiang Mai, Aye and his staff understand what vegan/vegetarian means, which is why vegans eating there can be 100% confident that the food contains no animal products. In my experience, that's actually pretty rare in Chiang Mai, especially when it comes to using fish or oyster sauce. Or even shrimp paste. You won't find any of these in Aye's food. One of the beauties of having your food cooked fresh, from scratch, is that the heat can be tailored to suit the customer, so if you're a chilli wimp like me (even after years of living in Asia), just ask for your food to be cooked "Mai phet" (not spicy) or "Phet farang" (foreigner-spicy). In recent years, there've been more and more veggie/vegan restaurants springing up in Chiang Mai; while some are good (many aren't), I've not found any to match the consistency and quality of Anchan, and it's still the first place I recommend to vegetarian and vegan newcomers to the city. I have to say that the taste is original, even there are many foreigners customer but the cooks still remind the standard of Thai food. It has enough menus and the ingredients are fresh. The price is reasonable. It is a must if you are looking for a vegetarian restaurant with the great taste.
Visited on the Trip Advisor rating and was left with a sour taste in my mouth by the service. Overpriced and unfriendly. Don't recommend. Go elsewhere.

We first visited this restaurant when we came to Chiang Mia last year and and a part reason for booking to come to Chiang Mia again this year was to eat at the Anchan. We were slightly worried that the quality of the food wouldn't be what we remembered on our 1st visit. No need to worry, we have been twice( so far ) this trip and the food is still amazing. It is so fresh and full of flavour and I would recommend this place not only to Veggies and Vegans but to carnivore's as well.

I haven't been here for a while. The food was average and just a take on Thai dishes with non-vegetarian ingredients taken out. Nothing basically wrong with the food but unimaginative and rather pricey. There is better now in Chiang Mai....

This is one of our favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai. Vegetarian with many vegan options, delicious curries and fruit smoothies. It can be a little hard to find, and it's a climb up some stairs to the restaurant, but it is well worth the hike. Every single thing I've tried here has been delicious, and we have been to this restaurant many times.

We were taken here by my wife's sister who lives in Chiang Mai. The food is amazing and there's plenty of it. Perhaps my favourite curry was here and I enjoyed the tofu even though I'm not vegetarian. Highly recommended

I have only been in Chiang Mai for a couple of days (been here previously in 2012), and decided to stay outside of Old Chiang Mai. I am in my 40s and need a break from the chaos. So i am staying in a condo in the Nimman area. The host of the condo kindly offered to take me out to this place, tucked into one of the Soi's off the main Nimman road. Its a second floor location the opens up onto the street. Not the best view but still quaint and quiet. The menu is not large but offers a good variety. We had a couple of the curries (massman and another with mushrooms), and fried mushroom salad. The service was quick, staff were friendly, and the flavors in the food were delicious. Prices are also reasonable. Would definitely go back. Read less
What can I say. Food cooked with love. So nourishing. 1st floor upstairs. Closes 8.30 and on Sunday Monday. Yummy.

Went here today and had both the yellow curry with veggies & pad Thai anchan noodle and they were both soooooo delicious! Everything tasted so fresh, the curry had big chunky veggies & tofu. I also had the lime soda smoothie which was great as well. Staff was friendly, helpful and the service was quick. I'll definitely be back! Highly recommend 😃
Went with a group of 4 and chose 4 dishes between us, plus mango sticky rice. Super good, super tasty and we left feeling satisfied. Easy to find location, just up some steps. Well signposted!
Really tasty food, good portion sizes and a nice atmosphere. The staff were also lovely, especially Piriya!

This was one of the best vegetarian experience in a lifetime! If you're vegetarian, you know that it is not enough just to remove meat from a recipe. You need to find a way to enrich the flavour and make a meal complete. Every dish I tasted in Anchan Restaurant was absolutely delicious, nothing to add :) Oh, btw, the portions are big and the staff very friendly and helpful. I would go back to Thailand just to have there another dinner!
Wow, what an amazing food, the 3 of us ordered 5 dishes and all where amazing, the place is quite and open on a 2nd floor.

Excellent Vegetarian food. Good food starts with the quality of the ingredients and the quality of the produce is excellent. The stir fried vegetable dish was perfectly cooked. The colors of the vegetables were still vibrant and the vegetables were crispy. We had the Red Curry and the Massaman curry which were both delish. I think the Massaman Curry had a hint of Cinnamon in it. Also the Stir fried Eggplant in Miso was superb. The portions are large and robust. Great experience. Will be back.
best vegetarian food i ever try in chiang mai! lovely greens and yellow currys, if you are in the area worth the visit

This place is so good that I've ate lunch here every day for the last 2 months. If you want to try traditional Thai food then this is the place. There's usually about 15 items on the menu each week, half of which are vegan and the rest vegeterian. The menu changes every week so there's always plenty of variety. The food is delicious, the vegatables and fruits are super-fresh, and the portion sizes are great (especially for Thailand). You can expect to pay 95-120 baht for a meal with rice, which is really cheap, especially with the size of the portions. I always leave feeling full :-) I've never had a bad meal here, it has been consistently great every time (and I have been here a lot). Favourites are the Indian yellow tempeh curry (although all the curries are good) and any of the tofu stir fries, but especially the one in pineapple sauce. The vegan chocolate cake with homemade jam is the perfect dessert and you can get a great Mango with sticky rice here too, along with a whole range of fresh smoothies. It's a really lovely, clean place inside and like most places in Thailand, the staff are incredbibly friendly and helpful. Highly recommended – you'll end up coming back time and time again! i had vegan pad thai. it was delicious! second floor, kinda cozy with an open balcony. prices are very reasonable. not sure why i see $$$ signs on the profile. it's more like a $$. I'd go again!
Organic food cooked to perfection that tantalises the taste buds. Absolutely the best restaurant in Chiang Mai.

OMG! What can I say. This was the best Tom Kha soup I have ever had. The Pad Thai and Red Curry was also amazing! You have to check this place out if you are vegan or even if you're not! Lovely people too!

This was the best food I've had in Thailand as a veggie. We had the mushroom pad thai, banana flower salad, and miso stir fried aubergine. Surpassed our expectations. Food was well priced for Chiang Mai. All three large dishes with two waters came to 405THB, about £9.50. Incredible food and service!

I am a digital nomad and was working in MANA most of the time. This place is just next door so it was very convenient for me. Also, they serve healthy and delicious food, so I kept coming here almost everyday for lunch. The staff is lovely too and I actually became friends with one of the waitresses. Piriya is super nice! Thanks!!

One of my favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai. Everything is freshly made, the food is tasty, the menu change every week with new dishes. The staff is very friendly and the price are really correct. The restaurant is located on the first floor on a quiet street, it's an open space so no cold airconditioning. Deserve 6 stars. :)

I really enjoyed the weekly changing menu, very creative dishes with big portion sizes. There are so many veggie places I'm Chiang Mai but Anchan in my opinion is the best. The owner cooks himself and it's like home made food without the hassle.

Simple menu but varied and, from the four items we chose on (what will be) our first visit of several, really well prepared. Clear and distinct flavours delivered through good portions at reasonable prices. Well worth it if you're looking for a selection of vege dishes and you're nearby.

I've eaten here many times and loved the food and the service. But the last couple of times both were not good. All the food is made individually, so you can order how you want it. The last time I asked for something not spicy. When the meal came, it was so spicy, I couldn't eat it. I asked the waitress why the food was spicy when I asked her for non-spicy. She said it has to have spice or doesn't taste good. But I asked for non-spicy. She just walked away and I was left with something I couldn't eat. I didn't go back after that. Maybe I'll try again soon.
I go here frequently for what I think is the best, most creative vegetarian Thai food in Chiang Mai. Generous portions.
This vegetarian thai restaurant is the best place to eat a vegan pad Thai . We think it's absolutely the best in town . Good also the curries and smoothies .

Very cute place! It is located above a coffee shop/coworking spot. Try to get a table on the balcony that looks out on the bustling Nimman Rd. I am not a vegetarian, but I thought the food was delicious. This is a great place to go if you're with friends, family, or traveling solo.

Quiet location, one floor above street level. Nice ventilation and cozy setting. Ideal for small groups 6 or less. The food is excellent, prepared by the owner/chef. Typical northern Thai menu, tastefully prepared, fresh ingredients, and made to order. Not often that you have a personal chef! Unless you are staying near Anchan's location, recommend getting tuk tuk or taxi to drop you off. The restaurant will help you get a ride once you are finished with the meal.
A quick simple review - I loved our lunch & yummy smoothies. Would come back. I had a nice simple salad.
Not easy to be a veggie in Chang Mai! Anchan is a good and simple place with a homely feel. Tasty food with fresh ingredients. Excellent find!

I'm not vegetarian and was, until Anchan, never quite charmed by veggie dishes. Kind of: cute but I go hungry. We went here with a friend who said it's really good and who knows his food. So, we went along, being polite and all... However... the food we got served totally changed my mind. It showed me that indeed one can make a truely great cuisine using only vegetables. Really, not only did I learn the food is great, but also that it knocks the socks of any 'western comfort food'. You're not leaving this place hungry! The three times ended up going we couldn't finish the plates, since the portions are so liberal. The place has a quiet location just of the main road and is decorated with a great mural on which the menu of the day is written. The order is taken with a clever fill in form, so never an issue with your order, and food is delivered swiftly. Great service to me. The owner, Khun Aye, personally prepares all the dishes to perfection. We ended up chatting to some folks who reside in Chiang Mai and we have become regulars. Anchan seems to be simply better than any of the other numerous veggie venues in Chiang Mai. Regarding price, I'd say midrange, but you're getting a very good deal, excellent service, fresh and surprising dishes and you can even get a carry on for later. They also have this glutenfree chocolate cake. Just do it. Next time in Chiang Mai we wil certainly go back here. Koen and Nancy Read less

Detail: I usually do not enjoy Thai Vegetarian food, because of the lack of veggies and over use of soy based product that is rubbery and tasteless...finding this place was wonderful, you can enjoy the fantastic Thai flavor and have your veggies to!! Great place to order multiple dishes to share with friends....also there are still meat options on the menu....so if you are very very strictly vegetarian, the kitchen still handles meat products. This is classic Thai dishes, not a trendy superfood fusion type place...very busy, at meal times, great to time before or after major eating times. Great staff, when busy can be a bit slow, but worth the time, you get piping hot food!

this place was recommended by our hotel staff and it was totally worth it. They serve up sumptuous, healthy local dishes prepared fresh in an open kitchen. The menu changes according to the fresh produce of the day, and don't always expect a dessert! must, must visit
Go visit this place and you won't be disappointed. Low key cafe style place with good selection of different veggie dishes. And very cheap. Good value.

Food was okay, but very spicy and nothing special.. plus the menu was pretty small. We've tried a few other vegetarian/vegan restaurants in Chiangmai which are much more worth a visit, especially Alice's Organic Vegetarian restaurant. Do not recommend.
We lived near Anchan in Chiang Mai, and were regulars. I'd have gone there every day if it wasn't so expensive. The Papaya smoothie is most memorable.

Great resto, situated on the first floor, nice choises of veggie food, wel balanced and very corrected prices. Proffesional staff, and the chef/owner mr aaey? Is one of the best veggie chefs i know Flamboyant help in service, mr wouter, who is the (anchan)FLOWER, off the business. I know, i schould go more often to this original place Best value for money.
Just fantastic food, well cooked and presented. Everything I have tried has been great. I love this place. Family run and friendly
Had a great meal at Anchan last night. The menu was interesting and the food delicious, what more could you want?

We visited Anchan on our last day in Chiang Mai, following the fantastic reviews. So glad we came, the food is the best we had in all of Chiang Mai! My husband was hesitant at first as he loves meat but even he was impressed. We had the tempura mushroom, basil fried rice and tom yum soup. Delicious! The service is great and the restaurant has a lovely ambiance away from the noise of the msin street. Highly recommend!
Nothing to add after saying excellent : place is quiet, food is tasty, people are nice. This restaurant could turn you into vegetarians ! We loved it.

Since we live around the corner and love the chef's food, we like to try different things here. His menu changes weekly, so you cant always get a specific dish if it is a special. Recently had pad Thai made with green papaya shredded for the noodles. It was amazing!! Also, red curry fried rice that is LOADED with veggies and sooo good. Have tried the yellow curry fried rice, which I think is even better! Closed on Sunday and Monday, so plan accordingly.

We meet 5 vegetarian restaurants in Chiang mai. Anchan was the most expensive (i cant understand - pad thai 120) and the worst. Iam sorry. So big peaces of vegetable in the soup. So oily pad thai. I dont know if something was changed in Anchan, but 3 meals and same level like on the street.
Cosy restaurant on a second floor, with few tables on the blacony as well. Nice choice of vegetarian and vegan delicious dishes and smoothies.

I love the curries in Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant (be careful if you don't like spicy). Another thing: they serve rice for free! The only thing that bothered me a little bit is that the menu is quiet small.
Upstairs, near Maya, large portions and servings, very tasty and a little spicy. 120 baht for a large bowl. Will come back. Closed Sundays. Among the best vegetarian food in Chiang Mai.

Anchan hasn't been open very long so the chef is still finding his strengths and trying new ideas out on the menu of the day. I had his stuffed sweet bell peppers and they were fantastic. I also really like their raw chocolates, guilt free chocolate eating. The restaurant is lovely with a fantastic mural on the wall and delicious food to eat in a friendly and pleasant atmosphere. I will go back and I am not even a vegetarian

i have a confession to make. i always felt that vegetarians get a bit of raw deal when it comes to eating in south east asian countries, especially if you don't eat seafood which many consider a part of vegetarian diet. but then i came to chiang mai and ate at anchan and did it blow my mind! i had an early dinner there today and couldn't wait to get back to my hotel and leave them a review. i had a bit of a trouble finding the place. google maps showed the place one street away. thankfully, i looked up some pictures of the place and one of them had a building next door. i recognised the building and found the place :) the place wasn't terribly busy as i got there around six in the evening but by the time i was leaving almost all tables were taken. i had read some reviews and the crispy banana flower salad seemed to be pretty popular so i ordered that for the popularity and the novelty (i hadn't tasted banana flower before). i also ordered for massaman curry and asked for it be made less spicy. the food arrived pretty fast and when i looked at it, i was like i will not be able to finish it. but finish it ALL i did :) both the salad and the curry hit the right spot. the salad was fresh, crunchy and the curry was aromatic and flavourful. this is by far the best asian meal i have had. it was one of those times when i wish i weren't traveling alone so that i could order more dishes. if time permits, i will go back again. the only thing i would have liked to be done differently is that the dishes be served one after the other and not together. the salad and the curry were brought to the table together and i kept alternating between the two worried that the salad would get soggy or the curry would get cold. but again, that's a minor quibble and the place still deserves 5 stars. Read less

We stayed right across from the street and this was a welcomed gem. The banana coconut smoothie is to die for as is the masaman curry (careful, it's spicy!) all around delicious and prices are very reasonable!

This is a small place situated on the first floor of a shoplot. The three of us ordered the following and paid 658 baht which is really very reasonable for such great tasting food. Massaman curry Glass noodle in soup Fried rice Mix vegetables stirfry Dragonfruit lime smoothie Ginger cinnamon tea Would definitely highly recommend this place for simple and delicious tasting food. The smoothie and tea was really great. Everything was tasty. Service was fast and efficient. Read less

You could never be bored with the food in Anchan as the menu changes every week. I've eaten here many times, and it's never failed to impress. What more could you ask than the freshest, tastiest of veggies cooked in an open kitchen, served by friendly smiling staff at a price that can't be beaten?

This place does not get the attention it deserves. I remember being here in 2012 when it was ranked number 1 in the city but dropped to 13th during my 3-month stay. I've been back now for 6 weeks and visited it weekly (weekly changing menu) and I have to say to everyone check this place out if you want mouth watering real healthy food that is cheap as chips for what you get. I have my sister with me on this trip and I have been telling her for years about Chiangmai and the amazing food available at this place and here we are. Last week's menu so impressed my sister she was upset it changed this week but I told her, each week is just as good. It closes 2 days a week (Unusual here in Chiangmai) so we are both looking forward to visiting tomorrow Tuesday (Menu appears on Facebook each week). The prices are very cheap, portions are large and the staff friendly. Note- If you arrive and you see a sign at the top of the stairs "FULL" , do not be put off straight away as a few times I have braved it to find the place emptyish, they forget to move the sign. Read less

Just had the best meal of our holiday, in fact in eating out in quite some time at this great little restaurant. Take the time to eat here if you can when you are in Chiang Mia. Not only was the taste amazing but it was also great value for money. My wife and I shared 4 main plates and 2 desserts and it came to a total of 680 Baht. By far the cheapest price we have paid since coming to Thailand.
