We eat there today which is Saturday and our only complaint is that the restaurant is closed on Sunday and Monday and we fly out on Tuesday morning. Seriously considering going back later and ordering take away to have tomorrow as luckily we are staying in a suite hotel.
For someone who always eats meat in almost every meal, I was very satisfied. The food was very tasty and the atmosphere was very laid back. Great place tucked off of the main road

5 of us ate lunch here & got to try 6 different dishes, which was good. None of us are vegetarian, but we all enjoy food. The food here is outstanding. Really fresh and tons of flavor. I love Masamam curry & had vegetarian Masamam for the first time. - YUM! The service is good & it's fast. We will be back to sample more.
The restaurant in nimman area not far from Maya shopping mall. Here have a very delicious vegetarian foods good tasty. Yummy

We visit here regularly and it is consistently good quality and value for vegetarian or vegan. Mussaman curry, pad thai, and eggplant dishes are marvelous as is their dragon fruit smoothie. (The eggplant dish and the smoothie are our all-time favorites) All dishes (we have tried them all) have fairly generous portions. Nice to sit on the balcony and watch the world go by while enjoying dinner there. A little hard to find for first timers. It's on the Soi between Soi6 & 8, west past the Hillside apartments and the Burmese restaurant, on the left, 2nd floor with the balcony.

Me and my husband came here for curry. It was really good they serve it with brown basmati rice. They do not have air conditioning so when it is really hot you might want to go somewhere else. The staff is kinda of nice not overly friendly. They may be like that because they have to work in the heat. We got the Mussuman Curry for 120 baht about 4 dollars.

I led a group of 15 backpackers from my hostel on a 45 minute walk to come here. The massaman curry was 100% worth that walk. An incredible meal in a great area, with friendly and helpful staff. Thank you Anchan!

Go with friends so you can share lots of the great dishes at this delightful restaurant. Situated upstairs in a nicely decorated room, there is a nice view. Wonderful service and great prices make this a perfect spot for vegetarians and meat eaters will love it too!

I think their Massaman curry is excellent! It's so tasty and the owner cares about the food he makes and uses great ingredients to prepare awesome Thai food. If you love curry and vegetable, this is a great place to visit!

I had the mushroom pad Thai and it was good. Not great, not horrible, but a solid meal. I would come back here to try there other dishes as I saw many that looked fantastic. The people were nice and my server in particular was super sweet and helpful. She sad the curries are super popular so maybe try one of those but their menu does change often so be aware of that! Thanks Anchan:)
Very cozy place, peaceful and nice. Food is really good and portions are very large. I like that you get wholegrain rice. White dragonfruit smoothie is yummy and not too sweet.

I ate at anchan almost 4 times a week after my first visit. I've yet to try a dish that doesn't blow my mind. The chef, who is the owner and the nicest man ever, genuinely loves what he's doing and has a true passion for delicious and organic vegetarian Thai food. Everything he makes is light on oil and almost everything can be made vegan. Most of his dishes are vegan already! He changes the menu weekly so I'm never bored, but the green curry is still my favorite ! I eat green curry almost everywhere I go in Thailand and his green curry is hands down my favorite one. You can tell that he only uses really high quality produce! I also really love his shakes, the fried banana leaf salad, the pumpkin and egg and his homemade passion fruit jam. seriously buy his jam it's the best jam I've ever had. He also usually makes vegan chocolate cake which is amazing !!! I want to tell him I love him after every meal. Read less

I have been to Anchan many times and always come back for their flavourful Thai food. My favourite dishes are the Massaman curry and the mushroom Pad Thai. The Massaman is go rich and creamy, it's the best Vegan curry in Chiang Mai in my opinion! If you cannot tolerate spices then request for no spices - I would describe it as medium spicy. It is pricer than usual but it is also a large serving. If you are a fan of Mushrooms this Pad Thai is something special, if you are Vegan just be sure to request without egg. The staff aren't always the warmest but the food speaks for itself!

I ate here at least once a week for more than a year. I still haven't tried everything on the rotating menu, but I never had a dish that wasn't delicious. They make the best vegetarian curries in Chiang Mai.

Amazing vegetarian flavors! We ordered the pad Thai, massaman curry and Thai basil fried rice. 2 drinks too. Moderately priced, whole meal was around 500 THB. But OMG the food was amazing! This was our 7th pad Thai in 8 days and their flavor was still somehow just different enough to stand out! Everything is bought from local markets so menu varies depending on what is available
More expensive than other places but very good food. Also good and welcoming service. The should do cooking classes!

In a cosy open-air room, you can enjoy every week different meals and desserts. The food is amazing (try the massaman curry) and the staff more than welcoming. It is a must for every vegetarian in Chiang Mai!
Very good Thai food, 100% vegetarian there is no meat/sea food in anything. All the dishes we ordered was very tasty and also very generous in size.

I went to Anchan recently for lunch and enjoyed all the food. I loved the banana flower tom yum soup as well as the vegetable tempura. The service is acceptable but not great. I wish they would make more personally recommendations. I will come back regularly, though.

I'm living in Chiang Mai, and I've eaten here a million times, in fact I would come every day if only I wasn't living a little further away. Why? Because it's by far the best vegetarian place in town!! The food is absolutely delicious, the vegetables are organic and fresh, you can easily taste the difference, these are really good, and they are cooked to perfection (someone here said the potatoes are, and I totally agree). The portions are huge and the flavour is great! I can't say enough good things. The atmosphere is cosy and the restaurant is very clean. The service is awesome too, the staffs are so friendly and welcoming, and attentive, the menu and the food always arrives in no time at all (also, they speak excellent English). I hope this convinces you to come here!!! If not, just look at the pictures of their curries (my favourites), no way to resist :) Read less

Good location albeit on the second floor. In trendy Nimman. Food is good but not great. Looks good on the plate but not super. They are basically a fancy Thai Vegetarian Restaurant but fail in the health end. They are ok though. I would recommend giving them a try. They offer some exotic health drinks but not much in shots or Fresh Vegetable Juice.
To be honest, I wasn't sure if I will like a vegetarian food, but this turned out to be excellent. Fried rice (brown) and cashew tofu with tomato base are a must have.

We visited for dinner around 7pm and were sat outside on the little balcony. We ordered the tempura vegetables and yellow Indian curry. The food was fantastic! The tempura was really tasty, the curry had just the right amount of spice (not too much), and the portion sizes were good. Prices are a little higher than some other places, but definitely worth it. Will be going back to try out the ever changing menu.

I have been to Anchan twice now and tried the Thai miso eggplant stir fry and the massaman curry, both were amazing. Prices reasonable and good serving sizes! I will definitely be back soon to try more!

This little spot has honed its menu and dishes to a high level of perfection. The service is on-point. All the dishes have a distinctive taste served with some style. Wish they were open 7 days a week, but will settle for Wednesday - Saturday. Give these friendly folks a visit and you'll leave with a happy tummy!

I love Anchan. I go there regularly to enjoy the fantastic variety of fresh, well well made vegetarian food. Plus the service is always lovely. And it's really not too expensive; all round a top quality vegetarian restaurant, highly recommended!

A quick disclaimer: The only reason that I did not give 5 stars was because of the prices. It is quite expensive. However, aside from that it was as good as it gets. Huge portions, friendly staff and convenient location on Nimmanhaemin Road. If you're vegan or veggie or simply appreciate great vegan/veggie food, you must stop by Anchan during your stay in Chiang Mai.

We happen to live in a near by condominium and we happen to try the vegetarian restaurant out of curiosity! We were pleasantly surprised and now we eat there once a week! Recently I needed a dish for a birthday pot luck! My kitchen is minimal right now so I decided to order the Massaman curry from Anchan. I called Aye and he quickly put together my order. I transferred it to a pot and took it to my friends home. He told everyone I cooked it and people commented on how you could taste the "love"! Thats what I said after I tried it the first time! We eat it in a restaurant and you know its from a restaurant, but it sure doesn't taste like typical restaurant food! it is so delicious!! I highly recommend you stop in if you want a meal that will fill not just your tummy but your soul! Read less

Tasty food a bit on the expensive side for Thai meals but nice location upstairs away from too much noise or crowds. Be careful to ask for not spicy if you can't handle it! No air con only fans and have a backup plan in case they're not open

Entrées were priced around 150 bhat. Curries highlighted the organic vegetable flavours rather than traditional Thai flavours. No need to worry about seafood sauces or artificial flavour enhancers here. Service was friendly. The moist, frost-less vegan chocolate cake, served with mango jam, was airy and lightly sweetened.

I had heard good things about this restaurant and decided to try it out. I was not disappointed! The menu was relatively small but everything on it sounded great. We ended up getting the massaman curry and the sweet and sour curry and both were really good. The banana honey coconut milk smoothie was also fantastic. I would definitely eat here again. It's on the second floor so keep an eye out for the sign by the stairs.

Found this place to be so average and I was very surprised considering the rave reviews it gets. The food was very average - you can find so much better in Chiang Mai. And the price wasn't great either considering what you got for it. The service was rubbish also. Will not return to this place.

Went to Anchan with a few friends and ordered a few different dishes to share. Everything was delicious and the staff were incredibly friendly and attentive. The pad Thai and green mango salad were so tasty. Their menu does change regularly, so check their facebook for updates.

I ate a massaman curry, tasty, delicious. I also tried the anchan juice. We went there with our baby and staff was friendly. We had to wait until the heavy rain stop, it was quiet and relax atmosphere.

I went there ladt evening and was absolutely impressed with the tasty authentic thai veg dishes prepared by Khun Aye the lovely owner. Khun Aye kindly explained to me how hi grandmother prepared the dishes we enjoyed such ad the crispy tofu and eggplant fry. Steeped in real thai family tradition and authentic recipes. Vegetarian heaven !!! Generous portions with a great price.... lovely place in the trendy heart of Neimenheimen

Went here for dinner, I had the stir fry miso Thai eggplant with brown rice and ginger iced tea and can honestly say it was one of the most flavorsome vegetarian meals I've ever had. The extensive menu changes weekly and I look forward to going back again. Generous serves at a reasonable price. Staff aren't overly friendly but this doesn't take away from the great meals served. Very busy on the Monday night I went in, would book if I wanted a guaranteed table next time, particularly if I wanted balcony seating.

I have visited this restaurant many times before always enjoying the tasty dishes. I am not a vegetarian but have never missed the meat. I recommend trying the Red Curry with Pineapple and the Tom Yam soup. Look at the specials blackboard. Be warned they close early they say 8.30 but I think this means they want to be shut at 8,30. They were turning people away at 8.10. Restaurant is up stairs no lift and no air conditioning but plenty of fans. In small Soi off Nimmerheimen Road at the side of Hillside 3 Condo block and a Burmese Kitchen.

A friend of mine who is a health nut took me to this Restauranf last week. I am not a vegetarian but thoroughly enjoyed a sumptuous there! I highly recommend it to non-vegetarians alike. I'll take pictures next time!

Went here for lunch yesterday based on reviews and was not disappointed. Good, quick, friendly service with a menu that changes weekly. We glanced at what others were eating and ordered based on that. Massaman curry and the green mango salad were amazing! The restaurant was located on the 2nd floor of a building down an alley by our hotel, with a nice balcony seating as well. Definitely suggest going here for a yummy lunch. Also, only vegetarian options. As someone who would NEVER go to a vegetarian restaurant typically, safe to say I would happily go back again.

I ordered the vegan Pad Thai and was blown away. It was delicious. The food came really fast too. Great prices. Menu marks vegan dishes with asterisks but if something isn't vegan just ask and they will adapt if they can.

If you're vegan in Chiang Mai: you have to go to this spot. It's a great local vegan and vegetarian Thai restaurant that has a ton of different options, and is relatively inexpensive. I loved the pad thai, eggplant curry, and of course mango sticky rice.

I've been here twice since I've been in Chiang Mai and neither time was I disappointed! Delicious, flavorful food and fast service! Every dish at the table was finished off completely! oh and the honey banana coconut milk smoothie was incredible.

We've eaten here twice with a couple of months break between visits. Tried four different dishes and not been particularly impressed with any of them. Prices are higher than average. Not only that but the staff were looking quite depressed too. I think there are more enjoyable vegetarian restaurants to visit in Chiang mai than this one.
love this vegetarian restaurant good food, lots of choices, fresh , clean,feel good food. comfortable and spacious. good service.

If you are looking for clean and healthy food this is the stop for you! We had a quick lunch on the way out of town and it was delicious. The staff was super friendly and helpful. Highly suggest for a lunch while your in town.
Best tasty food on Nimman, that is a bit healthy too. Rice oil, veg&vegan. Great soups and curry They can be very serious, some would say toooo much haha

We just ate here for lunch today. The menu looks great. It's not fully vegan but most items are. Unfortunate that the Pad Thai isn't vegan but you can ask for it that way. I had the Massaman curry and my husband had the Penang curry. Both were delicious in different ways. The Massaman was more sweet and had a wonderful paste that included peanuts. Both had coconut milk. The Penang was rich with strong flavors most notable the fresh basil. We had rice with our meal which was a lovely red/brown rice. The meals were not lacking flavor or salt. Yummy! I usually don't eat food with a lot of fat or salt but I make an exception when I'm on vacation cycling and walking a lot. This place is wonderful. Try it when you're in Chiang Mai! Read less

Now this is what Thai food is all about. Lovely little spot serving vegan/vegetarian food. Had the Massaman Curry which comes with rice. Absolutely heavenly and only 120 Baht. My friend raved about the Pad Thai (which she asked to be cooked in no oil). Also had a slice of the vegan chocolate cake. Yum! I came back the next day to have the Massaman Curry again for lunch. Highly recommended whether veggie or not. Quality food, good price. Opens 11:00-20:30 Read less

Excellent Thai vegetarian food. Very well run. Check off the items on their menu and they give you exactly what you wanted. Highly recommend it. Absolutely amazing and healthy food. I cannot stress how good the Massaman Curry dish is. easily in my top three favorite dishes of all time. Perfectly flavored, might be a tad on the spicy side for wimps like us, but we got it with a mild hotness the second time and it was heavenly. The rice is delicious, the vegetables are cooked to perfection, I will have dreams about it until I go back - which won't be too long from now. The staff are very kind and warm. Hope the secret doesn't get out because it'll be hard to find a table here.

We were a group of 6 and we love food. So we decided to order almost everything off the menu. I have to say they made some of the best vegetarian dishes we had tried in a long time. Great flavours throughout all the dishes. The banana flower salad, masaman curry, pad thai, all on the top notch. Don’t miss out on the smoothies, freshly made and super refreshing. A must try.

We spent 3 months in Chiang Mai and came back to Anchan at least once a week because it's seriously one of the best places in town. Their menu changes weekly, so there are always new dishes to try. All the vegetables are super fresh and the way they are flavoured is amazing. The staff is always super lovely and friendly. I don't have a single negative thing to say about this place - you'd be crazy not to like it.
service was pretty good here but the food was just average compared to some of the other great veggie places in town

I spent two weeks in Chiang Mai and had a mission to find the best curry and pad thai in the city. Anchan was brilliant! I found it early on thanks to many other excellent reviews, but after trying at least 30 other places Anchan reigned supreme. It became my go-to spot. My favorite items: - Masaman curry (pictured) - Fried tofu - Pad Thai - Cinnamon Ginger Tea The atmosphere is delightful, wi-fi is fast, and wait staff friendly. Go to Anchan! You won't be disappointed. Read less

We've been here for lunch. The food overall are ok and no surprise. The lady in the restaurant talked to us in some strange language. I believed that was mandarin, such as 'enjoy your meal" - she didn't speak very well but she didn't know that we were Cantonese so she should know the audience!!! That didn't impress us. Don't think we will go back. We can get better given such price.hwr, I like the restaurant setting.

Had a business lunch here and was blown away by the deliciousness! First, I had the Passionfruit smoothie and also the cinnamon ginger green iced tea. Amazing! Two of the best drinks I've ever had. Next, we had the ginger tofu stir fry and the Massuman curry. Amazing, rich flavors! Very happy with our choices. Lastly, we had the apple cinnamon cake, which was ok. I highly recommend this restaurant as it was delicious and had great service, and free wifi. Read less
A wide and diverse selection of very good low budget vegetarian dishes and refreshments. Locally made herbal products also available for purchase. Recommended.

pro: We were very happy to find this vegetarian restaurant, as Thailand is usually far from veggie-friendly. At Anchan, I've had the best Thai food in a restaurant so far - absolutely yummy, I was even thinking about asking for the recipes. Great prices. Great free WiFi. The 4 of us went to this place on 2 of 3 days of our stay in Chiang Mai (we would have loved to also go here on the 3rd day, but as this was a Sunday, Anchan was unfortunately closed). con: The staff didn't smile too much (but don't get me wrong: the staff was friendly and provided fast and good service). I don't care about lack of smiling, as the food was perfect, and I'm not into faked smiling anyway. :-) Read less

Four of us had lunch here and all were satisfied. The place, on the second floor, is clean and comfortable. The food is definitely the attraction. Distinctively spiced pumpkin curry, perfectly stewed eggplant and nicely-textured thai noodles were pleasing to the palate. Though you can see the kitchen from your table, ventilation is very good and the staff, polite and unobtrusive.

Look for the stairway to this upstairs restaurant and it won't disappoint . An airy, friendly and busy establishment that helps give vegetarian food a good name. Real veggie food is not easy to find in CM and the variety of dishes on offer will not bore you, even if you're a seasoned meat eater. If you are eating with three or more then you can pick a selection of dishes 'mezze' style, which I would recommend doing. You can choose how hot you want your curries and there are non-piquant options too.

Visited Anchan twice for lunch during my stay and enjoyed it both times. Menu selection is limited but what I had was delicious. Very relaxed, clean and pleasant environment, service was warm. It would be an even nicer experience once the construction across the road is over. Especially liked sitting on the balcony and enjoying the breeze.

Reminiscing about Thai food, my thoughts inevitably drift to eating at Anchan. Some of the best food I had in Chiang Mai, "clean" and affordably priced. The trick is to coordinate your orders so you can sample several different dishes (for example, the next day you would definitely regret ordering two curries...). It's good to balance out the pad thai with a curry, or to combine a soup with veg/or tofu stir-fry! Sometimes it was hard to get a seat, but it was difficult not to try if we were walking by... the "Organic Vegetable" shop at street level nearby is also fantastic and usually doing brisk business as well.

The chef is outstanding and although its vegetarian and I'm not. As he advertises, you don't miss the meat. The flavors are outstanding, and Ay's mushroom and tofu pad Thai is the best pad Thai I've had in 7 months of living here! Cooked to order, charming dining room and fresh organic deliciousness!

Ordered with no chile: Mushroom Phad Thai, stir fried vegetables and long eggplant with brown rice. There was plenty for two of us. Within walking distance of Kantary Hills Hotel and Serviced Apartments.

I read a lot of reviews about this place so decided to head down for my birthday. To be honest I don't think the dishes were anything to shout about. Just vegetarian versions of standard Thai dishes. Pun Pun in Wat Suan Dok blew this place away. Also beware very small and no orders after 8pm.

It was nice to discover this gem that served fresh healthy food. It was nice to have a curry served with brown rice. Definitely reserve a table or get there early. Many people were turned away while we were there.
It is easy to miss as Soi 8 is narrow and the restaurant itself is up some stairs but it is worth the difficulties in parking because the food is yumm.y

Probably the best vegetarian restaurant I have ever been to. And I am not a vegetarian but I found the place delightful! And they serve Beer Lao Dark, which is the best beer you can get in this region of Asia.

If Chiang Mai is a mountain of deliciously great food, Anchan is it's summit. This is possibly my favorite restaurant in the whole world, and I've eaten through quite a lot it. The food is local organic vegetarian and stonkingly good! The chef is simply gifted, a genius even, a gastronomical wizard. Bonne appétit! Read less

This place is a bit off the beaten path. It took a bit of explaining for our taxi driver to find it and were a little unsure when we arrived. However, once we walked in and were greeted by the Dutch co-owner we felt right at home. The kitchen only serves the freshest ingredients and the food was amazing. We just couldn't believe how good everything was. My wife is a vegan, but I am not. But we both loved the food. I would recommend this restaurant to anyone. The food is just so good and the atmosphere is very casual and friendly. It is worth the effort finding this gem. I rated it "mid range" since it is maybe more than the average Laos restaurant, but it was an incredible value.

My wife had the Pumpkin curry; she asked for it not to be spicy, but it was still significantly spicy (for her). It was good, but the taste was not much different than what you can find elsewhere. I had the Tom Yum rice, and asked for spicy. It was more or less as spicy as my wife's ''non-spicy'' curry. I was a little disappointed with my choice, I felt the taste was a little off. I understand everything is made from scratch and they don't fish sauce, msg, etc, so that is definitely a BIG plus; but I didn't feel like it was outstanding. Furthermore price is cheap (100-150BHT/dish) but you can definitely find better value elsewhere. Overall, good standard fare, but not heads above the others. Read less

My girlfriend and I had heard good things about Anchan, and to our pleasant surprise, discovered that it was located just outside where we were staying on Nimmanhaeminda Rd. We actually ate there two nights in a row, which we seldom do. The first night, we had the Veggie Tempura, Massaman Curry, and Tempeh Stir-Fry with Thai Hot Basil. We liked it a lot, though the curry was a touch spicy for my girlfriend (I, however, enjoyed it). The second night, we ordered the Pumpkin Red Curry and Mushroom Pad Thai--and again the Veggie Tempura. We asked if they could do the Pumpkin Red Curry with a little less spice, and it was perfect for her--flavorful with just a touch of heat (to our farang palates). We also sampled the light and dark varieties of Lao Beer (evidently brewed from rice), which we hadn't seen much elsewhere. Overall, I recommend Anchan for a casual, reasonably-priced vegetarian meal. Our server was very helpful and friendly (as was the other staff) and even gave me some tips on my woeful, absolute-beginner level attempts to say things in Thai, which was helpful. Fortunately, she spoke English very well. Read less

We enjoyed a delicious lunch with super friendly service. Just off the main road on the first floor. Open kitchen and very clean. We had the green vegetable curry which was very generous in size and an amazing aubergine and parmesan dish. Beer available too
Had massaman curry with rice and bottle water. I asked for curry to be less spicy, which meant western "hot", it was perfect. Will come back. Paid 135 baht plus tip for a great lunch.

Try the red curry and the banana flower salad, fantastic. I really recommend this place. Also a very nice terrace where you can look at the street life. I am not vegetarian but I will go back for sure
Tasty Thai vegetarian food. However I was expecting more. The variety was very limited. Also the restaurant shuts at 8:30 which is a little inconvenient.

Needed something spicy, so went to Anchan and ordered the pumpkin red curry - loved it. Tasty and thick curry, the spiciness was what I needed ('medium' spiciness for me :-)) and didn't overwhelm the taste of the dish. Had 2 plates of rice to go with it! Of course I also had my all-time favourite Banana Flower Salad.

It's a perfect t qaint place with fabulous service and great food ! I am a hard core non vegetarian but totally loved this one ! In spite of the fact that it's vegan and having coffee here isn't such a great idea but food is great !
Loved the place and Nong's service. So far, hands down, this is the best vegetarian food in town. Quality of another level!

Sitting at the small little soi on Nimmanhaemin Road that, for some reason, isn't numbered — confusingly, many people even call it Soi 13, even though that's on the opposite end of the road — Anchan provides solid, affordable vegetarian (and vegan) fare. The restaurant is cozy without being too plush, with seats by the balcony that, presently, only really offers a view of the condo construction site across the soi. The massaman curry is fantastic, but it might do to ask for it to be non-spicy: that's when I know I enjoy it the most, and I say that having grown up with a Southeast Asian palate and thirst for spice. It's a bit of a shame that they don't stay open late. Nowhere near the best vegetarian food in Chiang Mai, but a very respectable choice nonetheless. Read less

This is a really great choice if you are looking for something a little bit different and great food. It has the feel of being in someone's house and the family are cooing and serving you. The choice of food is good and the food itself was really yummy. It was extremely reasonably priced too.

Really nice place to get some fantastic food, if you're on a plant based or vegetarian diet. The food is delicious and the open kitchen is really nice, so you can actually see, how they prepare your food! Highly recommended!!!

Anchan is a quiet sanctuary away from the hustle and bustle of busy nimman road. The staff are not the friendliest but the service is fine otherwise. Portions are large so better onr dish shared between two. The food tends to be on the spicy side both times I've been - I like my curries spicy but this was a bit too much for me. Its great that they specify which dishes and drinks are vegan though.
Food was absolutely delicious. Yes, we didn't missed a meat. Planning to come back and try some other menu. Much love from Malaysia.
We had a bit of a hard time finding it, but we were glad we found it. Food was great and people were so friendly there , definitely worth the visit!
This place has really great food. The only reason i don't give it a 5 is that its a little expensive for Chiang Mai but it is in the Nimman area. Seriously good Massaman curry.

As a big fan of vegetarian food, I was pretty excited to try out Anchan after all the great things I had heard. It did not disappoint, as everything was delicious. Green curry, Masama, fried veggies. Even the desserts were delicious. Loved it, and will definitely go back!
Food here is great, very fresh and well done. I've been here many times and never disappointed. every week the menu is new !
Home style menu with really fresh curries, salads and shakes. The menu is changing too, which makes it good when your here for a while.
The service is fast the food is outstanding. We ordered the yellow curry, it had a nice spicy kick to it... I would recommend this place... And would come back again

I was looking for the purple noodle, but I think it's a incorrect information. I ordered a fried vegetables for myself but it's too much for one person...good tasty but too sweet for me, I think there will be better for me if less sugar.
Really enjoyed everything we had at this place. So many vege dishes to choose from and everything tasty and nicely flavoured.
This place serves without any doubt the best (vegan) Thai food in town. The food is full of flavour and tastes super fresh. The menu changes now and then but all the dishes we tried were outstanding!

I was looking for some authentic thai vegetarian food, however it ended up a quite westernized dishes. It is very friendly and easy place to visit and I would recommended if your appetizer is not too "Thai" spice yet, this is the place.
Delicious food, cozy place, excellent service, beautiful dishes Arrives regularly and never disappointed

Though I am not a vegetarian, I didn't miss meats at all while I was having their food. I had Red curry, Stir-fried tofu and Tom Yang soup with rice and these 3 dishes are all great. I could become a vegetarian if there would many vegetarian restaurants providing dishes with same quality and taste.
Good and small restaurant in Chiang Mai. Excellent stir fried mushrooms. Rice was ok, though. I would come back if i am in the neighborhood.

Anchan is my favourite, first and regular choice for vegetarian food. The service is friendly and they are able to adapt many of the dishes to suit individual requests and tastes. It's comfortable and just a pleasure to be in for lunch and dinner. The food, of course, is absolutely excellent - I love it and know you will not be disappointed

I wrote a review about this restaurant almost 3 years ago. I remember the day very clearly, I had Tom Yum soup with rice and I was just fascinated. I have been a regular customer since, I was hooked. Today again I had Tom Yum soup and wow, it was heaven. By the way I'm not vegetarian but I love my vegetables. Aye, the owner/chef, is passionate and creative. He definitely knows what he is doing. Anchan is all about organic, fresh and flavorful food. Thank You Aye <3 The service is great. If the restaurant isn't very busy you will get your food within 10-15 minutes. The menu changes weekly. Price range is 95-160 baht. Fresh smoothies, hot tea, cold beers and wine are served. It's closed on Sunday and Monday. Give it a try, you will not be disappointed, I promise :) Read less

We recently had our womans entrepreneurs group lunch at ancharn. The best of all - I felt relaxed, happy and comfortable to be in a beautiful space with kind helpful people and very importantly wonderful organic food. What I love about this restaurant, is that every customer is so well cared for. Thank you to Ancharn for a shining example to oter restaurants.

I think this is the best Vegan restaurant in town. They change their menu depending on what's available fresh from the market and they cook it after you order it. Absolutely the best Pad Thai I have ever had, good curries, the usual sitr-fried veggies, as well as tempura veggies and corn fritters. Side order of rice is healthy red rice and there's a selection of smoothie drinks in addition to my favorite soda water and lime. Some people complain it's outside the old city and others say the prices are high -- I find the prices (approx 120 baht a main dish) incredibly reasonable and the great food and lovely design of the place well worth the trip!

This is a small and simple second floor restaurant that serves delicious vegetarian Thai food. It's all about the food. You are given a sheet of paper with the entire menu on it with check boxes. Just check off what you want to order and turn it in. All the food is cooked to order, so it takes 10 to 15 minutes for it to arrive. They sell beer and have small desert menu.

Every time I eat at Anchan, it is a very pleasant experience with a relaxed atmosphere and lovely staff. Anchan gives you very flavorful, wholesome food and the portion sizes are generous. This restaurant does not disappoint!
Count on them for freshness and special flavor to delight. The friendly attitude seems to attract loyalty. :-)

I have reviewed this before maybe a year ago. Anchan is still my favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai because of the following: Quality ingredients and transparency about explaining their importance Good people working there Great flavors coming from a great chef Cooked to perfection unlike other restaurants who generally overcook Nice atmosphere upstairs with good furnishings Conveniently Located on Nimmanahaeminda Opposite Soi 13 where you see The Salad Concept. Down a short walk...look up for the sign Read less

I've been living in Chiang Mai for about 7 years, and always keep an eye out for restaurants that serve very tasty food and use the highest quality ingredients. Restaurants and cafes come and go frequently in this popular city. Year after year, Anchan remains my top restaurant choice, for myself, for my food purist friends, for first time visitors to Thailand, and for vegetarians and non-vegetarians alike. The owners diligently source local fresh organic herbs and vegetables and use quality ingredients like Himalayan Pink salt, unprocessed sugar, and Brown Rice. The flavors are TOP of the line tasty and balanced, with large serving sizes and good prices. They even cater to requests for spice and sweetness levels. They also sell clean ingredient herbal products, cleaning supplies, jams and salad dressings made right here in Chiang Mai. Check out this gem while you're in Chiang Mai! Read less

Great vegetarian food! So glad we went here after reading all the recommendations. It was far away from where we stayed but it was well worth it. We had the Massaman curry, Fried rice and Pad Thai and ended it with a great conversation with the owner who is an animal lover! Very highly recommend this place.

Having visited Chiang Mai a few times and heard Anchan's praises being sung on more than one occasion I decided to give it a go. I was not disappointed. I ordered a Mango smoothie and Massaman vegetarian curry. The smoothie was thick, rich and flavoursome - definite lifesaver in the sweltering heat. The curry was as good as, if not better than any massaman I've had with meat. Hearty porion size as well. The only thing letting this place down is the service. The server's speak very little English which isn't a problem, you tick what you want from the menu so it's made easy. The problem lies in their inattentiveness. Whenever someone's needed for a drink refill or an order, nobody seems to be looking in your way. A tad annoying but not the end of the world. The food is the real deal-sealer here and I'd encourage you to go and give it a go yourself. Read less

We are never disappointed when we eat at Anchan. We have been to all the many excellent veg restaurants in CMai many times but Anchan is still top dog. At first the prices may seem a little high but the portions are huge so we always order two main dishes instead of three as we would in other restaurants. The flavors are fantastic and the wide variety of ingredients are always the best quality. The results are as good or better then home cooking. Don't miss this restaurant!!!

Love this restaurant! Masaman curry and Banana Blossom Salad were delicious, and well as many other items we’ve tried. The food is delicious – it is cooked with love! Service is good & friendly, and no Internet so you can talk to each other! Don’t miss the great food!

Anchan is always on my list of my Vegan Eatery in Chiang Mai! The location is at Nimman road Soi 8 which is easy to find! Ordered a masaman curry with red and brown rice, and a passion fruit smoothies as a late lunch. Not exaggerating but the masaman really is THE BEST I've even had for masaman style. It came with tofu, potatoes and lots of vegetable. Smoothies was very fresh and come in large portion! Environment is great, deco is nice. Owner is friendly and they got some homemade jam too! Menu is very clear staying all good that are vegetarian, or vegan. Here's the place which is vegan friendly with nice food! My bill is just about 170B, real deal for nice vegan food in the town! Read less
We have had quite a few excellent meals here during our stay in Chiang Mai. The food tastes amazing and fresh.

This restaurant is without a doubt our favourite little local joint - and you don't even realise it's vegetarian! So meat-lovers - this is one for you too! The staff are friendly and attentive. The food is superb with only the freshest ingredients. Great value for money.The restaurant itself is fairly small and super clean. Seriously, go! Highly recommend.

I love Anchan! It's one of my favourite restaurants in Chiang Mai. I usually get the same dish - the stir-fry veggies with steamed rice and a mango smoothie. I instantly feel at peace when I enter the restaurant, with the calming music, the beautiful mural and the friendly faces that greet me. I also love how the kitchen is open and you can see the dishes being made from scratch with beautiful, vibrant plant-based ingredients. They are also happy to alter meals for you if you have special requirements or preferences. The only downside is that it's not entirely vegan, but luckily there are quite a few vegan options. Read less
Good food and good location. Fresh and real organic vegetable, many home made ingredients. Totally worth a visit.

Not only do I enjoy the brightly paints murals in this restaurant, but the food is divine. Cooked perfectly, willing to tailor make dishes for your needs, and delicious. No need to say more other than get to Anchan, you won't regret it.

Had dinner with family on the first day in Chiang Mai. We stayed at Nimman area so we can just walk there as it's just across the main road. We ordered rice with a few dishes, one of the dish that i loved the most was the curry with brinjal. Price range about 100-200baht per dish, reasonable because the portion is big. It was abit hot but bearable since it's on the 2nd floor, without window.

I've eaten here twice and ordered the curry. Once early lunch and once late lunch. First time my vegetables where still hard and raw, second time the only vegi's I got were Thai eggplants. Seems like the trick could be to come at exactly lunch time to avoid getting bottom of the bowl or not yet ready vegetables. Not cool if you ask me.
Menu well balanced and food freshly cooked to a high standard. Be sure to call and book as this place has limited seating and closes early evening.

I have been a veggie for 40 years and rate Anchan's food superb and the location in the middle of Nimman, Chiang Mai is perfect for those visits. Lack of aircon is the only downside (it is 'open air') but the food cannot be faulted.

They know how to do veggies, I had the basil stir fried tempeh which was delicious with fresh carrots, beans and large oyster mushrooms. They also refresh their menu all the time based on vegetable availability and season. No AC but lots of fans :)

Lovely little ambience on a side street in Nimma. Was able to sit at a two-top overlooking the street with an open air side. Massaman curry with brown rice paired with cinnamon ginger iced tea was the bomb!
The menu is basic but it's not about quantity, this place has quality! If only the balcony view wasn't of power lines & extreme cables dangling from the roof - food & service was 10/10!
Lovely restaurant with a rotating menu - really fresh ingredients with both vegetarian and vegan options. Definitely would recommend.

My husband and I have lived in Chiang Mai for about a year now and Anchan is by far our favorite restaurant! Aye and his staff are friendly and the food is fresh and delicious. The changing weekly menu offers great variety so you can never get bored even if you come here every day (which we often do). If you're in Chiang Mai, you have to stop by Anchan (and you'll want to do so early in your trip). We can't recommend it highly enough!
Any time we come to Chiang Mai this place is a must. The foof is fresh and tasty, even non-vegetarians are pleased, again and again.

So ok some of my best friends are vegetarians and usually they have to find something to eat on the menu of regular restaurants, but I heard such praise of Anchan that I finally agreed to give it a try. The food was excellent and I really did not miss the meat. Everything was fresh and tasty we tried a variety of dishes including pasta and curries. I hate to admit this but I would definitely eat here again. The restaurant is open for lunch and the again for dinner, check opening hours before going.

nice staff, good food, try the pasta... Staff always checks if everything is fine. It wasn't that sweet than in other places. In Chiang Mai you find everything very sweet... tell them if you don't want to have it sweet, p.e. smoothy, fruit drink...

My wife and I ate here for dinner this evening, and were thoroughly impressed. We split the vegetarian tom yum, which was arguably the best I've had since arriving to Thailand. For our entrees, I had the pasta with tempe ragu, and she had the green mango salad. As cliche as it sounds, these people really care about their food, and you can taste that everything is cooked with love. The ingredients are all so fresh, and the flavors are incredible. I will be returning here many times!

Last Saturday my wife and I made up our minds to go to Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant from the place where we live in San Sai to have lunch at this lovely restaurant, and we invited my mother-in-law to join us. Having read the enthusiastic reviews on Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant before I just did not want to postpone my first visit there, and it was absolutely worth-while to go there despite the traffic in this part of the city. The interior of the Anchan is nice with comfortable wooden tables and chairs and everything is well-kept and clean. There is an open kitchen so you can have a look how the meals get prepared. We were welcomed warmly with a smile, having made a reservation before as it was a Saturday. I had dishes from the menu which is changing weekly, and I had the purple eggplant mushroom pepper garlic stirfry, absolutely divine, and the pad thai mushroom and rice, very very delicious, and one glass of the wonderful anchan juice and later a papaya lime smoothie which was great as well. My dessert was a very tasty blueberry cheese pie, and being a German I could die for cheese cakes of course. The two ladies had different meals which they enjoyed as much as I did enjoy the meals which I had ordered. The quality of the treats was superb, the meals abundant and the attention and the kind service made our day. All of the staff were very kind and mindful and competent. We loved the place from the very start and will return again and again. We are not vegetarians, but we love health food, and here at the Anchan you will have fresh food and fantastic flavours and will be as happy and content as we were. I read in one review that Anchan is offering wines as well. I will gladly try this next time when we will be there on a cooler day. April and May in Thailand are not the appropriate months to have a wine accompanying a lunch. The prices are absolutely reasonable, the quality of what the eater gets served is definitely high standard, and I do GLADLY RECOMMEND this restaurant for every eater who wants to have a real real good vegetarian lunch or dinner. Anchan is fantastic!!! Thank you for this wonderful experience, dear Anchan. We will certainly recommend you to our family and to our friends, and we will be back soon. Read less
A great find, we went twice during our stay and were not disappointed. My husband is a meat lover, and he didn't miss his meat.

Good food, good value, chill place and service. Tempeh curry was good, although Im not a huge fan of the taste of the tempeh. It was softer and tasted a little fermented than Ive had before. On another visit, I got the Banana blossom salad. Its battered, fried and tossed with salad. That was on the pricey end (Asian cost), and hope the salad was organic for the price?

Ate at Anchan yesterday. They will really cater to your needs. If you don't want added sugar, or less oil or salt to your meals just specify and they will provide. Plus the base meals in and of themselves are fantastic. Very tasty!
Best meal in Chang Mai! We are not vegetarians, but didn't miss the meat, and were amazed with the flavors😜. The sweet pea/mushroom sauté, green mango salad and Masaman curry... All to die for!
My first vegetarian restaurant. Certainly hope to be back on this visit. Highly recommended. Particularly: mushroom pad Thai, and the banana-honey smoothie. But all our dishes were excellent.

We discovered this place when we were in Chiang Mai 2 years ago and were very excited to go back. Everything was great again! Banana Flower salad, Pumpkin curry, aubergine with Thai miso, & asparagus something.. Loved it! All was super fresh & very flavourful. I'm not a vegetarian but if I could cook like these guys I would never have to eat meat again!
I go here when I'm craving healthy food, and haven't ever been disappointed. SUCH big salads. I love the drinks. The smoothie with ginger in it is too gingery though, it's like fire.

All the dishes we ordered were cooked on the spot and the ingredients very fresh. The food was tasty and well presented. There was very little not to like apart from the price. It seemed a wee bit steep for Chiang Mai considering that portions were small. The interior and decor of Anchan was pleasant enough.

I have eaten here a number of time and the food is always good, I have requested due to dietary restrictions, dishes to be changed or made that were not on the menu and each time i was accommodated with out fuss. A notch above the other vegetarian restaurants in Chiangmai. Very friendly staff and service.
All the food was very well prepared and fresh. I'm only giving it a 4 due to my particular taste, but I would definitely go back.

Went with some Vegan Friends to Anchan. It was recommend by a few Friends who like Veg. I usually find Vegetarian places here kind if dull as they cater to the western palate and withhold seasoning or do not try and compensate for the ingredients they don't want to use. Anchan Does a good job of presenting Thai flavors in Vegetarian dishes. Though I think they should use more spice, the flavors are nice, the portions are ample, the ingredients were very fresh and the staff was quite attentive. They also handle some good wines to go with the food. We had a nice Spanish white that went very well with out dinner. The Waiter was very friendly and spent some time chatting about the wine and how they liked to cook. It was a warm night and the place was quite hot. I hope they have AC and are willing to use it soon. We found it to be pretty good but not exceptional. Still far above many of the Veg Places in town. Enjoy. Read less

This place can be a bit of a walk away but it was entirely worth it. The food was amazing! Plenty of vegetarian and plenty of vegan options including vegan curries. The massaman curry was absolutely delicious. It was only 95 baht and came with rice. The staff were very friendly and the place was clean. It is upstairs so don't be deterred if the doors on the ground floor are closed, you need to go up a stair case next to it. Don't be put off by the location, it is really a great place for meat eaters and non-meat eaters alike!

I think others have explained that this place is just off Nimmanahaeminda road, across from Soi 13, and just down the road on the left. I'm not quite sure why that road/alley/soi doesn't have a name. Anyway, it was worth the search. The massaman curry was especially delicious. Everything was fresh and yummy, and plenty of spice.

Foods are really good. We always order Thai dishes. Prices are not expensive. Good location, right on Nimman road. Always visit this place at least one meal every time we are in Chiang Mai. Live it No A/c in this restaurant and no pictures of their dishes on the menu.
This place was tucked away down a Soi and isn't flash but clean and neat and good service. We really enjoyed our food even though there was no meat. The fried rice was really yummy!
Everything is so flavoursome and tasty here. we kept coming back. Go here for the food and the food alone. Prepare to wait quite a while for your meal but once you get it, it is totally worth it.

Wonderful ambiance, with a great location, combines with delicious and nutritious food makes for a magnificent meal. Great for kids and family! Open air, and busy. Go early and go hungry. The pumpkin red curry, the long eggplant miso, and the pumpkin egg stir fry were all excellent. Quick service, plated family style.

Heard very good things about this place and saw all of the good reviews but was not wowed by the location (upstairs) and there is loud, dusty construction going on across the street. The whole Nimman area was disappointing as there is heavy street traffic and no real sidewalks anywhere. Chiang Mai is not very tourist friendly overall.
I don't normally eat at vegetarian restaurants, but the massaman curry is worth coming back for. If only I could find a place serving meat that made a curry this good.

First of all this place is definitely more of a day establishment, I wouldn't recommend going for dinner as it doesn't really have a great ambience and isn't particularly pretty. The food is just average in my opinion (my veg curry tasted of just coconut!), but you do get great, attentive service. Wish I liked the food more as the servers were so nice.

I am vegetariano AMD i love veggies. At Anchan I had The best eggplants ever! And The anchan rice was delicious! We ordered 2 disches each - pur eyes were bigger than our mouths- and it was definetely too much! They offer take away. Affordable prices as generous portions.Ah and the papaya lime sooth was delicious! So happy to have tried it!

We had planned to go to Pun Pun, recommended by Lonely Planet, but then we saw a review on TripAdvisor that said that Anchan was better and less expensive. It turned out to be a 5 minute walk from our hotel -- even better. We ordered an eggplant stir fry and the massaman curry. The curry was amazing, and the stir fry quite good. One reviewer noted the less than welcoming service, which is true. But if you're looking for great vegetarian food, you can hardly do better.

I agree with the other reviewers that the food here rocks...extremely flavorful and they really do make you "forget the meat". So why just 3 stars? Two reasons: First, they messed up my order and totally forgot to bring me my beverage, then charged me for it. This is a place where you fill in your own order by checking boxes on a menu slip, so it wasn't a language problem. Second issue is that as I was dining here, a rat ambled out from the potted plant on the restaurant balcony. Then, as I was leaving the restaurant later, I saw another rat at the base of the stairs leading up to the place. Two rats in the course of 30 minutes...maybe that's Thailand for you but it unsettled me nonetheless. Those minuses detracted from the overall experience. A pity because the food was delicious. Read less

I came there and the waitress gave me the menu without saying hi. When I asked for wifi the boss told me very unfriendly wifi is only for him. His restaurant is not an internet cafe and people come have just one drink and use wifi. There are so many cafes, restaurants etc around that area, I didn't understand this reaction at all... Not customer friendly!
I loved this restaurant! Delicious vegetarian food- it is a must if you are in chiang mai! My boyfriend teally enjoyed it too and he is not a vegetarian! The massaman curry is amazing!
Loved it all. So many flavours, with unusual as well as more well known dishes. Fresh and gorgeous. Well organised and friendly staff

I love finding a vegetarian restaurant that doesn't try to mimic meat in their dishes! At Anchan, the star of the dishes are the actual ingredients with flavors so well balanced you want to try more dishes! We wanted a simple yet light lunch and ordered the Asparagus Omelet and Fried Tofu in Tamarind Sauce. Both dishes were delicious and complemented each other. The omelet had asparagus, straw and king mushrooms for great texture and flavors - a little coconut milk gave it an extra richness making it so satisfying. The Fried Tofu gave this dish that "meaty" satisfaction with a sweet and sour from the tamarind sauce that was delicious. We chose the Cinnamon Ginger Iced Tea which was so refreshing - but I'm sure it would be a great hot beverage also. The only disappointment was that there were only 2 of us for lunch - it would have been better if we were 4 ordering several - or all - of the dishes - as the proprietress suggested, because the small menu changes weekly with what's available at the market, and they are closed Sunday and Monday - meaning they won't have the same dishes on the menu when we come back a second time next week! I really can't wait to come back for another visit here! 375THB or around $11 for 2 dishes and 2 beverages. Read less

Having covered the food, I must say that when I eat in a place this highly recommended,I have come to expect a few simple things concerning service. Let's start with a welcome a simple hello would suffice. As we looked for a table to choose we were told "that's reserved" OK no problem, we took seats at another and were given a menu to fill out. Still not a word spoken. We then had to get up to submit it while "owner, manager, server" was busy on Facebook, beneath the sign that read "no wifi talk to each other" the juxtaposition made us grin. We really felt like we were imposing. Never once inquired about our meal or needs. We found the restaurant itself very clean but somewhat clinical. Every table set squarely on the tile lines the room lacks any artistic flare, no art or photos, no plants or flower. And although out of their control and temporary a view overlooking wires and a huge construction project. Maybe I had high expectations but I left disappointed. I'd rather have food of average quality in a warm welcoming atmosphere, then great food where I feel unwelcome. Read less

Located upstairs, the place is run by a cute family of 5 (even the kids help). The food is fast but the flavors are fantastic! We got the Penang curry, and the fried tofu with tamarind sauce. Excellent! And both dishes under 100 baht each. Note they close at 8.30pm.

I came to this place because of a curious bit of different food. After Thai and Italian I wanted to try vegetarian food, and reading the good reviews on TA I have not hesitated from making a visit. The food is very good and fresh, I ate a good vegetable soup and a great juice. Simple but all good, including a beautiful and youthful atmosphere. I recommend it
The menu was basic but the food that came out was very good. In particular the Tamarind Tofu was like nothing else I have ever eaten!! Service was very good and everyone spoke English.
We shared several dishes together and all of them were really tasty ! Prices are very affordable , a dish cost between 50 and 150 Bath. A must try in Chiang Mai!
Every time I go back to Chiang Mai, I head straight for Anchan and I/m met with big smiles from the staff and superb Thai vegetarian food!

Of the many vegetarian meals we've had in Thailand, this is the best. The chef/owner cooks everything to order in an open kitchen and he manages flavor without the benefit of some of the typical Thai elements (e.g. fish sauce, dried shrimp, etc.)--which is a remarkable feat. He makes a fantastic Tom Yum soup, brimming with fresh tomatoes, so freshly prepared that the galangal and lemongrass retain a piquancy that more than compensates for the usual addition of shrimp. A big win for vegetarians in CM

In one night i went to Anchan and eat some Vegetarian foods, and i order two soup, and the soup is really spicy, the restaurant have many spicy dishes so you need to look carefully if you can't eat too spicy.I think the best dish is the omelette, and you must taste it! the omelette have basil leave at it taste excellent! After the soup and the egg they gave us a plate of red rice and eat the rice withe other dishes. Other dishes is a little too salty so you need to ask for some water.

Amazing tempura - fresh juice. The restaurant is really nice and clean small terrace on balcony the staff is really sweet - good price Francais Tres bon vegeterian resto tres propre avec 3 petites tables sur le balcon - petit prix

After weeks of meat-on-rice style local food, finding a place that serves up large portions of perfectly cooked, brightly coloured fresh vegetables was like stumbling upon an oasis. Papaya and lime smoothie was incredible, and myself & partner shared a veggie massaman currie and a vegetable stir fry. Both came with a very tasty brown/red rice. Beautiful atmosphere, location, helpful staff and perfect food. Excellent spot for vegetarians and those who aren't vegetarians such as ourselves, to re-stock on nutrients and delicious food. Will definitely return. Read less

We ate here twice and my other family members who stayed on the trip longer ate here 3 times. This was the only meal we ate that was super fresh, vibrant and had some thought and finese in the dishes that came out. We were approx a 15 mins songtaew ride away from this place but it was totally worth the trouble. It was in a very trendy area of Chiang mai. Loved the salad of banana flowers the best, a flavour sensation. It was cheaper then the fancier tourist restaurants in central Chiang mai and better quality. Make sure you book as it is popular. And you can get a glass of wine which is important for me !!
Authentic, mouth-watering and simply delicious! This was our favourite meal in Thailand by far. The curry had a kick, the drinks were thirst-quenching and the staff were calm and friendly.
The owner is a chef that makes vegetarian food to die for. The stuff is very helpful and nice And the food is superb. Will come back always 10 stars restaurant
First time here, definitely going back next time we visit! Friendly staff, cheap and authentic food. You won't regret going!

My boyfriend and I love the food at Anchan. It's Thai food, but somehow so much better and flavorful than any street food or fancy thai food. The cashew tempeh was fantastic, I though I knew what cashew dishes tasted like here but somehow Anchan just does it so well! Its delicious flavors and you can't help but eat everything in front of you. The staff is very nice and can answer any questions about the menu. You can't go wrong with any of the dishes, even if you are not vegetarian.
A little tricky to find, but worth it. Made to order great veg food and reasonably priced. The owners are very friendly and helpful.
I was taken there by a friend. I was thoroughly impressed! The owners are very kind and welcoming and their vegetable stir fry dishes are to die for!
We found it quite tricky to find, however by the time we did find it, it was definitely worth it. We had the massaman as it was vegan and it tasted sublime. Nice atmosphere and great food!

Great place to get healthy and fresh food and juices to your liking. In addition to providing vegan and vegetarian food they also customize their food if you have certain food allergies. Would definitely recommend!

Fresh tasty food. We ordered a variety of dishes and all were good. Not that cheap by Thailand standards but worth it for the taste. Service could have been more friendly but worth going for the quality of the food

By the time I reached Anchan, which I was determined to track down based on trip advisor reviews, I was desperate for vegetables and lots of them so I ordered a vegetable stir fry as well as Thai Green curry (which is my favourite). I had a banana and coconut shake too. It was all superb. I can't fault it. The veg was delicious - so many different varieties on one plate. A whole heap of goodness and the green curry was sensational. I wouldn't normally order two dishes but was so starving I ate it all. And the shake was amazing - almost like a dessert! Happy happy. I would recommend it, have recommended it and would love to go there again!

This place is great and I'm so glad I live around the corner! Even the simplest meal of stir-fired vegetables is delicious. All the ingredients taste very fresh, I think quite a few are grown by the owner and staff. I've already been several times, but keep eating the same thing (Pad Thai) because it's so good :) Everything from flavor, quality, portion size, ambiance, friendliness, and price and spot on here! Regardless of it being a vegetarian restaurant, I think tis a must try and try again in Chiang Mai!
Restaurant is very clean and the food is very fresh. Many choices for vegetarians, good service and creative chef. Would definitely recommend!

The freshest food I had in 2 weeks in Thailand. The place closes twice a week so they can source the local ingredients and it shows. The tempah burger and banan flower slald were delicious Really nice people and awesome beers...go for the Dark Beer lao

My favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai. My most recent visit I had a mushroom omelet which was lovely, my boyfriend had a massaman curry which was also very tasty. It was nice to eat Anchan tempura from the chefs own garden, something unique on the menu that you won't find at other restaurants. Top quality food that isn't expensive and very friendly service. I really love that you can see the kitchen from your table, it makes you feel at home. I know from speaking to the chefs that they go to great lengths to source the best organic vegetables and you can certainly taste that.

I love this place! Go there once a week at least. The chef changes the menu and makes all kinds of creative vegetarian dishes- LOVE the miso eggplant- crave it! Definitely worth a visit and a good place to sit and work with a computer.

I've lived and traveled in Asia for three years, and have eaten in so many places. This one is the very best. Everything I've had on the menu is supremely delicious. My favorite food on Planet Earth is their miso eggplant. Also, the cinnamon ginger iced tea is amazing!! You must try this place if you are in Chiang Mai.

Anchan is a must try while in Chiang Mai, and for those living more long term in the city, it is a place that should be frequented. They consistently provide some of the most delicious vegetarian food in the city. The price ranges from about 100 to 160 baht, which is a little more expensive, but the portions are huge and I almost always end up taking some home! I feel lucky to live within walking distance to this place.
Good food and good service.... Best vegetarian restaurant in town. I've been there many times. Anchan proves that vegetarian food can be both flavorable.

Foods fresh. Large servings. Decent pricing. Not much more you could ask for! Pretty sure the menu changes each week, and the owner goes to the market to pick the veggies himself. Would definitely return :)

The three of us had lunch there. Quite expensive, good taste, fair portions, very unpleasant service. Both the man and the woman in charge were sour faced, hardly responsive, looked unhappy from the moment we walked in till we left. This is not the Thai hospitality we are used to.

I've eaten at Anchan Vegetarian more than several times now and haven't found the vegetarian food disappointing at this venue as I have at some others here in town. , Banana flower salad lovely, raw chocolates wonderful, Greek Salad with homemade feta fantastic. I did try their "signature drink" (can't remember what it was called, but it was lilac/purple) and found it a bit overly sweet for my personal taste. Love sitting on the balcony and people watching on the Soi below, friendly attentive staff and good prices. Highly recommend this restaurant...even if you aren't a vegetarian. Marie (nannyre).

After seeing the Trip Advisor reviews we ventured to Anchan and were not disappointed. We loved this place so much we have been twice on this trip. The food is delicious, simply stunning and very cost effective. We paid approx. NZ$18.00 for two dishes, rice and two smoothies. We can fully understand why this is one of the top 5 restaurants in Chiang Mai. The ordering system is a little different from what you have back home. You get a sheet of paper with all the food choices on and you select how many you want of each dish. The first time we dined at Anchan we went with three mains between the two of us (this is sharing food) and it was way too much. The second time we went with two dishes and a smoothie, close to being too much again. The only disappointment was the fact that we were way to full to try the blueberry cheesecake desert. Even if you are not a vegetarian please go and give this place a try you won’t be disappointed. The restaurant is on the second floor of a building just off a busy street full of bars and restaurants. Yes there is a building site opposite but it was dark so we didn’t mind at all. Be warned that they stop taking orders at 8.30pm. Read less

The chef here is a GENIUS! If you want the best food and the best service, please check it out! You will not be disappointed! The Tom Yum Goong Soup and Massaman are out of this world as are all of their dishes! Even if you are not a Vegan or Vegetarian you will love this restaurant, peace.

Off Nimman Road on Soi Hillside 2 opposite soi 13, walk to end of Soi 2. Open 11:30 - 2:30pm & 5:00 to 8:30 pm. Relaxed setting for a good lunch, refreshing drinks. Also sells loose tea, like Moringa, and some natural body products by Sabu-Sabu.

I came here based on trip advisor users reviews. Although am not a vegetarian, still loved it and I will not miss meat even if i eat nowhere but here for the whole week. Just finished my panaeng curry, and it was out of this world, you can smell the freshness and see the open clean kitchen the moment you enter the restaurant. Portions are big too.

...or even vegetarian, but my wife loves healthy food, so I bought her here for a lunch date. I usually prefer to have some kind of meat with my meal, but I didn't miss it at all here. The food is clearly carefully made (the kitchen is open plan, so you can watch your meal being prepared), and very tasty. Their portions are big, and prices are very reasonable. To be honest, I'm a little surprised it's #3 for restaurants in Chiang Mai here on tripadvisor (at the time of writing), given the incredible variety and choice available in Chiang Mai, but we'll certainly be going back.

I am vegan, and I've brought many friends to Anchan who were meat eaters. Everyone agrees, it's just great food! Definitely one of my favorites in Chiang Mai. All food is prepared with incredible care, using local ingredients. You can have amazing Thai traditional dishes with a few others thrown in. English is spoken. You can't go wrong with any of their Curries. The menu changes each week depending on what is in season. Average dish is $3USD - $4USD, so slightly more expensive then street food, but not by much! And the quality is much better here.

Im not a vegetarian but the food here is really tasty and better than many of the meat equivalents. I wouldn't even say come here to be healthy but just to eat great food. I really liked the panang curry and eggplant with miso

The food at Anchan is delicious! Everything that we tasted was equally delightful, but we especially enjoyed the juices, the silky Massaman curry, and the tempura - the crispy vegetable and beautiful flower tempura disappeared so quickly that we had to order a second plate. Our group had a wonderful time and Wouter was very gracious and helpful in organizing our gathering. Thank You, Anchan! See you again soon.
This place is nice, clean and tidy. Also good vegetarian food. I love Panang Curry & Thai Spicy Salad recommended !!
One of the best food I have ever had. The staff was very nice. The food was just too good. Highly, highly recommend Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant

I always love the food at Anchan. Clean tidy premises, with an inspired menu from Aye that changes weekly. I've already booked to go again next week! The food is very fresh and made to order. That atmosphere is ambient with a small outdoor balcony. No smoking on balcony.
This was the best place we've eaten in Chiang Mai, I recommend to try out the tom yam, pad thai, pumpkin curry. Servings are big also.

I am not a vegetarian, but after reading about this place, we decided to try it. It was surprising how small the place was, also the situation is bit surprising but I had the best fried rice ever, and also the curries were so good. One thing though if you want some wine with your dinner, bring your own as it is often the case in small restaurants in Thailand as they do not drink wine, but beers or whiskey. Definitely worth your while as it is really delicious.

One of the best meal I had so far in thailand. Everything was delicious! (But I would skip the desert though). The anchan fried rice and banana flower salad we amazing. I really like meat, but as the other said already, you wont miss it.

Ate here after seeing such good reviews on Trip Advisors when searching for healthy, vegetarian food in Chinag Mai. Was a little difficult to find down an alleyway and upstairs, could do with some signage on main road, but worth the effort. All food was delicious and large servings, great value for money, and the fresh fruit smoothies were delicious. Love that they use organic produce and sell some great jams and natural health care products too. The service was excellent as well, just wish we had found it sooner so we could have eaten there again!!!

I had Massaman Curry, Mango/Banana Smoothies and Cacao truffles to top it off! Everything was so delicious! The owner was very kind and told me that he uses mostly organic produce. He hand picks the vegetables himself and creates a new menu weekly. Nice ambiance and good service!

Great vegetarian food without MSG run by a Belgian guy and his Thai boyfriend who is the chef. Super fresh and organic food. Great flavors in all of our dishes ... I really liked the yellow curry. Prices were quite reasonable given the quality -- we gorged on 5 dishes for 3 people, plus beer, and still only paid about 1000 baht. And we felt healthy afterwards. Great meal!

I was taken to dine there by western friends who liked the food served in this interesting place located upstairs in a building in a quiet side-street off Nimman road. We went there one week-day evening when it would be less crowded, to enjoy both the food and talking to the owners/cook. the tables are well spaced and spread out for privacy and conversation. The decor is a bit cool old chic, but very pleasant. We ordered spring-rolls, pumpkin soup, fried brocolli, fried rice, fried tofu and some other tofu-inspired and vegetable dishes - which are all very well prepared with the market-to-table fresh ingredients. They do serve beer, which goes well with the meal. For dessert, we were recommended the chocolate delights with ice-cream which were simply wonderful. We were told to expect to wait for a table in the evenings. if we do not arrive early. Altogether a wonderful meal and decent prices. Will go back next time in Chiangmai. Read less

After reading the positive reviews on trip adviser, we gave it a try in one evening. The owner was really nice and welcoming, also the ambient was great. Perhaps our expectation was too high or we chose the wrong dishes, we did not enjoy the food - boiled plain (bland) salad with really garlicky dressing and very spicy but watery curry. Only thing we did enjoy was the red rice which was cooked to a perfection. We also thought the price was in a bit pricy side in Chiang Mai. Obviously its targeted for the European vegetarian and if you are one, you will enjoy it. It made us think that we could not be a vegetarian as probably we didn't understand the subtly of the organic vegetables they serve. Read less
If you cisit chiang mai never miss this experience. Excellent food Service is good Kindliness of staff you will remember
Excellent food, ate here twice during a four day visit to Chiang Mai. The staff is very friendly and courteous and I wanted to come back right after my first meal there.

OMG...I am blown away. I have never had such a well seasoned and filling vegetarian meal like this. I actually thought I was eating meat when I had the Deepfried Tofu with Mushroom Sauce. I even bought a jar of their lychee jam which I am eating right now... and it is out of this world. Staff and service were amazing. Such a great experience all around. Hands down the best vegetarian restaurant in Chiang Mai!

Make sure to eat here!! The kitchen closes at 8:30 but if you're there by 8:20 it's fine. The menu changes each week based on the fresh ingredients. The owner was very nice and informed us on the dishes and the ingredients used. The first night we both got the red pumpkin curry - delicious. They serve wild rice which was a nice change. The vegan chocolate cake is so moist and decadent - must try! The second time we came we had the pad thai which was the best pad Thai we had our whole trip! And the anchan fried rice! Both delicious. Highly recommend!

We've eaten here twice now since arriving in Chang Mai. The food is delicious and served promptly by lovely and polite staff members. Well worth a visit, even if you're not vegetarian you'll enjoy the variety and flavours.

it's a simply little vegetarian restaurant located at a quiet location but not quite difficult to find on NimmanRd they have pretty simple menu,but change weekly. and the freshness of the ingredients and how they serve them shows u how much heart they put in it
Really amazing food - new menu every week. Only pretty simple first floor restaurant - one room 8 tables - so don't go for the atmosphere. But do go for the food.

I am not a vegetarian but I LOVE good food. Having seen this listed as the #4 restaurant in Chiang Mai we hopped in a "rot dang" from the center of town and were taken to the restaurant for 20THB! The food was sensational, definitely the best meal we have had since arriving 9 days ago - and that is saying something as everything has been food so far! We went for the som tam (papaya salad) and mushroom pad thai (mushroom thai fried noodles). We also had the tofu burger on homemade roll. The food was fresh, nourishing and full of flavour. The venue was spotless and the open plan kitchen was comforting. Even the bathroom was spotless and had a soap dispenser - rare, it seems, in Thailand! I cannot recommend this restaurant enough to anyone who wants to try a tasty meal. They have tempura, curries, stir fry, salads but the menu is not 39 pages long and it is easy to make your choices. Please go along! P.S if you ask to get dropped at Soi 13 on Nimmanahaeminda Road you will be on a street corner of a busy main road. Cross the road and keep walking straight ahead to what looks like a quiet dead end street. You will see the Anchan sign on the left. Read less
We came in here looking for a light meal and wow - this food is excellent! Simple, healthy and delicious vegetarian Thai food and lovely people running the place! Will be back soon!

First off, I'm a meat lover. The idea of 'vegetarian' usually elicits a sigh and sense of doubt. Not the case here. Hands down, one of the best meals I've eaten in Asia. On a side note, this is definitely a farang joint. Pros: Incredibly fresh Reasonably priced Nice owners Cons: Closed on Sundays Bottom Line: Eat here if you get a chance, you'll be satisfied. Read less

Have been here several times and need to leave my thoughts - visited last week 30/06/14. Restaurant is usually busy and closed Sunday and Monday. It is not air conditioned and there are only a few fans, if you are bothered by the heat at this time of year maybe don't go. We sat on the balcony over looking the dark soi at the side of Hillside 2 to Condo block. We walked here from where we are staying - parking is a little tricky around here. From Nimmerheiman road look for Hillside 2 and the Burmese Restaurant and the restaurant is the last on the left as you go down the little soi. Menus is smaller than I remember - I think maybe the time of year - you fill it in with tick boxes for the dishes you would like. All fresh and delicious - staff speak English and the happy chef/owner has fluent English. The tastes are fantastic don't be put off by the word vegetarian! Ambience is basic - it's just a cafe - quite plain - it upstairs take note if you have trouble with stairs. Open kitchen in the back of the cafe - this adds to the heat! They are licensed and serve beer. They close at 9pm! Read less
