As the title says, without a doubt some of the best food i have ever had. The staff & the owners are incredibly nice & genuine people. As a side note, the restaurant owners run a cooking class that i would highly recommend doing. The class runs a full day & starts off with a trip to the local market to gather some of the ingredients you will be using for the day. The best thing about the class is that it's hands on, the Chef will cook a dish & everyone tastes it, then you cook the same dish. After each dish you all sit down together, talk & enjoy the food you have just cooked. Highly, highly recommend Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant.

I'm here now. Been a vegetarian since birth. Lived in Chiang Mai for 10 years and also lived in Bangkok and Samui. For Thai style veggie food, there is simply no better than Anchan. (Sorry Blue Diamond and Khun Chern). The menu changes weekly and there has sometimes been Middle Eastern dishes, but usually it's just great and simple Thai food. The setting is very simple and now looks out at a construction site, but it's still pleasant and I keep coming back for more. Read less

I've been away from Chiang Mai for 1 year and have missed eating here so much (I came here almost daily for 3 months). I arrived this afternoon and might only stay 1-2 nights, so this place is on top of my list. They now have a weekly menu, although everything I Tried on their old menu was great too. I order tempeh with cashew nuts and it looks delicious. It tastes even better. So good to be back :-)

My friend and I had such a good time learning to cook delicious traditional Thai food with the owners of Anchan. They offer a personal setting by only allowing a small number of people in each class. We each got to cook our own dishes and brought home many left overs. If you want to be more than a number in a crowd then I highly recommend this cooking class. You will pay more than some other classes but it is well worth it in my opinion!!

I took our lovely chefs' cooking class and it was mind blowing. First of all the atmosphere was warm and friendly, actually kind of like family. Their max is 3 students so you get to cook your own food and get a lot of helpful feedback. Great experience and food.

But everything was very tasty and enjoyable! Naturally, all very good value too! Our tuk-tuk had trouble finding it but it is not too hard to find really! The staff were very nice and after the meal the owner / cook was interested to talk to us to ensure that we had a good experience.

After wasting the better part of an afternoon looking for this place due to the inaccurate maps on TA and Google, I finally asked a local expat who gave me the secret formula: it's on the same soi as the corner 7-11. The place is nothing elegant, and for some reason all of the inside tables seem to be perpetually reserved, so I sat on the balcony with the mosquitos and the other fa-rang. I ordered a banana flower salad just because... well, who wouldn't? That was a winner. The smoothie was a shot in the dark and turned out to be very bitter, but it was my own fault for not asking more questions. Service was attentive and proper, and well deserving of a tip. Clientele seemed a cut above that in the vegan joints in the Old City, but still a sprinkling of tattooed rastamen to remind you there's no meat on the menu. Limited selection compared to Khun Churn, but fresh and tasty, and not so expensive. Read less

The menu pleased all of our vegan, vegetarian, and carnivorous crowd. The food was fresh and made to order. The portions were huge and meant for sharing. Service was fast and friendly. The restaurant was open air and hot. I highly recommend the watermelon smoothie, mushroom tempura, asparagus stir fry...everything was truly delicious!

After reading the wonderful reviews of trip advisor, I decided to go and check this place out for myself. I was not dissaponted at all..the food was delicious ,well presented very tasty and very satisfying. The staff were all very friendly and so welcoming. I will definitely be back many times before I leave Chiang Mai in a months time! Will recommend to anyone coming to Chiang a Mai also.

Always fresh, always organic and delicious food, good service, very friendly staff. Sometimes when it's too busy you would need to wait for your order a little bit. But the food is so nice, it's worth waiting for. I recommend this place to all of my vegetarian friends.

Vegetarian and Vegan food I've found can be rather bland and boring, but not at Anchan. I have been here many times and each time I try something new. In fact, I've never been disappointed. You can tell that they use the highest quality ingredients and take great care when preparing the food. I've spoke with the owner several times and found out how much work goes into the food and maintaining the integrity of the restaurant. The ambiance is great and if you look around you'll notice there's beautiful art all around the walls done by local artists. Here's some of my favorites Banana Flower Salad, Pumpkin Curry, and the baba ganoush. Read less

I'm not a vegetarian, but being of south indian decent, I know good vegetarian when I have it. Anchan is very good and especially so due to their insistence on only using very high quality ingredients. I had the unique pleasure of sitting with the owner during lunch and learned what goes into their wonderful vegetarian fare. No expense spared when it comes to ingredients. The ambience is nice and open and the food is very fresh, light but satisfying (no food coma induced, but I left full and happy). The portions are large, and the menu rotates weekly depending on what seasonal ingredients are available that meet their strict quality criteria. This is place that you could easily eat at once a week and not get bored. The healthy cuisine is a nice bonus. Notable dishes were the Massaman Curry and their Fried Banana Flower Salad (especially good). Read less

Having read very positive reviews on Tripadvisor, i really wanted to try this vegetarian eatery. With an Unpretentious simple decor and friendly atmosphere, this second floor located restaurant offers a wide selection of vegetarian dishes. The 4 i've ordered were good, especially the stir fried eggplants with miso sauce, but 2 dishes were too sweet and the coconut soup was not made with fresh coconut milk....

I've never liked the vegetarian food until today, that was amazing! This restaurant has such a great, great Chef and exceptional staff, warm community feeling and I want to come back as much as possible. Just wanted to say thank you as I had a great lunch. The food was utterly delicious I loved the experience. Thank you again!

I went there last year with friends and from beginning to end was it a great experience in vegetarian kitchen. Very clean and tastefull dishes! Very friendly waiter! Very cosy to sit! You must go visit! Greetings from Belgium!
it was worth the trip to anchan, excellent food and service. when we go again to chiang mai we defentily go back to anchan.

I'm absolutely in love with this restaurant. The food is consistently fantastic at an amazing price. The owner is very friendly and will bend over to accommodate you. A little hard to find, but well worth the effort. You won't be disappointed.

Went there following the good rating on tripadvisor. This was a huge disappointment! The waiters tried their best to make up the missing pictures to the about 10 different meals. With a very estimate idea of what we ordered, the only difference was the colour of the sauce. Even the rice was bad. Also, the drinks their offer are NOT fresh (or tasty) and cost 50bath for a little glass. About the location. It's made decently nice, with a beautiful view to a construction site right next to it. In generall, I would not recommend going here to any of my friends..

Visited this lovely restaurant, situated on the first floor, several times during my stays in chiangmai. Remarquable veggiefood, made with the most frech ingrediënts you can find. Menu's are changing very often, depends What chef can find on the market, and by the local farms. Very lovely place, and very good service. Looking forward to return very quick

I took me a big detour to find the place and I almost given up to look for it. but after asking for direction, I arrived just in time before the evening crowd start. I ordered green curry and when it came, I thought it couldn't finish it but it was delicious that I finished the whole big bowl by myself. I also loved the anchan drink which is a nice purplish flower. If I am staying longer in Chiang Mai, I would definitely come back for another time. Better make reservation if you don't want to turn away.
This is a place that is well worth a visit- vegetarian or not. The food is delicious and service was so friendly. The best curry I have had in Thailand.

Decided to try Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant, prepared to be a little underwhelmed because I am not a vegetarian, and I love my meat. The lunch experience was wonderful - a pleasantly decorated (great mural!) open small restaurant, which served adventurous (at least for me) slow cooked food that was delicious, enticing to the eye and that left me glad I came. I had Coconut Palm Heart Red Curry, beautifully spiced, 130 baht, and a Lime and Ginger Shake 60 baht. Plenty of ginger and most refreshing. The service was excellent, with helpful explanations of items on the menu that interested me. The outdoor seating is just 2 tables on a small balcony - Anchan is up a short flight of stairs. The only reason I cannot give it 5 stars is its limited opening hours. I'll definitely be back! Read less
Really delicious and very Thai !tempura mushrooms are the best I have had ! You will need to make a reservation! Will for sure come back next time!

I love this restaurant, for its location, food, drinks, and friendly staff. The fresh menu changes regularly and everything I've tried has been delicious and packed full of delicious veggies! Not greasy/oily at all either. The juices are tasty as well. The place is often full or reserved in advance, and it's not a very big restaurant so be careful of this...too many times have I been disappointed because I was craving their food but couldn't get a table! (not their fault for being so delicious) The dishes are also huge portions. Read less

We have lived in Chiang Mai for almost 2 years and we celebrated the arrival of a new vegetarian place when Anchan opened. We have been constant dinner guests, eating there at least once (and sometimes 3-4 times) a week ever since! Aye, the owner and chef, makes the chili pastes from scratch, never using shrimp paste or fish sauce, which are commonly found in Thai kitchens. The massaman curry is by far the best dish we've ever tasted in Thailand, and we've introduced the restaurant to a number of our Thai friends and they know immediately that the food is deliciously authentic. That translates into spice levels that are often too much for our non-Thai tastebuds, but the staff are happy to tone down the spiciness. There isn't much on the menu we haven't tried, and the staff understand what it means to be vegan, which is a wonderful relief. Once, during one of our first few visits, we ordered a soup traditionally made with egg noodles. Before making the dish, and remembering that we're vegan from past visits, Aye came to our table and asked whether we wanted the soup with rice noodles instead. Absolutely amazing!!! The food is prepared with love in a small, open kitchen. The cozy dining room doesn't hold too many people and with the mouth-watering food, it's no surprise they're often fully booked for dinner. Be sure to call ahead to make a reservation if you're planning to visit with more than 2 people. Do not miss the opportunity to eat at Anchan when you're in Chiang Mai. They serve the best food in Thailand! Read less

Enjoyed the mixed vegetable stir fry with red rice. Large portions. Beer and other drinks are overpriced. Had large Leo for 110 baht whereas at Meechok Plaza restaurant I can get a Heineken for 90 baht. I will return to try their other dishes. Off the beaten path and not noisy

This is the second review I have done on Anchan but I figure that since I have eaten there more than any restaurant in Chiang Mai because it is so utterly delicious I should update with a second review! The restaurant is a lovely place to go and I say that as a single traveller who rocked up without any company. The chef Aye was so welcoming and kind as well as being an absolutely incredible chef that it pretty much became my second home in CM and as I met people during my three month stay I always took them to Anchan and they received the same unbelievable food and excellence service! I recommend everything on the menu. I can say that because I think over three months I ordered every dish! My personal favourites though were the banana flower salad, the tempura vegetables, Penang and Massaman curries (mmmm pumpkin) and the Miso eggplant! The restaurant also used to do the most delicious smoothies which I hope in time they bring back - if only two of them (lime and mint and passion fruit). Basically, eat here. It is lush. And I would say the best Thai food I have ever had - some claim given how long I have spent in Asia in the last twenty years…. Read less

This second floor restaurant is a bit hidden. Plus, they have limited hours of operation. 11:45-2:30, 5:30-8:00. But, once you've got there on time, it's a full satisfaction. Don't come very hungry as it's a "slow cook" style. It takes time.... Dishes, thought are relatively large and prices from 90 THB and up to 150 THB. Drinks are a bit pricey, soda for 40-50 THB it's too much. Very nice stuff and mostly foreigners. Very good place if you like vegetarian food.

Parking is not easy to come by and you must climb a flight of darkish stairs to get to the restaurant but these things pale when presented with a plate of perfectly prepared veggies, noodles or rice, tofu, sauces and heavenly herbs in delicious curries and hearty soups. The innovative menu is changed weekly and there are no more than half a dozen main dishes offered at one time. This does not include wonderfully fresh salads which can be a meal in themselves. The imaginative, healthy drinks and desserts are special treats. Service can be slow at peak times but the wait is always worthwhile. Reservations are highly recommended.This restaurant actually had to limit its service to 5 instead of 6 days a week because it had become too busy! If you are at all interested in quality, you will not be disappointed. Having tried many vegetarian restaurants (and those willing to offer veggie fare) for 40 years, we can attest that Anchan is one of the best. Tuesdays to Saturdays. Read less

This gem of a restaurant not only impressed the life out of me but also my meat-eating husband. Unlike many veggie restos, which play it safe with fare like wraps and salads, it tackles big Asian flavours and every dish packed a punch. The first night we went was Valentine's Day, and we had a set menu for 450BHT per head. This included a trio of starters (lightly battered mushrooms, asparagus and salsa and a yummy Asian salad), brown rice with cashews and pineapple for main plus a fab vegan carrot cake and chocolate cake that were moist and flavourful. Oh, and a ginger and mint smoothie on the side. The owner - unfortunately we didn't get his name - was the perfect host, chatty and charming and full of useful advice on Chiang Mai and back stories to the dishes (many of the ingredients come from a local farm. Think it's organic). The next day we went back, the food was just as good: greedily, we ordered massaman curry, a heady, spicy, tom yum soup - which I never get to try as it's always got prawns in it - and a really interesting dish of coconut palm and basil leaves. And some raw chocolate truffles. Anchan is not just a great veggie restaurant but a fab eatery in its own right. I would go back to Chiang Mai just to eat here - and only wish there was something comparable in Hong Kong. If you want to go, get there early (around 6 or 7) as there are lots of regular customers and you will regret not getting the chance to eat here. Read less

This is the best tasting food we ate during our 10 days in Thailand. Actually, I felt it's the best Thai food we have ever had! Everything was so fresh. The restaurant has an open kitchen you can see while seated. The owner is really friendly. He said his kitchen is open because he has nothing to hide. I would recommend to call and make sure it's open if possible. Because when we tired to go back a second time, it was closed during it's "open" hours. They also have some fresh spices/ goods I was happy to purchase and bring back.
I was in Anchan Vegetarian for 3 times with my husband and the only thing We can say is: Everything was Excellent, delicious!

Some of the most flavorful dishes we've experienced so far in Chiang Mai. Very large portions, we usually ordered two dishes for the three of us dining. Fast service. Highly recommended. We shall return again many times if we can.

Love this restaurant. It was the best food I ate during our three weeks in CM. I went multiple times, had three different dishes and loved them all. The banana flower salad is a must try. The portions are huge. One dish is enough for two people. They also have pretty good wine by the glass (wine by the glass in other restaurants is horrible). Make a reservation.
We are both meat person and we loved this place. You can tell how fresh the ingredients are. The mushroom and pumpkin are particularly decilious.

I was here last few years with my boyfriend and each time you go the menu presentation seems to change. first time we went we got a real menu, in mid 2013 it was ONLY written on the wall and now in 2014 you are give a piece of paper to mark on what you want. Owner is a cool guy who speaks 8 languages and is ever so friendly. Menu consists of 15 items which changes each week so its always different, drinks are great even the tea. NO Wi-fi is only downside. Food is tops - all of it Read less
Date_Written: on the wall and now in 2014 you are give a piece of paper to mark on what you want.

Detail: What a find. Upstairs down a soi not far from Doi Suthep. Had lunch which finishes at 1430, we got there at 1350ish, no probs. Green mango smoothie and banana smoothie, with green mango salad and another couple of dishes we can't remember. Food was really fresh, incredibly tasty, and really good value. Enjoy, we did.

Absolutely delicious organic Thai vegetarian and vegan food! Beautifully varied menu with many creative dishes. Friendly service in a 'feel-good' atmosphere. Above average prices, but here you really do get what you pay for. Treat yourself...you'll be happy you did! We love this place.

this is by far the best restaurant i have been to in chiang mai. make sure to reserve a table the day before because it is very small and usually full in the evening. owner speeks perfect english and the staff is very friendly.
Yummy authentic Thai food, nice easy going and relaxed atmosphere. The staff were very nice and the owner was very attentive Make sure you book before hand to avoid disappointment

Really nice fresh, innovative vegetarian food. Family-run place with even the kids chipping in! Very tasty, lovely juices, and great atmosphere. Meat-eaters won't feel like you've missed out - it's very good food.

I have been eating at this outstanding vegetarian restaurant for over a year. I try to go every week. The food is fresh and organic and the menu changes with the season. If it is fresh in the market or even grown at the cook's own home, you will find it featured on the board. Each dish really is cooked with care. I always take my out of town guests here who always leave with a smile. Even my non-veg friends. The atmosphere is bright and airy and fresh. Art on the walls, also a changing display. One never gets bored with the food at Anchan. Even the papaya salad was absolutely outstanding. Yes, I am a fan. Read less

My non vegetarian husband graciously agreed to come here with me. We had banana smoothies, tempura mushroom and red coconut curry and all were very yummy. We shared desserts which was lavender penna cotta and raw chocolate truffles. The desserts were good and worked well together. Good pricing for high quality food (about 90-120 baht per dish). Very fast service too.

I enjoyed the food here very much, I tried to post a review earlier, but it never showed up, so here goes a second attempt. The banana flower salad is simply divine, though you can replace the banana flower with any ingredient, as it is all about the Thai YUM dressing as I was told. I will certainly return here on my next visit. Keep up the good work. Read less

Whether you're vegetarian or not, this place has the most consistent, high quality, and well-made food in town. The kitchen is open for anyone to watch as the head chef is the only one who cooks all the dishes.. and for good reason b/c he's amazing! Their menu is based on whats available locally and in season.. so they will run out of things from time to time. The cool thing is that you never know what specials they'll have day to day due to the seasonality of the ingredients they source. I really loved their Banana Flower Salad along with the Roselle Kombucha.

The food here was very nice - healthy, fresh, but could have been more flavorful (we had the tofu tamarind and stir fry veggie). The banana smoothie, however, was spectacular! Very tasty, I think they make it with coconut milk. The staff were also very friendly and the atmosphere good.

The Anchan Vegetarian restaurant is fantastic but difficult to find.It is on the first floor overlooking a busy soi which is also the way to Hillsides 2 and 3. But everything is very fresh.I had a red curry and brown rice with green tea. It was all delicious-I will be back with friends

Anchan is hands down one of my favorite restaurants in town, and one of the best Thai culinary experiences I've had in general. I've been coming since they first opened, and have loved seeing the cuisine evolve. Their current approach is stellar, with an ever-changing menu dependent on what's fresh and available every day. I'm constantly surprised with what they're cooking up (tempeh burger, anyone?), even though I have my favorites by now (their tom yum is the best I've had, vegetarian or not, in the year and a half I've been in Thailand... it's spicy but tastes like a warm hug feels). Walter and his partner have brought delicacy and innovation to Thai vegetarian food (which is generally too sweet and not that interesting), and while every meal might not be mind-blowing, it's incredible to see a restaurant that's constantly evolving and breaking boundaries. If you're looking for a great meal, definitely go with one of their curries. Read less

I came here to drop off my donation -- until 7 Dec you can donate warm clothes or money for a village in Chiang Rai -- ... and decided to stay for lunch. I'm glad I did. The food was great and plenty of it: my friend and me shared the sweet & sour veggies, tofu w tamarind sauce, and banana flower salad, and brown rice and we couldn't finish it. My passion fruit smoothie had the right amount of sugar (unlike many that are overly sweet). The service was quick and good. And I appreciated the " we don't have wifi, speak to each other" tip on the black board. Will definitely go back and recommend to my friends! Bonne appétit! Read less

We are staying nearby and have visited twice in four days. Dining on the second floor balcony above the quiet soi caught our eye. Everything about this place is wonderful - the food, the service, the vibe. All natural, veggies bought at the market in the morning, cooked perfectly - bright, fresh, colorful, no additives or MSG or anything else weird. For instance, they use a vegan mushroom base instead of MSG, and it is incredibly flavorful. And they make all their own curry pastes on site, because anything that is supplied to restaurants will have shrimp paste in it. The menu is eclectic, smoothies are excellent, beer is cold. Just an excellent little place 50m off the buzzy Nimman Rd. Note they are open for lunch and dinner, with a break in between, and only til 9pm. Read less

Hi Chiang Mai Massive! I live down the road from this little gem of a restaurant! Originally I went because it was close and I was knackered. Thank god for my being lazy! It is brilliant. Initially, though I do not always eat meat as part of a meal I was thinking, "well it will be nice and I can always scoff chicken at dinner if I miss it"… Hmmm. One mouthful of the Penang Curry later I could not have cared less about chicken or any other meat. It is delicious! I have spent a lot of time in Asia and I would say the food I had that first visit (and the five or six visits since) is right up there… One night I even decided to ditch the Thai food. The Lasagne was gorgeous and actually quite light which was perfect. The chef is a genius with balancing flavour and so far I have not had a bad meal - actually I have not had anything but amazing food! Yay Anchan! Service wise I love the staff here. So friendly and helpful! The chef comes out to chat about the food. They have been lovely about substituting vegetables instead of Tofu as I cannot touch the stuff. I can never eat a whole portion and it is never any trouble getting the rest to take away.. The owner is also super friendly. I love it. pooh and PS the smoothies are yoghurt free and LUSH! Read less

I. Living with a compromised immune system for.... like forever... i have learned to navigate though the food subject with a lot of skill and wisdom. I cook most of my meals and even make my own chocolates (raw and sugar free they are) So, now that we have that out of the way and you know what a fussy eater i am, I can say what i want to say. I love Anchan restaurant in CM for many reasons. I love the people who run it, they are honest, creative and fun. I love the taste of food there and i love the great vibe. It is a perfect place to meet a friend, have a little gathering or just pop in for a quiet healthy meal with yourself. But mostly, I trust the integrity of the food in this little magical spot. I know that people who run it, go out of their way to get organics and make the best out of the best... The chef told me that he even makes his own spice that is based on the Himalayan salt. Passion for food, taste, joy and passion for life.. that is what i feel each time i eat there. Not to even mention healthy/yummy juices and sugar free raw chocolates. When it comes to food in my life, it is all about integrity. How many restaurants will serve you food that is made with love and awareness? What i mean is when our food is prepared with intention to make us healthier and better, to serve our cells and palate.... not just to make money out of us..... then we know it has integrity. Anna Suvorova, Life Coach, Writer, Yoga/Meditation teacher, a dreamer of a better world. Read less

My wife and I go there often and are always pleasantly surprised. Most dishes on their menu are exceptional but what I enjoy is the blackboard (it's really green) specials. We have taken people who say they don't like vegetarian and they love it too! No MSG or processed sugars but the flavors just jump onto your tongue. The kitchen is open so you can see it is freshly prepared and the staff is friendly. It is not FAST food and for that I am grateful. Believe me it is worth waiting for.

This restaurant looked a bit rundown and I was apprehensive at first but the food was simply delicious! Attractively presented, fresh ingredients and put together in a simple and wholesome way that was simply delicious.

Pls do not miss this place for a vegetarian meal. I am not a veggie but i feel fantastic there. The food is fresh and delicious for the massaman curry with tofu and banana salad. And you will be impressed by friendly staff. The price is reasonable, good value for a dinner. Highly recommended for changing your taste.

After reading reviews on a blog for vegetarian, I decided to have lunch at Anchan Vegetarian. I was impressed by the abundance of vegetarian food in here. I can't believe the chef can cook such many dishes from vegetables. It is so amazing. Vegetarian food was cooked excellent. The ingredients were fresh and healthy. I was really impressed by the flower salad. This dishes was beautiful and delicious. Smoothies were also good. Reasonable price and nice service. I love everything in here. Highly recommend. If I have a chance to visit Chiangmai again, I will get back here.

The atmosphere is cute and warm. Staff is friendly and professional. All dishes are fresh and pleasing. Massaman curry with tofu is great. You should definitely try vegetable soup with mushroom. Banana salad is good, too. And don't forget yummy special juice with blue flower.An ideal place for green and healthy meals with reasonable price.

I love eating at this restaurant. The smoothies are fresh and delicious. I really like that the food has no MSG. The menu changes with the seasons so the freshest food is always on the plate. Red/Brown rice is served which is the least processed rice. The prices are very reasonable and not expensive. I will be eating this fresh food frequently.

Definitely one of the best vegetarian restaurants in Chiang Mai. Always fresh and delicious. I've eaten there a dozens of times already and although the "Pasta Pesto" and "Yam Hua-pli Banana salad" remain my favorites, I strongly recommend any of their superb dishes.

Tasty and fresh vegetarian food, attractive prices, friendly and quite fast service, the owner is staying inside and very attentive to the guests. Can't say it's the best cafe and even the best vegetarian cafe in CM. Something was great (green curry), something was - nothing special (salads, lasagna). Definitely not for 'special occasion' but fine for lunch with friends. Worth to try for everyone who likes healthy food.

This place has one of the nicest atmospheres of restaurants in the area, a nice place to come and sit on the balcony overlooking the street. Corn cakes, massamam curry, banana leaf salad so yum. My only challenge is not to order too much...

We have eaten here a number of times and always enjoy the food even though we are not vegetarian. The food is organic and they limit the use of sugar and salt which is something unusual for Thai food. There is no MSG or other additives and there is no palm oil in sight, only olive oil. I would give it a five, only the service can be a little sketchy. Food is great.

We are vegetarian, and for the most part Thailand is quite accommodating, but Anchan was a head above the rest. I had the red curry, and it was to die for. I think they had different sized portions (?). I got the larger one and ate every bit of it. It is a little hard to find. Look for a large white, somewhat shady looking, apartment complex on the main road. It is just down the side street. The family who owns the restaurant could not be any nicer. Soi(s) confuse the hell out of me. They also sell a wonderful alternative to Tiger Balm. If you didn't know, Tiger Balm is made with all sorts of petroleum based nastiness. They have a nice menthol based itch reliever that worked wonders for us. WHEN we are back in Chiang Mai, we will be back!! Read less
I had no idea Vegetarian food was so nice, I prefer meat but honestly tbe flavours going on I tbe food served here is wonderful. Will be back.

We were initially going to go to one of the other vegetarian restaurants in town that was highly rated and instead went to Anchan. We are so pleased we did as it was one of the best vegetarian meals I have ever had. The soup (clear vegetable) was phenomenal and the mixed vegetables and mushrooms with tofu were mouth watering good. The staff was very friendly and overall it was a wonderful experience. I would have gone to it again had I been here. NOTE: Google maps and the map for Trip Advisor doesn't have a clue as to where this place is. Go to the 7-11 near it and turn down the street. It is on the second floor of the building, so look up. Most folks around the area will direct you there. It is outside the city walls, so it is a little far from the madding crowd, but worth the drive or walk! Read less

We have been here twice during our stay in Chiang Mai, luckily a friend informed us about the restaurant, this was Great , after having trouble finding tasty vegetarian food in bangkok . This place is a treat, everything they serve is absolutely fresh. They start cutting the veggies after you have ordered. The only downside is , there is so much choice and so little time. The two of us ordered 4 dishes each , turned out a dish was meant for two. But we did our best and ate on and on and on , finishing our food ,...so we skipped breakfast next morning. This was no problem at all. So far these were the best two meals i have had in Thailand. And by the way. I am not a vegetarian.

I have recently been in chiang mai and as always, following the recommendations of tripadvirsor saw this place and feel free to go, well, is what I recommend you now, do not hesitate to go. not easy to find but as another user put it encontraras.nosotros following this took a tuc tuc from the market which is in the center, the restaurant is Scary! as tuc tuc is cheap 70bhs led us. and that is the charm of the place, because you come out of the tourist, the middle is economical but not as much as if you go out and go to sites that have nothing to do with tourism. one thing if you have to keep in mind. to 9 pm closed the kitchen!! we arrived at nine minutes, and almost did not catch, I said I came from Spain and I had soooo much interest to dine there and in the end we attended, the truth is they are a family charm and cook great. you have to try their juice blue flowers, this best. tried several dishes that would not know the names but were not chosen by us and the truth we recommend valyl off and only left about 5 or 6 euros per person including beers, so do not hesitate if you go to chiang mai aim well the following address! eminda nimmanha soi13 road opposite the alley that has a 7-11, there is a Burmese restaurant a few meters will see anchan left hand is on the second floor you go up the stairs the front side is white. luck I hope you enjoy! Find this place on tripadviser! Really tasty. Took pasta pesto(100bt) and banana flowers salad(140bt) - big portion and nice served! No wifi (advise to talk each other!)). Will come back! But the location on tripadviser is wrong! This place is on the soi opposite salad concept restaurant. (At the left of Hillside 3)

This little restaurant was a big surprise. I am not a vegetarian but the food is the best Thai in Chiang Mai, undoubtedly. There are many types of dishes to choose from - curries, stir-fry, fried, salads and some western choices. I had the Sweet Thai Pumpkin with Mixed Vegetables in Red Curry and it was amazing! I had the medium size portion, comes with red rice, which was enough for both my lunch and dinner. I also had a banana shake. What can you do with a banana shake, right? Well, it was the best banana shake I have ever had. They added some coconut milk, palm sugar or honey and who knows what else but it was an impressive banana shake...lol!

Anchan was an amazing restaurant, the food quality is outstanding and very reasonably priced. The Massaman curry and brown rice was the best I have had in Thailand. The staff are very helpful and friendly. A must visit for vegetarians/ non vegetarians when in Chiang Mai.

I have 1 dinner here, it's really tasty, especially the curry eating with brown rice. You should visit here when in Chiang Mai (before 9pm) A little cons: Far from center The view from bacon tables not so good

The dishes were so well cooked, not a soggy vegetable in sight. I tried the massaman curry with tofu and it must have been one of the best curries I tried while in Chiang Mai. The banana leaf salad, as recommended by many others, was not a disappointment either. Not only was the food amazing, the service was equally great - extremely attentive, and apologetic about the smallest amount of waiting time. Also, the atmosphere and decor is so relaxing that you wouldn't be worrying about having to wait around.

Loved this restaurant, really relaxing atmosphere with really lovely staff. The food menu is amazing so much to choose from, I went for the banana blossom salad I can honestly say the best salad I have ever had! Presentation and taste 5 star. Even if you arnt vegetarian this is well worth a trip too.

Anchan is the one of my favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai. Actually, I don't eat vegetable at all but Anchan can help me to eat some vegetables. Mushroom green curry and Pasta Pesto are the best dishes here. If you want to find a good restaurant, I recommend you to go to Anchan. There has a good food, a high quality ingredients, good service and the price is not expensive.

I've been an Anchan regular since its opening and I can say that each time I come they never fail to provide the best vegetarian food in Chiang Mai. I look forward to trying their specials and seeing what new fantastic dishes they have created. Their tofu burger was absolutely delicious and lives up to the 'you won't miss the meat' slogan on their sign. I drag my meat-eating friends here often and they are always impressed by how flavorful and filling the food is without needing meat in the meal. Be sure to try the Corn Fritters, they make a nice replacement for the oh so traditional spring rolls.

We have been to Anchan twice now for lunch during our stay in Chiang Mai and we loved it. The food is beautiful and fresh with great flavours. We are planning on returning for dinner. Thai and English owners, so speak English which is a bonus. There are always blackboard specials which are great and smoothies awesome, definitely returning here, best food we have had in Chiang Mai.

We celebrated my fiance's birthday here with a few other people (about ten). They were very welcoming and everyone found something good to eat, including those omnivores among us. We go back here from time to time and will definitely continue to do so. Thank you Anchan :)

i went to Anchan with a friend,he highly recommanded this restaurant and the food there didn't let me down.and the dish i like the most will be the banana flower salad,ti never have food like that before so kinda new and surprise for me.the place is cozy and i love the painting on the wall,good spot to have a selfy,haha,didn't order much for 2 people,the price is reasonable,staffs are nice,food are delicious,it's the best place to go for vegetarian food.i think.:)

I have been to Anchan many times and generally enjoy the food. I went with a friend again last week and have to say I was as little disappointed with the service & quality. My Massaman Curry came after a 30 minutes but the soup followed 20 minutes later. By then I had finished my main course. I have to say the soup was not worth the wait and was watery and lacking in taste. Despite the disappointment I will still be eating at Anchan as their food is generally good. I do however think there are better vegetarian restaurants in Chiang Mai.

I've visited Anchan a few times now, and have been satisfied with good quality vegetarian dishes at a reasonable price. The place has a cosy, 'family-run' feel, and the staff are very pleasant. Service can be slow at busy times, so make sure you're not in a hurry! The smoothies are delicious, and the food generally tasty, although I have found some of the curries and soups a little too bland for my taste. It's not the best restaurant in Chiang Mai, or even the best vegetarian restaurant, but to be fair they are doing a good job and deserve some success. Hopefully they can continue to improve their dishes and I will definitely return. Read less

This was my favorite meal in Chiang Mai. The eggplant dish and the papaya salad were both lovely, as were the chocolate truffles for dessert. Cheerful atmosphere, elegant without being pretentious. We had a very early dinner and did not have to wait for a table, but when we left the place was full. So if you are planning to eat right at 7, maybe plan for a little wait. It will be worth it.

Rated #1 in Chiang Mai certainly creates a great expectation. Rather go there without any expectations and you’ll be pleased with the food. It’s low-key with friendly service. At times frequented by Americans, they can get a bit loud-mouthed at times, which I do not care for much, but if you go there when it’s quiet, the atmosphere is pleasant. I get the impression they work hard and try their best to keep their standards high.

Anchan Vegetarian is tucked down a side street in a very hip section of Chiang Mai. This was our last night in Thailand, so needless to say we'd had a variety of thai food at this point and this meal was a true highlight. The owner, Walter, was very attentive. We had a big group and instead of trying to order, we put our ordering into his hands, asking him to bring us his favorite dishes. We weren't disappointed. The green curry dish was a stand out favorite for me, as was the tom kha soup. Anchan uses only natural, organic ingredients, which is not the norm throughout thailand, so this meal was definitely a treat. The truffle chocolates for desert are made in house and should not be missed. Extra points for great ambiance and bug spray on hand for anyone in need! Read less

Anchan is one of my favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai. I am not a vegetarian, yet I do not miss the meat as their dishes are fresh, colorful and flavorful. The daily special menu features in-season foods. Their regular menu is extensive and complete. I've never been disappointed with a meal from this place. The staff is charming, friendly and efficient. The location is upstairs with an open air view of the soi below, and the room itself is colorful and warm. After your meal, try the house-made truffles.

We were so thrilled to see a vegetarian restaurant sitting at #1 on Trip Advisor!!! Of course we had to check it out. We were definitely impressed and it is up there with one of the best meals we have ever eaten in a restaurant. Firstly, the staff were helpful and friendly (always important). The menu is well set out and there is a lot to choose from - clearly shows vegan options. We started with the mushroom stew which was amazing. So hearty and tasty (easily could be a meal on its own). Also had the corn fritters as a starter too and they were delicious. For our mains we had a green curry and the stir fry asparagus with mushroom sauce. They all just had the most amazing flavours and were so fresh. I really wish that it was closer to our hotel as we would have eaten there for every if we could. It is definitely worthy of the number 1 spot on TA (as my title says)! Read less
All the starters were overly deep fried - give then a miss and head straight for mains which were all excellent. Good food, nice atmosphere, friendly (but v slow) - not #1 though....!

While not a complete vegetarian I do prefer not to eat meat, my other half however loves meat and does not get very excited about vegetarian food. Until now! The food at Anchan was absolutely delicious. We tried the banana leaf salad, the mixed mushrooms panaeng curry (unbelievably good), corn fritter salads amongst other delicious dishes – all were wonderful. I would almost go so far as to say that the panang curry was the best curry that I had in the 2 weeks that we were there! My boyfriend can attest to the fact that the food was ‘so delicious that he certainly did not miss meat’. I live in Chiang Mai and have been spoiled for years by unbelievably delicious food, but I must say that Anchan is on another level; very few places in town are comparable. This is some of the best food I've ever tasted. That's all that needs to be said. It's ranked #1 on TripAdvidor for a reason. Check it out--you won't be disappointed by the exquisitely fresh tasting food, and the owners are warm and friendly.

We came here based on the trip advisor reviews and were not disappointed. The food was delicious and the portions were very generous. Even my meat-loving husband really enjoyed the meal and suggested that we come back a second time! Our drinks-smoothies and ginger tea-were also very good and a nice addition. This restaurant offers great value, delicious food and friendly service. Highly recommended!

Anchan is a delicious vegetarian option in Chiang Mai. I was brought here by a friend, and was impressed with the food. Certainly, the prices are a bit more expensive than the street vendors. However, the food is high quality and the portion sizes are much bigger than you would get in the street. My entree, the mushroom green curry, was about at least twice the size of the curries at the cheaper restaurants. If you're concerned about price (which is still much cheaper than you'd ever find at home), split an entree with your companion. I am also pleased to support LGBTQ-friendly businesses in Chiang Mai. I will definitely return to Anchan on my next visit to Chiang Mai. Read less

We stopped by here for lunch today following all the tip reviews from here, and we certainly were not disappointed! By husband was a bit uncanny on the idea as he loves his meats of all kinds, but I did convince him. And he is glad we stopped by! For lunch we had the following; Mushroom omelette Green curry Passion fruit smoothie Mango smoothie The curry (served with organic wild rice) was TO DIE FOR. The most amazing vegetarian curry I have ever come across. The eggplant and bean cuts make it nice and hearty. The mushroom omelette my husband had (I sampled) was amazingly well seasoned and tasty enough without meat. He thoroughly enjoyed it. To finish, we tried heir 4 organic raw chocolate truffles. Users of trip advisor are the true heroes, because not get it wrong. This place really has the right to be first in the ranking, although the service is slow and having to wait , every dish is prepared with love seems to be at a friend's House, you just sit and wait for the chef do his work.We ordered two fried rice, one soup, one fried banana flower and two lemon shake. portions have very much to eat, total around 500 Bath.i recomended this place

Anchan Vegetarian is one of my favourite vegetarian restaurants in Chiang Mai. The locally-produced, organic, food is lovingly and expertly prepared by wonderful chef, Khun Aye, and can be ordered to suit one's own taste (not too spicy, a little bit spicy, spicy etc.). Dishes on offer range from Thai to Western, and all are simply delicious. I absolutely recommend the banana flower salad - it's to die for! The atmosphere is vibrant and friendly, and while there is background music playing, unlike a lot of establishments in CM, it's not overly loud, so it's easy to chat with friends. And speaking of friends, Anchan is a great place to go with a group of chums (groups of up to 12 can be catered for), yet equally, a lovely setting for a meal for two. While you are there - do take a look at the wonderful art on display (framed paintings, and murals); they're the work of local artist, Li Li Tan (http://www.lilitan.com/), and really add to the vibrancy of this restaurant. Anchan Vegetarian is open Tuesday to Saturday, 11am to 9pm. Reservations are recommended for evenings. Oh, and do try their anchan juice - it's beyond delicious! And their raw chocolate truffles. **le sigh!** Read less

Anchan Vegetarian provides the best vegetarian food in the world. June was very hot and i went looking for vegetarian food. I checked in Trip advisor and found this place which was excellent for my taste.

Nice place and nice friendly staff, Foods very cool and good taste , Good to see quality Vegan places around and i was very happy here , will return tomorrow ,,I like the feel or the place and the staff are very kind to guest..

I'm a vegetarian from the USA currently living in Chiang Mai. I was so excited to find out about this place - this little gem exceeded my expectations. The curry is my favorite (all of them), and the tofu is the best I've had. Everything is fresh and always delicious. The staff is so friendly, welcoming, polite, and generous. I love this place - you will too!
Very good and clean and fresh food and friendly staff and polite. Only please fix the climatic. Everything else was very well done and served.
Best thai curry ever, ate here last night, service food excellent, all freshly cooked, made to feel very welcome
I am vegetarian for the last 3 years and i totally recommend this restaurant to all vegetarians or not. It is quiet and gentle and the food is very well made plus the staff are wonderful.

I've been in Chiang Mai for over two weeks now and have had a really hard time sticking to my vegan diet. Some of the restaurants have vegetarian options but Anchan is the only one that I have found with vegan options. The food is excellent and very reasonably priced. My veggie fried rice(they use olive oil) was only 90 baht. I'll definitely be back!

If you like healthy, vegetarian, organic and DELICIOUS food, this is the place for you to go. If you just like a place where you can eat for a good price, in a friendly and well decorated environment and with a great service, this is the place too! :) And you even have a plus: an organic shop where you can buy all kinds of tasty and healthy things.

Food here is great! The banana flower salad, masaman curry and a stir fried rice dish with veggies are all delicious. The tofu fried wih mushrooms was only okay. The kitchen closes at 9 so go early! Wine not good... But it never is in Thailand. Homemade raw chocolates weren't the best either- some would love them, but just not my thing!

I've been coming to anchan since they opened. What sets Anchan apart from the rest is their passion for delivering healthy and organic food in a diverse and creative range of options. Don't miss out on their daily, seasonal specials! ;-)

I really love Anchan as they commit to organic when available and quality in everything they offer. Anchan provides a creative menu prefaced on their first page with what ingredients they use and why. They commit to using Himalayan Salt which is the very best salt available (in my opinion). Their vegetables are cooked perfectly remembering not to overcook and then have soggy vegetables. They are attentive to their customers requirements and listen to their suggestions. They are friendly and fun to be with!

Chiang Mai has many vegetarian restaurants but, for me, Anchan is one of the top two. I have eaten here often in the last few months and every dish I have tried has been delicious (the fried banana flower salad is a must-have). I also love the fact that it opens late in the evening and sells alcohol (who said vegetarians had to be teetotal for goodness sake?) - unlike most other vegetarian establishments in Chiang Mai. The staff are very friendly, the decor is cosy, and it has a nice little balcony. It's not quite as cheap as some veggie places in Chiang Mai (a low-cost city) but is still excellent value and an absolute steal when you compare prices to Bangkok or beyond. Soooo worth going to - frequently :) Read less

Anchan Vegetarian is everything you want it to be. The food is fresh, organic and delicious. The service is attentive and friendly. The ambiance is inviting and comfortable. The fried banana flower salad delicious, wonderful curries and the corn fritters a must, best shared to leave room for the other delights coming from the open kitchen.

The atmosphere is very friendly and the cooking smells wafting from the kitchen (which you can see from your table) will get your ordering as soon as you can. I had a passiofruit smoothie which was to die for and in Australia would have cost me $25 in passion fruits then I shared Tempura Veggies and Spring Rolls followed by an Asparagus Stir Fry then not wanting to leave I had raw cacao truffles. Mgarn

Having read several reviews, expectations were high, but it fully delivered! Just a little hard to find but just a few doors up the lane directly opposite Soi 13. Menu is really wide and varied, with choices like stir fried asparagus, tempura vegetables, etc., etc. service was excellent, food quality is first- class with their own preparation there are no worries about use of commercial pastes or sauces with nasties added. Dairy free smoothies were delightful. Prices are great value for such high quality. Setting is really pleasant with open space on first floor. I will be back again this week!

The restaurant is a bit hidden (side street of Nimmanhaemin Road, 7-eleven is on the corner), but worth the effort to find. It is on the first floor by the way. Really good food, amazing chef (you can see him cooking) and even a Belgium chocolate maker (also nice guy). It looks a bit that the whole family is involved. The venue itself is nice and you have a view over a small green park from the balcony. I went there with family, but for couples ask seat at balcony, and it is highly recommendable for everything in my view. Good service by nice people. Phone 083 581 1689.

I am not a vegetarian at all, but the good reviews on here brought me to Anchan. it was delicious and very reasonable. We made a reservation, and ordered so many items on the menu because the prices were good and we wanted a variety. Every single dish was delicious and fresh, and I certainly didn't miss meat because it was so flavorful. The portions were generous. You know it's fresh when you can see the staff cutting up fresh vegetables right before your eyes. This is a great stop whether you are vegetarian or not. I also liked that the tuk tuk ride took us out of the old city and we got to see a little bit more of Chiang Mai than we probably would have otherwise. It is a little difficult to find but it was worth it. There is a "salad" place with a big sign on the corner of the intersection where Anchan is, and Anchan is just across the street a few doors down. It is located on the second floor. It's not fancy, but it is authentic and deserves all the stars it has. Read less

First of all, it is quite difficult to find the place. It is located inside a lane without any prominent signage. Only when you go further inside you will see a small signage. The place is simple and the wall is decorated in colorful colours with some paintings. The feeling you get is this is home cooking style, nothing fancy. We tried the banana leaf salad (which was okay), yellow curry vegetables (this one is good), and also fried tofu with tamarind sauce (this was okay). Overall, we found this place is okay but there are other places which can cook equally tasty. I guess the plus point is that it is vegetarian food and organic. Read less

We took our bikes out to Anchan Vegetarian today based on the reviews we read on tripadvisor. Will you get good food? Yes, definitely. Is it 5-star worthy? I don't think so. I don't really have any complaints, as it was quite tasty and the staff was very kind (as are all Thais, for the most part). I felt like I was eating healthy, but I guess I expected more based on the reviews. I imagine you can find Vegetarian food in the Old City (or outskirts) for around the same price/quality. For 2 dishes (no drinks) it came to 195 baht (US $6.40). Not too shabby. On the way back to the Old City we took the back roads (down Soi 13, which is across from the street the restaurant is on). VERY cool area. We agreed that going to the restaurant just to see that neighborhood was worth it! Read less

Checked this place out today and i think it was as promised , fresh food cooked to order. Service was excellent but not the friendliest of waiters. I had the veggie spring rolls which were very good and I also had the Thai Omelette which was also good. I had coffee, - very nice and I tried three of their limited range of juices and smoothies all very good and my partner had two and we tried each others , all very good My partner had mushrooms in batter and for mains had a mushroom salad all which were also good. Total cost Baht 620 , very good value. Read less
Top quality vegetarian food and great staff. Great smoothies too.I drive across town twice a day just to eat some of the awesomeness

This restaurant is a little hard to find but it is so worth the effort. This was without doubt the best dining experience we had in Chiang Mai. Really fresh veggies used in the cooking and oh so well prepared. I never thought I would love vege tom yam soup, but I did! The corn fritters were divine and so were the veggie tempuras. Beautifully fried and oh so crispy. No gluten and other mushy stuff you find often find in Chinese vegetarian food. This was just plain fresh veggies prepared ingeniously. We didn't find a weak link in the cooking at all and the home made chocolates at the end of the meal were a real treat. The best compliment I can give Anchan is that I didn't miss any seafood or meat. Its a simple setting on the second floor of a shophouse. very informal which is one of the reasons we loved it. Very reasonable for the amount of food we got. I would highly recommend Anchan to anyone looking for a satisfying meal. A word of warning they close early...so get in about 7 and you are fine. They close at 9 pm I believe. Read less

Several of the reviews for Anchan describe it as "lovely." They are absolutely correct. The experience is just that. The tri-colored fried rices are quite flavorful, the banana blossom salad is a must and the raw chocolate truffles are nice and rich. Lovely.

We had a great lunch here. The Thai food was top notch, very fresh, and the green curry was one of the best we've had in Thailand. We are mostly vegetarian, so the veggie menu was a welcome experience. The whole family appears to be a part of the operation. Highly recommended a stop here if you're in the area.

While I'm not sure Anchan Vegetarian deserves the #1 spot of all the restaurants in Chiang Mai, we certainly enjoyed our visit there. Sitting on the balcony and sipping the namesake anchan flower juice, spying on the goings-on in the street below, stands out in my mind as a very enjoyable way to spend an afternoon. It's a few weeks since we visited and I forget what we ate, so it's not really a memorable meal, but I do remember the taste as being both varied and full of flavour and the rice as being exceptionally good. I'd recommend it to anyone who isn't a complete and utter carnivore Read less

Anchan is tucked down a small lane opposite the Salad Concept on Nimmanhaemin. It is a cosy restaurant with some lovely balcony seating. The food and service were very good and i would highly recommend it to tourists and locals alike.

9 women on a girls trip stopped in for lunch and had an amazing meal. We ordered 3 different, made from scratch curries and thet were all excellent. The mushroom-garlic dish was also delicious. Please try the Anchan drink, it was a beautiful purple and so refreshing!

I ate lunch here today with friends. EVERY dish was excellent and we had ordered about 9 different dishes. I loved the anchan juice for a refreshing drink. The stir fried mushrooms were probably my absolute favorite and I don't even usually love fungi. The sauces had us nearly licking our plates and we all left with happy bellies for 180 baht each for food and drinks and we didn't skimp on either. Foodies won't be disappointed and won't miss the lack of carnivorous ingredients.
Too often, vegetarian food is synonymous with bland and boring. But Anchan offers excellent and innovative Thai recipes. Vegetarian food with flavor and zest! What a concept.
It was a bit difficult to find this location. It is a small and simple restaurant. Nothing fancy at all. All the dishes we ordered were excellent. We loved and want to come back again!

I love this place, I have lived in Chiang Mai for 4 years and have tried every veggie resturaunt out there and I can tell you this is the freshest and best , I particularly love the home made curries, this is the only resturaunt that make their own curries with 'no animal product' in it such as shrimp paste and fish oil. They have a loyal customer for sure will be going back again and again ! Keep up thegood work Anchan I know its not easy but your giving out the best ! Read less

Well, Anchan vegetarian restaurant is small but it's not simple. There are many various kinds of menu which can make me surprised with the original Thai taste, but not too spicy. Went here tonight, and tried about 5-6 different dishes. They were good but it wasn't mind blowing, vegetarian or not. We were served by some kids and a very young waitress (family members likely). The food was tasty and colorful, but we didn't think it was anything special. Definitely not #1 of all Chiang Mai. We just don't get why this place is rated so high.

I am not vegetarian, and in fact due to health reasons cannot eat most vegetables in general! I did however go to Anchan with my girlfriend and friend and had an amazing experience! My vegetarian friend had eaten their very regularly after first finding it and required we check it out with him. I had the Anchan Juice, which was FANASTIC (if you are avoiding soda, this is your healthy replacement! Like healthy grape soda!!), and the very nice owner helped me order my plain dish (the server did not speak "well enough English" to understand I came to a vegetarian restaurant wanting only noodles, cheese and garlic, haha). The server was very respectful, just confused by my unique order! Even my simple noodle dish was great, and my girlfriend and friend loved their food as well. (she ordered the vegetable pasta and was almost too full to finish the whole bowl) I highly recommend this place, and based on its rating on TripAdvisor, it is apparent others do, too. The nice thing about seeing a #1 ranked restaurant live up to its status is when the portions are big, the food is tasty, and the service is of a high standard. All three of these requirements are met here! Not to mention, the owner is very genuine and kind, a big plus when visiting a farang (foreigner) owned restaurant. First finding Anchan can be a challenge - here is a hint - you can google "Salad Concept" in Nimman, then look across the main two lane road and you will see another Soi road, it is down there on the left, on the second level of a building! As a meat eater I didn't want to come to this restaurant as I find veggie food boring and bland. Absolutely amazing. If you appricate flavour and enjoy your food it is definitely worth coming. The service was immpecable, restaurant clean and with full view of the kitchen you really can see how fresh the food is. Everything is made to order, and nothing is pre made. We ate here tonight based on the positive Trip Advisor reviews. Within 5 minutes of taking our seats we had a drinks order taken & the manager/owner greet us (very proffessional) the place is light & brightly decodated tables well set out without being too close to each other be advised there is no aircon but its mostly open and not needed in any case. The Restaurant is very clean the bathroom facilities VERY clean (rare in Thailand) look the real hero here is of course the FOOD! The food is amazingly good I can recommend the Papaya salad, Pumpkin curry, Veg tempura ad the raw chocolate truffles are to die for. Another review claimed this place was pricey I disagree there were 4 of us we each had 2 drinks (alcoholic), 5 entrees to share & 4 mains & 2 serves of truffles to share the bill came to around 1900baht thats like $63 aus dollars!!!!! Read less
The place is very clean and the food is good. A litle bit pricy but good stuff and adorable atmosphere. Recommended

The waitresses here are very young. The youngest one could have been about 6 years old. The restaurant was not very busy when I was there, so this afforded the young waitresses lots of time to run around, play, and squeal. It was very charming. There is a really cool balcony instead of a wall on one side, so the whole place is basically open to the outside, which is nice. The food was also extremely delicious. I can't wait to go back for more pumpkin in coconut milk and herbal elixirs.

I eat meat an I have to say I didn't miss it during this lunch. The place is simple but very cosy and well decorated. The food is fresh and very good. We had the fried rice, vegi tempura and the mushroom Tom kha soup... All were very good but the soup was divine! The service was very welcoming... All 5 stars!

From the reviews, I expected an upscale,polished restaurant! What a delight to find such a sweet, down home family place. The owner and wife cook, the kids clear the table and bring the bill, then go back to playing and laughing at the family table. NO TV blaring,god I loved that. The food was delicious, besides being local, organic and conscientiously prepared AND not expensive. It's on the second floor, and not difficult to find-- just follow the owner's directions under "see more" No wonder it's rated so highly. Go, Eat, Enjoy!

We have been several times to Anchan now. The restaurant is fairly minimal and is tucked away on a side street on the second floor. The service id OK and can be a bit slow but the food is excellent. Every dish I have had was full of flavour and well worth waiting for. Highly recommended even if your not a vegetarian like me!

Tried this place last night as it was behind my hotel. As a red-blooded meat-eater, I prepared myself for a light meal. Tried their mushroom tempura spicy salad which was probably the best salad I've ever had. Followed with egg red curry, which was made from homemade curry paste. Ended with lavender honey panna cotta. One of my most memorable meals in a very long time. Come early as it tends to get packed, and the fresh produce aren't unlimited. Will definitely recommend to all friends visiting Chiang Mai. Vegetarian or not!

It was at the fourth time of trying that we finally ate at Anchan. The first, a blistering hot mid day, we could not find the place and could not find a place near to park. On the second attempt we decided to call ahead but were told it was closed for the Songkran festival. Undaunted we called again after Songkran to be told that the electric was off and they could not cook. Finally we called and they were open and they gave us idiot proof directions. It's opposite the Salad Concept on Niman just a little way up the small Soi. I can see why this place gets such rave reviews in T.A. It's a delightful place with its first floor balcony setting, a light, airy room thanks to the fans and a clean ,open kitchen. We were given a complimentary drink on arrival made from local fruit. We shared the sweetcorn fritters to start, served with a sweet chilli dipping sauce and followed with a Pumpkin and Mushroom Curry and Fried Organic Rice with Vegetables. Everything was totally delicious and the service by the owners young daughters ( I'm guessing) faultless and very charming. I wanted to try the Honey Panna Cotta or the Raw Chocolate Truffles but was just too full. Sometimes you wonder if you find a special place whether it's a good idea to share it, but these people deserve all the business they can get and I just hope that we can get a table next time, now we know how to find it. Read less

After travelling around Thailand for a month and eating lots of good food I saw Anchan Vegetarian on Tripadvisor so thought I had to try it. Wow. I'm glad I did! I was greeted by 3 lovely young Thai girls who were on holiday from school. One of them suggested I try the Anchan juice made from butterfly-pea flower. It was purple and sweet and tangy and delicious. As I read my book and waited for my meal which was prepared fresh the room filled with more and more amazing smells and I couldn't wait to try my food! I ate the green pumpkin curry with 3 colour jasmine rice and it was heaven. So many flavours. Garlic, chili, holy basil, coconut... I had a lovely chat with the owner who explained that all the ingredients are organic, free of genetically modified food with no baddies in sight. He was charming and I watched as he spoke to every table. He suggested I try the panacotta for pudding so I did. It was infused with lavender and honey and really good. It's my last night in Thailand tomorrow and I'll be going back to Anchan Thai to spend it there. (To get there: go down the road opposite Soi 13 on Nimmanhemin Road, walk with the food stalls on your left and there's a big sign that says Organic. Go up the stairs and enjoy!) Read less

This might be the best restaurant I have ever been too. The food is fresh and delicious. You should try the anchan juice. The service and atmosphere are really great as well. I can't recommend it enough.

This place is excellent because (1) the owner is super friendly, (2) the ingredients are very fresh (3) the place is an oasis of calm just off the busy activity of Nimmanhaemin (4) extremely inexpensive for a restaurant (but of course more than street food). It is not so easy to find. The location marker on the tripadvisor map is not quite right... On Nimmanhaemin, look for the big Salad Concept restaurant at the corner of Soi 13. Across the road is a simple food stall. You need to keep walking about 30m and you will see the ORGANIC sign for Anchan. The restaurant is on the second level. Read less
Very nice place but hard to find , no air con and very hot but service nice and different with children serving, had to wait long time for our food but was nice and hot when arrived, good taste

Went there a few times in the past week and I absolutely love the place! Food is absolutely wonderful, I can't remember when I last had food good as this. Lovely place too! I'd highly recommend this place!

After a few failed attempts to find it, tonight I can say that Anchan is definitely worth the time looking for! After ordering an Anchan juice, which is a drink of the blue flower with fresh lemon and brown sugar, we had a look at the menu. This time we were tempted by the Pesto pasta and deep fried tofu and I have to say the level of sophistication of the dishes was way beyond what I expected. Before we knew it the pesto pasta was served and it was beautifully balanced; good pesto with a nice amount of garlic, wonderfully cooked mushrooms and topped of with thin slices of parmezan cheese. Just when I was entranced by the pasta and when I thought life couldn't get any better, the deep fried tofu with tamarind sauce was served. Since we were sharing our dishes we both switched our cutlery to the newly arrived plate. The plate had much more on it than what you would expect from a typical Thai restaurant; it had a diverse variety of healthy vegetables sprinkled on top of it, including brown crispy onions. The tofu was crispy on the outside with a nice soft but consistent structure on the inside. I sliced the tofu in half, grabbed some vegetables and dipped it into the sweet sour tamarind sauce. What followed was an intense explosion of flavor in my mouth. We finished this wonderful feast with four different raw chocolates, each playing with flavor and texture in its own way. We washed away the wonderful taste of the pure chocolate with a homemade flower tea, which had sunflower in it straight from the garden of the chef. All in all it was an amazing experience, which I think any foody will enjoy (vegetarian or non-vegetarian). For sure this was the best dinner on my two month journey through Thailand and possible the best dinner of my life! Read less

This place is heaven for a vegetarian. For once, a menu card where I can choose between dozens of dishes, I'm sure they all taste great. All food is grown locally in a sustainable manner and they are creative with unusual combinations and spices. Kudos for Anchan!

Most delicious food ever. We discovered Anchan on day 1 and have come back for all the five days we stayed in Chiang Mai. Each time stuffing our faces! One of us is a true carnivore, but he didn't miss meat a bit and could not get enough of the flavourful and tasty food. We've been in Thailand almost 20 times now and have eaten at many authentic and great places, but this is clearly the icing on the cake. Don't get confused by the complicated descriptions on how to find it. It is very simple. On Nimmanhaeminda Road, Opposite Soi 13, the alley that has a Seven-11 and a big sign 'Burmese Restaurant & Library'. Walk 100m in and there you see Anchan on the left hand side (Level 2). It even has one at the beginning of the alley now as well. Our favourites are: Banana flower salad (out of this world), Green Curry, Panaeng curry with soy protein, Red Curry with Aspargus, Corn fritters and the papaya salad. Also the fried rice dishes are fantastic. We had fruit shakes/smoothies each time, I always had Spirulina added to it. Another win is that the food doesn't just taste like it was made in heaven, it also is very healthy. Staff is just lovely - we already miss all of Anchan! Read less

We came here twice and loved each visit. Got to meet the owner the second time. Looks like a family place. Colorful wall murals, fans, big menus and specials for the day. We ordered smoothies and juices before our food. The jasmine rice was amazing with the curry I ordered and the tofu mushroom dish my fiancé ordered. Plenty to eat, deposit no meat. Tofu was a tad over cooked the second time, but still good. They sell a few things there, all organic products etc. clean restrooms that smell of vanilla. Just off main Nimmenheman road past the starbucks on the right. Head down the alley and its on your left upstairs. Would definetly go back!

We saw this high on TA reveiws, but waited till the end of trip to come here. We are sad because we aren't vegetarians, but have found our best meals are from 3 other veggie places. This one is by far the best! Not to far off main road, a colorful cafe awaits. Nice, quick staff eager to help and please. I had the apple lemon juice and thoroughly enjoyed it. My fiancé had pineapple smoothie and liked it as well! I ordered a mushroom tofu stir fry and he had a curry dish that was amazing! The rice they serve is cooked to perfection, the sauces have so much flavor with the right amount of spice. Clean place, clean restrooms. Plenty of scooter parking! So reasonable for the healthy food you get!

Food is extremely fresh and unlike most of vegetarian restaurants the chef doesnt cook with fish sauce, oyster sauce or chicken stock, he knows his deal. the menu is great and very creative, if you are in Nimmanhaemin road, it definitely worth visiting, you are not going to be disappointed.

Let me start right off by saying - best food i have had in Thailand. Now i can expand upon my praise. As a vegan traveling, so often when you get meatless dishes in thailand i have either A) been concerned if what i am wating is really vegan, or B) been served food by people who clearly are just leaving out meat ingredients from dishes that need the meat for flavor, or C) have had oily and westernized versions of dishes. Anchan is none of those. The restraunt is on he second floor, so look up. We sat at clean and bright tables and weremetby warm greetings from what we assumed was the owners young daughter. The menues were easy to read, explained the origin of each dish anf the ingredients and our mouths immediately started watering... We asked the chef for recommendations and he obliged. We ordered two curries and a salad. I walked over to the kitchen area to look at some of the holistic products veing offered and watched the chef make our food from scratch. Each ingedient fresh, bright, colorful, vibrant... Nothing pre-made. Even the curry, with its scores of ingredients was made right there on the spot. In the end after one of the most memorable meals of my life... which i declared thatsfter each bite of the mixed herb curry... The billfor my wife and i (including drinks) was right around 400bh. I would give Anchan six stars if i could... Read less

As my titled stares it is the location of this restaurant that is its' outstanding feature relative to other restaurants in the area. I've been dining there for several weeks now. At the beginning the service was a bit shaky because the waiter taking my owner understood little English. Recently, however, I feel the service has improved. A friendlier staff would be welcomed. As for the food, it is good fare of organic fresh food with a bit of imagination in the preparation . For couples or singles the three set of double seats along the window facing a big empty park are the best place to sit. As it is situated on a dead end street there is no through traffic but only cars parking to visit other nearby restaurants as the street is filled with them. Anchan is the last one on the block on the second floor. The best way to find this restaurant is to find ,"Salad Concept" and look at the soi across the street. That is where Anchan is located. My only reservations is that smoking is allowed at the moment in these seats. I can never understand the brain of people desiring to smoke in an organic vegetarian restaurant. I chatted to a person connected to the restaurant and they are giving thoughts about continuing this practice or not. I think one has a better chance of avoiding this potential issue if one goes off the busier hours. Anchan is definitely a great addition to the eating venues in Chiang Mai. Read less

...this restaurant could number one anywhere!! We ate at Anchan because of its great reviews on TA and expected it to be good. We were amazed and this was among the best meals we've ever had. Service was great and we got suggestions on what to order from one the owners. The banana flower salad was "out of this world".. presented beautifully & the flavours were amazing. Our main courses (coconut palm heart green curry & asparagus mushroom stir fry) were both perfect. On their sign they claim "vegetarian food so delicious you won't miss the meat". This is definitely true. We aren't vegetarian but this inspires us to become one. We do a lot of travelling throughout the world and this is now our favourite restaurant! And the value is incredible. Please open a location in Switzerland! They've been open only 5 months & are already number 1 in Chiang Mai. Very worthy of their top spot!! Read less

I eat here 5-7 times per week, no joke. Sometimes 3 times in a day. I have been here about a year, this is the best restaurant in Thailand. Lived in Bangkok, Chiang Mai, and down south... Come here or waste your time somewhere else.

I have dined there 3 times this month, and every time I have found the service to be excellent, the food very tasty. They have got it 100% right. The menu has plenty to choose from, The premises are very very clean, and they bring your entree out first, with ordered drinks coming almost immediately. The waiters are very friendly and engaging, and you can see and taste that the food is made with love. Has to be my favorite restaurant of all time. A real find. Parking a bit of a problem, so we park in the next lane, and stroll around the corner.

The restaurant is located on the second floor of small building in Hill Side Condo alley. Food was great but nothing special nor creative. You would find a better in Chiangmai at the same or even cheper. Tofu with tamarine sauce was too sour for my taste. Although, red steam rice is really good.
