A most pleasant,clean and efficient place to eat with great choices. Never disappointed & always leave well satisfied. Down soi with little market on corner with Burmese restaurant sign. Closed Mon.

This place is a new find for me. The food is really fresh and delicious and not too pricey. A typical lunch between 100 and 200 baht for most (dishes start around 60 I think?). Well worth it! I love their anchan juice! It is a bit hard to find though- turn right next to the Burmese restaurant and library (across from Nimman soi 13) and look for it on the left about 100 yards in. They have a small sign that is usually out announcing specials, etc. It's up a small flight of stairs.

We tried this place out, it is small and a little hard to find but once you have you will love it. The pumpkin curry was divine. Love it that you can get spirulina added to your fruit shake and you can buy it there as well as they have a small store! It was a shame they had run out of the raw vegan chocolate truffles on our visit but we will be going back to try them! The place is quaint and cosy and to top it off the staff provide a very friendly and polite service. You must try this place out.

A bite hard to find but worth the hassle. The address is strange, but in Chiang Mai aren't they all. It is on the second floor down what looks like a alley way with a 7/11 on your right and an open air restaurant on your left. Go down about 100 yards an your left. We stayed in Chiang Mai 10 days and didn't find it until day 7. The food is ALL organic and ALL veggi. The owner is from Belgium and is friendly and a good host. The food is prepared almost in front of you one table at a time so you know it is fresh and not pre cooked. I can't say enough about how wonderful the food was. I wish I was still there to enjoy it.

Now this restaurant has it all: delightfully tasting foods, fresh, organic, locally grown and fair prices. What more could you hope for? We were very impressed by the detailed explanations of the different exotic ingredients that make for this unique culinary experience. Walter, the owner, has items on his menu that you won't find anywhere else. As a Belgian he knows a thing or two about chocolate, so if you are lucky and come a little later in the day you might be able to get some of his famous truffles but be careful, they are highly addictive!

If you like fresh and don't mind a changing menu then this is the place for you. Every dish is individually prepared by the cheerful happy owner, the food is fresh, the flavor is great, and the price is very reasonable. What more can you ask of any restaurant?

I am not a vegetarian but this was the best food I had in Chiang Mai! The food is cooked on the spot, with fresh ingredients. You can see the kitchen from the dining room, which leads to a cute little balcony. I never had such a flavorful green curry! The thai eggplant cooked in miso sauce was delicious. The raw chocolate truffles were a beautiful finish to the meal. The smoked oolong tea reminded me of the Chinese Pu’er tea. The owner blends his own teas and they are delicious! you could tell that the owner really cares for his food. He has high standard of quality and it shows.

Ate here several times on a short visit to Chiang Mai. Absolutely wonderful and creative food choices - unique takes on western and thai food. Everything on the menu was a giant win for us! Prices are also quite reasonable. Only wish that we had saved room for dessert one of the times. Vegetarian husband rates this place as one of the top two vegetarian restaurants he has ever been to in his life. When getting here, make sure to go to the second 'organic vegetable' place along the road and walk up the stairs - that is Anchan Vegetarian. Small balcony with set of three two person tables if you'd like to eat outside. Read less
We eat there at least once a week. Food is fresh, tasty, inventive and hey smoothies are to die for. What's not to like?? :))

As a vegan I enjoyed great curries and salads at Anchan. It inspires me that the menu card encourages one to ask for low sugar in the dishes as well as the fact that it's MSG free and ingredients organically grown. Went to Anchan more than five times now.

I'm new in vegetarianism and didn't eat so much in vegetarian places before. But I've tried probably most of them in Chiang Mai. And I have to say that Anchan Vegetarian is a really good place in all aspects - delicious food, friendly staff, excellent chef, nice ambience and the prices are reasonable. And the specials are superb. You can also buy many vegetarian and organic goods in their shop. And also they have a very advanced facebook page, which allows you to know all the news about the menu and products. And it's open till the evening!

One of my favorite vegetarian places to hit up when I'm in Chiang Mai, eat here at least once a week. The owner is super friendly and goes out of his way to welcome you to his restaurant and make you feel like friend sitting down at his dinner table. The food is fresh and flavorful, with unique takes on many traditional Thai and Asian dishes. As for the evenings, grab a glass of red wine from his carefully selected wine offering and some raw chocolates. You will not be disappointed.

I'm not a vegetarian, but I enjoy good vegetarian food. Anchan is a new vegetarian restaurant in Chiang Mai specialising in local organic produce. While it has a fine regular menu, which it devised with its customers in the first few weeks after opening, it also prepares specialty dishes when something unusual is available in the fresh produce market. Even the rice is delicious! It is a small intimate restaurant with an extremely friendly atmosphere. Aye, the maître d', is incredibly dedicated to his craft and to your meal there being enjoyable: he fusses over everything like a mother hen!!! I like to go there whenever I'm in Chiang Mai and especially to bring new people to experience it. By Thai standards it's not cheap; by its own standards you couldn't expect it to be; but then again it's far from expensive. This new business deserves to go from strength to strength. Read less

Some places are worth giving a great review and Anchan Vegetarian is one of those places. Where can you get organ, vegetarian Chiang Mai that has an unbelievable price. Anchan Vegetarian. We have eaten there several times since their opening 3 months ago and will definately return.

The newest addition to Chiang Mai's vegetarian scene is simply lovely! Wonderful food, wonderful atmosphere and a wonderful host (Chef Aey who works in his open kitchen). Everything is home-made and pesticide free which adds to the appeal. The banana flower salad is delicious, as well as the corn fritters, raw chocolates and signature Anchan juice!

My fav dishes here are the very tasty banana flower salad and the wonderful (and healthy) raw chocolates! Oh and the pumpkin pie when it is available. Cosy atmosphere, friendly chef and staff, lovely balcony to sit and relax on.

As a regular around the Nimman area, this is one of my favourite hideaways. But with such quality of food and service, it won't be a hideaway much longer. Situated opposite soi 13, down that dead end soi, look for the purple umbrellas. Excellent price/quality value, they have the best veggie dishes in town!!! Fresh every day, organic and chemical free produce make for tasty dishes filled with quality ingredients such as mushrooms, different kinds of beans and pulses, cabbages, herbs and spices. Recommended dishes: stir fried long eggplant with miso, stir fried egg tofu and curry powder, pasta pesto, all curries and my personal favorite banana flower salad. Give them a try, call the lovely owner Aye: 083 581 1689. Read less

I had lunch at Anchan a few days ago - my 4th meal there - and walked away very impressed. The Pasta Pasto with Mushrooms & Parmagiana I had was excellent, and the roselle drink a good, healthy, light and flavoursome accompaniment. The little touches like Himalayan salt, home made curry pastes and daily produce from the local organic markets set Anchan apart in terms of quality. And the fact that you can see into their immaculate open kitchen. Good prices, nice atmosphere and parking in the empty lot next door, which is pretty important in the busy Nimmanheiman area.

Friends took me to Anchan a couple of weeks ago and I returned with another friend last evening. Excellent food. Interesting preparation. High quality ingredients, which is not always the case for vegetarian food in Chiang Mai. Very reasonable prices are surprising for the quality of the food. Excellent service too. And the space is comfortable, but nice enough that you feel you're going to a special place. The owner is extremely friendly, even when busy cooking. All in all, I'll go back and would highly recommend this restaurant.

I eat at Anchan regularly, I am a neighbour. Very, very tasty food, always fresh. Always served quickly, prepared and served with great care. Lovely atmosphere! What I also adore: you get a normally-sized REAL napkin made of REAL fabric. And they have Bia Lao!

It's sometimes hard to get real organic vegetarian food in Chiang mai. Khun Aye at Anchan makes fantastic fresh vegetarian dishes. I liked the choice of music as well. There was great attention to detail and the combination of flavors were superb.I really like the simple clean decor.

Anchan Vegetarian Restaurant is new since Oct'12. We visited Nov22. Attention to detail is obvious at the outset. Owner operated boutique organic vegetarian. Spotless food preparation is in sight. The owners were happy to educate us regarding the organic food and it's origins. The decor is bright, spacious and full of colour! The value is amazing considering the reasonable prices. There are also organic food products, soaps etc for sale. Love it!

Overal an average experience. the asparagus salad was priced at 100 baht and was lacking a little umph. for that price would have liked to see a bigger portion, more ingredients or a lower price. the curries were good, but again 80 baht for a portion, not including rice. there are many other good organic vegetarian places that do curries for 60 or even 50 baht including organic brown rice. would go here again if someone else wanted to go, but if it was my choice id op for somewhere else.

Bell and I decided to try a new "vegetarian" restaurant for dinner last week. Neither one of us had eaten vegetarian before and we enjoyed a great meal! Food very fresh and flavorful, great atmosphere with small outside deck to eat on or just enjoy a glass of wine,,, Restaurant is located on small quiet soi. 3 dishes and 3 glasses of wine totaled just over 500 baht (approx. $15.00) "Aye", owner and chef, and his staff did a really great job making our first visit an enjoyable one!

Anchan hasn't been open very long so the chef is still finding his strengths and trying new ideas out on the menu of the day. I had his stuffed sweet bell peppers and they were fantastic. I also really like their raw chocolates, guilt free chocolate eating. The restaurant is lovely with a fantastic mural on the wall and delicious food to eat in a friendly and pleasant atmosphere. I will go back and I am not even a vegetarian.

What a surprise. A quiet elegant organic vegetarian restaurant in noisy crowded Nimmanhaemin. We went to Anchan Vegetarian for lunch and had an extraordinary meal. We ordered the mushroom soup, stirred mushrooms (we like mushrooms) and the brown rice salad. All of the dishes were well-prepared and flavorful but the Brown Rice Salad simply blew our taste buds away. It is a dish of brown rice with dried fruits and nuts and veggies and must be tried. The staff was warm and friendly and knew the menu well. The atmosphere is elegant with cloth napkins. Bright and warm interior with a balcony overlooking trees. The staff was top notch. The food changes based upon availability. It is fresh. It is organic. It is delicious. What more could you ask for? Read less

They are centrally located within the Nimman neighborhood. I was in the mood for a good burger. They did not disappoint. I had the bacon double cheeseburger and fries. My wife had the Marguerita pizza, tater tots, and jalapeno poppers. I loved the burger. It was one the best I've had in a while. My wife loved the tots and thought the pizza and poppers were ok. But again, the burger was amazing.

I ordered the Jalapeno Chimichurri burger and sweet potato fries. The burger was the best burger (out of two) I have had in Chiang Mai. The fries were delicious, I highly recommend the upgrade. Our table also ordered the Spicy Nduja which was good but the burger is what this place does best.
I've had the juicy Lucy, bacon cheeseburger and sweet potato fries - all very good. This place is legit! I'll likely return before I leave Chiang Mai in a few weeks. Recommend!

Beast Burger has great food and even better customer service. I did not have any Thai Baht on me since this was my first time to Chiang Mai and I thought U.S. dollars would be easy to pull from the ATM. The service employees ordered my burger, fries, and drinks through the Grab APP (extra delivery fee), which allowed me to order the food and drinks and eat in the restaurant. Beast Burger has excellent food and service!

A great place to have lunch or dinner, clean, organized and fresh environment, friendly, polite and helpful staff. The burgers are excellent, too bad they don't sell beer. Delicious sandwiches/burgers. Highly recommended!!!

Based on recommondation from a travel blog and Tripadvisor we went to Beast Burger hungry for something different after 10 days of great Thai food and we did not regret at all! I had the classic burger with cheese and bacon and it was simply perfect im all its ingredients: meat perfectly cooked, a nice bun, good dressing (not too much and too strong to kill everything else but simply good), crispy bacon and tasty cheese. I still think about it … 😋

Tasty burgers and quick friendly service. Tater tots were better than the chips. I had the spicy honey chicken burger and it was really good. Other half had the chimmichurri beef burger which was good but the chicken one definitely topped it. Take away/fast food environment, so no alcohol served here.
Freshly created burgers. Lots of flavours. Plenty of spices, the sweet potato fries are done just right. Would recommend for those in need of a quick fix of western food.
Great burger place with a short and easy menu (with great options), quick service, and great location. Limited parking, but that's par for the area.

Must admit the burgers are very good, juicy meat & the perfect portion for us. I found the chimichurri burger rather overpowering in taste, maybe a little less of the sauce would be better, the bacon burger was great! Recommended.
My burger, fries, and milkshake came in just under 500 baht (US$15), But boy was it tasty. I liked the rooftop seating.
Brilliant burgers and a large variety. If you enjoy a succulent, tasty burger then this is your place . There are also shakes and some sides in the menu

Went to Beast burger today asked for my usual Beast burger no bacon no onion. Lady behind the counter says no have it is now cheese burger that cost 200 baht compared to the Beast burger @ 190 baht. Amazing Thailand.

I have been going to Beast Burger for years and always great but today after not been for a while I had The Beast which was excellent but some Bright Spark changed the chips, why? If everything works great why change. Dumb founded
This is definitely the best burger I have had in Northern Thailand. The staff is very friendly and the place is very clean. Would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a great burger.

Just absolutely great burger. The hardest part was deciding which I would order. I went with the traditional and standard fries and glad I’m did. High quality bacon which is rare in these parts and the fries were fresh, handcut and hot. A bit disappointed I waited till the end of my trip to try it but probably for the best as I would have surely put on a view kilos.
NIce hamburger joint with friendly service and a nice selection of burgers. Fries were great and they have a nice rooftop set up.
The burgers are big and delicious. The sweet potato fries are great. I ordered take out ten times and was never disappointed in the quality. Highly recommend.
Stale dry bun,small skinny pattie,soggy yucky onions! Don't know how it's has good reviews!Chips were good....that's it.left still hungry.

As we were walking the streets, we stumbled upon this burger joint. We did a quick review check and decided to give it a try. After the first bite, we both looked at each other and said, "holy cow, this is amazeballs!" It is easily one of the top five burgers we have eaten. We highly recommend this to anyone visiting the area.

I have eaten here 4 time’s in the last month. It is the best burger joint in CM and comparable to any great place you may eat internationally (possibly better) I’m talking LA, NY, London ! A concise yet well thought out menu, each burger has well balanced and well executed flavours. I went off piste with the chicken thigh sandwich today also excellent with just a touch of spice. The curly fries and cheese sauce is also great. The staff are friendly and helpful and the wait for food is always short. Even the sauces and salt shakers are well kept dispensing condiments efficiently onto the well presented metal trays neatly lined with paper (details are important) A restaurant that knows what it does well and sticks to it! Bonus note - fries are free with all burgers! Just go! Read less

The staff speaks english which is a plus. The burgers are good, and the portions are good enough to satisfy an average person. The location is nice, but it is closed on Sunday which is a shame because most places in Nimman are. The burgers are good but a little too greasy for my taste and there are places in town that are better. I would however still recommend if you want a good size burger in Nimman. Read less
Tried their Burger with Mac and cheese bun. The deepfried Mac and cheese was nice to a good and juicy Burger. Can highly recommend this bygger.

Friends who have been living here for a while recommended me that spot saying it was one of the best burger in town, and damn, they were right ! Super juicy burger, fast service and cool spot. It became one of my must-eat spot at least twice a month!
Best burger I have ever eaten. The fries were also amazing. I would recommend getting their homemade cheese sauce to dip your fries into

We visited this restaurant for a very quick lunch before an afternoon of busy site seeing. Excellent value. The chicken burger is delicious and the sweet potato fries were crisp. Somewhere that 99% of UK take aways could learn from.

Luckily I found this place. Walking around Nimman road finding sth to fill myself. I order double Beast burger. Wow it's really good. The meat is medium well (their signature style) and so juicy with caramelize onion. Totally live it.
Really good cheeseburger, but a bit salty after staying away from fast food for a few weeks. Fries were also good, but not too plentiful

Great burgers and fries! Yummy...until 15 minutes after eating I got a major msg hit. I’m sensitive to it and needed to walk and get bottled water to stay awake. The headache is always a dead give away. So if you are not allergic to msg, this is the best juicy enhanced flavor you’ll enjoy in Chang Mai. Eat at your own risk.
Good service and juicy burger. No so many French fries but still very filling burger. Great location and well worth the trip to eat thete

It is a clean and tasty place to get the best burgers in Chiang Mai now. Since returning to Chiang Mai it has taken the burgers here to a new level. Be it Beef or Pork Burgers. It offers inside setting as well as rooftop seating.

This place has everything you want in a beef burger. Has that grilled flavor with just the right amount of grease. Comes with cheese and or bacon. And up to 4 beef patties. Definitely one of my favourites in Chiang Mai.
One of my favorite places for a burger! and for hangout with friends, the place easy to find out and serve with good service and great atmosphere.
One of my favourite burger place. Paddies are cooked perfectly with quality pickles and condiments. The double double is a must, the mushroom one is good as well.

I have eaten several burgers in Chiangmai, but this is one of my favorites. The burger is juicy and flavorful, not dry like some places. The Beast sauce is also very yummy! A wonderful thing about this place is there are many choices for burgers....you can do chicken, pork, beef, etc. Their sweet potato fries are also soooooo yummy! And their regular fries dipped in their ranch dressing is also a favorite of mine.
Living in Chiang Mai city and feeling lazy why not order the best burger in town and Grab Food will deliver it "Hot". No excuses do it now, great service from the Beast & Grab.
Been told several times that I must visit. I ordered Double Double with the famous Cheese dipping sauce. The most juicy burger I have ever had. Highly recommended.

You know how it is on holiday, local food, local food and more local food trying to immerse yourself into the culture of the country then, wham "I must have fries" and so we found ourselves in Beast burger, not the best burger I have ever had but nowhere near the worst. So when your "wham" moment comes you could do a lot worse.
Another hyped up burger place that is overpriced and just so so. It is a nice location and the patio is nice, the name is cool. The burgers though are underwhelming
Moist, seasoned, fantastic burger. I love the toppings on the "Original" (perfectly cooked bacon, onion, lettuce, tomaro). My family also enjoyed the burger with mushrooms. Delicious fries!

Previous visit ate the veggie burger which frankly was average this time just fried ...the fries are very good however Friends ate a meat burger which they said was ok ..staff a little bored no smiling ...in a peculiar glass greenhouse ... very expensive for Thailand ..limited drinks on offer ..I have no idea why this would be rated 4 star
Real Quality in every detail. The Buns are made with tasteful bread. I had the beastburger beef with water for 205 THB

I live in Chiangmai and usually have Thai, Japanese or Italian food, but on occasion really in the mood for a good burger. I've been to other places in town, Dukes, Rock me Burger, and decided to give this place a try due to good reviews. I found the burgers to be pretty good, a bit smaller than I expected but not bad. The fries were a little soggy. The burgers that we had were good, nothing special. The service was average, but pretty standard for ChiangMai. Personally I prefer Rock Me Burger on a nearby Soi, but burgers tend to be a matter of personal choice, everyone has their favorite. Overall it was a good option for a burger. Read less

I had the blue cheese burger and couldn’t fault it in anyway. The source had just the right amount of blue cheese to be flavourful and not to much to be overpowering. The drink selection is very basic. Plastic bottles of water and cans of soft drinks, that you take from the fridge yourself. Everything is self service like a McDonald’s and for that it can’t get more than 4 starts in my opinion. But still recommendable for good food at reasonable prices.

We have been traveling for well over 3 weeks. The kids and I had a craving for a good burger after a steady diet of asian fare. We did our research and this restaurant did not disappoint. Ordered the double double and a Bugogi burger (200-250thb). Both were juicy and excellent. There is also an 100thb option for a kids burger. Fries that came along with the meal were prefectly seasoned (not over salted). We chose to dine in the air conditioned lower section, but roof top al fresco dining available. Self serve, opens at 5pm for dinner. Is this the best burger that I have ever had? Nope. But it is a solid option to scratch an itch (especially in Chiang Mai). Definitely recommend and revisit.
Easily the best burger I’ve ever had. I had the mushroom one, oh boy it was delicious. Cooked to your liking, with add ons such as sweet potato fries, this place is worth a visit.
Was looking for a good burger and so glad we found this gem of a place. What a burger!!! One of the best I've ever had! Thank you beast burger

This place was an unrealll find!!!! Literally found it on Instagram and knew we had to go! It was unbelievable!!! The sweet potatoe fries with a side of cheese were to die for!! & as for the burgers omggg!! Amaze!!! Must try! And the service was so quick! Lovely staff!!

My wife and I stopped here for lunch today. All they serve is monstrous sized hamburgers with French fries. They serve various soft drinks and cold beer. There is parking next door for 50 baht - this is important to know in Chiang Mai as we saw two cars across the street that were ticketed and “booted”. I bet that cost more than 50 baht. The food is freshly prepared and excellent and, at a very low price point. It is well worth a visit. Read less
Served with love 💗 .. Restraunt is nicely located with sufficient seating . Clean but NO wifi so you can enjoy your burger in peace ✌️

I very rarely order a burger and never eat fasat food, when I do I usually go somewhere with good reviews. I really dont know what the fuss is about this place. Its fast food restaurant which is more expensive and they dont have everything on the menu. The burger really was average which was a shame. Chips were more like crinkle cut frozen fries as they were tastless as well. They had run out of onion rings and the waffle potatoes. Everyone in there was American so maybe that is what they are used to in the US but I would recommend it to anyone which is a shame as their marketing is good, cant believe everything you see in phtotos! Read less
Our family sampled three of the burgers on offer and loved them all. They were very juicy and required a stack of napkins to finish them off.
Amazing taste I had mushroom burger served with French fries an orgasm in the mouth. Pleasure for my palate. Definitely come back again

Went here for the vegan burger which I'd seem people talking about, but was a little disappointed when I was told that the bread buns contain butter. Instead I ordered it with salad and was still really happy! It was really tasty, just a shame it's a little pricey and not very filling.

Ate here twice on my recent trip to CM; really enjoyed on both occasions, speedy service and very high quality product in an excellent location. The team are friendly and efficient; may be a little more expensive than other options in town but for me it was a fair price for a superb product,.

I don't $6 to $10 for a burger if it's amazing flavour. But all the burgers that came out for the 8 people we were with were surprisingly small. And you had to pay extra for fries! I chose fries with cheese and it was flooded with cheese that wasn't tasty. Most burgers that I've had between $3 to $5 in Thailand and Cambodia off the street are bigger and better than these burgers. "Baby Burger" would be a more appropriate name for the company. Read less

We ordered burgers with their classy fries (well what else). I had the veggie burger which was great. Crunchy bun filled with a almost creamy patty, accompanied by a layer of crispy seaweed. Beast burger was also great! As well as the fries... next time we will need to enter their roof terrace. good value for money.

This was my first time at Beast Burger after hearing so many good stories. The Double Double burger was good, very good and maybe trying it once again could upgrade my review. With the cheesy fries option, it was a good size. The cheesy sauce isn't from cheese though. My friend enjoyed the Jalapeno burger.
They offer beef (imported from Australia) or pork for meat patties. The owner is a friend of a friend but never met the local genius. Hands down best experience in a big city.

Would be 5star, but sometimes they just undercook the meat so much and or it is "oily", not sure maybe they mix up my order or something. And give me pork instead of beef. I go like 2-3 times a week and maybe once a month there is something wrong, not sure why.
wow food not good at all food burnt or otherwise nondescript. we dident eat half of it and they dident care at all. anyway you cant do worse in chaing mai.
Best Thai local relaxed fine dining. Food is excellent, service as well. Black and his team took good care of us. This is a gem in Chiang Mai

We went based on the reviews for this restaurant but our experience was terrible. We arrived last night after a booking from our hotel and the server just made us stand around for half an hour checking on his phone without even offering us a seat. We then walked out and not even a sorry. Would not recommend this place

We had the Regional Thai Essence menu consisting of 13 courses (no joke). Every dish incorporated produce and influence from some part of Thailand. It concluded with 3 courses of dessert. The courses were small, but there were some which had rice in them and were therefore quite filling. I would have preferred the meal to be satisfying rather than over-sated, too much of a good thing. The restaurant is able to accommodate diners who have certain dislikes, e.g. replacing pork or beef with another meat. Service was good and friendly. Every course was explained to diners.

We have been to many michellin star restaurants in the UK. This was the best ever. The quality of food was outstanding The knowledge of the owner/chef was amazing. We had the wine pairing with the menu and it was 2nd to none. Every course was fresh, vibrant, exciting, tasty and different He took the time to speak with every table, on every course There are only 6 tables, but he has got everything right. We celebrated our 40th wedding anniversary here tonight, we were given a cocktail, a card and a rose for me, such a special touch. Thank you to everyone at the restaurant for making our special anniversary amazing ❤️ Read less
Outstanding. Service was stellar. Wine pairing was well curated. The food was exceptional. Creative, unusual but perfectly balanced and flavorful. We had the tasting menu. Highly recommend.

What a fabulous experience. Each of the many courses was explained- both the ingredients and the meaning and significance to the menu theme. As expected the food was also stunning visually and taste and balance of each dish was fantastic. We were celebrating our wedding anniversary and had booked ages ago and one of the owners of Blackitch (so sorry I don’t know your name but the wonderful lady with great taste in wine and a lovely smile ) kindly gave us some wonderful complimentary wine which was superb so thank you for that! Such a small restaurant but for anyone foodie who is looking for a special place book early and enjoy Read less

We visited Blackitch Artisan Kitchen just after the team returned from their holiday break, and they’d launched a brand-new tasting menu—perfect timing on our part. The place is not large but it’s right in the middle of the Nimman area, so it’s got that mix of hidden gem feel and city vibes. The new menu, Regional Thai Essence, is built around flavors from different parts of Thailand. It was interesting to see how each course represented a different region—from the South’s grilled pineapple curry to the North’s thick herbal soup and fermented soybean notes. Some dishes were more familiar in flavor, like the stuffed chicken wing with Sam-Klor herbs, while others were quite bold, like the smoked sheatfish with fermented rice and galangal broth. The progression felt thoughtful without being too heavy. The desserts were a nice finish. We especially liked the pineapple ice cream with tapioca coconut cake—it was simple, clean, and not overly sweet. The palm sugar mille-feuille was also good, though a bit more on the rich side. One thing that really added to the experience was the service. The manager speaks fluent English and clearly knows his stuff, including wine. He apparently seems to work in fine dining before. His explanations were helpful without being too formal, and it made the whole meal feel more approachable. Overall, Blackitch is a solid pick if you’re looking for something different in Chiang Mai. The attention to detail in both food and service makes it worth checking out—especially if you’re looking for Michelin Selected spots :-) Read less

Okay, it has a Bib, compared to other Thai fine dining restaurants (Le Du, Nahm, 80/20, Coda) I can’t find any difference The service was warm, very friendly and competent, rewind pairing exceptional and a food……. the food waste essence of Thailand: spicy, intense, balsamic, earthy - simply wonderful If you like Thai food, this is a place to be. And if you are in Chiang Mai this is the restaurant to go to.
Stylish little restaurant with amazing food. I’m vegetarian and the chef provided a fully vegetarian tasting menu, it was really inventive, sublime tastes and so much food!

Very well. 10 course menu. Local small and 2 x per night a “shift”. We had the 2nd shift and still had to wait 20 minutes. Since the room is very small we heard the girl explain the same thing every time and since I didn’t immediately understand everything in her English I could listen to the “neighbors”. The dishes are very fine and full of satisfying flavors. The wine pairing was ok. For those who want to take another step into the “world” there are a lot of bars full of young people in the street.

The biggest danger with dining here is that you don’t know when - if indeed ever - you will have such a good meal again. The venue is beautiful, the service is perfect, and the food is absolutely mind blowing. My partner wasn’t going to drink that night, and had a delicious alcohol free drink full with grown up complexity. However, when my wine pairing started to arrive he ended up sharing with me, so astutely were the tastes chosen to make the food sing. We both agreed that we have never eaten such good beef, ever. If you are in Chiang Mai looking for a special meal you absolutely must put this tasting menu on your itinerary. Plan ahead though, as you may need to book several months in advance. Read less

This is the place to go in Chiang Mai for an exiting (northern) Thai kitchen food experience! Very small place with friendly and knowledgeable service. The approach was spicy on and off, but never excessively so and always with lots of varying flavors! The concept is a 12 course menu with an optional wine pairing. The wine paring offered good wines with the flexibility of choosing the glas (-es) you prefer. Among the highlights were the initial Burmese tea leaf salad with raw fish, a great appetizer, the chawamushi layered fish with crab and a spicy sauce, the prawn and tomato relish with crunchy chicken skin and the noodle curry with smoked pork. A Western touch was the grilled duck. Read less

The menu consists of 11 courses and are all pictures. The presentation of the dishes is what you can expect from a very good restaurant. They are fresh local dishes. There is only room for 14 people. Hopefully this restaurant will get its 1st star soon. Staff were correct and very friendly. Highlight was the great dessert Definitely recommended for your visit to Bangkok If you are going to eat here you have to take into account that these types of restaurants are a lot more expensive than a street food restaurant Read less

Boom!!!!!! This was awesome! 16 seats in a small upstairs dining room with Chef Phanuphol Bulsuwan's awesome takes on the deconstructed flavors of Northern Thailand.. I had a great meal (it's a tasting menu) and would do it again on my next trip .. Everything was great but the duck and sausage were total standouts

Excellent dinner in this restaurant of the new Thai gastronomic wave. A complex and neat Thai meal in a contemporary atmosphere, but not snobbish. Well-studied wine list. A mix between Bangkok and Brooklyn! A real experience in a hyper dynamic neighborhood! We loved it. It smells like Michelin stars...

Fantastic experience for the mouth and soul! 11 delicious courses where you will be introduced to various ingredients and dishes from all over Thailand 🇹🇭. It is a culinary journey through the entire country and truly fantastic! Thank you for a wonderful evening

Happy to have had a table for two by booking 2 days before, there are only 14 places. The emphasis is on Thai products, each dish is explained, the origins indicated, the flavors are very varied. In a serene and efficient atmosphere, you choose your own pace to enjoy the dishes. The wine list is limited but you will find what you are looking for. A very good evening and if you want to have a gourmet restaurant in 3 weeks, this is the one to choose.

Menu composed of 9/10 small but exhaustive courses. Excellently and expertly assorted fish, meat and vegetables. Local and exceptional raw material. Good wine list with the presence of interesting bottles of sake. The desserts are also very good. Congratulations, very good

My wife and I chose Blackitch Artisan Kitchen for our NewYears celebration while in Chiang Mai. 10 course tasting menu was creatively conceived, preparation and presentation spot on. Hard to decide a favorite course. Staff was warm and welcoming and offered a thoughtful description of each course as served. We felt we left the Restaurant having made new friends! Chef even served a course or two and was available to thank at the end of this fantastic meal. As a career hospitality guy I seek out restaurants with engaged and present owner Chefs whenever I can. This restaurant obviously represents both his passion and the sincerity of the staff to provide exceptional dining experiences. I will often comment that I don’t generally bother to review average. This meal merited the 5 star review. I would eat the same meal next week if I could! I should also mention the well chosen wine list and the overall value Blackitch provides considering this level of culinary expertise! Read less
The menu was excellent and really shows the breath and depth of the produce available in Thailand. We loved the explanation and understanding of how they are working on sustainability.

Wonderfull dining experience and I would give this restaurant 6/5 stars if I could. We asked (in advance) for a vegetarian menu. And the chef did an amazing job acommodating to our request. It was genuinely on of the best vegetarian meals if have ever had. The menu was seasonal and knowing the products are locally sourced, made it even better. Thank you to the chef and staff. Highly recommended! Read less

Blackitch is a small, cozy restaurant where you can enjoy creative Thai food in a very relaxed atmosphere and attentive service. We had the 11-course menu composed of small bites. Raw fish, crab salad, grilled pork leg, slow cooked beef tongue were some of the highlights. Worth the adventure.

I was here 4 years ago and was excited about Blackitch. Now – Corona in between – I came back: and it is still great! In the meantime, the restaurant was refurbished and has now 6 tabled, but still very small and personal. And the food is still fantastic: Own interpretations of local food, many things with fermentation, balance of flavours, top notch cooking. Also goes very well with wine. Each dish a great composition balancing the flavours and also the presentation is top notch. The very friendly staff and owners do the rest to make this a great experience. Only backside: it is small! You need to book well in advance to get a table. Read less

This was easily the best meal we had in a two week stay. Had booked the restaurant with no particular expectations. It does rate a basic Michelin mention so worth a punt. What we received was an outstanding ten course tasting menu of exceptional quality that would be at home at the highest level anywhere in the world. Terrific ingredients handled with impressive technical skill and finesse in a beautifully balanced menu. Every dish exhibited great flavour, textural contrast and complexity. Close to as perfect a multi course experience that we have had for quite some time. Small but adequate wine list and plenty of other beverage options. The meal was well paced and the service was professional, friendly and informative. Elegant, minimalist room and high quality crockery, cutlery and wine glasses. Expensive in the Chiang Mai scheme of things but worth every baht. Michelin has done this restaurant a disservice. If anyone else in Chiang Mai is cooking at this level someone should let me know! Blackitch is at least, if not more , as accomplished as 80/20 in Bangkok which rates one Michelin star. If you're a foodie and visiting the city, this is a must. Read less

Blackitch Artisan Kitchen was a brilliant, Chiang Mai restaurant recommendation from our friend. Little did we know, our reservation timing was equally brilliant, we thank the Goddess for that! It was during a menu rotation that featured BEST 22 BITES OF 2022!!! It may have been Blackitch’s best of 2022, but it was our BEST MEAL EVER!!! Phanuphol Bulsuwan (Black), is Master Chef. He amazingly and personally described each dish for us. Engaging, entertaining, intelligent, sweet, and highly knowledgeable about every aspect of his restaurant agro-vision and its manifestation, he made our culinary experience even more memorable. This food is more than simply delicious Farm-To-Table fare… this is pure, food artistry magic wrapped up in love! We began our meal with a sip of the Kumen River New Zealand Pinot Noir 2021 which hits the tip of your tongue with a tiny explosion of minerals and finishes with a spicy cherry note. Then the feasting began… 1. Seafood Tart… an entire universe in one bite. 2. Ceviche- perfect palate cleanser. 3. Mountain Tart- Thai style tapas. 4. Baby Cos Salad bacon/fish roe crispy greens. 5. Stuffed chicken wing with native rice (varies with each day’s fresh shipment) pickled lotus finish. 6. Shrimp with Roti with pickled ginger and jam. 7. Tempura with Tofu and Chestnut with a chicken liver paste on a bed of Catfish eggs. 8 and 9. Vietnamese Chicken Salad with reconstructed fresh spring roll With Calimssarri orange splash. 10. River prawn with pomelo fruit shrimp paste served in a tasty, tiny, crunchy corn, rice/corn cup. 11. Catch of the Day (Sandfish today), with coconut crumb served with a Calamassi curd sprinkled with fresh spring onion. 12. River prawn with Sour curry pickled papaya fruit, coconut, grouper crumble, sea grass ,and a crispy shrimp crackers. 13 and 14.. Esan green curry with the surprise of fried okra bites, topped with a roll of grilled fish and Yam stuffing inside a grilled fish with a perfect popped rice garnish. 15. Northern Thai Pork Broth (Nam Ngew lighter, less spicy version), with Blackitch Udon with green onion, cilantro, roasted garlic and Belly. 16. Fried Oyster and southern curry fried on a (…) mushroom bed in green curry sauce topped with a Ginger flower. 17. Grilled Beef in a Black Garlic Sauce with pickled Radish served with blanched then roasted coriander mini-stalks. 18 and 19.. Black Chicken Ball soup with Blackitch Chicken, Japanese rice cooked risotto style served with pickled cucumbers, lotus, with a spicy fermented soybean dipping sauce. DESSERT 19 Amasake cheese Pie with passion fruit. 20. Mini-scoop of green tea ice cream With Orange Financier. 21, Mountain tart. 22. Banana Taco. … and we ate EVERY BITE, as did everyone in the restaurant during our seating!!! Read less

The shop is located on a busy road in a hip part of town. We were surprised to find a dog wondering the downstairs kitchen but at least it was cute. Dinner was just okay. Not Michelin level and not worth coming back or recommending. I was most frustrated when they tried to double bill us. We had paid ahead to reserve our seat but this was not reflected on the bill. Instead of rectifying it immediately from their end, they asked us to see evidence that we paid. Thankfully I screen shotted the transaction and had a printed copy. Not Michelin level service. Read less

Been here for the very first time after hearing very good reviews from several foodie friends. However, the food were not there yet...I could hardly recall anything impressive but just friendly and accommodative services. I can easily mention places with better version of their several dishes. Other good things = super friendly price for drinks :). Verdict: not really a place where you really have to plan for a visit but if you are in Chiang Mai and want some good long meal to socialise with friends, then here can be an option.

Nice farm to table experience using seasonal produce. The Aug - Sep menu is seafood-dominant and they could switch out dishes if one has dietary restriction. The 14-seater Michelin Guide restaurant is on the pricey side, ~2000 baht per person. They have 2 seating, 5pm and 7.30pm and the dinner takes ~2h. Near One Nimman and easily accessible.
Chef Black and team prepare a wonderful omakase styled dining experience. Reservations a must & use Grab to get there.

We went to this restaurant by our friends recommendation. It requires advance booking due to their service by schedule time slot. The place is very difficult to find and no side road parking, using cab is highly recommended. The restaurant has about 4-5 tables and accommodate only 12-16. Our group; had different food limitation/restrictions which we have need to advise upon arrival. The chief would re-design the menu accordingly. Their creations were excellent blend and suitable for our taste. The portions were nice not too heavy. Their ingredients were fresh and carefully selected from different sources.

My family took me to Blackitch on my first evening in Chiang Mai. I honestly had no idea what to expect, they simply told me there is no menu. When we entered the shophouse and walked up the narrow steps, I must honestly say, I was not too impressed, but when we entered the second floor, into the cozy dining room, I already felt that it would be something special. The menu was simply mind blowing. Each dish had its own uniqueness and fabulous combination of flavours. I am not a huge fish fan, but I enjoyed every single dish! This was a truly unique and unexpected experience, and the host was fabulous! I cannot wait to return to Chiang Mai to eat here once again. Read less
The 10 dish tasting so great! Love the concept of Chef's Table and all dishes bland with the local materials, not only care about the value that customers need but also care about the supplier too.

The 10 dish tasting was very good. I liked it all and my wife was 9 out of 10. It was quite filling despite what some reviewers said. There was limited wine choices so we had a strong ginger drink. The staff is excellent and explains everything in detail. Most amusing as you couldn't guess what some is. He ferments most things but in his own way. Really not like anywhere else. It's a bit expensive but this is special so the price is ok. When going look up for the sign as it's upstairs over a gelato shop.

We loved it. Bia the owner was a lovely host, explaining the story behind all 20 tastings. The venue is Nimanhemin hip and the vibe unpretentious. The upstairs room seats just 3-4 tables of 6 people so brings with it a ‘private’ feel. Each bite was interesting and delicious with Thai flavors throughout. The food is locally sourced and inspired. Great selection of wine too. We highly recommend this lovely experience.

The reviews were good but had not prepared us for how good this restaurant was. The menu is a degustation menu of beautiful Thai dishes but with a Japanese sensibility. It was structured so each dish stepped up from the prior one , each flavour becoming more complex. A masterpiece of a menu in design and execution. We had been to Saawaan in Bangkok the week before ( rated 1star by Michelin) and this well exceeded it ( and 2 dishes were the same , including a spectacular crab and stinky bean dish)... do not miss Blackitch!

Tasting menus come in bite-size dishes. It was very flavorful, complex, and simply delicious. Each dish has it own story and all the ingredients are organics. A must try and reservation is highly recommended. (Only show 1 dishes out of 14 in the photo)
We had a fabulous dinner at Blackitch. Course after course was creative and delicious! The staff was kind and helpful. Make sure you get a reservation! You will love the food here!

After a 50 minute wait (during which we were generously offered some water and a bowl of peanuts) we were finally seated in a small nondescript dining room, the casual decor of which belied the utter pretentiousness of what was to follow. After evading repeated queries regarding the menu we were eventually advised that we were to be served a tasting menu of 20 "bites". What followed was a series of at times inspired amuse-bouches which ultimately left us frustrated and unsatisfied. Almost any selection of these formed into full dishes could have made an excellent meal. Instead we had what? Pintxos served with a sh1t wine list and zero atmosphere as conversation was constantly interrupted by explanations from the chefs of the next tiny tidbit. Incredible food spoiled by pretentiousness and poor service. Read less

Looking for some "modern interpretation" of local food I found Blackitch in the just released Michelin guide. The restaurant is tiny - only 3 tables with app, 16 seats, hidden in the first floor of the house. But what is served here: truly exceptional. You have to book in advance and there is only one menu (of course you can indicate special requirements or allergies), but each dish was remarkable. They use the best from sea, river and mountain to create really creative dishes - and no "wannabe" creativity using aceto basamico, truffles oil or similar. Just to mention a few of the dishes: Japanese inspired sashimi -like, pomelo based fruit salad with seafood, river prawns, satay-like dish and so on. I also learned about some ingredients I never heard before like the Lime-like Calamansi. Also to mention that for each dish I received a detailed description of the components and where they come from. As Michelin does in the first year of review not give any stars Blackitch is "only" rated with the Michelin plate. But I am convinced that the place is among those top of the list for getting a star soon. Read less

Overall amazing experience! In a very select setting, we were pampered with a wide array of amazing dishes. Despite some of the other ‘fine dining’ locations in Chiang Mai, Blackitch sticks to the roots of the Thai cuisine, while introducing incredibly unique flavour combinations and innovative ingredients!

A chef’s table approach in an intimate setting, fantastic service and sublime, locally sourced, seasonal produce. Lots of clever cooking, layers of flavor and a creative eye for detail and presentation. Book ahead of time
The chef is very talented. The food is created from locally sourced ingredients. He presented in fine-dining way. Really Like it

Our third trip to the Blackkitch was as good as the previous two visits. This time our favourites included black chicken served with chilli and liver pate. Pork jowls served with a tangy larb and sweet potato like root vegetable from the North Thai mountains and best of all was desert a “sugar puff” with a pumpkin custard, ice cream and a flat meringue that was indescribably good. I’m not sure what was in it - but it was the perfect balance of everything that a meringue could possibly be. I was served a second plate of meringue as I must have looked like I was in heaven. I just wonder what a pavlova would taste like made from this meringue and coupled with local fresh Thai fruits. We will be back every time we are in Chiang Mai. Read less

The restaurant serve Japanese fusion twist with local ingredients. This is my idea. I would say every dish is extrodinary. Chef play along with all local ingredients from each part of Thailand. Some ingredient is quite difficult to cook or work it. He makes it perfect. I love every course here. U definitely shouldn't miss it.

Once you step through a unprepossing entrance you are directed upstairs to a small eating area with max 15 seats . Then treated to an unbelievable 9 course gastronomic delight , each course of superlative gods surpassing the previous one , elagantly explained by a charming host / joint head chef Quite sublime

Blackitch is the hidden treat of Chiang Mai. The chef and his small team work miracles in their tiny kitchen. I was disappointed that Michelin did not award them a star in their new 2019 guide. Blackitch certainly deserves a star! Maybe it's not expensive enough for the Michelin inspectors......

We had a delectable 10-course dinner courtesy of Khun Black . Superbly selected flavours from organic suppliers around Thailand (and the fish all caught by chef Black), and the most creative use of herbs and spices all combined for probably the best modern Thai cuisine we’ve ever tasted. Insanely enjoyable and memorable experience. We can't wait to go back to Chiang Mai to revisit Blackitch

One of the most interesting and creative meals I've ever had. The chef goes to great lengths to try new things (new flavor combinations, different fermentation techniques, etc), and even catches his own fish. He's also a lot of fun to chat with. Overall excellent meal and excellent experience!

The little place has just three tables and you have to walk through a deli and up stairs to reach it. You'll be rewarded with numerous courses of well crafted and innovative food. Good for pescatarians.

This restaurant only seats 16 people at two tables , and offers a 9 or more , course extravaganza which changes everyday ..all the food is locally sourced , mainly organic , much is fermented by the chef Black and sits all around the walls slowly turning into magic or not..he is constantly learning and experimenting .This is a set course , you dont get to choose , and it is not cheap

A hidden gem in Chiang Mai's Nimmanhaemin hip area. Fantastic concept of small eateries with few seats yet well executed twist of seafood dishes as main ingredients. Chef Black has stepped up his traits of fermentation various ingredients mixing with locally seasonal resources. Experience and be surprised! Try it before Michelin gets their mark on!

Quite possibly one of the most interesting meals I've had ever. One or two dishes didn't quite work for me. As can be expected I suppose when it comes to experimental menus like these. But if you're a foodie & you want to try something different, then this is definitely your go to. That gorgonzola & coconut milk sauce, who would have thought?? quite yummy....

This is the best meal that I ever ate in Chiang Mai. The food is so good, they can surprise you every single dish. The owner and his staffs are so generous. They make me love Chiang Mai more and more.

I eventually made it here, I have been visiting the area for many years, but, I had never arrange my time here. However, after a call to Secret Retreats they soon had my table booked and my food preferences noted. This was handled effortlessly and I was really impressed with their knowledge about the restaurant and its suitability to our group that particular day. We arrived, I will be honest it took us a few moments to work out where the restaurant is located, it is above a Gelato store I believe. Up the stairs and into a new world, it is not fine dining decor by any stretch, but oh my, it is fine dining! I loved the decor and with just 3 small tables means you are one of a select few to enjoy all that awaits. The food is all sourced locally when and where possible, using organic ingredients and seasonal so constantly changing. Which gives me a perfect excuse to return. The staff all genuinely smiling and friendly with a desire to help. Each dish is presented and then explained, we happened to be traveling around the time of Mothers Day in Thailand. So all the dishes are based on their mothers. I can honestly say, it is some of THE most authentic and delicious Thai food in my 20 years of frequent visits. Yes, the price may seem high compared to some, but consider the effort taken and the high quality of the ingredients and it easy to see it is actually great value. I was with two friends, who could not eat another morsel, one of whom is a native to Chiang Mai and had never heard of this restaurant and was amazed by how the taste reminder her of home! Which, I think says it all! I cannot wait to return and I would like to thank Beer for her wonderful description of each course and the teams work to make us truly happy! Read less
Been several times now and each time it is a new and original experience. A discovery of all native produces from Thailand sourced by Chef Black himself. recommended to all curious gourmets...

I can see how this might be a polarizing restaurant. There were 6 in our party. Four thought it was outstanding, 2 went out and had street food after dinner. There is an interesting corralation. The 4 who liked it like to cook, The 2 who didn't just get fed and don't find enjoyment in creating a meal. I was one of the 4 thinking it was outstanding, and I thought of the artistry and creativity that went into each of the 9 small plates. Eight of the 9 dishes I would order again.

Not sure where all these great reviews are coming from, but this was one of the worst meals I have ever eaten in Thailand. Although the hipster esthetic sought to evoke Wicker Park circa 1993 it missed the mark on every level. The 9 course meal dragged on for over 90 minutes and I could not describe any of the dishes as actually tasting good. Many of the dishes were cloyingly sweet, which is bizarre given that overly sweet is not something one often encounters in Thailand. In fact, one noodle dish was so sweet I could not eat it. The chef seemed to be going for a balance of bitter and sweet, but the execution was clumsy and the end product was just plain not good. Other short falls included a very limited drink menu, consisting of several beers, no wine, and only one mixed drink, a gin and tonic. I opted for a lemon soda, which was also, perhaps not surprisingly, overly sweet. Topping things off was the music which while good, clashed with the music coming in from the street. The result was a dissonance that just added to my desire to leave as soon as I could. The final insult came in the form of the check. 3700 Thai baht. By U.S or European standards this is not much, but its a small fortune in Chiang Mai. I have eaten at the much better Le Coq Dor for about this much, and left fully satisfied. I would note we were the only customers in the restaurant on a Saturday night, which perhaps should have been the first clue for what was in store. There are so many good restaurants in Chiang Mai, sadly this is not one of them. Read less

David takes pride in his small restaurant, making everything from scratch, you can see a lot of what he is working on on shelves next to the walls. Not only did we have an excellent 10 course meal - which was fairly priced considering the care and dedication that he put in, but he made sure to call us a cab (himself!) and make sure we got off in the direction of our hotel....

We stumbled across this diamond in the rough after dodging a restaurant with a sick (coughing/sneezing) waitress and another with no fan or a/c working. After dodging these first world problems we wandered past the nightlife at the surrounding bars and noticed Blackitch. Artisan kitchens can be rather hit and miss but this location is quaint coupled with enticing reviews welcomed us in. Obviously we didn't reserve a spot prior to dining and after a brief discussion with the kitchen hands the sous chef encouraged us to dine. We were unsure what to expect with no menu or information about the experience other than a drinks list. The house made plum wine was superb over ice and my partner enjoyed the root beer (sarsparilla for those playing in Aus). We were delighted from the first dish of the degustation through to the dessert. The chef showed pride and pleasure with our dining experience adding to the evening. Yes the price is significantly more than what you may pay elsewhere however the quality and personalised service for both of us including drinks for circa 4,500BHT is reasonable in comparison to other degustation dinings. Our favourite dish was the duck blood sausage followed by the banana fritter dessert. Unfortunately the only pic I managed was that of an empty bowl as I devoured the bouquet of flavours shortly after each serving. Recommended for a couples night of fine dining. Read less

I’ll start by saying I was disappointed and this may not resonate with others. First off, I read about Blackitch from the Guardian website, one I trust and refer to while traveling, so MY expectations were extremely high going in. We reserved for the tasting menu, which I had read was 4 courses (a price was not known). Other than us, there were 3 others diners here, so little to no ambience. The personal service from the sous chef was impressive, she explained every dish in great detail, her English was excellent and she was passionate about her dishes. 😃 It was not the presentation of the dishes that disappointed me but rather the tastes that didn’t jive - the river prawn dish came out undercooked and with a salty taste, which was unedible (& again I was to emphasize not satisfactory for MY tastes). After 4 courses, we asked how many more courses were to come, to be told there were a total of 10. With a combination of not being hungry and not being overwhelmed with what we were tasting, we asked to finish with desert, which was a delicious honey gelato. Understanding we were not very hungry, (having had a late lunch) the server (who was the sous chef) discounted the bill, which was very thoughtful and her gesture appreciated, however still extremely expensive given what we ate (3,500 Baht, which included 2 beers). This review is not meant to dissuade diners but merely a heads up for what to expect going in. Read less

If you really like great food cooked by passionate and friendly people, this is the restaurant you’re looking for. The food is first and foremost delicious. But it’s everything else that really makes this place shine. They focus on local ingredients, used with a modern sensibility in dishes that are still recognizably Thai. (Even having eaten around Thailand there was plenty we were unfamiliar with.) Each fantastic course comes with a chat with the chef - as brief or extensive as you want. These are people who want to share their love for their food with you. Honestly, as foodies, this is the meal we’d been looking for since we arrived.

Had no idea what to expect when we booked this restaurant, and not knowing even made it better. We almost left because we didn't know where the restaurant was, we entered the bar adjacent and they said it was next door but next door was a gelato place which was closed. Blackitch was above this gelato place but we felt weird entering a closed shop. Luckily as we were about to leave, a lady came out and let us in, led us up into this quaint and small space. It was the restaurant and it would only fit about 12 people (2 tables and a bar area) making it super intimate. The food consisted of a 9-course set menu which is meticulously prepared by the Chef who comes out to explain each course. It changes all the time and his philosophy is very farm-to-table, local, nose-to-mouth cooking. His cooking ideals are admirable and we would definitely go back to try the other dishes he comes up with as the dishes were delicious (lots of Thai flavors fused with other cuisines). A meal for 4 of us cost around 7000 baht with drink and corkage as be bought a bottle of wine along. Read less

Run - don't walk - to this culinary gem! Tucked away on Soi 7, Blackitch is easy to miss, and but for the recommendation of our Concierge, would never have been on our radar. Thankfully it was. The quirky but inviting dining area only holds three tables, creating a kitchen-table like atmosphere which complements Chef Black's generous and personal approach to cooking for his guests. The 9-course menu changes daily according to the freshest ingredients available that day. Our meal incorporated plants he had foraged at 6 am, deep dark honey he had carried back from Chiang Rai, beef and pork he had fermented for two weeks, and smoked short rice grains collected from the nearby hill tribes. The ice cream was being hand-churned in the gelato lab downstairs when we arrived. Each course is carefully introduced and explained, and as one sips the 2 year-aged plum wine (made by Chef Black of course), it is easy to get lost in the narrative. The food however speaks for itself, as does the warmth and sincerity of our host. A true Chiang Mai "Must"! Read less

The calibre of food served here was exceptional. I couldn't find much information online at all after reading about it in the Guardian earlier this year before we went so we took a gamble, and I'm so glad we did. Chef Black served us 9 courses, all surprises and chosen by him, throughout the evening, coming out and explaining in detail each one and why he paired ingredients together. Distinctive fine dining food with an asian influence - lots of fish! We paid 1200 Baht for a bottle of wine (which was absolutely delicious) and 1600 Baht each for the food. If you're some who loves fine food and the complexities of pairing, go with an open mind and you won't be disappointed.

A truly unique and distinctive experience at Blackitch Artisan Kitchen. Chef Black's philosophy around food and ethics comes through in all his dishes - local, creative, artisan and balanced. His food is innovative, experimental and he has a real flair for flavours. Chef Black explained each of his dishes as they came to the table and the processes - the majority of the food is organic and handmade. I was fascinated to hear about the fermentation processes and work he'd gone through to create the dishes - an unforgettable evening !

Chef Black is a true culinary master. His passion for food - from making his own soy sauces and spirits, to hand crafting seperate meals for all customers, is astonishing. Come visit this young virtuoso before the world comes knocking. Make sure to book in advance, and remember this is a small venue which would fit a max of 12 guests

Blackitch is unique. This restaurant must be booked a month in advance - the reason... There are three tables and everything is absolutely awesome. Chef Black creates a new menu every day. Each course is made from ingredients that are fresh... Either imported scallops from Japan flown in, or a selection of greens that are perfectly ripe from the local farm. So don't expect consistency - you will have a new experience with every course, and with some you may like and others not - but each course is made by a master painter experimenting with the wide variety of foods available. Chef Black creates each of the six courses table side and then explains in English or Thai why and how he put together the dish. There were three of us dining. We all choose different courses as our favourite dish - which is testament to the chef's versatility and style. Desert is made from the downstairs kitchen. If you are lucky, you might also get to try the house made plum wine - which was superb with desert. I expect one day the "kitch" will be expanded into a Michelin star restaurant - so be sure to book here before that happens. You will never forget the experience of being totally spoiled in this globally unique restaurant. Read less

Extensive menu. Exceptional food. Indian chefs. Gluten free and vegan information and options. Great service (we did experience a couple of long waits but the manager came to us personally to apologise).
We’ve eaten here twice now as a group of 6 and both times were great. Lots of gluten free options clearly labelled on the menu. The Mango Lassi is amazing!

I don’t have much experience of Indian cuisine so we chose three starters and two courses based on the names that spoke to us. One entry wasn't to our liking but the other two were great. The dishes were good but too bad the chicken tandoori was a bit dry, even with a little sauce. I think we should have had a dish in sauce like a curry, I saw it on the other tables it looked very good.

The owner came to talk to us, As this was our first encounter with Indian food he gave us advise what to order. We had delicious soup,delicate taste. After that a curry with naan and cold vegetables We can highly recommend this place Peter and Alexandra

Walking out of our hotel we spotted this restaurant on our way past and had a look at the menu which seemed promising. Reading the reviews we thought we wouldn’t be disappointed and we weren’t. Excellent food and excellent service. We are fans of Indian cuisine so have high expectations. Totally recommend this place.

Excellent Service Well prepared and tasty dish We took a dish too spicy for our taste The dish has been changed and we have been brought yogurt, in order to reduce the taste of chilli, the portions are generous. There's a good Price quality ratio. I highly recommend this address to enjoy Indian dishes in chiang mai

This is a gem and must not be missed! This restaurant was so good me and my family came back here twice. The food was luscious with rich hints of spice. The outside area was clean and the staff were so friendly and helpful. If you are looking for an Indian with a Thai twist this is the place you are looking for! They also put the cricket on🤣 Also it was near One Nimman (a lively place for all the family) Read less
Fantastic Indian restaurant! Delicious food full of flavor, quick and friendly service, and a warm, inviting atmosphere. A truly enjoyable dining experience—highly recommend!
Great food, great service. Enjoyed our meal. The food was amazing. And the workers were friendly. It was a good experience.

The food here is authentic and taste pretty good.The waiters are working quick and listen to ur orders with all ears.The price here is reasonable and the surrounding area is peaceful even though there's a road nearby.Only the air conditioning is a little low😅.

Happily I’m staying in the Nimman area of Chiang Mai so it was only a short trek to this unassuming but superb and authentic Indian restaurant. The menu caters extensively for vegetarian and vegan appetites but the menu also includes meat variations. The food is reasonably priced. I had a southern Indian chicken curry, rice and a mango lassi for under THB 300. The food was brought still bubbling in a clay pot and I have to say it was outstanding. The owner was very friendly, service great. Yeah I concur with all the other reviewers.

A delicious and hot meal with favors that I have not been able to find in Chiang Mai. I encourage Indian food enthusiastics to visit this restaurant because I was left wanting more of everything we ordered.

I have wanted to eat more vegan/vegetarian/Indian food and decided to try ChaiNThai. I wasn’t sure what to order and the waitress was very helpful and friendly. The food was excellent and the atmosphere is bright and happy. Lots of options to choose from. I will definitely be going back, and you should try for yourself.

Our first night in Chiang Mai and we were looking for an easy place to get a bite to eat. Chai N Thai was next to our hotel and was the perfect spot. The owner, JK, immediately greeted us and made us feel welcome. We opted to sit outside. As it was a bit warm, the staff insisted on bringing out a fan for us. As we perused the menu, we chatted with JK about the restaurant, Chiang Mai and life in general. He provided wonderful suggestions on menu items and other places to visit. When it came time to order, it was difficult to decide, but we were so happy with the samosa, garlic naan and curry to share. So, so good! Chai N Thai doesn't have a bar, however, the staff was only too happy to assist us in ordering beverages from the bar across the street and even going back and forth to pick them up and pay. It was an absolute delight to meet JK and the attentive staff at Chai N Thai. This place is highly recommended not only for the incredible food, but the excellent staff and service. Read less

This is by far the best place to have Indian food in Chang mai. The chicken curry arrives in an earthen pot and is piping hot. No food colour finally! The chicken tandoori was properly marinated and very flavourful. The south indian was quite good as well. The establishment and its members are very polite and go out of their way to meet your meal requests and expectations. I have seen some high ranking places serving Indian food in Chang Mai; they are very average in comparison to this joint. Highly recommend even if you are not sure as to what would match your palette. The staff helps you with your selection. It is as authentic as it gets in Chang mai. Everything on trip advisor did not really match in terms of which restaurant is best for Indian cuisine so I hit this place on my third go and third time was a charm. This place should have been the first in terms of ranking and I am not sure if there is a distant second but to be fair I haven’t been to the whole set. If you want good Indian food it wins my recommendation. Read less
So delicious! Recommended by a friend from South India. Highly recommend Paani Poori and Masala Dosa. We have visited many times

Happy to find South Indian food such as dosa, vada, pakoda etc in Chiang Mai. Tasty food. Ordered masala dosa, filter coffee, onion pakora and also gobi paratha and mango lassi and rava kesari. We packed some sweet pongal to go. Mild, not too spicy. Very quick service. Met the partner Mr. JaiKrishnan as well. Very friendly and helpful gentleman.
Amazing food. The Indian Thali had a great combination of curries. Try the mango Lassi. Definitely would return here for authentic Indian cuisine.
Very good Indian food from different regions of India. We had mutton, beef, and vegetable curries, with rice and garlic/cheese naan. Friendly service as well. Can recommend!

Looking for a change from Thai food, we researched for well-rated Indian restaurants in Chiang Mai. Chai N Thai appealed to us in particular. I ate my favorite Indian dish, palak paneer, and, it was actually one of the best palak paneers I've ever eaten. Together with the very friendly and attentive staff and the cozy atmosphere in the restaurant, this was a very nice evening. Highly recommended!

Myself and my husband went over to Chai N Thai for some Indian food. We read the reviews and thought we would give it a go seeing as all the reviews are rated 4/ 5 stars. We ordered a paneer butter masala, daal makhani and 2 garlic naan breads. The curries were very mild and didn’t have too much taste in there. The naan breads were nice and the rice. However, the next day when I woke up I had to use the bathroom at least 12 times in the day due to getting loose motions. My stomach wasn’t feeling the best and I believe the food had upset my stomach. As I didn’t have anything for lunch and had breakfast. It was quiet busy in the restaurant, maybe the curries weren’t cooked fresh and have been kept in the fridge but this had triggered my stomach. I’m sorry but I don’t think I will be visiting again. Read less

We love Thai food, but our favorite place in Chiang Mai was this Indian restaurant. We were staying on the same street and came here after walking by. We were in Chiang Mai for 5 night and we came here 3 times for breakfast. We loved the South Indian tea and the egg curry and egg parantha. Extremely nice people. I imagine everything they make here is delicious, we wanted to try more items but didn’t have time.

Wasn’t really sure what to expect on going to Chai n Thai for the first time. For some reason I had it in my head it was going to be a Thai/Indian Fusion place and expensive. Fortunately,this not the case at all and Chai n Thai exceeded all expectations. The restaurant has a very extensive and authentic Indian menu. The choice of tantalising mainly Southern Indian dishes was overwhelming and as a couple we could only manage a Chicken Tandoori, Aloo Gobi and a Lamb curry, with a accompaniment of naan bread and a spicy Masala Papadum. The service was very attentive and top notch in that staff were able to explain dishes and cater to all our requests in a very polite, friendly and efficient manner. The dishes were substantial and extremely tasty and well presented. The restaurant is not licensed but staff will happily purchase alcohol of your choice from a bar across the road and we were able to enjoy a couple of nice cold large Tiger beers with our meal. This is a well run restaurant that provides a wide range of extremely succulent Indian food at a very reasonable price in decent sized portions. Definitely worth a revisit Read less
Great food from both north and south India with great service and a very vegan-friendly menu with all sorts of treats!

Not really an atmosphere for having a lunch in peace. We sat down all the same but seeing no one coming into the dining room, we asked ourselves a few questions. There were only tourists seated and faced with this succession of bad omens, we decided to flee to the restaurant next door "Kopy" which was much more welcoming.

Just had the most amazing Indian dinner while visiting Chiangmai Mai. The restaurant is quite bright and vibrant to look at on approach which was not what i expected but it was quite busy so decided to give it a try based on the many other positive reviews. The menu had many dishes that I was not totally familiar with but the staff were very helpful in providing further detail and suggestions. The food ordered was amazing and I really hope and recommend that anyone reading this should take the opportunity to visit this restaurant if yhey can. Read less

What a fantastic place! The owner was super kind and spent quite some time explaining all the differences between the northern and southern Indian dishes. The food was exceptional, some of the best Indian food we have ever tasted. We had such an amazing time! Highly recommended!!!

Excellent food, both the north and South Indian items. The owner is very hands-on to make sure your meal is perfect, makes great suggestions, and will customize your dining experience to your tastes. Highly recommend!!

I craved Indian food after travelling for 2 weeks in Thailand. Happened to be this restaurant was next to my hotel. 10/0 for authentic Indian food. Excellent flavours. Not spicy as most Thai food I found I could not eat due to highly spiced with chillies. Staff friendly and very helpful. Chef Sanjay explains any ingredients and will make to your taste if required. I highly recommend this restaurant if you like Indian food. Indian tea with fresh ginger is a must. I ate here 4 times and will definitely return.

We have eaten here three days in a row and will be returning tomorrow if you haven’t tried this place I feel genuinely sorry for you good vibe lovely staff amazing food possibly best masala dosa we’ve ever had looking forward to tomorrow

Top quality! I had perfectly spiced biryani, great samosas, and even complimentary soup! The staff is friendly and the atmosphere is bright and comfortable. Highly recommended if you are looking for great Indian food.
