Food was delicious. Menu was really expansive- tons of options. Service was great. We ordered masala dosas, tandoori chicken and chicken biriyani. The dosa was so flavorful- definitely my favorite!
Food was fresh and authentic, specifically the masala dosas and biriyani. Super kind service. This is a great place for a lovely casual dinner.
Very tasty authentic food. Deffo must try I’ll definitely coming back before I leave Good quality food and great customer service
Had the perfect experience. Very delicious food. Came out very fast. Would highly recommend eating here! You also get a Delicious free soup before your meal! This place was overall great.

This is the first time I leave a review BEFORE I leave the restaurant, that’s how good it was! Not only the food is great and we’ll served but the staff if so kind and friendly. Will absolutely come back!
Amazing food here (certainly best Indian restaurant I've been to in Thailand). Complimentary starter and dessert was a bonus. Will be back if I visit Chiang Mai again.
amazing food. super friendly. almost as good as my grandma. try the curries. the avayil is incredible. you want to eat here

Mainly North & South Indian cuisine. Other Vegan food options and creative Western/Indian food mix available. Dosa was golden brown, crispy, crunchy and absolutely tasty . Loved it. Friendly staff, creative design reasonable price.

While in Chiang Mai, found this eatery just by chance. Selecting few dishes from the extensive menu is tough. Food is freshly prepared and served quite fast. Authentic Kerala dishes and impeccable service - what more to ask in a foreign land? Kudos Chai N Thai Read less

The food was amazing. Lots of complex tastes. The wet was so friendly and you can tell he has a passion for food. Explains well, takes time and care. Made to order given our preferences. Lots of variety and everything delicious. I loved trying new things. Highly recommended

We were walking through Nimman on a Monday night looking for a place to eat and didn’t expect to find such a gem of a restaurant. Only opened just post covid the quality of food and the genuine friendliness of all the staff are hard to beat. I don’t think I have had such good tasting and fresh Indian food outside of India. This is by far the better of the Indian restaurants in Nimman and definitely the one you should pick if you are choosing Indian cuisine in Chiang Mai. Read less

Tasted culinary delights from Masala Dosa,Malabar Chicken Biriyani to dessert-payasam. Never expected to find this warm,hospitable restaurant tucked in bylanes of Nimman.Travel ASEAN extensively- i must say this is one of the most authentic south indian & kerala experience. Kudos to the chef & host Jayakrishnan for the kerala experience in Chiang Mai.

Found this gem of a south indian restaurant tucked in lane 9@Nimman. Tasted Masala Dosa accompanied by yummy chutneys,Malabar Chicken Biriyani with pickle,raita & finished with hot payasam(desert). Travel extensively south asia & i must admit this is one of most authentic south Indian culinary experiences. Kudos to the chef and warm host Jayakrishnan

As an Indian I will say that this is one of the places I'm always happy to visit. I am partial also because they serve South Indian Food which is one of my favourite Indian Foods. Not easy to find especially in Thailand and so it's a welcome addition and I am glad that they are doing a good job and constantly improving. - South India - North Indian - Dosa: 3.5/5 - Idli: 3.5/5 - Sambhar: 4/5 - Rasam: 4.5/5 - Chutneys: 4.5/5 - Samosa: 3/5 - Paani - Poori: 2/5 - Medu and Dal Vada: 4/5 - Kuttoo Curry: 3.5/5 - Aviyal: 3.5 - Dals: 3.5/5 - Appam/Idiyappam: 4/5 - Breads: 4/5 - Rice: 3.5/5 - Mutton and Chicken Dishes: 4/5 All in all a satisfying experience. Also enjoy most of their stuff when they cater at various venues across Chiang Mai. Staff is polite, smiling, helpful and the chef knows his job. The owners are lovely people and all of them are always happy to say hello, share some conversations, information as well as join you at the table. Very warm, friendly and relaxed atmosphere. Clean and tidy including the seats. They are also open to feedback which is a great way to test the pulse of your audience. I do hope they increase the portion of the dips, Sambhar, Rasam and water and additives when serving paani-poori. Different days can also be different. You can always mention specific requirements like less or salt, chilly etc. Looking forward to eating there again. Happy to take my friends as well. Read less
Delicious South Indian food. Lots of dosa options as well as many other South Indian and Thai food. Very good!
Nothing to say it’s just the best place for eat indian food !!! Everything we order it’s just amazing. The best tikka masala I eat in my life !

First, Chiang Mai has great Indian restaurants. Not all have great decor but they mostly have good food. But Chai n Thai sticks out! Great to change things a bit- Have authentic South Indian food and also a great ambience, location, service and…flavor. Must try. My favorites- Masala Dosa, Fish curry and I love the Rasam soup.

I loved this place! I had ghee dosa with an out of this world peanut chutney dipping sauce, paani poori combination set that had a really yummy tamarind sauce and included butter chicken which was perfectly prepared. My mango lassi was cold and refreshing. The restaurant has colorful decor, with unique murals. There is indoor and outdoor seating. Service was quick and friendly and the restaurant is in an easy to reach location. Definitely will return to sample more. Read less
Staff were friendly and helpful, food was soooo good! The only problem is that I went alone and only try a couple of dishes. I will definitely be back.

We took a 15 minute taxi ride from the historical center to reach this restaurant. The night prior we had dined at the sister property, HOME BY GINGER, and had such a magical experience that we wanted to try this one. I can see why Michelin has dropped its rating as the "farm" was average at best. There were plenty of servers (not crowded), but they seemed aloof & more interested in chatting among themselves. My drink had to be returned to the bar. Food was edible, but nothing in comparison to last night. Sorely disappointed.

It was my 1st time at Ginger Farm Kitchen with my girlfriend. We were impressed at how aesthetically pleasing the food displayed were. The food was delicious too. Definitely worth a visit. The servers were friendly and warm. Service was good.

This place was recommended by our tour guide and we had noticed it was busy when we’d walked past on a few evenings so we booked and were not disappointed. It’s one of the few places offering a vegetarian Khao Soi (traditional northern Thai dish) so we ordered both the vegetarian and chicken versions and both were delicious and highly recommended. There’s lots of choice on the menu but this dish was enough with a side of veg and good vfm. Would recommend reservation if going in the evening, however staff are efficient so you probably wouldn’t have to wait too long for a table.

Every visit to Chiang Mai includes eating at the Ginger Farm Kitchen mainly because the food is so good. It’s not possible to book a table in advance so at busy times, its possible you have to wait for a table. We have always been fortunate and have never had to wait. There’s a very comprehensive menu but there’s no rush when ordering. One good fact here is that no MSG is used so the flavours of the food are natural. Service is excellent and the staff work hard, clearing and resetting tables and always have a smile on their faces. Once ordered, you don’t have to wait long for your food to arrive. The pork dishes are always tasty and the spring rolls were very good value. Papaya salad is a firm favourite. Prices are reasonable and probably about average for the area. Ginger Farm Kitchen is situated on a busy main road Qantas immediately adjacent to a very good market. The restaurant does not have customer toilets but public toilets are a couple of minutes walk away. Highly recommended. Read less
Food, service and price were excellent for a Michelin 2 star restaurant but they have no internal toilets which seems odd to me

Ginger Farm Kitchen was… fine. Nothing terrible, nothing impressive, but definitely not a place that feels anywhere close to Michelin level. The ingredients are fresh and good quality, but the dishes themselves don’t have much personality. They taste like something you could easily get at a well run chain restaurant — safe, predictable, and not particularly memorable. Service was on the slower side, and the interior had a very heavy food smell that lingered the whole time. The ventilation is clearly not strong enough, and it makes the dining experience a bit uncomfortable. I tried the pork belly and the fresh spring rolls with sausage. Both were okay, but neither stood out. The flavors were mild and lacked the depth or excitement you’d expect from a place with this reputation. For the price, I didn’t feel like the experience delivered. Good ingredients alone aren’t enough if the dishes don’t bring something special to the table. I don’t think I’d return. Read less

Today, i went to eat with my son. He got sick and he vomitted in a corner of the restaurant when he tried to reach the toilet. I know it's not good, but it's an unexpected event. However, they charged us 500 bath because of that, and they do charge 10% service fee on the total bill. It's not human at all. Although the food is good, i'll never eat here again.

Despite having several sites this is a really good restaurant with a really interesting menu. The food actually tastes healthier than in other places and still packs a punch in terms of flavour. It also gave me the chance to try Khao Soi as a vegetarian which many restaurants do not offer.

Every visit to Chiang Mai includes a meal at this very good restaurant which is extremely popular with Thais and tourists. It’s not possible to book a table in advance and it does get very busy but we have never had to wait for a table. The staff work extremely hard clearing and resetting tables. The menu is very comprehensive but there’s no rush to order. We certainly recommend the Hang Lay curry and the crispy pork belly with kale. There are a number of very tempting fish dishes. Once ordered, it doesn’t take long for the food to arrive. One thing we like is that this estuarine does not use MSG. Portions are generous and very tasty. If you’ve room for a sweet, then mango with sticky rice is very very good! There’s free Wi-Fi but no toilets on the premises. There are public toilets near by. Prices are about average for a restaurant of this standard and we paid just over 1000THB for fives dishes plus water. There is a 10% service charge added to the bill. Highly recommended. Read less

There is very delicious food here that offers more than the usual Asian menu. As an appetizer on the house we got an avocado dip, which was served in a small plant pot, along with vegetable sticks. The food was delicious. Prices are slightly higher than usual, but this is justified as attention is paid to organic and regional products.

Every time we visit Chiang Mai, we always eat here. Theqre is a very comprehensive menu catering for all tastes. The food is always well cooked and we like the fact that no MSG is used. It’s not possible to book a table in advance but we have never experienced any difficulty. The staff work hard to ensure any waiting time is kept to a minimum. The most difficult decision is what dishes to choose! We would certainly recommend the Hang lay curry along with the Khao soi curry. The papaya salad is a firm favourite also. If fish is preferred, there’s plenty of choice. For a sweet treat, try the mango and sticky rice…delicious. Prices are very reasonable and this restaurant is highly recommended. Read less

Disappointed, we ordered fried rice with fish it came with 3 small pieces of fish, also ordered pad thai and requested it to not be spicy the put the chili on the side of the dish and it cannot be separated, ruined the dish.
The food was amazing. It's truely a living kitchen. They provide vegan options and recommendations for guest that have allergies

Ginger Farm Kitchen is our favourite restaurant in Chiang Mai and we never miss eating here whenever we are in the city. It’s a very popular place and it’s not possible to reserve a table but the turnover is such that any waiting time is relatively short. There is a very comprehensive menu. They pride themselves on using organic produce and state that MSG is not used. Service is first class and once ordered, the dishes of food arrive quite quickly. We always enjoy the crispy pork belly which is one of the restaurant’s signature dishes. Khao soi ( curry with chicken ) and the spicy pork spare ribs were delicious. Altogether, we ordered five dishes plus jasmine and sticky rice plus drinks and we paid 1485THB which included 10% service charge which we thought was excellent value for money. Be aware that the restaurant does not have toilets but these are available in the nearby market. Very highly recommended. Read less

I had high expectations when we visited but we were very disappointed. We got a seating immediately on walking in. Service was good. The menu looks eye-catching. We ordered curry river prawns priced at Thai baht 490 and a paku vegetable. The vegetable was good though on the salty side. The curry River prawns was really not edible - the prawns were cold and covered with a lukewarm sauce. The prawns were not fresh too and were very overcooked. We told the waitress and she said they will make it hot but it came back slightly warmer only. We didn’t eat it. My rose tea was not fragrant too though it was served hot. I won’t re-visit this restaurant.

First impression was of Coolness after walking around on a Blazing Hot Day. Staff were very welcoming and attentive. Drinks were Cold an refreshing. Wife had the sweet and sour sea bass - Amazing. I had the Tom Yum Goong - Exceptional. Great Restaurant and one I can definitely recommend

The restaurant is located in One Nimman soi.....in a few words,everything was great!!! The food..........the decor.......the atmosphere....the staff(especially the lady called Popin{if i say correct...she speaks perfect English and it was very helpful with the menu}) i saw in front on the entrance a lot of Awards from Michelin(i do not know if they are original) but what i know the restaurant is one of the best in Chiang Mai!!! They specialized in Thai food(based on ginger and not only ginger) and healthy food The place is open until 11.00 pm but the last order (before kitchen is closed) is on 10 pm.....so you go early and enjoy your food as i told you the food is great!!!whatever we order it was delicious and yummy!!!the price is a little high(for Thai people)but it is worth it...a lot of non-Thai in the shop.. i surely go again and i recommend it this restaurant for nice atmosphere and good Thai Food Read less

A quick Bolt taxi took us from our hotel in the old town up to Nimman area for dinner at Ginger Farm Kitchen. It was a Tuesday and the restaurant was busy with walk-ins only. We were told a table would take about 30 minutes and having left our telephone number we wandered about the nearby area and had a drink. The restaurant called about 20 minutes later saying our table was ready. All staff were very friendly, attentive and helpful and we were seated at a small but perfectly adequate table for two in a bright and busy restaurant. The menu was interesting and focused on local specialities alongside more well known Thai specialities. We chose an appetiser of tempura chilli squid followed by the signature crispy belly pork, some amazing river prawns, fried whole eggs with tamarind, morning glory and a steamed rice. The table was a little small for all the plates but the food was outstanding. A very enjoyable evening and meal, thank you Ginger Farm Kitchen! Read less

The description and reviews make it sound like ginger farm kitchen will have a mom and pops feel to it but it feels very much like a chain. The hang lang curry was delicious but the spring rolls and spaghetti were very average.

Thai food good. Had Kao soi, traditional Chiang Mai food (soupy Thai crunchy with soft noodle, chicken). Very good but not a fan of chicken on the bone as it is such a busy dish). After few days trying kao soi here in Chiang Mai, I would rate this one good but definitely not great.
Very charming restaurant Localized in white market a good place to visit The food was very well served I strongly recommend
Excellent healthy colourful restaurant. Food is so fresh and beautifully presented at a very reasonable cost. Go! You will love it!

Came with a group of 8 people and we arrived early for dinner but reserve a table. The place was filled up a few minutes after we arrived and people are already cuing. Seems like a very popular restaurant since its a Michelins chef awardee. The food really good and there are lots good choices. Beverages and mix drinks as they are amazing.
Wonderful restaurant. provides a large assortment of Thai dishes, beverages, and desserts. I love the tasted of Tom Yum and fried rice.

I went before 5pm on December 31st and waited about 40 minutes, but during that time I was able to shop at Wonnimman and have dinner in a comfortable space. The taste of the food was generally satisfactory. However, like a hotel, there is a 10% service charge.

We never miss eating here whenever we visit Chiang Mai so it was first on our list of places to eat on our latest visit. Being Christmas, we tried to book a table but, for whatever reason, that didn’t prove possible. It’s a very busy restaurant popular with both tourists and locals but fortunately, when we arrived late afternoon, there was one table free. There’s a very good, comprehensive menu mainly featuring local produce. There’s no rush to order and the menu is illustrated and staff are always willing to advise about particular dishes. The Hang Lay curry together with Khao soi curry were very tasty indeed but all our dishes were exceptional. We ordered five savoury dishes together with jasmine rice and despite being rather full, we couldn’t resist the mango sticky rice as a sweet. Our meal for two including drinks and a 10% service charge cost just under 1400THB which we thought was good value. Very highly recommended. Read less

Ginger Farm Third try visiting GF at Nimman as it’s always crowded. The food was very clean tasting and you know they are using really good fresh local produce. Highly recommend this place but better odds dining outside of meal times to avoid a long wait for a table. Get yourself on the waiting list and then head to the lovely flea market at One Nimman to kill some time :) Their roasted pork belly was very delicious! Price for two mains and two drinks was about US$25. Read less

We dined at Ginger Farm Kitchen two nights in a row. Birthday dinner with friends and a return dinner as a couple. The food was delicious and focused on northern Thai cuisine. Service was excellent and enjoyed the vibe of diverse cultures enjoying their food.

Me and my sister have been there twice in the last two nights, might go there again tonight. We had sweat and sour fish one night then the stemmed sea bass they were both very tasteful I personally favoured the sweat and sour fish.

The atmosphere was nice, the staff was friendly and the food was okay. But not as good as you would expect from a Michelin recommended restaurant. Overall its a good restaurant for tourists, but it is not standing out.
High end Thai food, and reasonably priced. Make phone reservations or expect to wait a little while for a table for dinner.

We were lucky to get a table without reservation as the place was packed. Ordered egg omelette with pork, deep fried fish and a salad with raw fish n crab. The deep fried fish was excellent. crispy on the outside but soft and tasty flesh inside. Salad was uniquely tasty. Excellent service with quick serving and friendly waitresses. Very decent pricing.

The environment and service are very good, the waiters are very patient, and the location in the Ningman Road shopping center is very good. At noon, I ate a lot of recommended northern Thai snacks in Chiang Mai. Recommended.
A farm to table dining concept which impressed me a lot. Delicious food, friendly staffs with bright smile. One of my fav spot in town.
Welcoming atmosphere and good, quality food. Friendly staff. Better to book because it is quite popular. Absolutely recommendable
Very delicious food and waitresses are so nice, great atmosphere and we enjoy so much!we would definitely come next time!

We searched for northern Thai quisine in Chiang Mai. This restaurant came up. Did not except reservations, but were able to seat us < 30 minutes. Service was great, friendly. Location a mix of industrial and rural, sort of hip. Nice! Well done! The food was obviously different than our Bangkok style Thai flavours. Balanced. Choice in white wine was limitited to a chardonnay or a sauvignon. And the chardonney was all out. Per bottle a bit over 1100 BTH.. all in all a nice visit. NOT CHEAP.

Sorry, I was attracted by the “Michelin guide” sticker on the window and comments on the internet.... It was not good.... and very expensive... For the first time, I don't I don't understand the laudatory and even lying comments. On the internet it is said that there are Thai customers, this is false, the Thais flee this place, if they are poor it is for the price, if they are rich, it is because it is not good at all; It’s a restaurant for Chinese tourists who knew nothing about Thai cuisine. It was incredible, even the taste was horrible, a very strange taste, not good, nothing to do with sublime Thai cuisine, including the most rustic This is the first problem, the second is the horrible, abusive, disrespectful staff... My Thai friends couldn't believe it, they had never seen this in their country... Service, horrible, unpleasant, very long , unfriendly, even though we behaved perfectly. You can read in my profile that I am a more than attentive traveler... Enough is enough.... Red Card Read less
Tasty food in a nice place with a large choice of dishes. We recommand ! In a location we like very much : One Nimman - Chiang Mai.
I had red a out this restaurant but I could believe all , that was incredible , food also the decoration, but at the end all Was true

Alarm bell went off upon arrival — packed with tourists speaking in all languages except Thai. However a lovely break from the local street food full of MSG — the food here was without MSG. The taste was okay, no way near the quality and standard of food in Bangkok.

This is a popular restaurant with nice decor which made good photo backdrop. We arrived at 7pm and needed to wait for about 30mins. Not sure if they accept reservation. We liked their menu and decided to have dinner there impromptu. Food was good, more spicy than other restaurants, good service also. We thoroughly enjoyed our visit there. Their menu have some unique dishes which we haven't come across in other restaurants.

Located in a busy shopping centre this we fell upon and were delighted with everything. The menu was a perfect mix off Thai foods served to perfection. Everything tasted fresh and delicious. It was very reasonably priced and the staff we’re very friendly and helpful. Could do with a dimmer lighting switch in the evening but as the food was so good it was not an issue....

We have been eating there several times and it's always a nice place to have lunch or dinner. The staff is very helpful, attentive and kind. The decoration is chic, cheerful and tasteful. The clientele is very varied, a mixture of Thais and foreigners. The food has flavor. It is neat and well presented. It leaves a good impression. Read less

Good quality home style cooking. Order a few dishes for sharing - the stir fried morning glories were an amazing accompaniment. The place is busy, popular, noisy and lively. Atmosphere conducive to an informal evening out. Not expensive. We sat at a shared table and met an interesting family which made our evening even more enjoyable. They live in Chiang Mai and this is one of their favourite spots (they live in the neighborhood).

We live in Chiang Mai and know the restaurant scene well. We (a party of four) had high expectations for this restaurant but were very disappointed. The spring rolls were ice cold and had evidently been left for too long in the fridge. The main courses were bland and underwhelming. An order of fish and chips was almost inedible: the batter was undercooked and the chips limp. An expensive plate of fresh crab and pumpkin had two cubes of crab smothered with a heavy pumpkin sauce.
Five of us were here for dinner. The food is fresh and tasty and prices are very reasonable. Service is also good. A good place for dinner in Chiang Mai
It is a nice cozy place in a great location. Nice many with veg options. Food is ok. Service quite bad, i think there is a lack of management!!

Ginger Farm Kitchen is a popular eatery located in the heart of town. The atmosphere is relaxed, with plenty of rustic charm, including exposed brick walls and wooden furniture. However, it's not the decor that people come for; rather, it's the food. Unfortunately, my experience at Ginger Farm Kitchen wasn't quite what I had hoped for. The dishes themselves were okay- I had the Tom Kah Gai , pork belly and the prawn balls- but they lacked any real enthusiasm or care in their preparation. I was also disappointed by the lack of general lack of innovative ingredients. It didn't have the same home-cooked taste that many restaurants strive for, and it seemed like the staff were more preoccupied with getting through their shifts as quickly as possible than actually providing good customer service. Overall, my experience at Ginger Farm Kitchen left me feeling somewhat underwhelmed. I expected hearty and lovingly prepared than what was on offer here. I wouldn't recommend this eatery to anyone looking for that organic Thai culinary experience. Read less

First of all, I started to doubt how Michelin rated or recommended restaurants - based on decorations? Or it is something like marketing business? Their food and drinks are so average … nothing impressive…. I would prefer food at Jing Jai Market. Also, at the beginning, we were assigned to sit at the corner and that area with some weird smell and a big fly dancing around us … thanks to the waiters there, they allowed us to change our table. The staff there are helpful. If you dont have a high expectations, it is ok to visit. Just dont have too much fancy abt the word Michelin. Read less

I was extremely disappointed with my visit to this restaurant for the following reasons: 1. terrible customer service - many staff but not customer focussed. It was hard to get their attention for any service eg when we requested for a few more sachets of chilli powder, they only brought us one. When we had finished our meal and wanted to make payment, it took some time before we could get any attention from the staff. 2. we were very keen to try two of their signature dishes but were told they were not available for that day. You would have thought that signature dishes would always be on the menu. 3. I had been eating street food at markets and local cafes throughout my 12 day holiday and did not encounter any food poisoning but after eating at this Michelin starred restaurant, I had bad food poisoning throughout the night! Overall a most disappointing experience:( Read less

Thank goodness I realised I keyed in Ginger Farm Chiang Mai instead and was about 2/3 towards the wrong way. First time taking Grab in Thailand ;) Situated in a very young and vibrant area of this town and this happened to be a public holiday, the place was full but we managed to get a table around 8.15pm. The food was fantastic and it was definitely Michelin standard. We have prawn cake, grilled beef, pork belly, omelette and morning glory. I loved the crispy pork belly, you can literally hear the crust if I was a mukbang eater. Reasonable price, USD15-20+ per person including drinks Read less

Had the starter (leaf with fillings), curry prawn, steamed fish, pork belly. Wow. Everything tasted fresh and delicious. They do have a farm with a limited lunch menu. This is the place to come for the full thing.

Saw this recommended in Lonely planet guide and well worth a visit. Beautiful flavours, excellent quality ingredients, good presentation (we had thai beef curry, prawn and egg with vermicelli noodles) My passionfruit/apple/pineapple juice was delicious The service was excellent About s$35 (Sing dollars) / TBH 850 for 2 dishes, rice and 2 fruit juices - worth it for the quality and service Read less
We ate here while visiting Chiang Mai,a family of 7 off us and all was really impressed with all the dishes we had,can highly recommend

We stumbled across this place by chance and seeing how busy it was, and all the awards on the door, booked for the following night. The food was absolutely delicious, very hard to choose as all looked so tasty. Loads of fresh fish and healthy feeling foods. Highly recommend

In spite of their Michelin reputation and earning the world renowned batch for few years, we faced a very poor service from their waiter. Not to mention that our advance reservation was not recorded; but the restaurant was not to busy and they can find tables for us. The foods were tasty with fresh ingredients. Their northern local vegetable dip was delicious. Due to their menu didn’t highlight their signature nor popular or highly chief’s recommendations, we didn’t know which dish earned the Michelin batch or recommendation. We are not sure that we can recommend nor revisit again.

Fantastic food! We went twice during our holiday as it was so good. We had the sea bass, veg pad thai, veg green curry, sticky rice, spring rolls, mango sticky rice and sour mango dessert. There is plenty to eat for vegetarians and the restaurant is also very clean. Great decor as well. In London the meal would have cost £120 for two whereas it cost ~ £35 incl drinks. Would highly recommend

We went to this restaurant on a whim after reading some reviews. It was a bit of a hike from old town (we were walking), but well worth it! Food was great! Drinks were great! We're just disspointed that we didn't go back a second time!
This is a nicely decorated Thai restaurant with a good range of choice for food and beverage. The quality is high and the price is reasonable. It is overall good value for money.
This is one of the best restaurants in Chiang Mai. Always a good quality and most ingredients come straight from their farm in Sarapee

We happened to be out and about around one Nimman and wanted somewhere nice to have lunch. We check on the door, and it was a TripAdvisor awarded location so where satisfied that we would have a great meal at this location. We are not disappointed with the food, service and the styling of the restaurant. We had a drink each and they where massive. They did a great job of getting our orders correct and my wife had Pad Thai and I had the Tom Yum soup (Seafood) and what a great meal. They had WIFI and we had a great time in the restaurant and was confident to return again if where happened to be close. Great meal, very reasonable with a quick service and great drinks.

I liked the Thai food here as they use organic and locally produced ingredients. The meat and vegetable are from the local farms. The Khao Soi here was very delicious and so were the other cooked dishes. The drinks menu was good too as I liked the fruits shakes and smoothies.

This was our second visit and once again we weren’t disappointed. Despite few foreign tourists, it is advisable to book a table as this restaurant is very popular with Thai customers. There’s certainly a good atmosphere and the decor of the restaurant is very attractive and add to that some mouth watering food, and a good evening can be anticipated. There is a very comprehensive menu but there’s no rush to order and it’s certainly worth taking time in selecting the dishes. We particularly enjoyed the crispy pork belly and the wild betal leaf wrap. One really good thing here which is rarely found in Thai restaurants is that organic brown rice is available. The stand out dish though was the beef curry. Service was very efficient and friendly and the Covid precautions taken by staff were more than adequate. It was a pity that some customers couldn’t take the simplest of precautions. We thought prices were very reasonable…we had four dishes plus rice and some mango sticky rice along with drinks and we paid 1100 THB which included 10%service charge. Highly recommended. . Read less

Good range of food options, my boyfriend enjoyed his food. I would not recommend for anyone who is gluten free. The menu in the restaurant has covered up many of the gf symbols on dishes I’m assuming because they aren’t actually gf. I ordered a rice dish that was still labelled gf and confirmed with the waitress however I was very unwell that evening. So good food and a nice place if you do not have any dietary requirements.

At this time of Covid restrictions, one may think places are careful, and make the most of the few customers they get. One plain rice had human hair in it, my chicken khao Soi was not well cooked (very pink inside) and had to send it back. I could see inside the kitchen from our table and noticed 2 of the cooks not wearing face mask. Even after I told the waitress, one remained without face mask. They did apologize for the food and gave us a discount, but I will not go back

What a find this restaurant was! Depending where you are staying, it might be necessary to get some form of transport to get there but we used Grab which was very efficient and cheap. The restaurant is very popular with Thais which, in itself, is a good recommendation and to be on the safe side, it's well worth booking a table. There is a mouthwatering menu and the prices are very reasonable. We had four dishes plus rice and water and the bill totaled 885 baht. We would certainly recommend the Hang Lay curry and the crispy pork belly ( absolutely delicious). Added to those dishes were papaya salad and a wild betal leaf wrap. Staff are very friendly and helpful and leave the menu rather than stand there waiting for your order so there's no rush whatsoever. Once the food is ordered, we didn't have to wait too long for it to arrive. In addition to serving food, there is also a small shop which is worth exploring. Would we eat there again...definitely! Read less
Ginger Farm Kitchen is one of the good healthy restaurant. The quality of food and the service is very good. The taste is so so.

We ate here twice because the food was so delicious and sustainably raise. It is my favorite restaurant in Chiang Rai. I love the sautéed ferns and steamed sea bass. While many of the restaurants the promote organic and sustainable foods are expensive the Ginger Farm Kitchen is very reasonable making it available to more people .

Great ambience & positive vibes, the staff is friendly and attentive. Food serving is small and pricey by Thai standard, but it is well worth it! Fresh ingredients source directly from farms, the Chinese Kailan with crackling pork belly is to die for, so deliciously prepared it tasted like my mom's cooking. I am certainly return to try other dishes.
This place serves delicious organic food. It has a diverse menu and the staff was lovely. We will certainly come back.
A big choice of tasty meals with local farm sourced ingredients. Also an impresive list of non-alcoholic juice cocktails, smoothies etc. Very good customer service!

The decor and ethos to this restaurant is straight out of hipster Western culture. But it’s excellently executed. Good food, great produce. Absolutely order the Son-in-Law Eggs! You will not be disappointed. The One Nimman Mall is actually packed with amazing looking restaurants, designer shops and instagramable lighting - if that’s your thing.
Great food, very fresh and deliciously prepares. One point deduction because the reservation system is unreliable and seems to be outdated. Tested it on several occasions but it doesn't seem to work.

Very good food in a lively setting and excellent value for money. However it works on the principle that dishes are for sharing and will go out as soon as each is ready. It does not allow for one person being a meat eater and the other not. My partner finished his meal several minutes before mine arrived. Luckily it was so tasty it was almost worth the wait - organic acacia and prawns on glass noodles.

In a hype location. SURPRISE. Local ingredients and mostly organic. But DELICIOUSLY cooked. Each dish has its distinct flavour and taste. Book or go early. Get there to get your fill of northern Thai cookings here.

Simple and pleasant decorated, service is friendly and prompt. The supplies are mainly organic, no MSG which is a big plus. The food is tasty and delicious, the Tom Yam Kong and fried Kai Nam are done to perfection all flavours are in harmony. Yum The Roast Pork is with crispy skin but meat is on dry side. The eggs that are from free range chickens, well, we can't tell the difference from normal eggs. Still, worth recommending and a 4.5 Stars from me.

This restaurant is recommended by a local. It is in the modern district in a premise with many different restaurants. There are many choices in the menu. Food is served promptly and they all taste good. The price is slightly above average but it seems to be a very popular one among locals.

We spotted this when we were walking around in Chiang Mai and were attracted by the good reviews advertised in the window. We are so glad we choose to visit this restaurant ! We did not book, but were very lucky to get in as when we sat down the restaurant was basically full and the turned away many people after we sat down as there were no tables available. The restaurant is lively and has a great ambience. When the menu arrived it was massive with so much choice - we had a hard time picking but we tried to choose plates which would be unique to the North of Thailand with some favourites thrown in. Due to the amount of plates that we ordered (there was only two of us) we were actually moved to a bigger table !! So my advice would be to ask how big the dishes are first. We did manage to eat maybe 75% of the food because it was so tasty but we were extremely full. We stayed after our meal for a time to drink a couple more cocktails which was lovely and we were able to see the small square behind the restaurant where people were congregating and there was a dance class taking place. Overall we had a fantastic experience at Ginger Farm Kitchen where the food really stood out. It was relatively expensive for Chiang Mai prices but we did not mind paying this for the quality. I would recommend this restaurant and would return. Read less
Great and very nice staff. The food is very fresh but bit overpriced. Perfect for a day trip together with the family.
Wide range of food choice and after a few cafe hopping Just a bit a shame that I didn’t find this place earlier when we found this already quite full. Still... we can’t resist to order the desserts 😂
Great place to eat. Has many really healthy and different food options. Nice ambience and helpful staff.

This place has a very nice ambience. Good range of food variety. Taste good overall. Remember to check with the waitress / waiter on the food portion when you order. Note the price is on the high side.

Don't let the name fool you.... this place is great for all lovers of food. A great selection of meals using a wide range of produce. Beautiful decor within the restaurant. My advice would be to book in advance... is very busy.

You can never really go wrong with Thai food, and you can’t get any more correct-er than with this place - gorgeous interiors, really awesome reasonably priced food and instagram friendly lighting. 👍👍👍

The food served was excellent, great use of herbs and spices, very balanced. The portion is very generous and price is very reasonable too. We ordered a Thai green curry, some local fern and Kailan with roasted pork. It was an awesome experience. Will definitely visit again.

This place is situated in One Nimman so it's a nice place to stop over for dinner or lunch if you are shopping in the area. Food here is good but for us it lacks spice as we are spice enthusiasts. This is the reason why I'm not giving this place 5 stars as we told them we wanted spicy food but they did not meet our request. The tom yum was good and flavourful. Som tum was good but again, was not spicy at all as som tum should be. The crispy pork belly was just okay, the skin was not as crispy as should be and took a while to arrive. Would just avoid the pork belly. The ambience of the place was lovely albeit a little noisy so you might consider that if you are looking for a quiet meal. Drinks were good as well. Overall, I'll praise this restaurant for its presentation on food, some of the food had good flavour, the service was good. Perhaps my palate is just stronger. Read less

3 of us ordered a Thai green curry which was nice however the rice came out a while before the curry and started to go cold. However one of my friends ordered the chicken fried rice and it was very flavourless and very small portion compared to other places in Thailand. we ordered french fries and ended up getting pork cracklings which we do not eat pork, it was able to be changed but the communication barrier between was not helpful. To conclude would reccomend the Thai curry

We actually went there twice, not something we usually do but the food was really nice and we love the idea that everything is grown organically and locally The Thai food is varied and well prepared, the food is served super fast Prices are good, not the cheapest but not expensive either. Lovely deco in the restaurant, definitely worth going Read less

Love the food and the environment. The restaurant is not super crowded. We were lucky that we were immediately seated. We were a party of 2, went there for dinner and ordered 4 dishes. The portion was just good. We went back again for lunch the other day. My no. 1 choice is the crispy pork belly!

If you just looking outside with its beautiful deco inside, you probably guess it is expensive. That's not true, it is pretty reasonable though. We tried the Passion fruit smoothie, that was good, and the Crispy roasted pork was good too. PS: book ahead is advised as it is always busy.

This place seems like it would expensive due to its location off the busy and fashionable Nimman Road near a mall, but the prices are very reasonable. My boyfriend and I came here 3 times and split classic Thai dishes and freshly made juices. We tried seafood pineapple curry, pulled pork and kale, and cashew chicken. The mango sticky rice is to die for!

Gorgeous place, so fantastically decorated with colours and objects and all the details. Place is full with locals and tourists. Fresh and organic food prepared with attention. Suggested Salmon salad very good Farm chicken tasty Aubergine starter good but too spicy Fish and chips for our 5y old daughter not as we expected, two big fillet of fish with fries, our daughter didn’t like it all. That was a mistake. Suggested drink, beer passion fruit cooler was excellent. Overall lovely experience. Be aware there is no toilet in the restaurant, you need to go behind inside the One Nimman complex. Read less

We went twice to the ginger farm restaurant during our visit to Chiang Mai. Situated in the hip part of town, at the entrance of a cool gallery with loads of upscale craft stores and coffee bars, ginger farm is worth the trip. We thought that the drinks menu ( fruit cocktails, refreshing homemade lemonades, cocktails and beers) was well thought through and of outstanding quality. All the food that we sampled was fresh, simple yet refined. Service was polite, attentive and costumer orientated. Prices are fair(not the cheapest in the city but definitely worth every penny.) Both of us have extensive travel experience and both worked over many year in high class European hospitality. Take our word for it, you won’t be disappointed. Read less

I was here with a group of friends and although the service was a bit slow it was good. Each of us enjoyed the dishes we got. The fruity drinks are especially good. I had the lychee one and it was amazing. I also had the crispy roasted pork which was very good. Very clean atmosphere and air-conditioned.
Really pretty little restaurant, has a nice chilled atmosphere. Great decor and food presentation. The prices are a bit more expensive but the food and smoothies are really tasty!

Some of the best food we have had in Thailand. The price is a bit on the higher side but it is worth it. The food was amazing. They are also great option for people with food allergies. They have gluten free options. I highly recommend it.

The restaurant is located in a touristic area. Food was ok but not that wow to me. Service was good but not that great because they didn’t pay attention to details for what I asked not to put in my food. Decoration and atmosphere of the restaurant is nice. The price is quite high but they position themselves as a healthy and farm to table restaurant as use of organic product.

Came here for lunch while exploring Chiang Mai. I had the mango and passionfruit smoothie which was absolutely amazing so flavourful and fresh, and the vegan koi San noodles which were delicious. My boyfriend similarly had a great meal. Lovely atmosphere and good vibes. Although it is a bit more expensive than other places I believe you get what you pay for if you’re willing to fork out a bit more for one meal.

We came here for lunch while exploring Nimman Road. Like many other things in the area, it's more expensive than usual. The restaurant is well decorated and is part of a larger new mixed use development with lots of shops (again, expensive shops)

Went for dinner at our Concierge’s recommendation. Restaurant is an affiliate of House of Ginger (Ancient Town). We decided to try this “farm to city” restaurant located at One Ninman as we were told Ninman is Chiang Mai’s treny neighbourhood. The restaurant & food did not disappoint. Service attentive & prices reasonable (by our standards). You should ask hotel to book a table as the restaurant is pretty busy.

We went here twice. First visit - everything was fine given we got in without queuing (10pm). We are lucky as we are the last group to be accommodated after that they refuse even it is not yet 11pm. Food was good. Warm and right spiciness for a tourist. We asked for no sugar however it seems some of their dishes still have sugar. They specified in their menu to advise if there are any dietary restrictions but they dont deliver. Second Visit - we queued and gave our local number BUT the lady misplaced our number and never called us. Good thing we went back to check. This time our food was not delivered on time. They have to give each dish only after we follow-up. Food was not warm and spicy. Read less
Food is bland. Waitresses demanded I pay the bill on 2 occasions even though I paid it already. Not for authentic thai food lovers. Beware. Tourist trap.

I honestly can’t remember the last time I ate in a mall and thought wow! This place really surprised me. We shared the hang lei, crispy pork belly, Chiang Rai chicken, raw shrimps and every single dish was absolutely amazing! Hang Lei is my favourite Thai dish ever and these guys nailed it! The crispy pork is to die for, crispy, juicy and so tasty! The raw shrimps were amazingly juicy and fresh. I cannot fault any of it. Service was great, pricing was good and the whole thing just hit the mark! Read less

It was our last day of our trip and we were looking for a nice meal before we left Thailand . This resturant has very nice lovely ambience, homely and warm feel. The food was amazing. Every dish was a surprise. Very tasty, colourful and tempting. The fish was so yummy, even the plain rice had a wide range of variety. After the meal we ordered desert, jackfruit sticky rice. It was my first encounter with this desert and it turns out to be awesome. Glad we came. Nice great food from farm to table experience.

Excellent food - I would recommend the prawn balls and the mango and lychee smoothie. Food is ethically sourced and service is brilliant. It’s not the cheapest restaurant in Chiang Mai - our bill was around 900 THB for two people- but worth every penny!
Very good and refined Thai food. Organic ingredients. Love this place and the deco. Service level is average but food is good. Order their Thai style Green curry sauce. It’s good.

Nice restaurant in the hangout area, One Nimman, the food is very tasty and fresh vegetable. They serve both food and dessert with the reasonable price. If you come during the meal time, reservation is recommended.

Visited Ginger Farm Kitchen for an early dinner (only because it supposed to be packed). Walked right in around 6pm. The restaurant is on the ground floor of 1 Nimman right on the main drag. It's definitely a nice atmosphere and resembles a country farm house (think Laura Ashley decor). Albeit the past reviews, the staff were pretty nice but like everywhere else in Thailand, they tend to look at their cell phones A LOT. We had the all Thai appetizers; the lemongrass sausage, pork skins and the wings were the best. My wife had the whole fried fish and I had the laarb salad. It was very delicious! To me it's an upscale version of the yummy street food. Lots of vloggers in the restaurant that night doing their thing. Restrooms outside in the complex; expect to walk up 3-4 flights of stairs but they are super clean. Quick wifi. Overall we had a nice dinner and decently priced. Strolled in to the square right next to Ginger Farm to watch the evening light show afterwards and get some gelato. Overall it was a nice evening with nice tasting food and it didn't break the bank. Good service. Read less

We went for dinner, attracted by the nice setting and nice pictures in the menu. We order the fried fish in sour and sweet sauce - ok good. The pumpkin with crab is definitely average, the picture in the menu definitely better. The prawn cake took some time to come and we were already full - average. The mojito passion is very average. The smoothie passion was good. If u want dessert, order after you finished you main courses otherwise it may come all together, what's happened to next table, weird things. Read less

We have visited One Nimmarn shopping mall and I get a recommendation to visit this restaurant. When walking we find the interior design is very cozy cool and romantic. The food may get 4 stars with a bit better than an average compared to others, will be back again.
Great healthy food, lot of vegetarian options, very friendly staff and beautifully decorated restaurant.
I love all design and decorating restaurant, the ginger farm make me feeling like at home and staffs very nice and good service , speedy service for customers , and food yummy and fast service
I think thsy cook tasty for Northern local food but dish of Som Tum ( Papaya salad) is not recommended by me. By the way, foreigners might like som tum taste like this.

The interior is very beautiful with wooden benches and flowers. The menu is with pictures and in English. Service was quick and nice but the food not more than average but kind of expensive for what we got. The salmon burger was super juicy tho
Great location. Lovely decoration. The food tastes good, but quite expensive. Since there are many customers, the service is a bit slow.
Very disappointed with the food at this restaurant; I know it caters mainly for tourists and perhaps they wouldn't know much different but as an expat local I wouldn't recommend it.
Love the presentation of the food and the decor in the restaurant. Went on a Monday night and it was super busy. Everything we ordered we enjoyed and it was reasonably priced.

This is such a gorgeous restaurant. The staff is very friendly - excellent service!! The food was delicious with a huge menu. Would definitely recommend coming here. It’s right in the middle of a great shopping area too.

The décor and location of Ginger Farm Kitchen is lovely. However, last night it took one complaint and 30 minutes to be served a bottle of soda and a juice. It took two complaints and one hour waiting to be served our food. The curry was watery, had no taste and the pumpkin was not cooked enough. If they want to keep on attracting customers, they certainly need to improve what they are doing. I'll give them a 2nd chance, but not a 3rd one.

It was my first visit to One Nimman, and spotted this restaurant while I was exploring the area! The deco was nice, concept was from farm to city! This was a shop + restaurant and so happy that they had a veggie menu! I ordered a healthy salad with kale, beetroot, avocado etc
fresh and delicious food, moderate price, excellent service, very professional and friendly staff. Located very close to the U Nimman hotel.

Visited latish in the evening as it was after 9pm, the décor is great, well lite without being sear your retinas bright and has a slight chilled out beach vibe. Having been given menu’s they came back and asked about drinks orders and if we were ready to order. The selection of dishes is varied but also firmly a Thai restaurant, the pictures used showed great presentation of food and made the dishes all look so tempting. Once we order food and drinks and it was not long before the drinks arrived and the food started to arrive soon after, as is often with Thai places the food arrives randomly based on what cooks quickest which is not a bad thing as means everything arrives piping hot and freshly cooked. One by one the food arrived and I have to say was that the presentation of the food was as good if not better than the pictures in the menu. Everything single dish great and the chicken wings were amazing. All the ingredients are locally, organic and grown on the farm run by the restaurant. This was my first meal of 2 weeks in Thailand and have to say it was the stand out meal of the trip, the place is nice the staff friendly and helpfully the food was looked amazing and tasted as good as it looked. The cost of the meal was a bit higher than eating out at a restaurant in Thailand would normally be but was excellent considering the quality of the food and the taste. Without a second of hesitation I would recommend visiting here. Read less

The restaurant is located at the newly opened One Nimman mall. The restaurant includes a gift shop which sells beautiful plastic ware among other small objects. It is a farm to table restaurant using very fresh ingredients - lots of greens - and everything was deliciously prepared. It was the last meal of our trip and one of the best.

Lived in Chiang Mai for many year but this restaurant didnot fail to impress me... Everything i ordered tastes so good... If i had to, i would sell my kidney to eat here again... If u r looking for a traditional northern thai restaurant, look no further

Located in the heart of Nimman, a charming and delicious Thai cuisine is prepared with extremely fresh ingredients and served with a superb attentive staff, Busy place but doesn't take away from the charm and atmosphere created in this popular new restaurant. Went 3 times for dinner in 6 days. A bit expensive but worth every THB.

This is by far one of the best restaurants I've ever been to. I work with restaurants back in Europe, so I like to think I have pretty high standards. And this place just blows all the other restaurants in Thailand out of the water. The creations, the tastes, the presentation, the whole concept of the place - is absolutely breathtaking. I will be telling all my friends and family about this place without a shadow of a doubt. AND PLEASE GOD, WHEN YOU GO, TRY THE FLOURLESS CHOCOLATE CAKE!!!!!!!!! Incredible!!!!! Read less
Great food, beautiful setting, lots of interesting stuff for kids to do, even good cocktails. Great any time other than April when heat and smoke make outside eating less appealing.

The Ginger Farm Kitchen is a newish place in the new One Nimman development at the top of Nimmanheiman Road. It is, I think, run by the same people who run the excellent Ginger / House restaurant near the moat in the Old City. The food is very excellent and fresh, the service is fast and attentive, and the relaxed atmosphere is excellent.

Had dinner here today and all the dishes were delicious with authentic Thai taste. I highly recommend the fresh spring rolls and corn somtum. The drinks are really healthy and delicious - many different fresh pressed juices and smoothies. The staff are very attentive and the service was very good for a Thai restaurant.
5/5 very unique and old style restaurant with very special menu and taste , like it very much , fell like back to 1930’s , strongly recommend.

the place is located in Nimman road(near One Nimman soi)....it's a very cosy place(look like you are in Thai house for eat).....we had some small "problem" but except from this problem everything was great!! i begin with the "problem":we went in the place (in the morning)and we book a table for our dinner in the night....we book for 4 people and we go there at 8.00 pm we arrined there in 7.55 pm and we saw that some other people was sitting in our table! when we ask why is that happening,they told us:"do not worry...they leaving in 5 minutes...they already ask for the bill"....and theuy offer us another table (so we do not stand) until they other people leave....to tell you the truth,i got mad about this...the other company left at 8.15 pm...so we waited in another table 15 minutes....after they leave they clean up the table and we transfer there i know some people say that is not important but what is the meaning of booking a table???it's not to wait !!! anyway....except that incident everything was great...the food very good(this is a Thai reaturant...so no western food)...the decor also nice(cosy)....and the prices very good(i could say almost cheap)....,,very good service from the staff also..... the place is closing 10.30 pm(if i am not mistaken)...so you betetr go early to eat your Thai Food TIP:I tried the new Chang Beer Champagne(it is beer in a bottle of Champagne....the staff told us that is made the same way the make champagne...and of course more expensive than the normal beer) Guys....it was the same taste like normal Chang Beer!!!!so...you do not get fooled and order this!!!order normal Chang beer better!!! Read less
This shop is located in the stylish restaurant district of Chiang Mai. The food is reasonable and delicious. It is a laid-back atmosphere, but there are differences in how staff respond.
Great ambience and not too crowded. Food was reasonably priced and above average. Will go back regularly and try other stuff.

Located opposite One Nimman, this is a family run restaurant serving homecooked northern cuisines. Ordered the green curry chicken, fried crispy chicken, fried noodle n vege. Tasty and cheap but portions are small.

Reading other reviews I had to double check this was the same place we ate in this evening. Same place but definitely not same same. First warning sign was a menu with all the Thai food named as Thai but written in English. No Thai writing at all. My wife is Thai and was given the same menu. Two Thai dishes requested, nearly begged to be spicy. Both bland and boring. Pad Kra Pao and Gaeng Khiao Waan Gai. Two staples. How can these be made so poorly? No spice, overcooked vegetables, thin watery soup. Pork tasted burnt. Rice steamed long time. But only 500 baht. So if you want cheap and tasteless, go ahead. I won't be back. Read less
Date_Written: in English. No Thai writing at all. My wife is Thai and was given the same menu.

Detail: Visited as was unable to get into another local restaurant but had a really nice set menu here for 2 Soup was spicy, but blended ell with the rest of the meal. Great value..l

We're back to CM after 3 years and happy to report that Hong Tauw is just as good as it was in 2019. Delicious, authentic Thai and Northern Thai food at a very good price. Khao Soi...my favourite then and now. Pleasant antique surroundings and nice service.
Picked up some takeaway as convenient to where we are staying...and so delicious. Highly recommend for authentic Northern Thai food

We had khao soi chicken (60 thb) the local specialty and it was delicious! It is a lovely restaurant with quick service too. I would recommend to anyone who is coming to Chiang Mai and wants to try northern Thai cuisine.
Delicious and properly prepared traditional Thai dishes in a cosy atmosphere. A nice change from the busy and now tacky Nimanhaman area.

Ate widely in the Nimmanheim area and this restaurant called us back several times. Food was tasty and there were some interesting variations we hadn't found elsewhere. Service was fine and the bill always reasonable.

Coming back here after almost two years because the last time they served us wrong dish and did not bother to come back to apologize, also found hair in one of the dish. This time the food is not as good as I remembered. They are nice to make vegetarian but fail to make it well. Do not order tom kha as it’s like having a sweet coconut soup with boiled vegetables.
Decent local food but slow service. Feels like home cooked food. Soft shell crabs were decent as was the usual Tom yam goong. No mango sticky rice though which was a disappointment.

We were fortunate to have our Thai friend with us at this restaurant, every dish was ordered by our friend and it was GOOD!!! Somehow going as a tourist with locals ordering food for you is different when you try to order on your own. Truly Blessed. This is a good place to have your meals when in Chiang Mai
Went There with my friends fo lunch. I was recommended this place by locals so it was no supprise to me that the dishes were perfect.

Lovely little old-style Thai restaurant on Nimmanhaemin road with delicious food and excellent service. Their green curry and basil dishes are superb, much like most of their items. The nice thing is they allow BYO wine, unlike other similar places, and adjust their cooking to suit those who prefer non-seafood-based cooking.

Located along the main Nimmanhaemin road, Hong Tauw Inn is a very accessible and reasonably-priced restaurant serving a good range of Thai food. With excellent food and brilliant service, it is a place not to be missed. The quaint array of wall and grandfather clocks adds to the allure of the place. Worth visiting again and again.

We found this restaurant by chance while looking for another one and we ended up having dinner there 2 of our 3 nights in Chiang Mai!! The restaurant has a vintage feel and the menu is extensive! We tried many items in our 2 nights and they were all really delicious! I agree with other reviewers that the portions are on the smaller side but the taste really compensates for it! Highly recommend

Very bad service. As I was browsing TripAdvisor for recommendations, the waiter accuse me of taking photo. I show him my phone and he just walked away without apologising. Avoid this restaurant. There are better quality good elsewhere

Found this place by chance when I was walking back to the hotel. Dinner was great, everything, starting with the lemongrass juice and milk tea. The fried softshell crab with garlic was great, my SO ordered it and even though I'm not a fan of fried food I reallyyy liked it. Pad thai was delicious, aswell as the coconut soup. Read less
A group of 8 of us went there tonight. We chose 8 dishes, and then ate "family style," sharing all them. Not a complaint to be heard, as we all loved every dish.

Having been introduced to this quaint restaurant by some Thai friends, I brought my cousins to eat there for our first lunchl. They were so impressed and delighted that although I had booked a good (more posh!) restaurant for our final night they asked to return to the Inn. And these are foodies! A visit to Chiang Mai would be incomplete without a meal and Hong Tauw Inn. It is good to go early or to book.

We came here three years ago and am pleased to say the food is just as good the owners even remembered us. This is very close to the Maya Lifestyle Mall. it is cool inside and charmingly rustic in a faded colonial manner with large wooden old not working clocks on the wall. As long as this restaurant is here we will visit, hope you do to

Delicious, fresh and well prepared Thai food. Very large menu selection with excellent service at bargain prizes. My husband and I had Mee Krob, Stir Fried Morning Glory with Oyster Sauce, Kanom Jeen Saow Narm (fermented noodles and shrimp in a coconut cream sauce), and Kaeng Phed Waan Kai (chicken in red curry and jasmine rice). Delicious textures and flavors all at a cost of less than $20!

We love this restaurant! Delicious, authentic Thai food at a very reasonable price in a lovely setting with nice service. Northern Thai Section on the menu. We love the Khao Soi and the green curry with chicken, especially. It's very popular so service can be slow, depending on when you go. Worth a visit!

We were brought to this restaurant by 2 Thai friends and it was a wonderful experience of Northern Thai cuisine. Or course we left the ordering to them, but attest that it was a treat to our palates. It's not a touristy place and I am not sure when I next go there, which I will, we will be able to order the more unique dishes. But I'm sure we will not be disappointed.

This is a restaurant worth visiting. It was recomended by our thai friend. We love the food. The kway tiao, glass noodles, sausages are good. We ordered quite a big spread. One of the dish we had is some fluffy fried fish which I find it unique and tasty. Good service too. Price reasonable.

As usual favoring authentic local food over corrupted fusion it was nice to find Hong Tauw Inn, a stone-throw away from the very commercial One Nimman complex. Very efficient and attentive staff, nice simple but cozy environment, clean, inexpensive and offering an extensive menu including quite a few Northern Thai specials. Hong Tauw is mainly frequented by locals and I take it as a very good sign. We enjoyed our evening very much and looking forward to going dining there again. C/ Read less

Have enjoyed two dinners here in the past week. On the main Nimman drag on the north end, but one of the more civilized, peaceful, welcoming places on this street. Antiques, a clock collection, and late 19th-early 20th century architecture set the stage for this one-of-a-kind family run establishment that also serves takeout to much of the neighborhood. Excellent service, menu presentation and accuracy, and a full range of curries, noodle and rice dishes, as well as some nice seafood preparations. Quiet. Cold beer. and feel free to linger with friends or when its pouring out. They also do great coffees.
Food is nice and people there are nice too. The serving size is small and we had to order more because the food is so delicious 😀

When you walk past this restaurant and look in the windows , you are tempted to eat there by the decor, it looks like a living room. The menu outside has reasonable prices , so we tried it . English is spoken a little here, like in most tourist restaurants in Chiang Mai. There were the usual Thai favourites, but I fancied a change , something different, so we ordered the menu for 2 - five plates for 600 baht. The courses were quite small, more of a taster than a meal. All were well cooked but just as if you made it at home from Tesco lotus ingredients . Nothing tasted good but it was ok. Given the huge number of restaurants around, we probably won't eat there again. Just average Thai food Read less
The interior is quite beautiful, giving a homy feeling. The menu has variety. The food is good, authentic home-style cooking. Watermelon juice is plain though.
I enjoyed the quietness of the restaurant, after a busy day sightseeing. The food was tasty. Good Thai fare for lunch. Thank you!
Great food and atmosphere in authentic established restaurant near Maya Centre Been going there for 12yrs and always delivers!

We got hold of our drinks after 30 minutes only after reminding each of the waiters. We never got the food though, terrible service, restaurant manager was busy with accounting, so there was no management of any sort. Ambiance was gloomy, quite pricey for a mediocre restaurant.

This restaurant looks authentic and rustic Thai. The menu looks good and prices reasonable but that is where the positives ended. My companion and I were literally attacked by mosquitoes. When my friend complained, the owner smiled and brought out a mosquito repellent! So that is an indication that it is a normal affair! We ordered mango salad and 3 other main dishes and rice. The salad was really nice but what a small serve! Then rice came..was literally a couple of spoonfuls on a small dessert plate - not your normal dinner plate!! In fact we discover that every single dish comes on a small dessert plate and the serve is very small. For 2 of us, the food was miserly! We had a dish that was the morning glory fried with fish sauce - in asian countries we call that KangKong. There was hardly a few strands of this. Penang Beef curry was also served in the small dessert plate and hardly any beef visible and hardly any sauce. The 3rd main dish of deep fried softshell crab - we had to remind the owner 2x. The last time was when we had already finished our meal! All the dishes were very nice but the service and portion really needs improving. We paid 600bht for 2 which is 300 bht each ...ie AUD 12+ or USD 9+ and with 4 very miserly portioned dishes, we came away still not satisfied. Compared to the night before where we paid AUD 20 each in a table of 6 (at the Kao Pla), we had so much to eat in a fine dinning restaurant, this Hong Tauw really pales significantly in comparison! I would recommend this to anyone. The portion sizes on dessert plates are more suitable for single diner. Read less

It has been some decades since I have walked out in protest of hopeless service and disgusting food. We went there due to good reviews yet were appalled. The place is smelly and untidy inside and we waited ages for service which seemed to take ten people. The food was brought out in random order, things were incorrect or forgotten and the food was truly inedible. It looked like reheated leftovers from a year ago and smelled dreadful. We ended up walking out and just ate at the hotel. Unless you are a masochist avoid like the plague. Actually this is even worse than the plague...

Make the short trip out of the centre of Chiang Mai to trendy Nimanhaemin Road, only ignore the Tuk Tuk driver, it's at the northern end of the road, in a raised arcade on the west side of the street near Maya Mall. I was eager to go back here after happening on the place five years ago, it was better this time. Food equally as good, and wide ranging, but this time I had the mouth watering anticipation before getting there! You'll need some time to choose your dishes, there are 14 pages a la cartel and another 4 pages of set menus. I had the pineapple curry with shrimp, (good digestive pineapple), and a winged bean salad on the side. A starter and a large Chang beer too, all super tasty and served with but a smile, the whole lot was only Thai Baht 420. And they're happy to tell you what's in the sauce, (I had to know what adorned the Wing Bean Salad). Old school friendly ambience, goes well with the vintage decor, it's especially charming in the front half. You won't be disappointed. Read less
Good family owned Thai restaurant. The dishes are authentic northern Thai dish. We had a great lunch here I will let pictures do the talking.

Perfect place for a casual lunch in the trendy area along Nimanhaemin Road. Several friends shared a wide variety of very inexpensive, but well-prepared, Thai selections. Not too fancy, but wholesome and delicious.
We went there on a Saturday night, the restaurant was pretty crowded but not full. Arrive is professional and northern Thai food was very good. Prices are reasonable. I do recommend.

this must be the single most consistently excellent restaurant in the chiang mai, no, let me rephrase, the north of thailand. we returned there after a 2 years absence, may of the same staff, friendly and efficient, and the same incredibly authentic thai cuisine, not watered down, spicy and prepared to perfection. had to go back the next evening, another group of friends, all old asia hands and 2 from neighboring countries, everyone was full of praise. if you have missed your KHAO SOY at lunch, here you can get one for dinner (i know, i know, but then i had to introduce it to my friends that were leaving the next morning and haven't had it) try the northern specials - they have most of them and also dishes from isan and central Th. Ask the service for specials and recommendations. Read less

Hongteuaw food is culturally old style Thai food (Ahan Boran..in Thai)... have been a customer for 25 years...and never dissappointed... Polite service, consistently delicious food...A regular place to eat in comfort..

After eating at some Thai food places which felt westernized we decided to try this one. We were very happy we did. Flavors were spot on. Khan Tang Nah Tang fabulous. Olive fried rice spot on with flavors, as was Larb Gai and Mango Salad. All in all lovely little spot in the middle of a bustling part of town! Big thumbs up! Give this gem a go! Oh and good value for money.
Friendly staff serving delicious, authentic, home cooked food. Catered also for vegetarian alterations of the dishes. The kids loved it too....

From the outside it looks so nondescript. The decor is so un-thai like, it felt like we stepped into a kitschy cafe. The food however was really homely, authentic simple and flavourful thai fare and that was all that mattered to us. The restaurant also offers a wide menu selection of familiar and northern thai dishes. Fast, good quality cuisine and very well priced for the quality of food served.

We visited Hong Tauw Inn last June 3, 2017 with my family. We stayed at U Nimman so we just walked there. It's not that far from Maya Mall. There were plenty of patrons on the restaurants when we got there a little past 7. Staff were accommodating. Food came in no time and didn't disappoint. All the dishes tasted so good. Brown rice was excellent. Prices weren't bad at all. Would definitely come back if we had to stay longer in Chiangmai.

We came in here on a whim, as we had been searching for somewhere to eat and couldn't be bothered walking any longer. From the outside this place doesn't look anything special and the decor might look a bit outdated, but the food was delicious and very reasonable. We enjoyed the food so much we came back a second time before leaving Chiang Mai.

Went for dinner and was underwhelmed overall. Ordered morning glory, red curry, northern Thai sausage, rice and lemonade. Everything came at intervals of about 5+ minutes starting with the morning glory which was surprising. The rice was a very small portion. No dipping sauce or peppers, shallots and ginger for the sausage which we've gotten at every other place it's been ordered. The lemonade was extremely small considering the cost and in comparison with other places. The food wasn't bad but wasn't anything​ special either. Staff were friendly. Place is run down in the back area, missing ceiling tiles and some water damage.

Thai food doesn't get more authentic than that. Prices are reasonable. Service is good, the place is clean. Amosphere is quiet and comfortable. Food ranges from pretty good to great. I can't say every dish I ate there was the best Thai food I had in my life, but I can say that: I was never disappointed by a dish. And I've been a dozen times to this restaurant. This place feels like an old classic... When all restaurants will have closed on Nimman to be replaced by other restaurants, I believe this one will still be here, still serving the same menu... And that's a good thing. Read less

We waled along the Nimman road from the Maya centre trying to find a nice Thai restaurant. The restaurant is in an old building directly on the street and has an old colonial feel to is with high ceilings and fans. There are also antique clocks on every wall and table cloths. The good food was served quickly as was the Chang beer and the quality was excellent. The restaurant is air conditioned so good to get off the street in the hot weather.
Lovely place. The staff are nice and friendly. Had green curry with prawns which was delicious and had a kick to it. Very good prices as well.

Good variety of authentic Thai food. Fairly extensive menu which takes a while to study. Prices are reasonable for it's Nimmanhaemin Rd location. restaurant is quite large once you get inside. Caters to all nationalities.

We dined at Hong Tauw Inn on a rainy evening following recommendation from our hotel. With a good selection of local northern Thai cuisine as well as the popular thai favourites, the food was very nice. We tried a local pork curry, stir fried sunflower greens and a snakehead fish stirfry. The restaurant is cute with a collection of grandfather clocks around the walls! Good service and clean, a nice local Thai restaurant in the Nimmanhaemin area, very close to Maya Mall.

This is a restaurant you can’t afford to miss when visiting Nimmanhaemin. All the dishes are very authentic & full of flavour. Highly recommend the Narm Phrig Long-Reu which is a dip of very rich flavor &layers. The deep fried shrimp cake is phenomenal. Be curious when ordering in this restaurant (don’t just stick to fried rice/curry) & you won’t be disappointed. PS: Exceptionally good serve & every corner in the restaurant is an photo opp.

A serene, comfortable oasis on the chaotic Nimmenhaemin Rd. A restaurant that has existed for many years, serving a large variety of traditional Thai dishes, always fresh and well prepared. Moderate prices for quality food. Service is good with a friendly and accommodating staff. One of my favorite and frequently visited restaurants.

I just pick this restaurant by chance when we want to find some Thai restaurant at Ninman area. The curry noddle is very nice. Best i had at Chiang Mai. the deco is bit dated but well maintained. Good choice at Ninman for Thai food

Stumbled upon this place and it was so delicious. Service was fantastic, we felt like we were having a family dinner. We had the thick spicy basil noodles and khao soi. Both were delicious. Would definitely return.

Love this place which is only a few steps from our airbnb condo. Their portions are small but perfect for us, feels more like home cooking. Had Fried crispy catfish with mango salad and salt fish with Chinese kale, both are delicious. Bookmark for future returns.

Hong Tauw Inn had one of the best Koh Soi I've had in Chiang Mai. Paid around 600 baht for 3 people. The atmosphere is very nice however there's many better places to go to for cheaper food with good quality in Chiang Mai.
