This seems like a traditional old Thai restaurant. It is very nicely done - with lot of precious works of art or interest on display all over!!! Especially clocks! Food is good, we ate here few times and Thai food dishes are very well explained on menu - very interesting and useful menu though a bit showing some age. Food quality and taste wise is great. We tried pad Thai, pad kiusiu, khao soi, and must recommend homemade ice cream scoops!!! Location is right on main nimmanhammein road at the very prime spot. Hardly couple of min walk from big new mall Maya. It is neat and clean, and all air conditioned, yet prices only slightly more than elsewhere in this area. Read less

One of my favourite meals in Chiang Mai. Great Khao Soi, friendly service and traditional atmosphere. Some may say it's a little dated but it really maintains the character of past years. Highly recommended.

You can find some rare and difficultly made dishes of Thai food here with Central style (not Northern style) so that sweet and spicy is dominant. However, every dishes were made by selective material and seasoning resulting to a plenty of deliciousness. Foods were served in small portion size (relatively expensive) but valuable.

This is a very well known restaurant that has been around for a long time. Although the place looks small from the outside, the inside is quite large. It is not a trendy place, the inside being a bit dark and furnishings are old. However, the menu is extensive and the food is excellent. We ordered prawn fried rice and beef green curry with kanom been (a type of thai noodle). Both dishes were very good and priced at around 200 THB each which is reasonable. I would recommend this place and would definitely be back again when I am next in Chiangmai.
We've ordered Pad Thai and khao soi . Wonderful and authentic Thai food. Would be nice to visit when you're in this neighbor hood.

This was our last day but one in Thailand when we had already tried a lot of Thai food. Compared with other restaurants we visited, the food here was not very special. We ordered a coconut soup, a red curry, a stir-fried spinach and a stir-fried chicken with glass noodle. The amount was quite small. Even though the price was not very high, it was not a particularly good value. Service was only moderately good. They did not even ask us whether we needed a drink. This restaurant had exquisite decoration of old style which created a romantic environment.

Great food. Authentic Thai cuisine, traditional ambience with dirt cheap prices. Highly recommend. We ordered deep-fried soft crab and rice-noodle with coconut milk and shrimps, both amazing. We also ordered grilled pork for 100b, which is not on the menu. Fresh coconut only cost 50b each.
Great Thai food at reasonable price ( less than $40 US) including 4 large beers)in older hotel on a busy street. Air conditioned. The spicy cashew chicken dish was perfect.
Fresh vegies , fresh simple local dishes. No internet. Clean toilets. A bit of a slack service and not much English but the food was definitely good.

This is the only restaurant in Chiang Mai we went to multiple times, in part because it was very close to our apartment, but mostly because it was just so good! Truly delicious original and very affordable Thai food, cooked and spiced to perfection, with great smoothies to boot. All dishes were available in a vegetarian version when we asked - replace the meat with tofu or mushrooms and Bob's your uncle! The setting is kinda colonial-ish, with high ceilings, ceiling fans and plenty of grandfather clocks on the walls. The portions are very small though, so getting an appetiser before the main dish is recommended. The service is fast and friendly. Read less

We ate at Hong Tauw Inn twice, once for lunch and once for dinner. We liked the relaxed atmosphere and friendly service. The food was quite good and the price reasonable. This is not where you want to go for the best Thai food you've ever had, but if you want a reliable restaurant for good food at a good price, Hong Tauw is the place to go. Good Kaho soi.
My mum and I ordered the fried soft shell crab with garlic and the red duck curry with rice and we both feel that it was one of the best meals we've ever had.

We went there for a simple lunch in view of the good review. We have to say we seldom encounter disappointing Thai food in Thailand and this is one of them. We ordered a Khao Soi which is the very basic noodles in Chiangmai, a fried vegetable with fish, a fried Kale with salted fish, a fermented noodles with curry chicken and a sticky rice. The Khao Soi was really disappointing. The crispy noodles was hard and not crispy at all. The chicken that went with the noodles was tough and the curry sauce looked and tasted horrible. The sticky rice was dry. The fried vegetable with the fish tasted alright but the portion is really tiny and was finished in a few mouthful. The fermented noodles with curry chicken was okay though the chicken was very touch and tasteless. The best dish was the fried Kale with salted fish. It was interesting that they served the salted fish and the Kale separately . The fish was fried to perfection and juicy and the Kale was tender and hot and tasted very well. But overall it is still a disappointing meal and I will probably not come back for a second time. Read less

We have visited here in 2006 and 2007. The restaurant is full of old clocks and is bigger at the back of the building, first impression might be quite small. It is fully air-conditioned and only seating inside. Menu in English and Thai. Waitress understood and spoke English. Menu extensive with some Northern Thai dishes. We ordered three dishes a soup, stir fried chicken and a prawn spicy salad. Portion size was average but soup very generous with more prawns in than I've had anywhere in Thailand. Service was quick and restaurant was busy. This is in the heart of tourist land so menu prices did reflect this. If you need a cool off and good quality Thai food this is worth a try.

Food taste excellent but the portion is rather small. Be prepared to order 2 portion if that dish is your favorite. Very authentic style Thai cooking. Staff are friendly. Love the decor - wall was covered many grandfather clocks.
30-40 min waiting for simple dishes. They were good, but we ate better for lower prices and with quicker service elsewhere.

We went back to this quaint Thai restaurant a few times during our 3 weeks' stay at Chiangmai. Owner's father was a major ice cream manufacturer in Bangkok. Their pistachio and coconut ice cream are top. Mee Krob, a typical Thai dish, is best served here. Of course, they also have mouth watering curry. Price is very reasonable. Owner is a collector of hanging clocks and antiques. The deco gives an ambiance of travelling back to the good old days. Reservation is required as we went back one Saturday night at around 19:30, it was absolutely full!

After a morning visit to Doi Suthep, we were a bit tired and hungry when arriving at Hong Tauw Inn. Their lunch made me feel awake and utterly happy. The northern style curry is a good recommendation but their mango salad is the best ever.
We have eaten here three times during our short stay and their local dishes are very good and inexpensive. The staff speak english.

We ordered a few dishes for sharing. They are rather small in size. Delicious but could be spicier. Love the Khao Soi but the Khao noen jinn was not up to expectation. This is the 1st restaurant where we had our Norther platter, so it has set the benchmark with the Thai sausages. The place can get pretty crowded during dinner time. To us, the lemon grass tea was good. Bear in mind that this is a restaurant, so do expect to pay the price for the environment and service!

Best Thai food so far, even the simplest and common morning glory with oyster sauce was like a new discovery! Chose any dish and you'll understand how what you've experienced before could have been better. Only drawback is on quantity. Remember when you were young and looking at your burger, at the menu, at your burger...and thought you've been tricked ?! Well...here it's the same. Sometime far too small quantity..so you'll have to order three times the same. Luckily, it's affordable Read less

Yes there are lots of new restaurants large and small in the Nimman area... but my top pick for Thai food is an old, quaint family-run one that I keep going back to for its good, honest food, low prices, outstanding service and pleasant homey surroundings -- you feel you've walked into someone's home just in time for dinner; someone who collects old clocks! I also appreciate the reasonable portion size (not huge American style). If you want large portions just order double -- the average price is around $3 US, so no big deal! I also love that when I ask for "mai ped" (not spicey) it comes without so many chillis. Sometimes looks crowded if you peek in the windows -- but they've got a huge back room, so keep going. Read less

This restaurant is a favorite of friends and we had a pleasant Chinese dinner here. According to these friends there are not many ordinary food-oriented (rather than drinking oriented) fair priced places in this area of town, and this place is an old, classic place that is their go-to place for Chinese. Solid food...not the most memorable Chinese meal I had in CM but definitely good.

We pass by this beautiful restaurant in the day and we are sure to come back to have dinner here. That is a bright decision. Order Sontam papaya salad that to die for, and read in the Internet that the Pad Thai of this restaurant is the best one in Chiang Mai, that is true. We came to Thailand many time and we think it is the best Pad Thai we ever tried. The price is reasonable. The interior of restaurant is very beautiful and sophisticated. Nice staff serves with smile.

Substandard, overpriced food clearly catered to tourists. Service is lacklustre and unfriendly. We had the hors douerves which came with bland nam prik num and just 3 slices of sausage. The royal project salad was regular iceberg lettuce and some tomatoes. Tofu and minced pork soup was passable and the best dish of the meal. Fails to offer sticky rice on the menu which is an embarrassment for any restaurant serving Northern Thai food.

Very good Thai home cooking in simple comfortable setting. The restaurant has several traditional dishes as well as Northern Thai food. Their curry dishes are good. The price is not cheap but I would say it's reasonable to the quality of the food. Some dishes are on smaller side. The service is excellent. Several foreigners visit so the waiters communicate well in English and quite excellent at recommend dishes to first timers.

We've been coming to this place for years, and it has never disappointed. Try the pork and Thai herb curry, the pomelo salad, some relishes with pork crackling, sausage and veg and anything else that takes your fancy-you can't really go wrong here.

We went for dinner a few nights ago and were fairly underwhelmed. The food is pleasant and the decor is lovely but it just misses the mark somehow. For the price, I feel the dishes should have been more impressive. However, we had the pomelo salad which was lovely. The Khao Soi was okay but we can much better at our local stall for 30 baht. The tasting plate was very ordinary and the northern sausage not spicy enough

I love Thai food but am not always able to eat the very spicy dishes. But there are so many wonderful herbs and spice combination that I often wish they would just leave the chilli out. Well this lovely restaurant understand the farang palate and as most dishes are prepared on the spot , they will make it as Spicy or not as you like. There is a huge selection, my favourite is the tom Ka khoong and the marvelous brown rice. The restaurant itself is beautifully decorated with antiques and old clicks on the walls. It feels like eating in grandmas dining room. Warm and homely. The place is always busy and there are small tables for singles and couples as well as long tables for larger parties. Good food and reasonable prices. Read less

Delicious,Northen Thai food,It is recommended,clean big restaurant,everyfood is very tasty！I will go there next time,boss will serve you by himself,people lilke thai food could go there ,must enjoy yourself！
Authentic Thai food. Had several dishes on multiple occasions Good portion sizes. Cheap with Fast friendly service.

My husband and I visited this place one evening after reading the reviews on Trip Advisor, we were not disappointed. The menu is extensive, the portions decent and at a very good price. Just be prepared to be a little patient fir your food, but its worth it :-)
Try the pomelo salad! Many interesting dishes, like the panaeng with coconut hearts e.g. but that salad was absolutely superb.

This place is a gem. I really loved the atmosphere, and the food was delicious. The service was also excellent -- best I had in Thailand. The menu was extensive, and I wish I could have gone back to try many more of their dishes, but alas I discovered it on my last night in Chiang Mai. I also felt very comfortable eating there by myself. They made me feel right at home.

Loved the decor - old lamps, ceramics, clocks - reminded me of a house in the old quarter of Hong Kong; lunch was delicious and dessert was the best homemade green tea ice cream ever! A totally unique place to eat from another time.

Stopped by on a whim. Reasonably old decor inside but cosy enough. Food was a little above average but not great. My partner had papaya salad and even though she has a high chilli tolerance was overcome by the potency of the chilli in her salad. She felt quite ill for some time after so beware.

EXPERIMENT - On top of what everyone is saying, I recommend trying some of the less common dishes on top of some classics. No everyone liked them, but they were for sure an experience I have not had before. Recommend the Thai pickles. FAMILIES - you can ask all dishes to be made spice free for kids

Recommended by our hotel. Cozy and informal ambience, fantastic authentic Thai food, and incredible value for money. We had a range of dishes, including appetisers, curries, and noodles. The subtleties of the flavours and freshness and quality of the ingredients rival high-end Thai restaurants in the US/ Europe. They also provide home-made fruit juices and an ice-cream bar which was welcomed by the kids. In a week in Chiang Mai the Thai food was not bettered.

We ate here every night we were in Chiang Mai. It's a family owned place that gets busy because it's so delicious. We had their green and yellow curries, pad thai, pad seew, tom yum kai soup, friend tofu, friend chicken, spring rolls, and coconut chocolate chip ice cream. The only thing we would have liked that they didn't offer was sticky rice! I would highly recommend this place!

This is a great family run restaurant that is very well know in Chiang Mai. Try the herbal soup and any other dish that sounds good to you. The vegetables are as fresh as can be. Have never had a bad meal here in many many visits!!!

this was probably my favourite place to go to for my meals in Chiang Mai. Went there for 3 meals over 4 days. The atmosphere is quite nice. seems like family run place. The waiter who seems like is the boss there, was a little cold on my first visit but warmed up nicely on subsequent visits. Both me and my friend agreed that it was the best place we went to for our meals during our trip to chiang mai. Read less

We were a party of 4 adults who requested our tour van driver to take us to an authentic Thai restaurant frequented by The locals. This was an awesome place for very good food cooked to order. The crispy catfish salad was sublime as well as deep fried snapper. The crispy fried small shrimps pancake was delightful. We also had a fried tofu and a salad which were just as delicious. Service was good. This place has many collectible winding wall clocks on display. This is a must visit restaurant in Chiang Mai.
A favorite of the locals I understand. All Thai menu so if you can't read Thai just point to the picture. Lunch was very good and plain atmosphere.

A walk down Nimmanhaemin Rd, "Eat Street", served up some interesting haunts. This was the first we looked at, with plenty of patrons, comfortable well priced dishes listed on the menu...but, we kept walking for the elusive "one"!!. Bottom line was, we came back here, and no regrets at all. Restaurant was clean, fresh woven table cloths, and the food was very tasty, hot, the drinks cold, and all up for 5 of us added up to around $50AUD...including beers!!. Bargain The egg fried rice, pork spring rolls, BBQ chicken were scrumptious. Ambience is quirky, with lots of clocks, various aged antiquities..1 lamp behind us that went on and of at its pleasure. The staff were very polite, helpful and mature...couldn't fault them. Was a long way from our Hotel...otherwise we would have come here again. Read less

I've now eaten here four times in 3 weeks; which speaks for itself. My first impressions from outside were favourable as it presents itself well. Inside was even better, an attractive quirky decor almost reminiscent of an English tea room. A selection of clocks adorns the walls and the only one which worked had time dimensions of its own; each time I looked it had changed improbably with no human intervention. Great fun, when you're dining alone. The tables had smart cloth table cloths with co-ordinated napkins. This was both welcome and unexpected, given the inexpensive menu. The music is like a review of all our yesterdays, all the more enjoyable as it is kept at a suitably discreet level. I've found all the dishes I have sampled to date very satisfactory and the large bottle of Chang beer I invariably have to accompany them is very cheap. Many more basic restaurants charge the same or more. Many Thai customers eat in the restaurant, often in groups. Which also speaks for itself. Read less

Just by the end of Nimanhaemin road,after we exploring around,we found nice and pleasant Thai restaurant.The choice of the food here are lots,but we choose the set menu,so we could try lots of variety of Thai food from starter to dessert,with good value and delicious

We thought it looked interesting in a wonky sort of way and decided to try it. UNLIKE so many of the restaurant along the drag, this one was friendly, family oriented, and had curry to die for - specifically the kao soi, made with both crispy and non noodles. They certainly don't hurry you - in fact, my dish was all eaten by the time my husband's finally arrived. But I didn't come to Chiang Mai for fast food, and the two tiered service was worth it when the food did arrive.

Stumbled upon this place while looking to escape the heat in Chiang Mai. Food was ok. We had a couple of "nam prik" dips with steamed vegetables and some sausage and pork rind, also northern style curry. Drinks were very small which meant we had to order more which seemed to bulk out the bill a bit. What let it down for me was the atmosphere of the place. Not very friendly service really, staff seemed bored stiff. Maybe working too many hours? Young waitress spent some time stood in front of our table picking the dirt out of her nails between service. Felt like an unwelcome visitor at a strict aunts house!

As I am having dinner at this restaurant with my wife, I am typing this review. This is a family run Thai Chinese restaurant with 24 years history. It has a retro furnishing with quiet ambience, and soft background music playing. Basically unaffected by the bustling traffic and modern eateries. As we wanted some non-spicy food, the man boss introduced a couple of dishes which comprises steam fish, stir fried vegetables, clear vegetable pork soup and rice noodle chicken. My wife and I feel that this is a very satisfying dinner !

I had heard about this place and decided to give it a shot. It was a long walk from the centre but i didn't regret it. Nice simple decor, prompt service and a wide variety of dishes ! I tried a couple of soups, tom yum and a herbal soup, the sausage dish and a curry. Great stuff overall. Not overly expensive either.

I have no idea why anyone would think this food is good. It's in the bottom 2 in all of Thailand for me. And the other was maybe slightly better, but also awful. Nearly inedible. I do not recommend this at all. None of the 5 dishes we ordered were okay. And we kept trying and racking up the bill (although it's on the less expensive side-good thing). This was recommended by a local and I'm sorry we went here. I don't know if it's gotten worse over the years or if its always been awful, but please save yourself the pain. The waitstaff was extremely slow, but I'm not sure why because there were only a few tables. I saw them hanging out by the register, sort of staring into space.

This place is a marvel of some classical Northern Thai dishes you aren't likely to find elsewhere. The menu is very broad so it's nice if you have several people so you can try more dishes. The herbal soups are to die for and make it hard for me to go on to the next course. Many aromatic and herbal dishes delight your senses. We have been going here for many years and are never disappointed

We discovered Hong Tauw Inn through one of the Chiang Mai Facebook groups, and decided that since we only live a hundred or so metres away, we'd give it a try. It's now become one of our firm favourites! The vegetarian and fish dishes are consistently excellent (I can't speak for the meat ones as we've never had any of them), and we've been there so often that we are now confident enough to ask the staff whether they'll make us food that is not on the menu... the Chu-Chi Dohu we had a few evenings ago, for example, was superb! Service is great, and we are always greeted with a welcoming smile... plus, the staff often help us with our (somewhat dire) pronunciation! Prices are a little on the expensive side for local food but only compared to street vendors and some cafés; however, the ambience of the restaurant (with its myriad clocks!) makes paying that little extra worthwhile. Something else we love about Hong Tauw Inn is that we can (and do) buy locally-produced honey from them (which is utterly delicious)... plus they have a donations box for an elephant rescue charity, which we are always happy to support. If you want somewhere that's more luxurious than a pavement café but without being ostentatious and intimidating, which has a quiet and relaxing atmosphere with low lighting, and soft music playing in the background, that serves great food, and has lovely staff, then you could do a lot worse than go to Hong Tauw Inn! Read less
The menu was very extensive and covered almost all of the dishes of northern Thai cuisine. That said, none of the dishes was beyond average.

when you asked to a local people a restaurant not "for tourists" and where taste the thai food they suggested us to try this place; well was exactly what we were looking for! We spent all our dinners there (evein if the opposite direction of our hotel so need to take a tuk tuk). The Lady is very kind and let you feel at home, foods very very good so really perfect! Thank you Hong Tauwn Inn

Hong Tauw Inn was our must-visit place for our lunch whenever we passed through or stop by Chiangmai. This unique diner offered the Northern cuisine with affordable price and good taste. On our last visit we already noticed the change in the quality of their dishes and made a special remark that certain dishes should not be order again. However today we went back to give the place another chance. We ordered Nametord (deep fried northern sausage), grilled fish, vegetable yam, and fried rice with salty fish. After our lunch we both agreed that the taste of all dishes were relatively poor compare with other. We also crossed Hong Tauw Inn out from our must-visit list in the future. We hate to loose out another favorite diner for sentimental reason. Read less
This is one restaurant you need to try for great Thai food. You can order ala carte or a set menu for 2 to 4 people. You need to try the coconut chicken soup and the fried pork.

The Hong Tauw Inn is a great place for a nice Thai dinner. The menu is huge and everything I have ever had there (in my many visits) has been delicious! It is definitely a pricier place, but I think it has the ambiance and quality to match. If you're looking for a nicer night out, I'd recommend this place. It's not too formal, but it's fancier than a lot of places I generally eat. I take a lot of visitors here because it's a great place to try a lot of different kinds of Thai food!

We had a variety of dishes here and the quality of the food was good but the service was poor, they actually forgot one dish altogether, why not stop being clever and write the orders down? Ultimately there are many better places in Chaing Mai.

Great food, both central and northern Thai, in a pleasant, air-conditioned dining room. Particularly recommended dishes: Kanom Jeen Naam Prik, eggplant salad, pomelo salad, Naem Thod, red duck curry, the house special fried fish (Plaa Thod Hong Tauw). We take all of our visitors here, and have been for 5 years.

This restaurant is off north end of major road Nimmana Haeminda. I ordered papaya salad som tam, Kanon Jen snow Narm, brown rice, Phad pak ruam, total 243 baht = $8 us w/ bottled water. Very good food and easy listening jazz for music
This remains Chiang Mai's best Thai food. And at a very good price. Fish dishes, northern sausage, mee krob, curries excellent choices. And traditional desserts cap off a great meal.

This restaurant has a uniqe atmosphere with its tall ceilings stained glass and clocks round the wall. Somehow it evokes a colonial feel - cotton tablecloths aswell. The restaurant is essentially divided into two sections and the atmosphere in the front part is the best. Sometimes crowded at weekends and popuar with the locals shows the popularity of the food. We have eaten 5 times here and never been let down. Menus inEnglish with description of ingredients. There is a section called appetizers but that doesn't mean they come first. Like all good Thai rstaurants the food comes when its ready in the kitchen! Our favourites were ginger chicken and the crispy beef but take your pick its all beautifully done. Read less

We ate here twice during our visit to Chiang Mai. Both times food, service and atmosphere were exceptional. We ordered a few of the Northern Thai dishes with pork and egg - superb, the Raw Mango and Spicy Papaya salads - fresh and crunchy and some noodle and stir fry dishes - tasty and satisfying. The wall clocks and faded pictures provide a nice and relaxing background.

We arrived here at 4:00pm after the Lila Massage. It has two entrances and one on Nimexxxx Rd was not apparent. It was not full and only one table was occupied by tourists. Wtih the recommendaion of guidebook and staff, we ordered five to six traditional northern Thail food, all were delicious and reasonably priced. We like the vintage decoration there with many old clocks and old pictures hanging around. The serviced lady was patience to introduce the food to us one by one. At last, we ordered a dessert which was good also. I regard this as a must visit restaurant if you were in Chiangmai! Read less

We've been going to this restaurant for years. Started by an expat, festooned with clocks on the wall (an unusual sight in Asia), the cooking is authentically Thai, the kind of food served at home rather than at restaurants. There are set menus and à la carte items; everything we've tried is delicious, the curries, the salads, the sauces. We're especially partial to the "wing-bean salad," something we've found only in Thailand, not in Thai eateries in other countries: a subtle blend of chili paste, the crunchiest legume, minced pork, and an assortment of garnishments . When we arrive in Chiang Mai, we head for the Hong Tauw Inn for the first meal. This time, we ate, royally, the two of us, for 390 baht, or the equivalent of US$13. Read less

You can mistake this restaurant for a b & b becos of the name. It's tucked within a row of shops. A lot of clocks, probably saved when the inn stopped operation. A walk around to try and find out where guest rooms are located yielded no result. The decor is reminiscent of early 1900 era. Not all items taste great but the papaya salad, rice crackers are good snacks. The pad thai was disappointing, so it didn't prompt a second order. Food servings are large since there were 12 of us sharing w each of us having at least one bite of each dish. Prices are reasonable. Menu in English so description helps when ordering. Order smallest portion and a few dishes to start w if this is your first time trying Thai cuisine. Read less

We ate here on our first night in Chiang Mai and ordered and shared a range of dishes. The restaurant was very quiet that evening but had a nice atmosphere regardless. We enjoyed the food and thought it was very good value. Unfortunately I felt a bit unwell afterwards but blame this on not mentioning that I have an allergy and think that the food maybe was cooked in nut oil or had nuts in the sauce that I didn't notice. My partner was fine and thought the food was lovely.

We discovered a new favorite dish while at this restaurant and we loved their mango with sticky rice. It is a remarkably nice environment and offers good quality thai food at reasonable prices AND they take credit cards. Although we did not make use of it, they also had some italian wines available. We went back two nights in a row because we liked it THAT much.

This restaurant actually has a lot of tables but you could miss it if you aren't careful. As some other reviewers have pointed out, this is a tablecloth, cloth napkin kind of restaurant. Its decor is a bit dark but I find myself going here fairly often. The food is of a good standard. I suppose I could be more adventurous here, but I like what I usually order so I stick with it. I can recommend the curries. It's fairly priced as well. It's on the same block as the Doi Tung coffee shop on Soi Two. It does seem to attract its share of tourists which is surprising because it is a little out of the way.

when u come in u plunge into cozy atmosphere. interior is nontypical for thai restaurants. and looks very nice! we took pad thai, papaya salad and pork with veggies in eggs. everything was very good cooked. at first visit to new restaurant i always taste pad thai to understand food style & quality because this traditional dish varies in different parts of thailand. so it was good. as well as other dishes. prices r more than acceptable.

Hong Tauw is a charming old Thai restaurant a favorite with both locals and visitors. The food is typical of northern Thailand ...the portions are large and the price very fair. It is home cooking served in an old Thai Inn and not a place to miss even if you only have a short stay in Chiang Mai. They offer a tasting selection which will give you a sample of what we all enjoy eating in northern Thailand. Try it

I have eaten here a few times and had both good and bad expriences. Fist the decor is a little odd, with dozens of clocks marking time on the walls, which somehow does not set you at your ease (especially when they chime the hour). The food has differed each time I went, once I enjoyed a very good nuer da-deaw and pad siew moo, another time I was served the worst pad thai goong I have ever eaten and fishckaes which were so bland I could barely eat them. They managed to make the noodles in the pad thai watery and the shrimp were small and unpleasant looking. What a shame as if I had only been once it would have garnered 4 stars.
offers great thai food in a large well decorated restaurant. The food is well presented and reasonably priced.
Where you can get authentic Thai food. Not too pricey and tasty
Indoors with aircon; cloth napkins, pleasant quiet rooms, friendly service. On Nimmenhamen not far from Huay Kaew.

While the decor is nothing special, the tasty food and extremely cheap pricing more than make up for it. We particularly enjoyed the Kuay Teow Pad Kee Mao (stir fried spicy rice noodles with beef and thai basil) and the Kuay Teow Phad Hang Kai (stir fried dry noodle with chicken). Overall we were satisfied and would return again.

I've been in Nimma area for a couple of days, looked into the restaurant from the street and never did bothered until my companion suggested we give it a try. The deco and atmosphere is nice (it would be better if they don't play top 40 english music), food is nice esp the northern fare and I love the fermented pork. Price wise, value for monies and I had a great time there,
We went few times in 2009 and few times in 2010 but for 2011 we hadn't got time yet. We're 4 of us always and we'd always chosen the fishes, they can do the best with fishes and great curry! Try it!

We stumbled upon Hong Tauw Inn at the top end of Nimanhaemin Road and did not regeret our decision. A fantastic atmosphere - lots of old oriental objet d'art about the place. There is a good selection on the menu (they have English menus) and the food was served promptly and full of taste. The prices were reasonable and overall I think this place is a good option in an area where there are plenty of more western choices.
Very elegant, clean, good Thai food and they do not use MSG. Great service.

This is a fantastic Thai restaurant. Of course, there's nothing surprising about that...MOST Thai restaurants in Thailand are fantastic. Still, this is one of my favorites, not only because of the outstanding food at low prices, but also because of the atmostphere: you'll feel like you're eating in someone's somewhat funky home (and I mean that in a good way). The collection of grandfather clocks is an especially nice touch. Service is unhurried but friendly. I especially recommend the morning glory, pomelo salad, and larb gai. If you're staying across the street at the Amari Rincome Hotel, this will quickly become your favorite dining spot.

This Thai Restaurant which is open daily from 11am to 11pm is full of ambiance and popular with both tourists and residents of Chiang Mai. A long stay resident at our hotel appeared to dine there most evenings. During our 15 night stay we used this restaurant five times and each time we were rewarded with good attentive service and very yummy food. We tried several different dishes and would heartily recommend the following: Pla-Chonn Hong Tauw – deep fried fresh water fish with carrot dressing; Koong Foi Chup Peng Thod – deep fried little shrimp (see picture); and Naem Mog, Sai Ou – spicy grilled northern sausage. A meal for two with beer should not cost more than about 600 Thai Baht. The restaurant has a great collection of wall clocks and other curios scattered around the several rooms where they serve the food. Highly recommended – we will definitely go back on our return to Chiang Mai. Read less

Having just returned from a fortnight in Thailand, this is the only place we ate at that I would give a bad review of (and we ate out all the time). I think they are maybe taking advantage of their entry in the Rough Guide. We ordered a Set Menu but were served two out of the four items with different options than those stated, with no explanation or apology. The food was ok but not anything special.The service given to us was definitely seconday to the local thai people. When the bill came there were extra items on that we did not order. Although it's hard to be sure of the detail of the bill, as soon as we tried to get their attention, all three staff on looked at each other knowingly and someone came forward and immediately amended our bill! I was not impressed. Read less

This is a great choice if you're out shopping on Nimanhaemin Road, or even if you're after good Northern Thai food. The restaurant is just opposite Nimanhaemin Soi 1 (where a lot of the nice shops are). The atmosphere is quite nice (20's setting) and relaxing. I really enjoyed the Burmese Pork Curry and the Naem Tod (Fried pickled pork). The desserts and ice-creams are nice too! Read less

Beautiful restaurant well set, modern style. Thai dishes elaborated differently from traditional adding a little creativity. Very high food quality and slightly higher than average but justified price. Excellent service and quick turnaround. Availability of indoor and outdoor tables. Highly recommended
We enjoyed the Thai meal. Everything we Ordered tasted great. The price was reasonable and service was good. The nice people working at the restaurant didn't hurt either.

The reviews on Tripadvisor are not accurate at all. The first impression was great. Very nice place, great background music, very friendly service. We ordered 2 glasses of red wine, 2 starters, 2 main courses and a salad to the main course. For a long time, no one came. The red wine was completely forgotten, the appetizers came 20 minutes apart (the spring rolls stick together so much that you tear them apart when you pick them up and the chopped pork in the upper gin salad had a terribly tuberous consistency). Then the main course: one of the main courses was wrong and we had to send it back, the beef in my curry had a very funny taste and was extremely steeped in fat pieces - thus inedible. The papaya salad was good. To top it off, I spent the rest of the evening in the toilet. Read less

A very tasty lunch, in attractive art-filled air-conditioned space with outlook to shops and people. Prawn Pad Thai and yummy chicken khao soi both cooked to perfection and well presented. Great drinks list. Very well looked after by our host, TK, a lively Thai German guy who entertained us with great stories. He was kind enough to book us a Grab car back to our hotel and wait with us after finishing work until it arrived. Nice finish to a great day in trendy Nimman area.

The portions are ridiculously small, the prices for which were excessive compared to restaurants in the area. For example, a glass of wine is offered from 350 baht ( !!!). In addition, there will be another 10% tax etc. Even though the food was good in itself, I wouldn't go back here because you go out hungry. This is probably why the restaurant is usually poorly attended.

This was not a good restaurant for us. The Service was incredibly good! Not a bad word about that! They were extremely friendly, helpful,... but the food was really not ok... the chicken was completely dehydrated, the sauce with the chicken was really not tasty, the steak was extremely chewy,... not cheap and really not good food... too bad because the place is hugely beautiful and the staff really try very hard.
The dining scene is very good, the dishes are exquisite, the staff is excellent, the food is delicious.Thanks for the great experience we had, we will definitely return.

From the moment we walked in Neo, assistant manager, greated us by first name due to our reservation made months earlier. He was so personable and helpful in making our selection of wine and food. He even made some alterations to the offerings because we are pescatarians. The food was absolutely delicious and the attentivness to his patrons was of top notch. We got talking about the music being played and when we mentioned @billystrings he changed the music to Billy Strings just to make our experience there even better. Thanks Neo for a memorable night of good food, good wine, good conversation and of course good music.
Excellent small restaurant with very tasty food. The staff were really nice, and the decor was lovely. Nice wines too. We will be back.

We ate here on our first night in Chiang Mai having wandered the Nimman area and the White Market. Excellent dinner with superb, knowledgeable and relaxed service. Ordered curry and chicken with cashews accompanied by the house Chardonnay and Carmenere. The wine waiter understood the wine and was really friendly without imposing. A great night that meant we returned just for a wine on our final night, with a different but also friendly and engaging waitress and the same wine waiter who remembered us from our previous visit.
Went here for a delicious meal after a cocktail at Surr Bar upstairs. The food was amazing and the wine was also delicious!
Great service. Made an effort to to fit us in on a very busy night. Neoh was attentive to our needs and understood Gluten Free options for my wife. Food was solid!

Wow! The food and atmosphere are absolutelyr beautiful! Everything on the menu looked so appetizing, which made it hard to decide what to choose! Neo was so attentive and made great suggestions. Definitely will be back soon!

Curious by the reviews, I had booked this hip restaurant. Much attention to the layout of the plates, and the dishes were all delicious and the us matching. Nice ambiance and very good staff. You pay more here but given the high quality wik found that proportionate. Visiting this restaurant is definitely worth it if you want to escape the greasy bustle of Chiang Mai's many eateries. The only reason I give four points is that the lighting was not exactly on soft, so too bright.

Amazing food and wonderful team at Jarid, we were lucky this was part of our hotel Art Mai. Thank you to the wonderful staff, Nuch, Neo & Eak. Dream team! Thank you so much for making our stay so lovely. We were here with our children and they loved it too. A must visit for any time.
This is an amazing restaurant with the best food 😊 TK was our waiter & the kindest most loveliest person ever x highly recommend this restaurant xxx

We were very thankful to be here at Jarid twice in our short trip here in Chiangmai. TK from Jarid who served us was such a darling. He was genuinely sincere and accommodating to our requests. My companion loved the sesame rice ball dessert and requested to have it changed from one of the 4 desserts in the tea time menu. TK immediately said he was able to change it for us. He was also very forthcoming and introduced us to other things (drinks) from the menu. Even though we were here just for drinks and desserts, we felt very comfortable and welcomed. TK's outgoing personality and his smiles made us very happy during our stay here in Chiangmai. We hope to be back soon. 🙂 Read less

We had coconut milk with chicken, stir-fried chicken with cashew nuts, the steamed jasmine rice, pineapple fried rice, awesome smoothies and other things, all absolutely delicious. The service was impeccable, as first-rate as it gets. Highly recommend. We ate outdoors and it was very pleasant.

If i could put 4.5 stars i would! The food was very good! Staff friendly and food convenient. Only downside was the wine. Place is listed as “thai food and fine wines” but they had no white wine available at all by the glass (hence the 4 star). We also ordered a salad but theyre was missing ingredients as they were not available. If not for the wine, would have been 5 stars ez as the food was very good. Would recommend

We walked by this restaurant by concidence. We were pleasently surprised by this gem. Me and the missis ate four apetizers and a main dish to share. The food was excellent and the service was splendid and very friendly. The drinks were also top notch. The guitarist outside played a bit to loud for our taste, but other than that the evening was perfect. Higly reccomend.

Excellent starters, mains okay. No white wine at all despite the name mentioning wine! Apparently a supply issue but they should have told us when we went in rather than waiting until the food was served. Staff were amazing. My husband walked into the glass door injuring himself as there was no warning sticker on the door and we also witnessed one of the two members of staff do the same. The staff immediately came over with a first aid kit and attended to the injury. The restaurant owners really need to put safety stickers on their doors to keep staff and customers safe.

Great place with very professional and nice staff. The food is very good and it was an exceptional experience. The prices are excellent and the general atmosphere in the place is very warm and aesthetic. Strongly recommends!

Very good restaurant, good value for money. Excellent food, beautifully prepared and stylishly presented. Service was initially good, staff friendly. There were four of us. During the meal, two of us got the starter, the others didn't, then came the main courses, and only after we complained did the other two get the starter - after the main course. Absolutely unacceptable! After the meal, the table full of dishes was not cleared, even though the restaurant was not busy and three employees preferred to stand together and make small talk. This spoils the overall impression.
This is the best restaurant we visited in Chiang Mai. Fun drink menu, great service and ambience indoor and outdoor.
Excellent place! The setting of the place was very beautiful, the service was perfect and the food delicious! Widely recommended!!!! 10/10!!!!

We came to this restaurant based on Tripadvisor reviews which were very good and yet we were blown away by the quality and the amazing taste, structure and colors of the beautiful food , drinks and the amazing friendly and very helpful staff. We can highly recommend this restaurant 😀

Came here after just arriving off a late flight - the staff were so lovely and kind. I had the pad Thai is was so so delicious and was presented beautifully. Very decent pricing also - would recommend.

On the corner of a slightly hidden street, this small restaurant will seduce you with fine and delicate dishes with sweet spicy flavors! Special mention for the grilled pork and chicken skewers! The place is calm, barely disturbed by a guitar player who gives the whole thing a European note. The cocktails are good, choice of wines including French!!! Be careful, the prices are higher than in many restaurants in Chiang Mai but the quality is also higher! Go there for a sweet evening on the terrace!

We had such a wonderful experience at Jarid so we want other people to know about this gem! While Chiang Mai abounds with great dining options, Jarid is on a whole other level. Their chef's skills are incredible! Our meal was as beautiful as it was delicious and it was a treat to enjoy some really good wine with it. Every detail of the decor is attractive and conducive to relaxation and enjoyment. And the service? I can honestly say this is the best service I've received not only in Thailand, but anywhere in my travels around the world. Definitely coming back!

Amazing design foods pairing with cocktails at ArtMai Hotel inChiangmai! They are just reopening the restaurant newly gorgeous and I the same time cosy place to have dinner ! We really enjoyed all the delicious foods courses and they even have choices for vegetarian or vegan with delightful cocktails and many selections of wine too

Outstanding menu and very kind staff. The beef Massuman curry was the best I’ve ever tasted and I’ve been to Thailand 6 times. The portion was just right and the raspberry and blueberry smoothie was so refreshing to complement it. Excellent. I’ll come again and hope it stays open.

This is an upmarket, comfortable hotel restaurant with the added attraction of an art gallery. The surroundings are attractive without being overpowering, wait service is good and the food carefully (and artistically) presented. The menu, while not being very extensive , is carefully compiled. The prices are to match.

We came to this restaurant as we wanted to eat some good Thai food. Great Pa Thai with vegetables I highly recommend it. Great service and the food arrived shortly after we had ordered. Good value for money abd nice generous portions. A place I will return to for sure

We have been here before and it never fails to be perfect. This last visit has been a wonderful way to celebrate a birthday. All was great from service to food, attention to detail is superb. Highly recommended.
Wonderful food !! Especially ,”Tom yum Koong” “Roti satay Kai” I loved it !! Waitress are good ser vice and great res.taurant . Thank you.
Such a great place for Thai food, nice presentation and good taste. I ordered Somtam Kai yang and satay Kai I can say it one of the best in town, highly recommended.
Good taste.the food is so delicious and the presentation of food is beautiful. I want to come back again when I have chance. 😁
Restaurant with a nice terrace, use the promotion buy 1 get 1 free at the happy hour soo nice. Will come back for sure
Super nice place within Nimman. Food is really good. I deeply recommend appetizers « Miang Khram » then the hung meat « nab nuea charit ». Moreover, the selection of wines fits nicely their meals.

Walking through on the Nimman Road. I looking for thai restaurant and I found Jarid restaurant near one Nimman . Here have set luch menu. I tried northern thai curry noodles with chicken eat with ice lemon tea and get free 1 ice cream. Very good value and good staff.
Good service, good food, good drinks and good hotel thank you very much for take care. Coming soon I will be back 💕
thank you for fantastic night. 14/2 bravo service & food. ilke promotion buy1get1free is a good idea for nice time yummy. i'm will come back soon .
What a nice place to stop by either for lunch or dinner. The food is prepared to the gourmet standard and the cocktail drinks are excellent.🍹🍸🍹

At some night i can't remember ,am walk around nimman road for looking some restaurant because am so hungry i walked past this and i listing some voice am look around and then for staff hotel come talk to me is so funny and Friendly ,i Decide to this restaurant ,so staff talk to recommend menu for me and i order soft carb with Yellow sauce , fried rice sea food and water melon Smoothie ,i think is so perfect for me because fried rice and soft carb aren't olier and have someting for good smell ,and water melon Smoothie is not so sweet and have many water melon and restaurant staff is so nice erelations fir guest i like them talking with me so much because so funny ,and Finally i want come back here to jarid restaurant am so happy at here and i hope you all come to trial everything that here same me thanks you ,... I come to this hotel is alone but am don't re feel alone because am feel is stay with my friend,The Staff are takecare me so very very cool so fun,friendly same take with my friend and Restaurants is very good for nice food don't special but delicious and good for you look, beverage is so nice bartender so very hard, and view is soooo very very nice at centre Chaing Mai ,if you come to Chaing Mai am recommend you all must to stay at here thx

Located at Niemann, it is quite easy for everyone to find this restaurant. It is absolutely a worthy shot to try its food and wines if you are bored of those street food, for it can not only provide you a cozy and elegant eating atmosphere, the food is also modified in a milder way. 在宁曼路，位置非常的好。如果你厌倦了无聊，不卫生的街边食物，这里绝对是一个好去处。环境安逸，食物经过适当的改良，更适合大多数人。
Service is really good and food are great. Restaurant have good atmosphere, the restaurant look great and clean.
Clean and nice food restaurant, Suggest 4.5 great, Polite waitress Will come second and more time here

I try the lunch set, with "Khao Ob Sapparod", is a fried rice in a pineapple so so nice. With the set I have also one soft drink and 1 scoop of ice cream for free. The staff is friendly too. I will come back again.
This is our second lunch in Chiang Mai, the place is nice, service is good and the food very delicious, especially the curry soft shell, highly recommend👍🏻👍🏻

There are some great recommended signature dishes from famous Thai artists. I tried the first page of menu that is Kao Klondike Kab Pod by a famed pop singer of Pod Mordern Dog band. Also, the atmosphere feeling is very relaxing and good mood to chill out. Highly recommended :)
My meals were delicious and the staff are lovely! They could not do more to make you happy! So pleasant outside in the evening!

Went out in Chiang Mai for a nicer date night. Sorry I don't know the Thai names for each dish. Glass noodles with squid 4/5 Tom Yum Soup: 3/5 Crab Curry: 3/5, Curry was bland French fries: 3.5/5 Service was good just some miscommunications trying to order. Tip: They only serve alcohol at 5pm. There is no designated bartender so they only can make cocktails on the menu. Read less
To be honest, I can not find any highlights here. And I was kind of disappointed. I ordered duck with lychee, Australia Beef, soft crab...... It did not worth my trip.

We ordered the chicken satay and Penang duck — both were just above average. We also ordered a berry shake, which had no berries in it at all and was made with berry syrup which was very disappointing. The server was nice enough to change this for a watermelon shake made with just the fruit, upon request. Mostly tourists here. I’d recommend passing and going elsewhere for better food and pricing.
Good taste and Good service I recommend this restaurant If you want to have some romantic dinner with various types of Wine .

I had a fabulous dinner last night Food was bursting with flavour and beautifully presented Staff very professional attentive and lovely Very good selection of wine and lovely cocktail . Definitely to return to again and again
Thai restaurant, delicious food, very good service staff, smiling and friendly. The wine is excellent.
I have visited this restaurant one week ago and i think he’s the best in chang mai, perfect food and perfect wine!! Everything was good
The second time come here . nice staff and good food I recommend soft sell crab yellow curry and have to order rice eat together that so delicious
We came here with the guide of TripAdvisor and it didn't disappointed us! Very good environment and patient waitress. The food was very delicious with large plates. Beer buy 1 get 1 free.

This is a good spot for a special night out. Nice surroundings, good wine and tasty food made for a pleasant evening. The Thai food was excellent especially the deep fried fish. I was very thankful that they were willing and able to deal with gluten free. The staff worked very hard to accommodate us. We will return
specail juice, very spicy, but I like it~ delicious~ but the first dish with fish, a little fishy smell， I can't eat…
We were looking for an easy and relaxing spot for a quick meal and Jarid was ideal. Great food, calm vibe. Very enjoyable! Will have to come back for a longer meal and some wine!
The environment is good Good service Very clean Food decilous 第一次品尝。味道非常棒！服务很好，小姐姐们非常美丽漂亮~有机会来泰国还会再次光顾
Great service. We ordered a pineapple fried rice and a soft shell crab curry, both were delicious but the crab curry was really good!!!!

Jarid has top-class food, with superb service to make your dining experience an event to remember. There’s an overall pleasant feel about the restaurant, with comfortable seating and ample space to move about. What’s more, they have a little art gallery that contains exciting contemporary work by Thai creators. Definitely worth repeated visits!

Saturday night and the restaurant was virtually empty! A shame as the food is very good. The chicken satay starter is very good... 9/10. We were a bit disappointed with the main course... duck with penang curry sauce but the actual dish had much smaller pieces of duck than the picture in the menu showed. Service was very nice. They let us have happy hour drinks as our taxi was stuck in traffic. Read less
Staff friendly. Food and wine good. A little expensive so, arrive during happy hour (finishes at 2000).

Excellent atmosphere!.... Restaurant is located down a well lit alley with a sophisticated vibe. The food is of fine dining quality with a unique flair. Highly recommend experiencing the service and relaxed environment.
The soft shelled crab entree and “specialty” Thai sausage entree were both over priced and not tasty. House red wine was nice.

Jarid is a very well-run restaurant just outside the iconic One Nimman Centre. The food is really tasty and the service as efficient as any top-quality restaurant in the world. They do a very reasonably priced buffet breakfast that satisfies both eastern and western palates, and a scrumptious dinner with an extensive menu. Their happy hour Shiraz (two for the price of one) is worth a try. Quite a find.

We had dinner there with a small group of 4 people yesterday evening. I liked the decoration of the restaurant, modern chic. Service was good. Food was delicious but I’ve had better in Chiangmai. We had beers and cocktails during happy hour. I’ve had better cocktails in Ko Kut! Overall a nice place for fine dining. Read less
Stayed in Art Mai, staff reccommend to try their restaurant which was great we really enjoy both foods and service. Very nice combination love the atmosphere also great wine.
The food is delicious. The environment is good and the service is good， Cost effective ，we'll come again.
Restaurant is clean, well decorated and comfortable. Curry soft shell crabs crabs is delicious. Prawns cake is very nice. Mango smoothie is fair but coffee is good.
我們因為入住Art Mai gallery hotel.早餐在這裡食用，是buffet式餐點，有提供現場製作的蛋料理（fry, scrambled, boil）.麵包很一般，熟食也很普通。希望中午跟晚上餐廳是不同主廚規劃

We have good foods, good service, happiness time here. All friend Are satisfied with this meal and environment. Namtarn is very kindly to service us. 這邊有很好的食物跟環境和歡樂的時光. 所有的朋友都很滿足這邊的餐食和環境。Namtran 很熱情親切的招待我們, 這是很難忘的一次用餐經驗。
Very friendly waitresses. explained dishes we ordered. The place was not that big but really cozy. Most of all their dishes and cocktail tasted lovely. Wanna visit this place again:)
Good environment and food style very special.The staff very nice ,price is fair,location is convenience.

Had dinner here tonight. We chose duck and red curry and prawn in red curry. Happy hour is 5-8 so we had 2 for 1 cocktails. Yummiest dessert is mango, sticky rice and coconut ice cream. So good. Great meal and nice place and staff.

I searched the restaurant from trip advisor and saw lots of positive reviews. It was all true - delicious thai food with nice plating, kind and smiling staff, good interior and facility and even loved their choice for music! I truly recommend this restaurant to someone who enjoys quite nice food in good mood. You will like it, too!
The food is good on both appearance and taste. Portion is quite large and satisfied. The spicy is adjustable.
Nice little restaurant with good food and wine/drink selection. Located in the most popular area in Chiang Mai. The staffs are super friendly! Credit cards accepted.

I ordered on red duck curry and it tastes really good. However what i really like about this place is its atmosphere-very chic and modern u can hardly find:) u should come over in the evening instead of the afternoon.

This resturant is lovely and decently priced. The staff are so friendly especially “Tong” who’s so welcoming . We missed our breakfast time and Tong went the extra mile to make sure he provides food for us. The staff are always smiley, helpful and always up for a good conversation. I would defiantly recommend Jarid.

We read good review so we went to try and quite disappointed. Shrimp spring roll was fine, nothing special. Panaeng duck with lychi was not good at all, duck breast slice were very dry curry was not thick look almost like soup not as panaeng. Chicken with cashew nut was salty. Atmosphere is ok.
have a really nice environment and good service. Pad Thai is delicious and the pork is nice too. have a nice meal with my gf here！
The food is pretty good~The environment is nice and easy, it’s a nice place for people to come and eat .
We ordered Pad Thai, Pork fried rice and papaya chicken. All of them were very delicious. Their staff were very nice to us. It was a pity that they have sold out pineapple rice.

i just walked by this restaurant and was attacted by the atmosphere in there We ordered some handy tasties and it was so eye catchingThai western funsion, good tasty and Good staff service and hearted Will return Soon

Very upscale vibe at reasonable prices made this spot a real winner. The staff here do a real nice job. I had the Pineapple Fried Rice (recommenced by our waitress) and it was very very good. However, the taste definitely didn’t measure up to the visual presentation which was absolutely stunning. I’m usually the one mocking people who take pictures of their food, but for this I made an exception. Sides/appetizers were all very well done. For her main, my partner got a strange Crab Leg curry dish that she didn’t like which also didn’t look very appetizing. As long as you avoid that, definitely give this place a shot once you’ve finally had enough street food and are ready for a classy sit down dining experience. Read less

Excellent food choice and very well presented the choices were not too elaborate but has something for everyone the cocktails were well made and the prices reasonable there many other restaurant in the immediate vicinity
Good service, sweet smiles on the face of the staff; food are up to standard; the breakfast are unexpectedly nice with different food rotated every day.

By accident of finding the restaurant, it is better to find a place to be found. The surroundings are very nice and suitable for lovers. The staff are very friendly. We ordered the crabs very much. Thai fried shrimp is more sweet. It served to stir fried rice. It served with meat and vegetables.SpInneret fried well, too. Two of them ordered five.Overall, this restaurant is still pretty good.

We were looking for a restaurant near Nimmanheimen Road and cafe across Jarid Thai cafe. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The chicken satay starter was great but the spring rolls were rather greasy. The massaman curry sauce was tasty but the chicken meat was thigh with skin on so we had to be careful which parts to eat. The roti that accompanied the curry were extremely greasy. The pineapple fried rice served in a pineapple was tasty and well presented. Overall the food was mixed for us but everything else about the restaurant was lovely. Read less

Sensational food, amazing service. So many delicious choices. The make your own mix in beetle leaves was very refreshing and a favourite. The duck curry was also as good as I have ever had. Do yourself a favour and visit when in Chiang Mai.
Good atmosphere with nice and polite staff Price reasonable Food seems tasty Free snack given May come next time
great pinapple rice，better than we ate at in many other restaurants. great service &environment！服务和环境都非常好，值得尝一下菠萝炒饭～
perfect service and delicious food！ 非常好吃！强烈推荐！服务员小姐姐特别热情 特别温柔！总之就是很好啦 离let’s relax 也很近 棒棒的！快来吃 啦啦啦啦啦啦
the environment is nice and gorgeous. the location is easy to access and near to shopping mall MAYA. the staff is also friendly. the food is thai n western style. awesome.
Having dinner here with friends Very impressed Great food , Nice restaurant Good location 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻 Recommended!!!

I stayed in the hotel for three days and have enjoyed dining in the boutique restaurant. The restaurant offers excellent food and dining environment. Tong is the manager of the restaurant. He is a very nice guy and always offers help to his guests. You don’t want to miss getting to know him.

Made my way especially for the buffet breakfast at this boutique hotel. Wish I stayed here at this high affordable and art focused hotel instead of my old city 1 night 3 star layover. Lots of inspiring interior design ideas to takeaway for home improvements too.
Good ambience and friendly staff. We had lunch set menu, nice thai food at reasonable price. Much worth to visit!
Dishes are nice. We like the past thai and tom yum soup. The smoothie here is fresh.Staff are friendly with great services.
Delicious Thai food in Chiangmai Recommend to the people who visit to Chiangmai It’s nearby Nimman !
Decent wine list but food wasn’t very flavourful. Nice ambiance wirh very nice staff. If you want a good bottle of wine, it’s worth a visit. Go elsewhere if you want a great meal.
Went as a group of 4, service is very good good is just average. Really good presentation it’s just not that nice, wine is expensive and the recommended white was very ordinary.

I think the food just eat enough already. If the already delicious. But it's not what I think. I was wrong, particularly Thailand, where food must be served with hot steamed rice it is many times. Nice quiet corner next to an art gallery near Nin Man Road. Good service and nice food. Family friendly. recommend Pad thai, spaghetti kee, and khao pad moo.

So the bad news first, this is a hotel restaurant, and we all know what that means right: they have to have one for breakfast service, so you might as well grind out some derivative offering the rest of the day to try and squeeze some value out of all that kit in the kitchen and furniture out front. Wrong: this place is superb. Its a small space maybe 26 covers in, 20 out on the terrace, the hotel Art Mai Gallery Hotel would probably describe itself as boutique if that adjective had not become so cliche as to be now meaningless. Its decorated in the minimal industrial style that seems to have radiated out from Clerkenwell the globe over and the dining room is in the same vein. The restaurant offers traditional Thai dishes with a modern twist, my green papaya salad with grilled chicken (at £3.50) was of course 'deconstructed' but that was fine as they gave me a bowl so i could reconstruct it. It was vibrant, sweet and sour, from the tamarind water, but wow was festooned with red birds eye chillies with such prefundity it was eye-wateringly hot. You think a Cornwall Pasty Co. pasty is hot, you think the star Sol is hot, mere puppies on the Scoville scale to this thing. It took two litre bottles of Perrier to see it through (yes it does still exist) but I loved it. It was an assault on the senses but the chicken was savoury and the nuts gave a pleasant crunch and texture which delighted. It was delicious. So much so I took lunch in my hotel restaurant on the second day too- possibly a first - (although I did ask them to tone the heat down a tad), having had to find a 7/11 for what I shall simply refer to as soothing emollients. On the second day I toyed with one of chefs recommendations: Spicy and Sour Curry with Crispy Snakehead Fish, in fact all the curries look exotic, very little I recognise from my Thai restaurant back home, but finally plump for a wing bean salad (£5), in sure and certain knowledge I haven't a clue what it is. I seem to remember Rick Stein opining that the prawns and salads are the glory of Thailand so salad it is. When it arrives it is a big bowl of sliced green wing beans, which look like runner beans in cross section but have a slightly more kale-esque taste and are warm, they are dressed in a warm chilli and tamerind sauce which is deliciously savoury and the whole thing is covered in a deep fried crumb of something or other I can't identify initially but as the flavour builds realise is desiccated coconut. The flavours and textures are perfect and very well judged indeed. In addition the salad is garnished with a hens egg boiled and quartered and three king shrimp, deveined and grilled. This practice of focusing on either veggies or carbs with just a little meat/fish is common all over Asia, for reasons of economy, but I suspect is also very healthy and we would do well in the west to follow the philosophy, although I would focus on the veggies over carbs. Here the coconut provides plenty of additional protein. Washed down with an ice-cold beer; it's quiet a feast, a healthy feast; time therefore for another beer! Read less

Jarid Thai Food & Fines Wines is a modern, elegant space occupying a corner of the Art Mai hotel and serving fine dining at a surprisingly affordable price. Wine bottles line the walls in stacks of horizontal racks, and candle light flickers at each table in the dim lighting. There is an outdoor terrace that looks out onto the quiet street. Happy hour from 5 pm-8 pm is a must, with offerings ranging from tropical cocktails to smooth martinis. For appetizers, we had fried garlic and crispy chicken wings and a visually-stunning assortment of Thai sausages with Lanna fare such as fried pork skins and a chili side. The standout main dish came out of the left field: fusilli with Thai spices and Chiang Mai sausage, with each pasta tossed and lightly coated with explosive flavors. While the food runs anywhere from 180--300+ bahts, the wines are pricer, with most over 1000+. We had the house Australian shiraz, at 800 bahts, which proved smooth and delicious with the meal. The service was excellent and attentive, and the chef even made a special birthday dessert to accommodate allergies. A great place for a special occasion, or even a drop-in meal. Read less

The service was top-notch. The chef altered dishes to accommodate for vegetarians. The khao soi had mushrooms in place of meat and the meung kham had soy protein instead of dried shrimp. The atmosphere is quiet and elegant - perfect for a business dinner, couples, or for long chats with friends. Cocktails were 2 for 1 during happy hour - the passion fruit cocktail was yummy. If you have never had meung kham, you should try it. It's like assembling a small taco only with leaves as the base and fillings of fresh ginger, toasted coconut, onions, bits of lime, peanuts, chili, dried shrimp/soy protein and a palm sugar sauce - fold it all up and plop in your mouth - a really pleasant explosion of flavor. Alternatively, you can find meung kam at the weekend market - look carefully, (or ask a local), for wrapped green leaves folded up and skewered on a thin bamboo stick - three or four to a stick for about 20 or 30 baht. One of my favorite Thai snacks;) Read less
