ここは内装が豪華ですが、スタッフは笑顔を絶やさず、フレンドリーで居心地が最高です✩ their Interior is very gorgeous, but staff's smile and service are very friendly and comfortable. I like here ✩ 这里的装修是很豪华，不过服务员的服务态度是很好，面上带着笑容，动作和反应很快，让人觉得很舒服。 又多了一家喜欢的餐厅呢✩＾＾

Italian food is nicely presented and served to us. Huge portion for pad thai, recommended to share for small eater. Breakfast Egg Benedict is delicious which it flow out. Delicious food with nice ambience. Environment is cosy where we can relax. They greet everyone with smile on their face. Good customer service they gave to everyone.

One thing i appreciate the most during my visit was the service and the friendliness of the staff. They are very attentive and always have a very warm smile on their faces. I had the salmon eggs benedict and they are really good. They used brioche instead of muffins and the combination is just wonderful. The coffee is good and i like esp their fruit juices - healthy and fresh! I would come back and try their dinner menus one day.

Whilst Chiang Mai can boast many Italian restuarants at last Italics brings a touch of class to the city. A real 5 star quality restuarant with a great ambience serving delicious food wine and drinks. The restuarant is in its soft opening faze whislt the 5 star hotel is being completed, however the restuarant is completely finished and serving traditional Italian fare. Serving staff were friendly and professional. We chose the 4 cheese pizza, 13 inch thin base, this was the best pizza I have tasted in Chiang Mai, along with thier lasangne and a spicy Italian sausage dish, all delicious and not at all expensive. We will definatly be making a regular visit to "Italics"

As we had a long stay at nimman and we did had so much local food, khao soi, somtam. Finally we crave for something italian then italics restaurant at the new luxury hotel AKYRA is our choice. Foods is well present and great quality. Pretty authentic taste and many great thai menus as well. Recommended !
beautiful decoration and the food is yummy! especially the seafood soup! will try again if go to chiang mai. Highly recommend!
Every well decorated dinning room, the services staffs are super professional. The foods are very well cooked. Would come back again next time we're in chiang mai!

We arrived last night for dinner wondering where to park on soi 9 only to have an attended say "I'll park your car for you" From there it only got better. The setting is modern and very comfortable. The staff, uniformed and well trained, presented us a menu that had so many really nice options and very fair prices that we knew we would dine here more than once. The food presentation was like a picture, the tastes delicious and the portions required us to do "take away" for lunch the following day. Be sure to save room for dessert as it is both a tasty treat and a visual experience.During our meal the chef approached to introduce himself and be sure that all was as we wished. Italics is a wonderful addition to the Chiang Mai dining scene.We will return. . Read less
The restaurant just soft opening in Aug. Hotel will be opened soin. Staff are nice and always keep smile. Food and beverages are excellent. Will back again.

Relax ambience with good service. The pretty French Maitre d' is very helpful. Some inventive starters and good pizza especially where you can choose any 4 toppings from the menu like tasting 4 kinds of pizza. However, the linguine could have been more dante, it was a little bit overcooked.

My boyfriend and I ate at Italics on Friday night and we both enjoyed the impressive food, service and fancy atmosphere. The restaurant decor is magical - like sitting among giant floating candles, which are reflected back onto the ceiling and windows a thousand times. It's like a modern-chic version of the Great Hall in Hogwarts Castle. All dishes and drinks were beautifully presented and delicious. I had straw baked mussels, my boyfriend had cheese board. The mussels were served in the tinfoil tray on top of straw that they were baked in, which I really liked because you could see how it was cooked. For mains I had steak (฿750). It was cooked exactly how I specified & served with red wine sauce, potato croquette, rocket leaves and mushrooms. My partner had vegetarian pizza topped with tasty Royal Project vegetables (฿250). Dessert was creamy homemade banana cheesecake. SO GOOD. I also loved the milk, vodka and cinnamon signature cocktail 'Italics' - it was served over misty dry ice and tasted like a Christmas drink (฿350). I wish I'd ordered another. The chef Phubase was ready to answer questions and chat. The waiters were really friendly too, especially Bomb. I definitely recommend Italics, and I want to go back! Read less

We visited the new akyra Hotel in Nimmenheiman and had an awesome lunch at the italics restaurant. Khun Teera showed us around the stunning Hotel (which will open the next months) before we had our delicious 3 course Lunch Menu. The menu included: Four cheese pizza, caesar salad, slow poached prawns with parsley & shallots ceviche, seared tuna & prawn with black ink risotto, Mama's tiramisu. I wish i could have eaten more! Special thanks to khun Christoph and khun Bomb for the great service and hospitality!
Great service at Italics, lovely food, fab decor - unlike anywhere else in town! Congratulations, I look forward to my return visit.

Stopped by once for some delectable afternoon sweets which were simply sinfully seductive. Tonight had a great dinner with some highlight dishes such as the east meets west starter of endami and sausage cuts, bursting with flavour, a refreshing salad and so many other starter...we didn't actually get to the mains! Ended with the succulent banana something - woops, forgot name - which was rich and creamy without having the sweet saccharin aftertaste of so many sweets in this country. Service was personal and attentive (like wine ninjas, they'd stealthily fill up our glasses making sure we were well watered!) and the setting lovely and sexy. My new local? I think so. Next time will try to make it to the mains. Read less

I visited Italics at the new Akyra Hotel on Nimmanheiman, Soi 9 last night. I wanted to take my friend somewhere new and different for Mothers Day. The service started when I pulled up in front and asked where the parking garage was. Instead of directing me they offered to park for me. Excellent! The dining room was modern and attractive. The manager introduced herself and welcomed us and made great recommendations. The service staff was superb. I had the calamari starter which was cooked and seasoned perfectly. The calzone I had as a main was delicious. The real treat was the banana chocolate cheesecake! All in all a great experience. I expect to be a regular.

Italics restaurant in my opinion is the best Italian restaurant in Chiang Mai. I had a Vegetarian thin crust Pizza for lunch. Fresh baked bread was served with lunch. The quality of the pizza was what you would expect at a high end restaurant in Italy. The waiters and staff were all professional and friendly. I would highly recommend this restaurant for fine dinning. I'll return for sure.
Finally I found the best breakfast in nimman road . very fantastics in khai Krata menu at Italics restaurant in nimman 9 ..i will come back again in next month

We had an amazing lunch with a delicious italian pizza as starter - then a lovely piece of meat with great side served in a cocotte and a nice wine sauce served in a tiny pan. The restaurant design is very modern and original. And they have a great selection of wine which is important to notice in Thailand For sure this restaurant will become a big player in Chiang Mai city ! Bravo !

This must be the newest restaurant on Chiang Mai's dining scene and well worth a visit. Great location in the heart of Nimanheamin, Chiang Mai's hipster district and also the restaurant of soon to be the hippest new city hotel, Akyra Manor, opening in October I believe. The manager, Christophe, personally welcomed me and gave up his time to show me around the soon to open hotel, looks fantastic too. But back to the restaurant review, warm, friendly and professional service and superb food...the short rib beef with gnocchi was fantastic, I will be back for that, as were the mussels. They are so new that the pizza oven is yet to fire up but the chef, who actually came to see me at the table, lovely chap, told me that their pizzas will be Roman style, thin and crispy, all homemade on site in a wood fired oven which I could see at the back of the restaurant...so I will be taking my son there for pizza next week...see you there! Read less

From the second we sat down for dinner at Italics we were made to feel like royalty. They gave us a private table on their patio which made us feel like the only two people in the world. The customer service was superb. The server was attentive and the restaurant manager, Cyrielle Merveille, was outstanding. She came to check-up on us throughout our dinner and was extremely personable and professional. The food was delicious. Our favorite part was the gastronomy tiramisu dessert. Following the meal, both the executive chef and the resident manager of the hotel, Christophe Gestin, came to introduce themselves. We were truly made to feel special. The attention to detail from the service to the table to the food, was unbelievable. The staff truly made this experience a special and memorable part of our honeymoon. We will tell any of our friends and family traveling to Chaing Mai to visit the restaurant and stay at the beautiful hotel. Read less

Italics is a modern, Italian influenced restaurant with a difference in the exquisite Akyra Manor hotel in central Chiang Mai. The feeling that you have arrived at somewhere very special is evident as you fist arrive. The atmosphere is modern, sophisticated and polished while at the same time warm, friendly and welcoming. Start with a cocktail on the roof top bar with stunning sunset views, followed by a dinning experience you wont forget in a hurry. The menu offers classical Italian cuisine with an innovative, modern twist. You won't have a problem finding something interesting to drink on the international wine list. Highly recommend this place! We can't wait to come back on our next visit. Edward & Belinda Read less

Amazing steak and service, super attentive staff down to pouring your beer from your bottle when your glass gets low to letting you pick out your steak. Two live musicians playing relaxed music, really nice atmosphere. Was worth the longer trip if you are staying in the square.

Restaurants with a lot of culinary diversity and photo angles Whether it’s Thai or Southern, the food delivers. Importantly, the grilled meats are so diverse thanks to the pumpkin-like charcoal stove.

Good food and nice atmosphere, but despicable service. We haven’t had any good service, from wrong order coming to our tables, 2 orders lost, they were served after everyone had finished, serving dessert menu while the late dish were just getting started. Ignoring our table during the whole meal, not serving water to come at the end before we pay to empty 3 bottles we didn’t order. Then charging us the wrong amount for the bill. Given the price of that place, I would expect much higher standard

Stumbled across this place by accident but very pleased we did. Love the novelty of being able to pick your own piece of meat from the fridge added bonus of live singer creating chilled out atmosphere. Service was spot on, wine was great. Only one very small negative was that I didn’t find the seats very comfortable,the back cushions need to be bigger. Would definitely go back if we had more time. Also we thought it was reasonably priced compared to London.£70 ,2 steaks including sides, one glass of wine, one beer, one spaghetti Bolognese and soft drink.
A good experience with meat in Thailand. Very good quality meats Good service, menu with good options and a nice location.

The best gourmet restaurant in Chiang Mai in my eyes, and not overpriced considering the quality. A perfect mix of European and Asian dishes, the beef is delicious and the desserts are magical! The only downside is the service that is not up to the standard of what is on the plate, but it is forgotten from the first bite.

Came here for a steak dinner and the food quality didn't disappoint. The steak was cooked to the right doneness and very tasty. Each portion came with 2 sauces and 1 side. We ordered the peppercorn sauce which was very good. Not a 5 star rating because 1) the cold cuts appetizer was a little disappointing. The quality was only so so for the price and 2) the sides that came with the steak (we chose fries) was a very small portion compared to other places we've been. I think for the price they are charging, they can afford to be more generous with the fries. The dessert selection was also very interesting. Lots of choices. We really liked the live music when we were there. It was a guy with a guitar and the volume was just right, his singing was amazing. Really lovely experience! Read less
This restaurant has lots of cuisines, not just western cuisine, but also Thai and Chinese foods, we tried steaks, pork rib, clams, pasta, all tastes so good with reasonable price~
Two starters (both scallops).. arrived 25 minutes apart . And when the first one arrived it has to go back as not cooked ! Service was appalling … ended up paying and leaving before the mains !!!!

Mix is a notable restaurant known for its emphasis on quality meat and curated wine selection, sourced from various regions worldwide. Owned by a culinary expert, the restaurant promises a dining experience crafted with care and expertise. Situated in a bustling business district like Nimmanheamin Road, it caters to both locals and visitors alike. The restaurant’s standout feature is its meticulously chosen ingredients, promising a premium dining experience. The wine list, described as extremely interesting, adds an extra layer of sophistication to the meal. The staff’s attentiveness and the pleasant atmosphere further elevate the overall dining experience, making Mix restaurant a noteworthy destination for meat and wine enthusiasts alike. Read less
Wagyu meat was tasty until we saw a huge rat walking around. Employee was spoken to but without response. Perhaps a normal situation for them, but for me a sign of inadequate hygiene.
Excellent food, excellent service, Fantastic live music, Totally recommended. The best place I have eaten in Chiang Mai.
We had dinner here tonight and all I can say is WOW! All dishes are perfectly cooked. My favorite dish has been the premium pork ribs cooked for 48 hours! The dessert was exquisite too!

Shop welcoming guests from Baan Khaek Mueang Been eating here since ten years ago and keep coming every year. The taste of the food has never changed. What has changed is that the restaurant often has new menus. Both Thai and European After-meal cakes are presented and the place is continuously improved. It is one of the shops where you welcome friends from Bangkok. And it never disappoints. The atmosphere is good, the food is delicious, and there are singers in the early evening.

Impressive. 😀. The best Thai curry crab I have ever tried. 🦀 This Mix restaurant has left the best impressions! Hospitable hosts, delicious dishes, beautiful presentation, wide wine list and wonderful dessert. I recommend to everyone! I would like to come back here again and again. When you come to Nimman area in Chiangmai. Don’t miss. 🧡🎉🥃🦀

Delicious food, high quality, reasonable price, both Thai food and meat. There are many good wines to choose from. Whether you want to sip at the shop or buy souvenirs, it's good to come here and call it complete. The shop owner gives advice and takes care very well. The staff are lovely.
Famous restaurants in Chiang Mai that I would like to recommend you to try. There are many unusual dishes. And the taste is delicious and unforgettable.
Good vibes good restaurant and very good of food I really like this shop. The atmosphere is very good. There are singers too. The food is a 10/10.

I've lived in Chiang Mai since I was born, but I can't believe I've never eaten at this restaurant even though it's been open for more than 15 years. The food that impressed me was the sea crab stir-fried with yellow curry. The crab meat was very fresh. Because it's made from living crabs. The eggplant salad with grilled smoked salmon is also quite special. The salad is rich and has a hint of the sea. Satmon and eggplant have a light, smoky aroma that goes well together. The shop is beautiful both inside and outside. In the evening there is live music playing very beautifully. The staff here was very good service as well.

Came here for the first time. A friend brought me here. I was very impressed with the service, delicious food, and good atmosphere. There was live music. Secretly, I saw there were private rooms and a wine cellar. There were so many. I couldn't choose.
Delicious food, many good wines to choose from, and attentive staff. The beef steak is very good. You can park your car right in front of the shop, you don't have to walk far.

Four of us had dinner. Lovely outdoor secluded patio with atmosphere - at the end of the White Market. Extensive menu with many theatrical presentations - but it’s not all just for show: quality of the cooking is superb. Try a molecular cocktail and the signature blue dined dessert. One small note - service is sporadic and dinner does not arrive all at once. But it was still a memorable evening. All this and live music as well. Not inexpensive but we found it good value.

An unexpected fine dining experience worthy of a Michelin star at a fraction of the price. We found Mix by accident. A restaurant recommendation by Brazilians living in Chiang Mai to a Canadian couple we met at the poolside of our gorgeous boutique hotel! The 4 of us went together to forge a friendship, not expecting to be blown away by the food but we were. The ambience is lovely with outdoor seating in a coutyard on comfortable sofas. The menu expansive with a complete mix of Thai & European choices but specialising in steak. They even have a cold room from where you can choose your steaks from, with helpful staff offering full explanation of the cuts of meats. Having been in Thailand for 2 weeks, I felt the longing of less complex flavours of Thai food so opted for steak. Previous visits to Thailand, even in 5 star hotels, have been disappointing with steak offerings but Mix was exceptional. Our Friends had Salmon, Lamb rack and my parter Thai food. The pesentation and theatre of the dishes were only superceded by the flavours. Desserts were a work of art, something usually delivered by the best chefs in the UK on the Great British Menu. This restaurant easily deserves a Michelin star. Fabulous and so exceptional we returned on our 2nd evening in Chiang Mai. Only one minor criticism would be to deliver everyone's food at the same time but think that is more a cultural thing. This restaurant delivers excellent dining in a fabulous atmosphere with live music throughout the evening. Very attentive staff, exceptional value. Read less

We waited an hour after ordering before our food came. They brought out our steaks before our starters and all the food was completely cold. Don't waste your time going to this restaurant. Bad service, bad food and high prices. Most disappointing meal I've had on this trip!

I already went several times to MIX, which is really a venue which stands out in Chiangmai. The place is really great, with indoor and outdoor areas offering different atmospheres. When you arrive in the courtyard, you can't miss the extraordinary wine cellar, with a fantastic wine selection including the most renowned wines in the world. There are many wines on the menu with a very good choice of wines by the glass. The dinner was excellent and the beef perfectly cooked. Last but not least, keep room for a dessert when you go there, because they are amazing! Delicious and very well presented, worthy of the best pastry chefs. A special thanks to the owner Khun Chum and his team for their kindness and for the excellent service. Read less

Tom yum gong here is superb! The prawn is very big in the soup. It is a good combination of Thai, Western and Chinese food and our group was happy with the servings. Staff can improve their command of English if possible as we were lucky to have local with us. I saw the other table the customer were ordering using Google Translate.
Food here is spot on, great spot and excellent customer service. We went twice in 3 days. Both times as good as the other. I would totally recommend this place.

Little taxi ride to arrive at Mix. Warm welcome and the restaurant looks great. Service was very attentive and the wine selection excellent. We had 2 Thai starters, both very good. Afterwards we order Australian wagyu beef with sides. Very well cooked and presented. Of course when you order wine the overall bill is higher but very good quality and worth every bath. Definitely recommend when in Chiang Mai!

Worse experience ever. We booked a table in advance and we came from the other side of the city, after waiting for more than 1h and half for the food (there were many people and we were patient), they said that almost all dishes were finished (and still seing dishes we ordered pass to other tables). Meat was sticky and not ok. We complained and we asked the staff to bring the owner as he was around, but the owner decided to hide and not even apologize for the bad service. We spent almost 3h and we left asking the bill with our stomachs empty as they came 4 of our 11 dishes ordered for 6 people. Happy new year…..

Stunning all the way. The decor is pretty nice with tables inside (air-conditioned) and outside. Something unusual in most restaurants in Thailand: the menu is very nice to look at with a clear separation between each type of cuisine and beautiful pictures so pretty different than most menus in the country which are so often messy. As the name of the restaurant suggests there is a choice of different styles of kitchen but what is amazing is that the chef masters them all. Very difficult to suggest a particular dish as during our very many visits (20 at least) we have chosen many and all were between very good and fantastic. Products, even beef, are first-class. Preparations are very original but wonderfully tasty. This restaurant is clearly different from any other and the chef is not only very good but the man, charming we got to meet him, truly has ideas and good ones. Wine list is extensive at all price levels. As some other writers on trip advisor mentioned, wines are expensive but that is because Thailand's government had the pretty dumb idea for a country relying a lot on tourism to have the highest wine tax in the whole world at 400%. In fact wines at Mix are not more expensive than in other restaurants. A special mention for desserts, usually a disaster in Thailand. Here again, a huge choice and most of them are so pretty that one hesitates to touch them! What very few people now is that the restaurant has its own pastry shop named Saruda, 2 streets away on Soi 3. Service is nice and efficient. We live part of the year in Chiang Mai, have travelled the world and truly think that it is the best restaurant in town and you have many. Read less

Right smack in the middle of the vibrant Nimman area. The food was well presented, and quite interesting in term of the taste profile. A large variety in the menu encompassing li AL and international cuisines.

Nice outdoor seating and a friendly waiter encouraged us to order an expensive bottle of wine but it went downhill from there. The first dish to arrive was my main course, smaller than anticipated and already almost cold. Five minutes later my partner was told his starter was off so could he choose a different one. Another five minutes saw his main course arrive. Our starters came last (though still minutes apart). My smoked salmon on bread turned out to be tiny slivers of fish smothered in chopped tomato on rock-hard toast. I left hungry. Also the long menu had little for non meat eaters except prawn and crab dishes and almost nothing for vegetarians.
The food was decent. Order the ice cream balls at the end. Watching them prepare the golden one was very interesting. And the ice cream was tasty. Mango sticky rice was delicious too.

I have eaten several times at Mix in Bangkok and always enjoy it. The food in the Chiang Mai branch is equally good, although I did think the Roti served with the Green Curry were a little undercooked. The problem with the service was that I ordered a Mojito before later ordering the food. The mojito never arrived!.

This restaurant which is one of the top ten in the country is a must if one wishes to enjoy an excellent meal either in the afternoon or after dark. It serves mix Thai and western dishes and you will never be disappointed with their dishes. The price is above average but worth for what you pay out. The dessert they serve same like a crystal ball is a must.
Mix restaurant is a good restaurant for me have excellent food 🥘 service goood It's good chance to came this restaurant with my friends 🙍🏼👩🏻‍💼👨‍🔧👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓
This is the second time my girlfriend and I have ordered the “Toast” dessert. Perhaps one of the best desserts ever.... Highly recommend.

Been there before and took visiting family there for dinner. Service was appalling. 1hr waiting for starters I which time 1 main course arrived which we sent back. When the 4 main courses finally arrive every meal was cold and the one returned had been kept in the fridge and then partially microwaved as parts were ice cold. It is not a cheap restaurant and one would expect a much higher quality of food and service. Won't be back!

The food was fine. However, the service was exceptionally poor. The worst we have had in Thailand, in fact up there with the worst anywhere. At one point 7 staff were hanging about the front door chatting. When we needed to order anything we needed to attract their attention....this was difficult. Even when the manager or owner came out things didn't improve, only interested in the table who were clearly "instagramming" their entire meal. I have NO idea how this place wins awards. My friend broke a glass, it is unusual for a restaurant to charge 450b for this. We wouldn't have minded so much had the whole experience not been so poor and over priced. Maybe they need to focus on the basics like good food and service rather than gimics like dry ice coming out of meals. Read less
nice place to grab a drink and enjoy, quiet, and nice atmosfere, very innovative drinks, desserts and dishes

This restaurant has both outdoor and indoor table. For wine lovers, you will have something to go down with foods. Foods are nice & staff can get pretty busy at time. Give it a go when you are in Nimmanhaemin.

This trendy well-designed restaurant is located hidden at the bottom of Nimmanhaemin allay1 closed to One Nimman live style mall.They serve with Thai fusion cuisine all dishes are not only authentic look but also taste as real Thai style fine dining served.The seafood is extremely fresh as we’ve had in the southern.I do strongly recommend “Fried crab with curry powder” and “Fried Phuket lobster with noodles “ Both are so awesome and great for special events.Have to be back again & again absolutely.

Every time I come back to Chiang Mai I always come to Mix Bar & Restaurant. The food is excellent evertime. They use fresh and hard to find ingredients. The food both traditional and a mixture of European fine cuisine makes every meal taste exotic. I highly recommend. Don't miss out if you ever come to Chiang Mai this place is a must. You will not be dissappointed.
Very nice Thai food and they also served food with accurate taste for Thai standard very nice location as well surrounded by tourists placed! I enjoyed my food here.
Delicious food and very nice view of the restaurant, very good location as well Surrounding by many Tourist place we have many different tourist visit everyday around the world!!
Delicious food Nice service and great views of the restaurant good vibes. Not matter that you were here alone because you still have a wonderful time :)
Delicious food with friendly staff and pleasant surroundings. No loud music and have seating area inside and also inside with air con. Definitely worth a visit.

We've probably been to Mix four of five times now. The food is consistently good. The smoked duck salad with lemongrass relish, trademark crab cornets with red curry, and whole deep fried fish with Thai herbs all hit the mark. However, they still have the same issues with service that consistently drag the experience down. They certainly have enough staff, but most of them appear to be either playing on their phones or gazing off into space when you need them. The place desperately needs an experienced front of house manager to get things organised and show the pleasant but clueless staff how a restaurant at this price point should operate. And for us at least the novelty has pretty much worn off the round bottom cocktail glasses to the point where they are now just an inconvenience. Read less

Went there with all the wonderful reviews and definitely not recommended..... they have all the people in the restaurant, unfortunately, no ideas what they are doing.... spilled all the wine on the table with the fancy wine machine and did not even say a word or sorry.... food is mass production.....

So, went to Mix on a recommendation of a local friend. The dishes/meals were very good, fresh, testy etc but the service is just screwed up: mains come before starters, takes ages, drinks standing on the bar but nobody to serve us etc - if they get the service right, this place will be amazing!
Food are really yummy.There has many kind of food and drink to choose.Good atmosphere. One of the best restaurant in Chiangmai.
The crab dishes are so good. They are so fresh (Alive) . The way they cook the mud crabs are different from other places I have tried and they taste so good. Must visits when you are in CNX.

Every time I visit Chiang Mai, I have to come back to this restaurant. Amazing atmosphere and more importantly very impressive food presentation. The restaurant has a large variety of wine selection as well has well trained staff who was very informative and helpful.

Love everything here, especially the food and servers. Wanna come back and have wonderful dinner with my significant other again. Thanks my darling also to select such a wonderful restaurant like this on my birthday.
Good decoration, good services, nice workers and tasty foods. Variety of wines and easy to go. Signature food of restaurant is delicious.

Really nice decor and a fun fusion take on Thai food. We shared several small dishes and they were all good. There is a large selection and the only minus is that they didn’t have everything on the menu available.
I am totally annoyed by the poor service. The waiters and waitress are hanging in a group chitchatting. And didn’t take their job seriously, especially the waiter with eyeglasses.

The setting is nice and the food was fine. The waiting staff were hilariously bad. We had to repeat our order 3 times after our waiter forgot over and over. He then told us they were out of our starter, then came back later to tell us they were out of a main. In total we ordered our food 5 times. When our food arrived we had to ask 3 times for cutlery. They forgot the rice to go with the mains. After asking three times it was finally brought. The head waiter apologised and was very nice but the service let's down an otherwise decent restaurant.
Our first time here, it was awesome. You guys made our first dinner in CNX the best. We love your yellow curry crabmeat it's very fresh and also we tried red curry KU beef. We'll back for sure! :)
I ‘ve tried several times since i came back from Sydney. Food always splendid and dessert are fabulous !! Good place to hangout with family and friends. Friendly environment, lovely staff.
The best curry crabs in town. Nice place, Great food, Good staffs. Can not miss when you visit Chiangmai.

We arrived quite late (9pm) without a booking, but soon got seated indoors (it was too cold to sit outside would you believe!) The food and drinks menus are extensive with a large cocktail list, sushi, western and Thai food on offer, so allow at least 10-15 mins to go through it all. A couple of us opted for steaks which were very good, one had sushi and one had a duck dish, Everyone was very happy and the service was good too. Well worth a visit. PS they also do a cheesecake dessert under a large blue hemi-sphere which looked very tempting to try, but as it was late and we were all full, we didn't!

Architecturally an attractive place. Food (if and when it arrived) was quite OK. Service was so bad it turned into a comic farce. Probably OK if visited solo or in a small group, but don’t attempt with a large family, or be prepared to go home hungry Very disappointing !! Read less
The men is extensive and the food is fantastic with brilliant presentation, compared to Hua His the prices are reasonable.

My wife and I came here every night for a week. It has a nice relaxed atmosphere and very nice and attentive staff. The drinks are top notch and the appetizers really hit the spot. We will be back again the next time we visit CM.
A place that serves a wide variety of both food and drinks in the heart of the artsy fartsy Nimman area
I could not eat the below average sushi. Avoid it. The thai dishes were just ok, and pricey. The wine was not good. I like the branch in Bangkok way better.
Very arty.. Food is great.. The cake was amazing..the cake seems to be very famous in here. Friendly stuff.. Good place to hang out with friends.

The food were really good here. I tried the pork, its so delicious, every slice is so juicy, tender & taste so good. Highly recommended. The chicken salad is equally yummy! no doubt about it! Price is reasonable too! =)
We ordered seafood dishes: curry crab, lobster sashimi and hotpot and soft shell crab. The dishes were quite nice. Service was fast and the menu is very extensive.

Extensive menu, all with photos that represented the meals well. Great variety and outstanding deserts. We ate both here and at Dash which had a better rating on trip advisor, but we much preferred Mix. Also had an extensive wine collection. Would definitely go again!

We only had one drink and left. This restaurant appeared to be ok but the menu seemed to be mainly limited to seafood . We really had trouble communicating with our waiter as our thai language skills are poor and the waiters English was poor also. I'm sure this is a great place . It just didn't suit us after a long day

The menu is fusion and the food is confusion. While presented nicely, the appetizers we ordered, the uni bites, salmon and roe bites, scallop carpaccio, were all too salty. The uni and scallop ware not very fresh and the condiments over shadowed the ingredients. The curry crab was bland and hard to eat, they didn't offer us any wet towelettes or gloves. The BBQ ribs (for 4th of July) was lukewarm and forgettable, but nice to have a resemblance of home. They got our orders wrong despite repeated to us. For $80, not value for money.

Great place for family business and great place to hang out. Splendid food furnishing and nice taste. Perfect combination of Thai, Japanese, Chinese and Europe food including modern cuisine methods and the presentation. We love slow cook dishes both beef and pork, Japanese oyster , dessert. Well done - Thumb up !

Reading so many rave reviews of this restaurant, I checked out the photos and Facebook pages prior to arranging it as a meeting place to chill out with friends. Arriving at the restaurant, we were pleasantly greeted by the staff. Service was professional and always with a smile. Nice! We were ushered into the aircon room. The decor of the restaurant somehow does not quite reflect the professional photos. This is ok with us but we decided to change table after 15 minutes as we can hardly have a decent conversation with our friends who are visiting us. The crowd in the aircon room was largely tourists who can be quite vocal when talking. This is not a bad reflection of the restaurant in anyway. But I am pointing this out to pre-empt potential patrons who are considering to dine here on special occasions and who are looking for privacy and romantic dining. Well, this may not be the best place. We ordered several dishes such as grilled pork on lemongrass, river prawns salad, chicken pandanus leaves, fish mousse balls amongst others. It can however be frustrating to learn that not every dishes (including a red wine) we wanted were not available. But admittedly, the wait staff were professional to apologise and help us through the menu. The food is mediocre - nothing outstanding but nothing taste bad either. The presentation of the food especially the fish mousse balls and grilled pork on lemongrass looked amazing. But taste wise, maybe ... just maybe ... it was toned down to cater to the many tourists. The wine list is actually very good. The staple beer and cocktails are all in there. The beverages were priced similar to restaurants of the same range. Dining outdoor was actually very relaxing especially on a balmy evening under the stars. I have mixed feelings reviewing this restaurant as seeing the several awards it got awarded, I expect it to be better than what I have experienced. There are no hits and a couple of misses therefore warranting a very average rating. Read less
The food is super but the service is so so. All staff need to be improved their skill and their product knowledge is lacked.
My wife and I ate here in April 2017 but we make a point of stopping by every time we are in Chiang Mai.The variety and quality of food brings us back. The service and atmosphere are good too.

Was recommended by young guy at our hotel reception. Pleasantly supprised several other tables occupied by a mixture of people. Influence leaning Japanese but it is a real Asia Fusion menu with seperate Japanese Chinese Thai and Western food. Pull it off weell an not over the top expensive. Chicken curry northern style was very good as was a Chienese pork dish an a side of greens...kale I think. Service was OK and friendly. Comfortable couches with big pillows. Worth a visit. Read less

I came here with some of my Thai friends and it was a great night. The food was delicious and there was a huge range to choose from. I couldn't fault any of the taste of the presentation. So much so I forgot to take pictures of some dishes before I started eating! The menu was very accomodating having Thai, English and Chinese translations and the service was excellent as my beer was never empty! They even collaborated with my friends to give me a surprise birthday cake. Lovely place and I would definitely recommend

Our concierge recommended this restaurant to us . We were able to walk from the Akyra hotel. The 4 of us ate outside due to the wonderful weather . It is a very large menu with something that would appeal to everyone. My husband and I shared a few courses- the pad Thai was quite unusual in its preparation and delicious. Be sure to get the pineapple sorbet prepared and served table side . It is scooped out and mixed with liquid nitrogen. It is quite a show and so delicious!

We always choose this restaurant to celebrate something important, the food is very tasty, the cocktail are amazing and the service is perfect. For this quality of food its not expensive. I really recommend this place if you are living here or just passing around. A must do in Chiang Mai.
Really nice place for a night out, especially in the outside part of the restaurant. Friendly service, huge menu - but good overall quality.

The first time we visited Mix Restaurant - in late 2015 - was delightful. This time it was disappointing and we left upset feeling literally ripped off. Our food didn't arrive until 40+ minutes after ordering even though it wasn't crowded when we arrived. The food was beautifully presented but our 3 plates were all disappointing... not very tasty. For example, the coconut encrusted shrimp was undercooked (cool and doughey inside). WARNING: When we received the bill I gave them my credit card, 5 minutes later the waiter came back and said, "We can't accept your credit card unless if you spend more than 1000 Bhat."(US$29) That was written nowhere and they had a "Visa accepted" sign and I pointed these things out. He said, "It is your bank that says it must be over 1000 baht, yours is only 460 baht." I knew this was untrue, I'd used my credit card for much less many times in the region, and said so. He called over another person - manager? - who said, "No problem, sir" and returned with a receipt for me to sign. I signed the smaller credit card receipt and then he handed me the larger itemized receipt and I saw the total: 680 baht! (220 baht / US$6.30 and nearly 50% more than the original price). The prices were triple most of the other restaurants we ate at during our 12 days in Chiang Mai, but the food was just not that good (though the atmosphere was atractive and the food was prettily presented). The service was so-so, in part because of the language barrier, in part due to the popularity of the place, but in general the wait staff seemed overwhelmed. Read less
Date_Written: nowhere and they had a "Visa accepted" sign and I pointed these things out. He said, "It is your bank that says it must be over 1000 baht, yours is only 460 baht."

Detail: Hard to find, but it is definitely worth it if you have a few extra bucks to spend on a gourmet dinner or lunch. The menu is huge and has just about everything including items from other countries.
Good food ( fusion food) , atmosphere & service….Only the restroom is not at the right place ' (next to the kitchen)

Couldn't be a better place to take guests....they do it well...very well. The menu has an impressive amount of choice...they are the masters of old classics reborn in a new way......this is no second rate restaurant. First class service...cutlery and glassware state of the art..... The service was first rate, much like fine dining of old. They know how to handle wine, the staff are well taught in the principles of fine dining. It's a nice dining room, excellent food and beverage and service. What's truly surprising is the price......there's a reason why they consistently win fine dining awards.....this will continue if they keep up the standards they have Reached so far.....they get it right.....they even get the bombed Alaska right. Treat yourself to a good night, the place where it's nice to get dressed up and enjoy fine dining.......overall it's a 10 out of 10. Read less
This is a pretty trendy chic restaurant and the food is pretty darn good too. It's not overly expense either and the service is great.

It's been 18 months since our last visit, but Mix continues to impress. They've cleaned up the front area which has added a lot to the ambience. It now looks as good as it's food offerings. This time we went for the crab in cones, forest mushrooms with a great pork and shrimp relish, and a whole fried fish with Thai herbs. (Too full for dessert). All of it was terrific. Add a couple of Kamikaze cocktails and with a tip it's still under $A50. A great bargain. Service is friendly and enthusiastic, although not overly professional. Sit outside if the weather allows-they'll put a mosquito coil under your table. Highly recommended. Read less
We went to Mix with high expectations given the hype on the internet. The truth is that it is noting more than gimmicks. Food quality is mediocre and service is non-attentive. Will not recommend.

Sent to this place by our host. The menu is so big, we could not decide. Finally we ordered and they brought us the most delicious salmon sushi i have ever had. The presentation of the plates was spectacular! Recommend the chocolate dessert with orange. Love it!!

Wow.... What a pleasent surprise!!! They have a menu with about 200 dishes... Normally this is not a good sign in general... But.... Each and every dish we ordered was excellent!!! They have a lot original dishes that you don't find elsewhere, like curry cones, short rib in paneng curry, and some rare vegetables. Most dishes are Thai, or a twist on a traditonal Thai dish... But they also have a wide European selection... Try the wagyu beef, which you cook yourself on a little grill with a leaf on it.... Or the wagyu tartare which is delicious too. They have an extensive winelist, with an amazing Amarone 2010 for only 2650 Bath. Enjoy!!! Read less
Large selection menu, lots of sushi included. Recognized yearly as one of the best restaurants in the area. Nice ambiance seating in/outside. Recommend.
Went here with 2 other couples for dinner and ordered a bunch of appetizers and drinks. Their cocktails are quite interesting. I highly recommend the Crab meat curry dish...so good!
The restaurant is nice. A lot of food and drinks selections. The slow cooked paneng beef was really good. Although the price is quite up there, this place is recommended.

I'm usually very skeptically about fusion food. This restaurant is recommended by foreigner living in Chiang Mai. So I gave it a try. It is one of the better fusion food I experienced. The seafood with mixed vegetables still maintained the nice and strong Thai spices and taste. The scallop salad with fruits is fresh and well presented. They only accept cash if spending below B1000. For such an upscale restaurant, this is a bad, unwelcoming rule.
Fusion food, presentation and quality are both good. Sits in the end of an alley but it is easy to find. Must try their desserts - highly commended and amazing.

Great place to visit in Chiang Mai. Went twice in this restaurant and sit in their garden. Their innovative cocktails are a must try and food is exceptional from the starter to the dessert. Memorable experience

We went to this expensive restaurant on a prominent Soi 1 on nimmanhamein road of Chiangmai hoping to get to-die-for Thai food but was so disappointed. The restaurant is tastefully done, outdoors and indoors both. It was very noisy inside and service staff also need some professionalism. Almost had to step out and catch hold of them thrice to place an order or ask for bill! The menu is very bulky - and large. May be it is a style but perhaps need to be redone. Food we ordered was Thai masaman curry but when it arrived the portion was small, it was not even close to masaman curry we have had earlier in Thailand and came with very oily and very badly done roti breads. And to top it all the bill was almost 2-3 times what we would have paid in a good place elsewhere in Chiangmai. This might be a good place for rich to wine and dine and enjoy delicacies from international cuisine like Chinese food etc but for Thai food please go elsewhere! Read less

The settings Is in good location, plénty Of choices thai japanese chinese and western foods, reasonable price. Chang béer 100 bht. However , service staff could be improved, With a smile or learning How to serve.

The food was of a very good standard. We went as a group of 4 and tried a variety of dishes from the Thai, Japanese and western menu. However I tried to order 4 different beers from the menu and everytime I was told they didn't have any available. Also we had to go and look for staff if we wanted anything.. Most were sat inside on their phones. Good Food.. Service needs abit of improvement.

This was our last meal in Chiang Mai. Was expecting good quality of food, for its good rating in TripAdvisor. Maybe a variety of international food would taste better, we did not order since we wanted to have Thai food for our last meal. We were both quite disappointed with the Thai cuisines we ordered. Worst meal in our five-day trip. You could find far better Thai food in much cheaper restaurants. The stir fried glass vermicelli was terrible. Terribly oily. The salted egg yolk in the dish tasted like plastic and was badly flavoured. The crabs in the dish "deep fried soft shell crab" were nicely fried. But the fried green vegetable (sorry I couldn't recognize the type of vegetable) in the dish was oddly bitter and made the whole dish tasted weird. The banana milkshake was the only highlight in the meal. The rest of the dishes we ordered, I don't wish to name them all, I've tasted far better ones in much cheaper restaurants in Chiang Mai. Read less

This restaurant has a good selection of food (Thai, Chinese, Japanese & International) that is nicely presented when served and tastes quite good. There is a choice of outside, A/C and sushi bar seating. We chose outside as the temperature was not unpleasant but unfortunately, the flies are quite bad this year in Chiang Mai so we were constantly shooing them off the food. Not really the restaurants fault but maybe a few more bug zappers would help. They have an extensive wine selection and unlike many places here in Chiang Mai, appeared to actually them in stock, The service was adequate though the staff did have a tendency to group together and yak amongst themselves rather than taking care of their customers. I was also surprised because to get to the toilets, you basically have to walk through the kitchen. Minor annoyance and I will certainly go there again and recommend to others. Read less

This is a stand alone bungalow at the end of the road and didn't look like anything special - wrong! Comfortably airconditioned interior (there are tables outside for those of us who like the heat). The menu is large and varied. Interesting food, traditional ingredients many presented fusion style. What you order/receive may not bear a physical resemblance to what you think you're getting. Take "Deep fried Red Curry Crab" for instance, it comes in a holder that would normally be used for a Japanese Hand Roll (temaki to which it bears some resemblance) and is rather nice. We also had stir fried Sunflower sprouts with oyster sauce, 5 hour Braised Pork Belly Southern Thailand style, Steamed fish mousse in seafood chili paste, Green Pork Curry with Roti, Grilled pork with lemongrass served with hot spicy sauce, Spicy Banana Blossom salad served with slow cooked egg AND Crab Meat Dipping SauceThai style with Vegetable. For dessert, we had Special Dumpling in coconut cream modern Thai style, Candied Banana and a "red dome" with berries and ice cream. The desserts were nice but this "dome" was fun as flaming alcohol is poured on the dome which then collapses to reveal the berry contents- the contents were nice but it was the spectacle that was the fun. The food was good, the presentation pretty but for me the stand-out dish was the aforementioned Crab Meat Dipping SauceThai style with Vegetable ( i think it has a local name - "Lone"). It was not something i had tried before and it was incredibly moreish. It was a place i would have liked to have gone to again to try out other menu items had there been time but sadly it was not to be. Really worth the trying Read less

We took a couple of friends who were visiting from OS for a fairwell dinner. Ordered 9 courses, a bottle of wine and a couple of cocktails. Food was terrific, served promptly by friendly staff. The guests were completely blown away by the crab curry & pretty impressed by the entire Mix experience. It all came to 1000 Baht per head, which we all agreed was excellent value for money. I struggle with some of the negative feedback on this restaurant, we've been here many times & it has always been exceptional.

I had heard great things about MIX, even that it was in the top 150 restaurants of the world on more than 1 restaurant critics list. So I was really excited when I was invited to dine at MIX by friends who live in Chiang Mai.. We arrived Sunday night at the restaurant, noting there were only a few customers, and sat down in a beautiful setting. The ambiance is very nice, lot's of art and wonderful décor. We ordered 9 dishes to sample the recommended fare. The first dish was a chicken curry which for some reason had a lot of tiny broken bones mixed in. I couldn't eat it, and moved on to the next dish. The duck was good, not fantastic, no complaints there. The next dishes were ok, but nothing spectacular, the sauces tasted too sweet and very chemical, like they came out of a bottle or a can? They certainly were not comparable to other restaurants I had tried in Chiang Mai. The staff are very nice, but kept getting orders wrong, and didn't seem to know anything about the food, the contents or what was being served. I figure they must be new and that can happen anywhere in Thailand. The deserts were a nice touch, the display is well done, but the contents were small, although I must add they were the best thing I had eaten all night. We had problems ordering drinks, for some reason the bartender thought that Drambuie was Kahlua and it came down to us showing the bartender the difference between the contents of the bottles. It may have just been a bad night, or the chef, bartender, manager, and head waiter took Sunday night off. If that's the case they should be closed on Sundays. If not, the owners need to check up on the state of things, especially if they want return business. Read less

First time visiting a restaurant in the last few years with out reading the reviews before hand. The food was amazing and the presentation certainly matched.We shared the dishes and so glad we did as I my taste buds where in over drive. We sat outside and there looked to be a few different sections with an indoor bar area as well.I will let the photos do the rest of the talking.

Don't be put off by negative reviews of this place. They are probably written by the owners of surrounding restaurants! This place claims to be fabulous and I think the claim is justified. When you look at the menu you just immediately think "how can any restaurant offer such a variety and still nail every dish?" Well we had a table of 8 hungry And pretty fussy who nearly all ordered a different dish, from curries to pasta to duck breast and there were nothing but rave reviews about every dish. The service was spot on and the restaurant has a great look and feel whether you sit inside or out. Even the wines and the wine waiter were first class. Over the table including 2 bottles of French wine and other drinks the cost was about $25AUD a head.... Superb value for the quality, presentation and great taste of the food and the great service. If you want to get away from Thai this place is a must if your in the area... It's Asian fusion which means a bit of everything, including steaks, seafood, pasta, the works. And don't be afraid to go for almost anything on the menu. I'm sure it would all be excellent and we will be back to try some different dishes before we leave. Read less

When you look at the menu you just immediately think "how can any restaurant offer such a variety and still nail every dish?" Well we had a table of 8 hungry And pretty fussy who nearly all ordered a different dish, from curries to pasta to duck breast and there were nothing but rave reviews about every dish. The service was spot on and the restaurant has a great look and feel whether you sit inside or out. Even the wines and the wine waiter were first class. Over the table including 2 bottles of French wine and other drinks the cost was about $25AUD a head.... Superb value for the quality, presentation and great taste of the food and the great service.

Detail: We've been here a few times and really enjoyed the food and service. Unfortunately we ordered food for takeaway instead of eating at the restaurant. Two of four dishes we ordered, fried herb fish and soft shell crab, we inedible. The fish was old and chewy, not crispy. The crab had been packed with a lot of juice/water in a bag and was soggy instead of crispy. We returned to Mix to exchange or refund the dishes and the old lady manager did nothing but yell at us, insisting that there was nothing wrong with the dishes. No sorry, just yelling and rudeness. She inspected the bags/boxes we returned and decided we were entitled to a half refund. We did at some of both which is how we knew they were not any good. Ridiculous... I don't particularly like to make a second trip to a restaurant unless I truly have to when dishes cannot be eaten. I wouldn't even bother to write a review if we had gotten fresh replacements of full refunds and a "sorry." The location at Central Festival has always been great, so it's management issue at this location. We won't be back and will discourage others to avoid. Read less

The menu was heavy and full of heavily edited photos. It had a dated feeling, like it was fusion from the nineties. There were Chinese, Thai, and Western dishes, but based on my experiences, the menu exceeds the restaurant's ability to execute. I gave Mix a try because it was popular among locals (mostly expats). I ordered two dishes-one thai dish, hor mok (thai seafood mousse) and one western dish the seafood pasta plus pineapple juice. Both dishes were made without finesse or skill. The hor mok was large but didn't have much seafood in it. The pineapple juice didn't taste fresh and barely filled half of the glass. The pasta was not cooked well and the seafood was overcooked. This was by far the most expensive meal I had in Chiang Mai, and was very disappointing. Read less

We were recommended this restaurant by someone local and it didn't disappoint. The menu isn't Thai so don't expect a pad Thai but the sushi bar is so good and the cocktails are pretty cool. Was definitely worth the money, and the staff and setting are brilliant.
We liked the outdoor setting and decor very much and rhe food was good but service was slow qnd food arrived in he wrong order.
Good food, good atmosphere ! I love to have my dinner here with friends. Not noisy not crowned. delicious and worth to pay.

Nice gardenrestaurant with possibility of aircon room, delicious new tastes, good selection of vine, good desert (fried banana) good service, really worth the money. Remember to ask for the last wine in your bottle. Will soon be back to test some of the other good stuff!
Good food and good atmosphere many selection of wine and k.ten he very good take care. I will come back.
Last Night, I've gone to Mix again. It's still be good in taste and service. I love here. I'll come back more and more.
I'd like to say that the salad here tastes really good!! It's exactly the taste that I like. Food here is really tasty. And the location is peaceful and quiet.

If you want some fusion thai food or a break from thai food, it's a right place for you. The outdoor environment was nice for Dec night in Chiang Mai. I loved the salmon avocado tartare. We would recommend for friends who visit Chiang Mai.

We've had really great dining experiences in Chiang Mai so far, but our lucky streak ended here. First off, service was atrocious and the staff gives preferential treatment to certain customers: we were left stranded for 10 minutes to find our own seating. The huge stack of menus were hastily slapped on our tables, as our waiter was too busy bowing at other customers and leading them to their seats. (On the other hand, there were no bows or polite hand gestures of any sort directed to us, nor was there any sort of acknowledgement or goodbyes when we left) We were not informed that we would be charged extra for water and ice. The alleged "signature" drink recommended by our waiter was horrendous – completely made of syrup and artificial flavors. The menu is a mess: There are few restaurants that can pull off Japanese, Chinese, Thai and European food at the same time, and this place was NOT one of them. The shrimp soup with truffle foam I had as an appetizer was way over-seasoned, leaving me quenched for its (pricey) water. Overall, we probably would have had a much better time eating off one of the food stalls on Nimman Rd. Consider yourself warned. Read less

Lets say i didnt eat there . My and my husband just walk for ard 15 minutes before to reach the place It was hot and sweaty We asked if was ok to eat indoor. Place was empty but we cannot eat indoor So we saw there s a couple of westerner in the area with the bottle They reply us That they were rhe owners plus that area didnt open till 9 pm....so not luck again So they gave us a table in a terrible spot We left then. Is not important if food s good ....service is ridicolous. Never ever again Read less

We went there on Christmas night and although not a special night for Chiang Mai, it was a special treat for us! From appetizers with tempura like shrimps, fresh oysters wrapped in salmon, mango salad, to Northern style curry. Every dish was tasteful and an aroma explosion. It come out on a fair price for Thailand, not every night ...just every other night!

Today l had lunch at the latest branch of MIX at the North Hills Golf Course, decor is great, inside and outside dining, a very extensive menu of Thai and International dishes. They have an excellent bar area to relax with cocktails prior to dining, there is a good wine list at affordable prices, the staff are very experienced and friendly, l will be back soon, there are just so many dishes to taste, l wish you every success with this new venture

I was taken to this restaurant by some locals, and returned again the following night with my daughter. The décor is very chic, and the food a fusion mix of thai/Vietnamese/European taste. The cost was a little more than in standard restaurants, but definitely well worth it for the experience of the atmosphere and cuisine
This is my 2nd visit to the restaurant. The fried kway teow is very delicious which is very different from Singapore. Overall the food is perfect and price is reasonable.

Have visited Mix Restaurant many times - great extensive menu covering Thai, Italian, Chinese and Japanse cuisines. Eat inside or outside, wide selection of wines. Chefs making sushi in outside open kitchen. Parking easily available around the end of Soi 1 - Nimmerheimen Road.

When visiting Chang Mai, a friend who lives there had been told about this place. We went and the interior of the restaurant was a modern style fitting what they are trying to convey in a menu. Even though we are open to eating about everything, the menu really was not very impressive, and the prices were way too high for what you get. You can get much better tasting dishes in the nearby streets. The time to get drinks out was 15-20 minutes (even beer took that long), and the food took a long while too. The restaurant was getting a little worn out on the inside, the seats with the small circle cushions, a few places of paint on the wall needed retouched, and the bathrooms were disgusting. If you are in Chang Mai, don't take a side trip to this restaurant (if you can find it even). It is not worth the price, and the food is not great enough quality. Read less
The waitress recommendation was excellent. I tried their crab stick appetiser, steamed cod fish & their award winning signature dish - Panang pork curry which was superb.
Must try their signature stew curry pork, very nice. Service was good but make sure arrive before 2pm for lunch otherwise they will be closed until 6pm.

The food is not bad, but you can find better, far better even in the street. What I disliked ? We ordered cocktails and appetizers to begin with. We had the cocktails (separately, anyway), the appetizers just after, fine and then 2 minutes after the main courses, not at the same time. Weird to drink the cocktail with the food. We laughed with a disappointed local client nearby, who ordered a soup, a fish tartare and BBQ ribs, he got a weird order too, they first brought the ribs, ten minutes after the tartare and finally the soup... Lots of mosquitoes but that's part of the game and I don't blame the restaurant for this Read less

We tried a variety of Thai fusion delicacies like massanman curry with roti, deep fried shrimp with almonds. The highlight was the Global Warming dessert - a blue sky chocolate dome and secret dessert inside. Great value.

I been there on August 2015 with my friend from Japan. Restaurant atmosphere is good there is wine cellar private area too (good for private group around 10-20 people) Food is modern Thai cuisine and molecular cocktail style. tastes of food is average but restaurant service is great

We went for Friday lunch. Had all appetizers, 4 to share for two. Started with fried oysters in tartar. So light and delicious! Next was spicy crab in crunchy cones. Third was steamed squid in spicy dressing with red bird chiles. Wow. Last but not least, gorgeous scallops in red curry. Mmmmm! Can't wait to go back. Cool soundtrack, comfortable surroundings, good waitstaff. Five star baby!

Beautiful environment like fine dine restaurant but in cheaper price! The food are average yummy. I love the apple sobet as dessert. This is a fusion cuisine, there are lotsa choices to be chosen. Good place for dinner! =D

We made a return visit to Mix after an absence of 4 years, and were pleased that the food continues to impress, even as the empire has expanded. On a rainy night we chose to eat inside. The three dishes we had: smoked duck and lemongrass salad, crispy fish red curry, and a spicy beef curry (which turned out to be massaman style) were all excellent. They also have Beer Lao and good cocktails. The only change I'd be making is to ditch the TV (thankfully on mute for dinner) and get rid of the empty water bottles and other clutter from the front bar area, which would bring the surroundings up to the standard of the food. Not cheap, but highly recommended. Read less
Great taste !! Mix have various dishes, Nice desserts and drink. This restaurant is on the Best location on Nimmanhemin rd.
Food was good! Great atmosphere for friends and hangout. One of the waiter messed up our order but it's ok he apologized. Recommended.

This is a classic example of ambitions without the backing of craftsmanship. The restaurant is ambitious and that's great.But the decor and the presentation the food has taken over and what's missing is basically taste - both in food and decoration. Get me right, it's not bad. as in BAD! But it's just not interesting either. Everything is so so, nothing really takes off, but it's obvious th owners really wants to create something unique. Hope you get it right. In the mean time Iød recommend guests to try other options. Read less

The place look nice, but the good thing stop there. The service was maybe the worst i have seen. The waitress was pretentious but did not know anything about food or drinks. We order a cheeses plate who came full of fruits, and fruits who don't go along with cheeses like cherry and kiwi. We were planing on dinner her but we just wait for our friends and went another place. The cocktail were not good (not that bad either) but are charged like premium drinks. This place is really pretentious and overpriced, there are a lot off better options in Chiang Mai. Will definitely not going back Read less
Good food. Thai fusion. At very reasonable price. We asked to sit inside the air-con room but still got attacked by mosquitoes. So come prepared.
Great food, well-presentation! chiang mai popular hot spot for hang out! Feeling like having dinner at home

I've been to this restaurant many times and I've never been disappointed. Tasty food for a valuable price. Especially, I like how they create food and desserts that give us new experience. Mix is absolutely one of my favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai.
I have visited this restaurant over 10 times during the past three years, both for business dinners and pleasure with friends. I have never been disappointed even once.
My orders was served very quickly. Their are very good taste. I like 36hrs - pork ribs, and Apple Sorbet with Vodka. I'll come here again.

Great menu and must try for gastronomer. This place present delicacy modern thai cuisine (Incl. Intalian and Japanese) but yet maintain authentic taste. Nice ambience and friendly service. I like the 36hours pork spare rib served with red curry sauce the most,pairing with a bottle of Chateau Grand Destieu - Saint-Emilion Grand Cru 2010. It's amazing. Wonderfully finishing with Thai innovative desserts.

Mix restaurant Chiang mai have nice dessert ex Shredded coconut meringues chocolate Truffle in thai style with coconut ice cream and Tapioca pearls with Cantaloupe,milk, young coconut , tapioca pearls cracker and cantaloupe sorbet (but when u can wait 15 minutes) ^^ I confirm 2 menu nice more becoz 2 menu good taste ,good format beautiful And can eat at Mix restaurant Chiang mai only . ^^ I like Mix Restaurant Chiang mai 👍🏻

This restaurant was touted as the best in Chiang Mai, but it must be the best in only a very niche area that caters for tourists' tastes. The menu is very comprehensive and expensive ($10-20/dish), but the thai dishes do not fare well against just about every other affordable street side offerings. The crab is good but is a very small portion. The curried rice is nice, but there's really very little meat. It is fine (read: small) dining in one sense of the word. If you don't drink, you are given a place outside the air-conditioned area where mozzies roam and ignore the incense that the staff burn to ward them away. But it doesn't work; the mosquitoes know when a tourist is there. Save your time and eat where the locals eat. Read less

Tasted only fish and sea food, all excellent, loved the salmon avocado salad. Had some calamari , shrimp puffs, and curried scallops. 2 entrees and 2 main course dishes for 2 people came to just over 1000 baht, not cheap by local standards but worth every penny. Everything was tasty and beautifully presented. The place was full so obviously very popular. My only complaint was a restaurant of this standard should not have mosquitoes flying around in the indoor section, perhaps something can be done? Other than that top marks for everything.

Visited twice to this restaurant during my stay at Bangkok. Thought it will be expensive but found out that the price is quite average compare to the one we visited at the roadside. The food was delicious but less choices of beverages. Mostly are all alcohols. I wish they have coconut shake. Overall it was good. Can go try it when you are at Terminal 21!

Mix offers diverse fusion cuisine at a fairly steep price for Chiang Mai standards. However ,the food that is served justifies the prices that are offered. Someone complained about 300 baht corkage but in Thailand you can't buy a good red for less than 2000 baht due to their alcohol tax, so an extra 300 thb for wine drinkers is hardly a deal breaker. Comments about the service seem somewhat justified. Service was slow, haphazard, and reluctant to clear plates in a timely fashion. However, overall on food and dining experience I'd label it very good (i.e. above average). Read less

The setting & ambience at mix are chic eclectic, the staff are attentive, the dinning experience is well above average. The menu is extensive, it cover four major cuisines, Chinese, Japanese, European (French Italian influence) & of course Thai, thus the menu is segmented into these separate styles with crossover dishes appearing more than once. Never the less, a vast selection to choose from. The same can be said for the beverage list it to is extensive & of wide ranging quality. I could make many suggestions on what to order here, the food is very very good & there is something for everyone here except for those timid palettes who recoil at any food with flavour. My personal favourite is the crab curry & for that rare treat, the chocolate lava pudding. Read less

Generally try to avoid mall restaurants, but the Mix was a nice find. Extensive menu with cuisine ranging from Thai to all manner of western dishes. Nice selection of drinks as well.Attractive presentation of tasty dishes.
We stumbled upon this restaurant and what a find it was. The food was absolutely delicious. It is just off the beat and track. Lots of locals and few tourists.

So we had heard that the Nimarnhemin streets were full of nice little boutiques and worth an explore... what no-one told us is that they mostly shut at 6pm!! So, go in the day if you want to browse the shops first. Luckily we had seen reviews for Mix restaurant before we set out.... otherwise we are unlikely to have found it. It is at the very end of Soi 1 and set amongst apartments and smaller B&B type places. However, the restaurant itself is lovely... really nice seating options indoor and out. We sat outside as we were with our three kids and it seemed more informal out there. We had read so many mixed reviews so our expectations were low.... and we were really pleasantly surprised. Key points: The menu has photos of all dishes - so its easy to order and the food is actually very well presented. They have very good sushi options... it was way fresher than another sushi restaurant we had tried in Chiang Mai. You can totally mix and match what you order... you can have sushi, Thai, Indian.... (the last two have a contemporary twist in terms of their presentation). The food arrives when its ready so don't go there expecting to get your starters first....mains after.... it just doesn't work that way. If you adjust expectations accordingly, you will have a lovely evening. Prices were reasonable and came to about £50- £60 for a family of 5 (including wine & beer). Kids loved the strawberry smoothies. We loved the crab cones, soft shell crab curry, and green chicken curry with paratha. Salmon sashimi and rainbow rolls were also lovely. Read less

Our visit was on a Saturday night and Mix was buzzing, as was the local area. The first thing you notice is the extraordinarily large menus with pictures of each dish. We had crab cones, marinated prawns wraped in smoked salmon and a beautiful salmon terriaki dish. All had amazing presentation and treated great. If you like cocktails then the range is again awesome. I had a hot smoking mojito which had a nice dose of jalapeño. The service was excellent and the dishes arrived so fast that we decided to order one at a time. Hope your experience is as good as ours was. Last day today so cannot give it a second try this visit. Read less
It is on the good location, Nimmanhemin Soi 1. There are a lot of recommended menu. One of my favorite is "the Global Warming". It is a very creative dish!

Mix @ Nimarnhaemin Rd. Soi 1 Excellent food! The robust taste of this Thai fusion cusine was most delightful. The sushi bar was also excellent, sushi was super fresh and very high quality. Very hard to find this grade sushi in Thailand. A short chat with the sushi chef and I learned that almost everything in the sushi bar was flown in fresh daily. That was music to my ears "fresh" not frozen. My clients were delighted with the food and the large selection great wines. Though at the time of my visit it was quite busy, but the food was served in a timely manner. I highly recommend this place. Read less

Great atmosphere with modern and conservative decoration. The food was really amazing, every dishes just like a piece of art , very well present , really creative and mostly delicious with reasonable price. The staff were really friendly and helpful, We really enjoyed dinner and drinks there. Perfect night !!

There was little to find good about this venue. Staff were diffident, tables were not clean and one sits on stools. Food was very average and served in a haphazard manner. One main arrived before the starters. We sent it back but the next main arrived before anyone had finished their starter. Then other people had to wait twenty minutes for their main after the first person. We did have a nice bottle of wine though! The only saving grace of a very disappointing evening. Don't waste your time with this one!

Location: at the end of nimman soi 1. Surrounding: nicely open air and inside dining spaces, very relaxed. Thai and expats seemed to enjoy dining there, but we were unlucky to be seated next to some drunk rowdy guests. Food:: looked posh, tasted on the lower end of average, and it was also modified for the western palate. The serving order was bad, served deep fried fish before the salad... which does kill appetite. Pirce is good, considering the surroundings. Verdict: If u have Thai speaking friend, can consider coming, as you can specify your food preference better. Read less

But not much else aside from that to.give kudos for. My glass of wine came after my wife had finished her beer and the food kept coming when it was clear we were still in the middle of eating with no space on the table. Would avoid the lobster. Description mentioned truffles but must've seriously skimped as i didnt smell or taste a trace... nor did they provide the proper utensils - or the lemon water to wash our hands with. Ambience is terrific and staff while pleasant, could do better on the service. If good food is what youre after, would recomend elsewhere Read less

With all the good places to eat in Chiang Mai, choose someplace else. Went for a special dinner. Food came quickly, not good and was cold. Staff slow and oblivious. Cork fee of 300 baht....dessert took forever.

We had a lovely lunch here. The food was great and presented artfully. Service was a bit slow . We ordered apps and entrees each one came out slowly in the order that we ordered. Each dish was great when they all finally came. The atmosphere is trendy staff was attentive. The food was a work of art and worth the wait. A little pricey at 600TB each. We will be back.

Normally, I like original food taste and style. This is my first time to try fusions food and I can say that I enjoy it. Every dishes have nice presentation and good taste. We went there with 2 persons but order 5 dishes. It was too much but we really enjoy it. Only one comment for spaghetti. I ordered dark spaghetti with scallop. It seemed scallop was under cook. Price of wine per glass is too high. Parking space too limited as well as toilet is too small and not comfortable. Waiters and waitress are young and all good looking (surprise). Overall service is good but if they come with smiling face, it will be perfect.

Four of us went to Mix the other night and sat outside of this restaurant at the end of Soi 1. We ordered and shared about 4 dishes, fusion like, which were all very tasty. We really like the crab curry with so much flavor and a sizable portion, the scallops surrounded by broccoli which was a lovely presentation, steamed fish (bass I believe) sitting in a blend of aromatic herbs, and a salad with lot of greens. The service was great as was the ambience and lots of fun folks around us.

I read some of the bad reviews here and thought it was just an anomaly. I should have taken the advice and not go there. I've been there with my friends before and it was okay. This time the service was horrible. My friends ordered dishes from the picture menu and when the dishes came out and we noticed that 2 of them were not what we ordered we notified our waiter (who he says is also the manager) and he started to argue with us that we did. We told him we ordered the dishes with the pictures and to show us and he could not but insisted we did order them. Needless to say we did not want to argue and prolong the frustration.
