Smallish cafe specialising in roti meals. Great roti with chicken mince and rice with fried egg was enjoyed here on our first morning in Chiang Mai. Good coffee too. Well priced.
Disorganized service. My roti never arrived, so I had to reorder it, and the server completely forgot about my original order. Meanwhile, my partner received their breakfast, but I didn’t get mine.

This was my first time trying roti and decided to get the banana chocolate with cheese. The roti was delicious after I picked off the squares of American cheese on top (not a fan of processed food). I would definitely go back but skip the cheese.
There are a lot of choices of Roti. We ordered Banana Roti with Milo, Corn Roti and Banana Roti of Cheese. All of them are tasty. It just depends which ingredients you like.

I had walked past a few times and seen it quite busy, so when I went by on a lazy Sunday and it wasn’t crowded I decided to stop in. The Chicken Roti was great, just a little spice to tantalise, and coupled with the chicken popcorn, was a really good meal for about Aud$7.50 Free wifi and some comfy chairs, I’ll be back to try more of their wares over the next month.
Love this roti so so much, so recommend the Golden thread plus whip cream crispy roti. Serve with Hor tea or Thai milk tea, that was really good!
When we walked in, nobody greeted us. Their staff gave us a long face. We ordered the crispy roti and the chicken roti. Our food came out cold.

If you like your roti cooked right before your eyes, this is the place to visit. A very versatile food joint, with sweet roti, savoury roti, egg roti, curry roti ... the list is extensive. Or, if you prefer, you could order non-roti dishes. And prices are generally low. Plus, you get served free Thai tea. And great barista-made lattes.

Stopped in for a banana and chocolate roti. Ah, it was sooo good! 75 Baht. This location is right on Nimman Rd. Large selections of (nonalcoholic) drinks and wise variety of roti (street pancakes). Will definitely return.

One of the places that opens till late, 1:30am, even later than the bars around. Ordered the malt milk with Milo. The drink was ok and really sweet. If you are not having a sweet tooth craving, it can be overwhelming. The roti was just ok. I don’t find it good, probably because I’ve had better and crispier ones. The dough used is also bland and slightly thicker, so it can’t get the crisp out. Another place that I don’t think there’s a need to return to. Read less

Aberto até 1:30 AM, especializado em panquecas (roti), mas com óptimas opções de comida Thai. Local descolado, com bons preços, boas bebidas e serviço atencioso e rápido. Open until 1:30 AM, specializing in roti pancakes, but with great Thai food options. Cool place with good prices, good drinks and fast and attentive service.
We only there for dessert which is nice and unique. The theme of the dishes are well designed with roti as I main recipe. Recommended to try it for yourself!

Okay I started the day full of positive vibes wanted to site somewhere, people watch enjoy a morning coffee in a new place as our regular 30 Baht place closed for new years, this is the fluffy part of the story.... Here it goes south, we parked the bike saw a few people and tables and chairs on the corner... This can't be bad right... Upon being served off milk like warm coffee I kindly enquired as there was an odd taste, the replacement it with more luke warm foam, for 75 Baht a piece what a joke....not to mention the street smells like a sewer and to top a omelette and rice was the size of your hand.... Best part was my art photo but avoid like the plague and ill stick with Mcdonald large size for the sake price and warm Read less

Location Location Location. If it weren;t for their few meters of sidewalk on Nimman RD, this institue would be out of business. Overpriced and staffed by a bunch of jaded teenagers that have never heard of the words, respect or customer service. The Roti was not bad - but not worth triple the price you would pay to a good street vendor.

Guu has a great combination of Roti dishes as well as traditional Thai dishes. The Roti variety is impressive and there are pages and pages of both sweet and savory types on the menu. The atmosphere is nice and it is comfortably air conditioned. There's more than 1 location in Chiang Mai and they are all great. Read less

Guu Fusion Roti is located on Nimman Soi 3 You'll be able to find it however finding a parking space is very challenging. I recommend park at One Nimman, located on Soi 1, then walk to Guu. The price is reasonable. The staff is nice and friendly. Seat is limited. First come, first serve basis. We are group of six and the total bill is 3xx THB. Would love to be back and try other menu! Read less

Absolutely amazing. We have come back three days in a row and will be back every day for the remaining days in our trip. The Hawaiian, Mexican chicken, chicken with basil, and sweet samplers are to die for!
We loved the roti. Tea's are awesome, can't get over it. Fantastic combination of taste and texture. Not too sweet. Nice place. Staff's are great. Recommended!

We tried both savoury and sweet Roti. I forgot the name but both Roti were freshly made and tasted good! And the staff kept getting us more tea while we both were enjoying and chatting. The tea is free of charge. I felt very relaxing in this restaurant and price are very reasonable!

friendly staff and good location. Roti with cream and thai dessert (Foi Thong) is the best. also tried "Ka pro Kai" Roti is delicious. Thai Tea is not too sweet and smoothies is just ok. Open 9.30 - 1.30. a lot of people at night. not only Roti, there also serves noodle and rice or salad. you must try if you are in/visit Nimman Area
The roti that we got was very sweet and chocolatey! Very sweet but definitely recommended. We enjoyed it a lot :)

The first thing that strikes you when you visit here, is how popular it is, mainly with younger people maybe students. There are not enough tables and not enough menus. There is a huge amount of varied fillings and dishes available, sweet or savoury, soft or crispy. Many of the rotis sounded very delicious. You can buy tasty rotis in the street but with little variability and much worse hygiene. The rotis were pretty good, all were tasty around 90b each. There were plenty of other dishes, curry with pasta and a large variety of rather strange drinks (but perfectly normal to Thais). Its a very popular place with plenty of choices, with good rotis at a reasonable price. Recommended! Read less
An amazing place for Thai style crepes of every description: Savoury, sweet, There is a choice for you. As you can see I couldn't wait to dive into my chocolate banana rotee.

The cafe serves many kinds of roti that tastes good. Its price is reasonable but little bit expensive when considering the prices of street food. I highly recommend its signature tea which is mix of black tea and coffee.

I had thought the roti was bread, but it turned out to be thin sheets of crisps.. I had the basic which was roti with sweets and it was superb. Also tried the roti with minced meat and basil and this was delicious too. Place is exremely crowded after 8pm. Be prepared to wait for great roti!

We went to this restaurant back to back nights! The ice coffee select was excellent. The roti selection was endless! The dishes were authentic Thai dishes, and the staff service were sooo friendly! They were very accommodating when we needed a table for 5 and helping us with orders. The restaurant is located off the main road, so we sat on the patio area. We walked here from our hotel in the Nimman area alley 13. Very close. I would definitely recommend this place. Cheap eats and great hang out spot. Walking distance to anything! We will return!
Go here and get some super delicious roti! Open late and huge menu with lots of choices. I love the crispy roti with whipped cream.
Had the banana chocolate one and it was soooo good! Cheap too. Friendly staff. Fast service. I will definitly be back
Food was really good. Menu was full of choices.. Tried roti and milk and honey tea. Was delicious. Cheap food as well.. Must try.
best food ever , routi there was mu best experiance , i recomand it to every one and free tea beside food .

Simple Roti creatively serve in many style. I especially like the wrap with salmon! =) I also liked it with the chicken green curry, taste so good! It get real crowded some time, be there early or non peak hour to get good table inside. I will certainly visit again when I come back to Chiangmai. Thumb up!!

The sweets ~ well presented, look as delicious as they taste! Staff ~ quick, friendly, and attentive! Facilities ~ nice boutique feel, but for popularity, a bit small and can become overcrowded! Hours ~ Late night hours make for a great stop at after a bit of partying!
Very yummy and we really loved it! The ham & cheese roti and curry chicken roti were so good and I love my hot milk tea, not so sweet. We will go again before we leave CM today.

After passing here several times on my past visits to CM, we finally stopped in here just after midnight for a delicious Roti feed. And it was still busy This place is open til 1:30am Great selection of different flavours is sure to please. Sweet or savoury

Roti has always been one of my favourite Thai snacks from night market. Knowing Guu offers a physical stores with so many options of roti plus other snack ive been so looking forward to visiting it. Opening till 1 am also allow us go visit right after the night safari tour. Roti is good and up to standard as you experienced in night market, though not suprisingly good. But the insects, flies, moquito, ants are bit disturbing. Plus smell from the restroom might be some points shop owner can improve.

Went with my local friend there on Sunday afternoon, it was packed and we were lucky to get a table. Lots of choices on the menu, the waiter served hot "pandan" tea (green fragrance tea commonly used in South East Asia for cooking) right away when we were seated. In general it tasted great and fancy, sitting inside the restaurant get slight oily smell from kitchen (or it was just because we were sitting near to the kitchen). Worth a try but the price is costing more than the Roti(s) in Malaysia.

Delicious! This place has a LARGE menu of both savory and sweet roti dishes. Definitely try to the sweet roti with the whipped cream and yellow string things. I forget the name. And the northern curry is delish too!

It was so nice that they give a pot of tea for free! Roti is a bit more expensive than street one but better. And there are so many kind of Roti, hard to choose. Coffee is not so good but we were satisfied about another beveridge

If the thought of the inside of a cafe having a thick layer of dust on most surfaces puts you off then sit outside. The food here is super yummy and really good value, I would come here again but I would choose to sit out the front instead. We sat near the air conditioner and there was thick black dust on a lot of things around it. I would suggest that the staff get in there and give the place a good clean to make it more appealing.
Their roti and drink is ok but the place quite small and have limited of table if you go there on weekends or evening probably no seats for you
So many options so yummy. You will feel excited and pleasant only when you are reading the menu. I took few minutes to make the decision. It's so difficult to choose when you have only one stomach.

Loved it. At first when I heard that it was a roti restaurant I was worried if it would be okay for dinner but not at all. I had a wonderful time. I tried lots of types of rotis and every one of them was fantastic. They show how they cook the rotis so are allowed to just look if bored of waiting for the rotis. Give lots in one dish which makes it easy to handle. Really yummy place and strongly reccomend if go to Chiang Mai.
Great place to eat Roti a 100 different ways. The Banana Roti with Honey was a decent size, while some of the other ones we ordered seemed a little small. This place was busy non-stop.

Popped in here not really knowing what to expect. I had a meal roti, chicken and cheese. There was lots of flavour and a good amount of spice (not too much). The person I went with had a dessert dish. For a meal the portion was small and would be considered a light meal. The dessert dish was large for a dessert and would be a good sharing portion. Prices were good. About 330baht for two dishes and two drinks.

Crowded place for teenagers. We love the roti with the whipped cream, it was so soft and tasted good but what we did not like is the staff as they did not smile and not flexible to the customers at all

This is a really popular place with the locals and seems to be busy for most of the day. We dropped in after a visit to the temple on Dio Suthep. Needed a snack and a cold drink. We were greeted with a huge menu of roti, both savoury and sweet. We ordered banana roti with chocolate sauce, roti with egg, along with roti shredded chicken and sweet chilli paste. The place was busy so we had to wait a while. When the dishes arrived, I'd have to say, worth the wait. Very tasty indeed, in fact I was tempted to order more. Would recommend you visit this place if ever you are in Chiang Mai great food.

We had some Rosti and weren't disappointed. It's a pretty simple place with a varied menu and the staff were very friendly. Food was very tasty and they had some good smoothies too. It's on quite a busy road and if you don't want Rosti then avoid it :)

Our first day in CM, my wife was craving roti so we found Guu Fusion. Very hipster and trendy, but that doesn't mask the amazing choices. We tried various roti, the spicy chicken with tamarind sauce was a winner, the northern chicken curry with roti was amazing, I could eat that all day long. The sweet thai roti was very good. Thai food is so cheap and reasonable that after you order all these items with drinks, it feels like a $60 meal when it was only $18.
It's a very popular cafe, very long queue waiting outside, it serves delicious drinks and desert. It located by the road side and really popular place especially for locals.
An amazing place for Thai style crepes of every description. Savoury, sweet. There will be a choice for you. As you can I couldn't wait to dive into my chocolate banana rotee.
Ate their famous Banana crepe with choc&cheese. Was a good afternoon snack. Fries was so so. Service is good!

Stopped into this popular and busy spot to grab a desert roti to go and took it back to my room. Very tasty and large enough for a light dessert and also some left overs to have with my coffee in the morning.

We popped in to treat ourselves to banana roti desserts one evening. Unfortunately the place was very busy and they were very slow taking our order. It then took a very long time before anything arrived - and then it was not what we ordered. After yet another long wait we finally got what we wanted. And it was very good! Probably we just came at the wrong time and hit a bad patch!

We popped in here to get out of the rain and we were pleasantly surprised by the taste of the desert roti and the tea which was served whilst we waited. It seemed to be very popular with the local people - a lot of students. We enjoyed it so much that we returned later in the day for dinner - and it was just as good - certainly a place that we will visit again.
A friend told us that this is the best tea house in Chiang Mai, and she's right! Went there and the teas they serve are awesome, and even more so the roti dishes are delicious.
Guu was a famous cafe. However, the roti was too dry for me, and except sweetness,I did not get other taste of the ingredients. I did not like it. The tea was OK.

Second time in 2 days. Simple place. Tried salads and other foods beside the famous roti. Just awesome taste. Service a bit slow. Probably will go back tomorrow again and try some more :) we had 5-6 dishes and our bill turned out 600 baht

I'm not the one who addicted to the sweet, but the rotee with cream and shredded coconut was made me impressed. The rotee so crisp and taste was totally different with others from street. Although the cost was higher, it's definitely worth to visit for short break during the shopping and Thai food combo set.

When I was craving for sweetness, I went here. had banana and caramel lotti with iced coffee. Of course there were some best and recommanded ones on the menu, but just for diet! :) It tasted ok, and would love to visit here next time and try more various menu.

We stopped by for dessert and ordered one of the recommended roti desserts. As we waited for over 20 minutes for one single roti other tables filled up and received their orders before us. It became obvious our order had gotten lost in the shuffle. Our roti arrived - cold. The only satisfaction from the experience may have been for the bugs who had us for dessert!
Roti is nice, but too small place to sit. it is located in the centre of town. i love tissue roti, roti is nice.

The queue was long at night so we tried again the next day for a quick lunch. Waited 15 minutes for a table for 6. The roti (same as roti canai) was made in creative ways by adding cheese, fruits, cream etc....outcome is delicious. The other dishes we ordered was a soup noodle which tasted like instant noodles with eggs, meat and vege and a rice with meat dish. No free water, mineral water was 30baht a bottle. Surprisingly, even with heavy cooking, we didn't smell like roti after leaving the cafe. All in all a good dinning experience.

This restaurant serves up a huge variety of roti of all kinds of flavors. I really liked the banana and corn, as well as the banana and whipped cream. It's also nice that they give you a pot of free tea. I do think the prices are a bit high, but not too high to keep me from coming back.

This Place just in front of my hotel soi I drive past everyday and I did not have any chance to stop by till one day I walked out from hotel for this Roti and they wasn't disappointing me at all roti was very crispy and yummy finally I walked to this place almost every before I leave CNX very Impresssed

I've been here every time when I went to Chiangmai. My favorite menu is Roti tissue with mango and whipping cream. If you like dessert, I'd recommend you to try. Don't be worried about fat, it's fat.!! lol
I have been Chiang Mai many time but never come this restaurant. After I'm tried from shopping in Nimman Road and stop here for relax. This menu order as recommend by staff and good taste.
Don't forgot that this is a fusion roti shop. They have a huge selection of roti from snacks to main meals. Highly recommended.

I'm writing this review while sitting in the air-conditioned dining space of Guu Roti. They managed to pack a lot of seating in here, and they not only serve snacks, but a full menu of savoury Thai cuisines adapted to the roti concept. Having sampled dozens of street food places myself, I can tell that they use good quality ingredients here. The quality of the meat is always important to me. You an certainly tell when you're eating nothing but cartilage or something from 7-Eleven. Fortunately, you'll have none of that here at Guu. Not to mention the meal comes with a complimentary kettle of rice tea. They charge VAT here, 7% Read less

This Roti restaurant is different than other Roti restaurants, there are different kinds of Roti for you to choose. At this restaurant, there are also foods provided, this means that you can eat your meal and enjoy the tasty Rotis. There are also thai tea provided for you, it’s free and very good. The tea would go along with all the rotis you order. The restaurant is quite small. Sometimes, the tables are full and we have to wait for quite some time. There is also free wifi for us to use! The waiters are also kind. This Roti restaurant is very famous around Chiang Mai. Sometimes the second floor would be open but most of the time, it’s close. There are different kinds of Roti you can choose, there are about 55 Roti menu you can choose. I had never seen a restaurant with several Roti menu you can choose from. Most of the Rotis you order wouldn’t disappoint you too. You should give it a try! Read less
They have extensive range of Roti menu here. From a main dish to dessert. But dessert menu are generally better. Ps. This place could get really crowded at night.

Came here to try their chocolate and whip cream crepes. Both crepes were cooked to perfection and the crepes were incredibly yummy. I wasn't very hungry coming here but couldn't keep my hands off my girlfriends food. I also had the honey lemon soda which just tasted ok. Also disappointing was the service which was incredibly undermanned and unprofessional. They often brought the wrong food to the tables and often looked confused and clueless. Also, the tables were often very slowe to be cleared and cleaned. Obviously the staff cannot keep up with their delicious crepe dishes.

I came from a country where they Roti is a staple. Coming to this shop, they have came up with the most creative ways to eat the Roti!! There is like 50 different ways to have your Roti. It's definitely a crowded place for supper. Although business was good, the staff is very friendly and patient, hot tea is served for free. We ordered the Thai iced tea and Iced green tea latte. Both are good as well. Ordered the mixture plate for Roti (combination of cheese, fried, plain and banana). It was good. Things to note, since it's a cafe and not road side stall, do expect to pay a slight premium compared to the hawkers. Read less
The iced tea tastes good but with a price you might get double drinks from other local drink stand. We came here 2 nights in row, the banana pancake is soooo good.

Trendy restaurant, cramped environment, pricey. Not recommended for those from South Asia, Malaysia or Singapore. The main attraction is basically chicken, sausages, beef or any ingredients you choose , then cooked wrapped in roti prata to serve with a high price tag.
Very friendly staff. Definitely the place to go if you have a late night craving for food. I recommend the Mexican chicken Roti, very tasty. Also try the Thai sweet Roti, very good!
They have a great dessert - the crispy crape that you won't find anywhere else! Must try if you travel to the Nimmanhaemin Road.
Love this place and it's usually open rather late and includes unlimited tea with your roti, large selection of desert Roti's too choose from, friendly staff too

It was hot after a bit of walk around the Nimman area. We stopped by for some aircon and rest. The restaurant has a big selection of rotis and drinks. We tried the cheese roti which was delicious. It was light and fluffy. The other one we tried was Thai roti and it was filled was a yellow, sweet floss. I thought it was a little too sweet. We also had a Milo drink and a mixed Thai ice tea/coffee drink which was very refreshing. It was a good place for a break from the heat and sun Read less
Variety, nice, and fluffy roti both traditional and fusion. Drinks are normal as you can find it in other place.

Guu Fusion Roti changed the way I see Roti forever! This is by far and away one of my favourite spots to enjoy dessert in Nimman. The menu is extensive, presenting diners with dozens of sweet and savoury options. I must admit I've even enjoyed their curried chicken rotis for breakfast served up to me at the cafe next door, Ristr8tto. Only in Thailand, right?! I admit that I simply can't pass up stopping here for dessert with friends, sharing a couple of delicious, sweet, freshly cooked rotis and washing it down with a good coffee, or enjoying the complementary Thai style tea. What's not to like about that?

The place is easy to find just behind the famous Ristr8to on Nimman 3. It's a sanctuary of cool and comfort after a walk in a hot sun. There are basically 2 kinds of roti soft and crispy "tissue" thin. For the crispy kinds , our Milo was very good and the "Foythong" (gold egg yolk treads Thai) was indulgent but satisfying. For the soft kind, we had a choco banana which we enjoyed thoroughly. Washed all down with complimentary tea or order something from the menu if you're still on sugar rush. We notice the selections of roti with Green curry and other "Thai" roti which we want to try for next trip. The service was prompt and the toilet is super clean. Fully satisfying customers here.

Always lots of people in here, locals and farangs. An amazingly long list of roti and related possibilities. Our favourite is the matabah. Service can be a little flaky, its worth taking your time to ensure your waitron has your order straight.

This is a convenient place for snacks and drinks in the thick of things at the popular Nimanhaemin Road. Food and drinks were good. Cosy and comfortable seating but extremely cramped with no space to move. Very packed, so even the friendly staff cannot give any attention sometimes. Lots of flies that dash into your food and drinks, which are all very sweet. It is the kind of place to conveniently drop by for something nice if you're in the area (if you can ever get a seat). It's not worth hunting out for.

When you come, you get a friendly welcome from the staff and you will get a table with a really cute decoration. They serve a free thai hot tea which is really delicious! There is a lot of choice on the menu meal/dessert/drink. The roti are really yummy and the main dishes aswell! I recommand you to taste the thai tea (cold), one of the best I drink in Thailand. This restaurant is in my "do when come back" list for sure! Read less

The food is generally good and very reasonable. The free hot tea is also give a great valance with desert or meal roti. Every single staff is kind and gentle. It opens until late night(might be around 1am - need to check it by yourself.). Restaurants in chiangmai are usually with great welcoming.
good location. wide variety. For Roti itself,there's a lot better places. such as street shop between Changpuak boutique house and Chiangmai mosque.
-The atmosphere is very good -Good Staff caring -There are several kinds of desserts . -Nimman Rd. Soi 3
If you like roti, you will love this place. Very friendly staff providing great service and brilliant food. Well worth a visit if you are in Chiang Mai.
A great place to come and enjoy some delicious rotis. Don't worry about not being able to choose the best dishes. Try as many as you can. Worth the visit.

Real treasure for roti-lovers. Salty roti, sweet roti, crispy roti with whipped cream, and also good choice of Thai food and milk and fruit shakes. By the way they give free tea to every order. I reccomend try their white malt shake and crispy roti - nightmare of nutritionist, sweet and heavy, but OMG so good! Place is alvays full, most part of customers are locals, but service is fast. One of my favorite cafe in CNX.

After looking for a late lunch, we decided to try Guu Fusion and we certainly are glad that we did. The roti variety on the menu was extensive and had lots of options, both sweet and savory. Ordered a roti platter (with 4 different options) and a Chicken sausage roti. We were not disappointed! Free tea at each table,but also a wide variety of coffee/beverage options. Very reasonably priced.

I ordered Northern Chicken Curry Roti and Crispy Roti with Thai Sweet and Whipped Cream and Milk shake (But there was delicious hot Tea in freecharge. It was No sugar added! ). This shop is very busy but staff are friendly. Anyway everything is wonderful! I would certainly return here!!

the food in this restaurant is the best food i ever had, the fried rice is so yummy and the roti is excellent also the Number One Milk Drink is so yummy and you must try it! the service it good and the food is super yummy!
The waiters and waitresses were very friendly. The foods were reasonably priced and tastes were good. Especially, the chocolate drink called "Number One", my son loved it.
Food was delicious so was the drinks. As well as deserts you can get really nice chicken dishes and really nice fruit drinking ice teas. Only thing that let it down was the service was abit slow.

If you like roti you will not be disappointed. But service is slow. We were not there u a really busy time, waited a long time for our order, then got the wrong thing. What we did get was good, but again, a misunderstanding with our order.

Stopped in for lunch, having read not a single review this was complete chance based on the love of Roti. First impressions were of how busy this place was, literally every table and every inch of floor space was packed. We were quickly shown to a table and both ordered a roti dish and a juice. The juices were fresh, vibrant and full of flavour. Having no added sugar is such a rare thing in Thailand that when you get it you literally rejoice. When the roti appeared it was indeed freshly made, my Garlic and cheese one was indeed garlicky. I suspect the cheese was from the plasticky variety that comes pre sliced but it was very tasty indeed. My girlfriends dish of chicken curry roti again seemed very fresh and full of flavour. This seems to be the hip place to be, full of the young hipster brigade. I would certainly return of in the area, go here you shall not be disappointed. Read less
I had a roti with cheese and egg, it was very good. Small place and good for an afternoon break. The waitresses have limited English skills, but you will be able to communicate.

We ordered crispy roti with whipped cream, caramel, and banana. It was sooooo good. The whipped cream was not too sweet even though there's caramel in it. The fruit juice was also yummy and can be made according to your preference (we wanted one without salt and syrup, they didn't complain ;P). Free tea was also served (to go with the roti) and it was really nice. Well, I like this place a lot as you can see. :)

Had a great supper here sat outside watching the rotis being made through the window. Large menu with sweet and savoury rotis and some other Thai dishes. Iced milk tea and pineapple rotis were both delicious and service quick and friendly.

Came across this cute little place when we got dropped off in a red taxi on Nimmanhaemin near the end of the road! No regrets at all! Staff are friendly but we had to use our 'learn Thai' app to ask for no meat in the roti! Our most entertaining conversation yet! We had a Mexican chicken and omelet roti and both were yummy (as title suggests)! Many other savoury and sweet options to choose from! You also get free hot Thai tea!!! Defos worth a visit!! : )

How does this place not have more reviews?? Ended up eating here after we discovered that the coffee shop next door was closing. We ordered the milo roti bomb, the sweet roti and the crispy roti. I am reciting this from memory because they were THAT good, hahaha. Well, the crispy roti and sweet roti were the best. They were REALLY, REALLY good actually. A little pricey, but not too much so. Their drinks were so-so -- the coffee tea sounded like a good idea, but ended up tasting weird. My friend's white malk shake was more like condensed milk in frappucino form, if you're into that! It seemed like the local hang-out for college kids. I would definitely come back here. Read less

We happened by this place on our way home from another restaurant and we didn't regret it! We did pass by here on a later occasion also to find that just about everything they dish out is delicious! So, if you're in Chiang Mai make sure to pass by here and have some Roti, and make sure that you've saved some space as the dishes are both delicious and generous :-)

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: A truly exceptional dining experience awaits at Shishitei Chiang Mai! The A5 Wagyu beef is simply divine, melting in your mouth with each bite. The attentive service and inviting ambiance create a perfect setting for a memorable meal. Whether you're a seasoned foodie or simply appreciate a good meal, Shishitei Chiang Mai is a must-visit. I hope you find this review helpful.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Best wagyu outside of japan, you need to try the wagyu sushi!!! The staff is very friendly and attentive and we got our orders really fast

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: This is my second visit to Shishitei Chiangmai, and I absolutely love this place. This time, I had the Kaisendon set and matcha parfait. The Kaisendon set came with fresh seafood, along with a tomato marinated in a special Japanese sauce, miso soup, and tofu. Every dish was bursting with flavor and incredibly delicious. The attention to detail in both the preparation and presentation was impressive. The matcha parfait was the perfect way to end the meal—rich, creamy, and full of authentic matcha flavor. I look forward to visiting again for another unforgettable dining experience.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่

Detail: Shabu shabu is good the soup is not too sweet nor salty.Beef tonge also good. But for the sashimi better check the day that they delivered fish. Good environment so far.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Good place for Japanese style Sukiyaki and Shabu. Material and ingredient are great. Service is very nice and friendly staff. Find your experience here with your beloved people 😍

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Really good Shabu shabu and Sukiyaki! We also ordered a sashimi bowl, beef stew, and gyukatsu! So good forgot to take pictures!

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: This Japanese ShabuShabu restaurant (Shishitei) offers an exceptional dining experience with its premium service and outstanding taste. Featuring A5 beef from Kagoshima, the highest grade of Wagyu, the restaurant ensures a rich, melt-in-your-mouth experience. My favorite dish, other than the ShabuShabu, was the Korean Yukke—raw beef marinated with a flavorful Korean touch with fresh squid and Japanese spring onion. Despite specializing in beef, the seafood here surpasses even dedicated seafood restaurants in Chiangmai. From the moment you step in, the attentive staff ensures a memorable visit, guiding you through an exquisite selection of high-quality ingredients. This ShabuShabu restaurant is a must-visit for culinary delight while your stay in Chiangmai!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: This restaurant has delicious food, attentive service and elegant environment. It is a great choice for dinner and date. The price is reasonable and cost-effective. Highly recommended! ! ! !

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Excellent dinner. Soooooo good. Ordered all the recommended items and everything came out very delicious. We will come back

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: From the melt-in-your-mouth Japanese A5 meats to the perfectly balanced broths, every element of the sukiyaki here was divine. The atmosphere was also fantastic, providing a truly immersive Japanese dining experience. We will definitely be back to try other menu items and recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for the best sukiyaki in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: It is a very delicious restaurant with excellent service and smiling staff. All ingredients are fresh. After eating, I want to come back and eat again.

Definitely come again more than three times. And then there's free ice cream when you're done eating. The atmosphere is great. The service staff is excellent, attentive to customers, and always remembers customers.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Good service staff, polite The food is delicious, quality, fresh, high-grade ingredients, the restaurant is clean, the atmosphere is nice and private, but the food arrives a little slow, but it's delicious.

Detail: The atmosphere inside the restaurant has the feel of a great Japanese restaurant. Plus, the staff at the store are always on hand to provide good service and care. Regarding cleanliness inside the store, it is considered very clean. As for parking, you can park in the parking lot next to each other. You can also bring your parking ticket to use as a discount inside the store. Considering both savory and sweet food, service and convenience regarding parking... overall rating 4.5 Read less

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: The food is all delicious. The atmosphere is nice and the service is very good. It was my first time eating here and I was hooked. And the parking fee can be exchanged for a discount on food as well. I recommend it. 👍

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: The atmosphere is simple, elegant, comfortable, and warm. The staff provide very good service and advice. Parking tickets can also be used as discounts at restaurants. Really delicious, I'll tell you more.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Best Wagyu dises in Thai. Nice service, great ambience. Staff are so helpful and attentive!! You can not leave Chiang Mai without eating here Shishitei.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Exceptional service, top-notch ingredients, and a delightful ambience seamlessly combine to elevate the dining experience at this restaurant to unparalleled heights.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: This is our 2nd time. Very good quality of A5. We tried premium loin set 1st time n this time we tried special loin set and the quality is as good as premium. Sukiyaki is our most favorite.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Our second time here in Chiangmai branch. Exellent food, great service, nice atmostphere. We love it. Beef Sukiyaki is top quality.

Restaurant_Name: Shishitei เชียงใหม่
Detail: Just had greatest experience ever at Shishitei. A5 WAGYU here is the best WAGYU I 've ever had!! You can enjoy Japanese Authentic Sukiyaki and SHABUSHABU here as well as side Wagyu side dishes. Definitely Worth trying Shishitei,

Found this little breakfast gem up on the main road and couldn't wait to return. Prices are fantastic and quantities are very generous. I enjoyed a banana and espresso smoothie, plus eggs benedict with prosciutto and melon the first morning, then returned to enjoy a mango, carrot, pineapple and ginger juice followed by an espresso, eggs benedict on a waffle with ham and a hashbrown. Hubby thoroughly enjoyed his brekky choices too. Read less

There was a lot on the menu, so it seemed like a place you could stop by anytime for breakfast or a snack. Mango smoothies are available everywhere, but since it's cool in Chiang Mai, I wanted to drink mango juice, so I ordered this. My friend wanted to eat regular mango without sticky rice, so she ordered the mango. Both were delicious and not too expensive, so I was happy😊

The variation on eggs Benedict on the menu are all quite wonderful. And try the sandwich on whole grain bread -- spinach and cheese a favorite of mine. And the smoothies are reasonably priced and quite wonderful. Another favorite, pineapple-mango with low sugar. Enjoy. Some of the best casual non-Thai meals in the area.
This small place has some extraordinary empenadas! They were tasteful and made fresh. Also their bagels were really good.

One of my favourite breakfast / brunch places in Nimman. They open very early, you can go from 08am and their menu is incredible, from pancakes, to muesli, to salty toast, eggs… they have everything you can dream of for breakfast. I’ve been a couple of times and every time food is always amazing, don’t miss their avocado scramble eggs and melted cheese toast !

Right on Nimman Rd. in the heart of the trendiest neighborhood in Chiang Mai, this little cafe has the best breakfast and fresh fruit menu EVER! Smoothies, mixed juices, and many Western breakfast favorites in addition to all the usual Thai offerings. It’s a diamond in the rough!

We stayed near Smoothie Blues for two weeks and popped in multiple times for a quick smoothie or juice to go. Lots of variety, tasty and fast service! They also have a Thai food place next door that a local recommended to us and it was excellent! Very nice staff as well. They were the ones who helped steer us back multiple time’s.

Right on the corner of the old city of Chiang Mai. It is small and quirky. Quick service and a really good menu for lunch or breakfast. I had eggs Benedict with salmon on fresh bread and a coffee. Tasty and wanted more. Really cheap and worth the visit.

This is one of my favorite place for breakfast after a Saturday morning swim. Nice outside seating for breakfast ( Neman area is a zoo later in the day). The folks working are pretty good for the most part. Good coffee, omelets and a size of pan fried potatoes ( not on the menu)
Quaint restaurant with a wonderful all day breakfast menu. They do have Thai and other food to order from. Nice, clean place with friendly staff.
I was surprised to see this on the list of best places in the area. There was nothing wrong with my breakfast (eggs and toast) but nothing special either.

I was a regular at Smoothie Blues. They always gave me what I wanted Now, I go next door to Ruk Muang Thai. Same owner. But, Thai food. You can also order from the Smoothie Blues menu Both places are equally good. If you want a good breakfast, good fruit juices, sandwiches..Smoothie Blues If you want Thai where they will cook the way you want it. Ruk Muang Thai Now they have red wine also.. Not to be missed if you are in the Nimmanhemin area Read less
Found this restaurant in the top 10 brunchs in Chiang Mai. Was super excited to go there. The brunch was ok but nothing amazing and the prices are quite high for Chiang Mai.
Love everything about this place. Great western breakfast and smoothies. Was a regular for my 5 days in Chiang Mai.
Friendly staff, very good menu, and the food tastes great. Been coming here regularly and love the food

Coming here has been a family tradition for as long as I can remember, and Smoothie Blues never disappoints. The smoothies and dishes are always delicious, and the atmosphere is so bright and cozy (although we weren’t a big fan of the country music that was playing there today). A new room opened up this week, so there are now two indoor sections as well as a couple tables out in front. It’s very accessible and right on Nimmanhaemin. A quiet little place — perfect for breakfast with friends or family.

We come to Chiang Mai often, and we always manage to squeeze in a breakfast/brunch here. The food is always delicious and fresh, but mango and avocado season is our favorite time. The service is always friendly and the prices reasonable. Definitely worth a stop if you find yourself near Nimmanhaemin St.

If you’re craving a good western breakfast or delicious smoothie, visit Smoothie Blues. I eat here often and the food is consistently good. I’m a big fan of their peanut butter and banana smoothie. Nice staff, good prices. There is another location at Thapea Gate. Read less

Visiting from the US, this is the best breakfast place I have had in a month of travel through the Philippines and Thailand. It exceeds most of the places I eat in the US. I would like to steel their chef and bring them home to open a restaurant there. Only got a chance to try a few items, but they were all excellent. Definitely make a point to stop here and try the food out. They have very bold flavors in all of their ingredients. I will miss this place very much.

I have been to a lot of breakfast venues over a month of living in Chiang Mai and although Smoothie Blues was not the best (it was second best after the Hideout), it is still fantastic and gets 5 stars. Food is great, staff are lovely and Mango and Blueberry smoothie was always my fav. I also loved the avacado menu and couldnt help trying something on there very frequently. I visited this place at least 4 times since I have been in Chiang Mai.

We discovered the location just outside Tha phae gate last year. We love this place for a great lunch. The baguettes are the finest. Each sandwich is loaded with fresh food. Very tasty. We always get the mango stick rice too. The other location has the same menu and same great food! Enjoy.
Great breakfast spot! Had a great smoothie and bacon egg and cheese bagel with hashbrowns. Was exactly what I needed to start the day. Food was tastey and smoothie was excellent. Will be back!
I eat a few different dishes from this place and they are all very good. Very reasonable prices. They play good music, different types. The staff is very nice and friendly
The prices are on the higher side, but food is always good and their smoothies are made out of fresh fruits.

A small place in Chiang Mai serving a good smoothie. Not very many tables so when it gets busy it fills up. The prices were good and the service was good. It was not too busy when I came in but was starting to fill up when I left. They had a menu with food also, but I did not eat. A good place to get a smoothie and chill.
Smoothie blues is one of my favorites for breakfast, but you really need to try The Hideout. Amazing food.

Smoothly blues has been a go to place for expats for a good breakfast or lunch. It's in a great location, corner of Nimmenhaemin Soi 6. It's a casual place serving all day breakfast as well as a great selection of sandwiches. They do have a few Thai dishes on the menu but have never tried. The eggs, omelets, pancakes are excellent and the coffee is good. Overall a very nice, casual place to have breakfast or lunch.

Stopped in here and was impressed by the vast menu of food and drinks. So many choices. I opted for the French Toast with grilled banana and crispy bacon. Topped off with butter and maple syrup. Not the healthiest of meals but a special treat. It was devine! Ordered a mango, pineapple and passion fruit juice too. Lovely place, staff are friendly and polite. Prices very reasonable for quality food.
My wife and I have been to Chiang Mai twice and been to Smoothie Blues twice! They serve great pancakes and smoothies. Enjoyable place to grab a quick breakfast.
All day breakfast, not too expensive. If you drive a car maybe have to park on other side of the road. It’s located on Nimman road very easy to find. My favorite is BLT avocado!

This fine restaurant has the most extensive menu I have ever seen. They have the largest selection of breakfast items which can be had all day. Besides the many egg dishes, they have pancakes, French toast, waffles and crepe dishes. There is also a large selection of sandwiches. Besides Western Food, they also have Mexican and Thai dishes. On top of all this, they have 33 different smoothies. Fresh squeezed juices, coffee and tea can also be had. The two dishes I enjoyed there on separate occations were eggs benedict with veggies and chicken pad thai. (This was at there second location at Tha Pae Gate) Read less

Been here for breakfast. Although the menu choice is quite wide eastern food barely reach a mere 6 out of 10. Tried egg Benedict and it was just edible but nothing more than that. Coffee was good. Some more smiles would give this place more authentic Thai style. Will give it another try with a different meal next time.

We found out about Smoothie Blues while looking for breakfast spots on tripadvisor. We decided to give it a go and we weren't disappointed. We got the apple and cinnamon french toast and the Egg Benedict on waffles and they were both super yummy. The smoothies were definitely the highlight of our meal and we would come back again to try everything else on the menu.

Had my last meal in Chiang Mai here, and also got some pannini to take on the train to Bangkok (because, seriously, avoid eating anything on that train....) Had eggs benedict and it was delish. A really nice little place
Good food, good vibes, chill, not too expensive. Green curry was spicy. The Mango smoothie blues was quite delicious.

This is the nearest eating place to me on Nimmanhemin road. It is on corner of Soi 6. It has outside seating and aircon inside. It seems a bit of a family kitchen affair but with attractive menu chalked above the front inside. Considering it's called Smoothie bar, the smoothies were just average and a little expensive. The food is the same. There are some western specials particularly breakfasts. It closes quite early in the evening, 6:30pm, I think. Food is OK but does not really offer much value for money for Chiangmai. We have been here 3 times but no more visits Read less

This is our happy breakfast spot. We ended up eating here two days in a row and were so glad to be there. They open early and have an enormous menu, so we were able to experience a lot of diversity with our orders. Recommend the smoothies!

I have been eating here for 18 years. Great selection and quality for breakfast and lunch. Western food that will make you smile. They will cook it well done or what ever way you like. I just had an Avacado BLT on a bagette with crispy bacon. Wow.

A little bit more expensive then what my partner and I usually spend for breakfast, but that didn't stop us from coming back multiple times as the breakfasts are just so yummy! Smoothies are delicious too!
I don't think I have ever been to Chiangmai and not had breakfast here!! Good menu good service and great preparations. if you are looking for some western real breakfast options. This is your place.
Good selection of healthy and breakfast menu items - service is pleasant enough and efficient. Only negative is the coffee isn't great, but that's ok.
One of regular breakfast spots, in the AM the streets @ are not so busy. So you can eat outside with out all the traffic. Good menu are reasonable prices.
Small little place serve good and tasty western breakfast. But service can be a little bit slow when it is busy.
What more do you need to start the day as a Westerner in Chiang Mai - toast; waffles; eggs; bacon and smoothies. So nice even if the service was a bit slow due to the popularity of the place.
Great spot for breakfast/brunch. Delicious smoothies and the bacon is always cooked to perfection - crispy!
Great range of choice for breakfast with tasty eggs florentine. Busy with Western backpackers Attentive and kind staff who served you quickly. Coffee was strong enough to double as rocket fuel.

This breakfast sandwich was incredible! I got bacon and ham with avocado on their special toasted bread. It was perfectly soft but also crispy and toasted. It wasn’t too big so as to make you feel bloated or too full. It was just right! Add a “classic” smoothie and you’ve got a perfect breakfast!

Although we have gone to Smoothie Blues for sandwiches and smoothies, over the past several years, they do have a full menu of breakfast (served anytime) to dinner meals. They have the most fantastic baguettes that you should request even if the menu shows something different. They will accommodate. They use only fresh ingredients. They were promoting avocado on everything and if it’s offered be sure to order it. Today I had a grilled cheese, bacon and guacamole sandwich. Again, it’s served on toast but I felt it was so much better on a baguette. I’ve also has an egg and bacon on baguette. All delicious. While this location is near the hip trendier part of town, we were thrilled to find their second location next to Tha Phae gate. Same menu, great food and nice environment. Either one works. Read less
I love to go here for breakfast We always enjoy it here Parking is a bit tricky but we always manage It’s always busy here because their food is yummy

We stopped by for breakfaat and I have to say the quality was excellent. However, please do not visit if you have a bus or plane to catch or tour starting soon. There is a very small prep area so when busy the service is naturally a little slow. Some customers were getting quite uptight about this and all I can say is - Go to a bigger place if you are in a rush!

This is a small place on Nimman Road run by 3-4 ladies and one word comes to mind the moment you think of it- Superb! Service is prompt with a smile, interiors comfortable, the outdoor section also has ample space, menu quite extensive for a breakfast. Variations of omelettes and smoothies. Though I ordered a couple of them and didnt like them much, Omelettes served with sides were excellent, coconut water refreshing. Sumptious breakfast for the price, go for it! Crowd mostly western, place has a very good vibe to it! Read less
Great breakfast. As well I had a smoothie that was delicious. One of the better western breakfasts I had in Chiang Mai. I recommend it

This has become our favourite local option for breakfast/brunch. Everything we’ve ordered has been awesome. The food is tasty, smoothies are fresh and delicious and servings are generous. Highly recommend!

This place has a big Western menu that covers all of the picky eaters in your group. They have several pages of smoothies you can order for me or you can create your own as well. Beyond smoothies, they have everything from eggs to pancakes to waffles & even sandwiches for lunch. I enjoyed getting the Peach Lagoon smoothie & a hash brown for breakfast each day. The food comes out pretty slow but for the price you are paying, the food & smoothies are worth it!
I simply love the place. Good menu, food, smoothies and service. The price is fair too. Recommended!

Big selection, probably the most western option I found there, nowhere in Thailand has bacon hardly but they do, so that's cool. I got eggs Ben and it woulda been really good but I should have specified well done eggs because they were sooooo runny it ruined it

I frequent this restaurant on many mornings and the food is just great They are always willing to accommodate you if you want to order what is not on the menu. The staff are very helpful and friendly.
The food here was very good. I had two waffles, egg, and four pieces of bacon with a side salad included for 170 baht

My boyfriend and I were walking around looking for breakfast and stumbled upon this little gem. The smoothies are so fresh and delicious - I got the namesake Smoothie Blues and it was incredible. But the eggs Benedict is the real star of the show. It's the best we tasted in chiang mai for sure! Tons of creamy hollandaise and crispy bacon with runny eggs make it the perfect breakfast spot. Wish we had found this place sooner!!

I went here a couple of times and had their egg benedict with salmon and aspargus, really good. The place has a good menu, the staff is really service minded, the prices are mid-range for this kind of place. Though the location right at the busy Nimman road did not appeal to me, it is really a lot traffic, noisy and too much of everyting for me. But if you want to be in the middle of everything, you'll like what they offer, for sure.
Excellent selection of delicious breakfast choices all day. So many good choices that I cannot say that I have a particular recommendation, all 5 breakfasts that I had were great. Good location.

Only Downside: It's small in here... Now Onwards to the Upsides! Flavor, boom! Presentation: As if angels plated the food themselves Staff ~ cute. friendly. helpful. Uniqueness: They're a One-Off Facilities: Clean, tidy, inviting & peaceful atmosphere Worth the Visit? Worth the visit. Oh and prices? Ok it's not street food here, but $H*t, completely reasonable! Best for Lunch, but it's an anytime sort of place.. Read less
a nice place to have a meal with a soothing ambiance. the food, atmosphere,service are all good. the prices are fair and reasonable. they serve good american breakfast

The best smoothies and so many flavours to choose from. The menu gives many options of different cuisines. Good food, good prices, clean restaurant. It was very popular at lunchtime. Good for breakfast. Toasted sandwiches are fantastic.

After 10 months of living in Chiang Mai, my wife and I have found our favorite breakfast spot. Great service, menu options, and great food! We had the eggs benedict and also peanut butter/banana pancakes. Excellent coffee as well. Will be back soon.

The food is so good that I'm literally reviewing the place while still eating. I'm having the chicken and avocado sandwich. So good! The menu is diverse and I had a hard time picking something as everything looked good. Perfect spot for breakfast, lunch or anytime.
Great smoothie and Good sandwiches. They use real fruit unlike the cheap street stale that will use syrups for berries. The new location has a deal on. Get a sandwich and you get a free smoothie.
Smoothie Blues has opened a second location at Thapae Gate. I've been having breakfast there for the past few weeks. Great Eggs Benedict, sandwiches and outstanding coffe. Love this place.

Me and my boyfriend went to meet up with a friend for brunch. This place located on the corner of nimmanhaemin road and soi 6. The menu serves a mixture of American, Mexican and Thai Food. I ordered tuna with avocado salad and my boyfriend ordered waffles with bacon and egg. Our friend ordered eggs benedict which he enjoyed. There was a large selection of smoothies, including a "build your own smoothie" option choosing a combination of three out of about nine fruits. We ordered banana buzz and smoothie blues, which were both good. The price is average. We all happy for the meal :)

This is a great hidden gem, I eat here almost every morning when I'm in Chiang Mai for business. The breakfast burrito and enchiladas are absolutely delicious, they do fresh juices and even the coffee is good! Hard to go anywhere else if you're staying in the area.
Went here straight from the airport for a great Eggs and Caramel Latte. Highly recommend this place for their great menu
I was looking around this area for a breakfast place at 10.30am. Couldn't find any that do western breakfast and is also A/C exvept Smoothie Blues. Great wee place. Highly recommended.
Smoothie Blues is a Westerner's haven. Good omelettes and smoothies! Love the avocado menu. Good value.
Its a small little cafe with seating indoors and outdoors. The food is delicious and the smoothies are amazing. I think I visited at least 6 times over the space of a month!!

If you're looking for something that is western and not at a break your arm price, this is the place to go. Their food is top notch quality and so delicious. Highly recommended just a little tucked away cafe.
If you're missing a diner breakfast while traveling through Southeast Asia, this should help. The olive and feta omelet is perfect. The smoothies are also delicious.

If you're a fan of avocado, do look out for their seasonal avocado menu. I love their avocado toast and sandwich, both very delicious and priced at 135baht. However I doubted if the smoothie is 100% fresh as once ordered their strawberry+raspberry+banana smoothie and seen staff added some liquid instead of blending fresh berries. Overall recommend the food!
A very large choice of Breakfast/Brunch items as well as smoothies. The service was very good and we had an amazing sunday brunch.
Was in the mood for salmon benedict, went on trip advisor and found Smoothie Blues, went there and really enjoyed!!! Good coffee, clean, good service and good cinnamon buns! Recommended!
Very good place to have breakfast. Omelets, wafels, pancakes, muesli, fruit salad,... And the best smoothies.

Whenever I am staying in Chiang Mai for an extended period of time, Smoothie Blues is an absolute go to go. For a quick breakfast, lazy brunch or lunchtime treat it has it all. Never have I had a better avocado smoothie. It is honestly perfect for when you are craving a western homely meal.

Smoothie Blues has a very extensive menu with all kinds of western food (both breakfast and lunch foods). Meals are a standard size (not too big, not too small), prices are low and ingredients are fresh. The signage and furniture looks a bit tired, but it's worth looking past for the yummy food - and especially, the delicious smoothies!
Highly popular place. Quality food at reasonable prices. Its usually full of people and hard to get a seat around meal times

A great breakfast menu with plenty of sandwiches, waffles, pancakes, omelettes and the like. Everything sounds good and tastes excellent. The best spinach, potato and onion omelette - light, tasty and juicy.
This place had been recommended to me by a friend, so I biked to the area of town to check it out. Nice vibe, and for those craving a western type cafe (at western prices) this will hit the spot.
Nice spot if you have had enough exploration in the Thai gastronomical realm. Tasty breakfast, with a variety of fresh smoothies and nice sandwiches.
Ate here with my wife before we went on an all day tour - delicious breakfast options. Quick and easy - with fast wifi. Recommend for fast quick eats.

Our family had a breakfast in the morning while waiting to check in at the hotel, we had both western, egg benedict and thai food- fried rice with shrimp, both are very good taste. We also order hot cappuccino, but taste not ok at all, so got only 4 points. Atmosphere of table outside the shop are very pleasant.

Awesome breakfast, great eggs and fruit shakes. Took the kids there and sat outside , very nice and chill atmosphere. Gave it 4 stars because their latte is not made from a proper coffee machine. There are great coffee shops in Chiang Mai and especially in Nimman neighborhood. If SB had a proper latte I would give it 5 stars . Went to the Wawee coffee at Soi 6 to get my caffeine fix.
Brewed black coffee was some of the best I've had so far here in CM. The eggs Benedict with bacon was good too!

Smoothie Blues is the place to eat! They offer both western food and Thai food. So far I've had their Sweet and Sour Chicken, turkey with cranberry sauce sandwich, French toast, mango blueberry smoothie, and a mango smoothie. All of these items were delicious! You'll find mostly expats here. The service is quick and the staff is friendly. They offer their food to go as well.
We stayed just down the street from here, so it was decently located for a quick bite before we had to run off for the day. Nothing was overly amazing but for some reason we kept coming back.

I always come here when I stay in Chiang Mai. It is hands down the best breakfast place in Thailand, outside of very top hotels. The menu is huge and the options are very varied. Really is worth going out your way to try this, and much much better than the obligatory mixed omelet and fruit you will have in your hotel.

Food looks really good, but the prices are a bit high. Had a tough time finding a vegan dish but ended up having the taco salad and it is really good! If you're a vegan go somewhere else because there's not much to choose from. If you are not a vegan the place is great - they have everything :)

I have come to Smoothie Blues for breakfast and lunch and the quality, quantity and price have all been excellent. Whether it's their use of avocado's, strawberries or other seasonal fruit, the fact you get water without asking or just the pleasant surroundings, this is a great place to eat. As other reviewers have noted seating is limited and it can fill quickly at the most busy times, it is worth waiting. This visit the sandwiches were a pleasant surprise. I love the waffles and the french toast and the eggs Benedict are always a great choice too. You won't be disappointed.
I had an oatmeal with soy milk and mango. Also peanut butter bagel. Everything was delicious. Water was free, staff friendly. Gets packed at noon.

First time trying an avocado Smoothie, and I loved it! I have been back for 3 this week alone. Nice little cafe on the corner of the main street in Nimman, serving delicious breakfast and brunch. Breakfast Burrito hits the spot after a big night out on the Singha beers!

Smoothie Blues continues to be one of the best breakfast joints in the whole city. The bagel with smoked salmon, cream cheese and scrambled eggs was beautifully presented with a fresh, crisp salad with tasty tomatoes. My boyfriend ordered breakfast quesadilla which was jam-packed with eggs, bacon and cheese. The atmosphere was buzzing at 8.30am-a great start to the day! :)
Orderded a BLT + egg and chicken. Very tasty. Service was really fast but could have been a little more friendly.

Finally having amazing varied breakfast, lot of choices, from very healthy to American greasy breakfast. Will be back here anytime. Important to mention that prices are really more than reasonable. A gem in Chiang Mai.

With both Thai and Western selections on the menu, this cozy bakery restaurant aims to please. Loads of fresh avocado on generous portion sandwiches coupled with excellent fresh fruit smoothies left us completely content.

Can't beat this place in the Nimmaheimin area for breakfast, brunch or a light luncheon sandwich. We usually have the Eggs Benedict with Salmon, and anything with BACON (Bagel with Bacon, Eggs & Cheese, or a BLT, etc.) -- they know how to make BACON!!! If you've struggled finding a place that makes good BACON, this is your answer! Great selection of smoothies too. Inside or outside seating (in very nice garden area surrounded by flowers). 2 very filling breakfasts with 2 smoothies run us just a little over 400 Baht on average.

This is a great if you don't know what you fancy or a with a group of people who want different things. The breakfasts are great, the smoothies lovely and salads are huge! Friendly staff and good value too.

I've walked to Smoothie Blues four times just for breakfast alone. Amazing salmon eggs Benedict and regular breakfast dishes. I've had their dinner options and while good, nothing tops their breakfast menu.

Ate at Smoothie Blues today with three friends, vegan, vegetarian and carnivore. There is something for everyone on the extensive menu. Service is friendly, food excellent and we all left happily replete. Not a particularly cheap place but worth the little extra.
Visited here with three friends and all agreed it was a special place with excellent food Menu Choices excellent, page after page of choice. Great 70's and 80's music playing. Would highly recommend

Was there for a light pick-me-up after checking into my hotel, as I was walking around the Nimman area. Although this place was recommended on the map we bought, the menu was very lacklustre, and the staff was quite uninterested. I ordered an avocado shake and the nutella pancakes. The avocado shake was eggy and very bad, I could not finish it. The pancakes were quite delightful, fluffy but cold and the nutella was spread very unevenly and thinly. Wouldn't really recommend this place to anyone, though I haven't tried their salads or other items on the menu.

I went with my dog and the outdoor space feels cozy and cool even though it is located at a corner. The chicken spinach cheese sandwich was great although I think a bit of simple dressing would have made it perfect! The avocado smoothie was simply delightful! Service was easy and friendly. This is our first experience and we plan to return again for sure!

Yeah, OK is the best word to describe my experience. Nothing disapointing, but no WOW factor in the plate or in your mouth, as you can get at The Larder for example (or as you could have at the old Overstand). Price is also OK for the area. The disturbing thing is the place is quite small and there is a raw of 4 staff standing 2 meters from you, either with blank stares or watching you eat... I felt sorry for them.

The food is pretty nice and the fruit shakes are tasty (if a bit overpriced). The music is awful and the staff lack competence. Its a weird atmosphere, but the bacon, egg and cheese crepe is pretty good.
The food is good and reasonably priced. I didn't find any of the flavors or items very unique. They do make good fruit smoothies with little extra additives.

I went in here on Sunday morning with my family for breakfast and really enjoyed it. We all had a smoothly that was so large we all had to take it away with us! We all had a bagel with different fillings, one had smoked salmon and cream cheese, the other had American crispy bacon and i had avocado. They were brilliant. They even sold root beer which I love and find it so hard to find in Thailand. Brilliant place. Read less

We ate here twice on our recent visit to Chiang Mai. They have quite an extensive menu and the food was decent. The smoothies were a great value and quite big. The Eggs Benedict was good, but their pancakes left something to be desired.

This place has a really great selection of sandwiches and western breakfast foods. They also have a really wide variety of smoothies. I've been here twice and both times left me feeling healthy and satisfied. My girlfriend is especially fond of their French toast.
My friend had a chicken pesto panini and I had scrambled eggs, avocado and cheese melt on toast. We both liked it a lot! Smoothie are recommended too! Will come again soon!
This small corner restaurant is right on the main street so it is noisy but does have limited indoor seating. They have numerous western breakfast options with prompt service but without wifi.

We stopped by this cafe for a smoothie on the way back to our hotel and soon clocked the rather fantastic breakfast menu for the morning. We sat outside (repellent required) and ordered pancakes, smoothies, breakfast sandwiches, smoked salmon, eggs and coffee and had quite a feast! Everything seems to be made separately and by hand so the drinks particularly, came out one by one .... but very worth the wait!

It is easy to find on the corner of Soi 6. We walked by several times before stopping for breakfast and the restaurant always looked busy. There were just a couple tables available Sunday morning when we arrived. The quality of service was very good from the moment we were seated. The menu is extensive with variations of waffles, pancakes, eggs, muesli, sandwiches and all kinds of sides for standard Western dishes with some Thai standards. If you are familiar with American diner-style restaurants, this should be a hit for you. Breakfast for two will run about 300 baht, with drinks add 100 for 2 coffees or 150 baht for 2 smoothies. The bookshelf inside has English papers, The Nation & Bangkok Post, as well as magazines and local guides to browse while sipping a coffee or smoothie. Read less

There are only a few western breakfast places on the Nimman side of town. This is certainly the top 1 or 2 for food, quality, price and service. We feel like we're part of the Smoothie Blues family after showing up there for so many years. Omelets are great. Salads and juices are excellent and a great value. Smoothies OF COURSE! Great cinnamon rolls - our weakness. Coffee is hot and frech also. If we weren't watching waist lines and heart health we'd be here EVERYDAY for Eggs Benedict - ham, bacon or salmon!
If you get tired of Thai food, this is your place. Avocado sandwich, but very big. Perhaps you can share it with someone. Vegetarian options and good coffee.
The service was excellent, the food fresh and delicious! It was avocado season, and the specials were lovely.
If you're in the mood for some great sandwiches and smoothies, this place is perfect. The turkey is actually grilled turkey, and not cold cuts, the bread is excellent, and the smoothies are fresh.

A nice clean place with both inside and outside seating. A big surprise no WIFI???? I ordered a cheese steak and a smoothie. Well it's wasn't a philly cheese steak. It was served on white bread and had more mushrooms than beef. No tomato, dressing etc. It was actually pretty good but not what I expected. Smoothie was great and the meal costs 210 tb or $6 USD, a little expensive, but no WIFI, odd. Maybe worth another try.
Breakfast/brunch was okay. The reviews are very correct, some items were forgotten from my order and but the service was not bad. Good cheap breakfast.

I went here several times when staying in Chiang Mai, it is located on the trendy Nimman Road. I'm not sure what the opening hours are but most people seem to visit for lunch/brunch and the menu reflects this. There are lots of options, mainly western but also some Asian and plenty of choice for vegetarians. It is quite small and has a nice atmosphere with seating both inside & out, the service was always very good when I visited. It's not the cheapest place to eat in Chiang Mai but I'll definitely be back.
Cafe is quite nice but in last several months service and food quality went down. It's still good for European food or something like it, but nowadays it's somewhat overpriced and overcrowded.

This small restaurant in the heart of Chiang Mai's Nimmanhaemin district is a great place for a western style breakfast with good coffee. I hesitated to write this because as word spreads about the great breakfast fare offered here, it is becoming more and more difficult to find a seat in the airconditioned area although there is sufficient room for the overflow of diners in the shaded outside area. Oh, and for a special treat, don't forget to try the "Vietnamese eggs set". It's a great breakfast!

I've been to Smoothie Blues a couple of times with family and with friends visiting from overseas. Its a bit of a mare finding parking but if you can get somewhere along the road next to it and walk down; you'll be fine. The food itself is pretty good...simple with fresh smoothie options (obviously). My contention is the price...for such simple food...I'm not sure the price is warranted. In addition to the slightly higher prices...I don't think much of the portions. Western places, you get western prices but you also get western portions. This place has the prices but not the portions. I went with my wife, son, and parents - the bill was 1,200 and only my wife and I had breakfasts (which you always have to order a bit extra). My mum and dad had a pancake each which wasn't all that. Read less

While on a 3 day vacation here we went twice for breakfast and loved both times! We had eggs, waffles, real maple syrup, omelets, avocado sandwiches, smoothies and English muffins. They were out of some kinds of bagels which was disappointing but we made do! Well prepared, fast service..yummy food! They have tables both inside in AC and outside in courtyard..We ate in both places and enjoyed the vibes and friendly staff and customers. This is a must to eat at if you are in C.M.
Love the smoothies and the breakfast options here! The place is a little cramped, so try to go during off peak hours.

Such a convenient and cosy cafe, as we were staying just around the corner, and superb for breakfasts/brunches and smoothies, of course. Although on the corner of a very busy and noisy road (which isn't in Chiang Mai?) it is well shielded by the plants and hedges. Service always quietly efficient, comprehensive menu, food freshly prepared and amazing smoothies to choose from. For these reasons we called in at least once a day during our stay. Free wifi and charging facilities as well. Shame about the smokers outside - can anything ever be done about that?

I am currently studying abroad in Chiang Mai and after about two weeks of eating pad Thai for breakfast my friends and I decided we needed to find a western breakfast. Some other abroad students recommended this place to us. About a 40 minute walk from my apartment but I've done it about 5 times already and will probably go every weekend until I leave! Incredibly delicious sandwiches, smoothies, eggs. Very reasonable prices. I'll order a smoothie (HUGE glass), two scrambled eggs, two toast (again, very large, and tastes homemade), and double bacon and I'll pay a little less than 6USD total for the meal. Service could be better but food always comes quickly and the staff will bring you anything as long as you can flag them down and ask for it. Great hangover cure! Read less
The smoothies are fresh and well priced. We ordered crepes and egg sandwiches which were also very good. There is ample outside seating which is a plus with small children.

Food is fresh; however, the cook is pretty poor! Dishes with combination foods ( ex omelette with cheese and bacon ) the cook doesn't bother chopping it up, it's just whole bacon with eggs. The smoothies are delicious and their juices must be the coldest in town with little chunks of ice. Service is painfully auto-pilot with little to no personality, but it's one of the few places in town one can find a western style breakfast and fresh juices.

I went there with my bf a few times. The smoothies were great. Food were ok. The bad part was the service. The waitress could not care less for the customers. Needed to ask for menu, needed to call someone for order. The owner (or manager) also treated the staffs not good guessing on how she talked with them. Maybe that was why we didn't get a good service.

I had the pleasure of eating here 2 separate times during our recent visit to Chiang Mai. Our first visit was for lunch where I enjoyed a grilled cheese sandwich with onions, tomatoes, and extra cheese. This was one of the best grilled cheese sandwiches I've ever dad. I also enjoyed the french fries while my girl friend enjoyed her pad thai. I thought the vanilla shake with peanut butter was just average tasting and I didn't like my girl friends smoked salmon and avocado salad but I didn't try any dressing on the salad. The next morning we visited for breakfast and I had one of the best eggs benedict of my life. This coupled with the delicious grilled cheese sandwich the previous day solidifies this place as a solid 4 star TripAdvisor ranking. Read less
Great place for breakfast and lunch. Lots to choose from on the menu and delicious smoothies. Service is quick and the prices are very reasonable. 👍🏼
Very delicious breakfast. Their set breakfast are reasonably priced. We enjoyed their mocha coffee as we don't drink strong coffee.

Although is open by local thai but their food is unexpecting good and reasonable, we have a big group and most of us are satisfied for all the things we ordered, the menu has alot alot of choice and all of them is delicious

This Restuarant needs to start paying attention to details if they would like to keep regular customers. Orders are coming out wrong because serving staff don't understand or ask questions for understanding. Staff aprons are in a filthy state. The food is good but they often forget stuff or burn the bagel for example or don't care about finishing details. I assume this is because they don't care too much anymore and just wanna crank the food out. Staff are eating in front of you, chewing while bringing you condiments etc. The standards are low and getting lower. Please fix this smoothie blues.

This place rocks with a robust breakfast menu and a ton of smoothies. I had the avocado breakfast sandwich and it was first rate. Fresh avocados, super tasty sandwich, and perfect smoothies. 5 stars all the way.
We went for breakfast, and the food was very good. I had the Salmon with scrabbled eggs with toast, and it was delicious. I also had the mango, orange and lemon smoothie, also very good.

Western-style breakfast and good smoothies. I had an omelette with steak along with the Berrie Berrie smoothie. The omelette was good with cheese and mushrooms and was enough to keep me satisfied through lunch. They also offer a large selection of sandwiches. With a large breakfast menu you can't go wrong eating here.
My favourite breakfast spot in Nimaan area, they do very nice scrambled eggs, sausage and hash browns, the coffee is decent as well, nice spot to chill,
