Great smoothies, dependably good Western food, easy to communicate with the staff about what you want. The Smoothie Blues and Mango Blues smoothies are the best. Most breakfast items and paninis really solid too.

This is a great little cafe on Nimmanheim Road in the Nimman area for breakfast and lunch. It opens at 7.30am which is early for this area. Their menu is varied and would have to have something for even the fussiest eaters. We would highly recommend the fried spinach with onion and cheese with scrambled eggs and toast. They also make great fruit smoothies. This cafe was a great way to start the day.

I was highly disappointed with the sandwiches here. I was expecting something fresh, tasty, and overall amazing. However, what I received was an overpriced BLT that was less remarkable than a Subway sandwich. The staff were very friendly.

I've been in Chiang mai for 4 months and I'd thought I'd give this place a try to see what all the fuss is about. One time I order a sandwich which was very small, tasted average and was served on regular shop bought sliced bread. I came back for a second try and ordered a breakfast burrito. Literally the worst meal I've had in Thailand. Potatoes were raw and half the meal was large pieces of raw onion. I can only imagine people rate this place because they don't know any better. The Cat House, Dada Cafe and Baan Home Bakery will serve 100 times better sandwiches, breakfast buritos or smoothies and you'll pay almost half the price for them.

I have been in Chiang Mai for almost 3 weeks and I come here about 3 times a week. They have thee friendliest staff and their breakfast reminds me of being back in the states. They normally have Frank Sinatra playing and its always so bright and inviting. You cant go wrong with any of their breakfasts or smoothies, just choose what you like and enjoy.

Eggs benedicts very delicious. The first server was very polite, but she changed shift and left. Other waitress were very silent and even when i order something could not speak ( i am thai) or even give me a nod to acknowledge my order...

Having lived in Chiang Mai for 6 years, Smoothie Blues continues to be our absolute number 1 favorite place to have breakfast in Chiang Mai. We are a family of four with 2 small children. The staff is both friendly and helpful. They always repeat your order and are keen to make sure you get what you want. The quality of the food is excellent. The is an absolutely huge menu, with American breakfast favorites and Thai food as well. The croissants with eggs bacon and cheese and the Hawaii smoothie are our family favorites. The smoothies are made with fresh fruits. The only things that are just average are the omelets. It is a busy place, full of expats, located on the hip road Nimenhamen, so either go early or late on the weekends. They do not take credit cards, but there is a Bangkok Bank across the street, with many ATM's. You can check out their facebook page - https://www.facebook.com/pages/Smoothie-Blues/279831533600. I highly recommend it. Read less

Seeing great servings and amazing western menu, I took my family for a good breakfast. The place is small but very nice and clean. First after our order, a different waiter brought something which was totally not what we ordered. She walked into many tables before serving to the right table. Then they brought my order with a big over easy egg which I had not asked on my plate. I mostly don't criticize waiting service or the management unless it's terribly bad. Food wasn't good. Waffles, hash browns were all not fresh and tasted like reheated frozen food. Butter I think maybe margarine. Smoothie also tasted lame. We only liked the one sausage on the plate. Coffee was not freshly brewed. After eating both my wife and I were felt nauseous most of the day and survived on ginger ales. Restaurant looks nice, menu seems appetizing. But lack of fresh or real ingredients make the food far from you enter hope to munch on. Read less
reasonable pricing, nice location in the middle of nimman. Parking down soi 6 nimman makes this a nice stop for breakfast

At top of Nimm6, it's packed with expats, often families! Delivers fresh delicious nutritious food ! Avocado, salmon, beef, zucchini, squeezed & chilled orange juice, crepes, pancakes, nachos, croissants...anything almost is available! Never disappointed in my dozens of visits ...only wish they would print up new menus because the ones handed out are battered!

This restaurant is only open from 7am to 5pm. The best smoothies in town with a very wide selection of ingredients. Also a great place for breakfast or sandwiches geared to western tastes. English language newspapers available to read while you eat. Wait staff also speaks English. Highly recommended for Farangs.
We ordered there many times. Sometimes when you do home delivery it was a bit cold, but it was still very good. Anything we ordered was nice and had a good size.

Basically Smoothie Blues is the breakfast place to go when everywhere else is randomly closed. Their foods seem cheap and often pre-made. (Fake syrup, margarine butter, frozen hash browns, etc.) If you're craving quality, western breakfasts foods go to Butter is Better!

Need a killer smoothie and a little Western comfort food? This is the place! Great fruit smoothies, bagels, English breakfast, sandwiches, even a little Mexican food! An extensive menu, great for breakfast or lunch.
Have been visiting Smoothie Blues a few times per week for many months. It's the best place to get a proper breakfast in Chiang Mai... eggs benedict/florentine etc and a fantastic big smoothie.

5 of us had lunch. I had a delicious tuna salad with avocado added for the price of 15 baht. The smoothies were delicious. The service was good. I plan on returning for another smoothie and I would recommend this place to a friend:)

I've been going to this place 3-4 mornings per week for months. This morning I dropped a piece of bread on the floor and asked for another. They charged me 20 baht for the new piece. This is how they value good customers. Good sausage, decent food, but terrible attitude.

Generally good food that looks and tastes European - I've ha the eggs benedict. Toast & beans with mushrooms, friends have had the bacon which is ok. The smoothies are really good as are the pancakes muesli and fruit. Spot on. Only suggestion - treat staff better a they look miserable, and if you insist on using those nasty frozen patties for your hash browns, at least don't over cook them and serve them steeping in old oil please.
My Friend and i had a delicous breakfast there. The waitress are very good and polite. You MUST HAVE try the smoothie, it was the best smoothie that i've tried.
standard western breakfast. I had a lassi and bagel. clean and nice. don't go if you want traditional Thai experience.

I ate here regularly for a few weeks and usually ordered the omelet or quesadilla. I would gladly recommend either one. Add avocado if it's in season, and don't forget your coffee. It's better here than most places I tried around town - although I would prefer the cream was warm (maybe just a personal preference).

Yeah, yeah...blues as in you got me singing the blues. I decided to try this old standby and regret doing so. My smoothie was pricey and bland. Really no fruit taste at all. And the floor in the place looked like it was last cleaned during the Bush Administration (Bush #1). Finally, when I paid with a 500 Baht note (about $15 US) it caused all sorts of issues and I was left standing at the front counter 10 minutes while they went somewhere to get change. Ugh. Smoothie places are ubiquitous in Chiang Mai. With all the choices, don't wast your Baht on this lackluster has been.

I had the bacon and egg sandwich with avocado and a smoothie - both were delicious. I paid about $9 AUD - which is a little pricey for Chiang Mai - but I'd definitely eat here again. Word of warning though - this place is really popular, especially with Westerners, so finding a table can be tough and the service is a little slow. All in all though, it's the best breakfast I've had in Chiang Mai and I'll be coming back again. Read less

This is one of my favourite places to eat breakfast in Chiang Mai. I've eaten here on many occasions and never had one disappointing meal! A personal highlight is the vanilla or strawberry milkshake, it is so yummy! A definite must for those who are seeking the home comforts!
Cafe with no wifi, bacon was the saltiest I ever had it was like a block of salt, I had to soak my food in napkins to get rid of the greese and no parking

Had the bagel w/CC and eggs with a coffee. Bagel was ok, eggs good, and coffee was good. Service was really good and it was fairly busy with 4 tables inside full, 1 or 2 on the side full and a couple other tables in front! I'll go back!

I found this centrally located cafe perfect for breakfasts and lunches but also dined one evening. Staff were friendly and helpful. If the combination wasn't on the menus they would make you what you wanted. The service was excellent and very cheap. Def I had fantastic smoothies here.

Smoothie blues has the absolute best breakfast in town. Their eggs benedict is spot on. Their smoothies are amazing. Healthy food, dirt cheap prices and amazing tastes. I eat there everytime (daily) when in Chiang Mai. Usually take several bags to go for Bangkok.

I've lived in CNX for five years and I honestly, don't know why this cafe gets four stars, must be ratings from tourists who don't know there are much, much better options for western food.Same goes for the much vaunted Bake'n'Bite. Seriously overrated. Yes it is an improvement on your typical touristy, crap western food like breakfasts with mini sausage flowers, but not by much. Despite the cafe getting quite popular at one point, the owner is a cheapskate and persists in using very cheap ingredients in his food and charging high process for it. The bacon and cheese is literally the same stuff from 7-11. The bacon shrivels up and ends up looking like dark bacon chips. The cheese is literally processed slices (Kraft singles) and the prices are high because most falang don't know there are better options elsewhere. Great western breakfasts exist in Chiang Mai now. At The Larder & Natwat, you can get organic eggs, rosti, avocado, chorizo, rocket and olive oil and balsamic vinaigrette and cracked pepper for the same price!! No comparison. Their menu is also huge which is never a good sign. Give me a few lovingly prepared options any day. They don't even have a good coffee machine. I'm avoiding giving one star because they have very good smoothies. Read less
Great beverages, which outweigh the food, which was still a good imitation of North American breakfast cuisine.

Now they a seasonal additional avacado menu. I get the bacon avacado egg sandwich and it is really good. A bit expensive but worth it. They even have a really good omelet that is pretty good sized and inexpensive. Most of the rest of the menu is a little more expensive than other places but it's all really good. The pancakes are good but not typical. I think they are sugar coated and it gives them a different texture.
Great Western-style breakfast. Even the pancakes are good (a shocker!) I really enjoyed the eggs benedict, too, and made a temporary exception to my "well-done-eggs-only" rule. Try it out!
Had a delicious and affordable breakfast. The omelet was stuffed with grilled veggies. The bagel and juice was also great.

We started off our day with their famous 'Smoothie Blue' and 'Egg Benedict with Salmon topped with Hollandaise sauce', 'Thai Iced Milk Tea' & 'Caprese Sandwich'...Just perfect! Portion were big and delicious.. Definitely value for money😉👍

I used to go here as a kid in high school and it is just as good as I remember! They have the best smoothies with all sorts of different fruit mixtures and they have delicious food as well! All I can say is YUM! This is a must-visit for all fruit smoothie lovers.
Very good choice of sandwiches, fruit shakes and smoothies. Remarkable the chicken pesto sandwich and the eggs benedict. Crowded in the afternoon and evening.

Went there total of 3 times already. Still could not say anything great about this place. First time, the smoothie i tried was just meh taste more icy and flavorless. Second time, overpriced sticky rice with mango with nothing special to write home about, but smoothie i tried this time was much better than the first time. - Bad attitude server, young guy who ate behind the counter. Third time, a bad attitude waiter took the order and did not repeat what we ordered and totally miss our order. We say thank you after ordered our food and he just walked away... again he ate with his bare hands behind the counter and serving our food. Btw, the smoothie i tried this time was good. Overall, it is a nice place may not be the best in town, a bit on a not so clean place but what can I say it is in the tourist attraction area.... Read less

This place is great for breakfast and late breakfast. The menu shows a lot of sandwiches, omelettes and others. They also have a large choice of smoothies and Lassies. Try the Mango-Lassie. This is my favourite place for breakfast in Chiang Mai.

We stopped by late on a Sunday morning for smoothies and sandwiches after hearing about this little oasis from some expats in town. The menu featured every combination of fruits and juices one could imagine for blending into smoothies. The sandwich menu offered a lot of variety, too! Although we didn't sample the Muesli-fruit-yogurt combinations they offered I think we will try them the next time we visit. The sandwiches were well prepared and the whole grain bread was delicious. This is a good retreat for expats looking for a break from traditional Thai food. We'll be going back while we live here in Chiang Mai for the time being!

I lived next door for a while and came here all the time...because it is very delicious! Sandwiches are very nice and the breakfast omlette- packed full of potatoes, cheese, tomatoes, onion, and your choice of spinach, ham, or bacon is very filling and delicious! If you can't cook your own breakfast, this is second best. Smoothies are good too, but a bit overpriced.

Really enjoying my frequent visits to Smoothie Blues whenever in Chiang Mai. Great choice of fresh and tasty smoothies as well as their creative offer of bagels. My personal favourite is their unequalled Mango Lassi and classic Eggs Benedict.

Amazing hash browns, large portion of sausage, probably the best bacon in town and for an extra 15 baht you can get avocado with any dish. The panini sandwiches are great and come with a side salad and benedict is pretty good as well. Great smoothies or fresh juices and good coffee. Service isn't the fast or the best, but the food is good enough to make up for it.

I am just living around the corner from Smoothie Blues and have passed in many times since moving in but today I decide to give it a try. Anyone who can say food is bad is nuts, Great menu full of choices that are hard to find in other places, a great place for europeans and Americans as they serve all the western breakfast items , waffles, baked beans and all the other breakfast items. They do great healthy options like White scrambled eggs. Also spinach with scrambled eggs is wonderful. Juices and smoothies as well as the coffee all excellent . Average is around 100 to 140 baht but worth it i really think this place would be higher up on Trip advisor as the food is tops. Give it a try you will not be disappointed Opens until 8pm , somedays 9pm Only bummer is NO -Wi-fi. So not great for browsing the net while having breakfast Read less

This is a great place for having breakfast , lunch or dinner. Menu is great and food is excellent. All day breakfasts served and some great healthy options like white scrambled eggs. Good juices but you need to say "no sugar" and lovely coffee also. Staff friendly and plenty of smiles. I get a lovely order of Spinnach , scrambled eggs and cheese, lovely combination and amazing value at baht 149 Excellent selection of sandwiches also. ONLY downside is NO Wi-Fi (come on guys) Read less

I had espresso and banana milkshake for breakfast one day and it was probably the best milkshake I've ever had! I ordered Thai food for breakfast it was light-flavored but very good. Recommend this place!

I've been taking breakfast at Smoothie Blues from 2010 now and I simply love it! Their smoothies, pancakes, waffles, anything you try is simply delicious!! Whenever I go back to my country or far from Chiang Mai, this is the first place I miss!

One of my favorite smoothie places, and a good place to meet with friends in Chiang Mai. You can get a smoothie pretty much anywhere in town but the quality here is top notch. An extensive breakfast and snack menu makes it a good choice for breakfast, brunch or mid afternoon. And with NO WI-FI, its actually a place you can go and have a conversation and not be disturbed!

Ordered a variety of sandwiches for my lunch/dinner everyday via Meals on Wheels 4 U (food delivery). The sandwiches are awesome here! I'm like this guy every time I eat one: http://youtu.be/DcJFdCmN98s

The "Sweetie" smoothy is amazing, and the pesto veggie sandwich on a baguette with added avocado is perhaps the best sandwich I've ever had! Good portions for the price, and as another member noted - the food is always served with a smile, a genuine one. Great staff, great food, convenient location - I highly suggest trying this place out if you haven't already.

Smoothie Blues has become our favourite place for a leisurely start to the day during our stay in Chiang Mai, where we have consistently received courteous,attentive and efficient service. The menu offers an extensive choice of fruit or fruit and dairy smoothies ...we have tried lots of them and they are all delicious! Furthermore, the choice of breakfast and/or lunch dishes is vast, meaning you can visit everyday and select something different from the menu. I would thoroughly recommend the poached eggs which are consistently cooked to perfection. We are obviously not the only people to rate this cafe highly, as after only one week, we are recognising its regular customers!
Not a bad place to have a western breakfast once in a while ... A bit pricey in comparison to Thai breakfast cuisine.
When I'm in the Nieman area it's a favorite respit. All the food is yummy and a nice break from Thai food. Prices are very reasonable as well. Their shakes rock!
ALL the smoothies are great! And a large glass for each one makes them good value for money compared to other places in Chiang Mai. Good food and snack options too.
We enjoyed 2 supreme smoothies at this cafe after enjoying Wot Umong in Chiang Mai. Great atmosphere and the food was also very good.
A great place for breakfasts and lunch. An expat recommended this restaurant and it was well worth a visit. The smoothies were great but the sandwiches on baguette were delicious too.

I originally came here on recommendation and found myself eating here FAR too much when i visited Chiang Mai. I really couldn't get enough. The smoothies, milkshakes, selection of sandwiches, brunches, home tasting bacon and air-con were awesome. Who doesn't like the option of adding a fried egg to every sandwich?! My only small complaint is that it is quite pricey for the standard lunch in Chiang Mai. But once you've been there once i bet you wouldn't mind going back again. Yum.
I seem to be the only one who has had consistently bad food there, as my friends like the place. Location is good, has a cool name, but overpriced.
GREAT coffee and smoothies, delicious breakfasts as well. Lovely place to spend a morning. Not super cheap but brunch like that rarely is. Will definitely come here again. Highly recommended.

best eggs benedict in town. smoothies are top notch too. tried many item on the menu and have never been disappointed. they also have many take home items like bagels and fresh bread. nice little location in nimmenhemin rd, but you need to park in the soi behind it if coming by car. A must visit for breakfasts.
A great small cafe for breakfast. Their whole wheat pancake topped with yogurt and fresh fruit makes for a healthy way to start the day! I also like the fact that they have "brewed" coffee....

Smoothies, sandwiches and breakfast foods are available at this small but busy cafe. We ordered several different smoothie flavored and they were all delicious. If you need a refreshing break on a hot day, head to Smoothie Blues!

Love their sandwich selection and the fact that they are willing to make some changes if you want. When avocados are in season the roasted chicken, pesto and avocado sandwich is the only thing I eat. Service can be a bit slow sometimes but mostly its pretty good.
I just moved here from the States and very happy to find a place with really good western food. I've eaten both breakfast and lunch here and found the food, service and value to be great.

We had a quick early breakfast there which was really amazing, especially the blueberry pancake and the fruitshakes. It's not the cheapest place but you definetely get value for your money. Four stars because the location is not too nice (close to a big road).

Smoothie Blues is an absolute wonder! You'll love the fresh smoothies with such a variety of choices and fruits to combine, and absolutely healthy and fresh! The Blueberry pancakes are mouth smacking good, the beef pita was an wonderful hit for me! The amazing thing is not the fresh ingredients and wonderful taste but the prices make it a place you'll want to return to each day your staying in Chiang Mai. One recommendation, they are limited on space inside the Air Con, so you'll have to sit outside if you don't arrive early, and that's not enjoyable during the hot season.
This is the place! Great value for good old Western comfort food! And they'll add avocado to just about anything and still charge very low prices.
It's the place to go for breakfast food when you want REAL American Breakfast food! They have a great variety to pick from!

I LOVE weekend breakfasts at Smoothie Blues. The dining area is small so we try and get there earlier during the busy season if we want to sit inside. It's a little tight with our enormous stroller, but never a problem. The menu is ridiculously extensive, and that suits us just fine. As we are only ever in town on weekends we are happy to relax and take our time. The food is excellent, and we find the service top notch, not noticeably slow at all.

This is a cafe in a great location but suffers from an over loaded kitchen. The menu is to big for the operation. They should concentrate on trimming down on what they offer so they can do what they do best which is smoothies and sandwiches. Very busy at lunch times and it seems to take just ages to get your order. The coffee is filtered I think not what I would call proper coffee anyway. Not bad all the same and worth a visit

Finally a place in Chiang Mai that does GREAT western breakfast. Thai's are great when it comes to Thai food, and there is no shortage of great Thai resturaunts in Chiang Mai. However, when it comes to a good western breakfast, some places are the pit's. This place was recommended by a friend, so thought I would give it a try, I had a bacon and egg English muffin and my partner egg's Benedict (which I tried) both were excellent. Fresh English muffin and proper bacon. They have great cafe style menu and it is reasonably priced. I read a previous review that someone was not happy with the service. I cant say that I experienced that. The staff seemed very pleasant. This was is at the top of my list for a breakfast eatery in Chiang Mai. Side Note : they do have indoor (air con) and outdoor seating. Read less
The smoothies are pretty good. I've eaten other items here but it would never be my first or second choice for this part of Nimman Rd. The atmosphere is lacking.

We went to Smoothie Blues a couple of times for breakfast and we were always happy with the food - but not with the service. The waiters are very slow & while we are used to getting our dishes never at the same time in Thailand (and okay with it), our side order of hash browns once came 20 minutes after both of us had finished our food! The breakfasts are good too - we loved the Poached Eggs & Baked Beans on Toast and the Bagel with Cheese and Egg. There are dozens of breakfast options on the menu, and as the name suggests they also have Smoothies which are pretty good.

Smoothie Blues is an institution and a life saving organisation if you need healthy food in Nimmanhaemin. There are plenty of food places in Chiang Mai for dinner. So long as you don’t want to have a healthy breakfast you’ll find it a really good food city! But breakfast can be tough in CM, especially over in Nimmanhaemin. However, Smoothie Blues stands like a beacon in the middle of a sea of artificial colouring, pastry bits, coloured yoghurt and bobs, syrup coated junk and sugar infused fruit drinks in plastic cups that seem to abound in Chiang Mai. There are a few backpacker places that do breakfast well I guess, but nothing on the scale of Smoothie Blues and nothing has the same atmosphere and style. The smoothies are indeed the best I have ever had –any place. They are not those icy jobs that freeze your head without so much as a whiff of fruit. Nor are they the mean portions of gluggy goo or the ones that use frozen this or that in a plastic satchel hidden in a freezer like radioactive waste. Nope, they are the genuine fruit deal of massive proportions in a tall glass. I became quite addicted to them along with the toasted sandwiches and mango sticky rice. The food (in a really extensive menu) is unusually good not just for Thailand, but any place in the world. I’m not alone in this opinion either as it seems that Smoothie Blues has a very loyal following of breakfast and lunch Farang and quite a few long timers. It’s quite an interesting group of people; journalists, writers, human rights lawyers, teachers, academics from the uni close by etc. The family who run it seem to be genuinely good people and really concerned that you enjoy their food – which is not hard to do. I’ve become quite fond of the place. Nimman is a really happening area and worth a visit. It has that student vibe and a real pulse; art, nightlife and all sorts of things happening. Smoothie Blues is pretty central to it all. Read less
Love this place. A must in Chiang Mai. Excellent smoothies, breakfasts, huge salads, delicious sandwiches, and proper North American bagels ! Love it.
Really good food, nicely presented, very very good smoothies. Good service (if a wee bit slow), a bit pricier than other places but worth it.
Lovely spot for breakfast, great coffee and smoothies.

Smoothe Blues on the most happening (elite and expat) place in Chiang Mai - NimmanHamin road, opposite Soi 9, is the best place to have a breakfast, brunch or lunch. Very beautifully done in sky blue colour and white, they have one of the best muffins, bagels, waffles and muesli, as well as sandwiches and baguettes among other dishes. Their drink - 'Smoothies' is to die for - try Berry Berry Booster packed with raspberries, and blue, red and black berries. It opens at 8am and remains open till 9pm in the night. Delicious food - they also have outdoor seating for few people although we like the inside better! Strongly recommended

This cafe is great. The service is fantastic. The food is delicious. The atmosphere is clean and calming. I highly recommend trying an avocado, egg and cheese sandwich, which is huge and delicious. The smoothies are phenomenal and worth the 30 baht more than you would pay somewhere else. They make lovely coffee cakes, excellent grilled cheese and pretty good bagels too. If you want western food in Chiang Mai, this is the place to go.

I spent 18 days in Chiang Mai at the Amari Rincome from 1-18 October and rather than pay 500 BHT for breakfast there I looked for other alternatives close to the hotel and Smoothie Blues was one of those. It is, relatively speaking, expensive by Thai standards but the food and smoothies are excellent. Their portion sizes are reasonable (except the juices which are small) and when you just feel like a wholesome western breakfast this is really the place to go. On average I was paying around 220 BHT for my breakfast with a fresh juice. I would pay a similar price in Dubai (where I live) so it's definitely no bargain breakfast but cheaper than the hotel and I couldn't find many places open as early as I was taking breakfast in advance of being picked up for an outdoor activitiy (around 07:00). I found service quite slow personally which was frustrating on some days when I needed to get off quickly but generally this was not an issue as I liked the location and could easily sit here for a couple of hours just taking in the atmosphere. Would definitely go back here but would probably look for a cheaper alternative as well next time. Read less

This place has great breakfast! Try Egg benedict with smoked salmon or Breakfast Burrito. Smoothies and fresh fruit juice also great. The outside tables are nice in the early morning but it gets too noisy after 9 am as it is right on the street.

I like the vibe of this western style cafe. Its a place where I can sit back and enjoy a decent coffee or a tasty smoothie, as I people watch or read the latest English print newspapers which they supply. The menu it's self is interesting, with quite a different selection of breakfast meals that Ive never seen on offer in Thailand, like bacon & bannana on french toast or the breakfast buritto, fresh baggles, eggs benedict with smoked salmon and much much more. I little pricier than the average, but then again, this place is different than the average. is place, it has good coffee, excellent smothies Read less
This place is just pure awesome. Great food and great drinks. Super super good. Great Smoothies! Great breakfasts! Great atmoshpere! Great service!

I spent five months in Chiang Mai from Sept. 2008 until Feb. 2009. The best Western style breakfast in town is definately at Smoothie Blues. The lunches are good and so is dinner. I felt the portions and the prices were reasonable. Breakfast burritos are the best! Its a great family ran business and I would highly recommend visiting if you are in the area. Strike up a conversation and get to know the family. Som, Waii, the mom and dad are always there with a smile.

This particular restaurant seems to get the young ones (mostly foreigners) with money to spare. The smoothies are good and reasonable in price, the sandwiches are also not too bad. But dont try the hot western style food as it is overpriced and the portions are very small. On a final note I must say that all of the staff are very pleasant and attentive.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We had a wonderful last meal of 2025 here! We sat at the sushi counter and marveled at the talent and professionalism of the team of chefs. The servers alike were professional and attentive to our needs. We had the mixed Tempura, sashimi deluxe set, and super fresh rolls. It was all simply delicious. Thank you for a wonderful New Year’s Eve. We ended the evening full and happy!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Great quality for the sashimi. The salmon belly is so fresh and very creamy. Salmon slices are great too. Fresh and thick . The don with Japanese rice is well cooked. Beef tongue is not too nice. Grilled squid is well cooked. But super long waiting time. So much above expectations.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Fresh products of very good quality. Nice Mood. Good Price quality ratio. Nice Service.. think about booking.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Really good sushi, friendly service and when you sit at the bar the sushi is prepared in front of you. Clean, fresh and super tasty.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Service is so fast we don't have time to finish our order the food is already arriving. The sushi are amazing, just like the noodles. The green tea is unlimited refill and has no sugar which is perfect. I highly recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: One of my favorite addresses in Chiang Mai, by far the best sushi! In the restaurant in Nimman, you can watch the chefs in action and enjoy all these Japanese delights. The restaurant terrace in Nim City is very nice. I recommend both. Special mention for sushi cream cheese and spicy salmon.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The food here is so fresh. Everything is so delicious. It is also affordable too. One of the best Japanese restaurants I've ever been to.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The best sushi I ever had. Amazing experience, The service is great and they are so fast, we were a big table and we didn't even finish to order that the first dish were already arriving. Everything is so fresh and I recommend the sushi with avocado and prawn, a must-try!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Tried many japanese places here and this is consintely one of the very best. This type of price to quality is incredible. It can get busy sometimes so it's best to book beforehand. Tried about 10 dishes and loved all of them

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We were with family, very good service, quality, tasty dishes, abundant, varied, very nice place We arrived a while before the kitchen closed, we were also treated quickly and well

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Very busy place with lots of locals on a Sunday night. Handy with Wine Connections alongside to buy wine to dine with. Huge menu and choice at reasonable prices to enjoy eating outside or in. All fun was very tasty and service very good. Will definitely revisit.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Superb culinary experience! One of the best sushi of my life. I highly recommend this property. Reservation strongly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: My favorite restaurant of all Chiang Mai, not just sushi, everything is good. The service is impeccable and fast. And the value for money is really competitive. It is better to book outside for the beautiful atmosphere in the evening.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Took my girlfriend here for dinner last night . Not my first time . The food was excellent , it might cost a little more than some other Japanese in Chiang Mai but the quality is clearly better. Very satisfied.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This was some of the best sushi I have had in the world, much less chiang Mai. They nail the rice, the fish is extremely fresh, and the food is traditional and authentic Japanese. WOW. I came to Chiang Mai after Japan and it beats half the sushi I had in Tokyo at a fraction of the cost .

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Very good!!👏👏👏👏great atmosphere, great music and fantastic food…. We started with sushi, went through misoshiro and ended with shabu shabu... everything was great... it's a shame that the staff speak little English and that they only serve alcohol after 6pm!!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Excellent sushi in Nimman, the best I tried in Chiang mai! The salmon is fresh and service fast, I recommend it

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This is a gem. A friend referred me to this place. If you like or love sushi, this is special. Serene with definite Japanese feel to it. Great presentation and flavorful. Spicy tuna roll were cut on the large side and were a mouthful. An engaging mouthful with different textures and bursts of flavor. Tuna nigiri and unagi were very tasty! Enjoy in the Nimman area.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Better to tell you that here everything is good . . . You might be waiting as the restaurant is not big but it's worth it (lunch is a good option in this matter). There is 2 tables outside for those who cannot handle air conditioned. There is a very good free jazz musical background to make you feel trendy. Staff is very professional. The waiters are efficient and friendly. The 4 sushi masters work in front of the clients to serve high end products with an high-end presentation. The menu is offering a big choice of Japanese specialties. You better have a web connection to know what they are if you are not familiar with the Japanese terms. They are prepared on the order but it's served fast due to the 4 sushis chefs. Even their desserts are interesting. The bill is high because you cannot stop ordering but it's worth it. We cannot stop returning to this place because we spend there always a good time. On the second favorite Japanese, I put Uchi Gastro Bar. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I have been to Uchi(no1 trip advisor sushi restaurant in Chiang Mai) and its very good. But Umai was a delight and a whole level up. The sushi was simply divine. Nicely decorated, good atmosphere, chefs preparing your food in full view, great selection of food and beverages and for the high quality reasonably priced. I shall return soon. Do book to avoid disappointment.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Literally the best sushi I have ever had, and I have had a lot of sushi. The atmosphere was nice, we loved the music and felt lucky to get a spot at the bar! We are going back today!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: A travelling friend recommended sushi umai in chaiangmai. The restaurant has 2 outlets in maya region and nim. We settled for the one at maya. Very good japanese food and good service. We ordered 5 of their lunch specials one fish cheek. All the dishes were very fresh and well done. Chiangmai has many japanese restaurants and this has to be one of the popular and best one.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Excellent quality sushi and a pleasant atmosphere. Large selection including fancy cuts of tuna, foie gras etc.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Absolutely worth the money , it is the best sushi in chiangmai the staff are friendly . Cash payment get 10%off . I highly recommend this Japanese resteraunt!!!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Have dined here half dozen times before Covid. Unfortunately things have changed. Overpriced for portion size, poor service, had as part of dinner Foto Maki, poorly presented and sloppily constructed. No paper in toilet to dry hands. Very disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This is not my first time to dinning here. In the past 3-4 times were wonderful and this is the best Japanese restaurant in Chaing Mai for me. However, this time we were faced with 3 times wrong order, along with the bad solution. 1. Spicy Chicken Karake was served to our table, which we didn’t order (They automatically take it back to serve another table, this is fine for us ) 2. My friend order Salmon Spicy Sushi but turns out they served Salmon Gunkan sushi, which we’re not realized and my friend ate it already (It was serve together with others sushi and these 2 menu look almost the same, but different price). We only realized when I’m gonna order this for one more and I asked my friend how was it. Which she then see the picture in the menu and told me that what she ate just now were not the same as in the menu. So we ask to check with the staff that which menu that they charge us. After checked, a staff told me that they put the right order, but the chef did the wrong menu, the same staff asked did we still need the one we ordered, if we still need it, they will prepare for us, which they will charge us with the menu that we didn’t order. We asked the staff again that why you guys charged us the menu that we did not order. It was not about paying the higher price, but we feel like they force us to pay. 3. We did a second order for more sushi, which again they served the wrong order. So this time I check and make sure that all the orders were correct before eat. I ordered Salmon Hotate sushi, but there was no Hotate in it. Then I asked them that what is this menu, the same staff approached and said sorry the lady who took the second order was a new staff. So I just said fine cuz I didn’t wanna wait anymore. However, they said sorry without any offer to change at all, which they at least should have asked. Last but not least, when we pay for the bill the same staff told ask that they will not charge for the spicy salon sushi (2.) Which finally it was still in the bill. All of these mistaken make us reconsider again that we will come back in the future or not. The taste and quality of food were always great but if the service was bad, so it is not worth the money anyway. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Absolutely extraordinary. They’ve redone the interior very classy and elegant. Quality of the food was exceptional. A real treat to be able to have this level in Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: From the best sushi restaurant in Chiang Mai to very low Many years I been min. 1 time per week for dinner. Amazing. The last two times failed. Tuna very low quality, miso soup no taste and a only a bit warm, tea not hot, warm. Any another food looking very cheap quality. I can’t recommend any more

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Better than expected with decent size servings. Dishes looked and tasted authentic, prices are reasonable. Recommended for solo or group dining.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We all loved our food here. The textures and flavors are beautiful. We had tempura prawns and raw tuna. Worth the money!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: More expensive than most places but totally worth it. I would give 5 stars but they can be slightly inconsistent with their food. If they can consistently repeat how great their food is, Sushi Umai may become the best in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Reserved a table initially for 6 people for my fiance's birthday worrying that our starving-artist friends wouldn't be able to join us due to the price... However, artists know ART when they see it and the 14 of us who ate here all had a simply unforgettable experience full of utter satisfaction and bliss. From the staff being wonderfully supportive right from the start in changing the reservation to more seats, to providing a birthday sushi platter (free of charge!) complete with "Happy Birthday" written in dressing on the plate, to them turning off the lights for us to bring out the cake - they went above and beyond, serving us efficiently with grace and professionalism. My fiance used to manage a Sushi restaurant and Sushi Umai was my choice for her birthday and so I really needed it to live up to its Japanese name... And WOW! As you would hope the food here is an extension of thier chefs' and staffs' passion. The food kept going above and beyond all our expectations! I'll leave it at that to get you excited and your interests piqued. Go here, you really have to experience it for yourself. You wont be dissapointed. Read less
Date_Written: in dressing on the plate, to them turning off the lights for us to bring out the cake - they went above and beyond, serving us efficiently with grace and professionalism.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The best spicy salmon roll in Chiang Mai. Clean, comfortable and great staff. I recommend this place.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Was a little nervous about coming here so far from the sea. Everything I had was superb, very good atmosphere, and some very good discounts on the blackboard. You should definitely try here if you local and love sushi.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The sashimi and sushi are good. Anything grilled though takes forever. The karage is hit and miss...

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Good sushi with Thai’s taste: salty and spicy. Foods are fresh and friendly staff. You can get 10% discount for bill 800bath pay in cash.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: On our last night visiting Chiang Mai, my family had a sushi craving since we eat sushi at least once a week back in NJ. Searched the TripAdvisor reviews and decided on Sushi Umai. We arrived with low expectations. When we left, we had full bellies and a wide smile on our face. Sushi Umai serves better high quality food than many sushi restaurants we have eaten at in the US. We tried the tempura, several rolls (salmon and eel)the Yaki soba, seaweed salad, and also one of the assorted sushi platers. All was well made, fresh, with beautiful presentation, and served promptly. I grew up in Japan(13 yrs) and let me tell you, this place is definitely worth a visit if you are here in town and need your Japanese food fix. Well done. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Excellent cold soba on a hot day in Chiang Mai. Clean and airconditioned restaurant with really nice lunch.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Fresh sushi/sashimii. They have a lot of promotions. Nice ambiance. Very good service. When you become a regular customer, the servers remember your face

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Foods are fresh, good place clean and friendly. Price is not expensive. It near the chiangmai International airport about 15 minute.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I went with some friends and it is a bit pricier than I would normally spend but the qaulity and service was excellent. Worth the extra money.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Walked by this small sushi place and what a nice surprise!! The food was excellent- had salmon shushi, Udon noodles, and shrimp roll- all delicious and authentic. Crowded with locals, had just ran out of Asahi Beer, but washed meal down with iced lemon tea. In general, Chiang Mai does not serve up great meals- but this one worth seeking out!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Unintentionally, had another late dinner, close to 9pm. Wait service and food service were still exceptional. We had 4 different entrees, 3 different sushi entrees and 1 udon noodle dish. Once again a fantastic tasting experiences, 2x within 5 months.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We visited Sushi Umai tonight by the recommendation of a fellow traveller in Chiang Mai, and boy were we lucky we heard about this place! We definitely got excited and over ordered (5 dishes between 2), but also glad we did so we knew what we loved and would eat when we come back again. The salmon belly don was just incredible, as well as the spicy salmon maki. It seems like this place dishes out the best, and freshest fish and MOST GENEROUS portions I have ever had. We can’t wait to go back! Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We had some superb food here. Particularly the spicy salmon maki was outstanding. As most good sushi places you need to carefully budget your meal as some of the sishi platters do not come cheap.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: If you have an appetite for good or rare sushi or sashimi, this one room restaurant is highly recommended. However, make sure that you ask for the price of the dish before you order since some dishes may be pricy. We ordered a tasteful Kimmedai sashimi and it came 4 pieces with over a thousand baht price tag.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This was my first visit, we had 4 different orders of sushi, including prawn and veggie tempura, topped off with Kirin! It's a small place, but very popular so seating can be difficult to find. Had great service, and with seating at the chef's cutting table, we saw items being hand made while dining happily.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We went there many times. Staff is friendly even the place can quickly get packed. Good prices. We haven’t tried fresh fish but other food and sushi , and everything was good.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I love eating here. The food is delicious and the service is great! The first time I was there, the owner or manager was really nice and even offered me some tea on the house. I highly recommend the spicy salmon maki, the gyoza and the okonomiake.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Hands down best sushi in Chiang Mai. Worth every penny. Usually go atleast once a month to treat myself to "Salmon Avocado Maki".

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: In the middle of Nimman area Sushi Umai is not an ordinary Japanese restaurant with the very well selection of the raw fish and the craft technique of the chef made a incredible taste. The price is reasonable. I personally suggest you to try Chef's Omakase which you need to make a reservation in advance and you will see the true Sushi Umai

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I love eating sushi here. It was very yummy. The staff taking a good care to clients. There is several time that we didn't finish the dish like the sauce I still eat on dish, the staff took it without asking me, hmmm? Anyway, I still enjoy the food. Definitely recommend!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: A wonderful little nook. Locals and tourist equally visit this restaurant. Not everyone speaks English which makes the ordering difficult. Got a nice saki (junmai ginjo). Ate 1 roll, sashimi plate, uni, ramen, seaweed salad. I would not come here for ramen - the noodles were perfect but the broth was not a winner for me (sorry). All dishes were fresh and quickly prepared. Clean and neat with air conditioning.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We have been to Umai a few years ago. Our recent return visit reminded us of what we like about Sushi Umai. This smallish restaurant and sushi bar puts out the best sushi. The staff is competent, caring and efficient. They try to keep things moving and work as a team. Excellent presentation and high quality food. We particularly enjoyed the tempura. Check the posting for the busiest hours as Sushi Umai can fill up quickly.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Fantastic quality at reasonable prices!! We had the hotate mentai sushi, foie gras sushi, fresh oysters, tempura with cold soba and sashimi. Every dish was perfect!!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We had soba noodles, udon, adashi tofu, nigiri, thai salmon salad and the salmon truffle. Noodles and nigiri was expected but the 2 salmon dishes were really good, really soft and fatty which is right. A touch on the pricey side for thailand. 4 people about 2000baht (£45). however, this would be double in UK. The overall experience didn't blow me away, so only 4 stars. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The tempura was pretty good but the cha soba was disappointing as the soba was not springy and the sauce was too salty. Hope to see improvement in my next visit.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We went there on a recommendation from a friend and were not disappointed. The menu has a good selection of japanese food, with non fish / seafood options. Everything we have ordered was good. It is better to book or go early as this place is not big and quickly full.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We had the best sushi in chiang mai at pur last holidays. small place, but good location and very clean.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I ordered Jaru Udon and mixed salad. Both of them are perfect like original Japanese cuisine. No doubt for the quality. :-))

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Poorly run place. Came in @ 8.30 pm had no more salmon, no more tuna, no more Kirin beer. Lousy staff behind the sushi bar, poor waitressing, this isn't Thailand, it's see and be seen, pretentious and expensive. Will not visit again.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We sat in the “bar” and were able to see the art of making sushi! Great experience and of course super nice sushi! Would highly recommend this place over any other sushi place in the Nimman neighbourhood!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Possibly the best sushi in chiangmai that serves great sushi sashimi along with good service. Must visit ! Best sushi i 've ever had !

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Got excited because of the review and made a mistake of ordering a full set of nigiri but only able to eat 2 pieces bc the rest of them smelled really bad. Shame on them for serving rotten sushi. I paid and fled. Never again.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: They have many options of sushi and temakis. The food is very good. Prices are very nice for the quality of the food. I would recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: 일식이 땡겨 갔는데 나쁘지 않았어요. 한국에서 먹는 것에 비하면 좀 아쉽지만 거의 흡사합니다. 새우덴뿌라와 자루소바는 별로, 카레우동의 카레는 한국카레맛, 장어롤은 굿이었습니다. Roll was great, soba and denpura were not as good as expected.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Fresh. Amazing. Made in front of your eyes. Nothing else to say. 10/10 would recommend to any sushi/Japanese food fan 👍

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The best nigiri I have tasted, prepared by the owner in front of us as we sat at the bar. The quality is reflected in the price but by European standards this was very good value

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Everything we ordered was very tasty, fast service. We had eel maki, tempura, takoyaki, and two more dishes. All satisfying. Great experience.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: If you want to have japanese dishes after long trip in thai, I recommend you to visit sushi umai. I am fully satisfied with all foods I ate at this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Fabulous find, fresh plus, very tender cuts. Note promotional menu too. Place is pretty easy to find, sorry no exterior pic here.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Restaurant is a bit small, but it keeps it quaint. Decor is fitting and welcoming and creates a nice atmosphere, the facilities themselves are clean and tidy. The staff is attentive and kind and seem happy to work there and genuinely seem glad to help. The prices are a bit high, but all good sushi is... after travelling to several different countries and cities in Asia over the course of a year this seems to be one of the more authentic westernized sushi joints I've been to... and yeah the sushi is westernized, but hey that's the way the majority of us like it. Good place for a casual business meeting, friends, solo, and well-suited for almost any occasion. Definitely worth the visit for anyone who likes sushi or has a craving, should more than satisfy. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: If you go there you must absolutely try everything new the chef is doing : The sushi foie gras is my favorite :) The sushis, sashimis and soup are great and as I've eat there maybe ten time already I can garantie that the experience is always fantastic.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The food and service is perfect .Loved their sushi and sashimi , Foie gras sushi was fantastic . I highly recommend this Japanese restaurant in Town.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We were craving some sushi after having a lot of Thai food and we passed by this place during the day and thought we should stop in for dinner. The sushi was really good and the fish portions were generous. The only reason this review did not get a 5th star is because it is a bit on the pricey side for Thailand. Other than that the food and the atmosphere were great.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Love it so much ! the whole menu is just perfect. Salmon is amazing there always fresh and melting. Same quality than a great japanese restaurant in Osaka but 2 or 3 times cheaper

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The wife and I came out to try it. We both left really happy! Good food and service compared to many other sushi places in chiang mai. It's a little more pricy but you get what you pay for

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Everything was good as expected. Inexpensive Japanese food, well served, fresh. Not crowdy. Clean. Fast. Quite.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Ever since our first visit to Sushi Umai it has become our must go place in Chiangmai and has never been disappointed. We usually have the omakase style and the chef Banz will always prepare the best and freshest sushi for you. You could just let me how many pieces you want or any food preference you have and he will take good care of you. It is such a joy sitting at the sushi bar table and watch him preparing the shushi and pass it to you once it is done. You don't have to worry about the price at all cos it is very reasonable. It will definitely stay in my list of must go restaurants in Chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This place was surprising to us. We didn't expect to find such good sushi here, in Chiang Mai, it's even better than some of the better sushi restaurants at home.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: If you love Japanese, this is truly a great address: delicious food at a very affordable price. To bad we don't have an umai home... it was soo good that we returned two nights in a row. Another family we met went even 3 times during their stay! Bribg tour wine!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Im a huge sushi fan. What a great surprise! The makis were DELICIOUS and fresh! The service quick & was perfect. Nothing negative to say. I definately recommend!!!! Give it a try.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Food was OK but I think I have been spoilt living in New Zealand where we have superb sushi options. There was very little rice with the sashimi sushi set menu I chose. My partner's avocado maki rice was soggy and the nori was chewy. But I guess it is great that there is a sushi option here for locals with the a sushi addiction like mine. Service was good and the place seemed very popular with regulars.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Delicious sushi, great price and very friendly staff - lovely little place! Definitely worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Some of the best sushi I ever had, soft, delicate & delicious. I can not say anything bad about this place

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The fact that it was full with a waiting list bodes well, especially as we really didn't have to wait very long to get in. The fact that it was constantly full with guests leaving and arriving the whole evening confirmed that the place has made a name for itself and kept it. The service is excellent, the food amazing the air on welcome. We had the salmon salad, oysters, smoked eel sushi , salmon sushi, ramen, gyosa , spicy salmon sushi, all good.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Prices are not as high as many other places and quality is decent, making this great value for money. I ordered Salmon Sashimi (140 baht), Salmon Sushi (30 baht per piece) and a Ramen Dish (169 baht). The salmon looked fresh and tasted good. The ramen was also good. I don't think the taste was as high as I expected reading previous reviews but I would certainly eat here again.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I am so glad that I found this place thanks to TripAdvisor. Every single one of the reviews speaks the truth. The sushi tastes amazing and the fish is super fresh. My favorite was the spicy salmon, which was actually spicy! The place closes early (21.30 last orders) so be on time (but do go!!!)

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We didn't try sushi. We tried ramen and soba, which are not the best. The taste is average. They got the order wrong and happily changed to the right one, which is very nice. The staffs are probably the best part of the restaurant. They are nice and attentive. We observed the owner (seen at the sushi bar and on the menu) making the sushi, after he finished one plate, he got his iPhone out and took a picture of it. We are pretty sure he didn't wash his hands afterward. We think it is not so professional to touch the phone since he had to touch directly the fresh product. So consume at your own risks! We can't comment on the taste of sushi because we didn't try it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Sushi Umai is one hidden gem. Why? Because on the first look at the exterior and interior, nothing seems very impressive, not to mention it is hidden in A small lane in Nimmanheim 13 (it's salad concept's lane n turn Right at junction about 100m). However, if you chooses to venture inside, you will be rewarded with great food and services. We will not miss having a meal in Sushi Umai Everytime we are in Chiangmai. We have recommended our friends to dine here (both singaporean n locals) and they have full of praises. High-quality sushi and Japanese cuisine in a cozy setting. Price is reasonable in consideration of the quality. The texture of sashimi is awesomely fresh and yet retaining the natural sweetness of the seafood, the silkiness of Salmon and firmness of Swordfish is irresistible. Pure enjoyment if great food. The host/ Mod n Chef/Benz are both very hospitable n their team are very attentive n smiley. We can't wait to be back soon. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Upon seeing good reviews on trip adviser..decided to take the time to drive there. I was satisfied with the quality of food and service. Amazing place! Gonna be a regular visiting place for me in chiangmai. Love the sashimi and the prawn tempura maki.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We went over for dinner. Had a Lovely meal, fish was fresh and delicious. Our only problem was that they do not receive credit cards under 1000 baht- it took a while to understand that as the staff dont speak English. Other than that it was great. Great food for a fair price

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: It was delicious and the staff was very nice and helpful. We'll be back. They understood our special needs because we were with 4 kids. We loved the sushi and the noodles soup.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Living in Chiang Mai for several years now, and with Asian blood, I can say that this is a regular visit for me. Whenever I am looking to eat Japanese this is my go to place. I have been around 10 times so far and the quality has yet to falter. The seafood is delivered overnight directly from the Thai coast (as told to me by a waitress at the restaurant) and you can definitely taste the freshness. I recommend the takoyaki (a personal favorite) and the salmon sashimi. Note: The owner of the restaurant is seen at the sushi bar and looks like an Asian Ben Affleck.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: This was without a doubt the best sushi we have ever eaten (and we have eaten at A LOT of sushi places). Compared to the prices in Amsterdam this wasn't expensive at all, and we liked it so much that the prices didn't even matter. We we're completely full and spended about 25 euros. A must try when you're in Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Easily one of the best sushi restaurants in town. I love Japanese food, and finding authentic Japanese food is very hard. I have been to countless Japanese restaurants all over town, but nothing remained in my memory the way Sushi Umai did. I believe it's a restaurant you need to know about or tell others in order for it to be visited. Not many people walk by the street the restaurant is located in, so it would be nice to tell others about it. I believe the quality of the food for its price is outstanding. It is not too expensive and it is not too cheap. The only thing that I didn't enjoy were the oysters, but that's because Chiang Mai is far from the sea. The sushi, sashimi, nigiri, etc... all amazing. I am currently advising anybody who wants delicious Japanese food to go to Sushi Umai. My family will be visiting me real soon and I have already told them I will take them there. Last but not least, the staff is very helpful and polite, and often deserve a big tip! All in all, I would recommend this restaurant to everybody! Even if sushi isn't your favorite, there are other delicious meals such as curry, prawn tempura, miso soup, etc...Big thumbs up and 5-star for Sushi Umai! Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We have visit this restaurant two time, the sushi and sashimi are very fresh and delicious. Please don't forgot to order the prawn tempura, it is as good as over 100 year history Japanese restaurant in Sensoji Temple!!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We came across the restaurant during our evening walk and decided to give it a try as we saw the chef preparing the sushi in the sushi bar very attentively and thought it is usually a good sign. And we are not disappointed at all. We tried the assorted sushi which was just divine. The salmon belly melted in my mouth and the salmon roe juicy and fresh. We also tried the tempura, grilled Kamachi head, cold noodles. All were yummy but the sushi stood out from the rest and we have to say it is the best sushi we have ever head in Thailand and the most authentic ones we had in Thailand too. The portion of the rice is just right and the ways and technique he used to make the sushi showed you how well trained he is. He is very attentive to the details and one could see he really uses his heart to make the sushi and is not just preparing some routine stuff. Being so contented with the sushi we asked whether he provides omakase (which is somewhat like the tasting menu) too and he said yes, on Tuesday and Friday. So we booked the omakase on Tuesday lunch as the restaurant will be less busy at that time. We were served three appetisers before the 12 pieces of delicious sushi. Initially I thought I could not finish them but there were so delicious that I could finish them all. The sushi are all made of the freshest ingredients with the taste carefully balanced with various different sauces or seasoning. It is a nice experience too as we were sitting at the sushi bar and the Chef Benz told us about his training in London and how he came along opening his restaurant in Chiangmai. It really feels like talking to your friend while he is preparing the meals for you. The gorgeous meal finished with a prawn miso soup and green tea ice-cream and the bills are extremely reasonable for the quality and quantity of food we had. If you fancy sushi while you are in Chiangmai, Umai is no doubt the one your have to try. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Sushi Umai is located in the trendy Nimmanhaemin Road area, about 10 mins by vehicle from the old city. generous servings, and reasonable prices (quite possibly the cheapest Japanese meal we've ever had!) The nigiri salmon belly was smoky and creamy, and agedashi tofu and gyoza were just as you would expect; crisp and light on the outside and fluffy inside. The staff had good english too, and were so friendly and humble. If you're craving Japanese food in the north of Thailand, definitely come here.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Imported fish that's very fresh, owned by couple that works the restaurant, quality ingredients, this is a must if you love fish and sushi

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The sushi pieces were great, Salmon and Tuna pieces were big and very fresh. The Salmon Maki rolls were delicious and the avocado rolls were yummy as well. Prices were a bit on the expensive side but willing to pay for freshness. I think the Wasabi was homemade. It looked fresh and tasted amazing, don't put your regular amount on the sushi as it a lot more spicy and will kick you in your mouth! Will be back when my next sushi craving hits.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I found this place when I was walking and stopped in... It was some of the best sushi I have ever had. The owner and staff are extremely nice and accommodating... Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Very nice Japanese restaurant. They close at 10pm and they are pretty cheap. 150 bath for 6 california rolls, 100 bath for 6 maki salmon. They are really amazing. They made the sushis just in front of you + the staff is very friendly. Need to go there. I will be back for sure.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: The food was great but the customer service needs some tightening up. My order was mixed up, the staff forgot to serve one of my dishes and I was swiftly handed the bill at 14.25 as I was told they were closing at 14.30. It would have been nice to have been told about this early closure as I had just about finished my food. However, the food was tasty, the chef is a nice guy and despite the hiccups, the staff weren't unpleasant.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We tried a few different dishes on the menu today - and wish we had room in our bellies for more! The food is seriously good and so delicious. The artistic element of traditional Japanese food and sushi can be found here - and the price is great. We live out of town but will be sure to be back here whenever we need our sushi and teriyaki chicken cravings fulfilled! Arigato!!

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: It's really awesome when the very fresh fish and raw meat with small pieces of Japanese rice and special seasonings are put into your mouth. You should try the special menu of the day which will be changed according to the season and imported materials. I also love "Foie gras Maki". This restaurant is a household business. Chief himself cooks every pieces of sushi with his sharp knife and sharp heart. He can speak English very well. Other Japanese menu, for example, Japanese salad, salmon teriyaki, are also delicious. You should have lunch or dinner here once in your life if you visited Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: We visit ChiangMai every year. We travelled with 2 children age 8 and 11 and my son hardly eat Sashimi and here he ate a place of sashimi salmon slices and he requested one more place... We also love the Raw egg over Gyudon (beef rice with raw egg), its fabulous. The teriyaki grilled chicken with rice was with perfect seasoning and not too sweet. - absolutely delicious. The miso soup, it's not salty like any miso soup and it's perfect. We came the next day and tried the salmon charcoal grilled and it is not over/under cooked and perfect and taste great. Benz the chef and his wife, Mod are very hospitable and helpful. The waitress are very attentive and this made the whole dining sessions an enjoyable one. Agree with the other diners that this restaurant is by far the best we have had eaten in Chiangmai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Really good japanese restaurant!! Cute and small place where the fish is fresh the food is excellent and the service is great! The chef speaks very good english! To try absolutly if you come to Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Really good japanese restaurant!! Cute and small place where the fish is fresh, the food is excellent and the service is great! The chef speaks good english! To try absolutly if you come to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Authentic or original sushis, you choose, they're always good. There are quite a few sushi restaurants in the area, but this is definitively the best, by far. I've been here quite a few times and I was never disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: Great sushi. Small place relaxed atmosphere. Owner-chef operated. Big Japanese beers in the Thai tradition. The wasabi will kick your butt. ⚠️

Restaurant_Name: Sushi Umai Nimman ซูชิ อูมัย นิมมาน
Detail: I and my friend always go to this resteraunt . We love to order sushi and sashimi. They using freshness fish and ingredients . Chef can speak very good English . spicy tuna maki is the popular menu . Reccomend this place . Must try it .
Friendly staff, delicious sushi. Akami, chuhtoro and oyster clear soup 😋. Go on Friday night and after your meal here grab a desert from the street market on their doorstep!

In Nimman on a quiet residential street. They have a terrace on the side of the street but as it’s a quiet area there is virtually no traffic in the evenings. Very friendly and efficient staff. Really enjoyed the sushi here as well as the pork gyoza and the tofu. The only slight draw back is that the menu is large and so we could only taste a small part of it. A very very good Japanese restaurant. Highly recommended.

Great “Japanese” food. My wife and I wanted Japanese food for dinner and that’s exactly what UCHI delivered. Great attentive staff team, food was delicious, and the decor was beautiful. Thanks Uchi team for making dinner feel special. Definitely a night my wife and I will remember. Dōmo arigatō gozaimashita- hontō ni sugoi!
The sushi and the other food was different and very good: innovative and excellent quality. Usual sushi reasonably priced, special dishes more expensive but worth it.

We have been often at this restaurant and every time it was very delicious. There are so many special and rare dishes to choose from. We will definitely come again in the future. The restaurant has a great atmosphere, super elegant and cozy!
Best experience in chiangmai! Uchi Bar Chiang Mai has a cozy vibe, delicious fresh food, and friendly service. Loved their creative cocktails!
Good service, nice food, amazing drinks I would give this place a 10 out of 10. Overall this place is amazing I suggest you to come with your family for a dinner.
10 out of 10. Was here in covid times and glad to see it still open and as good as ever. Will come again next trip to Chian g Mai
Excellent, impeccable welcome, quality product and simply delicious. Slightly off-center but 8 min by taxi then it is still perfect. I would obviously recommend
The food was delicious. The quality of the food was beautiful. Today I brought the family with a great meal.
Great Experience at chiangmai , the best sushi and best service, Hope to come back to meet lovely staff and chef here love it!!
very good food and service. The grilled squit is nice. Sashimi and sushi are fresh and innovative. Good selection of drinks and dessert.
Tasty food, comfortable seating and friendly service. Recommend the teriyaki squid and garlic fried rice. Sushi was good quality too.

we made a reservarion for Valentine’s day and it could not be more perfect! Food was incredible as much as the service. They had live jazz that day. It is right next to the Nimman Area and its market, so if you wanna the vibe from the market you can take a place outdoor; but if you want a more private place, you can get a table inside.
The waiters gave us really good service and explained everything we asked, the food came really tasty and special in many form will come back again
An exceptional Japanese dining experience with fresh sushi, flavorful ramen, and attentive service. The ambiance is cozy, and every dish is crafted with precision. Perfect for food lovers!

As I stayed around the neighborhood, I'm visiting this place for over 50 times last year for a casual solo drink and some food. I'd say the food wuality worth the price and it has a good vibes perfect for conversation.
Amazing atmosphere, superb food. A little walk from the One Nimman. Worth the try. Server was helpful and so much section of fishes.

The atmosphere here feels super sophisticated and fancy. The food definitely matches this but in an affordable price range. Even though it’s a little on the higher end, the quality of the food and service is enough to justify the price tag. Definitely worth your visit!
Good atmosphere, delicious food, standard service, spotless cleanliness, beautiful decor, perfect for people who like to eat sushi.
Good overall atmosphere, foods were great tasting, wide variety of foods including western fusion, and the menu includes a dedicated section for vegans
The atmosphere is very nice, the dishes are gorgeous, the staff are lovely, the fish are fresh, and the meats are fresh from the outside.
In the middle of the Nimen, easy access, beautifully decorated interiors, a warm atmosphere, good staff, fresh food, good taste, everything is perfect.

The newly renovated Uchi offers a sophisticated dining experience. The glamorous interior sets the perfect backdrop for their excellent food and drink offerings. Combined with attentive service, this restaurant delivers on all fronts. Don't miss out on experiencing this gem - you won't be disappointed!
Highly recommended for a special fine dinning with your love. Love the atmosphere and their services. Food and beverages are great. Hardly find the kind of restaurant in Chiangmai
Great atmosphere. The cocktails are excellent and original. The food too.👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻 The service is lovely

I visited UCHI and had a great experience. The sushi was fresh and flavorful, with each piece made to perfection. The staff was friendly and welcoming, creating a comfortable dining atmosphere. The menu offered a good variety of options, and everything we tried was delicious. The uni was my favorite—highly recommended. The restaurant was clean, and the service was prompt. It’s a cozy place that’s perfect for enjoying quality sushi without being too fancy. I would definitely come back for another meal. A great spot for sushi lovers looking for something reliable!
The atmosphere is very good, it’s worth gathering with friends, the menu looks good, interesting, the menu is diverse, with food and drinks to eat.
