Very good food, the environment is nice and comfortable. But the service can be more sincere. Overall we enjoyed very much dinner here. Highly recommended
Very good sushi and very fresh. I would say it among the best in town. Waiter and waitress are friendly. The price is reasonable.
Good facility and service, near by road side very convenient. If you visit Chiangmai and find Japanese restaurants just come
The Japanese food is delicious, the food is fresh, the bar is delicious, the bartender can tell his taste buds.
Beautiful restaurant with parking, plenty of delicious Japanese dishes, nice cocktail bars and helpful staff.
Amazing food, fantastic ambiance and excellent service! We were blown away and loved everything! We hope to return if we get an opportunity.
It was very good food, the service was fantastic. I cost myself a lot ordered Wagyu A4, sushi bits 4 nigiri and tempura plate.
Amazing food. Amazing service. The sushi is the best We had in Asia! Price are not too bad either. Really good vibe at the resturant.
This is a private and chill cocktail bar and restaurant. The environment is cozy and friendly from a long day! Highly recommended
Sushi quality is so amazing … I dinner here randomly every dish is so fresh and service is also incredible ~!!! The vibe also nice , 10/10 would recommend 😍😍😍

Second time here. Reasonably priced and great quality! Lots of alcohol options available too. I remember having some kani salad on my first visit half a year ago which was very delicious but can’t find it on the menu anymore. Will recommend their Hokkaido scallop and pork steak.
Good atmosphere nice drinks and walking distance from Travelodge . Nice vibes great for big groups family and couple . But close to early !
Nice and cosy cocktail bar in Japanese restaurant. You can select based drink then bartender can help to customize for you. It’s very nice..
“Fantastic drinks and amazing cocktails! They can make any drink you want, customized perfectly to your favorite taste. Highly recommend at CNX!

Great spot with a nice vibe, perfect for a date. The cocktails were delicious, and the food was really good too. The prices were reasonable for the quality. Would definitely recommend for a chill night out.
The food tastes great, and the ingredients are fresh and clean. The service from the staff in the restaurant is excellent.
The atmosphere is very good. Great food, good drinks, good service. Loved the parking, customer-conscious staff, they can get their own cocktails.
I personally loved it, the atmosphere was very nice, the staff was helpful, the bar was fun talking, the food was delicious.
The food is delicious, the shop style is beautiful, the food is delicious, so I recommend ordering Twilight.
The food was delicious, the SALMON UZUKURI and FOIE GRAS ROLL dishes were delicious, I would recommend to try it, really good, and the personal drink I would recommend is PAPEDA PARADISE.

His sleeves were getting into all of the food he had packed aside from the salmon tartar so I threw out the stuff he packed when I got home. I had to pack the rest of the food because he was less able to pick up food with chopsticks than I am and I am white. We paid almost 5,500 thb and while the food quality is good the service is lacking and it seems like they don’t care about hygiene. If you are looking for an oasis of calm in the hustle and bustle of Thailand, you will find it here. In this restaurant, you can eat with your eyes in every respect. The rooms are stylishly furnished, the chairs comfortable, and if you are too annoyed by the colorful plastic dishes of the surrounding Thai restaurants, you can get decent dishes here and an overall very pleasant, well-chilled ambience. The food is a bit more expensive compared to the Thai standard, but it is worth it. The menu is well illustrated and very extensive and the tempura portion is surprisingly large. The fish is fresh and delicious, overall we had a wonderful meal and would love to come back. If you want to drop by spontaneously for lunch, go around 12 noon. It is still quiet then, most people don't come to eat until 1 p.m. Read less

I came with a friend for the first time. A slap. Simple and uncomplicated result: I went back every day for a week. Not out of habit, out of desire. And when a restaurant becomes a reflex, something serious happens. Clear advice: reserve. Between 7 p.m. and 8 p.m., even if the place is huge, with a very large outdoor terrace, it is easy to be without a table outside. Personally, I booked every night, always for 7 or 8 p.m., always outside. No regrets. It is an Italian restaurant. Pizza, pasta, meat, fish, antipasti. On paper, nothing original. Except that the difference is immediate: the products are of remarkable quality and above all authentically Italian. Not an imitation, not a rough adaptation. Real. And it changes everything. Vongole spaghetti is impeccable. Controlled pizzas, including dough. The antipasti set the tone: excellent olives, truffle sausage perfectly chosen, net products, frank, without shambles. Everything is fair. The prices for a European restaurant in Thailand are more than correct. Really okay. And in terms of quality, it becomes almost indecent. We pay for what we eat, not the decor or storytelling. The wine list alone deserves attention. Very broad, consistent, well thought out. Special mention for an Argentine Malbec at 990 baht: very good wine, frank, balanced. I took the bottle for myself... which I finished over two days. No shame. All wines are available by the glass, between 200 and 250 baht. And homemade wine, which I have not tasted but almost regret: 160 baht a glass, 900 baht a liter. At this price, it is not a trap, it is an invitation. And then there's the gin and tonic. We need to talk about it. Big drink. The tonic served separately, so it's not cracked by ice. And a small saucer with orange, lemon, cucumber and rosemary wedge, to dose yourself. I have never had a gin and tonic served like this in Thailand. And, honestly, even in Belgium, it is rare. The service is impeccable. There are many, maybe twenty, maybe thirty, whatever. They are present, attentive, smiling, efficient, without heaviness. The boss is Italian, of course. I have spoken several times with him: accessible, warm, professional. A real restaurateur, not a remote manager. If I return to Chiang Mai, this place will be my headquarters. Without hesitation. When you don’t want to eat Thai, or just want to eat very well, Why Not is a no-brainer. A restaurant that does exactly what it promises, and does it remarkably well. Read less

We stayed in this restaurant on the advice of a friend and congeiamo in turn. Excellent quality-price. Excellent cuisine and wine list. Nice and pleasant environment.

We stayed at Why not for 3 nights in a row. Immediately we felt calm, Emilio owner of the place and 'a champion: he entertained us with stories and advice on what to eat. Italian products, which travel half the world but that you can taste in a pasta to the norm, orecchiette alla barese. Of course the pizza is delicious. Thanks Emilio and thanks also to the staff, guys who want to work. Highly recommended!

Delicious food, fantastic service and a great atmosphere! Reasonably priced, make sure you book a table always busy. I had penne pesto simple but full of flavour. Perfect place if you want a break from Thai food.

Christmas eve, were there for Dinner, probably the 7th time...two years ago it was excellent, last year it was good, this time it was ok...still nice to spend time there but quality decreases. Then we were asked to go since they need the table....we were not informed before☹️

Awesome place, friendly atmosphere and great service. Owner is really friendly and every time I visit chiang mai we always pay a visit. Will be back sometime soon. Great choices and several areas to dine.
The restaurant has an amazing Italian selection from pastas, pizzas, seafood, and all kind of antipasti. The Italian owner is a great person.

Great Italian restaurant. We stayed there for two nights in a row! Great food, of the highest quality! Genuine and fresh products! Owner Emiddio is a really lovely person is very humble! Thank you for delighting our palates with your recipes! Highly recommended 😍

Recommended by our local tour operator, we went to Emidio being amazed by the variety of dishes offered, the speed of service and the goodness of the dishes, whose main ingredients are all of healthy import The restaurant has a nice dehors and therefore you can also eat outside! We returned for 2 days in a row and if we had stayed in Chiang never, even the third!
Pizza with spectacular wood oven with imported products of excellent quality. Raw Parma, Grana Padano, Gorgonzola, in short, we are in Italy! 🇮🇹 Highly recommended!

Excellent Italian restaurant in Chiang Mai. Very good pizza with high quality products. The pasta is also very good, perhaps you need to pay more attention to cooking. Well-stocked wine cellar. Extremely friendly staff, helpful and attentive to customer needs. Absolutely recommended!

Why not? ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE! Me and my wife came in and ordered the eggplant starter and the risotto truffle balls. They were both AWFUL, lacking taste and SALT. There was not an ounce of seasoning in the food. Once we complained to the manager about or experience we were told flat out that they would not remove the tasteless eggplant dish from our bill, as it was uneatable! As a guest I basically had to argue back and forth with the so called “manager” to be accommodated. Eventually we gave up, having ordered 3 main dishes as well my hopes were low so we asked for to pay for what we’ve had so far (2 drinks and the 2 disgusting apps) but were told we had to pay also for the food we hadn’t yet received! I refused and told them I will pay only for the drinks and the two appetizers we didn’t even like. I took a loss paying for dishes that were tasteless, but at least we got outta there before more disappointing dishes came out. Read less
Ottoma pizza, fragrant and well cooked pasta. Quality ingredients. Pasta cooked to perfection, carbonara without cream...! Friendly staff, reasonable prices. We'll be back!

The best of the three Italian restaurants I tried in Chiangmai. Food was very tasty but what I enjoyed most was the red wine. All their bottles show their VIVINO rating. Great idea! So often we don’t recognize the wines listed and this helps so much. And they are fairly priced.

On the advice of a friend we went to this Italian restaurant. Everything was surprising as focaccia, pizza and pasta were so good that you wouldn't regret Italy. Prices were normal, the owner was always present, kind and friendly with everyone, I absolutely recommend it

The quality of Italian restaurants in Chiang mai seems to have declined over the past few years. Why Not doesn't do anything to raise the standard. Most staff fail to smile or even offer a greeting on arrival. There is a stern looking European man who walks around like an overseer but doesn't say anything. Most unwelcoming. The food is of average quality but priced in the mid range. The arancini was of supermarket standard and basically tasteless. The minestrone was good but served with tiny pieces of bread. This is the first Italian restaurant I have been to that does not provide complimentary bread. Pizzas are acceptable if you are craving one. The wines are ok but nothing special. The best thing was the Italian beer on tap. I doubt I will return. Read less
If you are Italian and want to get away from Thai food, this is the right place to refresh the memory of your Italian taste buds. Good food, good wine, and good Italian espresso to finish.

We tested the pizza, nothing to say about the ingredients which are of good quality and in sufficient quantity, only the dough was too soft giving the whole thing a poor hold. The glass of wine, for its part, is really insufficient compared to the asking price of 230Tbh, from experience, even if the quality is there, it lacks generosity. In addition, the decoration does not live up to the standard of the menu.
One of the best Italian placvces outside of italy that is partly ciliac friendly. Great impressive wine by the glass menu and at Thai prices too! good attentive service.

I can't drink wine. Because of a severe headache. But why not? They served a very generous cold cut. I don't like sylami, so I asked if I could replace it with prosciutto (after checking the price), and they said yes. What if you can't drink wine? why not? Brie cheese and prosciutto are enough to make you happy. Gracie.

Great Italian restaurant with nice and friendly staff. The food was good and freshly made in an open outside kitchen. I think it was quite expensive for Thai standards, for us it was 1600THB including tip. We both had a starter, a main course and shared a small bottle of house wine(wich tasted just fine). I think quite expensive for in Thailand, but compared to Western prices still very good. After all would definitely recommend!

We had visited this restaurant a few years ago on a previous visit to Chiang Mai, and were very happy to see that it was still there and just as good as ever. We hadn’t booked but got a table inside as it was relatively early. By the time we left the indoor dining room was full and a few people were eating at tables in the lovely garden area. But in July that would be rather hot. Although you do get to see them preparing pizzas in the outdoor pizza oven. We shared the cold cuts platter and then 2 pasta dishes. Everything was delicious and freshly prepared. Great value authentic Italian food. Do try it if you are in the Nimmanhaemin area.

A charming Italian restaurant with great atmosphere serving authentic and very tasty Italian food. Wine selection a bit chaotic as no wine list (yet), available. Prices reasonable and consistent with a restaurant of this quality.

Great atmosphere, great food. We’ve been here four times now and not had a bad meal yet. Had the lamb cutlets last night, very tasty ( prefer mint sauce with lamb myself) cooked pink just as they should be. Extensive menu and a good wine list, reasonably priced 1000 bht for a nice Primitivo. Open plan kitchen which is always a good sign of a clean kitchen. The staff are quick friendly & attentive. Definitely will be coming back soon.

Aside from the jets flying directly overhead, this place has a brilliant atmosphere. Most of their dishes are good, but the pizza is a disaster. If you want Italian dishes other than pizza, this place is good. For pizza, avoid. The best pizza place in Chiang Mai begins with an A, and it's no contest.

This was a restaurant that used to be very good under it's previous owner but has gone massively downhill. Last night was my birthday and decided to give it one more chance. Oh how I wish we hadn't On the plus side we shared Beef Carpaccio which was ok. On the negative side we also shared the eggplant starter which was frankly tasteless and inedible. Presented in a soggy lump, looked a bit like a small lasagna but was utterly without taste or texture. But the "piece de resistance" was my main course, Fusili ( undercooked) with tomatoes ( overcooked and soggy) and Italian sausage ( invisible!) There was literally no sausage at all in my sausage dish, just a drizzle of some unidentifiable meat sauce in the bottom of the plate. Essentially I was eating undercooked pasta and overcooked tomatoes, and that was it. I m not a complainer. My partners seafood pasta was "ok" no better but should have been it was 499 baht. Never again. And that's after going there on and off for about 12 years Sadly it's a pale shadow of what it used to be and I left feeling like my pocket had been picked and I was still hungry. Read less

This is by far the best Italian restaurant I’ve visited in all of Thailand, and can actually compete with Italian restaurants in Europe. The dishes they serve are all cooked to order in an open kitchen. We had bruschetta and truffle arancini as starters, followed by penne alla vodka and pizza di parma as mains. Finished with the trio of tasters (tiramisu, pannacotta and chocolate mousse) and pistachio gelato. We are enthusiastic Italy lovers, visited there many times, and this restaurant in Chiang Mai, Thailand, is up there with the best of them. Compliments to the chefs!!! Read less

The lasagna was good but the 2nd dish - spinach and truffle raviali was horrible. Lacked flavour. And when i asked for replacement with another dish - they refused. And they even charged me for it even though I didn't eat it. Poor service and customer care.

My wife and I were essentially shocked at the complete lack of any effort at this restaurant to serve delicious, or at least somewhat tasty, meals this evening. We had eaten there years before and usually got pizza with a group of friends. The pizza wasn't bad but we would have to say, that we've had much better, for example, at Dukes or even Yummy Pizza, in South Chiang Mai. HOWEVER, THE MEALS WE HAD THIS EVENING WERE TOTALLY BLAND NEARLY COMPLETELY TASTELESS AND UNATTRACTIVELY SERVED. My wife got what they called the creamy selection of carbonara, they had another type, I got the eggplant parmesan and we shared a Caesar salad. The carbonara had almost no cream or anything creamy at all in it and about two or three little tiny pieces of what looked and tasted a little bit like bacon. The seasoning and it was almost completely lacking and the pasta itself was barely edible because it was so tasteless. The eggplant parmesan I had was even worse! It was a small portion, served in the middle of a plate and was nearly devoid of sauce of any kind. There was also very little cheese either on top or inside of the pasta layers. But the worst thing of all what's at the eggplant was nearly tasted it was almost like eating barely cooked eggplant, and it was not crispy fried and layered with cheese and sauce like every other eggplant parmesan I've ever had at any restaurant, anywhere. I often get eggplant parmesan because I really usually like it! But certainly not this time. I asked for a tomato sauce that I could put on it and I added at least a half a cup to it and poked it into the meal and try to eat it but only managed to eat half of it. The rest I brought home from my dog, and by the way, both meals were served COLD and had to be returned to be heated. WE ALSO BOTH USUALLY LOVE CARBONARA BUT THIS WAS BARELY EDIBLE , AGAIN WITH NO CREAMY CHEESE SAUCE. IT WAS MORE LIKE JUST EATING PASTA THAT HAVE BEEN HEATED UP AND NOTHING ELSE DONE TO IT. The Caesar salad we had was the worst ever also so little taste so little cheese and it was extremely hard to find any bacon at all and it wasn't fresh bacon, it was some type of little weird bacon chips that they threw in it, and I only found about six of these little tasteless "bacon" chips. It was a decent size salad but it consisted of lettuce these little chips and about four tiny thin strips of parmesan cheese on top. NEEDLESS TO SAY, WE AND ANYBODY WE KNOW, WILL NEVER BE BACK TO "WHY NOT" located between 11th and 13th Street, Just off of Neimanhaman. So very very disappointed in this meal and this restaurant. We had been so looking forward to going out and getting a good Italian meal, But when I got home I opened the refrigerator and ate a piece of chicken to feel a little better. MY DOG HOWEVER DID EAT THE REST OF IT, FAIRLY QUICKLY! Read less

The only place I have to come everytime visiting ChiangMai. The perfect combination of cold cut menu and pizza is always the best match for me. Lots of selection in menu to try on. Worth every pennies spent.

We ordered a Pizza and Spare Ribs . The Pizza was good, but the Spare Ribs were inedible. Very hard , salty and with almost no meat on it, I got angry how they even dare to serve such a dish. Absolutely ridiculous. Will never go there again.

The experience was pathetic and the food was not good too, we were accompanying another couple on our table and they are vegetarian and when they sprinkle the origaano over pizza, suddenly we something was crawling over it, what surprised me there was no attempt to make any service recovery. We send the slice or two back bcoz insects were crawling over them….just a sorry from waitress and no replacement or any kind of gesture of goodwill. Owner of the restaurant was standing there and talking to one guest with close body language. One server walked over entire restaurant asking everyone that how was your food or experience but he didn’t come to our table. For sure never going back and not recommend at all. Read less
Lovely Panini and loved the sauted potatoes. Great delivery service as always by Tuk. Looking forward to things getting back to normal so we can dine in with a glass of vino!!
For sure the best Italian Restaurant in Chiang Mai. Huge Wine selction. Very friendly staff and a Pizza which makes Italian people come back again. Great cold cuts with fine cheeses.

Went there for dinner with friends last week. The food was under average and over priced for such low quality. The service is bad, the so called manager was unbelievable rude when we complaint about one of dishes we ordered. The place is old and dirty. Won't go there again!

Fancied a pizza this evening, so obviously my first choice is always 'Why Not', they had a 'Combo' thingy where you can either get a free salad or dessert for 280Baht. The pizza goes without saying was delicious as was the dessert, Since discovering this 'new' delivery service called TUK, I have been ordering a lot more take-aways than I usually do. They are really quick on delivery, friendly, polite drivers who speak English. They offer Free Delivery depending on your area, I paid a 2 Baht delivery charge as I was a bit way out!! They also charge a lot less commission to the restaurants so I am all for that. Great Pizza--Great delivery service. ( Will not be using Grab again for various reasons) Really, really happy with TUK's delivery service. Read less

Intended to go out to dinner with a friend visiting from BKK, but became lazy, so decided to fetch some food & wine from Why Not instead & eat at home. Emidio has a wonderful Delicatessen with such varied, high quality meats & cheeses etc, it was quite a treat to decide on what to choose. Firstly, the wine—2 bottles of the most wonderful Pinot Grigio (Bottega) laden with subtle honyed flavours plus 2 of the Cabernet Savignon. To accompany those wines, we chose artichokes(fabulous), sun-dried tomatoes, mixed olives, spicy pork salami, cooked ham & the most wonderful & ( my new fav) Provolene Spicy cheese, of which the texture I adored( being an English Cheddar fan)—really, really enjoyed that! Got some Pasta E.Fagioli soup laden with beans, pasta & bacon(yum) followed by the most perfect Ricotta cheesecake. A perfect ‘light’ supper. Looking forward to trying out all the other goodies from the ‘Why Not Deli’. Delivery is offered (details on the web-page). Read less

As always, another fabulous evening at this wonderful eaterie! Popped in for an early dinner with friends, fancied red wine and Pizza- The Divialo was great, friends created their own which they loved. Later on as the place started filling up, bumped into loads of old friends & had a great evening. A wonderful atmosphere as usual! Looking forward to Xmas there!

A 'must try' in Chiang Mai. Probably the best meal for the price I've had in a long time. Nice atmosphere. Large place spreading out between soi 11 & soi 13 (two entrances from either soi), but it's split up so doesn't feel that large. Outdoor & Open Air dining on soi 11 side. Indoor dining on soi 13 side. Large menu (pizza, pasta, salads, soups, fish , beef, chicken, pork, desserts, etc.). Decent wine list too. We had salad & salmon, and I wouldn't hesitate to have the same again. I also like that the portions are good size but not crazy big like they serve in the "make you fat USA". Great service too from the polite & hard working young ladies. Like everywhere in the Nimman area, parking is not a strong point. They have 4 or 5 parking spaces on soi 13. Street parking on soi 11. Read less
Excellent selction of pasta, pizzain fact all the Italian favourites. Great tastes from the anti pasto to the mains.

Why not? There is no reason, you definitely should -- anyone passing through Chiang Mai with a craving for Italian food should definitely consider Why Not? for some solid Italian food. Most restaurants in Thailand have a very limited wine selection, at least in part, due to unique Thai customs regulations. Even if they do have wine, it tends to be over-priced by international standards. There is little restaurants can do about the costs from high customs fees, but this restaurant has a very broad selection, certainly much broader than most restaurants in Chiang Mai. Only some wines are served by the glass, but just bring some friends to share a bottle from their collection. Why Not? has both an open air seating area in front and then an air conditioned dining area further in back. One big advantage is Why Not? has a wood-fired oven for baking pizzas. Pizza cooked any way is generally edible, but there is something very special about pizzas baked in wood-fired ovens. The exterior crust is crispy, the inner bread is soft and airy, and they taste much better. Their pizzas are no exception, they are excellent. Why Not? is much more than just a place for pizza, they offer a full selection of Italian dishes. If you're in Chiang Mai, definitely give Why Not? an opportunity. Read less

Very popular Italian restaurant.... and it deserves this reputation! Excellent pasta (the gnocchis are exceptional), salad very fresh... and on top of it, the prices are very raisonnable Definitely a place to go

We have been there several times and it is really delicious. We like specially the pizza Dolcevitta and the carbonara. We love the cold meats as well, so much. It is really busy every time, so don't go there if you are in a hurry. You need to enjoy every bite! You will have an olive oil bottle on the table so you can dip your bread with the cold meats. Well, at least I do that! It is not the cheapest, but you will get good quality, for sure.

I’m not sure why this is not listed at #1 gourmet pizza in Chiang Mai. The ingredients are all super fresh and of great quality. The cold cuts and cheeses fridge is displayed in the restaurant, so you can see it is all great quality. The quattro formaggi was excellent, so was the Mediterranean. They accept credit cards.
True Italian style food at this restaurant, the pumpkin soup was delicious and the vegetarian pizza was superb, will be going back for Valentine’s Day, just loved it
Had a really lovely gnocchi dish here and it was a lovely, quiet restaurant. It's in an adorable neighborhood with alot of cafes, dessert places and restaurants.

From the expansive menu to the solid service and many authentic details, Why Not was a happy discovery tonight. My lasagna and my friend's tagliatelle were both delicious. Our bottle of Chianti, while pricey, elevated the meal to the next level. They even have all the classic Italian desserts ... or maybe chocolate lava cake is not a classic Italian dessert, but it sure tasted good. We will return!

Gluten free pasta available for additional 50 Baht. Was told that all pasta sauces were gluten free & double checked this with the waitress. Had puttanesca which was lovely at the time but very ill the next day. My bf had bolognese which he loved but not good for gluten free people

This is a good option if you need some good quality Italian food in Chiang Mai . There is both air conditioned and outdoor seating . The staff are attentive . The food quality is good and prices reasonable .

I love Italian food and this establishment did not disappoint. Service was excellent ( we even had a long chat to the owner who gave us the history of his beautiful restaurant) we sat outside in the garden which was so serene. You know the food is good when you take a piece of bread and wipe your dish clean. Price wise...very reasonable!

The food was excellent. I had pizza and my wife had pasta and we shared a salad . The portions was good. Service very attentive and the decor surrounding very serene and beautiful. We sat outside in the back garden. The food most important was excellent. My wife said the pasta sauce was one of the best she’s ever had and as for my pizza it definitely rates as the best hands down. The base and toppings was perfect. We were too full to order desert which looked amazing. The owner came over and had a long chat giving us the history of his life and his restaurant . Amazingly nice gentleman. Overall we highly recommend this restaurant as a must while in Chiang Mai. Don’t miss it.

It seems odd but if you’re sitting in the friendly garden courtyard of Why Not you can almost imagine a piazza in regional Italy. A pizza oven, simple wine list and many familiar favourites on the menu. But most important is the friendly service and the effort to offer an authentic table

Our friends held their combination birthday and wedding anniversary celebration at Why Not? We were about twenty people. The hosts paid for all the wine and prosecco, appetizers, salad, and desserts while each couple paid for their entrees on separate checks. I am Italian and am constantly disappointed with Italian restaurants due to over cooked pastas, congealed sauces, flavorless sauces, etc. One high end place we ate at last year, the bolognese sauce was like dogfood. Not to waste it, we ate it anyway. Why Not could have been a disaster trying to bring out all the entrees to serve all of us, but the food was very good. I had the veal marsala because I did not want to chance soggy pasta. It was thin and tender but too sweet for my palate and without a hint of lemon which I use when I prepare it. My husband’s lamb chops were EXCELLENT!!! ALL the antipasti, the salads, and the desserts were EXCELLENT! The quality of the cheeses and deli meats is very high. I really appreciated tasting authentic mortadella. The owner and chef is from Puglia and he should be congratulated for a great staff who made the night special in handling a large dinner party and making sure everyone had great service and a great meal! Sorry, I only give my own homemade Italian food a 5! Read less

When you had a couple of days of thai food, as a european you like to go back a few days to your own food. In that case finding an italian restaurant owned and managed by a true Italian owner is always a good option. We truly enjoyed the many dishes we selected and enjoyed the italian wine also. Correctly priced and open air kitchen visible from the terrace.

Generically good Italian food. It’s definitely a comfort. I give this 4 instead of 5 because of the interaction with the owner (?). As a restaurant which boasts its wine selection, I was looking to order a bottle of wine. I scanned the QR on their menu to see their full wine list (not knowing there is a full wine menu). I found a bottle that I wanted to enjoy. I asked the waitress if they had it. This is where I deduct. She asked if she could take my phone showing the wine. I said yes. She then had to search for who I presume is the owner. Another waitress came and took my order during this- which I am thankful for, so I didn’t wait forever for food. They went into the wine cellar and found a bottle. It was 7 years different from the online wine list. I asked about this. The waitress called the man over who said he couldn’t keep the online list updated. The interaction was very dismissing as if I’ve never ordered a bottle of wine before. He then brought me the actual physical wine list. Which again, didn’t have matching vintages. There is a note at the bottom that says vintages may vary. Understandable. He didn’t ask any questions or offer any suggestions based upon what I asked for. He dropped the menu off and quickly walked away. For me, this was an opportunity to showcase and highlight the wide selection offered here. I ended up getting the same bottle of wine as originally asked for, just 7 years (according to online) and 1 year (according to menu). As someone who enjoys wine, vintage is important. The dismissiveness of the conversation was in poor choice. I gladly enjoyed a newer vintage, and the food was excellent. I am just miffed at the man’s assumptive nature. I would come back for the food (I had the caprese, gnocchi, and tiramisu) and wine selection, as long as I don’t interact with the man again. Read less

I've been here many times with many different people. Most of the people I have gone with enjoy the food. Why Not? isn't a bad place by any means, but they don't really specialize in any one particular item that stands out. It's good food, but for me, nothing special. The atmosphere is great though. If you want a nice place to go with a large group or on a date and enjoy some Italian food, it's a good place to visit.

Such a great pleasure dining, wining & talking 'shop' with Emidio. He looks soooo good, Rabbit looks even better, service is meticulous AND the food orgasmic!! The Halibut is a MUST--you have to try it. Perfection from the Sea (not farmed), presentation was almost better that the fish!! The Wild Boar Ragu with pasta was ...gorgeous, meaty & soooo tasty---Lovely!! So many other great things on the menu that I cannot wait to try BUT' now-can-do' BECAUSE they are now open for lunch (finally) from 11am-11pm except Monday lunch-time. Watch this space!!
This place was kinda hard to find but worth it . Very good pizza, the dough was nice , over night fermentation, good quality toppings.

We came here twice during our 4 night stay in Chiang Mai as our hotel was nearby. I would recommend coming here if you want a nice meal in a good atmosphere. I had pasta both evenings which could have had more flavour but were nice enough. Luckily my partner shared his delicious pizza the evening he had that! Would definitely recommend their pizzas as they all looked good and the one I tried was extremely tasty. The starters we had were great as was the tiramisu and ice cream. Overall, the food experience was pretty good, atmosphere was nice and the staff were attentive. Read less

Why not simply avoid this overpriced Italian in Nimman. Enthusiastic waitstaff could not ease the disappointment of poorly timed and mediocre food. An Entree of bits of cheese and badly sliced prosciutto on a wooden platter did not start things well and obviously we took too long because the main course arrived cold. Whilst the handmade ravioli was tasty, the shrimps were frozen and had a strong fishy smell. To then be presented with a B1300 ($60) no alcohol bill was the final straw. There is much better Italian in Chiang Mai. Worth avoiding.

In spite of the attractive setting and the promising menu, the place failed to deliver satisfying experience. The melanzane alla permigiana was uneatable and the two dishes of pasta were just ok. Perhaps the pizza is great. I did expect more, to be honest.
Average Italian restaurant and overpriced. Good pizza dough but dishes were lacking flavor. ..... ...
This restaurant is right opposite the De Chai Oriental and is always busy. We ate there on our last day and I can see why it’s always busy. The food was absolutely delicious.

Found this place on TripAdvisor. As a family of four, we were not disappointed. The cold cuts were great, whilst the main of pizza and spaghetti were superb. All washed down nicely with a carafe of house Italian red. The service was great, and what I also liked is the fact that the owner took a genuine interest in how we found the food, the personal touch. The only downside is that I found it on my last night in Chiang Mai. We will be back for sure next year. Fantastic, a must visit if you fancy Italian!

Full disclosure... I am obsessed with this place in an unhealthy way. The best Thai food I have ever had in my life was in Orlando, Florida. How does that make sense living in Thailand? Well, my favorite Italian food is in Chiang Mai, Thailand. Not only is Why Not absolutely delicious with the most authentically amazing pizza in the world, but the service is world-class. Where other restaurants feel really stiff and inauthentic, Emidio and his staff are helpful, friendly, and never afraid to share lots of stories about Italy and the origins of his very impressive wine. The best bang for the buck is grabbing up the wine club membership. If you want a good meal without the fluff of the other top-rated places in Chiang Mai, this is it. Enjoy the cheeses, pizzas, and in my opinion, the only good wine served in Thailand. (I'm looking at you restaurants that chill your red wine). Read less
WE ate there 2 times. The service was always great. The pizza was very good- its Thailand and not Chicago- but still very good. I would eat there again.
Really good Italian food, good service and price. Both our dishes were very nice and plentiful and everything we saw go passed look very appetising. We would go back in a heart beat.

This place really ranks above most of the so called Italians in Chiang Mai. Excellent pizzas and pastas and I love their desserts. Kudos to the owner who has been here for a long time, no mean feat in the very competitive market. Special mention to the pepperoni pizza, what a crust!!

We had so much fun here last night. The location is great being in a lively and cute neighborhood. The restaurant is entertaining both outside and inside. We chose the air conditioned inside and were waited on by two adorable waitresses who could not have been sweeter. It struck us as a place where the cool, good looking locals come to hang out and enjoy some pizza and beer. The food was very good--not excellent but still at least 4 out of 5 stars on food. Come here for an inviting and lovely dinner with solidly delicious pizza and pasta. We had a delicious Vino Nobile di Montepulciano for about $40. Read less

Fantastic italian food outside of Italy. Loved everything we got. Definitely recommend the taster cheese and salami as well as the caprese. A must try in Chiang Mai if you’re looking for a break from Thai food!

This is an absolute MUST after a few days of Thai food for those that just once, need something different. The owner from Southern Italy is a great host making conversation with everyone, giving insights on country, food or anything else you might want to bring up. But his food is to die for. We had various starters, pasta and pizzas and not a single item was not cooked to perfection! The kids wanted to come back immediately.

The restaurant has a great location and an extensive food and wine menu. There are both indoor (air conditioned) and outdoor seating areas. It's hard to go wrong with the tasty offerings that they have and the atmosphere is always upbeat and lively. Whether you're going on a date, out with friends or for a nice family dinner, Why Not? is a great place to get Italian.

We went to this restaurant because I'd been to the dentist and couldn't chew as my teeth were sore and I wanted something soft to eat. But why would you normally go to an Italian restaurant when you're in Thailand? I don't know. All the Thai restaurants have been great with lovely food and cheap. Here the food was ok but 3 times the price of the local food. It was only partially full of all tourists. Not a local to be seen. Then to top it off I went to pay by credit card but they wouldn't let me as our bill wasn't over 1000baht. Close wasn't good enough. Hopefully my teeth will feel better tomorrow and I again can go and enjoy the local food.

First - service was impeccable. Very good service. I’d give ten stars on service alone. The salad was very good and very large. The bread with a more chopped up tomato spread was tasty. The pizza unfortunately wasn’t my favorite. I should have stuck to pasta. On a nice evening out the ambiance is very good. I wish I ordered pasta to give superb five stars. I’ll be back to try it soon!

Great food, nice atmosphere, if you want a decent Italian meal in Chiang Mai , this is the best place to go, not cheap but not overpriced as many Italian restaurants in Chiangmai seem to be in the habit of doing.

We checked out Why Not restaurant with my family using Google Maps. It was just a random idea to go there. They were pretty busy but we didn’t have to wait that long. The guy who assisted us (Italian) was really helpful and pleasant. We shared some pizza and some wine. We also checked out their Wine Cellar. It was a cool experience. We did try their yummy gelato.

My wife and I went for a casual dinner - relying solely on TripAdvisor . Why Not was an excellent choice !! Friendly greeting by the owner on arrival ( hadn’t made a reservation) but still managed to sort out a table. Prompt friendly service. Pizza was outstanding - thin base and generous toppings . Going back tonight 😀😀

On our first night wandering around lost in Nimman we stumbled upon Why Not?. We instantly fell in love: great food and, importantly, a reasonably priced carafe of decent red wine. It’s not easy being wine drinkers in Thailand where beer is the go-to alcoholic drink of choice. Many restaurants don’t even serve wine. We eat low-carb so can't speak to the pizza and pasta but there are tons of great low-carb choices among the starters - meats, cheeses, meatballs, sausages. Grilled meat and fish for mains. So Why Not? speaks to us on many levels. Emidio is a gracious host and is always there to greet customers with a smile and a “Buonasera!” There is indoor seating but we love the large covered patio for dining. We’ve been there three times already…we’re practically regulars! Read less
Really good food and very reasonably priced. If we’d known the bill was going to be that cheap we’d have ordered more

Found this restaurant on a walk, came back in the evening. Wonderful pergola (arbor) dining. Emido was a wonderful host, he worked in North Beach, SF before starting his restaurant 8 years ago but he is originally from Bari. I had the pizza, wonderful, my wife the vegetarian lasagna, also wonderful. Both were great but the best was the macchiato afterwards. Rich strong coffee, one of the best coffees we have had and we have traveled up and down Italy for fifty years. The menu is really varied, something for every taste. Do try it.

After reading the reviews we expected great Italian food and were not disappointed. My husband had the bolognese lasagne which he loved and I had spaghetti carbonara which was as good as any I've had and better than many. Friendly service. Decent wine and beer prices....give it a try!

We didn’t realize it was a Italian restaurant at first glance. After we had read the menu, we were sure what we would like to have. The restaurant has both indoor and outdoor seats. We were lucky to have a outdoor table. It was actually a huge garden, very nice. The kitchen is also at outside (Open air kitchen). We had pizza and white wine that evening, it was just delicious!

I am tonight at this place and we came very exited with a lot of expectations but once that I tried the soup and pizza I was disappointed. Nothing special, we tried 4 or 5 things and no one single thing would make me come back. Service was nice.

This place was recommended by a tour guide. Once we found it we quite enjoyed it. The atmosphere is pretty funky. They have some great Italian wine for sale by glass and bottle. Something I wouldn’t have e petted to find in this city. The pizza was made using a wood fired oven.it tasted like the pizza that I have had in Italy. My tablemate had spaghetti Bolognese which was cooked perfectly. I would recommend trying this place.

Pretty good food, eaten here 4/5 times haven’t had a bad meal yet. Pasta portions are smallish , for an Italian restaurant in Thailand its quite good, Italian speaking host his is friendly .Make sure you bring enough cash with you as it’s not cheap.Nice atmosphere and it’s a place you can enjoy with friends chatting for a few hours..Does get busy so maybe an idea to book.

I have just returned from a thoroughly satisfying meal at Why Not and decided to write about it right away. As I come towards the end of my 10 day stay in Chiang Mai, I am left with the very pleasant experience and impression of some very good Italian eateries here, all run by Italian expats. Secret Learning Experience is another one of them. Why Not of course is in a different league with its very inviting semi open courtyard for dining and a huge menu covering some very classic Italian dishes. Eggplant Parmiagane is one of my all time favorite dishes, and if done right, it is at the top of my list for ordering. It was on the menu and I grabbed the appetizer version of it. It was delectable, just perfect. There was the sweetness from the eggplant and the sauce along with the slight crunchiness on the edges from the baking. And since the mozzarella that is used here is of very good quality, it added to the flavors. I then had the Pacheri Primavera, a pasta that is rarely to be seen outside of certain remote corners of Italy. It was here and again, it was perfect. The only downside was the bread basket. First of all, unlike classic Italian restaurants taht would place the bread on the table even without asking, here there was a charge for it. Second, there was no Italian bread of any kind, only French baguette (that too toasted) or pizza bread. Either way, it was disappointing. The glass of red went perfect with the meal. However, the restaurant has a huge problem. Its success has led to shoddy service due to inadequate staff to take care of this large place. It was impossible to get any one's attention for anything. Nonetheless, even if you are not in the neighborhood, it is well worth a trip from the center of Chiang Mai. Read less

We found this place and were very happy to see a decent selection of cheeses on the menu. The servings of each cheese were decent and the selection was well rounded. Our favourite cheese Gorgonzola was there which was a welcomed surprise to find in Thailand! The pizzas are fairly authentic, we got the Margarita and Quattro Formaggi, both where decent size and cooked well. Although a little pricy the wine list was good and featured a good list. If you are looking for a cheaper wine the 1L house wine is a good option. Youo also can't go past an Aperol Spritzs here! Read less

We greatly enjoyed our two visits to this restaurant. Very good Italian food either indoors or in a garden setting. This is a busy and popular restaurant yet staff were attentive and well trained. The wine list offered a very good selection and the food menu is very comprehensive. The owner (we presume) came around the tables to all was OK which was a nice touch. Would definitely revisit if back in Chaing Mai.
Good food but need to catch someone to place order or pay bill... inattentive. I have been dining there on many occasions and this seems to be consistent with servers.

I'm pretty confused why this restaurant has raving reviews. I found the food, the service and the drinks to be mediocre at best. The wine pour was weak, the Caesar dressing was not even close to what Caesar dressing actually is and my pizza was burnt.

Seriously impressed by the extensive wine list and food menu. Many items imported and similar to those you would see in an authentic Italian restaurant in Sydney. The service was very good and we thought the food and specials were very tasty.
Food quality was very good and the service was as well. I had the lasagna and my wife had a pasta dish. Well prepared and served timely. We will give it another try.

I ordered basic margheritta pizza which is pretty everywhere in the world and here’s pizza is one of the best i have ever tried!Not only the food but atmosphere was also amazing. The outdoor seating area is highly recommended if you are traveling around november(weather’s great!). While I was eating good smell was coming from this small but honest open kitchen and it was seafood rissoto that someone ordered.. which i also had to order. Staffs are friendly. They consistently check if my plate or glass is empty. Highly recommend this place!
We had pizza tuna with cappers missing flavor the penne puttanesca are without cappers bad have basil leaves and chili looks like fusion food with chili and basil.

AS usual, dining here is always a pleasure with great service from Emidio, Tai & their team, perfectly cooked food & a wine list which is extensive & where one can actually buy a bottle for under a 1,000 THB which nowadays is a miracle! We shared the special cheese & truffle salami platter which was great value at 300 baht & delicious, followed by the most tender lamb chops from Oz & cooked to perfection as requested, finished off by a lovely, light Pannacotta !! The prices are so reasonable & affordable & worth every penny. The atmosphere is fabulous & fun & un-rushed. A delightful evening as always!!
Excellent food, wine and customer service. My favorite restaurant in Chiang Mai. The food is very good. Specially the pizzas and Mediterranean salad.
They have an "original" wooden fired stone oven. The ingredients are mostly imported from italy. The also have imported beer and wine. My favorite restaurant

Been living in Chiang Mai for two years, and this is one of our fav's if not our absolute fav! Casual atmosphere that perhaps undermines the quality of the food. Perhaps the exterior or even the interior of the restaurant needs to be completely renovated, but the food is the real star here, and its what keeps us coming back. Pizza's and Pizza bread is stunning, the bases literally melt in your mouth despite being crispy and dry and moist and chewy all at the same time! We always get a meat/cheese platter and a pizza and pasta and generally speaking we sit there and think there is no possible way to improve the actual quality of the food. Glasses of wine or a carafe of wine is fairly reasonably priced. Service probably could be classified as awesome, maybe even fractionally too attentive if anything? Just a great restaurant delivering extremely high class Italian food. Food is fresh and vibrant, they use REAL great quality olive oil. Its a real gem. We don't write reviews that often but this place deserves one. Thanks to all the staff and Emidio the owner. We are big fans of this place and recommend it to all who come to CM. Read less

Huge menu of Italian specialties. We all had pizza which was not the thin crispy crust but more thin and chewy - excellent! Bonus was a great selection of craft beers both local as well as some nice imports from USA. Great ambiance with nice outdoor seating area as well as indoor w aircon if you choose. Highly recommend.

Why Not has the best italian food in Chiang Mai. It is one of my go-to restaurants in the city. The pizza and pastas are great. There is always something interesting on the specials menu. And the crown jewel is the ambiance, eating in the outdoor garden is wonderful. I highly recommend this restaurant.
This place offers good quality italian food in a very comfortable environment. They also have an open area and a closed one with A/C, so you can choose depending on your prefferences and the weather.

I have been a customer of this restaurant for several years. It has gone from being my favorite Italian place in CNX, to being just average, quality seems to be up and down. Went last week and would say that it has improved from last year in terms of food quality and service, so it is back on my favorite list. The service was more professional and efficient than in the past, and the food quality was very good. In particular I found the pizza much better than last time, crust thin but crisp as I like it. Happy to see the improvement and recommend it.

This is maybe the best italian restaurant in Thailand. Authentic Wood Fire Oven Pizza and pasta. You can taste the original carbonara, lasagna and other dishes. Very good salads and best tiramisù in town. Prices are very low compared to other italian restaurant in Thailand, but quality is amazing.

I love the pizzas but also their pasta dishes and specials of the day. Their service is so good attending to everyone's desires so perfectly with a smile. The owner, Emidio, is such a good man and so gracious to his customers.
Lovely alfresco authentic Italian restaurant. The pizzas are to die for. Great wine selection. Prompt service and big fans outside to beat the city heat.

Have stayed in Chiang Mai for a while and we’ve been getting really desperate for some Italian food. This place really does the trick. Owned by a really nice Italian guy, it serves excellent authentic Italian food. The staff are extremely helpful and friendly. The food is really good and it has one of the best wine lists in Chiang Mai. The wine is still on the pricier side, but all the wine in Thailand is expensive because of the importing tax, so compared to other places it is reasonable. The choice is really excellent. Definitely worth checking out 👍

I've done about six or seven Italian places in town recently, and this (along with another reviewed recently) is my top pick for great food. this place has a couple things going for it: a wonderfully relaxing setting in a large outdoor dining area under awnings, umbrellas, and with solid flooring, but plants and trees to entice; a huge open-air kitchen with a cook staff of 10 that specialized in sections of your menu; an expansive and yet prolific and authentic menu of soups, pastas, main courses, salads, antipasti, desserts, grappas, wines, beers, and all the stuff in between. The menu here consisted of the top 80-100 dishes that you would find scattered across Italian restaurants in your own city or country, but its all pulled together here, so there's northern and southern foods to choose from. Umbrian, Tuscan, and other wines (and proseco) provide a solid backbone of choices for any palate, and the pours are fair; though be warned: wine prices in Thailand are really first world (well, not quite!). This is a great place for singles or couples; families will feel that they are welcome; and large groups of 8-12 can be readily accommodated. I don't think you can go wrong with the cheese plates, prosciuttos and capreses; but I found the simple house salad of greens to be light, delicate, and I had control over my own oil and basaltic addition (though there are caesars and other salads for those who want something beyond my zen sensibilities). Pastas are across the board one of the best deals here--both in terms of pasta texture and flavor, as well as the sauces, pestos, and gorgonzola creme sauces that go on them. fish and mussels, oysters, and shrimp are solidly in the middle of the menu--either in grill portions or in pastas--with some of this coming from sources in Europe, so that will be a treat for those missing their favorite dishes from France and Italy. Pizzas are generous and constructed with Eurocentric and american palates in mind, so they look more familiar (if less exotic) than some of what you see in the neighborhood. This is an uncrushed, uncrowded affair, with attention to your needs and also your privacy, and for those of us struggling with Thai, the English competencies of the three folks who served me were very good. My hat is off to the proprietor for giving us such a great restaurant and keeping his hand and vision on the wonderful people working the kitchen. Read less

My husband and I lived in the Nimman area of Chiang Mai for 6 months. We frequented Why Not weekly for the great food, great wine at good prices and great service. We had awesome conversations with the owner, Emilio, who was always happy to talk about food and wine and share his extensive wine knowledge and cellar with us. We miss you, Emilio!

So I looked on internet for a long time searching for a decent pizza place and the 15 minute tuktuk ride from our hotel was more than worth it! Honestly the pizza was probably number 3 in my all time list (behind Aqua Pazza in Venice and the Monte Carlo Beach Club in Monaco). The pizza was just superb! The restaurant was not a high end location but what does that matter when the pizza is world class? To top it all, if you make the effort to go inside and ask to see the wine cellar then , if you are a wine lover, it's an Ali Baba experience with superb wines from around the world at prices up to 20.000 baht for the very best. I chose a Marques de Riscal 2011 reasonably priced at 2800 baht. This place will now be a must-visit every time I go to Chiang Mai. Read less

We have been to this restaurant three times, ordered pizzas that were excellent, very tasty portions of pasta, excellent wine, and great service. No complaints. Everything was so good that I wanted to eat there every day

We stumbled across the restaurant while exploring the streets in Nimman We should of kept walking but after a day in a wonderful city exploring the sites dinner was sounding good . Did we make a mistake ! The restaurant looked very nice from the street , we were shown to a nice table and ordered our dinner , from here on it went downhill very quickly. The staff seem to be young people with no idea how to take an order . My wife ordered a soup to start by the time they brought the spoon the soup was Luke warm and not very good , tasted like it was from a can . We both ordered Pizzas , their is a so called wood fired oven , the pizzas are terrible! I know what l ordered but have no idea what was on the pizza ? I left my meal as it was so bad , l decided to have something back at the hotel . I told the waiter when he asked why l did not eat the food ,l explained to him it was not edible and l just wanted the bill and to leave . I received the bill and then the owner now decided to come to our table . The owner then went on to give me a lecture about his food , l have no interest in a rude man lecturing me on his cooking ! l had already paid the bill ,he should have come over when l told the waiter the food was no good ,not after l paid the bill ! The owner was just plain rude ,he would be better of learning how to cook than lecture his customers . This is one place that if you come across just keep walking . Read less

We ate dinner under canopy outside during a heavy thunderstorm. The staff were incredibly accommodating in helping everyone enjoy the meal during the downpour above. Furthermore, the lasagna was most certainly the best I've had so far in Chiang Mai!

We are visiting from Australia and fancied an Italian feast and we were not disappointed. The service here is fantastic, only surpassed by the food. My husband and I shared bruschetta and a plate of mixed olives for entree with a lovely glass of Italian Prosecco, yum! For mains my husband had fusilli pasta with spicy Italian sausage in tomato sauce, also most enjoyable. I think I had the best dish though! Mine was a ‘specials of the day’ dish with grilled swordfish, buttered potatoes and sautéed spinach and garlic. Wow! I have never had a better piece of swordfish cooked so perfectly. I am not particularly fussed on spinach but even that was very nice. We shared a lovely bottle of Australian red wine and finished with a coffee liquor martini style drink for dessert. The owner of the restaurant came and spoke with us before we left and gave us a good tip on where we could go next for a drink and music. This was a wonderful experience for a very reasonable price. We have a couple of weeks left in Chiang Mai and definitely will be returning before we leave! Many thanks Why Not 🤗🤗🤗 Read less

Great italian restaurant in chiang mai. The food is always tasty and the staff are really friendly. The owner is always there and interact with clients to make sure everything is ok. My friend took me to this place and since that day we are there once a month.

Had dinner here last night I’m gluten free and they had 3 types of pasta to chose from With any of the sauces available on the menu.... well of course I couldn’t resist the vongela... bad mistake not enjoyable just cooked dry spaghetti with clams thrown in. 12 hours later terrible stomach cramps and sick. My husband had the pizza and said it was delicious.

Why Not ? because the food and service is disgusting, their are many great restaurants in Chiang Mai ,this is most certainly not one of them . Food is bland ,salty and 3rd rate. If you expect a dinner in an Italian restaurant or looking for a great pizza ,better a dinner from Pizza Hut ,at least you know what you are eating. Pizzas are wet ,soggy basically disgusting. Soups are not even on par with instant soup from a packet. The owner obviously thinks that tourists are stupid ,that service doesn’t matter and food that is inedible is ok to serve . But this is not the case and when a restaurant serves food of this low standard with service to match then it does not deserve to be patronised . Read less

in my opinion, why not offers one of the best pizzas in chiang mai. Really good oven, fresh pizza base and very good ingredients too. Also the owner, stephano if I no remember bad, his a really good guy. we will come back again.
Very extensive menu covering various Italian options. We went for pizza from their traditional ovens and it was delicious.

Due to inclement weather and this restaurant being opposite the entrance to where we were staying, a mere 10 metres away we ended up dining there one evening. From what we could see the majority of the tables were located in the open to the front of the restaurant under canvas roofing. On the night we dined there, a mid-weekday it was full. The staff was attentive and we noted that they tried very hard with patrons that spoke no Thai. We elected to have both starters and main course. The starters were excellently presented and the taste matched the presentation. They had an extensive wine list and we selected a decent bottle of Sicilian wine which should really have been decanted, just a small observation Both our mains were delicious despite which my wife was unable to finish hers, due to the quantity. Over-all it was a delightful surprise as we had only settled on it due to its closeness to where we were staying and there being few other options. We would definitely elect to eat there again and would recommend it to friends. Read less

First time at this restaurant as it is near the hotel. Did not sit outside as it was very hot at this time of the year even in the evening. Friendly and helpful staff. Extensive wine cellar. Food prices are reasonable and come in quite a big portion. The pasta was very, very good. Very much like the Pappadelle all Anatra(homemade pasta with duck sauce). After three appetizers and two pasta dishes, no room to try the dessert. Maybe next time. Highly recommend this place if Italian food is on yiur list.

Why Not? is a great Italian restaurant in the Nimman area of chiang mai. It has both a mix of out door and indoor seating to be enjoyed. I strongly recommend their starters as well as their pasta! Their pizza is also very yummy. Ask for a friendly wine recommendation from the owner too as they have quite the selection! Would recommend and will be back!
I've been on a lasagna quest in Thailand for 3 months, and I can honestly say this is the best lasagna I've tried in Thailand.

We lived in Chiang Mai for 5 months and "Why not" was our most favourite spot to eat pizza. Their melon & prosciutto is also to die for...so is the lovely brie and honey on fresh baguette with a lovely glass of white wine. We loved sitting outside on the elevated terrace by the main restaurant. The Italian owner is very attentive to the service and quality of the food. Even if you are in Chiang Mai for a few days...make it to this unforgettable restaurant. Then walk to One Nimman for some shopping and chocolates (shops close at 22:00).

Great pizza, veal scallopine, superb tiramisu! We live 5 minutes away and have visited here more than any other restaurant during our stay in Chiang Mai. The outdoor setting if you can get a table is lovely, the service is generally excellent, but it's the food (and the house red) that keep us coming back for more. The Italian owner has trained the local staff to perfection. This a must visit. Dinner only (not open for lunch)
I had pasta which was good and some sea bass which was good but very small. Overall, we enoyed our meals and would probably eat there again.

If you want a treat, this is the place. Lovely garden, nice service but most importantly, excellent food. The pizza is generous and delicious, the salad is huge and fresh, wine is smartly priced. For a taste of home (Toronto), this is it. The lovely owner circulates and is happy to converse with you.

As always we had a fab dinner in a lovely setting & wonderful service from Emidio & his team. The lamb chops were superb, succulent & juicy, accompanied by a good Spanish red, the pizza was perfect as was the ricotta cheesecake & gelato. We had a great, relaxed evening as we always do when dining here. Looking forward to our next visit!

The place looks quite nice and is basically all outdoors, including the kitchen. Unfortunately, the people in our group who ordered cream based pasta got extremely sick. My friend had to take some days off work in Chiang Mai, and I am back home in BKK and I'm still recovering.

We came here with another family for dinner. Food was delicious and freah. service was fast and the staff was super friendly. Prices are reasonable a d the collection is wide and rich. Even when our daughters ran around the place they were really good and patient
We had to give ourselves a treat, first Xmas away from home and we didn’t get disappointed at all. So good food and hey even have porto wine. A little bit like home!!

If you need real, quality and tasteful Italian food don't go to Italy. Go to Emidio's place in Chiang Mai Thailand and you will have it. Friendly people, many choices, logical prices and amazing food. Definitely recommend it

Came here on a night our (very white) stomachs needed a break from Thai spices. Admittedly, we're New Yorkers and weren't expecting to find incredible Italian food in Chiang Mai, but we were happily wrong! The food far exceeded expectations, and was completely delicious. I always end up recommending this spot to friends, along with all the other incredible Thai places to eat in Chiang Mai. The atmosphere was great, the staff was wonderful, and the food was so good!

Came here on recommendation from some friends. Aldo it doesn't look much inviting from the outside , their pizza was fantastic. The right size and thickness and enough topping. Had the Pharma ham ,rocket salad pizza which is the best of Chiang Mai for now in my opinion. Will come back for more.
Had a wonderful time there. A lot of choices in meals and if you like whine you are at the right place.

I don't a 5 star evaluation easily. Better than many restaurants in Italia. Please don't change your recipes. The only thing I can ask you is to come to my place in Montreal since I won't go back to Chiang Mai for a long time.
We split the Vegetariano and Diavolo pizza, both were delicious. Ordered them to go, so we enjoyed a glass of wine while we waited (wine is about 180 bhat per glass).

My fiancé and I came here many times and alway the place for welcome our guests from another country. Apparently, we loved the people there and the food were lovely all the time (we loved pizza there a lots😊.) Well, we went to Why Not when they started the business and we found out that the food is getting better more and more and more. Highly recommend this place!!! Read less
Very Nice place with really good food and wine. The owner takes care of the guests in a charming and personal way. Location in Chiang Mai is on the Posh side. A bit pricey.

Very nice environment in nice area. Lots of restaurants everywhere. Nice garden to sit in. Prices are average on the low side for some dishes. Took a long time to get the food but the place was also full. Shrimp Cocktail 140 Baht, if the shrimps were not already dead they would have drowned in all the dressing. Half would have been enough and would have made it a nice shrimp cocktail. Carbonara 160 Baht was to my liking. Lots of parmesan on the side. Very well worth it. Friend had Lasagne and was not very pleased with it. My wife hade Spaghetti Kimao and said it was ordinary. Prices on those average. They advertise on the table for wine but they are all more expensive than the house wine which is only in the menue. 120 Baht instead of 180 Baht. We had a nice dinner but it could have been much better. Read less

This is the review for this restaurant that I posted to another in error - just in case anyone wonders!!! Super, fantastic and suuuuch good value you have to eat here!!! The menu is vast but we only went for the specials - the Puglia Burrata and the amazing fish soup were unbelievable yet they almost managed to surpass that with the salted cod/swordfish linguine for mains! My Mother said to me many years ago, never mind the surroundings, follow the crowd. So, so right here!! Did ask for Zabaglioni but could not have it.........would have deducted one star for any place other than this!! Grazie Mille Ps house red is great value! Read less

This restaurant has got to be the best Italian in Chiang Mai. The food is cooked to order and is always delicious. The desert menue is extremely tempting, if you look you will order! The wine list is extensive, but they do have specials at reasonable prices, plus a good selection of beers. The outdoor garden setting with plenty of umbrellas incase it rains is lovely. The candle lit tables make it romantic. The staff are all very friendly and helpful. The manager, whilst keeping a close watch on proceedings, makes a point of talking to his customers. There is also seating inside. Definitely worth a visit.

My boyfriend and I came here last Friday for dinner. We chose to sit inside which was really nice. The service was great and the food was awesome. We had the bruschetta, house bread with olive oil & balsamic, penne putanessca and spinach ravioli. All of it was amazing. It was definitely one of the more expensive dinners I’ve had in CM probably due to the wine (180 baht/glass) so if you wanna have a few (2 ppl) I think it works out cheaper to buy a bottle. Definitely worth it though. Would definitely be your place if you’re into cheese boards and cold cuts! Probably the most authentic italian place I’ve eaten at in my 1.5years here. Definitely recommend! Read less

Dined here with several friends. If you have a group you might consider reservations but for 2 or 3 - probably not needed. Lots of lovely seating indoors and out. A varied menu of Italian offerings. Wonderful Caesar Salad. All items at our table were great including pizza, chicken, eggplant and lasagna. Well worth the visit.

Don't let the entrance on Soi 13 fool you; the first time I went there I almost didn't go in. Once in go through to the large garden at the back; totally different; has a great atmosphere and somehow you don't feel crowded in. The staff are very good; I have noted one or two that are still working there two years after my first visit so that is always a good sign in my book. I have never been disappointed with the food and the one pizza I did order was really excellent (normally go for pasta). Small but very good choice in Italian beers (something different) and their wine selection is also very good and reasonable priced. Being in the heart of Nimmanhaemin parking is always difficult but Why Not is worth the effort. Read less

This was quite a pleasant surprise to find an authentic Italian restaurant with an Italian chef cooking up delightful food. Went with the family that included 4 young grandchildren. The staff could not have been more helpful and the food just delicious The house red wasn’t bad either Thank you for a lovely meal !! Read less

This is without a doubt, the best Italian food in Chiag Mai. I have had Italian food in Canada, U.S., Italy. Why Not? can rate up there with the Italian food I have had in these countries. The owner/chef is from Italy and knows his stuff. He and his Thai wife are very warm, friendly people. They import their ingredients and the food is very well done. The pizza is delicious. I can sit there and just eat the crust all day. Sign of a good pizza dough. Their Caesar Salad is the closest to the real thing in the whole city. Delicious. They have a pasta dish with a creamy walnut sauce that will blow you away. There are 2 sections of the restaurant. Indoor, and in the back, a large outdoor area. The staff is well trained. Read less

I came here as a solo traveller and ordered several dishes with the aim of taking some home for left overs. I started with the prawn cocktail as an entree which was a bit weird. The prawns and whatever else was in it was drowning in what I can only describe as an avocado mayonaise. The prawns had their tails on so you had to use your hands to eat them, which made it very messy to eat as they were covered in the avocado goop. I then ordered the gorgonzola pasta, which was nice and not too over powering, although it was just pasta and sauce and nothing else. It could have been improved by some brocolli or chicken pieces at an increased price. Although it was still nice, I have made this at home many times and enjoyed it more than I had at this restuarant. I also ordered the prawn and zuccini Pizza which I ended up taking most of it home for left overs. I have ro say this was the nicest pizza I have ever had. Nice thin base and just the right amount of cheese. If I came back here it would definately be for the pizza. As for the restuarant itself, it was all outdoor seating and I went home covered in mosquito bites. I think it would be beneficial to supply small bottles of repellent on the tables like I have seen in other restharants in Chiang Mai. There is also a lack of lighting so when reading the menu I had to use the torch on my phone to be able to see it. It also made it difficult to eat in the dark. Staff were extremelty friendly and attentive, which made it a better experience and the meals are reasonably priced, so no complaints there. I would come back to eat here but next time order differently to see if I just made bad menu choices when I ate here. Read less

I have been here several times, and quality is not what it used to be and the pizza we had today is not up to a good standard. Barely cooked and topping more than light. Ice cream that use to be the best in chiang mai were very sweet.

I've been to Italy. I was married to an Italian. :) I can say that this is by far the best Italian food I have ever had. My kids loved it, too. Even the bread was awesome. I had the Eggplant Parmesan, my son had the Spaghetti Carbonara and my daughter had a Rigatoni with a variety of cheeses, I believe. All were wonderful. It's priced OK too. If you are craving Italian food, don't miss this place. We can't wait to go back for the pizza. Read less

I have never left Why Not unsatisfied. The food is great, the pizza to die for, and the pasta and cheeses...I could go on and on. They also have a lovely table wine for a great price and they have limoncello and the desserts,,,,as I said the list could go on. OH OH OHHH and I almost forgot they have burrata which is to die for. Just go to this place. Will not dissapoint.

Went here looking for a bit of a change but not wanting to spend a fortune on Western food. Wasn't disappointed! Ordered a Spaghetti Carbonara and got a beautiful plate, a rather large portion (more like a western portion than a Thai one) for a very reasonable price. Staff were super friendly, and service was quick. Had a look at their pizza's and they seem a pretty decent size so might come back for one another day!

superb and authentic italian cuisine !! best pizza and pasta via an italian from san francisco !!! comfortable atmosphere and attentive Burmese waiters and the owner in true italian fashion greets you at the door , best pizza outside new york !!

Why Not continues to be the best value Italian cooking in Chiang Mai. Whether pizza or homemade pasta, it's always excellent The owner Emilio is extremely knowledgeable about specialty cheeses and wines from various regions and makes great personal recommendations. They also have an amazing wine cellar with great value wines and hard to find Italian vintages. This is our 4th year coming here and we are always delighted with Why Not. Sit in the garden and enjoy old Chiang Mai dining style.
Lovely outdoor area Smaller inside with air conditioning. Octopussy carpaccio is a discovery (asked for 2 as appetizers) Every dish was great (my kind of comfort food) Good quality/price

We decided to go Italian in Thailand for a change..As you do! This restaurant came up as being within a 5 min walk of our hotel- Kantary Hills- and so we strolled off to try it out. A very good choice it was too. Proper Pizzas with loverly dough and toppings. Great choice of "real" Italian dishes and local and international beers. Looks like a great wine cellar too, but as we were due to get up early in the morning, I couldn't sample. Still had a San Bourca to send me off to the land of dreams. The owner came and chatted to us, and said he hails from Bari. Been here for 5 or so years, and is passionate about Italian food. So when you are tied of Thai, try Why Not? You won't be disappointed. Read less

Why Not is a very nice place for Italian food. Pizzas are splendid, pastas lovely and if you enjoy wine, there's a marvelous selection of wines. The owner and the staff are very friendly. What can I say more? Buon appetito!!

Had a large group go to eat at Why Not? They extended our booking easily when more people showed up. Separate bills no problem. Payments were easy also. Food seemed to be good around the table. My Caesar Salad was a variation on normal Caesars and didn't have a typical creamy sauce, but it was still good. One problem however was it was still repeating on me all night. This is quite unusual. I will go again and try something different though.
The best pizza in the Chiang Mai. Before that I was fan of Rocco pizza. But now the #1 for me is pizza from why not. Thanks.

We enjoyed our meal. Do not get me wrong. The food was delicious. However, even though I pointed to the photo of the dish and said in paasaa Thai "Chan aw an nii, haa sip." This told the waiter "I want this one, number 50." It should have been an orecchiette pasta which resembles small ears. I know the waiter DID order the correct dish because it was as described: creamy with Italian sausage and fresh mushrooms. Very yummy. BUT, when you expect orecchiette pasta and you get a bowl of spaghetti instead, the entire dining experience is compromised. I explained the wrong pasta to a waitress who apologized. I explained it was OK because the dish tasted fine. It was disappointing, but not serious to ask for another plate. My husband had a homemade pasta with a pomodoro Bolognese sauce that was superb and very fresh tasting. We shared a caprese and a bruschetta before the first course. The caprese was excellent and there are bottles of olive oil on the table as well as balsamic vinegar. The bruschetta was not grilled, nowhere was there a hint of brown on the bread, it was warm though. Authentic bruschetta is grilled and should have grill marks. The tomato topping was generous and very fresh. I poured some olive oil over the slices to add more flavor since the bread was not grilled. The tiramisu was perfectly done and delicious.
