My husband enjoyed his wine. If we are in the neighborhood, we will go again. We knew the owner would not be there on Wednesdays. In fact, the friend who told us he eats at "Why Not" two to four times a week, only dines there when the owner is there. I hate to think the chef or person cooking the pasta tonight chose to use spaghetti instead of orecchiette because orecchiette takes longer to cook and it is more expensive as well. So, I was disappointed, but we will give it a try when the owner is there. I am Italian and I certainly look Italian so the person cooking should not have presumed I would not know my pasta! Training people to do what they should do when the owner is not there is important. Had I been a tourist and not a resident, I may have reacted differently, but I live here. Like they say "the mice will play when the cat is away" but it should not happen. Many people reading this may think I am making a mountain of a molehill, but really, this is not street food and a meal for two costing 1400 baht should give you what you order and not substitute without informing the customer first. Read less

This restaurant is the closest you can get to a trip to Italy without getting on a plane. Whether a oenophile, connoisseur or sommelier, the extensive wine collection is almost jaw dropping. Impeccable cuisine, service and atmosphere all contribute to a wonderful evening. Our party waded through the extensive menu lamenting only the fact that so many visits would be required to sample the many delightful offerings. However, we accept the challenge and will return again, and again and again, and.... Top Marks!!!
i am not sure if this is a 2nd outlet. there is one bar with the same name in the old city. it is more of a bar then a resto. nice place to chill out.
Great authentic Italian food! Good flavours and good looking food! Nice garden. Free wifi. We really enjoyed it!

Just needed a bit of comfort food tonight and we searched for Beer Republic only to find it was closed due to construction. Wandered the neighbourhood and happened upon this quaint patio pizza joint. Kept it simple with a couple of Singhas and a bufalina pizza. Very nice crust. Indulged in some delicious gelato to end the evening. All in all a good pizza place! But now we are ready to get back to some curries and pad Thais!!
Great place to come when your craving carbs/pizza. Lots of great starters and a wide choice of delicious pizza. Nice chilled fairly priced red wine by the carafe too.
Friendly staff and owner. You can sit inside with air con or enjoy the outside seating. We have tried the steaks, pizza, and lasagna. Recommended for sure!

What a perfect setting, outside, ambience and lighting was perfect. Waiters very friendly, the food was simply devine. One of the best pizzas my partner and I ever had, and the value for money was simply fantastic. If you're in Chiang Mai, this is a must!

Beautiful setting and great food pretty much sums up Why Not. Great choice of menu and when we finally made our decision we weren't disappointed as the food was excellent. Service was very good and friendly and the place is very comfortable and relaxing to chill and enjoy a great dinner.
Found a great pizza at this please! also the owner is very good guy! had a nice dinner and nice terrace too! we will come back for sure!

I had been here before and my first visit I thought the portion was a bit small, but I am a New Yorker and used to massive portions. On my second visit I found that the food was very good and the portions quite adequate. I ate in the air con part of the restaurant but they have a very nice outdoor area which is well lit and s nice place to bring a date.

Love the Italian here. Their pizzas are good, not the best in town, crust could be crispier and not as soggy, but still a really nice pizza. Their homemade duck ragu pasta is DELICIOUS! I've come back twice now for this pasta. I want to try more pasta next time because this was so good. Also their gelato is the real deal. Most other gelato places serve a version of ice cream. Why Not?'s gelato is that perfect grainy texture in your mouth and the rich rich flavor of whatever you get. I crave their gelato and will definitely be back for more! You can enter in from either Soi 11 or Soi 13. They have a big outdoor sitting area as well as a small space inside. They take credit cards but only 1,000 thb or more! Which is crazy to me. Owner is from Italy and you can frequently see him in there conversing with guests. Read less
Lovely restaurant, attentive staff and a very friendly owner. Beautiful pizzas made using authentic ingredients and a great wine list. Don't forget the tiramisu-delicious! We had a great evening.

Great pizza and pasta with quality ingredients - a nice find! The pizzas have a super thin crust and are not overloaded with cheese / sauce. The home made pasta - tried the one with the ragu and the gnocchi - were simple yet delicious!

Me and my friends have been going to Why Not regularly for the past 8 months. We have noticed that the owner of the restaurant never came over to our table to ask us how our food was, even though every time we went he seemed to go around all the other tables. We can only assume it was because we are young (early 20's) and therefore less likely to splash out. Despite this, we still enjoyed the food even though it is a bit expensive. However, today we had such a disappointing experience with the owner. After getting our bill we noticed that we had been charged an extra 7% tax service, and since this has never happened before we polity asked the waitress to explain why. The owner came over to our table and before we had the chance to say anything he rudely began shouting at us that he had the right to charge tax and lots of restaurants in Chiang mai did (we have never experienced this in Chiang Mai before). He then stormed off and seemed very angry, even though we had simply asked for an explanation and had not refused to pay it. Unfortunately he has lost some very regular customer due to his unnecessary rudeness. Read less

My friends and I have visited this restaurant at least a dozen times over the past 8 months. The manager has ignored us every single time we have visited, but continues to speak to other customers, maybe because we're just a group of 21 year olds? Well tonight was the first time he has spoken to us. When we received the bill there was a tax charge added to it. Now I wouldn't normally have a problem with this, but we have not been taxed any other time we have visited in the last 8 months so we were curious. When we asked the manager why were are being taxed this time, he responded so rudely. He tried to explain tax to us (as if we were children) and didn't even explain it correctly. He then walked off, ignoring us, before we could get a word in. When he came back I simply explained the situation, that we have been multiple times and it has never happened, and he shouted back saying this was a new thing he has introduced. Surely this doesn't seem right? He then walked off again, ignoring us, so we paid the bill and left. I have never been treated so disrespectfully in my life. We will not be returning to this restaurant during the remainder of our stay in Chiang Mai. We only came because it was close to our accommodation. There are plenty more Italian restaurants with better quality food, and more reasonably priced in Chiang Mai, where we won't get shouted at and treated so rudely. Read less
Went to this restaurant as it was v near to our hotel. Lucky to get a table, very busy even though it was mid week. Thought the food was good value for money and wine prices were ok to.

The name of this Italian restaurant is unusual but the food and wine were wonderfully Italian. With a varied range of menu from the typical cheeses, cured meats, pasta and Italian main dishes, there was ample to choose from. The wine list was wide and described the wines and prosseco well. We enjoyed the classical bruschetta with fresh tomatoes delicately sprinkled dressing. The smoked salmon with Gorgonzola cheese was perfect, light and tasty. The lasagna serving was rich and generous serving. Not too sure about the Alaskan crab claw but the New Zealand lamb chops were yummy. The gelato, tiramisu and affogato were perfect. Will be back the next I am in Chiang Mai.

My partner and I needed a break from all of the Thai food we had been eating (super tasty, but also super rich!)... So, we found 'Why Not' and ordered cheese and bread for starters, two pizzas (so many types to choose from) and mini lava cakes with a scoop of your choice of (again many) ice cream favours for dessert. All of this plus two non alcoholic drinks cost us less than NZ/Singapore $25 each. A bit more pricey than your average Thai restaurant, but totally worth the money as the service was friendly and attentive, food arrived promptly and was incredibly tasty and they are open late. Would definitely recommend!! Read less

I was in Chiang Mai for a few days and I just got burned out of eating Thai food. So I found Why Not? on Trip Advisor. I was not disappointed. I had pizza, which was a traditional Italian style pizza and a few glasses of wine. The food, service and menu selections were all very good. I enjoyed this place very much. If you're in Chiang Mai and you want to try something different, I'd recommend Why Not?

We orderes 3 pizza's and they were really nice! Big! Krispy crust and enough toppings. The service is really fast and friendly. The ambience is romantic and the lights and music top off the dining experience :) I would recommend this place. My pizza was around 8-9 euros which was totally worth it.

A big group of us went here for dinner & everyone agreed that the food was really good. I like to sample lots of different things so had vegetarian tapas/antipasto & they were all very tasty. Others had full meals (pasta, pizza, fish, steak) & really enjoyed.
After eating Thai for over a week I decided it was time to have some pizza. Found this place on-line and tried it out. I'm glad I did. It was amazing. Pizza was awesome. Atmosphere was very relaxing.

Good food, good service, in a very nice atmosphere. They have outdoor and indoor dining and this time we sat outside and it was very enjoyable, with an abundance of trees, lost of colored lighting and the tables are actually far enough apart, that you can carry on a nice conversation and not hear from the table next to you.
We were passing by, looking for a nice place to eat some pizza and this was a great surprise! Great pizza, great place, we absolutely loved it!
One of the best italian restaurants in Chiang Mai. The steaks are perfect, the house wine pretty good too. Fast service and a nice garden.
Visited tonight with a friend, lovely atmosphere and brilliant food. I had bruschetta to start and classic lasagne which were absolutely gorgeous! Would definitely recommend and return.

Located in a major tourist area, this restaurant has a pleasant ambience. Staff are very attentive and the service is fine. The food is quite good though not great. Well run. The wine cellar is extensive and prices are reasonable for Thailand. Overall. dining is a pleasant experience and it is well worth a visit.
Pretty fast service and the food is good too, had a pizza to get a night off from the street food and it was lovely.

Absolutly a must in Chiang Mai . Italian like in Italy, Menu card : big selection ! Wine and Food.Pizza is very gooood. Anything we tray was very good. Including dessert and espresso. In and outdoor , specially on outdoor season , must enjoy the place !

Highly attentive staff and owner, a great selection of quality food, a nice atmosphere at evening in the garden, and last but not least: the taste of what we could try from the menue until now was very good. Of course, the budget traveller should suppress his longing for a perfect pizza and better chose from the great local food offers, because the prices are appropriate to the imported constituants of the offered Italian Food. But if You have the chance to grant yourself with a good restaurant, than try "Why not?"

At first we were pretty excited about this place, cool atmosphere, the wine was good and the salad that we started with was pretty good. However the main courses were all pretty bland and uninspired. It's a pretty bad sign when everyone at the table is vigorously adding salt and pepper to everything. The salt shakers are also pretty frustrating, there is a 1mm hole on the top that clogs instantly. Pretty frustrating when the food is bland. We really wanted to like it but at the end we all agreed that it was pretty mediocre.
Always busy with ex-pats and western tourists, I've been here a few times and it's not bad at all. Friendly staff and ok pizza and pasta. Wine list is ok too

I've been eating regularly at Why Not? for the past five years, and I have never been disappointed. I believe the Caesar Salad is the best in Chiang Mai, and the thin crust pizzas are totally delicious. Everything I've tried, from Baked Spinach with Cheese to Salmon Lasagna, has been perfect. Tasty food served with a smile is definitely a winning combination, and owner Emedio is almost always on hand to chat with customers. This hands-on approach makes Why Not? stand out above the crowd, and the very pleasant garden setting is a bonus. Highly recommended!

Apparently the Italian owner arriving in Chiang Mai was asked about the idea of an Italian restaurant and his response was “Why Not”, hence the name. A gang of us booked a table for their Christmas buffet lunch where we were able to select whatever we wanted from their extensive special menu. The house wine is available in liters and is a very drinkable Merlot and we drank quite a lot. We managed about 20 servings in all and everyone was delicious over our 3 hour stay. Some place have it and this restaurant has it in spades delightfully situated in a Soi with garden seating or inside just a great place to visit and dine. Very good value and superb wait staff.

We had an amazing meal at Why Not? When we saw the menu we were in awe of how many options there were. We had the rice balls and bruschetta for appetizers and both were so delicious. After, I had a Parma ham pizza and my friend had a basil pesto pasta. For dessert, she had the profiteroles and I had gelato. Everything was absolutely amazing. We could not believe that we found some of the best Italian that we've had in a long time in Chiang Mai, Thailand. The owner (the man we are assuming is the owner) was also very friendly going around to each table to talk and ask about the food.
Perfect pasta and pizza, great choice of wine and good central location. Can definitely receommend and will be back.
Good value very tasty! I've had pasta, pizza, salad and antipasto here... all good. Staff are also very friendly, attentive and professional!! Will come here again.

We dined there tonight and enjoyed their seafood soup, Spaghetti Mussels and Sardine Alla Griglia topped off with a glass of house wine. We opted for the air conditioned area as there was also a lovely garden setting outside also. The retardant was very popular, the menu covered a wide range of Italian and Western food, they also had a great wine cellar that stocked wines from around the globe. A very comfortable and enjoyable venue and would strongly recommend.

7 of us went there last night. It's a nice large restaurant with an outdoor dining area, as well as indoor. All of the food was good, if not great. The Caesar salad was very good, as was the Mediteranean salad. The salads are very large, and even the small one can be shared. The pizza and meatballs were said to be good. The lemon chicken scallopine was ok, but didn't have much flavor. The lasagna was also ok, but again not a lot of flavor. The shake with real icecream, was not what we are used to in the west, as it was icecream blended with crushed ice, and was not enjoyable. So depending on what you order, might depend on how much you enjoy this restaurant or if you would go back. We would probably go back, and some of us try a different dish. Read less

I've eaten here a few times now and the food is delicious! I've ordered the lasagna classic, gnocchi, and pizza and all exceeded my expectations! The owner is from Italy and definitely knows how to reflect that in the dishes offered. This is an outdoor restaurant but there are tent canopies covering the tables so you can eat here in rain or shine. Their cab wine is very smooth tasting. Highly recommend this place!
time ago i was at this place, good Italian food, specially the pizza, good price and the Italian owner who i dont remember his name is very friendly guy and i like his restaurant!

We have eaten here many times in the past few years and it has never disappointed us. The food is solid, the atmosphere great and the service excellent. The staff is able to deal with gluten free which I appreciate. The salads served family style are worth ordering and the gelato is a good final touch.
Good location, nicely decorated and away from the street food traffic. Service was a bit slow, food great, very good pasta.

We were recommended to go to Why Not? by some tourists we met in our travels. We went here to celebrate our anniversary and it was incredible. The atmosphere is great and the food is even better! We had the caprese salad to start and I had the Gnocchi and my husband had a chicken dish. Both dishes were perfectly seasoned and just overall the best italian food we have ever had. I wish we lived closer so we could order from here more often. I did have the gelato and it was good, but not great. I highly recommend the mozzarella and gnocchi they were out of this world! Definitely recommending to all our friends and honeymooners!
Excellent pizza, antipasti and wine. Might have been the best pizza I've had outside of Italy. Highly recommend.

Good food, excellent wine collection and a very friendly owner. Nice atmosphere with both outdoor and a/c indoor seatings. Not truely gourmet but good enough to be in the top level Italians in Chianh Mai.

Why Not is the must go restaurant for us in Chiangmai. Revisit the restaurant after almost one year and we are not disappointed. Ordered the house red which is easy to drink and has excellent value for money. Had the roasted vege and the carpaccio as the starter and then the risotto with spiced sausage and mushroom risotto as the main course. The sausage was served in a tailor made wooden board and was delicious . Risotto was al dente with great favour of the mushroom. The staff is very friendly as usual and the bill very reasonable indeed. (cannot understand why some review said the dishes are expensive!). The standard of the restaurant has been very well kept over the past years and will definitely continues to be on my must visit list Read less

We ordered the Mediterranea. The top of the pizza appeared undercooked. We requested another 5 minutes but it came back undercooked such that the cheese had a toasty look. The cheese floated on the pizza. Permanent we came too early at 5 pm and the wood oven was not ready. Good ambience with jazz music. Friendly staff.
What a pleasant surprise this place is. The garden seating in the back is where it's at. The homemade gnocchi is decadent and the prosciutto e melone was perfect.

We were craving for Italian food and went to Why Not? after reading an online blog. It turned up to be great surprise. The pasta and pizza were excellent. Of the grill items, we liked the lamb the most. The Australian Wagyu was so-so while the salmon was below expectation. But the prices were most reasonable and the ambience lovely. Overall, it was a memorable dinner worthy of at least another visit
It's really nice to sit outdoors and have dinner. The setting is tranquil, staff is very attentive, the management is present and engaging guests. Lasagna is amazing and the wine selection is ample.

Another fabulous meal at Why Not! The salmon was lovely, the chicken dish was moist & tender and the Red Mullet was sensational!! The place was packed but Emidio & Rabbit accommodated us as always. Highly recommended for those of you that have not dined there yet!

This is an excellent place for visitors (and residents) of Chiang Mai who wish to stray away from Khao Soi, red curry chickens and green papaya salads. The pizzas are wonderfully thin and crusty and the toppings full of flavour. Tables are set in a lush garden and service is quick and friendly (as is to be expected in Chiang Mai). Altogether a very good choice for an alternative to Thai cuisine. Recommended..

For those of you who wish for a change from the local delights, Why Not is a welcome destination, especially for its wide range of pizza offerings and salads. The pizzas crusts are thin and crispy, their size ample and the toppings generous. Tables are set in a lush garden in a narrow street close to the bustle of Nimman St. Altogether a good choice for Italian food. Service is quick and professional. Prices are reasonable.

We ordered the Mediterranean pizza from here and it came out missing half the ingredients, completely soggy and lacking flavour. One of the worst pizzas we've had, and to top it off it was quite expensive. Our friend ordered a pasta and was less than impressed with it as well. Only good thing was the salads seemed to be quite large and the beer was cold. Won't be eating here ever again.

So delicious and the salads are so big, 2 people can share one. I had the chicken in a red mushroom sauce and everyone else had lasagna. The wine selection is big so enjoy. It is hard to find good wine in Thailand. Very good Italian food.

We visited one evening when we were not that hungry and ordered a pizza between us ....wow, it was probably the best pizza I have ever tasted! I cannot wait to return to try more from this full Italian menu with a specials' board that really whets the appetite! I enjoyed a glass of red wine for 120 bahts, which was very good quality at a reasonable price by Thai standards. The staff could not have been more attentive without being intrusive and the spacious garden seating area has a lovely friendly ambience. Even before it started raining, the staff were there with umbrellas and inviting us to move to a table under a gazebo. I can also recommend an Italian iced coffee at the end of your meal......mmm, enjoy!! Read less

Why not is the best Italian restaurant I dined at in Chiang Mai (and I went back several times!) Their hand made pasta is amazing, melts in your mouth, and the caesar salad is a great accompaniment. Plus they have a wonderful wine list (I worked in the Napa Valley in the wine business, and I'm picky!) So if you're in the mood for a change from Thai food and are an Italian fan, why not check out Why Not? Italian restaurant?

First a simple confession. I am not Italian. But I have eaten good Italian food in a dozen different countries including Italy so while that doesn't make me an expert, I am not a novice in rating Italian food. Why not? Was outstanding on the day we visited it. We had the bruschetta for starters, pizza margherita, and two different pastas - one with spicy sausage and then a plate of tortellini with spinach and ricotta. Taste wise amongst the best Italian we had eaten but more importantly felt like we were eating in an excellent Italian cooks home. Everything felt so fresh and innocent and I don't recall ever describing food that way. To close I tried their special cold coffee with coffee liquors. It felt like cold coffee with baileys but it was so amazingly balanced it just hit the spot. Bravissimo! Read less
My family and I visited here 2 times, the food were absolutely excellent we were enjoy lots. Service is ok and the price is reasonable compare to the location and quality.

I went with my family in this restaurant for consecutive, I'm Italian too so trust me...this is one of the best Italian restaurant that I ever been...and I ate in a lots of Italian restaurant in the world...staff is warm, truly Italian food with truly Italian ingredients...best part is Emilio(owner) absolutely friendly and expert about foods...a part from that he has a huge wines option rigorously Italian...price is quite competitive and quality is excellent....congratulations my dear you made my dinners better

I've visited here two nights in a row as the food on the first night was fabulous. Night one, sat inside with air conditioning. Food very very good. I was with 9 friends and nobody had anything negative to say about any of the dishes we were served. Night two sat in the garden with girlfriend and her cousin. Again the food was to a very high quality. Pizzas are huge, more than enough for two. I had the lasagna, wonderful. Everything is very reasonably priced. Although we had little interaction with the staff, they were all very attentive and I noticed the good nature that the regulars received, I don't doubt this would be given on my next visit. Some of the best food I've had in Chiang Mai and well worth a visit. Read less

I'm definitely not a negative review type person, but the experience we had with this restaurant does deserve to be public. We have ordered from this restaurant several times before, and the only issue we had was that they once forgot to include the rocket leaves on one of the pizzas. We ordered pickup this evening, and when I took the margherita pizza home, we found that there was only the tomato sauce with cheese. We had expected there to be tomato and basil as per the menu description (which says tomato, not tomato sauce.). As this was the second time that a topping was left off, I thought I'd call the owner and let them know. It didn't worry us too much, but as we are business owners ourselves, we like it when customers let us know that there has been a mistake so that we can ensure it doesn't happen again. The owner asked if we could send him a photo, via sms, so we did. We couldn't believe what happened next, but we'll leave you to decide whether or not he was reasonable. He said: "Tomato sauce is there" "Basil is usually 2 leaves on top, but we forget" "What you think the red thing on top?" We didn't reply at all, the only sms we sent was the pizza. We have attached a photo of the messages as proof, and you can also see the state of the pizza. And the 2 leaves of basil over 8 slices of pizza? That's just too generous... Read less
Had great selection of international wines and delicious cheeses and meats. I had the parpadelle ragu which was divine. Highly recommend for those wanting a taste of Italian - Puglia style.

Full disclosure: I'm Asian but luv Italian food... having lived in NYC I've been spoiled by the amazing quality of great Italian foods available, especially pizza. From NYC to New Haven pizza, I've had 'em all. Why Not? is a probably one of the best in Chiangmai. The other being Street Pizza. The place is a nice place for groups to eat family style. A variety of choices and a good selection of vino too. I had an order of the vongele (clams) cooked with wine and garlic in a simple but delicious broth. The clams were kinda small and lacked the brininess of the sea but with a slice of fine, warm, and crusty bread, it was nice. The margherita pizza was good too. The dough was really tasty and baked to a nice char underneath. Hot and crusty, making it nice to fold up to eat. A simple tomato sauce with cheese and fresh basil completed the package. Pepper infused oil and dried oregano made a nice accompaniment. Rounded out the meal with gelato which I would recommend. So if you're missing a little parmigiano and mama's Sunday gravy, try it -- Why Not? Read less

Maybe I caught them on an off day but I just did not feel welcomed at all; no atmosphere or warmth whatsoever. A nice pot plant and better lighting is not hard to do. Just look at the other (vibrant and busy!) places in the area. The owner (I assume it was him) spent the time chatting with his mates and the staff seemed utterly miserable. The food was less than average and I couldn't wait to leave. No wonder it was empty. A great shame. Read less
Had pizza and house wine here, both were delicious and staff were friendly. Loved the relaxed setting of tables in the front garden under gazebos to keep the rain off.

I have been here a few times and as a vegan they have been able to customise some items on the menu to accommodate my diet. They made a special pasta dish which was delicious and the pizzas are also great. You can choose from loads of veggie toppings and it makes for a great place to enjoy a pizza. Nice garden atmosphere. Good, rare imported beers on offer.

Nice quiet place, good jazz music played in the background. Vegetarian pizza was tasty, especially with fresh parmesan. The trio desert dish of chocolate mousse, tiramisu, and a gelatin desert (can't remember the name) was fantastic!

I was born and raised in Brooklyn NY. Living in an Italian neighborhood I would say this restaurant is at or above any home style or 5 star restaurant in NYC. Try their risotto, totally outstanding. I eat here at least once a week. My wife is from Thailand and she even enjoys some of the specialty dishes.

Price is right and a easy going atmosphere. I eat here about once a week. Its my go to for great food a great glass of wine. Never disappointed. They have quite a good wine cellar if you want to go an pick out something special ask for Emidio and he will find something perfect for you!

This is a brilliant restaurant . Authentic Italian food. Great atmosphere and a fabulous host. We had lovely wine , and the food was delicious . My husband loved the grappa and I loved the panna cotta. Don't miss this place. Well worth a stop.
Very good food and service we have eaten here six times and plan to returning again Chief owner very friendly

Their cold cuts antipasti (and cheese!) are trully recommended. Nice relax place for a romantic or even family dinner. Good value for money as well. Do visit Il Patio wine bar just a couple hundred meters away to have great drinks.

We ate here on the recommendation of a regular customer at this restaurant. Their pizza was good and the cheese and toppings are of good quality. The crust was perfectly cooked. We also ordered a seafood pasta dish, but it was rather bland. Go here for the pizza!

Looking for an italianr restaurant in Chiang Mai Why Not will surprise you At first the name sound a bit strange for an Italian Restaurant but I give it a try .., Good food at a very decent price Nice choice of wine even by the glass Cozy atmosphere in the heart of Chiang Mai A nice selection of cheese and cold cut You can even order cheese per 50 Gr .. A really nice surprise so Why Not Read less

I was really happy when I discovered this place right up until the food was served. I ordered the carbonara and it looked like a kids size. Yeah, it tasted really nice but I could have easily eaten another plate or two. So disappointed. Unless you get full easily, I say AVOID this place.

What a delicious find. The staff were so attentive, the prices very reasonable and the pizza was AMAZING. We also had the a couple starters and both were perfect as well. I would highly recommend this place for delicious food. Indoor and outdoor seating available on a nice quiet side street.
All hype, worst bang for your buck in the city. We went there and the service was sup bar and food was cold. There are much better Italian places in CM.

Well, I've cooked for the the last 5 evenings so we decided to try an Italian restaurant recommended to us and touted as the best in Chiang Mai. And, it was quite good. They have both indoor and outdoor dining. The restaurant was very clean and the service was quite good. There was a delay between our soup and our main courses, I think this was due to arriving after a party is 20 or so. We split a bowl of Pasta e Fagioli soup, our entrées were Spaghetti Carbonara, Mushroom Chicken Scaloppini with potatoes and 1/2 liter of house wine. Everything was quite good. I'm not sure it's the Best Italian in CM but when you consider the cost of 865 TB or $25 USD it maybe the best value, my favorite Italian is Ragu and my guess is that this meal would cost 1,300-1,400 TB at Ragu and maybe worth it but this dinner was quite good and priced to sell. So, A nice place and good value if Italian is on your mind. Read less

This is a restaurant I dine at frequently. Ambiance, mostly outside, casual and comfortable. The menu offers an extensive variety of well prepared and presented Italian dishes. I think the best pizza in Chiang Mai and it is gelato heaven. Servers always pleasant and the owner, Emidio, charming and knowledgeable about the food he serves. It is no wonder Why Not has the following it does.
I've only had their gelato, but it is delicious! I enjoy their rum raisin flavor. I have friends who've eaten dinner there and they've enjoyed it
Definitely fulfilled a craving for Italian food, I'm sure we'll be back again. Quiet atmosphere and helpful staff. Gelato bar in the back for dessert!

We just have NEVER had a bad meal here, always been sensational. This time I had the gorgeous Atlantic sardines--so yummy! My friend had the salmon tartare accompanied by a a glass or 2 of Prossecco, we followed that with the most delicious slow-cooked ragu served with penne, after a reasonable break I really wanted my friend to try the pizza and it was sensational--the ever-so creamy mozzarella is just orgasmic. Emidio was enthusiastic as always as we discussed the different dishes. I love this place, it's a real treat dining there.

A regular at this place ever since my daughter tried the Italian style pizza fresh from their wood fired oven. It's not as good as being in Napoli or anything but it's the best in Chiang Mai. They also have fresh seafood, especially mussels from time to time which are incredible. Emidio is a great host and gives excellent recommendations from his extensive wine cellar.

Listen. This place is a gem. I had their Gnocchi with walnuts and my taste buds are still thanking me for it. One of my favorite places to eat. nice for a special occasion or a splurge for the backpackers. It's consistently delicious.

-The total lack of service. Ordered melon and parma ham that was never served. -The insipid food. The ravioli with the walnut sauce were over cooked, the sauce desperately bland. -The pizza was the meal of the poor. I never ate one that reflected so poorly on a restaurant. -The wine tasted like it has been water-diluted. Served chilled, but with a taste that lingered long after the last sip making me think of the water-tempered wines served in carafes. -The simplistic desserts, not even good, but expensive. -The fact I had to ask for a detailed bill instead of a note with a total amount for “food and beverage” -The 3% charge on the top of the bill for having paid with a debit card (which is credited to their account immediately). -The no efforts made to present the food nicely, or to give something of value to the customers. Read less

I went here several times when in Chiang Mai, the food was consistent & reliable without being outstanding. There are seating areas both inside & outside. The menu has everything you would expect from an Italian but limited vegetarian/vegan options.
Excellent food for great price. Really nice atmosphere especially out the back. Would definitely recommend it. Really nice staff too.
very simple...if THIS restaurant is ranked 129th in ChiangMai, i can t wait to go eat in the 128 others. Italy at its finest with Emidio heading the orchestra.

I have been going to this restaurant on occasion for the past 3-4 years. It has been one of my favorite Italian places in Chiangmai, though in the past year I have detected a clear decline. On my last visit I was disappointed with the food, but having been there before figured it was an off night. I took a friend there recently. After being seated, after 10-15 minutes we had to wave our arms to get a servers attention to order. We were going to split a pizza and a pasta dish, and I asked the staff to bring the pizza first, and the pasta after we finish the pizza. A norla request, and reasonable to assume that the pasta will be prepared as the pizza is finished. Well, the brought the pasta dish ( spaghetti and white clam sauce) and the dish was cold. We told the staff to reheat it so it would be edible, but after a few minutes they brought it back still cold, the clams were dry. It is obvious that they cooked the pasta dish at the same time as the pizza and it just sat there till we were ready. I understand this is not a 5 star restaurant with professional waiters, but that is not acceptable. It's too bad, because it's a great location, the atmosphere is very nice, the prices reasonable, but the service and food not up to par. There are many good Italian restaurants in town, so in future I will patronize those. Read less

We decided to go for an Italian restaurant in Chiang Mai as I can't eat spicy food. I ordered a salad and seasoned it with the dressing bottles available on our table. Unfortunately the bottles were not labeled and one of them had a very spicy oil which ruined my salad. When the owner came by our table I've mentioned what happened and suggested that'd be helpful to label the bottles. The owner, not only didn't say sorry or thank me for the suggestion but calling me "picky" starts spilling the content of the bottles on a spare plate telling me that I should have done that to find out what was inside them. Well, I guess that's how people used to do, wasting time and product before labels were invented and for a person that doesn't like labels he was quite fast labeling his customer. Whoever brought those dressings to the table could, at least, warn the customers of the spicy one. The food was ok. My "Saltimbocca (veal)" portion was small. The "small" Mediterranean salad on the other hand was big enough for 3 people. The owner was inconsiderate and I really felt disrespected in front of my family so my recommendation, until he can improve his PR skills is: "Why Not" going somewhere else? We decided to go for an Italian restaurant in Chiang Mai as I can't eat spice food. I ordered a salad and seasoned it with the dressing bottles available on the our table. Unfortunately the bottles were not labeled and one of then had a very spicy oil which ruined my salad. When the owner came by our table I've mentioned what happened and suggested that'd be helpful to label the bottles. The owner, not only didn't say sorry or thank me for the suggestion but calling me "picky" starts trowing the content of the bottles on a spare plate telling me that I should have done that to find out what was inside them. Well, I guess that's how people used to do, wasting time and product before labels were invented. So, the owner doesn't like to label his bottles but he was quite fast labeling his customer. The food was ok. My "Saltimbocca (veal)" portion was a bit small. The "small" salad on the other hand was big enough for 3 people. I really felt disrespected in front of my family so my recommendation, until the owner can improve his PR skills is: "Why Not" going somewhere else. Read less

We chose to celebrate my husband's birthday in this restaurant because it was recommended to us by our son, who went there some years ago. I guess things have changed. We started with a Mediterranean salad that had no seasoning at all, so we helped ourselves with the seasoning bottles that were on the table. None of them had labels. My husband cannot have hot spicy food, and that's why we opted for an Italian right in the middle of Chiang Mai. The bottle that had the olive oil was spiked with hot chilies, but we had no idea, and after putting some on his plate with salad, it got completely ruined. When the owner came by our table to check if we liked our food choices, my husband mentioned that it would be better if they would label the bottles, so nobody else would have the same problem. The owner showed absolutely no PR and told him that he was PICKY, and then we chose not to say anything else. I see that this restaurant has many good reviews but I believe that if a customer shows a reasonable complain, it should NEVER be disregarded. Perhaps the owner should leave the PR for the Thai people he hired as they'd definitely do a better job. Read less

Mostly visited by expats this place serves probably the best Italian food in Chiang Mai. Most of what's in you plate is imported from Italy and it shows in the great taste of the food. Emidio is a wonderful and friendly host. Well worth a visit.
Good food, good wine, good service, good prices. Been hearing about this place for months from friends, Finally went and have been back three times in a month.

...I can't get enough of it! I drown every slice with the chilli oil they provide on the table. Why Not? is a go-to favourite for many of us - foreigners and Thai - who live in Chiang Mai. Great pizza. Informal atmosphere. Fun location - indoor and outdoor seating options. Can't go wrong, really.

The pizza here isn't bad but nothing special, very similar to other Italians in Old Town so if I didn't live close I wouldn't go out of my way to visit. Staff are nice. Nice vibe and atmosphere... Although think that it's absolutely crazy you are charged extra for a take away - even when you come collect it yourself! Disappointing.

After weeks of rice we felt like eating some western food. Coincidentally we bumped into 'why not'. From the backside it doesn't seem like an attractive restaurant but a smoking guest said that he had an amazing meal. We decided to try and found a really nice table in the amazing garden on the front side of the restaurant. We ordered cheese and salami. This was a great starter and for a moment we felt like we were in Italy. The carpaccio, the pizza and the wines we had were al very very good. The owner of this restaurant is Italian and runs this place perfectly to our opinion.
went for a dinner; food was good; italian guy was supervising local staff and communicating with customers; prices are very reasonable; the bill came at 500+Bt

We sat outside and enjoyed a very chilled Italian meal, reasonable house wine and lovely desserts. This is a great location very close to where we are staying, hope to come another evening. Excellent value for money.

Nice looking and serves solid basic food and reasonably priced house wine. All four of us had chicken scallopine, two with marsala, one saltimboca and one mushroom cream, all were tasty, the saltimboca the most. The appetizer of beef carpaccio was good, but small and bread was extra and also a small serving. We would go again.

Sitting in the garden of this restaurant is a haven from the hustle and bustle of Chiang Mai. Candle -lit tables set the mood for the wonderful food and service that you receive. We have been living in Asia for almost 8 years and this is the best Italian food so far. Great appetizer, salad, pizza, tiramisu and coffees. The owner roams the restaurant chatting with customers and making sure that all is well. We definitely will return during our stay.

Our party of 7 went to dinner here and this was fantastic!! Service great.....fast and oh so courteous! Great wine, great food, amazing prices!!! Drinks, appetizers, salads entrees, dessert and after diner coffee for a total of 3800baht? Amazing! We love this place!!! A must do in lovely setting! The coconut gelato was out of this world!!!!
We need a liitle break from the thai food, and looking for some westren food. Lovely place(hidden sitting area in the garden) excellent pizza, fair price Service 👍

Wow!!! Ok, I'm not Italian, but have traveled the world and have come to enjoy staff who attentive and positive, food that meets or exceeds expectations, and ambiance that is thoughtful and pleasant. This restaurant fits the bill. We were two families of 7 total, and ordered 5 courses and two drinks each, all for about $15 each. Incredible! The property is neat and clean, with indoor and outdoor seating, open view pizza oven and chef stations. Desserts are awesome. If you are looking for an Italian restaurant in the northwest corner of Chiang Mai, you should try this property. VERY PLEASED!!

I am more into the original Pizza verace from Naples, but this place offer a very nice thin crust pizza. The rest of the menu is also very good. I love that you can order salumi and cheeses by weight at a very reasonable price. there is a great selection of starters. In my visit with my friend Yvonne we had a great octopus carpaccio, arancini and a platter of cheese and salumi. First course pasta dishes in the menu are more interesting that the usual classics. We had pappardelle with duck ragu, risotto seafood and ravioli ai porcini. For desert we had a trittico, which is a taste of 3 different deserts served in very nice cups. The main restaurant is in a garden with very nice lighting. This is probably 1 of the best Italian in North Thailand. Read less

Thoroughly enjoyed our meal at this restaurant. We both had pizzas which were delicious. House wine was very good. Lovely atmosphere outside with candlelit tables. Staff were all very friendly. Highly recommended.

Our farewell dinner at “ Why Not?” was a pleasant surprise. We started with Antipasto Spinach Timbale that was served hot, just out of the oven; it was delicious. The second antipasto was Piatto Di Olive, a small bowl of olives and we enjoyed it also. My husband and our friend ordered Pizza Margherita, the one was plain, and another with Porcini mushrooms; both were excellent. The crust was just the way we all love. We probably should anticipate that there will be just a few pieces of delicious Porcini, and maybe order an additional topping. (I would love to have a few extra pieces of Porcini on that pizza.) I ordered Rigatoni Gorgonzola, and I really enjoyed my order with the glass of nice house wine and San Benedetto mineral water. At the end, each of us ordered two scoops of gelato – traditional flavors like pistachio, hazelnut, coffee, chocolate chips, coconut and lemon sorbet. I am not an ice-ream/gelato lover, so I probably should order a tiramisu. Maybe next time… The owner of “Why Not?”is from Puglia, Italy, and you could notice the Orecchiette pasta on menu. He stopped at our table, asked if we like the food, and shared with us how he discovered and implemented a unique way of making pizza dough. You see and hear how he is passionate about his restaurant. All was excellent: the food, the service, the ambiance, and location. I highly recommend this place. Read less

They've got wine! Seating in or outside, big area for sitting, candle lit tables, Italian menu ( pizza, pasta , etc.. ). I always order the salmon with small potatoes. Delicate, nicely cooked each time. One of my favorite restaurants for the area, nice to find places that sell wine.
Delicious pasta, good wine and good service! All this in a relaxing garden in the heart of Nimman and at a reasonable price. Great experience here!

What a wonderful meal we had as always! It was orgasmic and I'm not just talking about what was on the menu (as such),it was the pizza--what??!! Pizza is like having a sandwhich to me--it is NOT dinner,it's something I have twice a year,maybe--but Emidio had a serious wood-oven built in and he is so proud of it and rightly so--it was the most perfectly-cooked pizza I have had ,NO grease/oil just crisp,fresh ingredients, we suprised ourselves by enjoying it.How very dare we?? Do try it and prove me wrong.

After some days enjoying Thai food, we decided to change and followed Tripadvisor recommendations. Good idea!! Simple beef carpaccio, stuffed artichokes will open your appetite while the pizza was a real surprise, real Italy in your plate ! I could go on with the great wine presented, the great grappa choice (I'm a grappa lover...), but I fell completely when they brought the Tiramisu: I take Tiramisu every single time there is one on a menu. If they were a World Tiramisu contest, I would beJury n#1 and could also be a contender. So how was it?!! Fantastico !! Best Tiramisu outside Italy I had for years. Just for that, Why not would already deserve a 5 stars !! Read less

Went there for a nice dinner dec. 25! You are in Asia and suddenly you feel like being in Italy that's perfect! Winelist is huge and very good Food perfect Coffee excellent Grappa selection even better Last but no least a perfect host! Thanks to the owner, originally from Puglia, Italy. We will be back next time we visit Chiangmai. Read less

The perfect place for a relaxed dinner, in the heart of Chiang Mais answer to Soho: Nimman. Fabulous food, good wine and the owner makes a point of stopping by each table to chat. On this visit, with a total of two weeks in Chiang Mai, this Place has become our favourite neighborhood restaurant.

I have been here 5 times so far, everyone I have brought with me has been thrilled with the food, prices, environment and service. the house wine is okay for Chiang-Mai. I wish they would not serve it cold though. You can get a really good bottle for around 1000 baht. Which is the best choice if you have a higher budget. Because food that good deserves a great wine! I will continue to come here whenever I want to treat myself. Because it really is a wonderful restaurant!

We just spent Christmas day noon to 3 p.m. enjoying the special Christmas offerings at Why not. Most places put on a buffet where you get lukewarm, sitting in a pan who knows how long food. Why Not offered your choice of 6 appetizers, 4 pasta, 4 main course. All you can eat off that Christmas menu cooked to order. The choices were part of their regular menu but they had stocked up on ingredients. 5 friends went and we swapped tastes of our various food. The great thing was if we found something we liked we could order it. We started with 5 appetizers. 3 of us had mussels in white wine, one had smoked swordfish with fennel salad and another had the pizza bread with proscutto. We then repeated the antipasta with other choices, cold thin sliced veal with tuna paste, Bruschetta with fresh riccotta and spec. Everything was warm, fresh and beautifully prepared. Pasta was fusilli with porcini and parma ham, or paccheri with scallops, shrimp, and fresh mini tomatoes which make a lovely light sauce. Risotto with castelmagno cheese, honey and crushed walnuts. Everything was again warm and delicious. The only quibble was some people felt there was too much honey in the risotto. Main courses were french cod sauted in mediteranean sauce of olives, tomatoes, and white wine. Salmon fillet with italian herbs, braised beef with tomato and and vegetables. Desert was your choice of gelato. We brought our own wine (corkage fee of 150 baht versus 120 per glass). The beef was a bit dry but everything else was very good As a italophile the food was authentic. Service was attentive and the owner stopped by several times to make sure everything was going well. Read less
Highly recommended for reasonably priced and good quality Italian food! Service here is great too!!! The chef owner is a truly humble person. If you are lucky enough "Moules De Bouchot" MUST try!!!
Amazing atmosphere. Great service. The food is absolutely fantastic. The owner is a very nice guy. If you are in the mood for authentic Italian food it's a nust if you are in chaingmai.
Run by an italian guy, this is some real legitimate italian food. Pizza, pasta, salads. All delicious!

We love Why Not! Living in Chiang Mai over the course of 8 months we probably ate there at least 10 times. Great house wine, excellent pastas and pizzas. Only tip for the management involves our final pizza dinner. We ordered an Italian sausage pizza and the meat tasted very fishy. Not sure if it was stored in the same compartment as the fish/anchovies but both of us thought it had a really strong fish taste that took away from the overall pizza flavor.

We had dinner at the Why Not Restaurant and had a great experience. The restaurant has an outdoor kitchen with a brick pizza oven and nice outdoor seating. The wine was good and reasonably priced and the pasta dish we ordered was perfectly cooked. We can't wait to go back and try the pizza.

6 of us had dinner here and the food was really good. We had salads, pizza, pasta and salmon dishes and everyone was happy with their choice. Nice wine list, limoncello and lovely deserts. Looking forward to next time.

Run by an expat Italian, Why Not? offers a relaxing outdoor garden setting and an open kitchen for added entertainment. Clearly popular with locals and expats as well as tourists, this is a great spot. Good service, excellent food and good coffee. Highly recommended.

This is our third visit and definitely won't be the last. Again we met the warm welcome from the owner Elmidio. He intruduced us to his cellar where there was a good collection of wines with reasonable price taking into the crazy wine tax in Thailand. We tried the Burrata which was creamy and full of milk favor. Though not on the menu, Elmidio tailor made an assorted ham and salami for us which was very thoughtful indeed. We also tried the pizza from his new wood fire oven. It was just crispy and delicious. The home made pasta was al dente as usual though as course you have to tell Elmidio to make sure that the chef made it right. We also had the special of the day: the risotto with mushroom. The rice was creamy and full of mushroom favor. We finished off with the durian ice cream which was a house special too. And when the bill came we found the price was too reasonable to be true for 6 of us. I am already planning to go back in the weekend to try their seafood :) Read less

Once again a delicious dinner at "Why Not?". The seafood specials (imported from Italy and France) on the weekend were great again but this time I just settled for Burata (a very special, unique mozzarella, super fresh, right from Southern Italy) and a pizza from the famous wood fired oven (I would say that the pizza here is pretty much like in Italy and miles above the typical frozen pizza dough that even the fancier restaurants in Chiangmai offer). From the "wine by the glass list", I had an excellent Prosecco with the Burata and a delicious Sangiovese blend with the pizza. Perfect! For desert a home-made raisin-rum ice cream and an espresso (a real one, made from beans imported from Venice). A perfect meal! And the ambience is very nice too in the spacious garden setting. Read less
More expensive than the average place, but for the quality of food still very reasonable and delicious :) most dishes are 200 - 400 Baht. Steak much more expensive.

Last evening a dear friend and I visited the " Why Not " Restaurant located at # 14 Nimmanhemin Road Soi 11, Chiang Mai, Thailand. It was a Friday and the restaurant owner Mr. Emidio always lays on excellent seafood especially for the weekend. Last night was no exception. We started with a selection of fresh oysters on the half shell and they were a delight; succulent and sweet with no shell bits that often are found with improper care in the preparation. Next I had a salad with fennel,orange olives and smoked swordfish and it was absolutely astounding. The main course was a saute'd French Sea Bass the was prepared to perfection. I finished off the meal with a lemon sorbet and an espresso. The restaurant is charming whether sitting inside, on the veranda or in the garden. The service was impeccable with special attention from the owner. The restaurant also boasts a brilliant wine cellar. Although last evening I especially enjoyed the sea food there is a full menu that will suit every palate. I have enjoyed many visits to this restaurant and highly recommend it. Read less

I eat here often and I'm especially pleased by the quality of the pizza now that there is a new wood burning oven. There are many other great Italian dishes here as well. There is a great selection of wine here of all price ranges, but I must say that the house wine is also very drinkable compared to most other Chiang Mai western restaurants here that serve undrinkable crap. Thanks Why Not!
That's what we did.....came back tonight after last night experience we had to go back! Lovely and professional staff and the best Italian food experience north of Bangkok we had!
solid service, atmosphere, price. food is good but not great. when i go for italian food in chiang mai, it's hard to say no to why not? because it is usually a very pleasant experience on all fronts.

We returned again for pizza after a disappointing first experience of the new "wood fired oven" (the pizzas were burnt and cold, almost inedible) because maybe the last time we had come to an unlucky day (maybe "the pizza maker" was on vacation?). But also this time both pizzas were irretrievably burnt at the bottom. If it is easy to scrape off the "normal" (but carcinogenic) burns the top of the pizza, is rather impossible to scrape the burns of the lower pizza (which, if burnt, bitter and carcinogenic). In conclusion, we only ate mozzarella. Not the oven makes a good pizza, but only an “expert” pizza maker! Too bad, the pizza of the past (with electric oven) was made with frozen dough and pre-cooked, but at least you could eat! Read less

This was my first time to Why Not and I was pleasantly pleased. We were in a fairly private area and had a few wait staffed that helped us throughout the dinner. The group of 14 were all happy with the meal as I was also. The service was very good and the atmosphere was very nice. My only critique is that in most Italian restaurants I expect some bread on the table without the need for an extra order.

I've been meaning to write a review about Why Not Italian Restaurant and Wine Bar for a while now, as it thoroughly derserves a great wrap! We've been staying (living) in Chaing Mai on and off since July this year and friends who are locals took us here for dinner one night. It was love at first bite back then, and I believe that it's got even better since then. I'm not generally a pizza fan and had pasta on my first visit, but my partner and I decided to share a pizza the next time. WOW! Why Not have perfected the pizza packed full of flavour on a delicious base and without reservation, I can say this is the best pizza I've ever eaten. Yep, BIG CALL!! But true. We've been back many times since and always order pizza, along with some cold cut meat and sometimes a salad which are also right up there as being beautifully fresh and super tasty. We return to Australia in early October and we'll certainly miss our, at least weekly visit to Why Not?!!! Read less

I have been a guest here for many years and given the relaxed atmosphere and reasonable pricing, I was not too worried about their staff, wobbly tables or lack of parking. In the end it was always a place where everybody would feel well and where they had a wide variety of Italian food at reasonable prices. Salads and meat dishes always met my expectations. Recently though, I was served raw pasta twice. In the first occasion I decided to keep quiet. A second time I asked them to fix it. It came back a bit hotter but still the pasta was hard and floury inside. But then the broccoli was mushy. When chatting with the friendly owner who realized I did not eat the platter, he said "yeah, we put it back into a pan and heated it all up again." Besides the impossibility of cooking pasta only with heat, he obviously thought it was ok to sacrifice the veggies for their failed pasta cooking attempts. It is a bit of a shame as I like that place. Read less

I am retired and have lived in ChiangMai part time for several years. In prior years, Why Not was one of my favorite places in town, very good food At a reasonable price, good wine list. Hadn't been there in about 9 months and was Looking forward to visiting again. I found it to be a bit disappointing and not as good as I remembered. I asked a few pretty basic questions about the menu that the staff couldn't answer. The Pizza was okay, but nothing special.I used to enjoy their pizza. A pasta dish was a little overcooked and the sauce pretty bland. The wine list is still good for Chiangmai. Since I used to enjoy the restaurant I will go back, perhaps it was just an off nite or I ordered the wrong dish. It's a nice restaurant, if it isn't too hot it's pleasant to sit outdoors, and the indoor seating is Good. They used to have a weekly seafood night that was good so I will check if that is still done. Read less

Visited this place around 6 times while in Chiang Mai. Excellent pizza and pasta. The wine and coffee is great also. Has a nice relaxing atmosphere and the prices are very decent for the quality of food and drinks that are offered. Most of the ingredients and wine are imported from Italy and are fresh. Would recommend this place to anyone looking for a relaxing evening. Many local expats go here on a regular basis and have been enjoying this place since it opened. Been to a handful of Italian and pizza places in this city and by far this is the best!!!! Go ahead and try for yourself! Why Not? Read less

We visited the restuarant on a Friday evening and opted for the air conditioning part rather than the large garden area. The restuarant offers a large selection of Italian dishes including Pizza. We ordered a pasta dish, lasangne and a chicken dish along with wine and ice cream shakes. They have a large fridge with many flavours of home made ice cream, however when the shakes arrived they turned out to be very watery and icy, with very little flavour. My wifes pasta was undercooked and rather bland, the lasagne delicous and the Chicken in wine was OK. Overall we were disapointed by our expereince, however we will give it another try in case they were having an off day. Read less

OK comes to mind. An OK restaurant with OK food, an OK ambiance. If out looking for a place to eat, why not. If with friends, its not a place to impress. I did however enjoy my desert and found the service to be very good It does have indoor air conditioned and outdoor covered and uncovered seating plus some parking.
Good food and atmosphere. Service was fine. Went there twice in a week . Consistent both times. Interesting beer choice.

I go to this place once, if not twice a week. It offers delicious dishes and the staff is quite nice. The owner is always there and makes sure you know whats going on at all times. Best of all they offer gelato!

The restaurant was just across our hotel. Given the solid recommendations of others on Tripadvisor, we decided to give it a go, and had at least expectations of a decent Italian as to be encountered in many places in Europe. However, it was way better than expected. Really top-notch Italian food: delicious antipasti, great pizza (our 2 boys adored) and top pasta (particularly the rigatoni gorgonzola). Accompanied with the Italian tradition of the owner making a chat with all guests, we had a fantastic dinner. Even including a bottle of wine, we left the place (starters, main course and drinks) for around 60 EUR for 4 persons: not cheap to Thai standards, but definitely worth it! Looking for a great Italian for a change after some fantastic Thai meals: Why Not?! Read less

One can usually find what one craves in this Nimminhaemin eatery. Most of the seating is outside, and most of the year it is pleasant sitting to have dinner with the fans creating a breeze. Food is okay, service is good.

Great place to go for goood italian food. Great appetizers, just love them there. Emilio the host is extremely nice and always takes the time to come over and have a little chat. Friendly staff and good wines too. Also the desserts are very good. P.s. if you take a large pizza there, beware, they are really big!! Really recommended !!
Good food and well priced. Came with a group of 8 people and had a great time. Really recommend this place and especially the salad, which was huge and the pizza

This place offers perhaps one of the best composed menus in Nimman, with staff asking if you'd like to receive the food as ordered, appetizers first, main dish and dessert. I also enjoy them being one of the few places who actually hold serving until everyone's dish is ready, it's just nicer eating together, than having to watch the others get an 8 minute head-start. So, the pizza is good, carbonara was a bit tasteless and about a minute overcooked compared to the al dente I ordered, but still okay, I also had the parma ham which I also ordered a dish of bread/breadsticks/sourcream to go with the ham, and by God, I have never found good sourcream in Asia before, but this was the exception to the rule :) So, summing up here, great service, great food, but I'm dragging them a character down for not having anti-mosquito measures in place, there are ALOT of them seeing it's outside, in a garden, so the candle-reppelants would do an awesome job. My tasteless Carbonara was another letdown which also plays a part though, but all in all it's a great place, so if you find yourself in the mood for good Italian dining some day, then get over there :) Read less

After roaming the numerous sois around Nimman we decided to try something other than Thai for dinner. So happy we tried out Why Not. First, can't believe they have craft beer from Deachutes brewery in Oregon, both Mirror Pond and Chainbreaker. Of all the American craft beers these in my onion are some of the best! Food was also excellent, lots of flavor, medium size portion and reasonable rate for the quality. Service was also top notch, waiters checking in, very attentive and the owner also stopping by to ask about the experience. Yes it may be slightly more expensive than Thai food but well worth it for authentic Italian. Read less
We loved the food here! We never expected such good Italian food outside of Italy. The wind was good and the setting is lovely.
The small Caesar salad is excellent for 95 Baht, (less than US $3). Do not order the large one unless to share with 2 to 4 people. Excellent wine list, reasonably priced.

Was feeling fine all day, and did not eat anything out of the ordinary so am quite certain that we got sick from the caesar salad we ordered. It was not very good, but not terrible, and we were hungry so kept eating. I had the lasagna and my girlfriend had the carbonara. Both were kinda bland. Nothing special at all, but edible. Then, when we got back to the room we relaxed for a bit and around 3-4 hours later thats when we were both throwing up and having bouts of diarrhea. This continued through out the night and the next day we both went to the hospital for some antibiotics. Not a pleasant experience at all....:( . Read less

We found this restaurant by chance and we are so glad that we did! We had the choice of sitting outside in the lovely garden area or inside the air conditioned restaurant. We had Spaghetti Carbonara and a Pizza. Both were amazing and the service was fantastic. We finished our meal with a scoop of ice cream :) The bill was very reasonable. A must visit!

We went to this restaurant on a recommendation from Wendy. WONDERFUL. When I tour I rarely go to the same restaurant twice..... this one we returned to twice! The food is great, the atmosphere charming and the service perfect. Don't miss it. After weeks of great Thai food........it was just warm and comfortable to have fantastic Italian food.

This place seems to be severely underrated. I've been to hundreds of Italian restaurants inside and outside of Italy. It's not perfect but it's damn good. The wine selection is also outstanding. The only problem is its a bit pricey especially by Thailand standards but what do you expect when you import your product from half way around the world?

Perhaps our expectations were set too high - a friend recommended Why Not saying it was excellent and the reviews here were similarly good - but even so our experience at Why Not was disappointing. It started when we crossed the threshold and felt neither warmth nor any sort of welcome. There was some construction work going on outside - a new outdoor patio but we were seated next to the noise and dust and had to get up and move or we would have eaten our dinner in the midst of it all. Still hopeful we ordered, salad, bread and two pasta dishes. The salad (Caesar) bore no resemblance to any we have tried and we have sampled Caesars in many parts of the world. The bread was forgotten and when it did come was forgettable. One of the pasta dishes, salmon lasagna, was dry and boring. The other dish was simply boring although we had been promised it was spicy and flavourful. Overall a serious letdown - but you should judge for yourself - caveat emptor. Read less

My girlfirend heard about this place so she wanted to try it. It is a lovely location, and yes the staff are fine, but the food was hit and miss. Portions were too small, and some of the cold cuts we ordered just looked old and dry. Tast e was not bad, but the appearance kind of ruined the mood. I wish you luck, as the local and service was tops, but move that food faster.

After 3 days of Thai food, on our final day we decided to go for Italian food on Neiman road. There are mostly foreigners in this restaurant and the owner might be from Italy. The homemade pasta is really good, but the pizza we ordered was just so so. too thick and a bit dry. Decent price. Comfortable seating inside with aircon

We just had a delicious fresh pasta dinner on their patio. Lovely romantic ambiance and friendly welcoming staff. The owner is available to share some interesting background on his Italian homeland in Puglia. Our house red that accompanied our meal was exceptional and paired well with our selections of spaghetti with yellowfin tuna and papperdelle duck ragout. We also had a small mixed green salad crisp and fresh. Dai our server spoke some English and was very attentive and helpful. This is a lovely Italian restaurant with excellent food and service all at very reasonable prices. A must visit for some high quality comfort Italian dishes while in Chiang Mai. We are definitely going back to try their pizza. Read less

We went there twice during our one week stay in Chiangmai. Did not have much expectation during our first visit. But we were surprised to find a good selection of cold cuts and cheese. The bread came with two kinds of dips, the tomatoes and basil and the cheese dip which is thoughtful indeed. We had the pappardelle with duck meat sauce. The sauce is full of favour but the pasta was not al dente enough. We expressed it to the lady manager and she apologised and actually we understood their difficulty as Asian usually prefer softer pasta and so they have to make it soft. But she suggested that we could order it to be more al dente the next time. So few days later we came to the restaurant a second time. This time we have a look a their wine list which is quite a good collection in a small Italian restaurant in thailand . Then the owner came and we mentioned about the pasta we had. He told us that the manager has already told him about this and he will make sure that the pasta be cooked to our liking this time. We had the dry beef carparcio and the octopus carpaccio. The octopus one came as a surprise as we had not got the chance of having it before and it is full of favour. For the pasta we had the lobster linguine and the pappardelle again. They are perfectly done this time. The texture was just right. The linguine was spicy with lots of lobster meat inside. the pappardelle sauce was just as good and the texture perfectly al dente. We did not have the dessert as the portion was not small and there was no room for dessert. And the bill was more than reasonable and we will definitely come back again on our next visit to Chiangmai Read less

Second time here and just as good, some of my family live in Chiang Mai so we know it quite well. Food is excellent, Pizza's are very good and the atmosphere it very enjoyable. On the flight path but that ok, is pretty close you can just about wave to the pilot 😃 Anyways worth a visit if only to have a break from Asian food. I always enjoy going here.

We stopped in on 2 separate days. We had a very nice bottle of Pinot Grigio the first time. We also tried some bruschetta, cannelloni, and thin-crust pizza that was excellent. The cheesecake gelato was especially delicious. The owner was very personable and involved in taking care of his customers. We'd go back.

We go here often when we're in the mood for a great Pizza. (Sadly I have been very disappointed with the spaghetti bolognese and the meat Lasagna which tasted old and dry with no meat I could detect! Can get busy. Disappointing when you're craving some good comfort food! Pleasant atmosphere and conveniently situated off Niman, Soi 11.

We search tripadvisor for authentic italian restaurants, not only a pizzeria on the streets owned by a thai. Luckly we met a girl who told us about this place, it didn't show on the top of tripadvisor... But it should! Nice atmosphere, good wine and great food. italian owner Sergio has made it a perfect venue when youre tired of noodles and rice! The is really good, the price is really fair, surroundings are cozy, indoprs and outdoors! So if you love and miss Italy on your way through Chiang Mai, go to Why Not!!! Only downside is the english of little part of the staff, but that is not a problem unless you really want it to be... But then again, its like that in most of Thailand... So just go to here to have a great, authentic dinner and enjoy! Read less

i ordered the Pene Puttanesca only to find that the essential cappers and olives where missing. what i received was a Penne in a bland tomato sauce. i brought this to the attention of my waiter who said that he would "fix" it by having the chef "just add" the missing ingredients. i said forget about it and sent the dish back and left the restaurant. i was halfway down the street at another restaurant establishment when the owner of Why Not verbally accosted me. a heated argument ensued when i tried to explain the mistake and the fact that i am from Los Angeles and have been dining out at L.A's finest for over 40 years and in the rare event that the food is subpar that i am usually comped or offered an apology. i was told by the owner of Why Not that in his opinion that i should "pay for the food and just leave it, even if it was bad" anyone in the restaurant business should welcome feedback concerning the quality of both food and services in order to improve either or both. he could have simply said that i will address this and thank you. very immature. my friends and i will certainly not go back. Read less

Can't fault it. We dined here based on a recommendation from a local Thai/Australian girl. It did not disappoint. In fact we were really surprised at just how good this place is. We literally rolled out of there! The owner is from Puglia and was personally weaving his way through the tables to ensure everyone was happy. Party of 4 with 2 kids under 8. We shared lamb ragu, pizza and squid ink pasta, plus gelato. Every mouthful was a pleasure. We also had a bottle of house white which was excellent value for money. Love, love, love this place and will be recommending it to all our friends that visit Chiang Mai. Mostly outdoor seating so if it's wet I'd recommend calling them first.

My husband and I always seem to find the most amazing Italian restaurants on our travels and Why Not was one of those. It just happened to be across the street from our hotel so we gave it a try. Outstanding food. They have a great selection of salami and cheeses and they happened to have buffalo mozzarella the night we were there, our absolute favorite. Apparently they only have it a few times a month so we were very fortunate. The spinach tortellini in Parmesiano sauce was excellent. The Italian owner was very friendly and made sure to visit with each table. Having lived in Italy for 7 years eating here was a real treat. Felt like I was there again. I highly recommend a visit to this restaurant. Read less

There was some "Love" behind those pizzas! Finding good pizza in Thailand isn't so easy so if you are into crispy crust with quality cheese and toppings, save your self some time and go to Why Not. My partner and I had a small Pizza, Salad, veggie Lasagna along with a liter of house red wine (excellent price on the wine at 650THB). The prices are reasonable and the service was really good (also difficult to find in Thailand these days). Clearly they are well trained. The rest of the menu looked really nice so I will check out some of those items next time. Definitely worth the visit. Read less

This is by far my favourite restaurant in Chiang Mai for Italian food. I don't go there for the pizza, I go there for the other wonderful dishes that Emidio and his lovely lady serve in their very tastefully decorated restaurant. The garden has wonderful seating as well as indoor tables,the service is impeccable,the staff attentive and the wine list extensive & his gelato of course is the real deal. Emidio is so passionate about food I could listen to him all night (and I have)!! Whenever friends come to visit that is the first place I take them to as we can sit there for hours eating,drinking & chatting without being rushed. Sometimes we don't even look at the menu as I often ask Emidio for his suggestions and I've NEVER been disappointed--EVER!! Highly recommended for the serious foodie! Read less
Dined on pizza, lasagna, prosciutto, and chicken in lemon. Oh, and chocolate mousse. Everything was delicious.

Pizza at this place is far from what is a real Italian pizza. It's small, tasteless and you pay a fortune if you want to create your own one since you pay per ingredients. This is kind of ridiculous. I tried only a pizza there so cannot speak for pasta, but if the quality is the same of pizza dough, than I'll be glad to avoid it.

We went in from the small intimate side of this restaurant and then discovered a lovely garden area and outdoor seating, on the other side, both had great atmosphere, although busy, the staff was attentive , the food great, a lovely selection for all tastes of meat, fish and pasta dishes. Everything was tasty and well prepared, reasonable prices. Also amazing selection of cheeses and cold cuts and very good deserts. The house wine was reasonable and good. Will go there again.

Price and food is a good match, seating in the garden is relaxing after shopping on Nimminheim Road. Great place to meet friends because of location and has a great wine list and diverse menu to select from.

We have been living in the Nimman area of Chiang Mai for a couple of months and love the international food options available to us. This is authentic Italian food and the Italian owner is there to prove it. You can get a very large Margarita pizza for about 210 baht or $7 and I had a big Caesar chicken salad that was incredible, for about $3. I wasn't expecting much from the salad, afte rall we are in Thailand, but that Caesar dressing was perfect and severed with fresh parm cheese too. Yum, it is making my mouth water now. Maybe we need to walk over and have that for dinner tonight. They open for dinner at 5pm and have a beautiful outdoor seating area as well as nice tables indoors with AC. If you are there around dusk, I recommend eating indoors to avoid the Mozzies. Read less

We visited this restaurant on a Monday night, there were 12 people in our party, we received excellent service, and the menu caters for all tastes.
