The food was of a high quality and lovely fresh flavours. The waiter was amazing he could remember all our orders. The food is well priced. Would strongly recommend this restaurant and we will definitely return.

Been here a fair few times over last 2 years, I wish I'd had this account when I first came to Chiang Mai because I feel bad writing this. Last couple of meals have been desultory as opposed to lovely, other reviewers agree. not sure what to add other than hope things improve.

I went there once. I pick buffet because I wanna try many Italian menu. they taste good. But kinda don't like the cold cut. it's just normal taste for me. I went there early( around 5.30pm), so I got some cool pics for u.

I've eaten here once and had a single plate of pasta so cannot offer a balanced review. Other reviews are very mixed and some comment on the pleasant garden setting. I had Papardelle with a duck sauce and it was thoroughly pleasant. The pasta was home made, al dente and the sauce authentic. It did take quite some time in reaching me but was very enjoyable. I was disappointed, however, that no grissini were on the table to nibble while sipping the acceptable House red as I waited. Nor was bread served, inconceivable in italy. And when I asked for some, I was brought toasted bread with a dipping sauce for which I was charged as an additional dish. This was my experience, one I'm unlikely to repeat. Read less

I'm a resident of Chiangmai and have been to " Why Not" Italian many times. I used to consider it my favorite Italian restaurant in town, good quality food and wine, at a reasonable price. One of the best pizzas in town. I always recommended it to friends and new arrivals in town as a great, reasonably priced restaurant. When the weather is good, it's pleasant to dine outside in the garden. My last two visits were okay but a little disappointing. The service was very slow, waited a long time to place a drink order, though waitresses were walking past the table. The pizza was fine, though the pasta dish wasn't up to the usual standard, the sauce bland. It's still a good place, but I feel that the service and quality has slipped a little bit over the past year, thus the " Good" rather than " Very Good " rating. Read less
Great setting in a spacious garden in the Nimanhaemin area, plenty of choice and very reasonably priced. But ALL of the dishes we ordered lacked even the most basic seasoning. A pity ...!

Why am I not going back to Why Not? Well, my boyfriend had organized a birthday get-together there for about 25 people. We arrived at 9pm & it began to rain so hard that nobody could leave. Eventually my bf went to find out where my "birthday tiramisu" was, promised by the owner beforehand. By this time the lights were off & the staff were clearing away tables despite the fact it was still raining, we all had drinks left, & there were other customers. When the bills arrived we saw that we'd been charged for the tiramisu, & when questioned, the owner got really bratty, saying "Nobody ordered food - you brought all these people but no one ate." In fact, we'd ordered soup, cheese plates, breads and a couple ate their dinner there. We spent thousands on drinks. My boyfriend was very upset that we'd been treated like this on my birthday, & the fact that I work for a magazine that gives Why Not good exposure made no difference. The owner's behaviour was unbelievable! Not going back. Read less

While staying in Chiang Mai for the summer, I found it difficult to find places (other than chains) that had good pizza or good wine. I was really excited to try this Italian place, even though I had heard that it was a bit pricey. The owner of the restaurant came up and greeted us and showed us to the wine room to show us selections that were not on the menu. While the selections are somewhat limited (and the wine was a bit pricey) I was very pleased with his suggestions. I have eaten here multiple times, and have had a bit of everything. the risotto porcini and the giant pizzas are my favorite! The atmosphere is great, with quiet music in the background, soft lighting, and it even offers outdoor seating. They have a wine happy hour that is affordable and fun for a group outing. Also, there is wifi at the restaurant, which is a huge plus for travelers! Read less

This is really with a heavy heart I write this, I know the landlord, and meet the proprietor. We ordered a bottle of Pio Casera, a reliable producer from a wine list of around 10 pages, way to many reds, poor selection of alternative wines and a completely absent of Rose, which my guest actually asked for. The Cheese and cold cuts are sold by weight, so 100Gram is a nice portion, bread is extra, it is also noticeable Olive Oil and Vinegar were absent. Cheese and cold cuts were server first. The waiter had ignored or forgotten our order of a Penne Putanesca, so a polite reminder got it on the table way after the remaining food were finish. The dish was horrible this is a dry pasta, the plate floated in sour olive sauce, my guest told me politely that the dish was impossible for her to eat, unfortunately I had to agree. We ordered Lamb chops, these came, they were full of fat, no seasoning, no salt or pepper, not cooked in a way where we enjoyed them, what added to the disaster were the fried potatoes were undercooked, then fried to little to make the crisp, again we left these on the plate. Our first wine was not acceptable, honestly what was wrong with it was it lacked fruit, it was dead and stale, so I had to return it, I asked the proprietor to recommend a bottle, he selected a supermarket wine, 1500,. THB. This wine was not of, it was simply sweet in it first flavors ending in a dry tannin. Not an honorable choice. We decided for the last bottle, we got a bottle which been cooked, it was smelling horrid, I politely asked the owners his opinion. He told me to him there was no flaws in the wine. We had him take it away. Please note, this was the end of the service, my guest said it plainly, why did he get so upset about having to take back a bottle of wine smelling like juices kept outside in the warmth for days. It was of, what worried me is, does the manager expect people to drink and pay for damage wine? Will the next be putrefied steaks being charged top prices. We left aware our present was not desired, not would we be asked if we like to order coffee or dessert. So added up this place deserves the above review! More than any other place I´ve rated. Read less
Best risotto porcini outside Italy. Starter cold cuts and cheese imported from Italy is a must to try. Mr. Emidio and his Wife perfect host. Garden seating is very relaxing.

What a lovely place to enjoy Italian food. Outdoor seating, nice lighting, good service and excellent food will have us coming back for more. Our group of six was well cared for by the attentive staff. Delicious ravioli and squid ink pasta with seafood (highly recommended). Pizzas are huge and enough for two people to share (and ask for the chili oil if you fancy it). Of all the entrees we got, only the manicotti's portion size was a bit disappointing - three small pieces with no sides. However, everything tasted excellent. We will be coming back again!

I've been eating here regularly since October 2013. I enjoy the atmosphere and friendly, attentive service. The food is tasty and a good value. Their fresh, clean salads are great. Portion size and price allow for a multi course meal without breaking budget or belt. Food is consistently prepared each visit and the quality of ingredients is high. If you enjoy wine, I've not seen anything like Why Not's wine cellar elsewhere in Chiang Mai. Emidio is happy to help you find a bottle matching your taste and budget, or to recommend one of their reasonably priced glass wines. Lots of local, repeat customers and families contribute to the pleasant, relaxing atmosphere. All combines to make this place a favorite of mine in Chiang Mai. Comments regarding poor portion size and quality don't match my regular experiences over the last 7 months at all; quite the opposite. Read less

why you say when there are so many to choose from? but for a clean, pleasant atmosphere, WONDERFUL and consistantly good food, a very friendly staff and really good prices for the quality of food. It cannot be beat. I bring crowds of friends here, come with my husband, or just on my own when I need to get out. Never had a bad experience. Always glad I went. The best things on the menu? Well. Their Caesar salad is the best I have had in this city, their seafood soup is exquisite, though only offered from time to time. Their entrees are perfectly cooked. The thin crust pizza...wow. Also, quite an extensive wine list...for those who like wine. Even their cheap house wine is good. Its all go. Why not go? Read less

I'm a Chiang Mai resident of over 10 years and used to frequent this place a lot. It was always great food, atmosphere, service and value for money. I'm just back from a dinner with friends and family and it was such a disappointment. The food was still decent and good but the portions were more like tapas style, even for a main course. When we got the first dish we were all a kind of joking but when we saw all main dishes then we realised that there is something really wrong with Why Not. The place even used to be the meeting point of the Swiss Lanna Society but they gave up a long while ago (for what ever reason) while we still kept going back. As usual, Sergio came to our table and asked how the food was. When I told him that the portions are ridiculously small he just mentioned that we should then order more and walked away. What a service by the owner. Yes this was the last time I went there. I guess same happens to Why Not like what happened to his previous place in Chiang Mai. What a pity but luckily there is a lot of choice in Chiang Mai. After all we had no issue at all getting a table...surprised ? Good night Sergio ! I know you don't read this but you should :-) since you are not listening. Read less

I have been here once on my own and once with company. Both times, some of the dishes were okay, but I was also served rancid steak that tasted like soap. I am happy to say that the second time, the owner or manager came over, apologised profusely, agreed there was something wrong with it and gave me a sorbet. So they dealt with it very well.. but the fact is that I have only been served off meat 4 times in 22 years of restaurant going as an adult, and twice it was here. The other dishes were fine, of course, but pretty much everything is better at the Pasta Cafe, just a few hundred metres away in the same area: better food, better ambience, better service. Read less

The only thing I have tried here is a pizza, and my boyfriend and I used to come once a week for a couple months. I think their drinks are overpriced, but the house wine is usually quite good. They have a little icecream shop behind the restaurant, with some good choices but it is more expensive than other icecream places nearby (but open until 11pm, I think?). Anyways...I love the pizza! Thin crust and not greasy.

The place was overall nice. Candle lights, good service. But we ordered a lasagna and bot of us left this place hungry. The other dishes were a lot pricier so I'm really not sure if I would ever go back.

Ordered a salad and it was just mixed lettuce, no veggies. Weird. Mostly did not enjoy the four small children running around us and under our table, presumably the owners kids. I dig kids but it was 10:30pm and playtime shouldn't be in the dining area. We got the rest to go and will eat it when we get home. Otherwise I think it would be a nice spot, but like a schoolyard this night. Read less

We chanced upon Why Not, while we were walking in the area, and we are so glad that we did. Emilio, the owner, who is from Southern Italy, knows his Food and Wine. He has some very nice Cheeses that go with the Italian wine from the Umbria region (the wine is reasonably priced) a good robust full-bodied wine. We had the Vegetarian Pizza the first time, and he made it spicy as per our request. The breads are served warm and toasted with good dips - a number of restaurants serve their breads room temperature or cold. We were very happy the first time, and since we did not have a great appetite, could not taste their pastas. So, we went there again, and the Eggplant Parmigiana was really good, rightly spiced and so was the Arancini. We ordered the Vegetarian lasagna, and it was not too heavy and was really great. Even though the restaurant was really busy, he still took personal care of his customers - where I noticed that a lot of them were regulars, and some who were there while visiting Chiang Mai as well. The main thing is that, he caters to vegetarians, and not just salads but real good dishes as well - especially Lasagna, where a number of restaurants have it pre-made in meat, and they will not make a Vegetarian Lasagne. Keep up the great work Emilio!!! I will certainly recommend this place for people with a flair for good Italian pallete, along with the great wines. Read less

Over the last month I have been to dine at Why Not seven times. On all occasions the food has been tantalizingly delicious - fresh produce and subtle flavours. I have devoured a range of appetisers, meats, cheeses, pastas and pizzas - and all were delicious. Well done Why Not? staff - a truly amazing treat and I will come to dine again and again.

Did not expect to find such good Italian food for that price in Thailand. It is all fresh tasting and i had a great time there, i had some of their spicy mussels which were amazing and the pizza was fantastic. I ended up going there several times

Every dish we tried was superb. My wife is a caesar salad nut and couldn't get enough of the one 'Why Not' prepare. The staff were attentive and the service was spot on. The host made everyone feel welcome and clearly had much repeat business. I will definitely be back soon.
Just enjoyed a meal here with the family. Antipasto, spaghetti, lasagne, ravioli and more plus a bottle of wine. B2300. Nice ambience. Good service. Cannot complain. Will we go back. For sure.

We have been here before so I decided to take our friends to Why Not for a meal. The service was terrible. The waiter forgot to bring our drinks and went off without taking any order for one of our party. The food was terrible. The pizzas were dry with very little topping. The pasta pesto was overcooked and the sauce was tasteless. I think they must have used Thai basil. The chef or owner spent an hour talking to the people on the table next to us and never acknowledged us even when we left. I will not be going there again! Read less

Enjoyed a delicious Italian meal in a friendly restaurant with lively dinner mates. A good place to spend an evening. The food was authentic Italian. It was a nice change from Thai food which I enjoy greatly but once in awhile you need a change.

Consistently excellent food, at reasonable prices, and an extensive wine list of Italian wines. I go there at least once each week and have never been disappointed. The owners - Emedio & Dai - are charming and helpful. I highly recommend it.

A few friends decided to start out the Loi Kratong festivities with dinner at Why Not, nice garden seating. Good food, pizza was crispy, well topped a good menu with good desert selection. Will definatly go again

In the nice garden setting the three of us where supriced after 1 year back in Chiang Mai. Emilio, the new manager has changed a lot. The menu is Italian, but you can eat also with a spicy twist. Italian cheeses and meats smiles to you from the cooling vitrine. Home made bread sliced and lightly toasted served with two different sauses. Try the hidden beef carpaccio under the cheese sause. The risotto sausage and porcini (I left the sausage out)was delcious and not to heavy, the fusili were spicy as promissed and the papardella with meatsauce delicious. We shared a nice piece of Tallegio cheese with the toasted bread and went home satisfied and happy. Try it, WHY NOT? Cees and Theo Read less

Went to Why Not last night with friends who were visiting Chiang Mai, and the food was wonderful and fresh. Owned and run by an Italian man who has clearly put his heart into it. I am a regular here and have never been disappointed. A few recommendations: Italian Meatballs in Tomato (Wow - the best always) Beef Cappaccio with Gorgonzola - dressed with rocket and shaved Parmesan Baked Spinach and Cheese Chicken saltimbocca For the level of quality it is extremely well priced. Four of us dined for 2000 baht total ($60) so $15 per head, and everyone had a glass of wine. We also ordered plenty of food. For service, quality and value this one is hard one to beat. TIP: If the weather is nice the outdoor seating is great. If not indoor. Highly recommend the outdoor seating as it has a very nice vibe and atmosphere. Read less

Maybe I choose the wrong things or they were having an off day when I visited because what I experienced doesn't match up to most of the reviews here. I tried the lasagna and my friend had the anchovy pasta. The lasagna was sloppy and pretty tasteless, I had to use a lot of pepper, and my friend's pasta had no anchovy's in! The sauce just tasted a bit salty. We wouldn't go back again.

I have lived in Thailand for several years. I was never a big fan of Italian cuisine, especially after some recent restaurant disappointments.I dinned at the "Why Not" reluctantly with some friends. What a surprise. They offer both garden and air conditioned seating in a pleasant ambiance. The menu is very extensive. We shared a Caesar salad (160 baht) which was quite authentic and big enough for four people to each have a plate. I tried an Italian sausage appetizer served in a wine sauce which was delicious and a generous serving.(90 baht). My entree was a delicious cannelloni stuffed with Ricotta and spinach. (135 baht). My friend had the chicken scallopini and gave it high marks. It was so big he had to get a "doggie bag" The house red wine was quite, quite good too. They offer an extensive array of appetizers (antipasto), pasta, meat and seafood entrees. We peeked into the dessert cooler and saw numerous tantalizing treats but were too full to sample. I have a rekindled love of Italian food and will return here soon. Read less

It said: "Repeat performances aren't guaranteed. You got lucky. Now you'll find another way." I had a craving for my favorite place and my favorite wine, so I headed over to A street & area referred to as Nimmanheimin. This is the cool trendy area of Chiang Mai where Affluent Thais and foreigners hang out. At least half a dozen wine bars are located here. My choice for the evening was "I'm Chair". I love their house wine and gourmet food, even though the portions are small and the prices are high. I hopped a songtaew and got to Nimmanheimin Road a little early, so I decided to try a wine bar on Soi Eleven called House of Wine. They had a great selection of wines and were serving five red wines by the glass. I went with an Australian one. It was a blend and very good. I'm Chair opens at 6pm and it was ten after. I finished my wine and walked over to the restaurant, but it was closed. I think my worst fears have come to pass. The restaurant had little business on the times I had dinner there previously. And now its closed on a Saturday night and most likely gone out of business. Now what to do and where should I go? Why not go to......................................... "Why Not" a great little Italian restaurant just a few blocks away. This turned out to be a winner. From the wide selection of wines by the glass, to the Thai/Italian fusion dish that I devoured, along with dessert consisting of mixed berry ice cream drenched in Stoli. The atmosphere was excellent and the service was five star. The land of smiles was good to me tonight! Read less

The pizza is really nice, and not too expensive. The pasta is not too expensive either, but portions can be quite small. The owner and staff are very nice and friendly. The wine buffet is great. The seating outside is really nice and they play a pretty varied mix of eclectic music, which I like - sometimes even ballet soundtracks! Will be coming back here many more times in the future :) Read less

Thai foreign cuisine suffers from chefs who grew up in an utterly different culinary tradition and who have never even been to the foreign country in question. They're usually forced to use local ingredients in place of unobtainable or over-expensive imported ones and besides, the tropical climate plays its own tricks on our own perceptions and palates. That's why I usually avoid foreign restaurants like the plague: your chances of getting even a reasonable approximation of the cuisine you're looking for are remote. Why Not? (an English translation of the familiar Italian phrase, "Perche no?" is an exception. The Italian food here is as authentic as you'll find in Californian Italian restaurants, and comparable to those in New York and even London. At comparable prices – and this is a vital factor – it beats them all. The dishes are served promptly, exhibit excellent balance of flavors and, most importantly for Italian cooking, their textures are excellent. Try the ice-cream to get an idea of what I'm talking about, though the pastas are all al dente and thoroughly enjoyable. Sit outside, make allowances for the fact that the wines (bargains on Fridays and Saturdays) have travelled 10,000 miles and must survive tropical heat and storage challenges, and have yourself a fine time. It's now on my 'once a week' calendar. My only regret is that they don't open for lunch. Read less

One of the best discoveries of Chiang Mai, Why Not serves really good authentic Italian food at decent prices. Their pizzas are a definate favourite! The house wine is good (better than alot of places for wine in chiang mai) and they have a Friday night wine buffet - yet to try! All in all a great place to eat, drink and chill.

Why Not offers a great wine buffet option on both Friday and Saturday evenings. Please arrive before 6:30 to make the most of it as it end around 8pm. The food is also very good but be warned the large pizzas really are large. We ended up taking a doggie bag home and there were 2 of us eating it. You could easily have a glass on wine each and a large pizza for under 500baht.

This Italian restaurant has amazingly tasty lasagnas for about 80 to 105 baht, depending on the content, from vegetarian to beef to salmon. And the spaghetti carbonara is out of this world. The atmosphere is very pleasant and casual with lots of al fresco dining, although there is an elegant inside dining room also. The owner is from Italy and makes sure the customers are enjoying themselves. There is an array of fresh cheeses for sale as well. The food was so good we took a "to go" package of lasagna to a friend in the hospital, who also raved about it. It has become, in a few visits, one of our favorite Chiang Mai restaurants, and even Thai food lovers like my girlfriend love the food. Read less

This is a fairly mixed sort of place. The inside seating area is rather disappointing, being cramped and quite noisy. The outside seating at the rear however looks lovely - our mistake was not to sit there. The staff were generally fairly attentive and the food came quickly. (My wife's requested bottle of water never appeared, but....) The food however was disappointing. The portions were very small, albeit cheap - the latter should have made this obvious - and not having eaten all day, I was glad to have had a starter. The pizzas looked generous, but I thought were pricey, the "make your own pizza" being the only option available, with some toppings being B90 each. Far too much for a restaurant of this quality. It is "okay", but nothing more. There are other Italians in this area, and I won't be rushing back. Read less

Having had an ok meal on our first visit (Greek salad, huge but with very little Feta and Seafood pasta, small portion) we decided to give Why Not a second chance as the reviews for the pizza were promising and we had really enjoyed the pretty outside eating area. Three of us went and we all ordered pizza, having selected the base size from either 12" or 18" there is a long list of items at various prices to chose from to add as toppings (40-80baht each) a good way to be sure you get your favourite combo. I chose a mix of garlic, onion, pepper and pesto sauce but when the pizza arrived it was completely smothered in a thick green sauce that didn't really resemble pesto at all, the garlic and the onion were almost raw and the base was more like one found on a frozen supermarket pizza . All in all very disappointing, my two companions had a slightly better experience (minus the sea of pesto sauce) but were not overly impressed. The setting is lovely, the staff very friendly and attentive but the owner needs to have a good look at the standard of the food being served. Read less

I have a thing for checking out Italian food in Thailand. This has been a place I have repeatedly visited in the last two months because the service, the prices and the food are all very good. I usually get pizza or pasta/lasagna. The pizza is really cheap for the size and quality. The owner and staff are very friendly and the setting is nice (both indoors with AC and outdoors in a large garden). It is definitely a place anyone passing through should try out if they are looking for good pizza/Italian!

this is a restaurant on famous Nimanhemin Road that offers a lovely garden setting. We visit this restaurant on a regular basis. The food is consistently good and fresh. The wine selection is great, too.

This was my third visit to this restaurant in Chiang Mai and I must say what a very pleasant dining experience it is. The restaurant has seating indoor and outdoor, the are staff very helpful and attentive and treat the customers with the respect and politeness you would expect from Thailand. We were a party of 6 people and our selection from the menu was varied all the food was excellent, salad, steak, pizzas and pasta all at affordable prices. Cant wait to go back there again and highly recommend Thai residents and foreign tourists to include a meal at this excellent restaurant on their itinerary. Why Not? well worth a visit.

From beginning to end, this restaurant was a mildly unhappy experience for many people, kindof the worst of a foreign-owned restaurant and an extra dose of Thailand's laid back: Lots of people were left with nothing to drink right through their meals. There was very little effort to flex for vegans -- both local and Italian cuisine can usually manage that. Food was expensive for this area though not surprisingly so for Italian food, and quality may have varied, certainly the vegan contingent just nibbled. The final blow: they combined the checks of everyone who knew one person in our group, including a second separate table that came later and left later. We were all milling around waiting for the bill to be figured out for half an hour, with lots of people already gone. It was clean, it was fine, no one got sick or anything, but the restaurant peppered the evening with frustration. * Don't bring a big group * Don't bring people with dietary needs Read less

We are living in Asia since 8 years and got used to struggle with sub quality pizza's around the region until we got in Why Not. We have been blown away by the fresh and crusty pizza. In our clan of 9 people we all concluded it was the best pizza since ages and outside Italy. A MUST go place if you are in ChiangMai and love pizza. Renzo
Good service, great patio, good wine, and decent food. Really nice place over all and decent value. Their deserts are good,

This is one of our regular haunts. I especially love the crispy thin pizzas where you decide yourself on the toppings. The other food is nice, rather small portions at reasonable prices which gives you the chance to order more than one dish.

My wife ,son(10) and I quite enjoyed why not , service was great and the owner tried really hard to help with wine and menu choices . We chose from the special seafood menu , my wife had Lobster and I , Salmon . The salmon was poor , small portion and totally cooked to death !!! The lobster tagliatelle had very little lobster in it ? Our shared Caesar salad was enormous and very good as was my sons pizza . The desserts were good and the bottle of Italian Chardonnay was nicely chilled and reasonably priced . The ambiance is really good especially in the colourfully lit garden . We will go again but will avoid seafood specials!!!. Read less
Fun , nice and good economical Italian food. Good selection of pastas and cold cuts . Home made ice cream and desserts

Food not very good. This is the same ownership and management as Pizza y Vino at Thaphae Gate but it is a little cheaper, maybe because the area is a little less touristy than Thaphae Gate. The menu is similar. Don't get too thrilled by the 250-baht wine buffet -- it is all low-quality table wine in 5-litre cases. The thing going for this place is that there is a good lawn that is good for parties and people who like to come in groups.

Why Not is one of Chiang Mai's better Italian restaurants. Pizza dough and pasta are both made freshly on the premises and the owner is both wonderfully passionate and highly knowledgeable about his produce. I have never been disappointed by anything I've eaten there, however, the quality and authenticity of some regional dishes are better than others. My advice is to get recommendations from the owner. He is forever perfecting sauces and trying new pasta stuffings and as he imports much of his ingredients and wine himself, his advice will often lead to a sensational dining experience. Read less

We have been back to Why Not about five times since my last review, both as a couple and with various friends and relatives. Everyone enjoyed the food and agreed that this is a fine place for al fresco dining, with an excellent menu and a good selection of well-priced wines. The menu reflects Emidio's willingness to experiment with quality imported seafoods and his love of delicious sorbets and gelati with liqueurs. The scallops were especially notable this time, and the orange sorbet with cointreau is irresistable.

1.) All you can drink wine buffet on Friday nights (which we affectionately call "Wine Not") 2.) A big, beautiful area for outdoor dining in Nimman 3.) Authentic Italian food from an authentic Italian 4.) Terrific pizzas with perfect, thin crust 5.) The best tiramisu in Chiang Mai?

We sat in the garden at a table for two, but the very helpful waitress suggested we move to a larger table which would comfortably accommodate my husband's super-large pizza as well as the big salad and the octopus, wine, etc. The garden ambience was very pleasant and free of smokers...luckily. Imidio introduced himself and told us of his background in the restaurant business in both Italy and the US. Later he showed us his most impressive wine cellar where you can purchase carefully selected quality wines from several countries, all stored at precisely the right temperature. Oh, heaven! Then there is the gelato display. Irresistable! All the food was delicious and servings very generous. Caesar salad came with a superb anchovy dressing and nice thin shavings of cheese. The pizza topping with gorgonzola was very tasty and the thin crispy crust nice and light and easy to eat. This is by far the best Italian food I have eaten in Chiang Mai. After I have dieted off the effects of my over-indulgence, I'll be back! Read less

I went there with my girlfriend (thai) because she loves pasta and never get to eat Italian food as it is too expensive. Quite a romantic place and lots of space, can cater for dozen of people! welcoming and open. Boss very friendly and helpful and trying hard to be sure all is OK. so service is very good! Portion are a bit small but for me it is good as you can choose something else from the menu and try different things! Pasta Spagetthi were very Aldente more than we do in France. my big ravioli were good with the help of plenty of Parmesan! Pizza was light, parm ham good, but dough was very bready which is not to my liking. Desert i love Milleufeuille but the italian equivalent is typical asia puff pastry kept in the fridge so unfortunately too wet and no way near french cakes. the other desert triple choice of Tiramasu, and something else, something: looked very small and not very fresh. Pistachio ice cream was good. cost 600 bath including soda water. so not too bad...would i recommend it, yes try, would i go back...hum not sure...i would try somewhere else first...i usually avoid European restaurant in Asia cos they are expensive and no way near back home! waist of money ;))) Read less

We went there Sunday evening as part of a large group, sat under the large tent in the garden, I liked the changes the new owner has implemented. Had a short chat about different wines in his cellar, he introduced me to an for me unknown grape from Italy. It went well with our food. The lamb I had was absolutely perfect, the potatoes too, no complains, the could have been a little Rosemary on the Lamb and Thyme on the potatoes, I full understand the absent as Thais in general detest both herbs. Servings were correct in size, ambiance and location nice. Service good, just a little better than I had expected. Well done. Read less

We were just driving into the small lanes off Nimmanhaimen looking for some continental food after eating Thai food for 2 days and spotted this unusual named restaurant. Best thing we did was to get out of the car to check the menu kept outside. A beautiful outdoor with some tables and a neat inside eating option make it a lovely place to sit and enjoy your drinks and food. Emidio the Italian manager is a very friendly chap who welcomed us as we stood around discussing whether we should eat there but at no time was he pushy or boasted about the place. We really enjoyed talking to him and decided to not only eat there but let him do the ordering for us after indication our preferences. From the excellent wine to the bruschetta, followed by the pizza to the lasagna and the chicken with mushroom sauce, everything was outstanding. We weren't really expecting such authentic tasting food (mind you, being Indians, our taste buds are automatically tuned for spicy food and to taste such outstanding subtle ingredients was a treat) and were delighted with Emidio's choice. The portions were just right for the male members but the ladies found them a bit large but i disagree. The portions were just right! All this while, the very friendly staff kept us in good humour with their attention and service and Emidio kept dropping in after every course to check if we were enjoying ourselves or if a course correction was required? Don't forget the fantastic Tiramisu and the gelatos and if you can, try the days special desserts. Despite being a diabetic, I could not help but gorge on the desserts. Excellent! We went to this place a second time after a couple of days and were treated to some excellent grappa that Emidio recommended. What a character! Genuine and sincere! Our second outing to this place was just as delightful as the first and we truly really enjoyed ourselves. Heartily recommend this place to everyone and shall go again and again whenever we are in Chiang mai. Read less

My husband and I were lucky enough to find this place during our first week in Nimmenhamin. Emidgio, one of the owners, is a warm and wonderful guy from southern Italy who showed us his wine cellar and talked with us for a while about wine, and his experiences in Thailand and Chiang Mai. The wine selection is the best around, and the food is really outstanding as well; we had an Argentinian Malbec and a plate of amazing prosciutto and cheeses. Normally we don't order dessert, but the home made dessert trio was too good to pass up. All three things were amazing. On Friday nights there is a wine buffet 6:30-8:00 for 250 THB. It is the house wines, but they are actually very good. Add in a great venue, nice outside area and a nice arty inside area and you've got a winner of a place. We plan to be regulars here. Read less
What a great little place. Amazing menu that really delivers, a very friendly host and staff, god wine options and free wifi. A great place and I will go again!

Some friends that live here in Chiang Mai (i only live here 4-5 months a year) brought me to this new Italian restaurant the other night...and oh my god what a beautiful night we had, this place really rocks!! First and most important for me: the food : we start with their mix cold cuts and cheese plate, which was fantastic with his 4 different kind of cold cuts and 3 of cheeses (and they even have a bigger selection if you like!!) and we also had some carpaccio (delicious), some caprese salad...you could tell the mozzarella was imported from Italy and not any local imitation...really fresh and tasty! After we all shared some main courses, all of them, from the specials pastas to the lasagna, from the grilled salmon to grilled sausage (nice and spicy), was so good i couldn't believe it!! Then they introduce me to the manager of the restaurant, who is actually from Italy, and i understood the reason why the food was so authentic and so good : in fact he explained to us how important for him is to keep all of his dishes real Italian, using the best quality ingredients available here in Thailand. Before dinner we were also walked in to their beautiful wine cellar, where we choose our wine, which it wasn't easy...they probably have more than 500 different kinds of wines, mostly from Italy, but also France, Spain, Argentina, etc..and all range of prices...simply amazing!! When we finished up our dinner, we order some of their homemade gelato and some grappas from their big selection, and once again...outstanding!! I will go back to this place over and over until I'm here in town, and i really suggest to anybody that want to eat some real Italian food for a night to go check this place out, you won't be disappointed!! P.S. - their prices are unbelievably low, compare to the quality and the other Italian restaurant in the city!! Read less

I really enjoyed my meal here, this place used to be a buffet all you can eat but has now turned into a normal order of the menu restaurant. I def think their is an italian in the kitchen or behind the scenes somewhere. The menu offers a good choice of the usual Italian affair, but the quality of ingredients is evident. There is also a little deli here offerering cheeses etc. Portions sizes were just right. Nice place to enjoy a glass of wine and a pleasant meal with friends. And the best bit is the price!

Why Not is actually an Italian restaurant located in the trendy part of Chiang Mai. Easy to get to, just head to Nimmanhaemin Road, and it is one the left hand side down Soi 11. The food is obviously cooked by someone who knows a great deal about Italian cooking. I highly recommend the beef carpaccio with gorzonzola. The staff are very professional and friendly too. One small criticism was that I found the serving sizes to be a little on the small side, but on balance the prices were reasonable. They have very nice outdoor seating which provides for a great atmosphere. I would certainly go back to Why Not. Read less

What a wonderful find this was! The wine list and cellar is extensive with wines from all over the world and the food is absolutely delicious. The owner is attentive, friendly and extremely knowledgeable about his fare. The staff are very attentive,friendly and proffessional. The prices are also reasonable and very good value for money. Not just your 'run of the mill' Italian food at all but has that little bit extra which makes it special, We shall be going back again, again & again. Try it--You won't be dissappointed ! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: Came here with some friends for lunch. The place has a modern feel to it and is very welcoming. The service is prompt and the workers are friendly. Their menu is simple, with photos of their popular means shown. I ordered the premium beef khao soi and it was delicious, but the beef quality didn't seem premium. My friend ordered the tom yum noodle soup and was impressed by the flavor. Overall I would return again to try their other dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: I have been blessed to have chosen an apartment near some of the best restaurants in Chiang Mai (see my recent reviews). Ontong Noodle is another spectacular place to try if you're visiting. The restaurant has a very upscale feel to it, while a bowl of deliciousness is around 70 baht (khao soi) and they have a variety of great drinks to try as well. I always feel welcomed by the kind staff and love the environment and of course the amazing food!

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: We were the only tourists in the restaurant, which is always a good sign. The menu was in several languages, and the waitstaff are friendly. There are vegetarian options and the food came to the table quickly.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: I've lived in Chiang Mai for about five months and eaten here at least once a week during that time. The food has always been good, the staff are nice, and the service is prompt. I've never had any issues here over the dozens of times I've been. This place is certainly a lot better than most other places in Chiang Mai. Definitely check it out if you're in the area.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: Had a local friend take us here and we were pleasantly surprised by the noodles! Realised I forgot to take pictures of the food by the time I was done. Great flavourful food, nice ambience, but strangely not very well known on Tripadvisor it seems. Definitely worth a visit if you want to have local food in a trendy joint.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: Had the boat noodles and the wanton skin salad... really yummy! Noodles was spicy, just to our liking and the salad was super appetizing. Already planning our next meal there!

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: The only packed place full of locals. Flavoursome local food but with a modern twist. Fantastic service and value

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: After reading some reviews off the internet so we decided to give this place a try. So many great noodles places in CM. The restaurant offers some parking spots. We went there almost 1 pm and had to wait outside for the indoor seat. The noodle soup I got was kind of bland. 80 baht of 4 tiny pork spare ribs with some bean sprout....... Service was ok but watch out for your change........ cashier and server need to look carefully when customer pay... almost did not get the right change!

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: Very nice restaurant serving local northern Thai food, in nice surroundings, inexpensive food and drinks, you can sit inside air con or outside, helpful staff who can speak good English, highly recommend this restaurant for people who would like to try the northern food of Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: Came here with some friends and got a number of dishes. All of them were quite good. The ambience is nice and the interior of the restaurant is clean and modern. The staff are also quite friendly. Worth checking out if you're in the area.

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: What's great about this place is the ambience... very contemporary as against most Thai restaurants which go the ethnic way. Having said that the food is great and very affordable. Their premium beef noodle soup is fabulous and so is the pork khau seuy! Highly recommended

Restaurant_Name: ก๋วยเตี๋ยวเรือ อองตอง
Detail: We enjoyed standard Thai noodle dishes and cool drinks at reasonable prices in a clean, cool and fresh setting. This noodle joint is run by a young entrepreneur with model looks (featured in Uniqlo ads). Several outlets in Chiengmai.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We stopped here for lunch after shopping at One Nimman. It seemed like a busy place but we only waited a few minutes to get a table outside where there is no AC, but fans too keep you cool. The menu has a good selection of items but of course we had to try the roast chicken. Our server was not very helpful, so we just checked off what we wanted and the server took the sheet away. Our food arrived within a reasonable time. The chicken was good. But for a place that roast chicken is their specialty, I was not particularly blown away. Maybe my expectations were too high. I did enjoy the corn salad – something different than the usual. Portions are enough for two people (or one hungry person) and prices are reasonable. Overall we had a better than average experience at Cherng Doi Roast Chicken. Drop by if you’re in the area. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: It's not like a dog. Switch to a restaurant in the north. Calm down. It's like a spicy snack, it's like a loaf of bread, it's like an orange, it's like a snack at home.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Great place to have lunch and quite busy - so it must be a popular spot. Excellent service and excellent food at a reasonable price.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Great food good price. Chicken was crunchy yet juicy, pork was tasty, nice sticky rice. Easy place to come for tasty lunch.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: If you like Thai food, this place is heaven on earth! We ordered 6 different dishes and we rated them all 15 out of 10!!! Tasty…perfect portion size….We have been traveling for a week in Thailand and so far this is the best. The place looks local and traditional but very clean and comfortable. Can’t recommend this place high enough!!!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We visited this restaurant when we stayed in Nimman area in November 2022. The grilled chicken is JUST ok in comparison with other restaurants or street foods nearby, som tum is not good as food centre at Food Centre, Maya mall, Tom yum with pork ribs is with a thumb down, not exactly is Issan foods as we tried in many Issan restaurants around Thailand, especially in Chiang Mai and Chiang Rai. Their small packet of rice noodle is extra cost and it's. expensive as GOLD. The portion of the foods are not generous, seems to us, more expensive than other due to the award of Michelin guide few years ago. The attitude of staffs are not a welcome to their property, rushed, not friendly, and took quite bit of time to bring the remaining ordered foods to the table, same as missing other dishes, with few reminder, wait and wait, while other served dishes turn cold. There are many good foods restaurants, stalls around Nimman area, more value for your $$$. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The restaurant specializes in Isan cuisine and they're well known for their roasted chicken. The portion is small (we had to order 2) but the chicken, with its crispy skin, was delicious. The restaurant isn't very big and the set-up is simple. It's about an 8-minute walk from Maya Shopping Centre.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Incredible restaurant tucked away in a naman si full stop this is another place recommended by David from David's kitchen. Simply outstanding Thai food and barbecued food

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: A pretty small simple restaurant. The food is good and the decor and ambiance is pretty just ok. The chicken they are known for was simple yet still tasty. I enjoyed their papaya salad quite a bit, it wasn't complex, but just sort of hit the spot. Probably a little bit on the spicier side, went well with the chicken. Add some water and maybe a Chang and you're good to go.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Rude and disrespectful staff listening on headphones and looking at us like we were irritating them. I don’t care if the food is good. I don’t go out to face rudeness.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Chicken plate was good but somtam was just OK. Service wasn't particularly good but I was going there for the chicken and it was OK. Wouldn't overly recommend this place.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Chicken was just okay, came out cold and overpriced. The pork ‘snack’ just pork rinds, were stale. Sauce was nothing to right home about. There’s a much better similar chicken place across the main road- higher quality, cheaper prices, better portions and sauce. Don’t believe this hype.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Obviously appearing in a Guardian article makes them a bit popular which means now the staff are lazy, can't be bothered with toursits or 'falangs', look bored and play on their phones the whole time. If the food was decent they may be forgiven but the sauce which I think is the restaurants selling point, they gave us one tiny bowl between three people. The friend papaya salad was disgustingly greasy (I mean it was fried but really). Honestly, there are wayyyyyy better places. The standard of food in Chiang Mai is really high so don't be a sheep and fall for the positive reviews, it's not that great here. Again, the sauce is good but they don't give you much and other places are just as good and better. GO ELSEWHERE, stop giving them your money until they leanr to treat their customers better. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This place is pricier than others with similar quality - probably because they remove the bones before grilling your chicken. The skin is nice and crispy and the meat is tender. Very nice. The pork salad and different types of som tam are good - pretty sweet but that seems normal in Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Great grilled chicken. We enjoyed the dishes we ordered and would recommend for reasonable prices. If you’re squeamish about eating in a typical SE Asian spot, probably not for you.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The chicken is ok, but please avoid the other dishes. Their taste is overly salty and not balanced. Young crew and with hidden kitchen. There are better options in Nimman area and the old city. I will not return.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This restaurant is famous for its grilled chicken, but I’m not a fan of thigh meat so it didn’t work for me, although it did for one of my friends. I enjoyed all the other dishes: Pork Bone Tamarind Soup, Som Tam, Deep-Fried Tom Yam (yes, really!) and Mushroom Laban, which was especially delicious. The decor is simple, the service is fast and the food is not too spicy; It’s tasty though, which probably explains why the majority of customers are Thai.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The place is OK environment, service is fast. We bought wine there but they have no wine opener and wine glasses. The grill chicken is the BEST I had tasted so far, Crispy, Crunchy skin with tender meat. The dipping sauce with seasame is just too delicious. But other dishes not that good ,Spicy Pork(Nam Tok) is pretty good, SomTam on the salty side. The Pork Neck not well cooked, the Yam is bad. For the chicken, this is a MUST visit.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The chicken was better than other visits to this place. I though, “oh,,, this is why people rave about this place.” The chicken was delicious (dip in the sauce). The sticky rice as firm but soft. My partner said the somtam was very tasty and we both liked the muu namtok. We told them we liked spicy food when we put in our order.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I came here all excited from the reviews from the many blogs that raved about the food but frankly, while the chicken was pretty good (tender meat, crispy skin, and smoky)，it's really not anything you can't get elsewhere. The side dishes weren't good. The soft bone pork soup was super salty and the mango salad was just alright. The chrysanthemum tea was super sweet and I had to wait for the ice to melt before I could even bring myself to sip it. So while the main dish of grilled chicken was pretty good, it wasn't to die for and certainly didn't live up to the raving on the blogs. Overrated--give it a miss.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Arrived here on my last day with my son to share some nice meat and chicken with rice. When we arrived they looked at us like idiots. We stood there wondering if anyone will give us a seat so i had to ask. Then we made our mind up on ordering what we wanted and when i summoned the guy over he told me they had no rice !!!! OMG what Asian place runs out of rice are you kidding me i had to ask twice to see if he was joking. A complete waste of time and trip to go there and had to then end up eating in the basement food court at Maya again not happy on my last night in CM.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The food is great but service is terrible!! We ordered our food and after 10 min the waiter said that we have to choose other plate because they didn’t have enough and other colleague has sold to other client! Then we asked for the bill and the waiter went smoking, so we had to wait for him to finish it which is ridiculous!!!!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We took Grab Car to go to this place from our hotel in the old city area. It didn’t disappoint in terms of the dishes we ordered. Definitely the chicken is a must. I must warn though if you’re the type who is scared of lizards climbing everywhere then don’t come here. Also upon entering the place I could small the musky non-so-pleasant smell of the place but I let it pass. We settled at a nearby bench and enjoyed our meal in an open air concept place. No frills. Price reasonable. We ordered the salad (corn) twice however no matter what we say (no spicy at all) it was still spicy in the end.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Definitely trying this out since it’s very close to my stay. If you are staying at Sanae Hotel, definitely it’s a must to try. Had the signature chicken and the mushroom Tom yum soup. I think the soup is just ok, nothing to rave about. The rice was also quite hard and not as fragrant as many Thai restaurants. If it’s better, would definitely be a 4/5*. The pork shoulder looks good too but didn’t manage to try it. Definitely try the chicken for the crispy skin and tender meat. The bone is removed so it’s easy to go for everyone. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The crispy grilled chicken is the best. In fact all items on the menu are very well done. The location is good. This is a place I like to take out of town visitors or meet local friends, Everyone is always happy with the choice. The prices are reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Very ordinary, almost guest are foreign tourist. Their cook are very "clever":putted the burnt side under. Roast chicken is old and dry. It can cause cancer if you eat it!!! Other dishes are sweet and spicy, too. Fried papaya, too oily, eating nausea! It's NOT worth a try. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We had planned on going to Cherng Doi Grilled Chicken even before our trip, as it was suggested by a friend. Ended up going for a late lunch around 4pm. Not crowded at all, but there were a few other diners. Easy to understand menu (pictures helped). Food was what it lived up to be. The chicken was excellent, as was the larb. It was excellent food at an excellent price. Would highly recommend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The crispy skin Issaan style grilled chicken was so yummy, we ordered a second plate. The somtum was normal, the sticky rice was hot & fresh. The Tom zab kradukmoo pork soup was really good, too. Google sent us to the next street over, but we parked it and walked (maybe better, another narrow CM street). Want to try SP to compare.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Came highly recommended and did not disappoint. Good selection of quickly cooked food. As per previous reviews place focuses on food so no table cloths and fussy service just fun local dining experience. Slightly out of the main tourist area but you will not be disappointed

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We came here after seeing it pop up in several searches for must eat places in Chiang Mai. It did not disappoint! This is a small, cute outside garden space. No frills, but does crispy chicken and sticky rice really well. We also tried the fried papaya salad which is worth getting for a fun twist and the cucumber salad which was light and refreshing. Good cheap eats for sure!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Loved this little place , great food , went back twice, the chicken and pork shoulder dishes really stood out ! Small plates so you can order maybe 4 dishes + rice for 2 ppl, about 60-70 bht each , I’d go quite early as they do sell out some of the slow cooked cuts Altho I would say if you have the Sence of self entitlement that some of the ppl leaving reviews on here do give it a miss , this place is about food and not bending over backward to please fussy guests with western style table side service, there will be more food for ppl that appreciate it !! Juts get up order your food it will be amazing , walk to the counter and pay the bill . Simple !!!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Had the roast chicken (NO. 1 on menu) and the green papaya salad. Both were excellent. Service is a bit slow, but it’s worth the wait. Do arrive early, the place gets crowded quickly.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I was really expecting more here. The chicken tasted great. But thats about the only positive. The other dishes were OK, service Sucked!! And so did the papaya salad- worst ive had. Place is just sucking in tourist $, and sacrificing service. I would not go back here.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Wow! What a dirty and rude place. I had such high hopes. Sure the chicken looked good, but I saw two rats running about and the people were so incredibly rude and uninviting. My advice would be to avoid this hole and certainly don’t queue for it. There are so many excellent places in Chiang Mai where you can eat wonderful, local food that is hygienic and delicious with people that welcome you with a smile... that includes the many many street food places which are run by people who would be so grateful for your custom. If you want proper Lanna or general Thai cuisine, go to Chang Phuak gate and order what looks good (there is a lady that does much much better grilled chicken and Chiang Mai sausage! There’s also seafood to die for!) Go to Wararot road and try the som tum, grilled pork or delicious noodle soups. If it’s an Isaan chicken restaurant you’re after, go to SP chicken, where they’re much cleaner and nicer. Or even just try one of the lovely family run establishments that are all over the city. Follow your eyes and your nose and you’ll never stop eating delicious food in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I had lunch at Cherng Doi Grilled Chicken restaurant on my last day in Chiang Mai (CM). During my last visit to CM, I ate at SP Chicken, which is another grilled chicken restaurant in CM widely regarded by locals and tourists as THE place for THE best local-style grilled chicken. I ordered a plate of the grilled chicken along with the grilled pork deep fried with basil and garlic and the tom yam mushroom soup, all restaurant-recommended dishes. Everything was delicious. In particular, the grilled chicken with its crispy skin was an absolute delight with every yummy bite! Truly a wow factor that will sure guarantee my repeat business at this restaurant during my future trips to this charming Thai province. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Having previously lived in CM and returning, this place was first on my list to show my partner. In a city known for amazing food, that speaks volume! Kai yang, steak jaew, som tam and khao neaw is a combination of food that will never, ever disappoint. Having read some reviews on here, it’s so harsh to call it overrated. It’s CM comfort food. Its not a celebration of Thai flavour and extravagance, it’s simply everyday grilled chicken done right! And the price point you simply cannot argue with. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The grilled chicken is pretty good with nice moisture, smoky flavor and crispy skin - it goes well with sticky rice and some papaya salad. But that’s it. We ordered 2 soups but both were so salty that I could not even take a full tablespoon. Other dishes were so so, not that impressive (other places would do better). The portion is small but the price is reasonable so that’s a nice balance I think. Expect to spend some effort if you want extra ice cubes (ice is self-serving). Don’t expect smiles, and the table guys barely speak. I would recommend a try (for the chicken) if you are around (it’s less than 5 mins walking from One Nimman). I won’t spend half an hour walking or 40 baht on a songthaew just to have a meal there. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: After two days in Thailand we’d been blown away by the amazing service and friendly people. Not here! No one greeted us on arrival. After a while a guy in a football shirt told us to go ‘through the back’. Didn’t look or act like he worked there but turns out he is front of house. Menu with pictures and you mark what you want on a card. We did. Waitress then told us one of our choices was ‘finish’. But walked off with the menu before we could choose an alternative?! Went after her and managed to switch tables to outside. Then came to tell us they had no Chang beer left, only Singha. 10 bhat extra, ok no problem. Food came and the chicken was good but not worth travelling across town for. Pork was sub par but the fried papaya was very tasty. Beer arrived with a glass full of ice. Maybe it’s a cultural thing but for me beer does not take ice. We enjoyed the food overall but atmosphere lacking. Not helped by a loud Chinese guy next to us shouting ‘fork fork’ as they brought him no cutlery. He was clearly aggravated with the service. The Brit in me is much too reserved to take that tact abroad but I wanted to and could sympathise, even if I didn’t agree with his poor demeanour. Then to wrap things up they had a power cut. Total darkness Kitchen staff shuffled out from the back and a little later a waiter wearing a head torch. Must happen a lot for him to have such a big head torch handy. Still, not a single word to the diners who had to muddle on by phone light, wondering if power would ever be restored and whether the kitchen could even prepare any more orders. The lights did come back on after 10 minutes or so, if only to reveal the couple just arrived and seated next to us enjoying two Chang beers. Ah well. Needless to say we won’t be recommending this place to anyone soon! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This place doesn’t disappoint! Simple menu, inexpensive and very good. The only thing keeping this place from getting 5 stars from me is the service as it is not real welcoming and friendly. Ordered the chicken (#1), steak (#2), and papaya salad (#16). While this place is famous for its chicken, we almost preferred the steak.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Our favourite place for roast chicken in Chiang Mai. Cute venue with friendly staff, affordable meal with some good options on the menu.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: They use only boneless chicken leg meat which is generally more tender. The flavors were spot on with the crispy skin. Other dishes also fared well - grilled pork neck and a sourish soup with tamarind and pork ribs. Very reasonably priced too. The only downside of dining here was that it gets rather warm. We were sweating buckets although we were the only diners there when we visited (visited after lunch hours) so I would imagine that it would be much worse when the place is packed.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Nothing bad to say about this place, it just rocks... Make the effort to find it, in between Soi 2 & 4 about 100m up on the right. The photo attached demonstrates how good this food is :)

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Found this place as it was near to our hotel. We had 4 dishes mainly their signature dishes such as roast chicken, pork, tom yum mushroom & corn som tum. Delicious and would recommend this place to anyone.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Very disappointed with this place, ordered the chicken and it was luke warm and absolutely tasteless. Shocked that so many people are raving about how amazing the chicken is, don't waste your money on this place.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This place will change your life...for the better! Super simple food that maybe doesn’t look like much but is packed full of flavor. Order: 1 (roasted chicken), rice, and 16 (papaya salad) if wanting more 2 (steak) is great too. Yum!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: There were rats running around on the floor. I pointed this out to the staff, who thought it was just an interesting observation. The service was rubbish, and the food bore no resemblance to the pictures on the menu or at the front entrance. You have to help yourself to ice from a cooler, which is unhygienic. Apart from that, I'd recommend the place.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Extremely local setup, but I was really amazed by how fantastic the grilled chicken was! It was crispy yet still succulent, and was really tasty when paired with the sticky rice!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I was torn on how many stars I should rate this place. The food was really good, the roasted chicken was everything other people described it: juicy and crispy. We ordered four plates for two people and 2 Thai teas and it was about 350 baht. On the other hand, the service was awful. I read other reviews before coming here and thought it was going to be okay. The service was very off putting. The girl did not say a single word, no eye contact and no smile. I thought Thailand was known for the Land of Smiles.... The Thai tea came about 20 mins later, we were already halfway done with our meal. Luckily, i had a water bottle in my purse. It was good but 5 sips in, you realize 90%of your cup is filled with ice. We left the place and still no word or smiles from the staff. I really hope they improve with their service because their food is pretty good. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I am on a diet and so everything tastes great but I was very impressed with the price/quality ratio of the food here. The portions were small but tasty and everything was accompanied by a nice sauce. It seems to have Isaan dishes and general Thai meals too. You are supposed to look at the menu book with pictures and after write down your requests on the provided piece of paper. The tables for 2 are small and there are many dishes. If you are a farang I think they automatically put less chilli in the food. Grilled Chicken and pork were great. The desserts look good too. Did I mention the great price too?

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Crispy chicken was unreal good. Also very solid som tum and laab. Great value. 3 of us stuffed ourselves with beer for 1000 baht.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We had a very tasty lunch of grilled chicken, sticky rice, papaya salad and cucumber salad. Everything was delicious especially the chicken which came with a really yummy sauce. Service wasn’t as bad as others claim though admittedly is quite perfunctory. Not many smiles but they do have a pretty efficient system and the food came quickly with no mix ups. The menu is in English with photos and all the ingredients listed for each dish. The dishes are numbered, you note your order on a slip and hand it to the waitress. Of course the dishes won’t necessarily come out all together but that’s normal here. We got our chicken and rice before our salads and couldn’t resist so had scoffed most of it by the time the salads arrived so we ordered another portion of chicken. Yum! We will be back. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Somtum restaurant located at the center of Nimman road, very easy to find. The grill roasted chicken is very very tasty and not oily but other dishes are just so so. The taste is for foreigner and tourist and suitable for Thai people.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We ordered roast chicken, pork shoulder, pig skin and some other dishes. Taste was great, with or without the chili sauce. Recommend to sit outside instead of inside the restaurant, if can.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Zero stars for service. Rude and obnoxious waitresses. Food is good though. The service does put me off coming more regularly.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Salads are quite spicy, but chicken is really good! Perfect for visiting in large groups: there are big tables.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Came to this restraunt by chance. First thing you will notice is how poor the service is. Our chicken was bought out with no plates or cutlery, and it is literally impossible to get a waiters attention, so it sat at our table for 10 minutes until our next dish arrived and Was luke warm by the time we could eat it. All of your dishes will come out sepperate so dont bother ordering a side such as rice as it could come out last. They forgot one of our drinks and our last dish and seemed rather annoyed when we reminded them. But yea, have to admit, the food tastes pretty good, if it isnt sitting infront of you for 10 minutes.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Great restaurant.... just off the Main Street... easy to find.... very busy! The grilled chicken with crispy skin is a must! Simple delicious food! An absolute value for money!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This is the second time we have visited this charming little restaurant in a leafy soi off Nimmen Rd. We ordered their signature crispy chicken with a cucumber salad and mushroom laab. The chicken was so tasty we ordered another plate. Everything was delicious. Cosy atmosphere, limited menu. Unusually for such a restaurant the service was very quick. There were many local customers. We will be returning.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Nice local restaurant serving all the traditional food at a very reasonable price. Mainly Thai customers which made us feel like we were getting a real local meal.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Lovely restaurant, chicken is great. Good selection of food, service is pretty prompt. You tick what you want on the list - there’s a menu with pictures to make it easy. Atmosphere is good and location great.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Just stumbled upon this place and what a find. Flavour explosions, a great atmosphere and lovely staff. The food is awesome and can't be faulted. Go for it.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The restaurant looks like a home with tables under a porch around the the courtyard entrance. Grilled chicken & thin pork chops & pork collar were well marinated - grilled to perfection with crispy chicken skin. The fried pig liver was done just enough to retain a spring to the bite. Everything was good - papaya salad, mushroom tomyam was full of flavour with a kick, & the glutinous rice served in cute little baskets. Come & try their food - no regrets!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I mean the place is called grilled chicken get the chicken it's really tasty! As is the coconut ice cream

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We had a fantastic quick dinner with them of their signature chicken and sticky rice. Tender, delicious and full of flavour! Would love to try more of their menu next time.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Cheap and reliable. We were hungry on the way back from the zoo. 350tbh and rolling to the bike😉 Grilled beef, nice cooked chicken and Thai salads. Yummy

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I stayed in Chiangmai just for a couple of days, didnt try all the cusions here, but during my trip this is the most delicious restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I discovered this place about one year ago and I went many times, and brought almost all my friends. I love the roast chicken with the crispy skin, their nr. 1. But also other dishes are very good, my friends appreciate so much som tam (papaya salad). The staff is very kindly, the service and the place are very basic.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We ordered the grilled chicken and grilled pork, theres not much to it but its a good option for quick lunch. We also ordered the tamarind soup, it was good but extremely spicy and we couldn't finish it.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We ordered the papaya salad and an order of the chicken and we were blown away. We were full, but we couldn't resist ordering a second plate of the chicken because it was that good! There are menus with pictures available that really help since the order cards are all in Thai.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Heading for dinner at 7pm.I had order the roast chicken and pork which recommend by waitress. But the food portion is too little for three person to eat, including an infant. And the sticky rice comes with plastic bag makes me felt worried upon toxic, and it doesn't serve with hot but cold kind. But still can see it has been steamed but not hot at all.Request steam rice or rice noodle also none although it stated on the menu. We didn't felt full with drinks though. So gotta look for other restaurant to fulfill our hunger

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Food here is amazing and authentic - get the grilled chicken, sticky rice, and papaya salad. It is absolutely delicious and a must visit in Thailand. No frills restaurant/very casual. They have English menus with pictures you can use to order.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Ordered the chicken, salads, rice, ice lemon tea etc... everything was very tasty! Quite cheap as well.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: My husband and I stumbled upon this place while we were looking for a place to eat at. When you walk in, you receive a paper menu in English and mark what you want. All I can say is wow - the roasted chicken is fantastic! The papaya salad was fresh as well. I recommend ordering a few dishes to try. Definitely a solid spot for food in the area. Very affordable as well.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: 7 of us walked in last nights got and ordered a variety of meals to share. The flavours were refreshing, not too much chilli 🌶- but the heat and flavour was definitely present! The kids loved their signature grilled chicken dish with its tangerine 🍊 dipping sauce - mouthwatering! Would definitely recommend this place. Meals varied from 50-100THB, very affordable - I think the 8 of us with drinks cost around $800THB

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The grilled chicken is pretty good--skin is crispy but the inside is juicy. However, the serving size is irregular. Don't expect much service, unless you are a regular.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Food was really tasty and cheap but the staff seemed a bit rude. Would recommend the chicken and crispy pork

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I have eaten at plenty of places serving grilled chicken, and I still go here 5 to 1 anywhere else in Chiang Mai. I can eat here every day. Their tamarind/toasted rice dipping sauce is fantastic, the crispy skin is always on point. Their melon dessert is one of my wife's favorite desserts. Quite cheap, particularly for Nimman, and so so delicious.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We had the crispy chicken (first thing on the menu) with sticky rice and salad. Was amazing!!! I was surprised that there wasn't a whole heap of chicken on the menu, a lot of pork. Food came out quick and tasted fresh and delicious. For 2 people it was 300 baht. Enough food and drink to make us feel satisfied!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This place is frequented by locals (and people in the know). It's tucked away inside a lane and worth the short walk from the main road. Try their signature roast chicken, and anything else on the menu that catches your fancy---I've tried most of the dishes on the menu and can attest to the fact that they are almost all good!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Good food. Especially the chicken, and a verry tasty sauce to go with it. Papaya salad was good too. Grilled pork tasted very good too, small portion but that's usually in Thailand

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Came here based on my Thai teacher's recommendation, and it was soooo good. We had two portions of grilled chicken, grilled pork, fried pork with basil, papaya salad, tom yum soup and sticky rice. Needless to say, we were stuffed. The portions were small but the chicken was really tender and full of flavour! This place is definitely recommended. The only one thing that I didn't like was that the sticky rice was cold, and a bit hard when it came.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Can be difficult to find as it's situated in a dark alley (it's next to Sanae Hotel Chiangmai & about 10 minutes walking distance from Maya Lifestyle Shopping Mall). Menu choices are quite simple (they have an English menu), you choose what you wanted on a piece of paper & give it to the server. Clean & service is fast. Try the somtam, grilled chicken & pork which was simply delicious.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Had fantastic som tum (papaya salad) plus pork and chicken grilled with tamarind sauce; sticky rice. Small restaurant, a bit out-of-the-way, but a fantastic and affordable stop if you're in the area. They forgot our order of drinking-water, but the food was delivered quickly, and very delicious.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We decided to try some of the eating places that we read about in an article online by a food writer for the Guardian UK. This was the first and was amazing and cheap. Chicken of course was everything we expected but also the crispy pork and the pork Larb were out of this world. Phil

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We are here on 3 January, the last day of the New Year holiday, and had to wait quite a time as it was packed. Worth waiting however, as the food was delicious. We had a salad with pork, Tom yam Thai, and the signature dish, roast chicken with dipping sauce. Really good.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The restaurant is a little hidden, but it is a precious find. Always packed with locals, which is always a good sign! The special is grilled chicken with crispy skin and it is absolutely delicious. Try also the tempura papaya salad and the lamb moo. Do not forget your serving of sticky rice and a cold beer. Very reasonably priced.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Probably the best chicken and pork we have tried in a long time. Simple restaurant very inexpensive no frills but the food says it all!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This restaurant is a little hidden, but it is worth the find. They are known for their grilled chicken and now I understand why. It's crispy and has a great sauce which isn't too spicy.
