Their sticky rice is the stickiest rice I've had in Nimman. Highly recommend this place. Please note that you write your order down on a piece of paper and the hand it to the staff. If you need an English order paper, just ask a staff member.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Their specialty is grilled chicken with crispy skin, and it doesn't disappoint. Their mango somtam is also good. The portions are small so you can order a variety of great dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Cherng Doi is a small, no frills restaurant 100m off for Nimmanhaemin Rd, the trendy Asian area of Chiang Mai. It has the most fantastic grilled chicken, crispy pork or som tam in town. When washed down with an ice cold beer it tastes divine. great service, lots of smiles and incredibly cheap.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Wandering around the streets of Chiang Mai, we had heard a great many things about Cherng Doi (and it even has a write-up in The Guardian).. So we decided to make a lunch visit and.....I am pleased to say that we were not disappointed. The restaurant itself is located down a "side street" off a main road on Chiang Mai, with table seating outside being rather "basic" and "street style", but comfortable and clean enough for visitors. We were presented with an english menu upon arrival and proceeded to select our choices - including the grilled chicken, som tum, grilled pork steak, stir fry pork and basil - apparently, some of their "signature" dishes. The dishes all came out fairly quickly, with the portion sizes more tapas-style (but generous enough given the cheap price point). I really didn't know what to expect as the description for these dishes seemed to suggest that they were uncomplicated and basic, but WoW was I surprised- in particular, the taste of each of these dishes was absolutely sensational and stunning, with the grilled chicken so succulent that I wanted to eat the plate and the other dishes were also equally amazing and just as memorable (especially the Som Tum). I really wanted to go back every day of my visit to Chiang Mai (but it is closed on Mondays apparently). This meal was also "super cheap", the staff were friendly and accommodating, and we walked out feeling like champions. Putting aside the fact that it is a "cheap-eat", I would comfortably say that this establishment is still an absolute "undeniable" must visit for any foodie if passing through Chiang Mai... Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I got the grilled chicken with sticky rice and the cantaloupe dessert. The chicken did not disappoint - it was juicy, boneless, and satisfyingly crispy. Nicely seasoned, but the spicy-sweet tamarind sauce really makes it. Ask for extra sauce! Sticky rice pairs nicely with the chicken. The cantaloupe dessert was just okay. It comes with tapioca pearls, crushed ice, coconut milk, and cantaloupe syrup. A little sweet for my taste and I think it was a little pricey for the amount. I would skip this. I went around 11:00AM and there was no wait. Great time to go! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: A friend took us here and we immediately fell in love with the chicken and steak. We had been looking for a place with good crispy chicken and weren't disappointed. Highly recommend the crispy chicken and the steak.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We stayed at Sanae Hotel, right next to Cherng Doi, and couldn't believe we only found out they had the best BBQ chicken towards the end of our journey!!! We would order BBQ chicken to go and enjoyed it as late-night snacks at the hotel. But please make sure you come here before 7 as their chicken might be sold out.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: A friend recommended this place to my husband and I and she was totally right!! It's a simple tiny place but the fried pork with basil, grilled chicken and papaya salad were unbelievable!!! I cannot believe this hidden gem! Definitely check it out. It's better quality street food but in a restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I have been eating here for over two years the food quality and service has been declining recently. The service has always been bad, Just recently they seem to act like they don't want customers, I watch as other customers spend 5 minutes trying to get the waitresses attention. Poorly designed and managed resturant. I won't be going back.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I've tried the following: 1) Crab Som Tam (green papaya salad) 2) Grill Chicken with Tamarind Sauce 3) Grilled pork steak with Jaew sauce I would strongly recommend (2) and (3). The Som Tam is nice, but I don't like the preserved crab. It is salty and doesn't look appetizing in black!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: If you want a street food on a table nice and clean this is the place, and so chip. Very friendly and also good looking place

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I have been visiting this place for more than two years now - the've never failed: chicken is great, variety of sides is delicious and spicy but edibly so.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I went for the Crispy skin chicken and was happy I made the effort. It isn't fast food crispy but somewhat different, still very good with tender chicken meat underneath. My friend grabbed a salad we shared which was also good with a sweet Thai Iced Coffee to wrap it all up, Delicious!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I love this place! I have been here several times and I always suggest to go with a group so you can order most of the menu. I love the Chicken, the bamboo salad, the spicy mushroom soup, grill pro neck, the sauce with it is amazing, the fried papaya salad is also great and all of the som toms (papaya salads), they have many types so up to you which ones to order. Last timeI was there with 5 people and we ordered the Thai papaya salad, the Lao, the fried, corn and tomato and the one made with fruit. The place is not fancy and also great for lunch. I highly recommend eating here and I hope you enjoy it as much as I do.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We ordered too much, but ate it all. We had the grilled chicken with the crispy skin again. It was still tasty. We had it with som tam and sticky rice. Everything was very good. We'll be coming back soon.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Came here for the roast chicken, found so much more to love. The roast chicken itself is delicious, but there are also several other small plates that I enjoy, including the pork steak with spicy sauce, larb, fried pork with garlic/basil, deep fried papaya salad (I didnt know that even existed!) and tender pork shoulder(?). Usually get a combination of those, add in some sticky rice, and I'm a happy camper.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: If you love chicken as much as I do, this is a must for your visit to Chiang Mai. The food is just beautiful, although some portions are somewhat small. Nevertheless, the place is very well organised and most visitors seemed to be local. Service could be a little friendlier.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I was taken to this place by a local. His recommendation was absolutely the roast chicken which for the place is famous. There were no questions about my order anymore, I had it! It is really amazing, recommended! Nice place, you can sit in the garden and there is an eco/nature shop inside.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This is one of the best place to eat good, affordable meals. Their steak and chicken is amazing! Me and my wife always get the fried papaya salad. If you come here you will not be disappointed p.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Some of the best grilled chicken and pork I have had in Asia. Order with a side of sticky rice and finish with the coconut ice cream. Delicious!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Great little place on Suk Sukame road (opp. Ristr8to in Nimman). Specialise in BBQ chicken with crispy skin and thai som yum which is a green papaya salad, have with sticky rice. Don't be tempted by anything else... A great meal for only 115 baht They spice it down for farang so if you like spicy ask.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We arrived close to closing (8:30pm closing i think) and ordered chicken and papaya salad. Both dishes were tasty and went down well with a beer. Not fancy, but food was worth it.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This place had amazing food, that was constantly full with locals and traveller. The menu wasn't extensive but every dish was great, especially nice was the chicken with tamarind, crispy pork with basil and papaya salad......yummo

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: While the chicken doesn't look quite as succulent as it does on the poster, it's tasty enough. Fairly limited menu. Roast chicken strips and pork bits, and a range of som tam dishes make up most of the offerings. Crispy morning glory was tasty, if not recommended by the Heart Council.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Get the grilled chicken, the som tum and the rice. Have some beer with it. The menu is simple but the food is inexpensive and tasty and comes fast. What more do you want? Who doesn't like tasty chicken!?

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Satisfied my cravings, roasted chicken with crisp skin & tender meat, papaya salad with pig skin quite refreshing, fried pork & Thai basel leaves. For sure I'll be back again very soon. 👍👍👍👍👍💚 Ordered my Thai milk tea with less ice but forgot to mention less sweetness. Shop name pronunciation in Thai is "Kai Yang Cherng Doi"

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We were staying at Nimman Mai Hotel back in September and been to this restaurant for lunch, good lil restaurant, food is great and staff is nice. And yes!! Their roast chicken is delicious too :)

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Good but not great. Price-wice it is ok but does not explode your mind. Had better in many places. Still worth of visit when around Nimman area.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: My boyfriend was keen to check this place out as he had heard the chicken was fantastic. As a vego I tagged along just happy to get out of the heat and have a beer. It turns out they have an extensive menu with plenty of non meat options including salads, fried mushroom dishes and more. The portions are small (but affordable priced) so order multiple things and sample as much as you can. I would definitely come back.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: It was a little difficult to find as some places in little sois are but we found it! We were seated and attended to quite quickly and given water for the table. We ordered grilled chicken and pork and some soup and some larb. The portions were on the smaller size so good for a snack. We ended up ordering more after we had our first courses! Everything we ordered was tasty. It was lovely and a great lunch but I have seen this place described as the 'best chicken in Chiang Mai' and I beg to differ. I wouldn't go out of my way to try and find it again after being here but it is great to know of its location if you are in the area.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I just had dinner with my family at this place. It 's just amazing. The food is excellent, the service is good and quick and the waitresses are very nice and friendly. It s a small venue (10 tables) with very simple features but don't be scared by the look. They have a descriptive menu that come along with an order sheet where you just put the number of dish you want. My favourite are the roast chicken and the crispy fried papaya. Last but not least it is very cheap for the quality of food you get. The 3 of us had about 8-10 different dishes + soft drinks and we paid 600bht.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We have walked past this restaurant many times and it is always busy so decided to pop in, 2 cokes, 2 rice and 3 main dishes (couldn't choose just one) all for £4 This is half the price of most meals we have had in Thailand and by far the best. I highly recommend the roast chicken. If your not staying in the area it is well worth a trip out of your way!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Love this restaurant, we have tried many different thai restaurants suggested by tripadvisor yet it would be too fancy or too touristy or not yummy at all! Yet this place is where many locals go, resonably priced, relaxing atmosphere and most important, superbbb yummy food! Must tried the roast chicken and beef and sticky rice. Go perfectly with thai tea. If you love sour food, the mushroom tom yum is a perfect start of the meal to make you have appetite for the best thai in town!

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: We had take away roast chicken from here on several occasions during our stay in Chiang Mai. It is a sweet operation excellent Thai food at reasonable prices. Service is friendly and efficient and the place is usually full of locals. It is famous here in the city but off the track on Sukasame road in Nimman. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: Hidden along the Nimmanhaemin Road, Cherng Doi is a great restaurant that bridges the gap between the super-cheap local places (plastic chairs and tables) and the more touristy places. It's ALWAYS full of Thai people, which is a good enough sign for me, and the service is always friendly and quick. It's one of those places where you tick the dishes you want on a bit of paper rather than have someone take your order. The forms left on the tables are in Thai, but they have some in English behind the counter - for the most part they'll bring some over straight away when they see you come in. The chicken is really, really tasty and expertly cooked and there's also pork steak or shoulder if you want to mix it up a bit. The rest of the menu consists of soups, a variety of spicy Thai salads and sticky rice. We've been living in Chiang Mai for a couple of months now and this is one of a few default places we head to when we want a reliably good and filling meal. I would definitely recommend eating here, check it out! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: The slow roasted chicken is wonderful and when accompanied by your choice of laab, sticky rice and a beer... well this is a meal dreams are made of.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: I have never had chicken like they way they do it at Cherng Doi. The women who runs the place is soooo nice and the food is always fresh, clean, and delicious. Its one of my top favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai and the value is excellent. I love the atmosphere, very cozy and simple. I highly recommend this restaurant as a real find and tell all of your friends about it. You won't be disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: It's always in the small corners of a city that one finds the truly precious food gems and this is one of them. Having been in Thailand for years now - and being an incurable foodie - I can say without a doubt that this is the best roast chicken I've had in Chiang Mai or anywhere for that matter. The magic is in the preparation - the chef only uses fresh chicken breast - carefully marinating and manicuring the meat to produce juicy, succulent crisp roast chicken. They also do a roast pork that is equally tantalizing. There are a variety of other aunthetic northern thai dishes - including a wonderfully light and delicious crisp som tam. The restaurant is located off of Nimmenhamen on Sukkasame (between soi 4 and soi 2). It's a nice little cafe style place. The owner is really nice and welcoming. This is definitely one of my favourite restaurants in Chiang Mai! Do not leave without a visit! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ไก่ย่างเชิงดอย
Detail: This is one of our favourite restaurants in Chiang Mai (having eaten our way through the city over the past year or so). In addition to the always succulent and crispy grilled chicken and grilled pork, there's a range of other dishes, inclusing chicken and muchroom larbs (famous northern dish), a few variations on the classic som-tam (papaya salad) and more. The reason we keep going back is that food here just seems to have a touch of class beyond other thai restaurants (which is saying a lot in this country). They've recently moved into this new location, very close to nimmanhimen and are now providing free wifi and nice background music so this is also becoming a nice litte rest stop in addition to serving great food at very cheap prices. Don't miss it! Soi sukasem is opposite the SCB and K-Bank. the restaurent is about 40m down the soi on the right. I think they close Mondays. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Over-rated. Pork was too tough. In my opinion, its over-priced and food quality so-so only. Was better during my 1st visit last year in 2025. Will not go aagin.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: What can I say? Had high hopes. Just landed at Chiangmai airport and before we went to visit the family , we wanted some good food. The wife searched up and found this place. We ordered the Khao soi Talay , satay Kai , satay nua and the wife ordered some other noodle dish. Have to say. Not very impressed. The beef was a bit tough , chicken not fresh . We sent some back. The seafood khao soi was okay. But not great. Wife not impressed with her meal either. To be honest I would probably never go there again. I’ll stick to the little mum and dad places for Khao soi and satay.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Good, really, but to queue for o get in, pay so much more than the usual ”catch” and then get a meal that is no better than a 65 baths meal (o there are plenty of fantastic cheap ”catch”) so I think this is worth a visit absolutely but you should bear in mind that it is over prized. Have eaten so many at least as good and sometimes better for so much less money.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The quality of the food is poor then they used to be The teste of the food not delicious like they used to be, but they so it is still nice

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This spot had a cool atmosphere, but that staff was not very friendly. Food was fine, but not the best we have had on this trip.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Khao Soi Super Bowl: A really good meal. The creamy portion of curry juice and the tissue contrast left by the crispy noodle oil created a perfect balance. Especially the aroma of the broth—heavy but not heavy, layered. What was most surprised was the aroma of lemongrass and limestone in Sai Ua. The spice is strong yet clean, but also greases the curry juice and creates a sweet-spicy balance. The octopus and shrimp were unexpected but delightful details; the salty shrimp of seafood keeps the curry lively. Pairing with Chang’s beer was perfect—every sip refreshed the intensity of the curry. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I had the chicken Kao Soi and spring rolls. My wife had egg Kao Soi. We enjoyed everything. While we do have a favorite Kao Soi restaurant, this was still excellent. The service was efficient. They have a lively atmosphere. Definitely an enjoyable time.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Was first time having the famous Kao Soy; amazing! 1000 baht total for a family of 4 at a high standard restaurant is great value.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: So, we read the reviews and hype about the best khao soi in chiang mai....well, reality is. The waiter's are at best indifferent, no smiles no welcome....we're busy, just sit down feeling. Menu comes, ordering a shared starter, main beef khan soi and steamed vegetables.....within a minute the khao soi arrives....NO starter, vegetables arrived, no starter....asked for starter, it came second and it was all on the table at once, no refinement here. The biggest issue is the bowl of beef noodles everyone raves about was 50% beef fat, the rest, waifer thin overcooked beef...sauce was tasty. Chicken satay was nice, veg nothing special. Overall, totally overrated and we left disappointed....look for better elsewhere. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This restaurant was recommended by some friends , there was a wait for 15 mins to get it . We ordered the Beef Kao soy , eggplant salad , chicken satay . I personally loved the beef Kao soy and the egg plant salad . Highly recommend for a meal while in Changmai .

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Delicious Beef Khao Soi and Chicken Satay. We didn’t have to wait for so long. The service was great. They take credit cards. We would go back 100%.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: There are a plethora of places to try kao soy in Chiang Mai, but we really loved Kao Soy Nimman the most, especially their beef kao soy. The beef is braised deliciously and super tender. The soup is a little on the soupier side, as opposed to creamy, but extremely flavorful - really enjoyed the combination of everything here. It gets crowded during peak hours, but the food comes out fast, so turnover of tables is pretty fast. Definitely give this spot a try if you're in the downtown /Nimman area.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We were staying at Hotel Amber just down the street and called in here for a quick meal, so glad we did because we came back time and time again. Delicious food at great prices! Do yourself a favour and come in for a bite, you will not be disappointed! Best food in the area and I've been craving it ever since!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Dragged her by my addict wife... second time. Both have been... amazing! It is simple, cheap and utterly delicious food. Nimman itself is ultra 'in' right now so expect to see rows of coffee shops next door to each other but Khaosoi Nimman stands out as a venue. Might have to queue as it is 'on the map' but do it... delicious. One query... it shows as $$ on Tripadvisor but it really isn't. A slight increase over standard Thai noodle prices but amazing taste and quality for, literally, pennies. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: You can find kao soy all over Chiang Mai. What makes Kao Soy Nimman stand out is their soft shell crab kao soy! It is an absolute must!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I stayed here last October twice on their terrace, and loved the food and found it good value for money. I came back now a week ago and for the mosquitoes we sat inside the restaurant. They had neither beer nor wine, we ordered water, wine at about 40 degrees temperature, nor did they have ice. The Kao Soy was good, but the regular atmosphere. A disappointment this time.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Magnificent food. Good service and the beef and the chicken Kao soy excellent. Very happy. Fast efficient and delicious

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Incredible khao soy! We had the pleasure of ordering beed and pork khao soy and the beef one was incredible! Service is super fast and efficient! Unlike a previous review; we simply arrived at a reasonable hour (instead of 4 minutes before closing) and we had great service and great food. This place is a must if you want to have a proper khao soy

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Off the hook good.. food at this price that is this good should be illegal.. you can see why they are in the Michelin guide.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I wouldn’t recommend if you have dietary restrictions. I informed the server about my seafood allergy and that I was vegetarian and he redirected what I wanted to something else as he let me know that the first option contained chicken broth. Which was totally fine and appreciated. When the food was brought out, they brought me the first option anyway (the one with chicken broth) and I didn’t notice it until I was quite a few bites in. I informed them that the wrong dish was brought out and then they let me know they accidentally made the dish I intended to order with pork and were currently remaking it. When the check came out, we were charged for the dish containing chicken that I did not order. I tried to communicate the situation but ultimately they made us pay for it because “I tasted it” even though I tasted something that may make my stomach sick later since I haven’t had meat in 14 years. It was just an overall frustrating situation. I would make sure everything is communicated very clearly if you choose to eat here with any restrictions. I’m just lucky that the dish they first brought out didn’t contain anything I’m allergic to. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This place is really famous, possibly because of a Michelin recommendation. The Khao Soy is what everybody queues up for. It was good, but certainly not exceptional. If you are lucky and there isnt a queue, no harm having a bowl. But please dont bother waiting for a table.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: What kind of food was every dish you ordered? I'm impressed that the staff cares about children. Free fried rice is served to children in large portions. Daughter eats deliciously I especially like the omelet with mushroom chili paste. The khao soi is also very good.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I chanced upon this place by fluke, and was a tad deterred by the unending line outside it. Then again, long lines are more often than not proof of a promising meal so I gave my name & tapped my toes impatiently while the staff gave me indulging ‘we assure you, it is worth it’ smiles. The food is exquisite & wholesome, all at once. I opted for the Khao Soi Beef, the eggplant salad, the chicken satay, the passion fruit juice & the iced dessert (As you can tell by now, I have an impressive appetite & fortunately for me an equally robust metabolism). The Khao Soi Beef was hearty & full of flavour, not to mention a sizeable portion in itself. I’d eat it thrice a day, every day, if I could. The chicken satay was good but was exceptional about it was the accompanying dip. That dip was the stuff of legends & I do so wish that they would retail it in bottles, for us to take back with us. The eggplant salad was spicy with a kick & a heart topping of fried garlic, accompanied by 2 soft boiled eggs. I have never known as surprising yet delightful a pairing as that! The fresh passion fruit juice turned out to be an excellent decision while in retrospect, I could have avoided having the dessert. Not because it wasn’t good but rather I was quite full by them. I happen to however be greedy too so my body is quite adept at making space even when bursting at the seams :) I highly highly highly recommend a visit to this restaurant. Your stomach’s heart will be full, I promise Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Great food, worth the wait over lunch. We had soft shell crab kai soy and it was the best we have had in Chiang Mai. Service was efficient, food selection extensive. Drink selection a little small.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: One evening around 7:45 p.m. in Nimman, it is sometimes difficult to find a restaurant that serves you because many close at 8:00 p.m. We walked past this restaurant and took a chance, still seeing many customers seated. Faced with our dismay, the employees of Kao Soi Nimman, after consulting the kitchen, kindly agreed to quickly serve us a Khao Soi. Chance sometimes doing things well, it was excellent and we enjoyed ourselves. The place is a kind of large covered terrace, very pleasant. Friendly and efficient staff. An unpretentious but highly recommendable place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Great northern style Thai food in Nimman area. As I have been living in Thailand for a year I can say this is one if the best restaurants I have been to. Tried the Larb moo, Khao soy and northern style sausage, all foods were great! Restaurant should keep open longer than 9pm, i’m sure there would be customers!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We came here on our last evening what amazing food we ate. The Kao Soy stewed beef was incredible. The Yod Fuk Mae Nam Man Hoi vegetables was delicious. Also enjoyed the vegetable spring rolls. We ended our dinner with a yummy Thai Iced dessert.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Not too far from where we stayed in Nimman, a short walk from Stay with Nimman. The restaurant is not fancy, just a simple set up with both seating inside (A/C) and outdoor, great vibes. Few choices of khao soi on its menu + other Northern dishes, We had the chicken khao soi and other plate of Nam Prik Num with fermented sour sausages and vegetable, yummy and we enjoyed. The soup of the khao soi is so much texture in it, creamy, had to order another bowl. Will revisit in the future when we are back to Nimman. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Packed with flavors. Good and fast service. Polite and accommodating staff. Overall, authentic and gastronomical. ❤️

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: One of the top local places for khao soi . Place is always full with queues. Different types of Khao soi . Featured in Michelin guide

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This has been the best khao soy I have ever had in my life. I would recommend this place to anyone, anytime. LOVE the food here.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Absolutely delicious khao soy and so inexpensive. Would have ordered another bowl if I could have eaten it. Will 100% return

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This it the first time for me for Chiang Mai and for Khao Soi that make me amazing. It's very good for first experience, many people in the restaurant;Thai and foreigner. All of staffs are helpful also a drink that very creat, there use local herb serve in the Thai style bowl but so sweet.However the next time we will come back agsin.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The food here is insane, the service we where warned was slow however they have installed bells on the table. (There was no need to use them as the staff where attentive) We had the Kao Soy with beef, as well as pork jerky and spring rolls!!! Make sure to add this to you dinning out list during your stay!!!!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: A quaint and popular Khao Soy restaurant in the Nimman area. I find the deco very nice and calming with a selection of air conditioned and outdoor dining area available. The place was quite crowded when I dropped by during lunch. However the service was prompt with English menu booklet available. Basically, the menu just consists of Khao Soy noodles with different toppings of both vegetarian and non vegetarian options. Food was served promptly, but I find the portion a little small for the price paid. Overall, it was a delicious bowl of noodle and this is a good place to try one of the signature Chiang Mai dish for first timers!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Beautiful restaurant, beautiful food! My friends all had variations of the traditional Northern Thai dish Khoa Soy, 2 had the vegetarian option of mushroom, and the other roast pork. They were fresh & slightly spicy but not blow your head off spicy! I had the Fish curry which came with rice noodles, ginger, Thai crackers, crushed walnuts & chillies. It was devine. Spicy, coconut creamy & the fish in the curry was Thai fish balls. There’s a hell at the table you press for service which is quite nice. Press & a friendly waiter/waitress quickly arrives at the table. Only drawback was paying for ice in our drinks but apart from that, it’s a must eat for Chaing Mai 😊 Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We went here by a recommendation from a friend and really enjoyed our meals, especially for the cost! Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Nice ambient, friendly service with indoor and outdoor seating. The Koa Soy is delicious, noodle are done to correct texture. Fragrant curry broth, slightly a bit too sweet otherwise balance in Flavour. The Northern Thai pork fish (Hors D-oeuvre Muang) is well presented, meats in different styles done to our liking. The 2 dipping sauces are tasty. The Kang Ho mixed Curry is not done to our liking, sweet and lack of flavour. Still, an enjoyable lunch.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We were exploring the area, chanced upon this place and decided to give it a try, as the place seemed popular with locals and tourist alike. The Kao Soi definitely did not disappoint! I had Kao Soi with mushroom, while my husband had their Signature big bowl of Kao Soi. We loved it so much that we came back within 2 days :) Portion size, for the noodles we felt that it could have been a bit more, so for our 2nd time around, we ordered additional noodles (at 20 baht each). A plus point is that service is brisk and our food came fast. A must visit! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The best Kao Soy is served as street food but this place saved us from almost leaving Chiang Mai without having eaten Kao Soy! There’s a ton of variety and it’s one of the only places you can get Kao Soy in the evening, since it’s typically served as breakfast or lunch (before 12). When we arrived, it looked like there was a big line but it moved very quickly and they provide seating and menus while you wait. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: My husband loves khao soi, so we were on the hunt for the best khao soi in town, and saw the great reviews on this place! Although not the best khao soi, it was pretty delicious! When we got there around lunch time, there was a huge line up! We contemplated waiting or not, but ultimately decided to since we were there anyways and we really wanted to try this place out. We got our number from the hostess and she told us there were 20 numbers ahead of us so we were prepared to wait for at least an hour. There is a bubble tea place right beside it so we ordered a bubble tea while we waited. To our surprise, the wait only took about 25 minutes! We got seated in the air-conditioned space at the back, which was good because it was such a hot day out! I ordered the chicken khao soi, my husband ordered the beef khao soi, and we also got the crispy wontons and steamed vegetables to share. The food came within 10 minutes, which was very reasonable. The khao soi tasted quite good, just the right amount of richness, and not too spicy. Since this place is so popular among tourists, we felt that perhaps the dish was not truly authentic, compared to some other khao soi we have tried. Delicious food nonetheless! The food was definitely tourist pricing since it was pricier than most other local restaurants, but we expected that going in so no surprises there. I would definitely return to this place if I find myself in Chiang Mai again, but I’d probably try to go a bit earlier to avoid the lunch time rush. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Sorry to say this didn't live up to expectations. Khao soi was very spicy, oily and red, I've had this multiple times and is usually mild and yellow. Admittedly I am not good with spice but my partner who loves spice said he thought it was spicy (he was sweating!) My advice is come if you want a spicy Khao soi, but not if you want it how it should actually taste. Portions are also on the smaller side and more expensive than we had paid previously. We got a fried rice which tasted good.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: highly recon this place, specially for ppl eho loves curry soup Came here for d second time in 6 days

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The beef Kao Soy was ok but not fantastic. It was mild in flavor and came out lukewarm. We also ordered the calamari which was not good because it was 75% thick batter and not much squid. We also didn’t like the pork jerky which was too sweet. The only dish we all liked was the chicken satay. But the worse part was the dirty bathroom. Can’t recommend a restaurant if they can’t manage to clean their bathroom.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The kao soy at this place was incredible! Excellent price, great service and even better food. They have options for vegetarians too!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Escorted by Joe, my Thai's friend, on a street in Nimman Road area, Kao Soy Nimman restaurant is one of a must visit destination while wondering around Chiang Mai. Its authentic dish of Kao Soy Kai (Chicken Northern Thai Noddle) has made my taste experience rise to an high elevated level. It also make me loved the dish so much. Six days of meeting in Chiang Mai has made me crazy in love with this food.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We came to Kay Soy Nimman after a really long day full of chaos. We were lucky enough to get our table after about 2 minutes of waiting only. The staff were very helpful and attentive. We had our dishes served very quickly after our order is placed. The space, the vibe and their decoration were very nice too, especially the dishes. A beautiful looking dish definitely makes your meal taste even better. Food and desserts were all very yummy. Totally worth the money and our dinner at Kao Soy that night was a silver lining indeed!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I had to try the northern dish Khao Soi, while in Chiang Mai. We had walked past the restaurant, the day before and it was packed. We came back and were very pleased with the dish. My wife had the option with chicken leg, and I the fried pork. There is seating both inside and outside. The chili paste was also excellent.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Wanted to try the Khao Soi Pa Nao opposite but it was quite crowded so tried this instead. Much quieter (less popular and not so nice??) and got a table easily. My friend ordered their THB120 Khao Soi and I had chicken bee hoon soup due to bad tummy. Overall the food was tasty and no regrets. Free fruits for dessert after the meal. Opposite place seems to be more for the younger crowd. There were posters of Teresa Teng inside. Lady boss looked a little unfriendly but I think that's' the way she appears

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Found this place while walking around. Friendly and quick service. Food was tasty. They have air con and outside options. Sat outside. It was excellent. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Myself and my partner went here for lunch. It took about 5mins waiting to be seated as it was busy. But once inside we were served quickly and the food was good and reasonably priced. Can't really complain about it.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We went there due to one of our friend’s research online. To be honest, the food was not bad, but it definitely didn’t blow our minds. We got two different kinds of khao soi, one with chicken and another with seafood, some northern Thai veggies, and some grilled pork with garlic (which was the highlight of the meal for sure!) However, they charged at a relatively premium price to other khao soi places, so for me it’s not worth the price of admission. If you don’t mind paying extra for a place that has a little better ambience, then I would recommend this place. If you want a hole in the wall/mom & pop shop place with more authentic northern Thai food, then look somewhere else.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This place offers very good Kao Soy--a must-eat dish in Chiang Mai. The restaurant has a nice atmosphere. The prices are not superb though, with most of the dishes costing at least 79THB. There are cheaper options for Kao Soy.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We came here to try something other than street food, and it was tasty. Overpriced compared to the street vendors that make very delicious food as well, but if you’re not into the street food scene, it’s a good way to eat local cuisine in a restaurant type place.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I find Khao Soy Mae Sai is definitely taste better and with tender chicken served. Strategic location and expected to Q for sure.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: My boyfriend and I wanted to try Khao Soi and this place came up on many "best lists". We were sat and ordered a few minutes later. Shortly after our food arrived we were given the bill, which was thought was strange since this was a trendy sit down restaurant and not a McDonalds or KFC. Instead of allowing us to enjoy our meal in peace, the server who brought the bill continued to stand at our table which made us feel incredibly awkward. Then our waitress, who spoke English, informed us that since the restaurant closed at 8pm we were required to pay at this time. As my boyfriend paid, I looked at my watch expecting it to be close to closing time. To my annoyed surprise, it was 40 minutes until closing time! If it had been 10-15 minutes, I would have understood but rushing customers 30+ minutes before closing time is unacceptable! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The restaurant is so tidy and modern, just perfect for foreigner tourists who wish to enjoy authetic local tastes of Thailand in Chiang Mai with no compromise for the cleaness and atmosphere. Staff can communicate in English. Their signature curry noodle is so perfect with generous portion of meat!! I preferred the pork version :)

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Visited Kao Soy after recommendations from tour guides from a cooking class and elephant conservatory that my friends and I visited. Food was served relatively quickly and was delicious. Spice was just the right kind of “tourist spicy”— unsure if you can ask for more spice. Some interesting things of note: Boba Tea is from the place next door and they require you to pay when you get your drink. Last call for food/alcohol is 7:30. They have a service bell, but I found that getting the attention of the wait staff was typically much quicker. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I and my family come here on the first day stay in One nimman hotel. It is famous restaurant for tourists who want to try some kind of Chiang mai foods, Especially delicious is Kao, Hot dog and sweet soup. The price are reasonable for tourists. Because it is famous so that if you want to eat here you should go early. The first time we went here at 5 PM and we have table, the second time we come back at 7h30 PM and all the tables are full and we have to wait for 30 minutes. All the food is delicious and some of them spicy so that you should order them spicy or not spicy. If you go for shopping in one nimman you should go there and try.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Based on their name, I would have expected their Khao Soi to be outstanding, but I thought it was just OK, not great. That being said, their tom yum soup was a standout (and made us sweat). Fried chicken w/cold beer was a must and that was great. We went there late (8:30pm), but I think they wanted to close by 9pm, so everything was sort of rushed. Other than that service was OK.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Enjoyed the tasty beef Kao Soy here. The beef was soft and the curry for the noodles was really nice. Worth a visit if you are in the area. Yummy!!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Tried their Kad Soy. It's very tasty and we also ordered the dessert and some side dishes. You won't want to miss out this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The first meal after checked in the hotel! The location is not hard to find because I was calling Grab hehee The restaurant was packed. It had tourist, local mixed. Menu is quite simple, curry mee with different ingredients. We ordered Combo curry mee and beef satay. Its my first time to taste Curry mee in Thailand. I did tried in Malaysia and Macau but first time in Chiang Mai. Back to the food. Soup is impressive, thick with beautiful balance of spicy and sweetness. Condiments with raw red onion, lime, and pickled sour veggies. Seems pickled Sour veggies is not popular in Thai but is it affected by China because of the Chiang Mai location? Overall everything is delicious! No doubt for the noodles, the beef satay crispy outside with very juicy meat. The spicy sauce is a great touch for it. Great experience in Chiang Mai! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: It is located in small path of Nimmana Haemindra Road. Recommended menu is Beef Kao Soy (it is a beef Northern Style Noodle with Spicy Soup). The Chicken Satay is also recommended.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: My red truck driver recommended the restaurant. Excellent choice. Had sseafood Kao Soy (soup). My driver came back to pick me up. Thai people are friendly and honest. Wonderful place.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The chicken kao soy was delicious, totally worth a 20 min Grab Taxi from the Old City. I also liked the variety of herbal drink. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I checked the review and it said it’s the best Kao soi I’m Chiang Mai. But after I took a red car to here for 20mins, disappointed. The food is not worthy of the price. Worse than the street food I tried last few days. I like curry soup. So when I see the soup is way less, I asked can I add more. They showed me, add soup 20bath. Excuse me!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Khoa Soi being the national dish in Chiang Mai, you will read many reviews for many locations but this place is really the deal.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Koa Soy Nimman Restaurant, Chiang Mai, Thailand Great Khoa Soi Selection We just arrived back to Chiang mai and are on a mission to find the best Khao Soi places in Chiang Mai within the next 3 weeks, so this is the first place we visited on our list. Location, taking a central spot within the Nissam district, just a 5 minute walk from Maya Mall and the New One Nissam Mall. Orientation, north west west of the Chiang Mai Square. From the outside, Open and green, within a nice quiet street in this busy district. There is also a seating area for people waiting to eat as it is a very popular place, so expect to wait 5 to 10 minutes if busy. From the inside, Open with lots of different seating options to suit most group sizes from solo traveller to large groups. Very spacious with lots of greenery around. Menu and drinks, the menu was quite diverse in regards to offering a large selection of Khoa Soi meals, from chicken, pork to seafood including a large bowl option for the hungry. There is also a large selection of curries with a new presentation style, a bowl of curry with a side of noodles, bean sprouts and cabbage to add to the curry. This is a nice option as you can eat the sides separately or within the curry. They also have a good selection of starters and drinks, crispy prawns to cool fusion drinks. The Food, was really good but we were split on the Khoa Soi as we are used to eating only vegan/vegetarian based Khoa Soi’s so this was a different experience. We ordered Khao Soi Shrimp, Red Curry Tray, Won Ton, and Crispy shrimp. The khao soi was very good from my partners perspective, from my side it could have had a little more soup and it felt a little greasy on the tongue but I am pretty Picky when it comes to food hence being a food blogger. The Red curry was super delicious with bags of flavour and plenty of soup. I really liked the option of having everything on the tray and adding to your curry however you wanted. The crispy prawns were 100% crispy and the wan ton was pretty good too. Food was delivered in reverse order, the Khao Soi came out first then the crispy prawn, then the red curry then wan ton. A little wait in each as it is very busy, but worth it. The staff, were really friendly and welcoming, giving us a number to wait for the nest free table. The waiting staff took our order once we pushed the “i want to order button” on the table, and were nicely presented and polite. Recommendation If you enjoy a good thai curry or khao soi then give it a go and see what you think, my partner enjoyed the Khao soi 5/4 but i gave it a 3.5/5. Get there early for a table or expect to wait around 5 to 10 minutes, people are coming in and out frequently. Well done to the staff and restaurant for offering something unique in the area. #LetsGuide #fsya #localguides #travelblogger #garystribe Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We were staying nearby and kept seeing long line ups of local Thais at this restaurant so decided to go for lunch one day. It was well worth it. They are incredibly organized. There was a long line up so they were handing out tickets with numbers for those in line. We only waited about 10 minutes and were seated inside. The table had a button you pushed when you are ready to order. Within seconds of pushing the button, the server came. The food was delicious and well priced. Excellent chicken satay with spicy peanut sauce and the spring rolls were very delicious too. Great choice!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We stopped by this place for lunch since it was real close to our hotel. We got the pad thai and a flat rice noodle dish. The noodles were a bit too soft, almost overcooked but the flavor was decent.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Location - parking space available nearby Staff - Friendly Price - Reasonable Food - Kao Soy was delicious, but Nam Prik Noom was spicier than expected Ambience - Nice seating arrangement with little waiting time

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Hubby and I decided to go to this location because it was convenient to where we were staying. There was another khao soy restaurant that had better reviews but a bit further than we wanted to go. That being said we were so glad we went here. The khao soy was so good and this place isn't hurting for business. They opened at 11 and by 12 this place is packed. Prices are a bit higher than others but flavor definitely made up for it! This place was so good that we double dipped and ate here again for our last meal in Chiang Mai. Unfortunately I will never forget this place as I will always compare whatever crap khao soy I can get in the states to this lovely gem in Chiang Mai😣😭 Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Located down one of the numerous side streets in the Nimman area, this restaurant has a nice outdoor eating area with lots of seating. Even though it seemed busy, we were still seated right away. The Khao Soi that we ordered was very delicious and served quickly after we ordered. I highly recommend the fried pork Khao Soi: the pork was tasty and a little bit crispy and the curry was slightly sweet and spicy.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Went here twice went visiting Chiang Mai. Had khao soi and spicy sausages. Both very good, the sausages I especially enjoyed. Very spicy and flavorful. This place does get crowded fast, which means you might need to wait for a seat if you dont want to go somewhere else, not sure it's THAT worth it with other options nearby. Service can be slow at times, but the food itself comes fast once you've ordered. A good option overall if you're staying in this area of Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I got a northern Thai curry dish. It was not bad. Not good. Just average and nothing better than you can get from a street stand. WiFi is poor and keeps dropping connection so don’t depend on that.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The food here is great. Good price good quality but the service is terrible. It's one of those places where they have a 'million' waiters/waitresses but everything takes for ever to arrive and you can't seem to get the attention of any of them. And when you do get service it's with a sour face and a bad attitude. Shame really. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Really good! We had Green curry khao soi, khao soi moo and khao soi gai and all were absolutely amazing! The portion size is good, the price is fair and the place is very nice

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Very average khao soi, you can get this standard almost everywhere. The gravy was served only lukewarm, spoilt the whole dining experience. At this price n this quality, dont think i will come back again

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Three years ago our friends introduced us to Khao Soi and we fell in love with it. Haven’t yet had it in a better place than this, but the search goes on :). Fast forward to now: the place was packed but I got in very quickly and my food arrived quickly. I think this is a must eat at place if you come to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We went based on the positive reviews. The atmosphere is pleasant, and the service is pretty good. Prices are fair and the food is OK. I had the "Super Khao Soy" and my wife had a pork noodle soup. We shared a fried spring roll appetizer. One big upside is that they actually delivered the appetizer first which is unusual for here. The downside is the spring rolls were a bit oily, but they were quite crispy. We thought this place was OK, but not a favorite here.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: One of many Northern Thai food that I wanted to eat when I visit Chiang Mai was Kao Soy and it was authentic and a bit spicy. Otherwise good crowd and there are tables located outdoor as well indoor.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Place was very easy to find, in a small alley but Google map is exactly correct. Located in the middle of tourist area anyway. Parking area only for some bikes and motorbikes. Lovely, peaceful and green in garden-like atmosphere. Rather small tables, won't be very convenient for very large group. Had khao soi moo tod for 69B. Taste quite delicious, but IMO too tailored for tourist (i.e not using enough spice). Large enough dish size, pretty presentation. Altogether worth the price.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The first restaurant where I tried the famed Khao Soi when I touched down in Chiang Mai and it certainly didn’t disappoint! Their northern style Thai curry noodles had the right amount of spices and coconut milk added. The portion of cooked and fried noodles are just nice too. Complete the meal with their roselle tea and mango sticky rice for dessert 👍

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Costs a little more than a roadside stall (it's a restaurant, so...) but it is totally worth the extra cash. First place I take people when they arrive in town.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Great food at reasonable prices , friendly service. Nice outdoor seating. Portion sizes are good. Must go early or long queue.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Local friends recommended this restaurant and they were absolutely the best Khao Soy. The broth is full of coconut flavor without the overwhelming heat as other places have. The chicken thigh is huge and price is reasonable for the portion size. Chicken satay was just as good and flavorful. Service was fast, friendly, and efficient.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I don’t really like Kao Soi that much but the pork one was pretty good. Still has a kick to it but it’s also a little sweet. I also had fried worms which were good. Their chicken satay was delicious. Their spring rolls were yummy too!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This is the 3rd time we had KaoSoy in Northen Thai. This restaurant is one of the best ! Both the KaoSoy and Satay chicken taste very good and we will go again next time when we came to Chiang Mai !

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The Kao Soy was pretty good, with a large selection. There’s a nice indoor air conditioned area as well as a patio. I thought the prices were reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Very good joint, popular for locals and tourists. Famous for its northern Thai style noodles. Will certainly want to go back.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Nice dining area with indoor and outdoor seating, friendly staff. The kao soy soup is amazing, especially the beef variation

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Food is great and not expensive. Service is good. Drink is varieties and located is centre of Nimman area

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Khao soy was very tasty! Spring rolls were fresh and crispy and tasted a lot like soul food for me. Service was exceptionally fast at seating us and food was quick although the check took a little while to eat. The food is dense so make sure you don’t have a ton or you’ll be due for a nap!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Not a bad place to go if you would like some local food. But this is more like a place where the younger generation of the locals will hang, rather that a family meal place. Food is a bit more spicy than in Bangkok, but their interesting way of making the northern style Kao Soy noodle is pretty good.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Our local tour guide knew we wanted to eat some awesome khao Soy so be dropped us off here for dinner. We were the only Westerners there and had no problem getting a table in the outdoor patio. The four of us each ordered a different version of the khao Soy and we all LOVED it! Such amazing food, in a charming atmosphere, for such little money!!!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This place was packed for lunch when I went. I had to wait maybe 10mins, but it was worth the wait cus the khao soy was bomb dot com!! I slurped every bit off that bowl. I also ordered chicken sauté and the peanut sauce is so good with it. Food yummy, but the service was kinda slow. They seemed too busy with every thing else and I felt like they kinda forgot about me. Maybe you’ll be lucky!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This restaurant served the best Khao Soi I have ever had! The restaurant itself is very nice and has a beautiful patio. It is very popular, but we came around 6pm and got a table right away before it became super crowded.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Nice setting. Staff was good but a little spaced out. No waiting Saturday night 7 pm - good table. Kao Soi was excellent! We also had the red curried chicken with mushrooms (very good), Northern Thai sausages (so-so) and stir fried fried kale (fantastic). With 2 beers and a club soda, cost was 575B. Bit too pricey vis-a-vis what's around, but we were looking for good kao soi and we got it!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We order shrimp khao soy and pork khao soy. The pork is better. The coconut broth with the spices and egg noodles and crunch of crispy noodles on top sing melodies in your mouth. Just enough spice to make you feel a little heat but not to make you sweat. Worth the wait- which was not long at all.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Food is tasty and reliable, service needs to be improved as staff are slow and sometimes rude. They look like they don’t want to be here and don’t mind the customers noticing. Q is long because the staff do not clean tables quickly or take much notice of what is going on. Food can come out wrong also.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Place has a good food! Kao Soy is good and the appetizers too! The place was full but the waiting time as 5 to 10 min. Food was served pretty quick. 4 dishes 380 bath.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I tried many Kao soi places in Chiang Mai, and this was easily considered decent. However, it was too pricey (~70BHT), you could have easily got it at 40BHT for something way better at Kao Soi Mae Sai

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We were recommended to go here for northern Thai food by another Thai restaurant owner up the road. We were not at all disappointed! Classic food, with imagination and flavours to die for. All served up by a great team of warm people.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Best Khao soi I’ve had. Try the beef! It’s normally served with chicken, but the beef was done so well I could cut it w my spoons. Definitely worth checking out.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Please take note that you will Need to take queue number when you reach the restaurant. Crowded most of the time. As for the noodle, it is delicious. Just that the soup given is not as much as compare to other places I have been too. Satay is great too.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Service is spotty, waitress was friendly, a slightly long wait time for food, food is sent to the table in spurts such that half the ordered dishes are delivered and the other half 20 minutes later - perhaps it was a busy 3 pm for them. Kao Soy was not bad, Green Curry chicken was nice, fried spring rolls a tad oily. Desserts are underwhelming.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: KhaoSoi is good, lots of topping you can choose which different from other shop. Other food is just okay. Nice staff and good service.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I visited with my parents, and it's our first Kao Soi. The Kao Soi was so delicious, and the place was clean. However, the staffs had a careless attitude.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Place truly is incredible. Recommended by a local and completely worth the Uber to the other side of town. Ordered Khao Soi with fried pork, spring rolls, and coconut. Fyi, if you can't handle any level of spice, place may not be for you. If you can handle a little spice, should be just fine. Dishes cost around 80 bhat, menu in English.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This is a bit touristy catering to both Thai and western customers. However they have a full selection of northern dishes, including nam neo, nam law, gang kae, Khao soi, sai ooa, nam prik nom, ong, kha, boo, etc. Very good flavor with A/C room inside.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Live in Thailand for 10 years, went with thai friends ... speechless! there is no difference between each kind of Khao soi… all with the same curry soup. and the soup too watery, lots of MSG…. Not for local …. Don't know why so many people.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We had a very good khao soi. The place is amazing. The waiter was not as polite as he should be plus they don’t serve bottled water. That’s why I gave 4/5. But I will come again for sure.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Loved this dish! Kao soy is classic in chiang mai, had it once but this time it was better. I would recommend to come here and eat...

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I tried this place based on a recommendation of the Thai owner of the condo i am renting. She said it was the best Kao Soy in the area. I ordered the Kao Soy He'd (northern thai noodle with mushrooms). Having never tried this type of soup, i have nothing to compare it to. However, it was delicious. I also had the tempura shrimp, and for dessert the sweet vermicelli with coconut milk. Service was fast (there was a line when i arrived but it went quickly), and even though most staff didn't speak English, the menu was detailed enough i could pick and point. Few foreigners were there when i was there, so must be appreciated by most locals...so my experience was way different from other recent review. If there weren't so many other places to try, i would go back. I thought the prices were very reasonable. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Nice atmosphere , clean restroom. Kaosoi is not good for me as a Thai but should be ok for foreigners or tourist. I don't see much Thai or local here.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Perfect taste for people who cannot eat hot & spicy. One of my favorite restaurants when I visit Chiangmai. Kao Soy Nimman is easy to eat and delicious. And I like Kao Soy Moo Yor .

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: One of the best if not the best Khao Soi I've had in Chiang Mai. It was fantastic. Slightly more expensive than the average Thai place at 79 baht, but I had it with Northern sausage.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Nice restaurant as feels like dinning in home. Recommend all expats and tourists to come here. All menus so awesome !!! I've tried all ^_^ Nice and friendly staffs there too. Wanna try some differrent as contemporary mixed and match food style with delicious. You dont't miss this restuarant !!!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Noodles were very tasty in a delicious curry broth. The serving size was a bit small and the 20thb can of coke was a tiny size also although it was supplied with an abundance of ice to go with it. Overall a tasty meal.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We loved the outside setting, really chilled! It was our first time having Kao Soy and we really enjoyed it. I struggle to eat any type of spicy food, but this was enjoyable (even though towards the end of my dish my mouth was burning up), then again this is related to personal taste. The pork was delicious. Nice place to go to for a quick lunch!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I went here twice with my wife, this place it's really good and super cheap ;) Highly recommended, if you are visiting this town .

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I went back and it was even better, probably wasn't as hung over this time. This northern Thai dish has curry soup base, various meats to choice from, crunchy pickled vegetables and fried wanton-skin garnish. This place just gets it all right. The locals love it. I love it. Simple local food at its best. A must try for those traveling through Chiang Mai. Many other restaurants have this noodle soup dish on the menu but none are quite like Kao Soy Nimman.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Yummy and Clean. You must try khaosoy.very delicious!! Deep fried spring rolls too!! Not too expensive 😋😋

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Thoroughly enjoyed our meal here. Great vibe and ambiance and tables inside and outdoors depending on your preference (bring mosquito spray if outdoors) Lots of things to try on menu and one of the best meals (value and flavour which we enjoyed in Chiang Mai) Service could have been friendlier. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: The price is quite cheap but the store looks fancy. The kao soy just tastes good ad it looks. The coconut juice was served with a whole intact peel-off coconut pulp!!! And very thick!!! This is the first time I see a coconut juice like this, do worth a try~

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Very tasty Khao Soi! They have about 8 different varieties with vegetarian options that include egg Khao Soi and mushroom Khao Soi. Great environment and very friendly staff. A must try for sure!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We had a great dinner at Kao Soy Nimman. I chose to try the namesake dish, Kao Soy. That is a delicious noodle soup with extra crispy fried noodles on top. The staff are extremely attentive and willing to help you with your order.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Not that bad Kao Soy noodle with northern sausage. Well mix and match! Price is reasonable 65-75 THB for noodle. Good location in town. They do have many thai food and northern food. Restaurant is located in center (small Soi), and easy to find. Last order at 7.30pm .Try it!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Just my kind of place. Authentic as locals highly recommended the restaurant. Wide variety of different kao soy. Other tasty northern Thai dishes for nibbling. I had the combination kao soy, good first choice. Not a dive, but definitely a local feel & experience.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Had lunch here as it was recommended for Khai soi. The Khai soi was just ok and I missed the finer flavours. It was more a stomach filling meal than an experience. Also tried the shredded jackfruit which I had never had before - unusual taste but quite good. Service was excellent and with a smile. In the end the food wasn't so much to my liking so I guess it's just a personal thing. Restaurant was crowded for sure - and the price is unbelievably low.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Definitely one of our favorite spot in Chiang Mai. The Khao Soi was truly delicious (we had it with fried pork and with sausage and both were very tasty) and very fairly priced!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: My family and I enjoyed the Kao Soy here. Simple and very tasty. They have a good selection of other simple dishes, price is very reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This was a good find - a number of different types of Kao Soi and not just chicken or beef - Kao Soi was okay good. Atmosphere is fine indoors and outdoors. Service is good too, thanks! Best highlight was the peeled coconut drink we had after the dinner - strongly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Kao soi was just average but they offered a variety of khao soi not limited to chicken or beef. There are also deep fried pork and omelet khao soi as well. The price is not very expensive.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Great food, especially if you wish to try some more local northern thai dishes instead of the classic thai dishes served elsewhere and very reasonable prices.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Restaurant is providing really nice food. You have the choice of Khao soy and also have one bigger bowl which 5 ingredient can be choose. Staff are friendly and the price is pretty cheap for restaurant. Easy to go there by Tuk tuk

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We randomly came across this restaurant from Google map. Their Kao soi was very good, think soup and tasty. We tried something else as well but it was not good. We only like Kao soi. Open only during the day.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This is one of the few places where you can find dinner if you want the northern specialty know soi (spelled different ways). It's a hearty flavorful noodle soup in a rich creamy curry broth. We tried the vegetarian option wth oyster mushrooms but you can get pork, chicken, squid, and shrimp if you want. I did not see tofu on the menu. The calamari is also good but a small portion. They have soft drinks, beer, no wine. There are curries and other dishes also which we didn't try. The staff are kind but English is not really spoken. We pointed, that worked. Really inexpensive, and a fun district with many bars, restaurants, places for massage, etc, on the west side of Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: This place is nice and decoration. The food is little salt taste and the price is ok. The service is fine.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We stumbled upon this little gem by accident and enjoyed it so much we are back for a second visit. The Thai green chicken curry is the best I've ever had.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Sampling many different Kao Soi dishes around town I figured it was time to try Kao Soi Nimman. They have a variety of different ones to choose from. I went with the northern Thai style sausage which was great. Nice amount of spicy, mid sized portion. 75 baht prices it slightly higher than a true local Kao Soi stall but the atmosphere is pleasant and the staff are friendly.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: I was recommended to try the local regional delicacy Kao Soi. I went for the Kao Soi green chicken curry. Really liked the noodles, including the fried noodle garnish. The noodles are like Italian tagliatelle, bit of a bite to them and not at all slimy. Very reasonably priced too. Chicken livers added to the chicken were not to my taste. Staff very friendly, attentive but not in your face.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We tried Kao Soi Nimman through a delivery service, so I can only speak to the food, not the service. I'd never had kao soi before, but it's quickly become one of my favorite Thai dishes! We also tried their garlic pork and garlic shrimp. Both were delicious, but VERY garlicky. There sticky rice seemed a little old (definitely could have been because of delivery) and the green curry was not as flavorful as others I've had in the city.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Read a review from someone living in Asia 20 years. Was not disappointed. Had dinner one night, had to go back the next week for lunch. Had khao soi twice, recommend with tasty northern sausage. Ordered crispy calamari, was excellent! It is a favorite, so I tend to judge a bit harshly. Can't wait to have it again. Very inexpensive too. Little to no English spoken but who cares?

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: After having numerous bowls of this northern Thailand dish, I found this place to be rather average for the inflated price. I've had bigger and better bowls at smaller family run restaurants. I order the squid Khao soy and it had four pieces of squid and not a lot of noodles. Was charged for ice also which was lame. Two bowls of soups, one bottle of water and ice = 190 B

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Located in the heart of the Nimman area, Kao Soi Nimman serves one of the most delicious Kao Soi that I've tasted in Chiang Mai. The menu has a wide variety of Thai food to choose from. I had Kao Soi with northern Thai sausage (pork) and crispy fried wonton. The service is great and fast. The restaurant has a beautiful and spacious garden area to sit at, as well as an A/C indoor area.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: First time we went by it said they open at 4pm when we arrived at 4pm it said 5pm. We took no chance and showed up at 6, waited for a table for 10min while drinking a slighty warm Singh at the price of 100baht. The kao soi was good but nothing more, one dish was minced porc in banana leaf was not good at all, bones everywhere and bad taste. Rest of the dishes was ok. Service was amateur on a good Day. More lukewarm Singha arrived after asking for it twice. Will not return.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We went there the other day in search for the best Khao Soi Soup in Nimman area. The Khao Soi soup is a bit more pricey here, about 50 - 75 Baht, usually you can buy the soup at most places for around 40 Baht or even for 30 Baht on street food stalls. But OK I was willing to pay more since the place is well located and looks nice with an inviting outdoor seating. We ordered 2 Khao Soi soup and a coke. The waiter brought the food within minutes. First I thought, wow, a big serving, but actually only the bowl is big, it's not even half filled with soup. So the portions are not that amazing. The crunchy noodles were delicious. But the soup itself was very oily and extremely sweet, definitely too sweet for me. I would give the soup 3 stars out of 5. So far the best Khao Soi soup I ate at Hong Tauw Restaurant on Nimman Road. Nevertheless, I would not eat again at Khao Soi Nimman. When we asked for the bill we had a very unpleasant surprise. The waiter charged us extra 15 baht for the ice cubes in our coke. Excuse me? I didn't even order ice cubes. I never paid anywhere extra for the ice cubes. Well, I swallowed my anger since I know it's not the waiters fault and paid for it. But this was enough to leave me angry and I decided I won't return to this place. Ever. Charging extra for ice is also a way of getting rid of your customers. Ridiculous! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: We were going to Thailand to see our son studying abroad, so we found some information in a New York Times Article about Chiang Mai. This restaurant is featured. It is a typical laid back open air place serving classic Kao Soi Noddles. Great eating and a cool part of town.

Restaurant_Name: ข้าวซอยนิมมาน
Detail: Have tried Kai Soi in alot of locations in my two years of living in Chiang Mai and have settled on this being the best. For those who don't know what Kao Soi is, its the northern laksa (with or without coconut milk), it is more earthy though than your singapore or malaysia laksa. It usually contains a chicken leg, but can also contain pork or beef. Kao Soi Nimman has a good variety of alternatives, make sure you put the pickle over the top. They also have a terrific selection of other Lanna specialties such as Hung Lay Curry (sweet pork curry), Naem (Fermented Sausage) with egg (Kai) and a traditional kantoke selection of assorted vegetables, sausage dipping sauce and sticky rice. Its directly opposite Beconfinno's. Read less

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: Chiang Mai is a local dining restaurant in the mainland area. Kunmoukijin has a good Google Reviews rating. A place where spices aren't strong and can be eaten comfortably. There is no air conditioning inside and no air conditioning outside. Most Thai food was available for order. It was especially delicious with Pudong, which came from the north. Morning Glory, you can't miss the steak. Most menus seem to be delicious, will be visited again on the next Chiang Mai trip.

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: This was the best Thai food we had on our trip. The place is cozy and the people are very nice! I recommend the Kao soi with chicken! We came back everyday for our time in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: Khun Mor is a very special place for us as we have been coming here since 2006 when we relocated to Chiang Mai first. Very good quality Thai food and air conditioned seating separated by a glass wall, as well as very cozy outdoor covered open area seating with water fountains and plants and all. Wooden work is exceptionally high quality and so is the team. Run by dedicated women staff especially this is always a good experience for us. Location is on Soi 17 of Nimmanhaemin, Strongly recommended. Salutes to its founders Read less

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: We went to the Khun Mor branch in Central Festival, Chiang Mai and were pleased with the overall experience.My fried fish with salad was perfectly cooked and the portion generous. My wife had the Lanna pork in soup which was also tasty. Service was fine - attentive without being intrusive. Prices, while not cheap, are fair.

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: Khun Mor is an established Thai restaurant located on Nimman Soi 17 close to the main Nimman Road.p, so location is good, and there is parking across the street. The restaurant is very spacious and comfortable with indoor and outdoor seating. The menu is extensive, with standard Thai dishes as well as a lot of Northern Thai specialties. There are a few set meals if you want to sample some different Lanna food. I thought the food quality was good, not great, and reasonably priced though some of the seafood dishes a bit pricey for the area. There is a good selection of drinks, including juices as well as cocktails, wine and beer. The staff were efficient and friendly. It’s a nice place for a good, reasonably priced Thai meal, though there are places with better food in ChiangMai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: I like this restaurant because it provides good combination sets of northern Thai food that are good for both foreigners and Thai people from other parts of the country who want to have a taste of local food. People who are already familiar with local food would also be satisfied with what they could have from their order. The location is very good, convenient, easy access from the main Nimman Road.

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: We were 5 so we ordered set menu which included 8 dishes and 1 dessert. We didn't wait long for the food although there were 9 diahes. The food was in average portion and tasty. We liked the mango salad most. Recommended!

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
Detail: This Restaurant is under rated in my opinion. I have been this restaurant for few time and every time meals been very good. Grilled Chicken and salads also Laap is very good. Also Crab neat fried rice is very good. This northern Thai restaurant is underrated. Prices are reasonable and staff are very friendly.

Restaurant_Name: คุณหมอคูซีน
