Detail: The salads here are really delicious and there are many options. I had the pumpkin soup that was yummy as well, although the price of my soup of a bit confusing on the menu. The dressing choices are unique and the 3 that I tried were all delicious.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I was not a fan of salad before I went to Salad Concept, but I'm a convert! Their mix and match style of salad creation, where you choose your own toppings and special additions, is really good and excellent value. The drinks are varied, all healthy and very tasty. There are also some FANTASTIC cakes and naughty snacks on offer. Also has free wifi. I now go there regularly.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The Salad concept is a good choice, the servings are large, tasty and the salad thanks to the turn over of food always fresh. The only drawback is the absent of trained staff, who at time seem surprised that cleaning of a greasy table is requested by the visitors. Service shows a bit lack of management as they rather stand around socializing instead of working, when or if this is attended the place will imporve significantly. It is a good choice for lunch or light dinner, Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I'm so glad I find it, favorite salad place in town, the salad is fresh, with lots of tuna and beans which are my favorite, the drinks are also very good, what's most pleasant is the price la, can never have enough of it.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Excelent idea of making of home made salads by order. They have a number of ingredients and you can create your own combination, the choise of dressings is also avalable. Also there is choise of cream soups and fresh herbal juices. Nice place both for vegetarian and non-vegetarian.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I had seen the pictures and comments of this place so many times. When I have a chance I wanna have my own experience. The main Salad dish is good, you pick what you like but I was expecting more variety of choices, like some fruits and dried fruits.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: no fruit options to put on the salads, no raisins. the "main veggies they give you to choose for free are all very basic like cucumber, corn, onions act and the fancy toppings are where they get you. the dressings are unique and creative, but not enough to get me to go back considering there are a lot of great organic restaurants and cafes that serve salads for half the price.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Just a really clean, delicious salad joint. Right off boutiquey Nimmanhaemin Road. They have yummy smoothies and a couple of good detox juices too. Plenty of seating inside and outside in the front and back. Lots of different options for types of lettuce, toppings, and dressings. Quick service.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The name had put me off this restaurant so I avoided it for a while but once I decided to give it a go I was surprised by how much I like the 'concept' and have been back many times. There are set choices, some tasty looking deserts and bits and pieces to choose from but it's the build your own salad that brings me back. That's something I never thought I'd say - salad isn't exciting really is it? You get a base of local leaves, then you choose 5 choices of things to build your salad - things like tomatoes, seeds, olives, peppers, onion, egg. You then get a variety of tasty dressings to choose from. This is for the set, cheap price you can then add on other bits and pieces - meat, cheese, potatoe salad etc for a small amount extra. You order by ticking off the items on your forms and just handing them over so it's easy and straightforward. It really is great to refine something to your tastes or fancies and the veg and other products are all really great quality. The surroundings are quirky and stylish and the place is popular and has a nice vibe. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Make your own salad? What I want in my salad? The salad concept is a nice place to be, the atmosphere is quite relaxing and gives me enough room to feel comfortable. The food and beverage is refreshing. Able to order what's on the menu as well as making your own. I feel it gives me the choices I want and the price is quite reasonable!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I go to Chiang Mai a lot and this is always my first stop, the freshest salads you'll find anywhere. Eclectic mix of patrons, locals and expats. Staff is young vibrant and friendly Thai who are always willing to help. Niemenheimen is the student area of Chiang Mai, away from the touristy places, real good vibes up and down the street, lots of nice bars off the main road also. Get away from it all, enjoy a healthy delicious meal. Recommend the build your own salad and banana bran shake.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Do not look further, if you are in Chiang Mai and really want to get a good salad just go to the salad concept. You came make your own salad or take one that is already on the menu. Their smooties are also pretty good and refreshing. Good qualitiy and resonable price !! You definitly to check this restaurant !

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The place is nice, service is quick and salads are delicious even though the selection is not vast. A good option is that you can actually select the ingredients of your salad and decide what dressing to put on. Good to go there once a week if you like greens. BTW...the salad come from hydroponic farms and it is not supposed to be fully organic

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Some of the reviews here are a little over the top. "The idea of being able to design your own salad is just brilliant!" OK maybe 20 years ago when that concept first started showing up everywhere in the states you could say it was brilliant. Not a brilliant idea. Not an original idea. It's fair to say that it was a good idea for CM. I eat here a couple times a week. You can get full for not a lot of money and feel like you did something somewhat healthy for yourself. The prices here are good. I usually pay under 100 baht for a nice salad. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: There is usually one or two things I don't like in salads so to make your own is genius! With loads of options to choose from, you end up with a huge plate of fresh amazingness all for roughly 100 baht! After almost giving up on finding pumpkin or butternut soup in Thailand, I was very happy to see they had it on their menu and even happier when it tasted great! They have a great variety of shakes which are very well priced and delicious! They make their own wheatgrass!!! And just as i'd given up finding good cake in Thailand, they have a lovely selection from root beer and devil's food cake to apple streudel and low fat diet vanilla pudding! The service was amazing, they had english menus with no grammar or spelling mistakes, the host was lovely and we got our food in record time despite the place being busy. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We went a few people, had a real nice salad. the place is nice, very Thai in its set up. The salads were large and fresh, many people. I would though say that a little more creativity can be applied in the whole place, but overall satisfied. worth trying inexpensive.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We ususally avoid anywhere in the hip and trendy areas as they are generally full of bright young things and overpriced. The Salad Concept is an exception. Thai friends took us along there and we took no convincing that it's a great restaurant. Choose from the standard menu salads or create your own culinary feast with different ingredients. The portions are huge, so definitely value for money. There is a good drinks menu as well and the fruit smoothies and health drinks are scrummy. There are desserts, but if you still have room, you've not eaten all your main meal! Ample parking in the soi at the rear.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Salad Concept is delicious and reasonably-priced. I live in Chiang Mai and go there at least once a week. They even let me bring my dog and sit out back. My dog loves lettuce. And so do I! The lettuce they use is clean and of many different varieties, so it's a good base. Then you get to pick your toppings and dressing. Everyone can find something they like. I take all of my friends there. the soups are tasty and the drinks are really healthy. I like their wheat grass and detox drinks, which are made with fresh-squeezed juices right there at the counter. Their smoothies are really good and healthy too. I end up usually ordering two or three drinks! And their cakes...wow. They're so delicious, but dangerously sweet. Luckily they have introduced lower-sugar options. Otherwise the healthy feeling you get from the salads gets replaced with a sugar high and then a sugar crash, which isn't fun. The only thing I generally dislike is the music, which is the usual Chiang Mai mix of covers of foreign pop songs sung by Asian singers. Last week they had smooth jazz, which was a nice change. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I arrived in Chiang Mai 2 weeks ago and have already been 5 times to Salad Concept. The idea of being able to design your own salad, and combine your favorite vegetables, toppings and dressings, is just brilliant! I was never a big fan of salads before but thanks to Salad Concept, I am realizing how yummy a healthy salad can be and how it can fill you up, totally guilt-free. I am planning on eating there as often as possible. Try the juices too, my favorite is the anti-cancer one that has wheatgrass. Anyway, rush to Salad Concept and get ready to become a happy Salad junkie.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I usually like salad and so when a friend recommended having Salad for lunch I was all out. At first glance the place appear to be posh and pricey, however when I saw the menu I was pretty surprised to see that most of the salad was around THB100 (approx. USD3) I chose to create my own salad which starts at THB50 including salad + 5 other ingredients and 1 of many dressings available. Being a fan of salmon, I asked them to add that to the salad for an extra cost. And the results can be seen in the picture. On the whole I really enjoyed my salad but felt that they were a bit low on salmon, which I paid extra for, and olives, but everything else was fine. I'd recommend anyone in the vicinity to check it out, they also have a soup selection and some healthy drinks. Really hoping they'd open a branch in Bangkok. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The salad is great. We ordered a caesar salad. But I think the ala carte ones look as fine. But the soup, we ordered a mushroom is watery, not thick. Perhaps it is for non fattening purpose, without milk? By the way, we got to know that the lady boss is a celebrity, pretty and helpful, very hands on.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: "Apple Joy" juice is very good. ^^ I like caesar salad!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Don't really like anything green but hey they got salads with loads of creamy sauce, bacon, potato's and put some extra beef and feta cheese... voila! Even meaterian will enjoy this stuff!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Iv been here many times and will continue to revist the salad concept because of its great atmosphere and amazing food. It's simple but delicious and it's good value. The best part is the fresh fruit shakes with their custom twists, I'd have to say one of the best shakes iv had in Thailand. The great thing about it is you can easily add additional toppings until your happy and full. What ever salad you get, try the pumpkin salad dressings because it is the best! The location is also amazing as its close to bars and other night spots. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I came across this "Gem" while I was walking on Nimmanhaemin Rd. I stayed for over a month in March of 2012. My condo was very near by and one day, tired of noodles, rice, etc. I noticed this restaurant. The concept is great, the food fresh and the portions are very big and filling. I became a "regular" eating there at least three times a week!! Many reviewers have written here, so I wont continue to embellish. More expensive than your average noodle-rice place, but very healthy, reasonably priced and nice change, I would highly recommend it. Read less
Date_Written: here, so I wont continue to embellish.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: This busy little spot on Nimmanhaemin is always packed to the rafters with locals and expats alike. It serves up lovely fresh salads with a range of different ingredients and some great smoothies too. Some friends believe its a little on the expensive side but for the huge servings, you can't go wrong! I've even bought one to take home to share with my partner before and we have both eaten til we are full.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We found this busy lunch bar - restaurant last year. Upon relocating to Chiang Mai it was our first stop for a cheap yet tasty and nutritious lunch. They've extended the indoors out toward the soi where outdoor seating didn't really work in this climate. The food, mainly exotic salads, is fresh and inexpensive from ~ 50 baht up. A Caesar salad costs just 85 baht. Desserts and cake are delightfully home-made and inexpensive.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The Salad Concept is the sort of place that it's easy to trick yourself into thinking is cool and "out of this world" and funky and all that other nonsense. Fact is, it serves salad and it charges you a relative fortune for it. It's not a vegetarian restaurant (did anyone notice the steak and bacon and chicken and fish options?) and it's not even especially good value (given where it is, you can bet the rent's not cheap) but it will do if you don't feel like Thai food or some disgusting "English Breakfast" again. My main gripe with the place is that the service is absolutely terrible. For a place that runs off little bits of paper with checkmarks on them, it shouldn't be much of a challenge to be a waiter there. Somehow though, it still seems to be. All the waiters there need to do is take a piece of paper from here to there and then bring a drink and some food. I had no idea that that was so difficult. I really wouldn't make this a regular stop on my eating itinerary for the sole reason that the service is just shamefully awful. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Huge salads with lots of goodies for a good price. Some of the best desserts in Chiang Mai as well. Love this place. Always packed!!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Very good salads and smoothies with the wait staff using a system where the customer fills out a request form for his particular salad. An excellent idea because the staff speaks only a smattering of English. They don't seem very well trained at all but there are a lot of them. The Thai public seems to like the concept as well though it attracts a large proportion of tourists. It's positioned right on Niemman Road. It's a healthy option in an area where there are not that many.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We're in love with this restaurant - the salads are always fresh and yummy. You get a little notepad on which you mark which ingredients you'd like - broccoli, almonds, carrots, cucumber, tomato, sweetcorn, pumpkin, kidney beans, etc... - 5 ingredients are included in the 50 Baht version, every additional ingredient is 5 Bht. Cheese & tofu cost more (20 Bht) and you choose between several dressings. The portions are always very generous. Even though the place is called Salad Concept, they also have a good selection of fresh muffins, cakes and deserts. The fruit shakes are also good and the decor of the restaurant is nice - overall highly recommended if you like salads (or cakes!).

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We've eaten at this relatively new restaurant 3 times and find it a refreshing change of pace. Up to now, salads have not been easy to come by in Thailand, short of hotels and upscale restaurants. This place has hit a very responsive chord with health conscious Thais and is always busy. They offer a couple of fixed salads like a Caesar (my wife thinks its yummy) but their specialty is a "choose your own toppings" salad. It comes with a few delicious different mixed greens and you pick 5 toppings, ranging from radishes to olives (hard come by in Thailand). You can beef the salad up for a surcharge, with chicken, bacon, tuna and other selections. While the salads are excellent the dressings tend to be more Thai style than falang which means they are on the sweet side. My favorite is the Thai mint dressing which is delightfully tangy and not sweet. You can also order rolls and/or soups. Service is "spotty", your order doesn't always come out correct. You have to fill out an order form for your waiter. All in all it's a very pleasant change. Prices are reasonable. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Fresh organic produce, 5 selections and a dressing all for 50baht. I eat here up to 3 times per week and enjoy it every time. For low fat high flavor option try the carrot dressing; it is garlicy and rich without being full of oil.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Great place for a change of pace after eating exclusively spicy Thai food for four days. The salad is extremely crisp and fresh. Large servings and reasonable prices. Clean restaurant with modern decor. Would definitely go back again.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: If this restaurant were closer to my home I'd be there every day. I love the idea of choosing my own ingredients and then having the waiter bring my "custom made" salad to the table. The vegetables and other ingredients are fresh and flavorful...and their fruit smoothies are delicious as well.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Great fresh ingredients, innovative dressings. Although it sounds like it might be purely vegetarian, many salads include meat and hearty vegetables. Nice ambiance; gets very crowded around noon.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Not too much to say other than this is a great place for a good lunch if you want a salad. They have some really good, healthy smoothies and good desserts too!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I love Japanese dressing with my own creation of salad. I don't like soup and dessert, they are not doing well. Salad is superb!! A must try.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The food was delicious (chooses your own salad ingredients from a list or one of their special salads) as well as fast and freshly made. The highlight was the gorgeous passionfruit soda with real passfruits! yum! staff were a little disinterested and slow to serve even though there were at least 9 members of staff!!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: If you want a salad this is the place to go. You can pretty much make your salad exactly how you want it and for a decent price. Some okay desserts and drinks too but for sure the mainstay are the salads as per the name.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Salads are nice and they have a decent variety. Healthy yes, but depends on what dressing you put on your salad some of them are jam packed full of calories. They also do great smoothies. Read a few reviews that the staff aren't friendly. This is definitely not my experience as I have always found them to be cheerful and have eaten there on many occasions. One of the few places in the Nimman area that has off-street parking which is great as this area is terrible for parking. One small criticism is that they do have a habit of closing randomly, so wise to check they are open first. One all health salads (watch the dressings) and reasonably priced. Oh, and in and outdoor seating.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: My 1 st time eating at salad concept since every time I see the place is pack I don't wanna bother waiting...for salad...you know what I mean! Ha ha! the food was great (I have beef salad and passion fruit soda) and the price was relatively cheap for such good quality stuffs they use ;-) will return!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: After 20 days of delicious thai cuisine, there comes a time when a colorful bowl of fresh salad is all one craves! Their large selection of raw ingredients is a refreshing alternative to stir/deep-fried dishes and spicy delicacies, their juices (with promising names like 'Detox') are wonderful and the presence of locals, students, tourists and expats makes this place really lively and always packed!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The title says it all - healthy, fresh, fast, cheap, and tasty! Lots of great options for customizable salads. Reminds me of restaurants you'd find in Los Angeles or Malibu, but at a fraction of the cost. Great, fast service, and a nice change when you've had enough typical Thai food in the area. Went twice in one week and would probably go every week if we lived in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: in the upcoming nimman area. a very nice idea for a restaurant / coffee. the "do it yourself " salads are a great idea and very delicious. nice variety of drinks and smoothies. compared to some earlier reviews i think that the staff had learnt a little bit. go there!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The salads and soup is good here, but the service is hit and miss. My food took a long time and then I got the wrong salad. When I did get the correct one, it was good. The mushroom soup also is tasty. They try to be sort of a healthy place, but I can suggest to take out that chemical laced odor remover air stick. It is unhealthy to breathe those fumes. Maybe put in some pieces of lemon or lime, or potpourri, or aroma oil burner , etc. These are very effective and not toxic. Which I hope is your concept.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: For everyone,all salad ,smoothies, cake are very nice and reasonable price.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: The salad concept is extremely popular place for not only westerners but many young Thais. The restaurant has been operating for a little over 1 year and it has already expanded to meet the high demand of its diners. I think it is a bit boring but the salads are decent and you can "customize" your own salad to your liking. The prices are very reasonable so you can't really go wrong here, and it feels good to get a healthy, light meal. The only negative aspect is the poor service of the staff, but the diners don't seem to be phased by this poor attitude. I guess they are not there for good service but to get a decent meal at a reasonable price. The restaurant is located in an area growing in popularity and is considered the up-scale part of Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I don't normally eat salad but I love it here. The concept of this place is brillaint. Huge selection of healthy food, selection of topping to go with your salad, wonderful salad dressing. I had a big bowl of salad with marinated beef and a glass of smoothie for THB 150. It was really nice and not expensive at all. Only one bad point I found is the service. I agreed with many reviews here that staffs are not so friendly. They hardly smile to customers. More trainings must be given to them. Although the place is super nice, bad service always drives customers away. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Choose your own hydroponically grown veggies plus an assortment of meats and cheeses (including feta!!!), whole wheat roll, or soup. SO delicious, wonderful dressings, and friendly staff. The coffee is a bit expensive, but it's nice to meet friends for coffee and cake if you're not in the mood for salad.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: We are not salad affectionados, but we enjoyed the concept very much. The place is designed mostly around building your own, how you like it, which is fun. If you're looking for a recommendation, they have a few "house" salads and usually a daily special as well, but its not extensive. Given the variety, it will take a few trips to figure out our favorite mix of very fresh greens, scores of toppings, and meat selection (if you choose). We tried the house beef salad which was great and built our own salmon. The portion of the salmon was small in comparison, albeit typical. The Soy dressing was great, the French was veeery vinegary. Price and portion was good to great, we left full and satisfied. The smoothies were well priced, large and very filling as well. The service, as other reviews, left some to be desired. In ALL Thai places we're always greeted with at minimum the appropriate hello, how are you in Thai. Not here, nothing. But, we found a seat, menus were brought, and order was taken all promptly. Without any words exchanged - nor smiles or anything. Official closing time being 10pm, all the other patrons had left by half past 9 and ALL the tables/chairs were already stacked, ready to shut the doors. Feeling uncomfortable now, thankfully we'd finished our meal, we got the check and left. Summary = what everyone else says...good to great salads, poor but not horrible service. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Always fresh and tasty vegetables. Nice system of ordering by ticking off a list to get just the mix of veggies you want. Many home made fresh dressings to choose from. Resonable priced. Nice coffees and cakes for dessert. Waiters not always very attentive to the customers. Can be difficult to get their attention.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: BAD service pure and simple. No matter what you can say about the food, the decor, the atmosphere, etc., crappy service is ALWAYS a deal breaker. Good food eaten in a bad mood is not worth any amount of money in my book. Menus weren't offered. They couldn't get a drink order right. No smiles. No service. Waitstaff standing around staring blankly at the walls as they watch the paint dry or faces glued to their mobile phones as we waited for someone to take our order. IT wasn't the waitstaff from hell, but they could have been sooooo much better with just a little bit of training. THEY DIDN"T KNOW THE MEANING OF S-E-R-V-I-C-E. Oh well. hope they get it right sometime in the future, but I highly doubt it. The staff are young wannabees without attention to detail or a service mind. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Made-to-order fresh salads with a variety of ingredient choices! Also great fresh juices, wheatgrass, etc. Indoor and outdoor seating, very nice.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Create your own salad with fresh veggies, free 5 additional toppings, such as cashew nuts, cherry tomatoes, sweetcorn, carrots, croutons , and more. You also have a choice of one chargeable meat of your choice such as grilled beef, tuna, salmon etc. Lastly, you have a choice of salad dressing. A great healthy way to create your own tasty salad. The vegetables are fresh and crunchy, dressing is tasty, the meat is of good quality for the price you pay. Staffs are very efficient and friendly, willing to provide you with all the details you need. The one waitress brought us the wrong dish, but quickly checked the order and changed it for us immediately. The ambience of the restaurant is just right, not too crowded nor noisy, even during the busy time of the day. They also serve cakes, healthy drinks, teas and coffee, to compliment the delicious salad of your choice. Highly recommended place, we'll definitely go back! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: I'm prompted to review this place because I found myself there almost every day while in Chiang Mai for a week. The healthy salads are huge - for only $1-3 you can fill up and feel great. A wonderful 'concept' for the many westerners who visit or live in Chiang Mai and crave crunchy, fresh veggies once in while. If you're on Nimmanhemin Street look for the green salad concept sign and enjoy!

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Very inexpensive, tons of topping choices, a lovely atmosphere...we have visited many times in the last few months and have never been disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: เดอะ สลัด คอนเซ็ปท์
Detail: Just at the corner of Soi 13 Nimmanhaemin road, you won't miss it. They serve salads & dressings, juices, coffee & desserts. Nice Place, cozy room, healthy organic salads & juices. run by 2 beautiful sisters who just love eat salad. Try their famous Tofu Salad with japanese soy sauce dressing, 85 baht ( you can also change to another dressing if you like). You can also make your own salad start from 50 baht with home-made dressing of your choice (soy sauce, thousand islands, pumpkin, tofu, tamarind, etc) .Their cakes are also low fat. Close on every 2nd wednesday of the month Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went to this restaurant based on location which was close to hotel and high ratings, and it did not disappoint. The restaurant had indoor and outdoor seating, but there is no walls so basically all open. There was a small line, had to sign in for seating and it moved quickly. There was English announcements on number that you get when checking in. The service was prompt, and there was English spoken by waiters, guessing that there are plenty of tourists eating there, but I also noted there were plenty of local Thai families eating there, which testify to the authenticity and the good pricing of the food. We ordered some BBQ sausage (a must order), pork ribs, all very tasty and excellent. Some soups and stir-fried vegetables, and pork dishes, again, all tasty and aromatic. Pricing was excellent. Would highly recommend this place! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I had a hard time finishing my dish but my colleagues enjoyed the restaurant so maybe I'm the problem lol A lot of people have to wait in line, not too expensive.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Nimman area is akin to being in ThongLor in Bangkok. Its a hip trendy area. We stumbled upon this dinner place by chance, as we were walking on the side streets. This place dont take reservations, and you need to stand in line and await for a table. They serve up northern thai cuisine, and not your usual bangkok food fare. We were there around 6pm, and there was already a line forming. We were no. 6 in line for a party of 3. After 45 minute wait, we were given our table. As we walk in, food aroma wafted through the air from the kitchen as it no doubt cooked a storm for the burgeoning dinner crowd. A good mix of locals, expats. an d tourists were in the restaurant. Food quality is good. Value for money. Ambience is cozy even thought it is like an outside sitting arrangement on bench seats. Many young waitering staff who added buzz and fast service | Overall a place to be seen, i guess. and to post a pic that you've been there. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We had the best grilled pork in Chiangmai here and its local fare was really good as well. They had a specialty, steamed crab roe of some sort but we didn’t quite like that. It was too creamy and too strong. Among all the known restaurants offering northern Thai cuisines, this place is one of my top picks.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: A restaurant for good Thai/Asian cuisine. From the outside inconspicuous but cozy inside. The portions are not too large, which is good, since you can try several dishes. The taste is perfectly fine and authentic. I like it quite a bit here. I'm sure I'll be back. The service fast and attentive. Food also comes very quickly. I can only recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Visit this place with my GF and friend to try this restaurant after many reviews watched & read. Honestly, the foods are in mixed some are good (Northern Thai Style Hors d'oeuvres, and all their grill dishes) some are so so as there are many Northern Style restaurants in Chiang Mai that serves more delicious one such as Northern Thai Style Minced Meat Spicy Salad (ลาบเมือง). However, it's good to tried and known. From my point of view, this place is good for bringing foreigner friend(s) who want to try local Northern Tha Style foods as it's not THAT spicy and once you are more familiar with spicyness, you have tons of local restaurants to explore then.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There can be many reasons for a restaurant to be "good". One is popularity and Tong Tem Toh is very popular - it's a great place for Thais to drink beer, socialize and eat cheap but okay food. It's definitely not a good culinary experience unless you like your mushrooms hard and Snakehead Fish Whatever..

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Traditional food with good taste! And reasonable price. The restaurant is famous but quite small so you have to wait in line for this restaurant. We recommend you to come early and do not come when you are already hungry! (quite small portion)

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: First time trying Lana food. The restaurant came recommended by a local Thai who had eaten there a few times. Prices are very reasonable and portion sizes generous. If it's your first time, be aware there is a queue. When you arrive, inform the lady at the entrance and you get given a number. The numbers are shouted in Thai except yours which will be in English if you are a non Thai. There is also a screen which shows the number in case you are wondering. The menu is also in English. Payment can be made with mastercard. Just be aware, they take your credit card away to the cashier. So, if you are worried, walk with the waiter to the cashier.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Best food we have had in Chiang Mai! It’s very busy so there is a queuing system, we waited about 30 minutes but went around the corner and grabbed a beer. The food was so so good that we ordered a couple dishes twice. Well worth the wait.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: local restaurant in Chiang Mai province The food menu has a variety of delicious flavors to choose from. This shop is also popular with tourists. Both Thais and foreigners

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is absolutely one of the most rated restaurant in Chiang Mai I ever been. Food is awful, after 40 mins waiting for the table, I ordered a mush room soup, northern sausage and fried vegetables with egg, beer. All the dishes are way much salty expect beer. Mushroom is too old to chew, sausage doesn’t taste good as well, just could not finish the meal. Not original northern food. Popular among tourist.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Soh is a traditional Northern Thai restaurant with quite extensive menu selections. Ambience is pretty basic, foods are quite delicious with reasonable prices.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I love this restaurant because it has a lot of food to eat especially local food that I haven't tasted before.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We had been given a recommendation for this restaurant thru our hotel and since they had been spot on the entire time with their recommendations we thought that we would try it out. First of all we ordered the hors d’oeuvres plate. Most of it was cold. Maybe that was how it was supposed to be but I’ve just never ate cold sausage or whatever that meat was. Then came our dinner. My husband had soup with pork and something else and it was average. I had curry chicken with rice. My chicken was ice cold. I called over the server and asked for it to be redone. They stuck it in the microwave and brought it back to me. Then when we went to pay our bill we were told they didn’t accept credit cards. I never saw a sign that said that. Luckily we had enough baht to cover it. Our bill was 788 baht. We gave them 1000 baht and they brought us back 112 baht. I called over our server and said you shorted us 100. He took it back up to the cash register and I could see him talking or maybe it was laughing with the person who was running the register. Eventually we got or correct change. Overall experience was less than par. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Définitely a restaurant for the people who are living in Chiang Mai. Popular, moderne, clean and local flavours... Full of local flavours. Don t expect a cuisine for tourist and try to be local without complex and... Spice 😁. The service is perfect, restaurant airy and really clean. If you want be far from the touristic Road.. Go for it

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Passed by here on a tour and it looked really lively! I went and forgot to tell them my spice tolerance is low (my goof) but from what I did enjoy I liked it. Seems like a local spot!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Recommended by a Singaporean friend, this was a great place for lunch. Superb flavour and texture. Fresh and healthy ingredients. Very reasonably priced. Superb.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This restaurant serves Northern Thai cuisine and located in the Nimman vicinity. The ambient is relaxing and set in an open air house. Their specialty include vegetables with chilli dip, Northern sausages, clear spicy soup, fried pork belly and also the Burmese pork curry. All the food was very delicious. The quality of the food was very good. The portion of the food was just right and the price too. The only Northern dish missing from the menu is the Khao Soi noodle. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This restaurant was recommended by my friend who lives in Chiang Mai and she did not let me down. I was there in year 2018. All of the menus were very good. Food was served very fast whether there were many customers in the restaurant. In the menu included many Northern food. my most favorite menu is Northern Larb. It was so flavorful and you can request your level of spiciness. Moreover, the price is also reasonable which is not too expensive and the atmosphere in the restaurant feel relax and decorated with northern style.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I went there with friends for the first time. I would say that I expected that there was a very long queue before our arrival and it was indeed. We waited approximately 30 minutes for a table even though we preserved a queue on a mobile application. But it's worth the wait! We ordered A LOT OF FOOD and it turned to be very delicious! I don't care whether locals have been telling me that this restaurant serves northern food that is modified to fit the taste of non-locals. It was indeed a blissful meal. You should try. I think you won't be disappointed at all. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Excellent value for top notch Northern Thai dishes in a relaxed and beautiful open air restaurant. Very popular with Thais. The Burmese curry with fatty pork is to die for.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: After a day in Chiangmai, we are hunger for northern food and we found Tong tem toh, The northern food restaurant near our place. Upon arrived the restaurant at around 1800 hours. It was packed with local and foreigner. Picked a queue and after a while we were seated. Foods served just after 5 mins due to a pre-order we made while waiting for seats. Environment is acceptable while foods, both quality and quantity are more than expected with reasonable price. Our dinners cost only 460 Bht (more than we can eat). Highly recommened.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Excellent northern food at very reasonable prices . There is very often a queue to get in . The decor is nothing fancy - just a basic covered outdoor restaurant but the food is good . Well worth going to if you are in the area

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Had to queue for 40mins even though I had made advance reservation via QueQ app. But I must say the staff estimates the queue time very well, so if you might want to ask them before walking away upon seeing the snaking queue. Only ordered 3 meat dishes shared among 2 of us as we weren't very hungry. Food came pretty fast and were hot. Some staff spoke better English than others. Accept credit card for payment. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is popular place for Northern Thai food. The food is great, the atmosphere is relax and the price is not expensive when compared to other establishment of this level.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Food was great, price is reasonable, just the queuing time is a bit long. It's a place you can try all the famous local dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We came here because I love Burmese style pork and had heard this restaurant serves it done well. Overall, I liked Tong Tem Toh but do wish their food was a little spicier and the prices a little lower. They’re located in what seems to be a very trendy neighborhood in Chiang Mai. We didn’t have to wait long, even though we didn’t have the QueQ app downloaded. Food was definitely tasty but my expectations were too high perhaps. The fried pork in vegetables was tastier than the Burmese style pork curry.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This one is definitely one of the most hyped restaurants in town. There is always long line in the front. To avoid queuing and better save your time, you can download the app QueQ and have your number in advance and get notification when your turn is coming. Food was served very quickly and the portions were a bit small so you can try many different dishes. Their menu is available in the queuing area so you can get your picks while waiting. We had a great lunch here and the price is surprisingly cheap.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We have been meaning to try this for a while due to walking passed it several times and noticing a line. Finally tried it to day and we ordered an assortment of things that were reasonably priced for the area, but their menu and staff didn't really give much help so we ended up with 2-3 dishes which we were pretty unsatisfied with. Ultimately we ended up eating 2 lunches because the soup my girlfriend ordered had very little edible meat/veggies in it with most of them being hard. I might try it again if I know what to order ahead of time, but we felt so rushed through the whole thing I left feeling pretty dissatisfied.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: On October 28,2019 we lunch at Tong Temtor because opposites of the hotel we order around 3-4 menu every menu very well 👏👏👏 #local food

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: After reading all the rave reviews and witnessing huge queues even outside of the usual lunch and dinner times, I decided to give it a go, as it was a short walk from our hotel. There is no clear signage in English around the restaurant, and it was only because of the queues (and google map) that we discovered it. It is located next to a shop called Fairytales. We reached there around 1.30pm and there was a small queue. We approached the guy at the door and he quickly punched out a waiting number. After about 15minutes we were seated and service was quick. We ordered an assortment of vegetarian and non-veg dishes and soup. All the flavours were just average- there wasn't anything stunning or mind-blowing that earns it any high-flying sobriquets. For eight items we paid under 600 baht- good value for money. But definitely nothing that justifies the crazy long queues at all times. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Somehow this establishment manages to get customers queueing up most evenings . Maybe it is the illusion of 'real northern cooking'. We have been there twice inside a few days. First time was very disappointing but I thought they (or we) had a bad day off so we tried again (all those people queuing up surely can't be wrong?). We had 6 dishes and only one was reasonably OK. Next to the entrance they have a barbecue grilling mainly pork. We sadly ordered some of that. It was totally dry and overcooked resembling cardboard with Chilli Sauce (as other TA reviews mention) . No wonder. The same slabs of pork was sizzling away at least the whole hour were there. Service was again poor. After food and drinks was deliver no one came back to check if things were ok. We had to stand up to get attention to pay. Nimman has many very good eating laces and a few really great. This is not one of those. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We have been here twice now. Its a cozy atmosphere with friendly staff. W had the hors d'oeuvres platter the first time with local sausages, dipping sauces, veggies and soft boiled eggs. Tasty, but a little too rich for just two people in my opinion. Great for larger groups. The second time we had the grilled intestines with THE MOST AMAZING SAUCE (seriously if anyone knows the ingredients, please tell). They were delicious. We also had the northern beef soup that was very fragrant with lots of lemongrass, galangal and kaffir lime. They have a huge menu with lots of options. Other tables seem to order the omelets and the fish often.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went to Tong Tem Toh for my first night in Chiang Mai. My friends who are living in Chiang Mai recommended this restaurant to us. The restaurant is within walking distance if you stay somewhere near One Nimman. Very cosy atmosphere and delicious food. A bonus point for friendly and smiley staffs :) Highly recommend to give it a try if you visit Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Waited about 30 minutes for dinner. The food was great and very reasonably priced. We loved all the dishes we ordered but the grilled pork was too dry. Definitely recommend it to try at least once when you're in Chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This restaurant serves amazing and diversified food. The Burmese Curry was extremely fragrant, and the Thai sausages were delicious. One downside was that this restaurant is overly popular. There is often a huge crowd outside the restaurant. Therefore, if you don't enjoy waiting, there are better food options elsewhere.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong tem toh is worth the wait, you will probably have to stand in line for a chance to eat at this place. Your patience will be rewarded with super authentic and crazy good food! We had 4 dishes, drinks and rice for 2 for 330B, such a bargain!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We had dinner at Tong Tem Toh. The food was great tasting and great value (750 baht for 4 people, including beers). There were some unusual and tempting dishes on the menu like tamarind leaf salad. The fried pork came with an excellent sweet and sour sauce and we tried a Burmese curry on he recommendation of the waiter. The restaurant is on a quiet Soi off the Nimman Road. We sat outside on a warm breezy evening. Great place to go if you want some Northern Thai cuisine!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We found this place on Tripadvisor with many good comments and decided to experience by ourself. The location is easy to find and the place has nice decoration. The waiter and waitress are all friendly and fast serving. There was a bit of waiting but all worth it. The food was delicious with unique flavor. We were really enjoy our dinner here. Great experience and highly recommendable.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Walking around the area, we did a quick search for a restaurant for lunch, and found this was a popular location. There was a bit of a wait, but we were seated and eating within about 20 mins. It was very bustling, and it was obvious that there were lots of locals eating there. We ordered a few different things, and enjoyed everything we had. The flavours were delicious, and I would love to go back and try more next time I’m in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Just finger licking delicious food. Oyster Mushrooms with galangal chilli paste is to die for. We went there for lunch ,quick service and delicious thai food . Burmese pork curry is great .

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were told the place is packed, luckily we were there at low season, so we had no problem getting a table for the 5 of us. Service was friendly and quick. Menu us huge with a great selection of dishes and we picked a variety, all of them delicious. Highly recommendable!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tucked away on a minor road this restaurant has the most incredible food. It is rustic, minimalist and has lots of fans as it is under a shelter with not sides. But the food is amazing from bbq pork with a mildly spicy dipping sauce to good vegetarian dishes (great morning glory) and for the first time I tried pigs gizzards deep fried... and liked it! From seafood to chicken to omelets and the most delicious mango sticky rice this is fantastic. Generally you have to line up and wait but we went at at down time and got straight in. Make sure you do this one!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Heard it would be packed and would have to wait. Came around noon for lunch and no wait because got lucky. Tried the sampler platter with the dipping sauce and a fried snake head fish. The fish stole the show. Fresh & juicy.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I definitely recommend this place for the taste of the food and the pleasant atmosphere! We tried 6 different courses and my favorite (as a spicy lover) is the spicy jackfruit.. but both fish and meet specialties were really good. Sharing is caring :-) Service is fast and professional.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great restaurant with lots of variety and cheap - gets really busy and you often have to que- but worth it

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Northern Thai Sausage and BBQ Pork, beautifully done, A sweet leaves soup with ant eggs, bit strange but quite delicious and the minced pork salad, best I have had in Thailand, it’s a bit of a trek from the centre but well worth the trip.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Wow!!! Came here with two friends who are Michelin Star chefs in Bangkok upon their recommendation and it was amazing!! The northern Thai sausage, bbq pork, and spicy vegetable curry were all out of this world and the meal of 8+ dishes, 2 large Chang, and 4 sticky rice was less than 800 baht. Should be a stop for anyone looking for traditional northern Thai cooking. Can be a 30 min wait but it is worth it.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Every ad and commentary keeps saying this is an authentic local restaurant. Perhaps it is, but we felt that isn't true anymore after years of tourists going there. The food, vegetables, soup, and both chicken and pork, was ok. We didn't feel very confortable at the tables and there were flying insects. Finally in our opinion the price is not as cheap as the guides claim.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh is a relatively famous street food place that became trendy and popular so morphed into a dining garden. There is a long queue outside most evenings where patrons are handed a number and wait (up to an hour) for it to be called. To avoid this, we decided to try it at lunch and had only a short wait. The traditional northern Thai food was indeed very good, especially the sai ua (pork sausage). Lunch was a very reasonable 280 Baht ($9US) for the two of us.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I think for Thai, we have talked about the name Tem Toh - full on the table Toh Tem - table is full ( all reserved) Tong Tem Toh is all the time Toh Tem 😄because it’s popularity, so if you wanna go there just remind yourself you may need to wait at least 40 mins to get the table after all is reserved, and yes believe me, it is full pretty quick! It is my first time to this restaurant, and I’m so curious why it’s popular so I will wait. I arrive around 18.30 while the queue is called “no.42”. I request for the number to wait, mine is no.71. I get the seat around 19.20, I am not sure if i should say it’s already quick? 😅 If you really come here for local food, i recommend you to try just the Lanna Hors d’oeuvre with sticky rice. That’s really good set of northern food. The portion is big enough for two person or even three. Another Menu is Laab Muang. These 2 menus are Easy to eat & delicious. I enjoy them. In my opinion, the food here tastes good. But if you stay in other areas in CM, I don’t think you need to spend your time in the traffic, then in the long queue just to eat here. The menus are simple, and you can find at almost every other local cuisines in CM. You should rather find the one in your area instead. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Our hotel was located in the Nimman area and asked the concierge for a nearby local restaurant. Even midafternoon, the restaurant had at least a 20 minute wait. Each group was given a number. The restaurant was mostly open air with tables and benches. This was our first meal in Thailand so we wanted to sample the local cuisine: coconut chicken soup, northern chicken soup, beef stir fry, and their speciality which was the bean dip. We simply couldn't eat the bean dip and thought overall the food was average. Over the course of the three days in Chiang Mai, we found restaurants we enjoyed. Our meal for four people came to $15. Probably would not recommend coming here. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you plan to dine in, you must arrive at the restaurant 1.5 hours in advance and wait in line. The food was good but not authentic. Some locals do not eat here.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I came back here again with my partner and friends and wow, this place always delivers. The queue can be a little bit annoying but that queue is there for a reason. And the fact it’s pretty much 100% locals, you know it’s good. I’m yet to leave Tong Tem Toh disappointed, whether I go at lunch or for dinner, the whole menu just hits the spot. The star of the show this time, eclipsing my all time favourite of Gaeng Hang Lei, was the fermented pork in banana leaf. It’s unreal! If you’re in CM and especially the Nimman area, this place is a must! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Absolutely over-rated dining place. Due to "popular" rated here, so you will join in a long queue outside the restaurant. I waited for 45-mins for an average food quality. The waiter/waitress too busy watching the queue outside and didn't bother to further recommend/advise your orders whether sufficient for 4 persons or any signature dishes should not be missed. Was a quiet ordering process by just pin-pointing on the menu! :( I didn't get any good memory to dine in this place, no good food nor good service. The ONLY "fun" I had is QUEUEing. :( Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Traditional northern food, not the best in Chiang mai but the best in Nimman area. The queue is super long, so go early if you can. Price is really cheap, we paid about 2000 baht for 9 people dinner!!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The food was good!! But the food is good most places in Chiang Mai. 30 minute wait was not really worth the hype. Very reasonable prices and nice setting.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place is populated with tourists, expect to wait at least 40mins during peak hours. I went down 3 times this trip, the first 2 rounds all the dishes are above my expectation. I had 3 dishes each time. 3rd time because of their grilled dishes as many has highly recommended it. The rating was pulled down by the grilled dish which only serve after 6pm. The grilled items was overly dry and nothing to talk about it. Every trip cost me around 250-350baht. Pretty reasonable for a restaurant. Food serve very fast but not much of service. Don't expect them to explain any from the menu. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We had tempura pork, sunflower sprout and omelette. They were great. We tired omelette in 3 other places and concluded that Tong Tem Toh serves the best. It was crispy! The tempura pork was delicious too. Tender pork in crispy and flavourful batter. The sunflower sprout was tasty too. And all for less than 300THB. A must visit when you come to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We visited for lunch (because the lines at dinner time were far too long). We found the staff were pleasant when we were able to get their attention. The food was very good and very cheap, and is similar to local prices. We had the curry and although we were told it is not spicy, people sensitive to heat may feel otherwise. Overall good experience.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It was busy when we arrived and they give you a menu and order slip to order while you are waiting so you cannot really ask any questions about the menu items. We ended up ordering a few dishes we really did not like. It was probably our fault but I would not go back because of how you order. I need a little help ordering Northern Thai dishes. Reasonable prices, the beer was good.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This was almost unanimously recommended for Northern Thai cuisine, but we weren’t that impressed with the dishes here. We had the Burmese curry, bamboo shoot soup, stir fried sweet leaves with egg, grilled pork neck and puff ball mushrooms with pork. We had high expectations of the grilled pork neck since this was a recommended dish, but we found it a bit too dry. All of the dishes were not bad, but not great either.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There was a short queue when we arrived on Saturday 3pm. The food items are supposedly from Northern Thailand, and we got the sampler plate together with Northern Thai chicken soup/curry and a fried omelet. The food was different from the usual Thai fare we were used to, and some items on the sample plate seem exotic. The items are quite reasonably priced, as our total bill was 366 THB for the sample plate and 2 dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Easy to find but for the car park may difficulty. The variety of northern foods, the price is reasonable. The food is very delicious and recommended.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is my go to place when in CM. Northern Thai/Lanna food is my favourite and this place does the best I've ever tasted. Gaeng Hang Lei is my personal favourite with some khao niew for good measure. The ant egg soup is delicious as are all the fermented dishes they serve. Getting a table can be hard work but well worth the wait for stunning food and a great atmosphere.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The food is great and priced reasonably. The place can get crowded as its quite popular among locals and tourists. Try their grilled stuffs. They have an english menu and pictures to help with choosing what to order. Or ask for a waiter/waitress that can understand English for assistance in what is popular. We definitely don't mind going back to this place if we visit Chiang Mai again.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I ate here twice. Once for lunch and once for dinner. The food was excellent and the price was also very good. I had a curry, rice, and a small bottle of water for less than 100 baht. The service was quick and they had english menus. I sat on a covered outside area. It was nice. A great restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We came for lunch early. The place does fill up pretty quickly by 12pm. It does seem a lot more tourists frequent the restaurant than locals. Though pricing did not seem to be very high (but definitely higher than true local street food). As the first time to eat here we ordered mostly the recommended dishes for which they had a menu for. For this time the pork neck appeared a little overcooked and was a little hard. Otherwise the food is quite alright though we would not rate it as fantastic yet.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is a very popular Thai North restaurant. Long queue, but taste is very good together with value price.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is our 4th visit to this Chiang Mai . we come every year. Of course we visit this restaurant everytime. This is our favourite Thai// Northern foods , the food is absolutely delicious !

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It looks like a fancy restaurant in the university area of Chiang Mai. At first it looks a bit more expensive than other places, but it’s still very cheap and the food and service is really good. It’s always crowded but the wait is really short. We were 2 and ordered 4 dishes. The northern sausage omelette was really good (and non spicy). Unfortunately the other 3 (a soup, minced pork and chicken) we forgot to ask for non spicy and it were really spicy for us and we couldn’t finish the food (but it tasted great).

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: A traditional meal. If you wish to try this restaurant you have to come very early. Queue a little bit long, I waited for 45 mins. The taste is quite normal.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: An energizing kinda funky joint, half indoor-half terrace, very vigilant and well-trained staff running the place like bosses. TTT offers purely authentic Thai food and it shows: most patrons are locals. They might be the trendy kind from Nimman (the trendiest area of Chiang Mai) but they know their food and are probably cruelly demanding. I love the fact that many of the dishes were unknown to me. When you eat as much Thai food as I do you're very pleased to discover dishes and get away from the usual phad thai, larb gai or papaya salad. The eggplant salad, the crab chili dip, the minced pork-chili... all are authentic, very well balanced in taste, spicy as they should and -thanks the God of Foodies- made without sugar! THANK YOU!! It started pouring rain in the middle of the lunch, our table wasn't the prettiest thing we sat at but you know what? As long as you're being served good food with a smile the weather don't matter. Totally recommend Tong Tem Toh and will definitely come back for more culinary discovery! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Delicious!!! Ordered while we waited for a table and food came promptly after we were seated. Outdoor seating was comfortable.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Watched long queues for a couple of weeks outside this restaurant. Friends had highly recommended so thought we'd try. Almost entirely Chinese folk dining there the night we went. Must be popular in Chinese Trip Advisor - we got in straight away but the Chinese were queuing up big time by the time we left. Service very fast except our vegetable dish arrived after we'd finished. Bit late then folks. Ordered Burmese pork curry and northern Thai sausage plus rice and greens. Both meat dishes were nice enough but we thought they weren't anywhere as good as others we've tried. Both of us really disappointed. Bain-marie style food. Vegetables with egg received our inaugural "worst dish in Thailand" award. Not truly horrible but not good either. I ate it because I need my greens but would never have again. Overall a disappointment but perhaps we just picked the wrong things. Bit hit and miss. If you don't make it here, personally I don't think it's a loss. There's heaps of other places that are tonnes better. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My sons Thai girlfriend booked a table here and took care of our orders as we arrived a little late. The food was absolutely fantastic and the portion sizes were plentiful. Service was excellent, fast and efficient. This was a fab restaurant with a very friendly atmosphere and we will definitely be going again.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The food here is very good and the service is incredibly fast. The food was good, but a bit hit or miss. We had several things we loved (like the Beef spicy soup) and other things that were not so great. Be aware that when you check in with the host, they will give you a piece of paper and a menu. You are supposed to write down what you would like (the numbers) so that you can hand it to them to be seated. This is what ensures the quick service.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Delicious food and friendly staff, highly recommend the local sausage and the fermented pork and egg.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It is highly recommended! You may have to line up and wait a long queue so please be there right when it opens.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I've worked in the restaurant industry for almost 15 years and this was the Thunderdome of dining. They flipped about 18 tables in half an hour while we waited in line - there's no messing around. The deal is: you put your name down, they give you a number and a sheet of paper. What we didn't know was that you write the numbers of the dishes down while you wait so once you're sat down, the food takes a matter of minutes to get to your table. And the food! It was one of the only places we found that was actually spicy when advertised (maybe it was my Canadianness that other places were mellowing out the flavour). This restaurant had so much energy, and when a fairly big restaurant is full almost every night you walk past, you've GOT to give it a try. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We walked from hotel and smell good before we arrived at the entrance. The BBQ pork was great. Grilled fish was crispy and nice. Seating environment although no view , the feeling like in a garden. Many people still eating when we were there about 2pm. Good price and recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We'd heard that this restaurant served traditional Northern Thai food and that you often had to queue for a table, so arrived here in the early evening. At that time we were the only Western people. Most of the Thai people had come direct from work or school. Great ambience and great food. We had a Northern Thai sharing platter for Baht 187, containing raw laab, pork sausage, lanna sausage, duck egg, pork crackling, vegetables and two delicious red and green spicy dips. This seemed to be a very popular dish - lots of tables ordered it. Really really tasty! Lots of other delicious dishes on the menu. By the time we left the queue was about 20 deep! Definitely recommend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: They have some nice local northern cuisine here. Prices are reasonable. There are a lot of Chinese tourists here during lunch and dinner times, so plan accordingly.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We went at 1:30pm and there were plenty of tables available. But it was strange at first to see a local restaurant down Nimman sois to be so popular. I sensed something special, we just came across this restaurant serependiciously but I remembered walking past once one evening with a queue outside. We ordered the "Lanna Hors d'oeuvres" , one of the more expensive things on the menu for 180b but most things in the menu were reasonably priced . Another thing that seemed above average were the very good waiters . The food was good Thai standard and interesting , Chiang mai sausage fermented pork ,pot of an interesting curry . That's all we had, it filled 2 of us up anyway . So this is only a partial review ! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Out of all the places I've eaten at this was the most regular in Chiang mai. I can't recommend their steamed fermented sausage enough. It comes in a banana leaf steamed with an egg covering it. It's simple but perfect. 5 stars.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The food there was so so...not as good as the recommend from the internet. I ordered pork Jowl, but chewy. And the fried pork belly is very normal, not recommend to order those. The price is reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Under my vegeterian Thai Omlet was some meat.. I was shocked.. I would never go back to this Restaurant again.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you want to try local Chiang Mai food, here is the place. Food was delicious, price also cheap. Nice service and environment.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Our hotel recommend this restaurant to us Easy walk from our hotel It’s around lunch time and we are lucky to get a table There are a lot of choices on the menu Including chicken beef pork fish vegetables The prices are reasonable and the the taste is amazing good We ordered 5 dishes and it fits 4 of us Let’s try if you are around Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We walked past this around 7pm and there was a huge queue, so we came back the next day for a late lunch (around 2.30pm) and the place was only about a third full. The food was excellent - especially recommend the Burmese Pork Curry and Grilled Northern Thai Sausage. Prices very reasonable too and service was attentive. Also usefully they accept credit cards.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If your idea of Thai cuisine is only Thai favorites like pad Thai or green curry, then this restaurant devoted to Northern Thai cuisine will be a huge surprise. Here the options are considerably more esoteric, including ant egg soup, sweet fried leaves, 'fermented' raw pork and other Northern recipes. We would recommend the grilled Northern sausage, the puffball mushrooms and the Burmese pork curry, all of which are outstanding. Get here before 5 pm if you want to be guaranteed a table. The place is a Chiang Mai Institution and it has the queues to prove it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Chiang Mai has a lot of Chinese influence, and it shows up in the restaurants. We found it challenging to find a local place, but when I started asking travelers who had been here a few times, Tong Tem Toh popped onto the screen. We got there relatively early, were given a number, and waited about 20 minutes to get in. ADVICE: while you're waiting, you grab a menu and a pencil ... write your choices, with the item number, on the back of your queue number. Many items have photos, and the specialties of the house are on the first page. We went for it, selecting spicy Thai sausage (which sliced and presented with no sauce ... was great), spicy beef (spicy here is relative, but yes ... it's spicy for a gal from Ohio, but I *love* it!), beef curry, and puff mushroom soup (a specialty). We also ordered steamed rice, which was a good idea for the curry in particular. The puff mushroom soup was really really good. The mushrooms are about the size of a fingernail and slightly leathery in texture. They serve them in a broth with ground pork and a chiffonade of lime leaves. This wonderful mix of flavors is WHY we travel. Never in a million years could I put together these flavors, and I'm so happy they've figured out lime leaves! Although the beef curry wasn't on the specialty page, it was utterly the best curry I've ever had. They add peanuts (of course), and you'll have to try pretty hard not to lick the bowl. The atmosphere of al fresco dining has a magic all its own, and you'll love the simple tables beneath the trees and lanterns. Even on a hot night, we were comfortable. If you get there later and have a longer wait ... don't be put off. Go get a drink at a nearby hotel or stroll around. Just don't come back late, as I'm not sure they allow you back in queue. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I was attracted by the big crowd and those Chinese certificates on the entrance claiming it's a "popular" choice. Actually, it's true this restaurant is very popular. However, I was quite disappointed since l can not find anything special. Both the foods and service are just ordinary to me. Somehow, it's personal. Others may find it interesting though. Pricing is above average comparing with nearby restaurants. Atmosphere is good.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Here is only place in Nimman road where you can taste a real Thai Northern food. They always busy so ques up for your table it can be expecting. The food can make it quick when you ordered and the taste is amazingly good compare to the price is was not too expensive. Strongly recommend to visit.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you want local northern Thai food, this is the place to go. However, there is always a wait since this place is so popular in nimman. I would recommend going early. Luckily our friend got there first and put our name on the queue, we only waited 15mins. I can’t remember exactly what we ordered but it was good. I wished I ordered more dishes to try it all.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My husband and I had a late lunch here (because the lineups were long from 6:15 - 7:30). We met some long-time expats there who helped us order our dishes. We had Burmese pork curry (best dish ever during our Thailand stay); stir fried chayote greens; barbecued pork shoulder blade (grilled at the front); larb - Laotian minced pork with herbs; and homemade Northern Thai spiced sausage. We forgot to order the young tamarind leaves spicy salad...but will do that next time. For 2 people, cost was 400 baht for a very memorable meal. Brought home another portion of food as leftovers (No beer between 2-5 pm - we were out of luck). Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The menu says that it is northern thai food. I don't know about northern thai food. But well, the food was amazing and that's what's basically important. Be aware of the waiting time. Go there, get a number and like 30 minutes, the table is ready. Go and get a drink in a nearby bar in the meantime. The food is worth the waiting time!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I went at 3:00 on a Saturday expecting things to be relatively quiet, but even so had to wait 30 minutes or so for a table. The outdoor seating with the fountain was quite pleasant, and there seemed to be a number of locals out enjoying their meals. I had the tamarind leaf salad (nice, but not life-changing) and the Burmese curry, which was excellent. I’d go again if I were in the neighborhood - certainly a good value for the money.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My husband got a bad case of food poisoning from this restaurant. We ordered the appetizer sampler, which came out in about 1 minute and was all room temperature (perhaps this is standard). We think it was something on this plate that made him sick (it was not the fermented pork, as neither of us ate that). For entrees we both ordered off the recommended list - he got a pork curry that was tasty but nothing to write home about. I got the sauteed beef, which was pretty grey in color and sour tasting and rubbery. I ate very little of anything and did not get sick. Exactly 12 hours later he got sick.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you stay in Nimman area and don’t have a car, Tong Tem Toh is a convenient choice for local Thai northern food. However, given the waiting time for a table, it doesn’t worth the wait. My friend and I went there at 3pm and there were 20 queues ahead of me (they gave out the queue number and you must be there when your number is called). We decided to wait as we were curious about the hype and 20 was not that bad compared the the night before that we walked by and asked...40 in the queue! The food was not bad but, as I said, didn’t worth the wait. If you are there and don’t see the queue (or its a short queue), go ahead and try the local cuisine! The food is quite spicy (spicier than other local Thai northern food places I went). The price is moderate. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It serves northern Thai dishes. Many people here and the queue outside is also long despite arriving at 3pm. The deep fried chicken was excellent. If u are a fan of northern Thai dishes like the chili dip, must try.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Has the hors devrou or something, it was a beautiful dish and not too pricy for the setting ether (189b) one of the best dishes so far, went there twice.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Every dishes are very yummy and cheap so easy to try and experice nothern thai food! and also located in nimman if you are around must try! However recently you have to wait for hours..to get seat.. recommend to avoid busy time ( dinner and lunch) and they close 9:30 pm.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I visited there on 24 November 2017. I waited for an hour to get inside. We ordered Northern set, Deep fried chicken wings, Fried morning glory with garlic, Grilled pork, Chicken soup and Curry chicken. I was disappointed because the taste of some of the dishes were not delicious as what I expected. The soup was too salty and the Grilled pork was not good. The remaining dishes were of average quality. The total amount of my bill was around 1700 Bahts. We had nine people all together. It was cheap. But I would like to try another restaurant if I visit Chaing Mai again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I read from many reviews from internet and hope that I will try when I go to Chiang Mai. Indeed that I stayed next Soi and went to TongTem Toh 2 times. Thai cuisines were delicious and some menus were interesting. The ice has been put in a bowl. Good service and fast served. I will be back again when I come to Ching Mai. I recommended!!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The burmese pork curry was again, the saving grace. The grilled pork was ok but nothing to rave about. The recommended puff mushroom soup thingy was atrocious. I hated it. We hated it. 1 spoonful each and that was all. Overhyped. The passion fruit drink is delicious though.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Excellent spicy Northern Thai food. The Sau Oua (Northern Thai sausage) was perfectly balanced, and the puff-ball mushrooms were spicy and interesting in texture. Good value for money, excellent service at lunchtime.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I think this restaurant is overhyped. They offer decent food but i had better thai food elsewhere. Service is pretty good and responsive, price is okay too for the price we paid

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Between the lines and the slow service, it is hard to love this place. Food was good and fairly priced but service was slowest we had in 2 weeks of Chiang Mai. Zero English spoken as well which I never expect or downgrade for (it is their country) but just as a FYI for those that may struggle.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It is one of good place to taste the northern thai style food. The environment of this restaurant is nice with music. So relax and enjoy the time in Chiang mai. Must go to eat next time to Chiang mai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The taste is not really good compare to the original taste. I think the visitor coming to this restaurant because of review and the reviewer they do not know what is the real taste of the northern food. Conclude the tasty think in this restaurant is pain water and ice.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place specialises in Northern Thai cuisine and has been favourably reviewed by many who have dined here. I came for dinner and waited roughly 20 minutes for a table for two. Disappointingly the meal did not meet my expectation. The sharing platter looked better than it tasted, the spicy chicken soup lacked the flavour I had expected. The soup was purely spicy without any additional taste to it, the only good dish was probably the fried omelette, which was not why I came to the eatery.
