Service was quick though, waiters tried to accommodate my requests though they speak broken English. Nevertheless I don't think I will come back here next time I'm in town

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Regular visitor to this fantastic Thai restaurant. Food is always excellent. Highly recommend for take away also.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great food! The restaurant was full of locals with a great vibe. This place is best at night when you can fully enjoy a table outside.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There is always long queue outside the restaurant but its definitely worthwhile.... even when I was waiting in the rain. All dishes are delicious and fresh, especially the Burma curry pork, fried sweet leaves with eggs and chicken soup.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Sweet leaf with egg is good. Fried Chicken wing was ok. Grilled pork was mediocre. Nice vibe. Suitable for group/ family.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great northern thai food found in Nimman Road! I especially like to chicken wings and fried eggs. Hopefully will come back soon during my next trip to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It was busy when we arrived (afternoon around 2pm) but we were able to get a table within 10 minutes. The food we ate was all superb, and I highly highly recommend the northern thai sausage. I could probably eat this all day.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: A great place to have local food especially northern cuisine. No air-conditioned. Seating is ok. The restaurant can be crowded and long queue. Better avoid the peak lunch hour. Price is reasonable, not the cheapest but value the money. (around 10 USD per person)

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This restaurant is apparently well known and popular by locals and travellers. We were told about it by our local travel agent. When we arrived, family with 3 daughters (7, 10 and 13), we were informed that waiting time was around 40 minutes. These became only 20 minutes, and more than worth while. The food is very authentic and well prepared conform local Thai recipes. Prices are more than affordable, we paid around 850 bath for a 5 persons diner....The quality of food and entourage will make us come back to this little food paradise in Chiang Mai. A must do when around.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: When I opened the menu I found that most of the dishes are not typically what you expect of the Thai fare in Bangkok. The menu is distinct to ChiangMai. The place is popular with locals as well as Korean, China and Singaporeans.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is a good local joint for those who crave for Northern cuisine. The menu is extensive covering various dips to stir fries, BBQ meats to curries. My family had lunch here and enjoyed a spread of Northern dips, Gaeng Hang Lae Curry, Chiang Mai Larb, Chiang Mai Sausages, and a stir fried vegetables. The authentic dips, curry and larb were great despite the spiciness was toned down a little for the many tourists who visit the restaurant. The food service and staff hospitality were prompt although service with a smile would certainly not hurt at all! The meal for the entire group of four was reasonable. Obviously for a meal elsewhere with a similar ambience and cuisine will cost a fraction cheaper, Tong Tem Toh is however in Nimman occupying a prime location. The little premium charged is therefore acceptable. Our overall experience was a positive one and the unsmiling staff and mediocre sausages and stir-fry is the reason for the 3.5 - 4 star rating. Will I go back for a meal, ok ... I admit ... yes! :p Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I specifically told the waitress taking our order my food must be "little spicy". She repeated it back to me. The meal I got burnt the inside of my mouth from start to finish with no real taste except hot. My husband (who enjoys hot food) was equally unhappy with his meal - hot and no other flavor. Very disappointing meal!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I had an average experience here for two main reasons - I was dining on my own and I didn't know anything about the food. I have no complaints about the service or the setting. It's all open-air seating, so I'd recommend lots of insect repellent to be on the safe side. I got there at about 6.30pm on a Friday and there was already a crowd, mostly tourists. A smiling waitress gave me a number on a piece of paper and I waited for about 15-20mins until my number was called. While waiting, I wrote the numbers of the menu items I wanted. A few of the staff seemed able to speak some English which was helpful. Because I was on my own, I only ordered three dishes - deep fried chicken wings, spicy beef curry and vegetable, and stir fried leaf vegetable (chayote?) with egg, plus steamed rice and a soft drink. The prices were good, with 3 dishes, rice and a soft drink all adding up to about 300 baht. I would recommend going with a group, and going with someone who is a local or else knows about Thai food to have the best experience. I picked dishes that seemed "safe", not knowing what I would or wouldn't like or what the specialty was, and so I didn't end up loving what I ordered. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: You will see many of tourists in front of this restaurant as it is famous place. Food are very delicious and you can taste tranditional northern food. Menu is easy to understand with picture and you can order by the number of picture.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Really enjoyed the food here. I'm new to northern food so I tried as many different dishes as I could. I loved all of it, especially the yum jin gai, han lay and the namprik. It's cheap and delicious, which is why you have to line up if you don't get there early.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My first day in Chiang Mai and the best way to start my travels with an authentic northern thailand meal. Upon arriving it was obvious this place is popular with a small line of people waiting for a table. The smell of the bbq grill drifting across the street. The food and service was reasonably fast. The food did not dissapoint. I found the menu to have a good variety to choose from. The flavours in the dishes we ordered were memorable and gave us the "mmmmm" feeling upon eating. The seating and atmosphere was okay. Could be presented a little better but hey! The food is most important and did not dissapoint. Recommend you visit if in the area. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Very good food. Had 4 different servers in one hour but no one to present the bill. Overall, good experience

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went there for late lunch and the place was still busy. Food was good, price was good. The staff were friendly but the lack paying attention to details and kind of forgetful. We had to follow up with rice and drinking water. They just forgot to bring other things except cooked food.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There is a long queue, so be ready for waiting. The thing we did not like with my husband was, that the waiter gave us a number and said we have to wait around 30 minutes for a table, so we went to a coffee shop next to it since we still feel a jetlag...after coffee, no more than 15 minutes we came back and our number was no longer valid...so we could get a new number and wait again what we did not appreciate...and went to eat somewhere else in the end because we found it really bad approach... if they would say straight away that it is MAX 30 mins or something like that we would have be there and not go for a quick coffee...in the end we went to 'eat is life' and it was great! Really recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Like most of the resturants with good reviews in tripadviser this is great simple Thai food. Well cooked with good variety.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Everything is great from food to staff. Prepare yourself for a very long queue. Worth waiting. Price is also reasonable, I can say cheap!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Happy to say I've been here at last It's was so busy with people lining up with a waitlist to dine. We order while we were still waiting to be seated. All dishes except one were served almost immediately. Last dish took a few requests to finally arrive. To be honest, I've had food just as tasty as the food here but now I can say I've been here...

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Overall the food is nice and cheap, but the grill pork neck is too hard and not so nice. Must try dish - mix platter.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great traditional northern style dishes and tasty. Locate in Nimmahemin road, surrounding by many convenient stores, restaurants and hotel near by. Many tasteful dishes worth trying.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Delicious Northern Thai cuisine. Stumbled across this place and seemed very popular. I'd really like to return before I leave CM.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Me and my family came here when we arrived Chiang Mai and it was awesome! I love the food and the ambiance is great! The only thing is the table is too small(thats because we ordered a lot of food!) All in all. I love it and will surely eat here again when i come back to Chiang Mai. Thumbs up!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The first sign of Tong Tem Toh's greatness is the lack of tourists. The place is consistently packed with Thais. I read a lot about problems with waiting, but my party of 6 had no issues. You can find some Thai signature dishes, but this place focuses on northern specialities. The flavors are unique and prepared by competent staff. Small dishes meant to be eaten family style, so be ready to try new things!! Highly recommend the fried Cod

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The sweet leave was so good that we ordered again. The grilled fish and pork were also very nice. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Feel lucky to find such an Authentic Northern Thai Restaurant on our first dinner in Chiangmai. Again, thanks to TripAdvisor lead us to this wonderful dining place. They set their BBQ pork pit right in front of the entrance and the great smell directly come to us before entering the restaurant and made us feel more hungry 😋. The food is very delicious at economical price and the restaurant ambience is full of authentic Thai feel. Lucky we reached early at 6.15pm as by the time we finished eating at 7pm, there are huge crowds waiting outside the restaurant for tables. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We wanted to sample genuine northern Thai cuisine and went to Tong Tem Toh on the recommendation of our hotel. We were not disappointed. But this restaurant is so popular there was a queue to get in. But we happily shared a bottle of Leo beer while we waited. We had the famous and popular Mu Hunglay, a unique pork curry that has its origins over the border in Myanmar. We also enjoyed a sour and slightly bitter soup of vegetables and chicken, and a plate of stir fried young pumpkin leaves and sprouts. The four of us voted Ton Tem Toh top marks. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: During 2 weeks in Chiang Mai I ate here 3-4 times. This is not fine dining, just good authentic Thai food with good casual service. The menu is extensive but their first page offers a bunch of suggested favorites that covers pretty much all areas of the menu. I'd stick to this page until you are familiar with what you like. I do not have a huge appetite and found two dishes to be the right amount to satisfy my hunger. Rice is ordered on the side. If there is a wait you will place your order before being seated. You will be given you a piece of paper with a number on it. Grab a pencil and a menu and write your order on this piece of paper and then give it back to the host/hostess. Once your sat the food comes quickly. I recommend giving it a shot. Seems to start getting crowded around 6:00 or 6:30. Wait was pretty short in my experience. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Local food of northern very good. If u want to go there u have to go before 6.00 p.m. because it is full

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: First, don't patronize this place unless you can sit on a stool without any back support. Second, the food is below expectations. The supposedly freshly grilled, medium-rare beef seems like overcooked leftovers. The appetizer platter looks better than it tastes. Service can be hit or miss. Given that there are so many good restaurants in this area, I won't return.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The service were the best The waiters and waitresses were so attentive, nice, and getting things done swiftly. I got no. 14, people who had been waiting there got 5, 7 and it took only 10 mins before I got my table. I kept asking several times while waiting, yet, they were responding nicely. I could order in advance. Waiting for the table actually gave me time to look online for the dishes I wanted and I showed to the waitress who helped write them down in the order slip. The food was great. If you know what to order, they have good range of local food. And now as I am writing a lot of reviews of CM and complaining about mosquitoes at many places, no mosquitoes at TTT. This is a must when you're in Chiangmai Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: You can get some really good northern Thai food here. This is where I took my family when they were in town visiting to show them some northern Thai cuisine. It's usually full of locals and to a lesser degree tourists. There is always a wait. Usually it'll be 30 minutes to an hour. Your best bet is to go up and order on a slip of paper and have have a drink somewhere else while they prepare your food. Just a heads up, portions tend to be smaller here.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I am taste Thai northern style food; it is a set with eggs, vegetable, two kinds’ sauce and sticky rice. I order some of grill pork and chicken, I recommend to try deep fried fish , it is very delicious. Only one concern, there have mosquito to bit you in sunset .

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This popular eatery is casual -- you sit outdoors at picnic tables and place your orders by going up to a waiter if one doesn't come to you. The menu is varied and the food is tasty. Be prepared to wait in line for a table at this reasonably-priced place; for four we paid less than $20.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great place for dining on Nimmanhemin 13. Easy to go with parking place. Lots of people go there. If you want to have a seat, you need to be there by 4-5 pm. Food is delicious. Shouldn't miss if you travel to Chiang Mai and wanna taste northern food.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I am staying at a hotel around the corner and walked by one night and saw this surprisingly long queue waiting to get in so the next night I decided to find out what the fuss was all about. At 6pm the place was full and I was already #22 and by the time I sat down they were giving out #47. The food was very good and I like spicy but one of the dishes set my mouth on fire. Having said that I finished it all!! Good to go in a group.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We looked at the reviews and came early at 5:20pm. Still had to wait 15 min but could pre order. Had the pork blade shoulder (the BBQ that smells good outside), burmanese pork curry, and the sweet leaves with eggs. The portions are not big but all very delicious. This is the first meal in six days where I felt like I really tasted something authentic from northern Thailand, vs the typical pad Thai, green/yellow curry, tomyom gong.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Amazing authentic flavours, great dishes and good prices. That sums up Tong Tem Toh. I've been here twice and I never had to queue up longer than 5 minutes. So if you're in a rush or don't want to queue at all don't go eat here. If you want to eat where the locals eat then this is the place to be. Try the Northern Thai grilled sausage, boiled eggs and green eggplant dish, fried chicken wings with fish sauce and coconut curry (can't remember the name). JUST AMAZING! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It is a very nice restaurant, great food and great staff. The food is more than expected and we cannot finish it. It is a great place to try Northern Thailand good. Would very recommend to try. But be careful the 'Q' is very long and need to wait more than one hour.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This was one of our very favorite meals from our 3 weeks in Thailand. Very busy hip restaurant - go early and put your name in (we were there at 6p, they said an hour so we walked around the hood and came back). (Not sure if they take reservations?) Great outdoor space, we were the only westerners the day we went. The Gaang Hang Lei is out of this world - must order! True Northern Thai food - must try for a change of pace from the typical. They had us order before we were seated and then food was prompt so don't be discouraged by the throngs of people outside.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The wait was a bit long. You show up and put your name on the list and get a number and a menu. you choose the items you want to order and give the slip to the hostess who will give it to the kitchen once you've been seated. food was pretty good, but not sure if I can't say it was worth it, but glad we did try.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Our wait was around 20 minutes but it was worth it (I guess because we weren't starving at the time). Tong Tem Toh is a great place to try authentic Northern Thai food here and the food we had absolutely delicious!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Two and half hour waiting time, and when the head waitress calling the table number, missed calling our number while we were just sitting closest to the restaurant. When you waited that long, you would expect the food to be amazing, but actually not all, we ordered 7 dishes, 5 out of 7 was great. If you are the person like to enjoy the good no matter what circumstances are, then go for it, if you love food also care about customer service, environment of the restaurant, I don't think this one will fulfils your 100% requirement. Good luck and enjoy Chiangmai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I went there and saw a long queue. I thought that must be a good sign. Unfortunately, my long time waiting went in vain as the meat was not very good and really well done. The service isn't great, I think the place is playing on it fame to downgrade the service.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We picked a number 69 when it's called at 47. We wait about 30-40 mins to get a table. I found out that half of the people waiting are locals. So, i realized that this restaurant must serve great local food. I was not disappointed. The local northern thai food is very good. I like the platter and local vege with egg. Beef is so so.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Our family enjoyed the meal & services they provided. The price is amazing compare to foods & services. I do recommend you to visit. It s worth to wait for.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: For dinner, arrive before at least 8.30pm in order to get a number and be prepared to wait. The restaurant will not take in any more customers after that time. The food is good, so is the ambience. I was seated 1/2 hour later, being the last table of customers to be served. I was very surprised at the price, because for the quality of food they serve, the cost was minimal. I would certainly go there again if and when I am next in CNX.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We four girls ordered 8 dishes. Taste is different from foods in Bangkok, think it's typical north Thai foods! It costed THB 9xx in total! Very fair!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It was pouring with rain on our first evening in Chiang Mai. Tong Tem Toh is only a 2 minute walk from the hotel we were staying at (Art Mai Hotel), so that is where we headed for. We had to cram in under the shelter together with the queue of people waiting for a table and we were quickly given a number. In the end we waited about 40 minutes, but it was an entertainment just watching the workers and seeing the dishes coming out of the kitchen. While we were still waiting they took our order and the food arrived within minutes of our sitting down. And of course the food was worth waiting for!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: A lot of customers and long queue. But well organised and quick on serving the dishes. Delicious Northern food and reasonable price.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Delicious Thai cuisines near Nimman road , just a few walk from the road. The recommended food are all delicious and not so spicy for the foreigners. The cron ia only long queue and at least half an hour for waiting in front of the restaurant .

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Large menu of Northern Thai dishes to share, great to discover different flavours first hand. The grilled dishes are good and so is the stir fried pork. The spicy dishes were a bit too much for our European palette, not a first when trying bits of Thai cuisine I admit. Very crowded place, lots of locals. Expect to wait before being seated. Good price quality place.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were so intrigued by this restaurant because of the queue outside! We had to wait 30minutes which we didn't mind. We tried overall 6 dished. Portions are small so it is better to order 2 p.p. We were a bit disappointed. Everything was good but we didn't find anything outstanding but the burmese pork curry.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: we ordered a couple of famous dishes such as grilled pork and some tamarind leaf salad,hot soup and larb pork with two sticky rices and cola. we paid less than 400 THB for great meal. we went there around 7 pm and have to wait for table around half and hour

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We stayed around the corner from this place and found it thanks to TA. We ended up going here two nights in a row because it was such good, authentic northern Thai food. We had to queue both nights, but the line moved quickly. The service is great and the prices are reasonable. We definitely recommend it; the Burmese curry and the chicken wings were especially good.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We ate here a couple of times and really enjoyed the local cuisine as the restaurant specialise in Northern Thai fare. The local sausage and marianated pork are very tasty and filling. Be prepared to wait, there was always a queue waiting for a table although the tables do turn over quite quickly.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Upon our arrival of BED Nimman Hotel, we asked the front desk for suggestion of good restaurant that served Thai cuisine around the district. The staff suggested us to visit Tong Tem Toh. The restaurant was located just a 10 minutes walk from our hotel. However, we took almost half an hour to locate the place. We had barbecue chicken wing, curry pork, barbecue pork neck meat and fried sweet leaves. Yes, the restaurant did serve very good northern Thai food, but the restaurant was not air conditioned. We found it very hot eating there. So, we ordered two cold drinks, passion fruit juice and roselle. The whole meal for the 3 of us costed 420 baht.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Splurged some extra cash to go here on our last night in Chiang Mai. So so worth it. Thai sausages, Burmese pork curry, the jackfruit salad felt like the meatiest of the lot. All superb.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I suspect this is very authentic Thai food as most of the patrons seemed to be locals, and unlike many other places, English was not commonly spoken by all the staff. We ordered 4 or 5 dishes and a couple were just okay. The grilled chicken was good. I got a soup, which was tasty but incredibly SPICY that I couldn't finish it. Overall, I thought it was average.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were taken here by our tuk tuk driver after our first choice was closed for the evening. This is supposedly one of the better Northern Thai style restaurants in Chiang Mai. It's set in a cute little area about a 10 minute ride from old town. We definitely let our guard down and explored the menu! We order 5 things including the fried snake fish head. That was good, but the sauce they served with it was fantastic! However the star dishes were the pork Burmese curry (It was amazing) and the drilled pork shoulder. I would go back there for that curry! However I'd skip the Sai Oua (northern Thai sausage). It was dry and lacked the flavor that you would find from a street vendor.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great taste of northern Thai Food on Nimmarn Road. The problem was only they have no parking lot for you, try to find somewhere nearby then. I was enjoying variety of foods from the menu. And of course, value for money!!! Not expensive at all

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It's a local place, and packed with people. So suggest to get there around 12pm for a waiting time of 15 min. The food was so yummy that we went for a second time the next day. Great value as well, 900 baht for 6 dishes for two. A must-go for authentic northern Thai cuisine.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Enjoy local Northern thai foods here! You have to wait and queue up for 5-10mins. The taste is good.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We head to this place after our long trip from Chiang Rai for dinner. The pork is the ultimate favorite menu ordered here. My friend order the Hours de'Ouvre and I think they have the mos beautiful boiled eggs where the yolk is totally golden! I ordered omelette and puff ball mushroom, and the Sweet Lead with eggs. It was ok and the service is quite good dor a busy restaurant. The waitresses have a limited understanding of English. The safest way is to point your order in the menu while they note it down (instead you saying it). Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great taste of good value local cuisine in a nice restaurant setting. Full of locals which is always a good sign. Cheap! The Burmese curry is unmissable

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Came here because of all the good reviews and surely enough the food was delicious. They offer a wide variety of northern thai dishes, too bad we are not able to try all of them. The barbecue pork and intestines dish is the highlight of the meal and all dishes are reasonably priced at an average of 10ish baht. The food portion are quite small but we get to try more dishes that way. Definitely go try if you like to have local food.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were living close to Ninman Road. Walking distance to Tong Tem Toh. But not that appealing to us all along and later discover it's the local restaurant around the corner. Gotta ask until we discover! Good local food. Reasonable price. Worth trying!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We came all the way here on a Thai friends recommendation. Reading the reviews, I think they are somewhat over exaggerated. This place is average at best. Food is a blend of northern style and I think is bland and confused although spicy. That being said it was a nice experience in the sense it was a nice change to eat northern cuisine for a change but I certainly wouldn't rush back here in a hurry. FYI - Toilets are terrible and don't order the sour fish soup. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It's nice and delicious food ! Local food is awesome, quick service and not too expensive. Very popular place and is highly recommended. Try the bbq or Thai fish dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This amazing place was a little hard to find but look for the queue of people waiting to be seated! We had to wait for maybe 20 minutes but it was definitely worth it! The Burmese pork curry is amazing, definitely the best curry I've ever had! Portion sizes here are quite small (SE Asian sizes) so if there's 2 people order maybe 3 things and if there's more people get more! About 10 mins on a bicycle from the NW corner of the square!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Heard a lot about this restaurant so we had high expectation. The food was ok but not that great. You could find many better local restaurants.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This isn't a lie, the food is spicy. I knew this going in so went for a steamed option. My partner wanted to try it and after all the good reviews I kind of did too. However just knew I'd ask for no spice, I was told it had to have a little bit of spice in order for it to be made. But wow, it was so spicy. I only say this because for health reasons I'm not allowed overly spicy food. But my partner had the grilled pork that wasn't spicy at all and tasted lovely - so just a pre-warning in case you did what I did. Nice atmosphere here too.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is a busy restaurant in Mimman Rd, the food is great, staff friendly and the gardens give it a quiet tropical feel and yet it is a cool retreat from the heat. I'll be going back again.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went here on our first day in Chiang Mai. Good food, some interesting dishes. Puff ball mushroom with chili was interesting, grilled pork with dipping sauce amazing! Couldn't order beer at lunch for some reason even though it was on the menu. Overall i liked this place - not the best ever but good ambiance and decent feed.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: All portions are really small -. Food is good though, bit too spicy for european standards. Good location (out of the old city).

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The beef salad was super and first time trying the popping mushrooms, a local delicacy. Sai Ou was top notch and so was burmese curry. All in all a great meal and good for larger groups. Nice setting decent service. Not too expensive.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Perfect! All things are good! I like the soup best! And I will go again!!完美！所有东西都很好吃。我相信我还会再去的。最爱最爱的是这里的汤。喝了一口以后，感动到快哭了。居然有比冬阴功还好喝的汤！

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: North of thai local food that in the nice area. The taste of food is so amazing. The price is not expensive. But we have to wait in long Que.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My first time here, quite amazing with Thai Northern foods , all Dishes taste good & are original. I would love to recommend namprik with vegetables dish ( Thai Northern Hor de 'oruvre ) they have plenty of choices & many kind of food serve here. You don't have to worry about choosing dishes since they could recommend, but all dishes are good, im very impressed. They have a nice scenery which is based off northern local town styles, it makes the atmosphere great and go awesome with the food.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: After 3 days we decided to go away from Chiang Mai touristy area and move were the locals are. Although there is not much to do, very western, we discover this place were you try dishes that move away form the typical. Try the Burmese Curry, Spicy Pork and the pork shoulder. Service is good and people as nice as the Thais are :)

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Awesome selection of northern Thai food recommended by hotel staff. Love the mixed local curry which was a mix of herbs instead of the usual creamy curry sauce. Great ambience and attentive service made the wait worthwhile.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were greeted by the smoke from a barbeque grill in front of the patio entry and the crowd outside waiting for their seating. While waiting, we scanned the menu; and once we were seating we had to order immediately as the restaurant was trying to turn the tables over. The dining area was al fresco or open to the patio. We tried the pork grill (w red chili sauce), chicken in Northern-style soup (v spicy), sunflower sprouts, and fried fish (w brown sauce). The food ranged from spicy to v spicy, and all very good.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We found this place when the nearby restaurant someone recommended turned out to have disappeared. We had to wait about 35 minutes for a table (it’s quite popular), and it appears that the smart customers put in their dinner orders before they are seated, so the food is ready as soon as their table is. We ordered after we sat down, but didn’t have to wait too long … except that our “starter” (the combination hors d’oeuvres) arrived well after we finished our main course -- the staff seems somewhat frazzled. However, it was worth the wait. I thought it was some of the best food I had in a week in Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Local restaurant with a wide variety of dishes that gives one a taste of the northern cuisine. Reasonably priced and excellent local vibe. Try the grilled pork shoulder and chicken wings!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went to Chiang Mai, think that should try the northern Thai cuisine. Therefore, we found that there are a lot of good comments about this restaurant and so we tried this. We had ordered several dishes and the curry is good! But, we think that maybe the taste of northern Thai food is a little bit too strong to us. Anyway, it is a good try!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We dropped by Tong Tem Toh for an early lunch, and were quite surprised to find the place packed (with Thai people) always a good sign. As a few others have mentioned, the pork curry is delicious, rich flavours and the pork is slow cooked, and pulls apart. The Sai Ua and the BBQ pork shoulder are also delicious. We sat in the outdoor area under the shade of trees and umbrellas, great ambience, and no bugs.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Based on many recommendations, we decided to try the food at Tong Tem Toh and we were not disappointed. The restaurant has a very laid back and casual atmosphere. We ordered the fermented pork and egg cooked in banana leaf, which was very unusual and quite tasty. The Burmese pork curry was excellent. And we also ordered the sweet leaves cooked with egg which was also good. Their menu is quite extensive and if you're adventurous there are many other things to try ;) Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The place was nice in a quite street. The menu is separated into types of food and you can order different things of different dishes. The portions can be shared by group of friends. Be careful if you don't like spicy food avoid this place. If you like it just tell them not to too spicy because you will burn your mouth. Otherwise tasteful and friendly people.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Basic northen Thai restaurant, informal bench seating mainly outside. Lower cost dishes from expensive northern Thai menu. Well worth a try but please don't go if you don't like spices food or pork.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place is such a great introduction to Chiang Mai food. If you eat meat, order the pork belly curry - it's a MUST. Excellent customer service as well. If you see a vendor outside cooking on a cart.. you've come to the right place. This place is by an Italian restaurant, Why Not, and right across Secret Garden (candle store) in the guise of a fruit market. There may be a large queue... it's worth the wait.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I ate this place 3 times in one week that I have been there and they never disappointed me good food with good service I would recommended this place once you have to try

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Good price for the food which is not so big so it means you can try more stuff in one meal. There are lots of northern food which you might have no idea what it is but worth to try! There are also common food if you wanna play safe. Also make sure you ask how spicy each food is before.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We have read the reviews and know how good or authentic this northern Thai place is and thus we have decided to give it a try. Worst decision of our entire trip. The surrounding was hot and filled with flying bugs sharing food with us the entire meal. The food are all spicy, including those that do not include the word spicy in it. Warning: this place is not for anyone who is not up for an unforgettable adventure both physically or orally.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Top standard great food that never disappoints!!! Here you find food for people that can really take the spices on - but also food that young kids love. Authentic northern Thai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Food is pretty good! Maybe not worth the wait and the trip there though (we're staying on the other side of town). They also tried to serve us a dish of dried chilli flakes as one of the spicy dips (im guessing they ran out) which was pretty dodgy but we just sent it back. All of the food we've eaten in Chiang Mai has been amazing, both local restaurants and food stalls, so unless you're staying near here it's probably not worth it.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: With the Northern style of local food there are good taste of many dishes. This resteraunt located in the Nimman area that easy to go there but bot easy to find the carpark.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My partner had been wanting to try this restaurant as he had heard great things about it. The restaurant was busy however we found a table quickly. Service was a little slow and the staff only knew a little bit of english however it didn't stop us from being able to order. The food came out quickly and was pretty good. It tasted incredibly fresh but I felt lacked a bit of flavour. It was on the spicy side so beware.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I had "Chinsom" - a pork and egg dish served in a banana leaf. Tasted absolutely amazing! The waiting staff were excellent, the restaurant was clean, and the decor of the place gives a really relaxing feel. Definitely an underrated and place in Chiang Mai and I'd recommend this to anyone!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We had a couple of great meals at Tong Tem Toh during our trip to Chiang Mai. I don't know northern Thai food and can't comment on the authenticity, but our first visit there was the real eye-opener - lots of amazing flavours; very different to Bangkok and thereabouts. We went back again ... Even so, we didn't manage to try everything we wanted to. The northern Thai starter plate was the standout thing for us, though everything we had was great. The one exception was an odd puffball soup with tamarind leaves. Not that it was bad - just something you'd have to get used to, I think ... It was busy every time we went. On one occasion, although we got a table, they'd sold out of a lot of dishes. The lesson - try to get there at a reasonable time ... I will definitely go back if I get the chance to go to Chiang Mai again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The food we got not only looked mediocre, but it also did not deliver on flavor. Very disappointing, considering there was not a single table open. Kind of confusing how they could be so busy given the unimpressive meal. The other dishes we saw being brought out did not look any better. But oh well, it's Thailand...only lost $7 (for 2 sodas, 3 entrees, 2 rice, and the 10 baht tub of ice)

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you are the northern Thai people, you will know well that all dishes's taste aren't the authentic food much. But it's ok to try if you are a foreigner, not too spicy much.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were staying on Nimmanahaeminda road for two weeks so tried many cafes and restaurants around Soi 13. Tong Tem Toh was the least enjoyable. Setting and service were both very good but food was a disappointment. I tried one of the recommended meals, my husband something else from the menu. There was very little flavour in either and it seemed like the chilli was being relied upon too much. The chicken in my dish tasted dry, although the meal itself wasn't. Although not expensive, for a third of the price, and admittedly without the potted plants and uniformly dressed staff, try the Burmese/Thai open air spot on the corner of Nimmanahaeminda Road and Soi 8, which is directly opposite Soi 13. For under 100 baht you can have one of the best meals in the area. The Burmese chicken curry as a side dish and the Pad Thai noodles are hard to go past. The water is free. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: 1. Not easy to find as the restaurant only shows its name in Thai. 2. Fast service, meals are served within 10 mins after we ordered. 3. Burmese pork curry was a stand out. 4. Every dish basically is spicey, thus not recommended for those cannot accept spicy tastes. 5. Nice atmosphere with pleasant music.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh is northern resturant in Nimmana Haeminda Rd Lane 13, Chiangmai Thailand.Local food is simple but so delicious example Pork snack with Northern green chili dip ,Hang-le curry etc.If i go Chiangmai , i will go here again.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Don't be put off by the wait to get a table as it moves very quickly and is worth it. We came to Tong Tem Toh because we wanted to try some northern Thai dishes and we weren't disappointed. Would recommend the northern sausage, Burmese curry, and bbq (which you can see them cooking outside). It was very reasonably priced. The beers came in big bottles so you don't need to order one each!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: My girlfriend and I visited Tong Tem Toh the other day and had a fantastic meal here. We shared some northern sausage, BBQd pork which was excellently smokey from the grill out front, a wonderful northern Thai chicken curry, some very good mixed vegetables and sticky rice. Really great meal with good service. We had to wait for about 20 mins before but it was certainly worth it.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Love to visit this restaurant everytime I travel to Chiang Mai. Always crowded with customers from many nationalities. The food is yummy. Dont miss the local mushroom called hed-tob.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: the local staff in my hostel recommended this Tong for some local taste. strongly recommend the bbq pork. this dish is very freshly cooked because you can see the staff roasting the pork in front of the restaurant. to taste like a local you must order sticky rice instead of the normal rice. fried vegetable with eggs is also very delicious. the most important thing is that the price is very friendly.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Great northern Thailand food. Expect to wait for 20 to 30 mins to be seated during dinner time. The Burmese Pork curry was great and I would definitely recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I have passed this restaurant for many times,every time there are lots of people waiting outside. The BBQ is well-taste,I like the sauce.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: It's worth waiting for a seat in this casual and friendly restaurant. That's because it serves some exciting, spicy and deeply delicious food. While not every dish is highly spiced or delectable, the ones that I went back for over and over were a stand out. Try the salad with baby tamarind leaves, the Burmese curry or the pork shoulder and you'll become a repeat diner. If you're really bold, sip on the Northern Thai spicy soup and be prepared for such deep, red hot flavours. I should add the servers are young and approachable, the guy grilling at the front is a sweetheart. Nice bathroom too. Writing this makes me wish I was back in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh is a popular Northern local food shop locate in Nimman-hemin road. They sell original food that you can taste the northern style food. Good taste. The price is not expensive. Easy to go there from Nimman-hemin road. For foreigner, I recommend you to try this once, you can ask the local people to the direction to the shop. I love Gang jor, Sai oua and Nam prik noom the most.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Small portion good for 2, very reasonable price and love their sweet leaves with ants egg and grilled intestine.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh is hip for Chiang Mai locals and younger tourists. It is located in the hip area of Chiang Mai as well. If you know northern Thai food, I do not need to tell you which one you should order, just order, you won't be disappointed. The place is hard to find and not many place on the street to park. I suggest getting there by public transportation. It is always a line to get the table but the turn over was quick the day I went. Every staff member was nice and patient with our request and questions. Will go back again next time.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Every day they are standing in line to get in. We walked by the other day and it had only been open for 20 minutes so went went to see what's all the fuss. Maybe I've lived here too long. The Burmese style pork curry was a stand out. The rest was okay but have too many great places and definitely won't wait in a queue to get in!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is the our second visit to the restaurant and we are ready for the long queue and waiting time. We arrived at 7:30pm or so and waited for almost one hour before we got in. We ordered the grilled pork with the intestine, grilled snake head fish, fried vegetable with eggs, grilled eggplants, pork soup with bamboo shoots. We were then told that they have run out of the snake head fish and we have to change to another type of fish and it was fine with us. Then they said they have finished the egg plants and so we have decided to skip the dish. Then we were told that the vegetable we ordered were also finished and ask us to change to another one. It was also fine with us. The grilled pork and the intestines were delicious as usual. The pork soup was good too. The vegetable was so tiny in portion compared with their usual portion. We were wondering whether it is the last bit of vegetable they have left . We then waited for a long time for the fish and when we checked it out with the waitress we were told they have sold out all the fish. But no one has told us about this as otherwise we did not have to keep waiting . So we checked the bill and when we left we found the guy in the BBQ stove at the entrance was grilling egg plants : the egg plants they told me that have been sold out!!!!! Will I come back again ? May be, but better do it at lunch time Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The location can be hidden for a visitor but the food is super yummy here. Price are reasonable as well. Please prepare to see the long queue during dinner time. I would suggest you to take the number and go to the nearby cafe/pub to have a drink which what I did to kill the waiting time.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Andy Ricker of Pok-Pok fame highly recommends this place...yes, you may have to wait a bit to get in--lunch time is easier than dinner time...yes, the food is spicy...yes, the menu is mostly full of northern Thai dishes...and yes, your fellow diners will include students, office workers, and lots of Chinese folks. . .but it is all worth it because everything served at this wonderful restaurant is full of flavor, delicious, delectable, correctly prepared, and FABULOUS. The staff are all helpful and spot-on. . .just order away--doesn't really matter what specific things you eat--all of it will be tasty and satisfying. Wash everything down with an icy-cold Chang or Leo, and you will be good to go. This is one of our favorite food joints in all of Chiang Mai. . .enjoy! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is a no-frills eatery that serves up a wide variety of Lanna-style food in a beautiful courtyard. The food is nicely spiced and features fresh, authentic ingredients. The menu includes some Burmese-influenced dishes such as gaeng hang lay, but is otherwise quite well constrained to Northern Thai food. It can get very busy; we arrived at 12:15pm on a Thursday and only had to wait a few minutes. It seems that the lunch crowd consists of many university students and local office workers, so lunch may be the best time to come if you want to avoid a long wait. The menu, like most in Thailand, is hard to decipher if you don't read Thai; the names of dishes are in Thai script and the descriptions are badly translated into English with no transliteration of the item name. You can stick to the Recommended section of the menu, or if you're hunting for something in particular, do your research before you come so you know how it's written in Thai script. If you order rice, make sure to clearly specify whether you want sticky or steamed; they will default to steamed if you are a foreigner. I try not to repeat restaurants while traveling, but with Tong Tem Toh, I may end up making an exception... Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Went during a late afternoon and had no wait !! Incredibly lucky after reading everyone else's reviews. Food was delicious and SPICY !

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: LOVED this meal. Even though we waited an hour to get in, it was wellworth it. The barbecue pork rocked and we had a couple of interesting soups that were terrific. Wish we could go back for seconds!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: After having read great reviews we decided to go here- the farthest restaurant from our hotel,for dinner. Called to reserve and after a long discussion understood that you need to go there ,take a number and wait.Got there at 7.40 and were told initially,they were full for the night! On the phone they had said come by 9. Only due to hubby's persuasion skills he got a number-197! Went off to look for a bar and found Beer Republic- spent about an hour plus 15 there before we came back,waited another 15 before our number was called! After all this- food was just okay.We had much better food on our way to the artisan villages,on the left- big restaurant with a small pond and tables around it and in the building. All in all,unless you are planning on partying later in this district or living nearby- not worth the trip.If you do go,get there before 7 to have a meal in another hour. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Hipster nonsense. Waited an hour, then they gleefully revealed half menu unavailable. Waiter in red tshirt was horrible

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: You won't see many tourists here (always refreshing), and I can see how some people won't appreciate the cuisine (it's not a Pad Thai place), but if you you are a real foodie and open to some new flavors, do yourself a favor and go eat at Tong Tem Toh. This hip and crowded yet modest restaurant serves high quality delicious northern Thailand dishes. Atmosphere is great as the place attracts cool younger crowd from the Nimman neighborhood. Dishes are small and not expensive, so don't worry to order 2-4 dishes per person. Our favorites were the Burmese pork curry, Northern style Chinese soup, omelette, and chicken wings. The place has lineups for dinner so I would recommend going there for lunch if you want to avoid them. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We waited for over 1 hour before we can get the table. Very tasty authentic Northern Thai food. It's on the spicy side. The fermented pork and egg was perfectly cooked, coupled with some raw garlic and hot pepper intensify the taste. We also loved the sticky rice and chicken wrapped in banana leaves and also the spicy fish. Overall, this is a place to go to in Chiang Mai if anyone would want to experience authentic Northern Thai cuisine.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We went to this area of town after reading it was the place to be but on arrival found the bars and restaurants were deserted. Except for this place which looked busy so we tried to get a table to be to be told to take a number and join the queue on the street for 45mins. We didn't mind and went for beer near by. When we got back we still waited for what seemed like ages. Made worse by the bbq area being on the street outside so it just really rubs your nose in it! However when stuck intestines on the bbq the place did start to lose its allure. When the couple before us were shown in we were given a menu and asked for our order while still standing on the street. We ordered and shortly after were taken to our seats. Food was then all ready straight away and we were given a flat open bottle of beer to go with it. I don't remember asking for a flat one! The food was ok but nothing special, no careful presentation or anything given to you to accompany it like bread. No mention of desert. Portions a bit small so was hungry as soon as we left. But the problems is because the food was thrown at you as soon as you sat down you ended up spending more time stood queuing on the street outside than enjoying your evening meal. Why the place was so popular I will never know! It is cheap though so didn't bother complaining about the flat beer which didn't get drunk or the fact they charged us for a box of ice which we didn't ask for or use! To be honest I wanted to see the west side of town but having done so I would suggest that you would be better off in the old town with the tourists where there is an atmosphere and better food with no queue. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This cuisine is unlike the typical dishes we are used to in terms of Thai cuisine. It's more of the northern Thai food. It is exquisite but not quite suitable for our taste, especially for kids, my little one doesn't like it at all. I believe however some of my northern Thai friends will appreciate it better. The price of the food was reasonable though.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: After a trip on the Art scene - creative marked we went to get lunch at this beatiful little place. The service was good The price very fair The food absolutely excellent The dinning area located in a small garden

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh is located in the center of Niman area (Soi13) Chiangmai. No private parking offered. You can find many local dishes here with reasonable price. Food is a bit spicy but tasty. Guests are recommended to be there early otherwise queueing.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Ordered 6 dishes (a mixture of curry, soup, stir fry and vegetables) and all were poor: 1. The quality of food was seriously bad 2. The only flavour I could taste was overwhelmingly chilli (and I love spicy food). Seriously disappointed given the strong reviews on tripadvisor and foodie websites. Maybe an off day but I can't recommend this place.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Amazing food! The fermentet pork was amazing, actually everything i had was well presented and cooked. Service was friendly and the atmosphere was perfect! It took me a while until i found it, but worth the truble.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: In the lookout for good authentic local cuisines, I would normally avoid the trendy looking restaurant with English menu and boy, this restaurant is one of the exceptions! Situated not too far from the old city wall, the crowd build up as early as 6.30pm. I arrived on the weekday 7.00pm and had to queue for 25 minutes for a table for 2. You can order your food while in the queue. Although the restaurant has no air conditions, the diners are cooled by many fans around at the indoor sitting area (semi open air). Open air sitting areas takes up another half of the restaurant, Most of the food in the menu feature northern Thai food (Lanna cuisine) and there are a few photos in the menu as reference. Staff are very attentive and energetic, some with good english commands, some are with limited english understanding. However that's not a huge issue when ordering food. Food arrives in 10 minutes time after we are seated. Food wise, it is really out of this world GOOD! I've ordered the grilled spiced sausages, jackfruit salad, sweet vegetable soup with ant's eggs (yes you read that right), grilled pork and intestines, stir fry ball mushrooms with herbs and spices, 1 large bottle of beer, 1 soft drink and 1 bottle of mineral water. All food taste amazing! But one word of caution, as it is very authentically thai, some food might be too spicy to your taste bud. The waiter will ask you when taking your orders, try to request to have mild spiciness if you are sensitive to spiciness. From all the food I've ordered, the grilled pork stands out to be the best of all. It is so good so that I've ordered another plate of the grilled pork! The total bill comes up to about 610 bahts ( ~ 11 pounds). Credit cards are usually accepted in this restaurant but the credit card terminal was down while I was there. Bottom line, eat here if you want to have a taste of authentic northern Thai food without fearing of hygienic issue or breaking the bank. Be there early to avoid queuing up for too long. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: If you want to try the traditional northern style of thai food here is the most recommended @nimman 17. Cheap price + good taste. Should visit before 17.00 after that you have to get a queue

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This is a super Thai restaurant. Authentic and the locals come here. The northern Thai spicy sausages are super as is the pork curry. Very laid back and cool music. Good value for money and not swamped with tourists. Thank you from Beijing.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Normally in Thailand if you want to go for authentic and tasty, you need to go to a food stall or a hole in the wall. The trendy, nicely designed venues with comfortable seating normally serve far less tasty fare. This restaurant is one of the exceptions. Trendy and nicely designed, it still serves authentic, great Lanna food. Every evening people - mainly locals - will queue up to get a seat. especially the items from the grill are excellent.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Recently spent a week in Chiang Mai and on my last day my host (a Thai faculty member at Chiang Mai University) took me here for lunch....one of her favorite places in the neighborhood. It was a scrumptious lunch, we feasted on a sampler appetizer plate with sticky rice, and also had a delectable green leafy vegetable curry (a light brothy curry) with ant eggs, a wet season delicacy. I only wished I had learned of this place earlier, as it was a place I would have liked to have eaten at multiple times! A favorite place!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place is a bit hidden, but when you find it, you won't be disappointed. The food is really good and the restaurant has nice outdoorsy patio vibe. Keep in mind this is and outdoorsy place, therefore, could be a bit hot at times, but that wouldn't stop you from going there and trying some of their delicious dishes. Below are some of my recommendations. I've rate them from a scale of 1- 5, five being the best! Burmese pork curry - 3.5 Deep fried cut Tilapia fish in fish sauce - 3.5 Northern style steamed chicken with herbs in banana leaf- 3 Deep fried pork- 3.5 Spicy and sour chicken soup- 4 Young tamarind leaf spicy salad- 3 I highly recommend this place for a delicious authentic northern Thai cuisine. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place is really popular with locals and tourists. We got there at 6pm and there is already a short waitlist. We got our table in about 10 minutes. Service is very efficient and fast. Food arrived quickly. The portions are not big so you can try a big variety. We shared 6 dished between 2 adults and a small child. The food is delicious! We tried a tomato dip, local veggie with egg, young tamarind leaf salad, fried fish, steamed chicken in banana leaf and pork with coconut. Everything was great!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We were in search of a great authentic dishes while in Chiang Mai since we heard so much about the cuisine - and found plenty of it - but Tom Tem Toh took the cake!! It was one of the only restaurants where we didn't recognize many of the dishes, so we ordered nearly half the menu. Best decision ever. Everything was amazing and the condiment station was set up next to your table instead of taking precious space on the table (so necessary). We ate family-style which most others seemed to do as well. I can't say enough about this place. Order it all. Service was slow but we were there late and it did seem to close earlier than other restaurants nearby. But our waitress was awesome and super accommodating to us 'green' Americans. It's 100% worth the visit - just a short scooter ride from the Old City. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tasty and fair-priced northern Thai cuisine. Portions are fair for a small group of friends. Fairly balanced menu for meat, vegetables and snacks.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Enjoyed the good Northern Thai food here! The Burmese Pork Curry is a must try, along with the spicy pork sausage! Affordable and value for money. Plus they play pretty good music here as well.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I've eaten here several times with my Thai friends who love Northern Thai dishes. They think the food is quite good. For me, it's just very ordinary food, relatively poor service and nothing to rave about at all. My problem may be that I have been to some of the best Thai restaurants in Thailand, including the Sra Bua by Kiin Kiin at the Siam Kempiniski hotel in Bangkok and Lan Na Thai and Bo.lan, both in Bangkok. I paid considerably more, especially at Sra Bua, but as they say, you get what you pay for. I recommend the the Tong Tem Toh if you're in chiang Mai, so you get a taste for Northern Thai food. It can get quite crowded during the dinner hours (6-8pm), so be prepared to wait sometime before even getting a table, let alone your the food you ordered. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Recommended by local Thai friends as a must try in Chiang Mai. The restaurant is trendy but not pretentious. Quality of the food is remarkable; a must try if you want to sample northern Thai dishes. The outdoor seating gets a bit hot during the day but the food is worth it. Take some time to walk around the neighborhood with bike shops and hipster cafes.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Stopped of here for lunch, to indulge in a Northern Thai feast. Very prompt service from an extensive menu. Lot of vegetable options, I would claim it is vegetarian, but it is possible to avoid meat per se. I had a Burmese pork curry in a sweet tamarind sauce, with rice; northern thai sausage; fermented sour sausage with egg steamed in banana leaf, and green chilies and pepper relish with steamed vegetables, accompanied by 2 soda waters all were excellent and it came to 233 Bht. less than £5 or $7. its a little may down Soi 13 off the Nimmanhaeminda Road Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Good selection of Northern-Thai food. I think one of the best food in Chiang Mai. So, Don't miss visit there.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: loved this resto - its laidback cool, serving really food lanna food - thai sausages stuffed with herbs, a slightly bitter, more-ish eggplant salad, a pork curry with banana stem. Nimanhaemin road is a little bit of tokyo in chiang mai. super cool neighbourhood full of little restos and bars, great stores, loads of chic hairdressers. so its a great neighbourhood to hang out in. definitely reccomended for those interested in eating good northern thai food

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Tong Tem Toh offers a good selection of Northern-Thai food. Some tastes authentic enough and I am saying as a Northern-Thai origin while some are not really close to what Northern-Thai cuisine tastes or looks like.Still I could not deny that they offer definitely tasty dishes here. Try the Appetiser dish, some Tom Saab, Sai Oau and various chilly pastes with sticky rice.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Personally speaking, it is so normal for me. I have ordered the most famous three dishes. They are all ok but with no-surprise. Plus, we have told them not to make us spicy soup but they still do. Even after we asked them to change us to the not spicy one, the soup they bring us was still spicy.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: I thought the food was pretty good - just didn't care for the salad we ordered. Just as we were finishing our meal, a huge amount og some flying insect came pouring out of a tree near our table. Otherwise the outdoor setting was nice.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Sorry, have to give this place a 1-star review. Food is good and scene is very nice, but service is terrible. 45 minutes to wait just for 2 people and basic dishes.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: "Tong Tem Toh" is very popular among Chinese and Thai tourists. If you want to eat it, you will have to queue.. Recommended to eat during the day time because the dinner with people eat a lot. If the service is good neither you are not impressed with this restaurant The food flavor traditional not spicy. Westerners can eat many menu. Recommended: hors d'oeuvre mixed... you will taste the local food types such as pork, eggs, vegetables and pork, chili paste folk

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Do come here early to ensure that you get a place and the desired dishes. Queue starts even before 6pm! Well that was what I observed on a Saturday evening. Since we were way behind the packing order, we went for an hour massage nearby before returning to this restaurant for dinner. Its northern Thai food was great and really really spicy! Forewarn to ask the level of spiciness of the dishes before ordering. Staff service was great and efficient, food service was pretty fast. Dining ambiance is very lively. There's outdoor setting as well. All in all, definitely a recommended restaurant to try in Nimman area. By the way, this place closes early too, by 9pm if I am not wrong. So do be here early! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There's a long queue at lunchtime for this spot so grab a number and then pop across the road for a beer while you wait. Once seated the food is pretty quick, be aware that some popular dishes will sell out. The venue is not fancy but you will enjoy the meal.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: The place is alright, food is good but u need to wait for a long time to be served and it's always packed. It was ok to try once but i wouldn't come back.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: One of my favorite northern food restaurant in Chiang mai, restaurant always packed! Good food highly recommend with reasonable price. Best taste and its in Nimman Area.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Nice restaurant, family friendly, but food quite spicy. Inside outside seating. Must try their Northern Thai pork curry with ginger

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We went there on the recommendation of the bank where we exchange Thai currency. The young lady was very informative and gave us walking direction to the restaurant. The main road is Nimmanhaemin Rd and the restaurant is one of the many small joints located on the soi. Tong Tem Toh is very tranquil where an outside patio and an inside with fans are separated with trees and plants. There is also a square wooden well in the outside area. Our server was very friendly and took time to explain the different dishes to us. They are very northern Thai and the menu is extensive with meat, seafood, curries and soup. There are also the traditional white rice and the sticky rice. We had ordered seafood curry and a chiangmai chicken soup. Really do not how they made chicken so tender when they have to boil it to make the soup base. As the food was so good so we took their menu and took photos of the name and location of the restaurant. As we were paying, our server handed us a Japanese tourist guide of chiangmai. Though we were mistaken to be Japanese, her kindness and the usefulness of the guide have certainly marked my impression. I love it there and will certainly recommend this charm to anyone who's visiting the area. By the way, the Nimman area is worth spending the time to explore. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This place is great and packed with locals. We tried some chilli fried pork, stir fried morning glory, Chiang Mai sausage, and crispy pork with Nam Prik Ong – a chilli dip that was so delicious I finished it off without any thing to dip in it at the end! I have reviewed it with some great street food finds here: http://floandelle.blogspot.co.uk/2014/06/a-homage-to-chiang-mai-street-food.html

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: We came upon this place via Mark Wiens' indispensable Eating Thai Food blog, and are glad we did. The restaurant is a funky venue for traditional Northern Thai food, all of which is excellent. We tried the fermented sausage and egg--sounds weird, but delicious--as well as a pork curry and a few other things. The restaurant seems to be a favourite of relatively upmarket locals, but is still priced in line with most Chiang Mai places; ie, dinner for 2 including a couple of beers shouldn't run much more than 500 baht. As a bonus, the restaurant is located in the fun Niman area, which is a sort of hipster bar-and-restaurant zone about a million times nicer to wander around than the grimy tourist precincts around Tha Phae and central Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: This was such a fantastic find... Probably the best dinner we had in Thailand and maybe anywhere! Located in the coolest neighborhood in Chaing Mai, it has a killer, simple ambiance and the food was amazing. We brought our 2.5 year old daughter with us and she loved it as well.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: For north's food, I'm very appreciate with this restaurant. Everyone have smile and nice service. Good food and good taste, You will enjoy with north's food at Chieng Mai (Soi Nimman 13)

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Came across this local restaurant while walking aimlessly along the area. We were attracted by its robust atmosphere. The eating area is in a nice garden, partly covered. Almost all the guests are local, so the food must be authentic. It serves northern Thai food. The menu is in Thai & English and has a page of recommended dishes if you are a foreigner. The food is good, though too spicy for us. Price is reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: Very good, the BBQ pork and chicken must order, and very cheap but delicious ^^ Many Thai people go there , very near Mango Tango!

Restaurant_Name: ต๋องเต็มโต๊ะ
Detail: There were 3 rice bugs in my sticky rice and told to the waiter. Without apologizing he brought a new pouch of sticky rice and there were another rice bug in it. No apologies whatsoever. When I got the bill there was a bucket of ice in thai language included sneakily. When i asked them to remove that and sticky rice from the bill, the waiter shout at me saying "but you ate it!". I ate it before I found rice bugs in it and stopped eating it when I found them. About 2spoonful? In the end they deducted sticky rice and sneakily included ice bycket price but it was one of the most unpleasant and unappetite experience in Thailand. Be aware of the Rice Bugs in the rice!!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Cook and some employees When ordering food His expression was not welcoming, he sat still and didn't want to please. The worst service Why do you come to do service work like this? It's known as an a la carte restaurant. I feel very bad. with Burmese employees who come to serve customers like this Please go back to Burma.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Food was tasty but they overcharged us (Asian foreigner and Western foreigner). Before we ordered, I asked for the prices of some items and was told it was 30 - 40 THB per item. When my Western foreigner husband was paying the bill, we were charged 40 - 50 THB per item. It was the same waitress who told us the price and got us the bill. The people running this place are dishonest. If you don't like being discriminated against because of your white skin, avoid this place.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I had the tea leaf salad and it was delicious. Very cheap too. It's an outside restaurant so no AC, but definitely worth a visit if you're interested in Burmese food.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: A little gem of a restaurant. The food is fresh and tasty and the service is really good. Be aware the portions are extremely generous so worth choosing carefully.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Yooo i found this place by accident cause i was around Nimmanhaenim rd and looking for authentic cheap Thai food. My foot just brought me to this place and was surprisingly g00000d!!! I ordered the shrimp pad thai and my boyf ordered beef stew. Once the pad thai entered my mouth, i just couldnt talk! It was so goooood. I opened tripadvisor and read all reviews. This place was quite famous. There were no prices on the menu but it was cheap. Spent 110 bath for 2 people. Worth a shot guys!!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Friendly service. Ice and water provided. There were no prices on our menu but it was cheap. Eggplant curry 40 baht Chicken curry 50 baht Chicken fried rice 45 baht (cans with soup) Steamed rice 10 baht Pepsi bottle 10 baht. Although the portions appeared small, they were tasty and filling. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I live on Nimman Soi 6, so this restaurant is in the neighborhood. It is a small place that serves both Thai and Burmese food, though I have only had the Burmese dishes. It's a great place for a very cheap, tasty meal. There is covered seating but no A/C, but this is a very basic place, go for the food not the ambiance. I have gone several times, usually order the tea leaf salad which is tasty, have tried both the beef and pork curries, which were very good, and the tamarind salad. They do sell beer and soda. It's a great value, most dishes are I think 40 or 50 baht each, the service is pretty quick, though the staff are young and I don't think speak any English. I recommend this nice little restaurant. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I must admit their potato curry and eggplant curry are delicious. With no doubt I recommend visit on this tinny place 😎

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Address on Google is wrong, it's Soi 8, off main Nimman road. Humble restaurant, but the salads are so yummy & inexpensive. This is street food at it's finest. My favorite was the tea leaf salad, but the pennywort salad was good, as was the young tamarind leaf salad. They were out of the pumpkin soup, so we got some chicken pad Thai, which was decent and had julienne strips of fried tofu in it (is this a Chiang Mai thing?). 4 menus for 165 B.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: What a wonderful way to start our time here. The food was tasty and although the place was a little basic the ambiance was nice and the price was ridiculously low. We thoroughly recommend if you're after a cheap but tasty meal.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: 120 THB for these 3 plates. Dirt Cheap! Ive been coming here for years. I have yet to find another place which serves such delicious salads. Shan style rice (center), what a unique dish, oily mashed rice with great flavour. On the far left, my top salad of the world of all times, Tamarind Leaf Salad. I usually finish this plate under 1 minute. Then on the right, Tea Leaf salad with crunchy fried beans. 120 THB / 3.4 euro for all 3 dished. Can this be beaten anywhere?? This restaurant gets a 10 out of 10 for me! Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: The food here is awesome. This is my first time to taste and I was completely impressed. The taste is very delicious, the serving size is also very good, the price is very reasonable. it is so different. It is definitely worth coming here. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This place is a duo menu "Burma - Thai" For myself, as I can eat good thai food very easily in Thailand, I always order burmese food and it's always a full pleasure. Sincerly, the burmese food is simply delicious and you can order alot because sooo cheap. You will enjoy for sure

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: The place may not be fancy, but the food is amazing. It takes a little while for the staff to warm up to you, but once they do, they are very nice and have a great sense of humor. I go there all the time and I love it! It's DELICIOUS!!! And it's soooo cheap!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We ate well here. It was our first night in Chiang Mai, and I hadn't had Burmese food since the restaurant near my office closed. We had tea leaf salad, tamarind salad, and pork curry. The tea leaf salad was so good that we ordered a second plate of it.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I recommend the tea leaf salad, tomato salad, tamarind leaf salad, Shan style rice, and a chicken and potato curry. That will feed two. So many delicious flavours and aromas.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Look for the shop front sign saying ..phad thai boran.. traditional phad thai.... They serve both Thai and Burmese food..two separate menus. Reasonable price..palatable..easy to find once you are in Nimman area. I had the tea leaf salad #13 and a prawn dish #18 with some rice for 100 baht total. Yum.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: A group of 3 of us had dinner here last night per the recommendation of a friend. Unlike other places we have eaten so far, the portions here are manageable and perfect for sharing. We ended up ordering the tea leaf salad twice because we liked it so much. All in all, we had 6 dishes for 3 people plus 3 big beers for a total of 440 baht!!!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We were brought here by Mouis from A Chef's Tour. We sat in the back room and were brought tea leaf salad and tamarind leaf salad. Didn't like the salads very much, but the chilli dips were delicious and other items on the menu looked good. The owner was interested to learn that we were going to Myanmar as part of our holiday. Very basic restaurant but toilets were fine!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This place is not fancy but local home feeling. Food was amazing and so was the price. Highly recommended if you want to try Burmese cuisine

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Cheap food quickly delivered and good service. This is not fine dining by any means, just great local food, freshly cooked. Love the tea leaf salad, chicken or pork fried rice and the stir fries. On the corner of Nimman Rd and Soi 8 - check out what they've got cooking in the large pot at the front, often their famous bean dish.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Tamarind salad, egg curry, rice. Excellent food, very tasty, average portions. Would recommend if in the area.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Cheap , nice and easy going. I love tamarind salad , bean soup and fraud egg. The staff is very polite. I come here almost everyday. Food is cheap. They even cook everything for me without tomato. Keep it up guys.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
