Detail: Don't expect the Ritz as it's more of a street food eatery but it's authentic and wonderful Burmese food. There's street side as well as an inside room and it was quite busy when we were there for lunch. The tea leaf salad (almost their national dish) was amazing, as was the penny wort salad and goat curry. The inside room or "library" (with no books) has a history to it which is worth researching.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Have eaten here three times. Good superb, highly recommend the eggplant curry. It's like home cooking. Sure the place is not fine dining but neither is eating from a Thai food cart. Their bean soup is like a mildly spiced dal. Very good

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Not the cleanest appearance--you can enjoy basic Burmese food in an atmosphere that presents life as it was 40 years ago in CM--pre-Lonely Planet etc inspired travel boom, let alone Internet. Why do you travel, to dine in trendy newly created spaces? want to step out of the tourist bubble, for just a moment for a decent meal? Besides the basic curries, try the tamarind (young) leaf salad. All our curries were light in spiciness. Staff friendly and food inexpensive.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Went here after we went to another burmese place by the west gate,which was way better. The prices were better here but I would rather pay for more flavor

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Restaurant is at the top of Soi 8 (opposite Soi 13) Nimmanhaemin Road, across the road from the 7-11 at the corner of Soi 8. Check what is on the menu by looking at the pots on the table at the front by the cooking area. Authentic Myanmar taste, prices very budget friendly. I recommend the dhal soup, aubergine curry, tomato puree OR roselle with bamboo shoots to go with the curry, the last two are quite spicy. Tea leaf salad is an acquired taste for those who have visited Myanmar. Service could be faster, but everything is heated up again before serving, which could account for this. Good tasty street food. Tip: There is a separate room attached with more tables in an enclosed space, not immediately visible at first glance. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Low-key but popular eating place, with Burmese and Thai menus, and quite extensive range of food to try, including salads and noodle dishes. Tea leaf salad worth trying.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I followed the Guardian website which highly recommended on this typical restaurant. I didnt try the Burmese food. But the Thai food was delicious and super cheap!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: They have the best tamarind salad! I've tried the mango salad and tea leaf salad and they are good as well. Very cheap and quick service, although the place might need a boost up.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: ...but a great place for every day dinner. Nong Bee's offer a friendly service and great burmese dishes, served in a very simple way. It's cheap, the place doesn't show up with a beautiful decoration but the quality and authenticity connected with the nice staff is unbeatable. Have a look for the pots close to the cooking place - they contain a kind of daily menu. Don't forget to have a look for the Thai dishes. Places like this making Chiang Mai a place to stay.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This is one of our favorite restaurants in Chiang Mai. It's no-fuss restaurant that serves really good and inexpensive Burmese food. We like the potato & chicken curry, tea leaf salad, eggplant curry. The staff are all so nice.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This restaurant was recommended to us by a local who told us it was one of the best places to try real Burmese food outside of Burma. The restaurant is just off the main road and is easy to find. It is fairly basic - a few tables on the road and a few under cover. Staff were all helpful and service was prompt. There is a WC but don't expect too much! The salads (tea leaf and tamarind) were very good. The currys (beef and chicken) were too bland for my liking - I do appreciate this is typical of Burmese cooking but were not to my taste, hence why I have only given the restaurant 3 stars. Definitely worth a visit - extremely cheap, good quality ingredients and an opportunity to try traditional Burmese foods but for me, unfortunately, it didn't live up to the high reviews posted by others on this site. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: A friend took me here the first time a little over a year ago. Then I moved to the same block, and now eat here at least 5x a week. Sometimes twice a day. Aon cooks great Thai food, but the Burmese flavors and dishes are just amazing. We fed 6 people here one day for about $12US, and there was leftovers.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: When you lay eyes on this place, you might be hesitant. BUT eat here! It was one of the best meals we've had in Thailand - three of us feasted (bean soup, 2 tea leaf salads, chicken biryani, vegetable biryani and two LARGE beers) for about $11. Yes I said 3 people, delicious feast, and 11 dollars! Do it.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Several of the dishes I ordered were out, so I changed it up and cancelled one. The dishes are small, so order several if you go here. I got chicken curry with gourd, tamarind leaf salad, and tomato salad (that last one I don't remember ordering, but they brought it). They were out of tea leaf salad and the sour soups. I loved the flavor of the chicken curry...wish it had included more than 3 tiny pieces of chicken. It had a richer, warmer spice than typical Thai flavor profile. Tamarind salad was good combo of zingy flavors and crunchy texture. Tomato salad was on the boring side. Staff seemed confused and unfriendly, but some other Fareng were successfully making conversation with them, so maybe it was just me? At the end they either messed up the bill or were trying to bilk me out of an extra 50 bhat. Glad I did the math! The waiter seemed unwilling to get his colleague with better english skills to explain the extra charges. I think the map on the listing is incorrect... It is further south on Nimman. Google has the correct address. Overall I'd like to try Burmese food again, but did not have the glowing experience of some other reviewers. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Decided to make the journey across town to try this restaurant. The tamarind leaf salad was fantastic, as many other reviewers have mentioned. And, at 30 Baht, it made for an affordable light lunch.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This is the best food in the city! My friends and I ate here every chance we got. The curry is amazing, but the Tamarind salad is the best thing on the menu. It can be hard to see on the street because it is small, so look for the Burmese Library sign!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Great food, freshly prepared with great value. Recommend tamarind leaf salad that traditional food from upper Myanmar and people believe good for health, tea leaf salad that is deliciously unique and egg curry with fresh tomatoes. Prices are so reasonable here we pay less than 300 baht for 5 dishes, two rice and two water bottles. Staff are nice and the one prepare salads uses gloves. More Farangs(white) and Africans seem to enjoy eating there than Thais. This restaurant is not far from salad concepts - European style salads and coffee wish with excellent coconut pie.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We've eaten all over in Thailand including Bangkok, Phi Phi, Railey, Krabi and Ko Phangnan and this restaurant by far is the best. The Flavors are unreal and the service was incredible! This is a must for any traveler!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Really good Burmese and Thai food. Recently returning from Burma I've had a craving for tea leaf salad. They nailed the tea leaf salad, fresh tomatoes and a hint of spicy. Also tried the pad kra pow off the Thai menu which was also very good. Minced flavorful pork with a side of white rice. Not much of an atmosphere but the food makes up for that!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Always perfect for when you are lost for choice for a spot to eat, the food is hearty and affordable,

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I have been coming here every other day for the past month as we moved in to an apartment close to Nimman street for our 2 month stay. I always order the Burmese Tea Leaf Salad because it's so refreshingly delicious. Antioxidants from fermented green tea leaves and a mixture of crunchy lentils and broad beans, countered with juicy diced tomatoes, all with a bit of kick. When I'm really hungry, I order the salad along with the pork and basil stir fry with a bowl of rice. All of this would be cheap at 70 Baht, dishes average at 30-50 baht each. This restaurant is more of a street-style type place offering good cheap eats. It has actual tables and chairs and a real roof overhead with some tables on the sidewalk. It can be hard to find and is diagonally across from The Salad Concept under the awning on the Soi and moron Nimman Rd. Free water with ice is available and is safe to drink. Though the coconut is delicious (you get the whole coconut and a spoon to eat the coconut meat). Staff is very nice but they sometimes take a little while to get you the menu and take orders, but the food is served up rather quickly. They will provide you with 2 menus, one for Thai food and the other Burmese food and a notepad so you can write your dishes down. Enjoy the taste of Thai and Burma here. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We have been coming here pretty regularly since arriving in Chiang Mai. The Tea Leaf salad is our favorite. I also like the Chicken and Potato curry. It's a great place for inexpensive food. We typically can get 3 dishes + rice for around 130 baht.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Some friends took me to this small and a little hidden place in the middle of the busy Nimmanhemin and after that I went back every week in the reaming time I was in Chiang Mai. It is indeed a simple place but what really matters is the food and that is delicious. Every dish that I had was amazing and so tasty. It is a really great option If you want to try another kind of Asian food, especially because Burmese cuisine is not very popular. The staff is really kind and sweet. The price range is very affordable. I wish I could go back there every week as I was doing. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Northern Thai/Lanna cuisine may be the epicentre of any foodie while in Chiang Mai, but the food from the neighbouring countries of Burma/Vietnam/Laos also makes its small presence felt in the city. While you'd find some cross-over dishes like Khao Soi/Noodle soup, the Burmese Restaurant and Library has a lot of authentic Burmese fare to offer (along with some Thai dishes too). Finding this place turned out to be a bit of a task for me, but its not very difficult. With you back towards the 'Salad Concept', simply cross the road to the otherside in a small lane and this place will be to your left. I got here at 4 pm, so service was a bit slow as the staff was busy with the evening preparations I think. Ordered the famous Tea leaf salad and Shan rice. The former needs no introduction and surely a must eat while here; the later was something new for me. Shan province is in the Eastern part of Burma (that's all I know). The dish was sticky rice cooked in tomato paste. This combination was incredible and nothing that I'd ever tried before. The salad was the best vegetarian dish I've had outside of India. The rice too was pretty impressive. Unfortunately, couldn't try any of their other Burmese offerings as I was stuffed. Be sure to visit here for their Burmese fare. They have loads of vegetarian options. And this place is easy on the pocket too. Staff speaks limited English, but that's fine as they have the menu in English. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: The salads here are great and very cheap. A must try is the tea leaves salad and the tamarind leaves salad. The tea leaves salad is very unique, I love it. The main dishes I wasent too exited about. But for the salads I walked 3 times 5km each way, In april for lunch!!!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This is one of the cheapest options in the trendy Nimman area. My friend and I sampled 4 dishes, and we each had our favourites. I love the tea leaf salad. It's tangy and fresh, but my friend found it too tart. He preferred the chicken curry, which to me had too much bone and skin. We both enjoyed crispy stir-fried morning glory and the Burmese rice which is mixed with tomato and onion. The bill was 120 baht, which included water. The staff here are super friendly, so it's fun to chat if you're sitting near the outdoor kitchen.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We went there 3 times and loved the salads also: tea leaf salad, tamarind salad, tomato salad, a very interesting crunchy burmese fried rice, as well as the pork curry. My husband never orders salads but he was surprised how good these were. This is a very basic place but you can byo a beer from the 7eleven and enjoy the street experience, and they do get very busy so be patient.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Me and my wife eat there almost everyday for 3 months! Its fresh, tasty and cheap! Try tea leaf salad, tamarin salad, chicken garlic, chicken broccoli with egg, and the pork curry is also good!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: After reading all these stellar reviews, I was sure I would be disappointed. But no, this place is a true Chiang Mai gem. Again, trust what you read - get the tea leaf salad. Personally I also prefer the beef dishes (braised beef especially) to the pork dishes, but that might be more personal preference. We now make excuses to be around Nimmanhaemin just to go here.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I have been eating in this place many times and I cannot get enough. It is always a difficiult choice between the Tea Leaf Salad or Tamarind Salad. But good thing is you do not have to make a decision. You can have both... Or more, as dishes are very reasonably priced, to say the least. And this place is definitely not something that you would expect in the heart of the trendy Nimmanhaemin. Be prepared to wait a bit for your order, but use that time to look what the cook makes. Sometimes you will spot something that is not on the menu.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I've been eating at the Burmese Restaurant at least 2-3x's per week for the last year. And I'm still hooked! Ridiculously cheap ($1USD/dish) and delicious. If you are super hungry, you may have to order a number of dishes though. My favorite 2 Burmese "salads" are the Tea Leaf Salad and the Tamarind Leaf Salad. Hard to describe how the blend of slightly pickled tea leaves matches the tangy crunch of the dried nuts tossed in, but it works. Really amazingly.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Some of the dishes here can be a bit bland but stick to the winners and you can't go wrong. The Burmese salads are all great as is the pork curry. Can't argue with the prices either.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: As many of the other reviewers have said, you must try the salads (tamarind leaf salad and tea leaf salad)!! We tried several of their other dishes such as the green curry (more Thai dishes), but you can get similar kinds of food throughout Thailand. However, the prices are the best! We had 3 dishes, 3 bowls of rice, and water and only payed about 170 baht ($5.30). The staff are friendly (unlike other reviews have said), but this is a street food type place so do not expect 5 star service. This place is not downtown and make sure to read the other reviews for the exact location.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This is one of the best spots on Nimmanhaemin if you love street food. Tea leaf and tamarind salad are really amazing! I eat here regularly and have tried so many things, everything tastes fantastic! I love it!! 30 THB for the salads, crazy to get such amazing food for such a price. Great location to start a Nimmanhaemin exploration!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: OMG I can't praise this place enough. We eat there every day. Sometimes even twice. I tried so many different things and really everything tastes amazing. The tea leaf salad is out of this world, so tasty and crunchy, I just love it. We ordered several times the chicken with cashew nuts and the garlic chicken, both delicious too. Another great salad is the tamarind leaf salad. the green curry is amazing too. So is the Pad Thai! Eat there, you won't get better food for such a low price! There are no prices on the menu, but every dish is between 30 and 35 Baht (US$1). Enjoy!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Had the braised pork and tomato salad and it was some of the best street food I've had! The tomato salad had a peanut dressing that went really well with the meat and rice. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Was looking forward to trying the tea leaf salad. From the start we were made to feel unwelcome. Guy who took our order and guy who served our drinks had serious attitude. Why stay and eat somewhere where they can't even manage basic manners? Paid for drinks and walked.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I really really liked this place for a while (30 baht a meal) but then I noticed I started having an upset stomach after every time I ate there..maybe it has to do with what the food is fried in or something. Otherwise, quite nice.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This place is basically a street vendor. We bypassed it a few times without noticing before coming here based on reviews. We ordered bean soup, tea leaf salad and chicken curry. Everything is truely delicious. The bean soap is out of this world. It takes a while for them to cook the meals, so you may want to order an extra soup just in case.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: An enjoyable and cheap meal on the roadside. The tea leaf salad was very tasty, the pork fried rice okay but the chicken curry disappointingly bland. No frills dining and at only 100 baht for three dishes, who can complain?

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: The Mango Salad here is fantastic. Its based off of green mango and in some kind of sweet, sour, salty tomato based sauce which perfectly compliments the tangy flavor of green mango. The Tea Leaf Salad was made from a mix.. I watched them as they made it.. kind of disappointing considering some of the other Burmese places in town who make it freshly to order. I could taste the difference too.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We went to Burmese Restaurant and Library based on another review. Delicious! We had tried dishes we had never eaten before, including a tea leaf salad (crunchy and tangy), green mango salad (with peanuts!) and a curry chicken and potatoes (slow cooked tender, no coconut milk). We like it so much we ended up ordering more items to-go for dinner later that night. The menu is super cheap - seven (small) dishes later with three servings of rice came out to less than 200 baht ($7 USD.) We will be coming back! This restaurant only has a few tables (outdoor seating), small plastic chairs, spice tray on the tables and self serve water. There is plenty of parking along the soi it is located on (directly across from Salad Concept) off of Nimmanhaemin Rd. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: drove by this place one night and realized how I was craving some Burmese food. The family who runs it are Shan Burmese people and the food is authentic. It's also amazingly cheap and incredibly tasty. Make sure to get their burmese tea, man how I miss that stuff.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Love the salads here, especially tamarind leaf and tea leaf. The curries are probably less to western tastes as they're oily but very good and portions aren't that big anyway. Customers are a mix of foreigners (mostly American I think) and locals. Atmosphere is basic but seriously you do not need to worry about hygiene at places like this - 100% fine! including the water they provide you.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I discovered tea leaf salad here and its now pretty much one of my favorite things, ever, ever, ever. If you've never had it before, it's not like a leafy green salad, but rather a crunchy, garlicky, amazing mixture. If you are squeamish about cleanliness, don't go here. We went with a friend and she almost wanted to leave, whereas we were totally fine... so it depends on your comfort level. It's full of farang, and we've never gotten sick, so I think you're fine. It didn't bother us. The people who work there speak very little English and the menu is a little tricky to understand. We almost always get the tea leaf salad, bean soup, and one other item. They are often out of many things on the menu. The food is so cheap that it's always around 100 baht for 2 of us to have 3 dishes plus 2 orders of rice. Definitely a favorite in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Words cannot express how much I love this restaurant! The appearance isn't much to take in (it looks like a food stall with a tables behind it), but the menu is extensive and written in Thai and English. The green curry fried rice and tamarind salad is a must! Also, the prices range from 30-35 baht so it's easy to try new things without breaking the bank. A must go if you're in Chiang Mai!
Date_Written: in Thai and English. The green curry fried rice and tamarind salad is a must! Also, the prices range from 30-35 baht so it's easy to try new things without breaking the bank. A must go if you're in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This is for those of you travellers who are more interested in taste than ambiance. It's really just a sort of glorified sidewalk food stall, but it is home to truly great Burmese food at very low prices -- and perfect for "take away" if you are staying in a hotel or apartment in the Nimman area. Their tea leaf salad is as good as Free Bird's and my favorite new thing is tamarind leaf salad. The menu is in Burmese, Thai and English, so no worries. The Burmese Library, by the way, is at the other end of the opposite soi, Nimmanhaeminda Soi 13. NOTE THAT THIS RESTAURANT IS ON NIMMANHAEMINDA SOI 8 (even numbered side of the street -- not soi 13), across the street from the 7-11 with the parking lot. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Myself, my wife and two sons went at lunchtime and it was quite easy to find with a large sign hanging over the entrance and being opposite Soi 13, Nimanheimin Street and close to the 711 on the same side of the street. We were staying nearby which made it easy to find the place. There are a couple of basic tables and chairs outside and several tables inside. This is street food but as nobody suffered any nasty after-effects, it is safe, definitely recommended and we will be back there next time we are in Chiang Mai. We ordered six dishes, two versions of a pad thai, two versions of a ginger dish, the tealeaf salad and a red-hot salad with what we thought were sliced runner beans, only to realise they were sliced green chilis.... plus rice and four soft drinks. We enjoyed all the dishes and returned another day. Little English is spoken here but this was not a problem at all as the staff were pleasant and willing to help. Service was basic but friendly and you can relax here. We had a chat with a Thai taxi driver of Burmese ethnic origins. The prices were crossed out on the menu and I feared the worst after the publicity they have received on Trip Advisor. However, the bill on the first visit came to a total of US$9 and the value for money is outstanding. We learnt that Thai (and Burmese dishes seem to be no different in this stall- cum- restaurant) often use more fruit and vegetables with a corresponding reduction in the amount of meat and this helps keep the price down and also makes for a healthy meal. Go ahead and enjoy yourselves. My rating is based on a basic street stall and little atmosphere, just very tasty, clean, safe, food and excellent value for money. I live in the Philippines at the moment with little to recommend it and food and prices like this are a a timely reminder of what can be achieved. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: If you're after tasty, authentic and cheap Burmese eats, this is the place to find :) To get there it's about a 10 minute SongTao ride out of the centre of Chiang Mai, but it's worth the visit. Ask to go to Nimmanhaemin. I think the cost of getting there can be around 20-40 baht each depending on how many people are heading out that way. To paint you a picture: Once you find the place, you'll find that you've stumbled into an unassuming, slightly 'dingy' open-air venue with the typical cheap plastic tables and chairs and frilly plastic tablecloths. Up on the wall are posters of Burmese ex political prisoner Aung San Suu Kyi, with quotes and the like.. The staff are very friendly and funny to chat with, although they cannot speak very much english so it's usual to resort to pointing, nodding, repeating and smiling when ordering from the menu. We would go there as a group of two or three and get a mix of things.. usually two plates each and share. As we were staying at a friends house near Nimmanhaemin, we ended up eating here on numerous occassions, even though that area of Chiang Mai actually has a lot of culinary delights to be found. It's worth exploring :) The Tea Leaf Salad (made from fermented tea leaves and crispy fried broad-beans/other legumes is the star.. it's definitely a good plan to order more than one plate. The Tamarind leaf salad is another favourite - for those who don't know, young, tender tamarind leaves are nice and tangy :) The Pennywort salad is my favourite.. if you google Pennywort leaf benefits you'll find a bucket load of info on the health benefits of these leaves... Totally delish and refreshing :D There is also a huge menu of curries and other meat dishes but most of the time they don't have everything on the menu available, so don't be dissapointed if they don't have your first choice... From my experience I always loved the dish I ended up with. Hope this helps :) Oh and PS: I don't think there's actually a 'library' Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: This little restaurant is tucked away in one of Nimman's sois, and if you don't know about it, you're unlikely to stumble across it... and even if you do, you might not take any notice of it. It doesn't exactly advertise its presence! This understated and unassuming eatery serves some of the best food I've had in Chiang Mai, at incredibly reasonable prices; the tea leaf salad just has to be experienced, as do the prawns. In fact, just go in, order half a dozen dishes, and revel in the glorious gastronomic experience of Shan cuisine! Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: A place we would always go back. Hearty Burmese dishes for an average of 30 Bahts; excellent value and great taste. If you don't mind the plastic chairs and tables, of course.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I love their Tea Leaf Salad. I go for their Tea Leaf Salad & I have other good dishes but the Tea Leaf Salad is what sticks in my memory.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: I wasn't very hungry by the time we made it here so all I had was the tomato salad. It tasted fresh & authentic and was pretty cheap. My fiance had some sort of chicken curry....a rather small portion, but I tasted it and it tasted good too. The restaurant feels more like a sidewalk stand with tables & chairs. Nothing elegant that's for sure and you'll def. notice that there are a lot of flies and other insects buzzing around. I'm also suspicious of the preparation/cleanliness of this place since I did have some bowel trouble later in the day/night after eating here....but since there were other consumables throughout the day I can't officially lay blame here. Overall, it was pretty good tasting food and it was cheap. Read less

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We love coming here as the food is utterly delicious and affordable. We had the pork and chicken curries with both the tamarind and tea leaf salads. It's worth just ordering a number of items from the menu and then sharing them, not being afraid to try something new.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: We ate here on a busy afternoon with just enough shade under the awning to sit out on the street. The chicken curry was delicious, although if you like a lot of meat you might want one each. And we loved the tamarind leaf salad, and the tea leaf and tomato salad - real depth of flavour but also light and tangy. This place is 20 mins by tuktuk from where we are staying, and we are already thinking about a return visit.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Very light and delicious salads. Curries are good too. I like the tamarind salad best but the tea leaf and tomato salads are good too. Sometimes they don't have things on the menu like milk tea, Shan fried rice, etc........a nice place for a quick cheap bite to eat and quite healthy if you like salad with a little bit of rice.

Restaurant_Name: น้องบีอาหารเมียนมา
Detail: Nong Bee's restaurant is very simple and serves traditional Burmese and Shan dishes and also and extensive Thai menu, but it is the Shan Burmese dishes I love. I have been eating here at least once a week since May 2011 and sometimes daily. I'm not vegetarian but for the many vegetarians living and visiting Chiang Mai, this is a great change of flavors. My favorites: Pennywort Salad (finely minced herbal green with thin sliced tomato, shallot and lightly dressed. The Tomato Salad, the Tamarind Salad, you must have the Shan Rice --- steamed rice that is mixed with a fresh stewed tomato with finely minced fried shallot. The Shan chili --- a dry mixture served with leaf lettuce, cucumber slices --- great with all of the above. There are a few tables outside and others inside. It is SIMPLE and friendly and extremely affordable! Opposite Nimmanhaemin Soi 13 -- can't miss it by the sign: Burmese Restaurant & Library Read less

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Plastic bottles. Not able to give water not from the overpriced single use plastic. And yes not recyclable.nthebthainteebheebheenallnover and food...better to order cheaper elsewhere.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: I've been living in Chiang Mai for almost a year but I recently discovered this little gem of a restaurant. At night you can easily miss the restaurant when walking by. The ambiance is cozy. There's a 2nd floor but its a bit cold up there so I usually stay downstairs. The menu is simple I go there usually for the ramen soup but the dumplings are also very good. They have a nice selection of local beers and saki. My wife and I usually spend around $8-15USD depending on what you eat. The waitresses are very nice and attentive. On the way out everyone will say thank you in Japanese.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: I have had my share of ramen and this was the best one I have ever had! I got the salt based butter broth and my boyfriend got the Tonkatsu broth. Portions were huge and all toppings were perfectly cooked. Can't wait to go again!

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Pork bone broth ramen was very tasty; the small size was still large enough that I took leftovers home (109 THB). The spicy oil condiment is extremely hot. Open for lunch and dinner, but closed for a few hours in between and closed after lunch on Thursday. Check the times before going. Not a huge space but the meal comes quickly and there’s a pretty rapid turnover. You won’t have to wait long.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: The first time I came here I had the Tonkotsu Ramen and was very impressed with the flavour's and seasoning. I have now been there 6 times and it is the only thing I have tried so far - I still can't move on. If you want to sit downstairs you need to get there early. Fills up very quickly.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: This is one of my “comfort food” places. I have come back for the pork bone soup based noodles multiple times. You can ask to change the default ramen (thin and soft) to udon (thick and chewy) or soba (thin and strong). The portion is very generous, by sizing up you will also get more meat not just noodle. Noodle starts at 89 baht for small size, depending on the meat/toppings, then 20 baht to size up. I usually go with the most basic (first on the list of the pork bone soup), to me simple is the best. The soup is rich and flavourful, comes in many choices (pork bone, miso, soy paste, spicy soy paste, butter). The butter soup base is slightly heavy for my likings though, but is an interesting take. There are also sashimi/sushi/tempura options. I only tried the fatty salmon sashimi and it was very fresh with thick fat cuts. Overall, very good prices for Japanese food. The restaurant also offers delivery but funny they don’t have a menu to take away! Read less

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Food is good and price is fine. It was our first time trying ramen food so it’s not an expert comment but we were very happy with this experience. Staff was friendly and helpful to help us choose. Many many choices on the menu. Recommended

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: The ramen is some of the best I’ve ever tasted! Highly recommend. I had the tonkotsu (pork/bone broth).

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: My friend recommends me this restaurant. I went there with her. It was a small restaurant with warm feeling, welcoming and good service. The waitresses are so friendly. Food is worth the price and delicious. P.S. there is no carpark.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: We were royal customer of this restaurant. This time we found bugs in our food which we pointed out to waitress. They didn’t apologize to us, no sorry, no anything happened. Everything was like nothing happened! This is not how you treat your customers! 我们经常来这里吃饭，这次饭里有虫子，到我们指出来到结账。没有任何抱歉和对不起等最基本的礼貌！我们微笑指出，你没有最最基本的服务态度。那我就只能先礼后兵了。负分差评！

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: I go here about once a week to get my ramen fix. The tonkatsu ramen is amazing, the broth is perfectly balanced in flavor. I also highly recommend the gyoza and takoyaki.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: First off, amazing ramen. My boyfriend ordered the Bone broth and I got the spicy miso. We tried to go on a Wednesday night for dinner but just 30 minute after opening they were already filled up. However, Friday during the lunch rush we got a table no problem. easily some of the best ramen I've had.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: This is my go-to ramen place whenever I'm back in Chiang Mai. The tonkotsu ramen is delicious and the broth is full of flavor and very rich. The pork is not too fatty and you can add several condiments as well. The restaurant gets pretty busy, so the food can sometimes take a while but I think it is well worth the wait.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: My boyfriend and I came here because he really wanted to try ramen. It was SO GOOD. The sushi was so fresh and the ramen was so tasty. And the coconut frappe was bomb. Great value for money. Would definitely come back.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Value for money. Good service, lots of choice, very tasty. Upstairs and a downstairs and a takeaway service, frequented by locals mainly. Closed Thursday. Best in chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: wonderful soup you ever can get We got VIP-table as my friend is a regular cstomer the building was at the moment when I visited on reconstruction so half of the resturang wqs opened, seems to be a very popular resturang

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Really good and authentic Japanese restaurant. We were the only tourist, everybody else seemed to be Japanese incl. the waitress. Staff spoke only a little English, but the food was good, the price ok, allthough more expensive then it would have been at a thai restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: We've been around Thailand for weeks, trying all sorts of food, but this one beats it all. Ramen is wonderful, sushi is very good, sashimi excellent. Definitely give it a chance. The only unlokable thing is that to access their wifi you need to give them a like in FB, which frankly they wouldn't need at all. Be careful with address, it can be hard to find. Better consult their fcb page.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: This place has good food for an affordable price. Its almost competing with the very expnsive Tengoku where you pay 800 baht for the buffet. Its being visited by many Japanese people, so I think they agree that its good food :-) I can recomend the salmon sashimi and the yakisoba udon beef.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Ramen is okay but probably the best you can fond in chiangmai, price is very reasonable, restaurant is small but clean and waiters are attentive. Standard sushi nothing special

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Excellent food, at an affordable price. The place, may be hard to find, is an extremely good surprise. We've been there twice in two days.

Restaurant_Name: นินจา ราเมง
Detail: Ninja Ramen is the best ramen joint I ever found in Chiang Mai and I lived there for a year. It's small, quaint, delicious and affordable. It's packed at lunch so get there early. Just writing this review makes me salivate. Well worth repeat visits! They used to have lunch specials which bought you a bowl of ramen, some gyoza and tea. Not sure if that's still in effect.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Good for a break from Thai flavours if seeking something a bit more European. Warm and welcoming service. We fancied a quick sandwich and this place hit the spot. Croque Monsieur and cheese and tomato panini were tasty using ingredients grown themselves or locally sourced. The fresh juices are also recommended as are the cookies.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We enjoyed three breakfasts at Manifreshto during our stay in Chiang Mai. The restaurant is so cute with red checked tablecloths and lovely outdoor spaces to enjoy your meal. Everything we ordered was fabulous: Croque Madame, croissant with mushrooms and melted mozzarella + poached eggs and croissant with scrambled eggs and ham. Washed down with a double shot Thai iced coffee we were fully fuelled for our days in Chiang Mai. Staff are friendly and efficient. We never had to wait to long for our food. I'd highly recommend Manifreshto. Read less

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The place is great......it is a small place(in videos i saw look like bigger)...Anyway...this is no big thing for me.....has outdoor and indoor area....but the Main thing is the food!!! Great food!!!and big plates with big portions!!!the staff fantastic and they bring us the food fast(eventhough they were almost full)....i take ham and cheese sandwich and omellete with tomato in the pan..............all great!!!!the price was good i surely will go again and i recommend it for breakfast and brunch!!!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: A lovely place for breakfast, the staff are all very nice and speak English. We had a smoothie bowl and a full English. Quick service and prices that we could afford!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Kept coming back during our stay in Chiang Mai. Really fresh food and one of the few places in thailand that does good bacon! Great range of juices also got tea with fresh milk! The staff are very friendly and attentive and service was always fast even when they were busy. Fantastic!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Good food. Bread is not sweetened. Very decent tosties and panini’s. Definitely will come again. It’s a good place for lunch.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Skip the Eggs Benedict or other breakfasts with ham. The meat is a think slice that appears to come from a luncheon pack. Everything else was excellent though. The smoothies were extremely thick and delicious, and (other than the ham) the other meals we tried were also quite good.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We came here for brunch and the menu offered many healthy yet delicious food. They used organic and local ingredients. Many of the ingredients are home made too. The portion of the food was just right and the price was reasonable too. The juices and shakes were awesome too. The place was casual and relaxing. Read less

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: This is my second time come to the delicious restaurant. The food is greatt! And I recommend you guys to come and try some!!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Soft and gooey Peanut Butter and good varieties of breakfast. The desert selections are the best. Defo try the Peanut Butter Brownie and Cookies

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Came for Breakfast/Brunch and went for the build your own breakfast option. The food was great and the staff very friendly. I also got a Peanut Butter Brownie to go, that was also delicious.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Loved this breakfast place, you can make your own plates for a good price! The F smoothie is delicious. We went many times during our 2 weeks stay in Chiang Mai. Also have great sandwiches and is vegetarian friendly

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The smoothies are absolutely incredible! There's a very wide choice of flavour combinations and I appreciated that they indicate the sweetness level for each and don't add sugar nor ice (they use frozen fruit). We tried the brisket BBQ sauce burger which was a revelation! Incredibly delicious and the BBQ sauce is one pf the best I have ever had. We went back the next day for lunch again because we loved it so much. We tried the pulled pork sandwich and the pulled pork with mozzarella and caramelized onions on toast. Both very delicious, too! There's good vegetarian options, too. Read less

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: This place has REAL smoothies: all fruit. The brisket is so good and the pulled pork sandwiches are also brilliant. We’ve eaten here two days in a row and will probably keep it up!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Had smoothies and cookies there! amazing! smoothies are the best in Chiang Mai (no ice, no sugar syrup, just real things) also they serve breakfast, lunch and dinner

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Visited several times during my stay!Open from 7:30 this restaurant is perfect for brunch,lunch,dinner.Tasty omlettes,fresh crispy salads and perfect pulled porc and beef burgers for reasonable price!!!(dishes are served with fruits & salads+homemade ketchup) For breakfast I recommend the peanut butter brownies:) They serve no coffee but you can bring your OWN.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Absolutely lovely food and a calm, relaxed atmosphere. The service was great. The restaurant is sustainable and served drinking water to avoid plastic waste, used glass straws and ethically sourced produce.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We came here for lunch and ordered from the breakfast menu. I was able to customise my own order which was brilliant because I am vegtarian so could order exactly what I wanted. We also got a smoothie that was delicious. I would definitley recommend to visit while in Chiang Mai. They also serve dinner which has a wide range of food. My sister and I loved it here.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: This is a lovely cafe with delicious juices and smoothies. They also offer a “DIY” breakfast option where you tick the box and make up your own. Very reasonable prices and the staff are nice and friendly. Delicious pastries too, will definitely come back again. 😊

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Our favourite smoothies that we had while in Nimman. And the beef brisket was great as well. Cute little spot - the pictures don’t do it justice. Grab a cookie or snack for the road too!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Delicious and tender brisket! The brisket Benedict was truly one of the best breakfast dishes I've ever had, tho not a traditional breakfast. Tasty hollandaise that's not too overpowering, yummy balsamic reduction spread on the bread (or carmaslised onions, I didn't take the time to disect it), perfectly cooked asparagus and poached eggs. 10/10 My wife also had the build your own breakfast, and everything there was tasty and cooked well. Love this place :))

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Came here one day for breakfast after reading a review and was not disappointed! The fruit smoothies are the best you will have in Thailand. Had to come back again to get another one on my last day!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: This humble little place in a tree-lined corner, not far from the One Nimman family shopping/dining/entertainment building, is a true delight. We went there for breakfast and were delighted to find that they served home-sourced, fresh eggs. Their menu includes most of the basics of a good, healthy breakfast. It would be great if they also serve lattes, cappuccinos, etc. as black coffee isn’t everyone’s cup of tea. All in all, a very pleasant experience.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Great place to eat. Charming off the beating path on small back roads from the mall. Everything was great, all fresh. Definitely we will be back.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: A varied menu in a nice quiet back street in Chiang Mai. Only breakfast spot open early with delicious smoothies and hearty breakfasts. Reasonably priced. Well worth a trip.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: While out looking for a healthy breakfast, we stumbled upon an unpretentious little cafe in a tree-lined part of Soi 5 in Chiang Mai. Quite a discovery! Their menu was limited, but it contained the fresh food we were looking for - organic eggs, home-grown greens, fresh butter instead of oil, personalised meals, black coffee - I could go on. And great service! All at very manageable prices. I don’t think Chiang Mai has any other matching food-outlet.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Cozy cafe with a variety of breakfast options. Focused on egg-based dishes in all forms and sizes. Vegan and vegetarian options available. Vavegetarianvevegetariaoopt

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Great to have fresh juice for detox in the hot weather ~! Diet juice comes with celery ,beetroot ,carrot and apple ! Detox comes with carrot and apple ~!They give you free cookies too !

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We were searching a place to have breakfast and we stumble upon Manifreshto. Love the vibes from the place, they have great service and the food is fresh with local ingredients. Love that they care for the planet and have glass straws for your smoothies 🙌🏻.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Very good breakfast very good juice! You can customise you own eggplate Bad thing they dont accept credit card. But its still a nice place.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Quick service, excellent smoothies, relaxed vibe. Many of the smoothies are vegan friendly and their menu has a complete breakdown of what's in the drinks do it's easy to tell. They use glass reusable straws for their drinks. Cute shop, definitely recommend.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Being able to customise your own meal is such a wonderful idea!! The food was delicious and the smoothie the best I have had. This is the first place I have ever been to that knows butter is better!! The location under the trees was cool and appreciated. Staff were nice & friendly.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Lovely breakfast and good selection. You can do your own breakfast also. Nice staff and good service.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I love monster cookie and brownie. They are super tasty. Also the English breakfast is great too. The food look so healthy and smoothies is great!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Finally! I had my crispy bacon here! On top of customized breakfast, I tried their smoothie bowl as well. It was an overall pleasant experience with good food and excellent service!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Stopped here for lunch on the way back from the mountains and was very pleasantly surprised by the selection of food which were exactly what we were craving! My boyfriend had the full English which was great and I had a very fresh croissant, we will definitely be back for breakfast!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I am a foodie and I highly recommend this place. Works perfect especially if you go out with other people as you can customize your breakfast and the food is delicious. Customization of breakfast is such a great idea, it makes it a perfect place for both hungry people and for those who don’t eat too much.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I like the option to make your own breakfast. Very convenient and let's me adapt to my diet and mood. The smoothies are well priced and delicious also

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Manifreshto serves delicious fresh and organic meals. I highly recommend Manifreshto the staff is friendly, restaurant is small and very clean. Very good quality food as they use home grown hormone egg free, real butter and no perservatives. We ordered: Vegan super smoothie bowl tropical aromatic (fresh mint, passion fruit, pineapple; Eggs Benedict with Salmon; Croque Madame; English Breakfast and Mango smoothie.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I just left this restaurant hoping to enjoy a hearty breakfast.I arrived early with only a few others seated and placed my order.I waited patiently and when those that arrived after me received their food I inquired about mine.I received blank looks when they confessed to having overlooked my order even though I was sitting inside in plain view.I paid for my smoothie and walked out deciding to eat elsewhere.It seems others enjoyed their food but I can’t speak of my own experience.I guess living in Thailand more than 20 years and speaking Thai does not guarantee good service

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Great spot for a very fresh and organic breakfast. Have been twice and the quality of food is excellent. Fruit smoothies and amazing with no additives. Both baked egg dishes I had were very yummy and super fresh. It has been busy both times I was here so service was slow but we did not mind to sit and chat while enjoying fresh smoothie or coffee. Will return for sure.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We love Manifreshto for breakfast/ brunch. All food sourced from the owners families farm- so fresh and tasty. Juices also wonderful- The Baked Eggs- number 5 on menu are brilliant.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The staffs are very polite and friendly. Good quality of food as they use home grown hormone free eggs, real butter (not margarine) , rice bran oil for cooking.. plus they as environmentally friendly !!!! They serve food in biodegradable cups ! Many options on menu. portion is small which is perfect for me but it probabaly be not enough for some. I would recommend it for sure !

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Love their eggs. Fresh & tasty. We also ordered local beef curry panini. Very tender. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Decent breakfast, and the mango smoothie was exceptional. A recommended visit if you are in the area

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: My hubby and I had dinner at this very tiny but cute place in Chiang Mai. Burgers were better then the more expensive ones down the road, peanut butter brownies are delicious and price was reasonable for non Thai traditional food! Only down side, we didn’t eat here until are last night in CM. This place needs help in advertising there great food, maybe photos with price displayed clearly. And the vegetable juices are not well advertised until you walk in.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The staff is welcoming and friendly. Unlike the other hip, western inspired places nearby they laugh and smile. I hope it stays like this. Their smoothies are more pricey than from the street vendors. But its consistency and flavours are making up for it. The breakfast options are lovely and the staff can adjust to your wishes. I have been here many times. On my last day I have been welcomed with "sawasdee ka, beef massaman?". Say no more

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: 5 Stars for breakfast and cookies! who come to Nimman must try *Open 7.30 AM - 2.00 PM Nice service and friendly stuff

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We had an amazing breakfast: croissant with eggs and ham, croque monsieur and a carrot/apple/mango smoothie. Everything was really tasty! I’ll definetaly come back.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Cosy house breakfast in town, lovely breakfast and good services, highly recommend.if you looking for breakfast in nimman here is the best

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The best vegan breakfast I had in Chiangmai! Highly recommended for vegans and in general 😊😊🍪🍩🍎

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The food came fast. It was delicious and the flavor was perfect and super tasty. Located in a very nice area, the place looks nice and cosy

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The breakfast tastes good, staffs here are so kind. I like here. And i will be here next time. Thank you

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We were looking for place we can eat a breakfast and the Manifreshto was just perfect. Food was great, staff nice and they could speak English what made it easier for us.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Great place, you can compose your own breakfast or choose something from the menu. Two very important things: English speaking staff and very fast WiFi.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Very cosy little place, the breakfast options and smoothies are insanely yummy!!! Salad dressing was kind of strange though. Very affordable and huge value for this quality

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We came with my friends and we loved it. Cozy atmosphere, super friendly staff,amazing smoothies and food! Would definitely come back when l visit Thailand again! Recommend it 100%!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Mingling all the selection of fruits your heart desired with an authentic freshness. Recommended for anyone who thirst for great beverage :)

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Delicious food and smoothies- very fresh and healthy in a cute little cafe. Loved the vibes and the relaxed breakfast atmosphere

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Friendly staff even we arrived in the early morning. Lots of variety for food combination. Smoothie is refreshing and made with real fruit. Smoothie lover should come!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Yummy smoothies. Made with all fruit and veggies. No water, ice or sugar. Delicious combinations. Great homemade cookies too.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: We have visited Manifesto couple of times for breakfast and we were never disappointed!!! All their food is so delicious, created with lots of care and attention to detail. The food presentation is absolutely fabulous. And the owner really puts her heart into the business. Even in the busiest time she would take the time to chat to the customers and is geniuanly interest to improve the quality of her service and food. We have also visited almost every day for their absolutely delicious smoothies - no sugar, no ice, no syrop added. Also, the eggs they use in their breakfast are homegrown and antibiotic free. Would recommend or to anyone visiting Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: With their superb hollandaise sauce, my egg benedict dish was so perfect. Also the non syrup smoothie and nice muffins boosted up my day freshly. I would love to gave this restaurant the best in town.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Just been here and we really enjoyed the smoothies. I had the tropical and my wife had sour n sweet. They were really delicious and so fresh in taste! We also had one of there peanut butter brownies to share and was soooo good! Get yourself down here seriously, it’s a really nice change from just going to a coffee shop and having something refreshing. The staff are lovely too, keep up the good work and I’m sure queues will be forming before you know it!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: The staff was very nice. Recommended Smoothies it was so good you can feel the taste of real fruits and of course no syrup in there! Banana muffin was awesome and fresh made straight from the oven.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Seriously delicious, thick, scrumptious smoothies! No ice nor syrup added. Yumm. We did not try the cakes nor the biscuits but they surely looked delicious.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I've enjoyed many a smoothie here and I love it every time. Real fruit, really quick, really nice staff.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Delicious, fresh, real fruit, good price, good customer service, etc! We loved it!!!! Muffins are just perfect ... Banana and chocolate is our suggestion to start!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: It's located in the hip, comfortable Nimman Str district, next to a cool art gallery and underneath the Japanese restaurant Nori. The best Smoothies by far and worth every penny. No sugar or ice added. There's a concept! Have been looking for a place like this the whole time in Chiang Mai. The secret to the quenching tasty beverage is, she freezes the fruit the night before, so no watering down with ice and need to add sugar. And the juice that is added is organic and no added artificial sugar. Highly recommend the mango carrot apple. Might just see you there!

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: Manifreshto's smoothies are made with 100% natural ingredients. No syrups or sugars added. Fruit and yogurt only! And they are very cheap; only 55 baht! AND SO DELICIOUS.

Restaurant_Name: มานิเฟรชโต้
Detail: I was wandering around and exploring to the northwest of the old city of Chiang Mai and happened upon this little gem of hole-in-the-wall. This young woman has just opened her shop within the past year and has created a menu of all-fruit smoothies that is fantastic. No ice, no syrup, just fresh fruit she has frozen herself and then blended together for a delicious refreshment. While you are there, sample her baked goods as well!

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: A HK chef operated restaurant specialize in Cantonese cuisine. Simple in decor, it has 2 section of dining halls. It is very clean and service is top notch.--5* Dim Sum lives up to Michelin billing,high and fine quality. Ingredients are fresh and the prawns are sweet and crunchy. The Pau are delicious.--5* BBQ- The roast pork has crisp and crunchy skin, but pretty meaty. An layer of fats in between will make it more desirable. The roast goose is well seasoned, crisp skin but meat can be more tender.--4* We enjoyed our dim sum here, 4.5* for me. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: We came here to eat dim sum. There is a double-sided menu just for the dumplings. There are three dim sum per dish, but be careful, they are huge! Three are just as filling as five elsewhere. On the evening we were there, almost none of the staff spoke English. We still managed to order, albeit with gestures. The mixed vegetables that we ordered with the dumplings had a lot of garlic and a lot of fried iceberg lettuce. I have eaten a lot of dim sum in my life and, to my regret, did not find this one outstanding. Not bad, but not as tasty as in Taiwan. The atmosphere in the restaurant is cool, as is the air conditioning. The fish waiting to be eaten in the much too small aquarium arouse pity and a certain inhibition to eat. There were a few Chinese people in the restaurant. It probably suits Chinese tastes. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: The restaurant is pretty from the outside, with its New England cottage look, but we were confined to a rather inhospitable closed veranda, the room being probably reserved for Chinese tour operators, an incessant ballet of small gray buses that punctuated our entire meal. The tables were also unremarkable and the menus faded. Service was chaotic with a matronly head waitress watching us out of the corner of her eye for literally the entire meal, pretending to write down orders. Pretty unpleasant feeling. Has she received instructions to prevent customers from leaving without paying? The service is long and the dishes are not served at the same time. The dishes seemed rather ordinary to us, without flavor, although certainly homemade. Not sure that tour operator customers got the same treatment. The note is salty for such a bland cuisine but it is recommended by Michelin, which may explain that. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Really good, authentic, Chinese food. We found this little gem as it is listed in the Michelin Guide. Some really interesting dishes on the menu. The half aromatic duck was delicious as was the stewed courgettes on glass noodles with dried shrimp - real depth of flavour. But for me the standout dish was the BBQ ribs: spicy, aromatic, sticky and utterly delicious. The staff speak hardly any English, but English is included on the menu. The staff were also a bit "cold". However, we'd really recommend this place; we left very happy.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Good Chinese food and very reasonable price. There are both Dim Sum or A La Carte for lunch. Restaurant located between Nimman Soi 5 and Soi 7. Most of the dish are deliciuos. We love dim sum, fried vegetable and fried noodle. Must try first and repeat your favorite again.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Last month, I came to this restaurant and was impressed by their cuisine. However, I did not have the opportunity to take photos and wrote a review. My appetizers, the Jellyfish and Drunken chicken were well prepared. Particularly, I have never saw this size Jellyfish in any restaurants in Chiang Mai. Their Peking duck at THB 998.00 is a good deal and you can pay THB 100.00 to have the chef cook 2 dishes from their recommended dishes using the duck meat. This restaurant has became my top choice for Chinese cuisine.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Their dim sum is very nice. Especially egg custard bun. Garlic Chives and prawn wrap is also very good.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Ate here with a large group for business. Round tables in a private function room. Service was exceptional. Food amazing.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: I have lived and worked in Malaysia with a Malaysian Chinese company and also travelled to China on many occasions but I can't recall ever having such poor Dim Sum. The service was rather slow and the staff somewhat po-faced; the main entrance door (wood and glass) constantly banged as staff (and customers) just let it go (why can't they install a door closer so it closes softly and quietly?), but the main disappointment was the food - just not impressed at all and even my wife (who was looking to take her 80 year-old Dad to this resturant as a treat) said "we won't be going there again"!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Went for lunch and we ordered off the dim sum menu. Good choice!! Finally had as authentic Chinese dim sum since San Francisco, haven't been to China yet. The dumplings were the stars. The shanghai dumplings are filled with soup and are amazing and interesting, just be careful as the soup inside is hot! The Szechuan dumplings were deliciously spicy and I'm wanting them again already. The savory dumplings were more sweet then savory for me, but delicious non the less. Special dumplings were delicate scallops topped with a truffle "caviar". Again, incredible. Had to try the ubiquitous spring roll, crispy and light with no greasiness. We were the only non-Asians in the restaurant which we took as a good sign. Can't wait to go next weekend!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: We went there for lunch but we were 5 mins late.They already closed.But the owner is very kind, she served for us and it was so good we had to go back again the next day for dinner. The dinner was awesome, the best chinese food ever.Normally I can feel Msg or chicken booster in this kind of food. But it is qaulity and no msg at all! Highly recommended if you are in Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: We visited this This place two times in two days the food is fantastic, the service is attentive. The owner of the resturant came out to see us after our meal witch was a nice touch. I will defiantly come back again.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: They openly say they don,t like foreigner customers because foreigners complain, in fact when they make mistake they don,t want to admit and they always bring starter to the last or in the middle of the dinner, when you tell them they don,t like it, it happens all the time. They always bring the food first and the rice a lot later , you will have to eat the rice without food. The tea is very price and you are not allow to share, the price is per cup and per a pot of tea, If you ask for one more cup, they charge one tea price more. Horrible service manner. The food was very good but they need to satisfy local customers, they cook more Thai taste and style now. I don,t go back anymore, used to go almost every month or more. Once my friend went and at 9:30 they started to pack and clean their restaurant, my friend ask the owner why they do that while the customers still eating and the restaurant should be closed at 10, the owner told my friend to leave if he did not like it. That was really bad attitude. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: At this restaurant you can order normal Cantonese dishes or dim sum dishes or a combination. This was our second visit and we ordered dim sum dishes. All dishes were excellent, easily the best Chinese food we've come across in Chiang Mai in 4 years. The service was good but a smile would have helped sell himself and his service just a little better. It is expensive for what you receive but you need to weigh up price against quality and in the quality stakes it came up trumps.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: The restaurant name 扬子江 (simplified Chinese) in common English is Yangtze River that flows through Shanghai in northern China. Odd that that name was chosen for a restaurant serving southern Chinese Cantonese cuisine. I sampled the Shanghai dumplings (xiao long bao), har gow, and shrimp and chive dumplings (my favorite). There was three each in a bamboo steamer, and various sauces with red peppers (this is Thailand after all) all. All were well prepared, the shrimps were large and crunchy. It was necessary to ask for a bowl to use for the Shanghai dumplings that are filed with a liquid filling. Excellent.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: A friend recommended this restaurant to us and said if we might need decent quick dim sum, noodles and congee in Chiangmai, this will be the one. After a easy stroll in Nimman area, we paid a visit at almost 2p.m. The restaurant was quite quiet at the time. Only one table at the main dining room. We simply just ordered some Har Gao, Sumai, barbecued pork bun and puff, fried turnip cake, drunken chicken. A bowl of wonton noodle and a bowl of congee. The best way to judge good cantonese food is just to order wonton and the congee, simple food that will give you a glimpses of their skill. We found that all the dishes were satisfying. They came piping hot though not so quick as they did not prepare in advance for the steamed dishes. The service was quite good and the price tag was quite cheap compared to those in Bangkok at the same quality. Highly recommended this restaurant when you crept for Chinese food here in Chiangmai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: I was told by my wife that we had to eat at this restaurant as it was promoted on some Thai TV channel. Wow, what a disappointment. When we first arrived there, even my wife wasn’t sure anymore if we should really eat there or just find something else. Anyway, sometimes the interior of a restaurant doesn’t have to reflect the food and therefore we gave it a try. Unfortunately in this case the cheap interior does reflect exactly the food and service of the restaurant. We were looking forward to order a Beijing duck, but as we were only 2 as our son was not in any eating mood, we were talked in by the service staff to better order the “Aromatic crispy duck”. At that point I expected some tasty sliced duck breast with crispy skin. As I always like to sample a few dishes, I ordered as well the chicken in orange sauce, beef Honk Kong style and braised eggplant in soy sauce. I don’t want to claim myself as expert in Chinese food, but I have worked as an Executive Chef in Hong Kong, Tianjin and Sanya and had as well in other locations Chinese restaurants under my responsibility. Apart from Chinese restaurants in the 5 star hotels, I went as well many times to local restaurants in China (if basic hygiene standards applied). Anyway, what was served to us at the Yangzi Jiang restaurant was not good. The aromatic crispy duck was nothing else than deep-fried shredded duck meat, which looked for me like the left-over meat from some once Beijing duck. At least it was presented like that. I took a few pancakes with the duck, but was also not impressed by its taste. The chicken in orange sauce was ok, but a bit overpowered with sugar. The beef Honk Kong style was so much tenderized with baking powder, that the meat would have most properly dissolved itself within the next 2 days. The braised eggplant in soy sauce was topped with amounts of shredded fresh garlic, which was not pleasant anymore to eat. The food, the service and the whole dining experience in the restaurant was for me a big let down. The whole experience just showed me once more that there are too many self proclaimed food experts around who show is HiSo faces on TV and promote something from what they actually have no clue. Sorry to say so, but I would not recommend this restaurant. Ok, for everyone who doesn’t care too much about taste and presentation, it is quite inexpensive for Chinese food and for certain “Foodies” cheap means good. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: I am not sure why do most review states that this restaurant serves the best Dim Sum. The Dim Sum was very horrible when compared to Dim Sum that I have eaten in Hong kong and Singapore. The custard bun seems to be factory made. It wasn't even watery at all. The shanghai dumpling was tasteless. The fried dumpling tasted weird This restuarant is very very expensive. It cost 45 Baht per person for Chrysanthemum tea. Vegetables cost around 160 to 180 Baht. Peking duck cost 1000 Baht. The service was so-so and the staff do not understand English at all. The ambiance is so so as well. There is also a 7% VAT. In comparison, China Kitchen at Shangri-La Hotel charges only 170 Baht for a pot of Chrysanthemum Tea, vegetables only cost 60 Baht to 100 Baht. And the Peking duck cost 700 Baht. The service was top-notch. The staff understand English and they kept on refilling the tea. There is 10% service charge and 7% VAT. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: This is possibly the best Chinese food in Chiang Mai. We go there for great Dim Sum lunches and Great Dinners. The Dim sum is at a high level and though not inexpensive quite delicious. The Best Dish is the The Peking Duck. It is crispy and marvelous and they will make the rest of the duck for you in a wide choice of ways. Love the Black pepper sauce! The Pork and other Chinese dishes are quite good too. Its a great departure from the regular Thai food offered in that part of town. Staff is helpful but little English is spoken. It is in an old house with nice furnishings. Upstairs has private rooms for groups. Atmosphere in OK. There is onsite Parking, a bonus in that neighborhood! Try all the dishes! Enjoy Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: They have a very good and fresh seafood in Canton style. Also roasted pork, duck is also very special dishes. The most popular appetizer is chicken with liquor and jelly fish with spicy source.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Nobody speaks any english here, didnt understand water. This is normally no problem if they are serviceminded and willing. Here everything took forever and the ambience was pretty bad. We ordered 7 dishes( i recommend to order 1 or 2 before going all in) and one was good, some boring and some not good. Crystal porc was not good, fried squid was way too much batter and the squid was chewy, i could go on and on but no. All the food was lacking salt and flavour, simply boring. And we paid 1700 baht for 7 dishes, 2 water and 1 ice tea and for Thailand its very expensive. We had a nice dinner the day before at a neighbourhood japanese restaurant and the bill was 2500baht but it was worth it. Avoid this place if you want good cantonese food and good service. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: This treasure of a restaurant serve amazing Dim Sum for lunch, and awesome Peking duck with full Chinese menu in the evenings. If you are into Chinese food this IS the place in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: I called the day before and made reservations for four people as well as reserved/ordered a Peking duck. All of the food overall was very delicious and tasty and we were happy with everything we ordered. The only problem was that Peking duck- when they brought it out-they only brought out a small portion of the duck even though we had paid for an entire duck. After about 10 minutes of bickering with the owner she reluctantly went back to the kitchen and brought out the rest of the duck we had paid for in a stirfry form and in soup form. I found this annoying because they obviously wanted to save part of the meal we had already paid for and sell it again but once we had it on the table we devoured it it because it was delicious. Delicious but weird experience. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: We loved the food at this restaurant. Good variety, more sophisticated than usual Chinese. We came late before closing and the service was still good and very attentative.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Was pleasantly surprised with Yangzi Jiang, the Peking Duck was excellent as was several other dishes we sampled. Service was good without being overbearing, and the price….WOW, far cheeper than expected for what we had, great value for money. I can only compare with IATA Patu in Bangkok where the prices are more than double.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Ate here as part of a large group to celebrate Chinese New Year. The food was excellent but the portions were very small and the general feeling was we did not get value for money, my impression was that very few, if any of the group, would make a return visit. It may well be the case that 2 or 3 ordering and eating would not encounter the same problem. The decor was excellent but more could have been done to emphasise the festive nature of the event ie Chinese New Year.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: Yummy almost every dishes . Good service . Only chrysanthemum juice is too dissolved and no aroma at all. the room in 1st floor had unpleasant smell.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: delicious snow fish, Chinese spinach, spicy chicken Yunnan-style, and some rather fine rice with egg combined to a light and tasty Christmas dinner. Good attentive and friendly service, even though the duck dish we ordered was not available. Ambiente acceptable but not particularly exciting. The food is the objective in this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: This restaurant is situated in a former house in a short, quiet side street, Soi 5, off the main Nimmanhaemin Road. If you enter from the main road, the soi numbers are well marked, just look up a bit, and the red truck (songtow) driver will have no difficulties getting you to at least the junction of Soi 5 and the main road. The restaurant has a large sign hanging outside and you enter through the garden. It is a middle-of-the-road restaurant, quite nicely decorated and pleasant inside. We (a family of four) had lunch there at least three times and enjoyed every visit but we are partial to Chinese food. We tried the dim sum, which were good, the basic vegetable dishes were nice and the XO noodles seemed to be the pick of the noodle dishes. The dishes are authentic - Cantonese - the price is reasonable for the quality and language is not a problem. It has been noted elsewhere that patience is needed with the waitresses as service can be a little slow and this is true (not deliberately rude, they just need a competent supervisor) but relax and you will not have too long to wait for some tasty food. We will certainly be back during our next visit and it is easy to recommend the place. When you leave, turn left out of the restaurant, walk the few yards back to the main Nimmanhaemin Road and catching a red truck will be easy - if you need one. I cannot comment on an evening meal. My rating is for a middle-of-the-road restaurant. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: After a very good dim sum lunch here, we returned a few days later for dinner. While the Peking duck skin course was crisp and fat-free, the duck meat main course was almost inedible; meat was fried & extremely greasy, and tasted like it had hung around unrefrigerated for much too long prior to cooking. Adding insult to injury was the unannounced extra 100 baht "duck meat cooking charge" which appeared on the bill- after being charged a high 1,000 baht for the duck! Orange chicken was a deep-fried slab of pounded chicken meat with some gluey orange sauce dumped on top. Calamari was heavily breaded and served with completely uncooked garlic. Shanghai noodles had good texture and sliced black mushrooms, but copious quantities of oil. Service was amateurish and slow (as other reviews have noted also). Overall, worth a visit for dim sum lunch, but studiously avoid for dinner. Read less

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: yangzi jiang is chinese restaurant on nimman soi 5, opened not so long ago, probably just a couple years I think. The food there tastes really good, high quality and fresh ingredient, professionally cooked. I enjoyed every menus I have ordered (which around 10-12dishes) roasted duck very yummy. the skin really crispy and meat so soft. and as well as crisypy pork. for dim sum, it's second to none. can easily beat chef man in BKK. highly recommmend. and pls make a reservation too.

Restaurant_Name: แยงซีเกียง
Detail: I've been to the other original place Muay Jiang, this place is way much better for the food. Well, service still needs improvement in terms that most of the students are mostly students and trainees. The location is way much better since it is not far all the way in Chiang Mai Land area. I have eaten the "Hot Sour Soups with Seafood"...very delicious than the other place. Stir-fried beans Kenya style, boiled chicken in white wine sauce, stew roast duck with black beans...all delicious...and finish it with a mango pudding.
