Detail: Consistently delicious Thai food at a very reasonable price. Lots of variety on the menu and good vegetarian options. Service was friendly and the food came out quickly. Me and my partner came back here frequently throughout our trip. Plus there was a cat! Winner! :D

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: it is a good place where you can taste thay food good service quickly and fresh food with amazing persons and a boss cat on the desk

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great busy place to eat. Good menu variety and the food is really tasty and cooked at the front of the cafe, so you can see all the food preparation and the creating of your meal. We went there a few times and we were always happy with our meals. Staff were happy and friendly and service was quick. Each visit the staff wear a different color Tshirt! Fun concept !! Definitely a place to eat and enjoy the fresh, tasty food. Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: My husband and I visited Aroy Dee Restaurant for lunch today.They have an open kitchen so you can see the chefs cooking.The kitchen is clean and wait staff attentive.The food is very fresh.My husband ordered sweet and sour chicken and I ordered deep fried fish in green curry.Both dishes were amazing.So two dishes rice and 4 beers was 360 Baht about $15 Aus.I would recommend this restaurant.It is only a 10minute walk from the Tha Phae Gate.Enjoy

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I loved this place! The staff are incredibly friendly, the prices are ridiculously low and the food is delicious! There are certainly nicer places you could go, but we stopped here just about every day. It's a good location, nice vibe, great food and service... I'm shocked it has received any poor reviews.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Have eaten there twice and both times loved the dishes we got. Prices are good, food comes out fast and staff is friendly. Nice atmosphere. Clean and yummy. Get whatever you want. It’s all yummy!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We ordered a thai green curry and fried chicken with basil, and felt we could have done better ourselves at home! The portions were quite small and left feeling hungry. The chicken with basil was very plain and felt they'd tried to make it for westerners but just ended up boring. Service was good and its fun to see them cook. Not very impressed.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Very healthy, Thai recipe food, always fresh meat and vegetables. Great variety of vegetarian and non vegeterian food.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I will be back next year. Looking forward to another great meal and catch up Surin. If you haven’t tried the

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Never got a chance to try the food as it took more than 50 minutes till we had enough of waiting. The table next to us decided to bring in snacks from nearby stalls during their wait. After we saw them trying to figure out the orders we couldn't take it anymore and left. The bottled water was fantastic. As an aside, we were here three years ago, before they expanded and it was fine. The staff seem to be ok with bringing in outside food and drink and I would recommend doing the same.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: My 2nd visit. Still no wifi. I ordered the Khao Soi which I use as a standard. The Khao soi was average. There were plenty of noodles and veg but the amount of chicken was low. The dish was 60 Baht. A small Chang was 50 Baht. Grazie next door is much better value for money as is Lucky Too not far away. I don't think I'll be back. There are too many better places close by

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We went in october, my friends and I (7 pearson). We are from Spain. The manager is very kind and he explain us a lot of things about Thailand, his English is so good! The food is so delicious. We went every days when we were in Chiang Mai. The restaurant is very clean and cheap, the food is amazing, authentic thai food. For me, the best meal is pineapple rice or the pad thai. Totally recommended!!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Come on! Are you kidding me? This place is unavoidable, big wooden table inside and half a dozen table on the street opposite the pavement at night. The reason it's busy is because the place is located near the wet and veggies market. This is also the backpackers area to converge, the epicenter of cheap guest houses. No wonder you will see mostly if no only, foreigners and the usual vulgar china tourists. AVOID THIS PLACE AT ALL COST, you need to do a little leg work and don'rt be a foul. This is not because the place swam with foreigners, that is good. On the contrary, the place is a joke and the food is total crap. not Thai cuisine at all. We order 'saute' mixed veggies with chicken and specifically asked for 'SPICY'. Instead, the waiter who barely could speak English, delivered our food on a white pathetic plastic plate, the food was so pathetic and poorly prepared. It was NOT spicy at all as I we had asked. The Tom Yam soup looked more like washing dishes water with an array of spice... RUBBISH. And, frankly, disgusting COOKING and NOT THAI FOOD AT ALL. A total disappointment and a place to avoid. DON'T BE FOULED BY THE CROWD SEATING AT TABLE, they don't know what they eat. IT TOTAL RUBBISH COOKING Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: My friends and I found this place on the first evening there. For dinner, there are tables also in the street, a very nice atmosphere. We liked so much the food and went there again for lunch another day. The food was delicious and the service was great.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Everything is perfect starting from the Owner, the staff and the best thai food! I was staying in Chiang Mai for a month and ate there every day!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We wait too long for our meal around 1hour for eating dinner. Although foods are good taste, we do not accept your bad serving.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Take long time (around 1 hour) to wait food, staff's attitude is bad. Remind staff many times for order food

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Stumbled upon this gem while wandering the streets of Chaing Mai. We ended up eating here a few times as it was so tasty! Very friendly staff and clean restaurant

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Super nice people running the restaurant, food is great! Spring rolls are a must, yellow curry is really good and I quite enjoyed the clear noodle soup, it tasted a lot like chicken noodle soup which I like! Also can accommodate a good amount of people with no fuss which is really helpful! Great place!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Found this place by accident and it seemed to be very popular so went for some noodles. Oh my god! SO GOOD! Came back three times after that. The food was super cheap and portions were large. Everything we tried was delicious (except the curry prawns which were not to our liking). Staff was friendly and welcoming. Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We walked in by sheer chance as we were getting peckish and haven't regretted it. We came by for a total of 4 meals during our visit, it was THAT good. Everything is freshly cooked and you can even see employees leave and come back with ingredients from the nearby market. My absolute favourite was the clear noodle soup.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I went there with a lady friend, after having seen the reviews and ranking on trip advisor. I live in Chiangmai, and my friend is Thai. On arrival, the restaurant was packed! Lots of backpackers. A packed place is often a good sign. We ordered a tom yam kung (she is a fan!) for her and a clear soup for me. Her tom yam kung was one of the worse she's ever had. Thai people like to eat spicy, of course this was "foreigner style" and not spicy at all. No trace of any mushrooms in the soup (she loves them) and totally tasteless. My clear soup was tasteless too. Nothing to rave home about. The food is clean of course, that's why I don't rate them "terrible". All the dishes are overpriced compared to the other Thai restaurants, even in the old city. They are cheap by American or European standards of course, but not by Thai standards. And I am always happy to pay a higher price when the food is worth it. But sadly I felt is was not the case. I won't go back. Sad because their location is very convenient. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This restaurant is on the east side of the old city. It opens till late night so if you get hungry at night you can go there and eat something. I had thai fried rice and it was really good. I liked the fact that they put grinded peanut on the table.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The food was very delicious,fresh and cheap would recommend that place to everyone. Had a orange curry the beat so far in thailand

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Finest selection of local foods, delicious and diversified tastes along a very quick and efficient service. A must try no matter how much time spent you have in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Was walking by and looked in and the food ingredients looked really fresh. I told the guy there I'd come back at lunch. Glad I did. Was excellent. So good I came back the next day.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Saw many locals sitting inside or on the street and decided to give it a try. Best thai food i had up untill now, and so cheap. At night sometimes busy and have to wait for tables when raining. Very fast service and tasty so cheap food.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Must try brown rice with grains and a meat of your choice (also has Tofu option) and the green noodles (also vegetarian-friendly choice). Absolutely delicious. Loved loved loved the green noodles! Staff was lovely and spoke very good English. Will definitely come back!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We were on our way to lunch and switched places after smelling what Aroy Dee was making - literally. Vey tasty food, cold Chang beer, and good service. Definitely recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We ate here every day in Chiang Mai. The food was cheap (pad Thai 50b, chicken fried rice 50b) and good size portions. The staff we always very friendly and welcoming especially the restaurant manager. The food was stored hygienically not just left on the side like some places. not Would definitely recommend trying this place.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Very friendly staff, nice clean environment. Most importantly they are open late and have a good selection of vegetarian friendly Thai food!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Maybe the chef was not there.. I come for many years in Thailand now. I'm a positive guy.. But guys this was an horrible experience. My rice with vegetable and cashewnuts with chicken came on a greasy plate. It had a greasy taste.. And the chicken? I thought it was fried chicken skin. I am not a complainer but it was the worst served food to me. So I complained at th manager. He told me it was normal chickenmeat treated with powder? Took a picture of this "breast meat"

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great reasonably priced food and drinks, nice choice of food with a menu well laid out! Polite and attentive staff always ready to help Visited twice and would reccomend to all Aroy ;)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Around 60bht a meal, perfect size portions. Food comes when ready, so your partner may have eaten by the time you get yours(you'll probably help!)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Myself and my boyfriend visited Around Dee on our first night in Chaing Mai. The food and the service were great. The kitchen is all open and at the front of the restaurant so you can see it all being prepared, and all the fresh ingredients. I chose to try the green noodles as it was something g i hadn't heard of before, also being g a vegetarian there's not a huge choice in Thailand for trying something really different. I asked the waitress what the green noodles were made from. She quickly went and got the restaurant owner to come o we and explain how the noodles at made and how he has tried many different recipes i. The past and that this is the best one. He was a very friendly man who was extremely interesting. He explained to us how he got into cooking and how he is training up his chefs now to be the best they can be. He is a very passionate man who truly loves what he does. The food tastes great and the price is very good. We have been back once since and think we will probably visit again before leaving g Chaing Mai. The only downside is, it's too far a walk from where we are staying, or i think we would eat here every day. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Prices were very reasonable. Everything was good but there's no free wifi. It's a minor point I know but I would go back to Cooking love more often because of it. Food and service are both very good but Cooking love have free wifi. CL also has local Chiang Mai craft beers

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We ate here every day when we were in Chaing Mai. The food was delicious and the staff were so friendly.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I'm the person who put this on TripAdvisor a few years back. Happy to report that it's STILL delicious and still affordable. What has changed is TripAdvisor has made it impossible for me to add little gems like this. We all suffer for it.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We found this place by accident on our first trip to Chiang Mai and have now eaten here several times whenever we visit. Amazing tasting food at prices only a little higher than average street stalls (food ranges from around 50-90 baht). The meat curries are delicious and cheap (60 baht with rice). Amazing pad Thai for 50 baht or Cashew Chicken with rice for 80. Great hole in the wall restaurant. Highly reccommend if you want some delicious but moderately priced food.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The food was good and the service was fast. We could see the food being prepared from the table. The dishes were around 50-60 each.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We lost count of how many times we ate here--and how many friends we introduced to the terrific curries, drunken noodles, everything really. Prices are budget, but the quality is far above that. It's fascinating to watch staff select ingredients, hand them to the streetside chefs for immediate cooking, and have the dish in front of you within a couple of minutes. The restaurant gets busy but it's worth the wait.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This place is always full house, it's advisable to have your lunch/dinner slightly earlier than the typical meal times if you are planning for your meal here. We ordered pad thai with chicken, fried pineapple rice, fried drunken noodles with shrimp, fried brown rice with pork, fried morning glory, green curry chicken and tom yum goong. Every dish was amazing and delicious. All the staff were friendly and this place is definitely one of those local shop to get local food at wallet friendly prices. Besides being a little hot when it is crowded, everything else was awesome. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Visited on a number of occasions and left pleased every time. There is an excellent variety of fresh authentic Thai cuisine. The staff are friendly & efficient and the menu offers enough tasty dishes to please all. Also the food is really well priced and there is a great atmosphere around the place. Would highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The food was so great and well priced at Aroy Dee that me and my partner ate there numerous times during our stay in Chiang Mai. The staff are also very friendly and attentive. Their food is so fresh and really tasty.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We wondered why ths restaurant was always busy. Then we tried it! It was not only one of the best meals we have head in Chiang Mai, it was by far the cheapest. That is great value for your money. Mr. Surin Boonkum personally sat us down and took care of us.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: An efficiently run restaurant. The kitchen and dining is in one common room. The ingredients were in full view, and always fresh. The menu was traditional Chiang Mai, simple, and tasty food. There was a noodle soup that was rich and filling. I also enjoyed the fried rice. The staff was efficient, the service excellent.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We've eaten here several times during our stay - if you want a tasty, inexpensive Thai-style meal in a simple setting this is just right. The food is all cooked to order in woks while you wait and service is quick and efficient. The pineapple rice with chicken and roasted wide noodles with prawns are our recommendations.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I came back a couple of times! Really good and really cheap. Good option if you're a vegetarian - you can choose tofu for almost all of the options. Very nice!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This place serves average Thai food - adjusted to foreigner's taste-buds. For Asians and local Thai, the food would taste a bit bland and lack authenticity. The prices are not that cheap either. So we guess whether it is truly "Aroy" will depend on which nationality the reviewer is...

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: One of the best places to go if you want to eat cheap and delicious. Always many people at dinner time (means it is good), so sometimes you have to wait a while but worth it. Friendly owner, young staff, open kitchen, fair prices. Along the road, but you can sit inside as well. We have been there several times, never disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This place does not look like much but we stopped in as it was so busy! The food is crazy good! We went back again and will be going back again once we return to chang mai! The food is so cheap and so good. Everything is cooked in front of you in a wok station. I had the organic veg and rice which was one of the tastiest dishes I have ever been served. We also tried the fried rice and Khao Soy which were also great. Can have dinner for two with a drink for under 200 baht. You cant beat that value!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I've been here a number of times. The food is consistently good and it is delicious! Can't beat it! Perfect for backpackers budget :)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Don't expect fancy cutlery or other things like that when you come in here, but expect smiles and tasty thai food, i had the pad thai and it was amongst the best in thailand, the prices are extremely cheap for what you get

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Cheap ingredients, particularly the veg, my wife's prawn dish had 4 small prawns in it, my chicken was in tiny pieces in batter and fried, it tasted of nothing. The only saving grace is that it was cheap, otherwise it would have been a waste of money.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Ordered a noodle soup lunch to go one day just to fill the hunger spot, sat on the grass along the moat and enjoyed my meal. Was very tasty, so decided to pop in for dinner one night. Was busy so waited a few minutes for a table (was solo and another person was also waiting, so we sat together and conversed). Ordered a Pad Thai (delicious) and Khao Soi (absolutely awesome taste) and a beer. Total cost was not much more than 100 B. One of the great local eats in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The pineapple fried rice was delicious as well as their Khao Soi. Additionally I left my camera and case in the restaurant after we finished lunch. I didn't realize this until I was going to bed and wanted to copy the photos from that day. I ran back to the restaurant and they had secured it in their office until I came to get it. This saved me a great deal of anxiety. I owe them a great deal. But seriously their food was very good.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great variety of Thai food to choose from. Very clean and delicious dishes. Absolutely worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Really delicious food and great atmosphere at Aroy Dee. The kitchen is out in the open meaning that you can watch as your food is being skilfully prepared. There was a queue outside which sometimes leads to a disappointment when it doesn't live up to the hype but this certainly did. Also, the queue went down really quickly and there was seating but standing watching them cook everyone's meal was a good way to pass the time and build up an appetite. Would highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Amazing little place we found by coincidence. If you like cooking you will love it! Inside, there is a big table with all the veggies and herbs freshly cut, the noodle pre-cooked. When you order, you can go outside and look at how they prepare your dish. I got the drunken noodles (the wide ones) and it was simply AMAZING! I definitely recommend this place! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Randomly stumbled upon this place once in Chiangmai. The food is good, the decor is lovely yet more authentic than in most central Chiangmai places, the prices are very reasonable. Nothing truly outstanding, but a pleasant experience all around.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great location....had the omelette..NO MSG! quite a good omelette for the price..Fluffy, 2 egg omlette with veggies.. Nice staff who tries to please.....open air place.... It does have a Chinese feel to it but I loved the omelette////

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Amazing Thai food at low prices. Staff are all super friendly and accommodating. Was never a night we went where it wasn't full! Highly suggest to all. 50 baht for a curry... perfect.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Ordered a chicken styr fry dish. I am not even sure I got chicken as the meat had been deep fried before hand so it might as well have been pork, lamb, beef... or... surprise surprise that my stomach felt sick afterwards! Next time I really hope that they use fresh chicken. The stir fry itself was tasteless. It was supposed to be one with a lot of thai flavors in. There was absolutely nothing. Just vegetables a questionable meet styr fried a few seconds in oil. I even asked to get the dish spicy. There was no chili in whatsoever. No dried chili, no fresh chili, no powder chili. Nothing. Very disappointing to pay three times the price of a similar dish that you can get in a street stall just outside (and with that dish from the street stall you can be sure to get taste). Only positive thing was the staff. They were friendly. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: It felt more like a chinese restaurant than a thai one and perhaps this showed in some of the dishes. Certainly what we ordered had some random vegetables in that we were not expecting, nor were they listed in the ingredients on the menu. Still, it was ok. Wouldn't rush to go back as the food wasnt particularly memorable.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great food, Great prices, great crew. Everything you need in to eat you'll find here! We recommend for 100%

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We have eaten here numerous times and it never fails to impress. Great service, great food and a friendly atmosphere. Even when its busy wait times aren't long and the food is always great. Prices are good and portions aren't small. Would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone eating in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Food was cheap, and delicious. Food portions were great good atmosphere. Staff didn't speak a ton of English but no problems communicating.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Restaurant with seating outside on the other side of the sidewalk. The food is cooked in the open and this is how I noticed this restaurant. Chicken and veggies was amazing, not a lot of places in Chiang Mai give veggies, especially at this price.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Open kitchen insight the restaurant. Fresh food which was super delicious. We went there several times and were happy with all dishes we ordered. Always again!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Lovely People, Great Service, Good Hygiene, Good Food. Perfect! A quick and easy place to grab a meal or hang out for a while. The food is good and portions are reasonable. Dishes are aimed at a Western palate, but each table has the full range of Thai condiments, so you can spice your food up to taste. Great location,on the Eastern moat of the Old City. Good memories, we made a lot of new friends here. Each dish is prepared to order, in full view, nothing dodgy here at all. Main dishes from 50 Baht. That's very cheap for Chiang Mai's tourist eateries. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great food cooked in the open. Tasty dishes around the THB 80 mark. Limited choice of beverges but otherwise superb.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We always stay on Moon Muang road and are looking for good Thai Food. We were passing by and noticed this restaurant so we entered and we're welcomed by the owner we had a great lunch.The place is clean and the service was quick with freshly cooked food. Will be back again on our next trip to CHIANGMAI.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Delicious food choices and great service. Owner Surin Boonkum welcomes his customers personally. A nice touch that makes you feel appreciated. Friendly waiters. Clean restaurant with nice decoration.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We discovered this place on our last day at lunch time. So good - delicious, clean and some many fresh veggies, and good value they we came back for dinner. Probably our favourite meal of the tri and one of the cheapest.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Good food, good value and nice atmosphere. Wondered in Aroy Dee whilst looking for another place to eat. This seemed the better option. Would recommend to all travellers.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I've been coming here ever since this place was a restaurant no bigger than a bathroom with two sad lonely table and chairs outside on the street. I've watched arooy dee grow into a culinary palace, one of the finest establishments in the old city of Chiang Mai...the yellow noodles are delightful, the chicken cashew nut a glorious dream you woke up from that you spend the entire morning trying to remember in enough vivid detail to relive it. I had my first kao soi here. I think I had my first Tom yum though it may have been my second...either way I have memories here that will live on way beyond the freshly refurbished walls. And the owner is a legend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This is my third time visiting Chiang Mai and the food at Aroy Dee is still great, especially their Pad Thai and chicken fried rice! They are yummy and you can have a full meal at an affordable price! The servers are nice and welcoming. A great place to grab a quick bite to eat!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: No doubt Aroy Dee is the best place with the best quality of food, the team is just awesome, always smily, the food is fresh and simply delicious. You could ask for spicy or non spicy thai food and you should know that this restaurant is really authentic, exactly like the state of mind of thai people. If you are reading us ( it's Pierre & Nicky from France ) we miss you sooooo much !!!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great food, friendly service! You can eat in the restaurant or you can ask the food for take away! WiFi never works :)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: A good find on Moonmoung Rd along the moat. Yes, it's Thai food but a little more upscale and relaxing. The food is good and not over powering. The staff are very available and take good care. Really nice restaurant in an area that is being dressed up!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We came here for almost every meal during our 3 night stay in Chiang Mai. The staff are unbelievably friendly and did not swindle us into coming in off the street like other restaurants do. The food is amazing and very affordable. 50 baht for Pad Thai I think? We spoke to presumably the owner who says he buys food fresh everyday from the markets and ensures no MSG. Clearly staff here take pride in their work as this restaurant is already a popular venue. We'll be back pho real Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This lil place in Chiang Mai is great, budget eats, friendly staff, veggo vegan friendly and no MSG , eaten here heaps and loved it !!!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: It's 5am and I've been up all night violently sick on both ends from my curry here . Mind you , I have been in Thailand for over a month now , eating everything from street fair spring rolls to fancy . This is the only place that made me ill . Stay away !!!!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I came across this place walking down the street and noticed the amazing smell. The food is very good, authentic and seems to be fresh. It's a great cheap eat. After trying quite a few different dishes here on different occasions, I've come to the conclusion that the Khao Soi and chicken stir fried with cashew nuts are the best options. It's really worth a visit if you are ever strolling down moon muang and need somewhere to eat.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We got recommendation to go here. We went twice our first visit we both got thai green curry it was nice but small portion second time got shrimp noodle was bland both times very small portions it was a good price for a restaurant at 120baht foe 2 dishes .but could get 3 dishes bigger on street. The staff were very friendly and restaurant is busy

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The food here is so cheap, my partner and I ordered two dishes for lunch; morning glory with pork and chicken with basil on rice and some water - the bill came to 110 baht! We also loved that you can watch them cooking your food, you know it's fresh! Also the portion sizes and the quality of the food is great for such a low price. Will be returning for sure :)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Although what everyone says about the fresh ingredients and large portions is true unfortunately there's one thing missing; flavour. I gave Aroy Dee three chances during my stay in Chiang Mai and was disappointed each time. The restaurant seems well suited to an American palate but just like Alex says below I found the food quite bland. If you want some real northern Thai food, get away from the tourist traps and cross the moat. There are some real gems right around the Tha Phae gate area.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We stumbled across this restaurant while walking home from Somphet Market and we enjoyed our lunch so much that we returned for dinner the following night. All the food we tried was delicious but the Khao Soi Gai was particularly good, and excellent value at just 50 baht! Beers and food are both well priced and the service was very friendly from all staff members. Definitely worth a visit for good quality, good value local food.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I've eaten here twice so far whilst in Chiang Mai and both times I've throughly enjoyed my food. All dishes on the menu are very cheap and tonight infact they kindly amended something for me as I'm a bit full of the old rice....... The staff are very sweet and super friendly and no doubt I will be eating there again before I leave CM. Yum Yum!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We strolled into Aroy Dee and are so glad that we did, it is simply fantastic! The food is fresh and comes quickly and not to mention is the tastiest Thai food we've had in Thailand! The service was wonderful and would recommend this to everyone.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I've been on this earth more than seven decades, and have consumed substantial amounts of soup. This khao soi is clearly the finest soup ever. Do not miss this.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This is a nice friendly restaurant where they cook out the front where you can see. So far I have tried 3 dishes here. Tofu pad Thai, Kao Soi & sticky mango with rice. All very tasty and cheap. I will be eating here again.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I visited here by accident and I was so suprised that there is really nice hidden restaurant. The food is very tasty and the atmosphere is very nitty and nice.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: On the first day we arrived in Chiang Mai we saw this restaurant cooking food behind a glass window. We stopped and watched how the food was prepared and cocked. We were so impressed that we came back in the evening and ordered the same food I saw earlier. I ate Chicken and vegetable with cashew nut. It was so fantastic. My wife had Chicken red curry with rice which was also lovely. I was so impressed with the food that I had to speak to the owner. He cooked the Chicken with Cashew nut for me. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: This must be the best Thai food restaurant in Chiang Mai and one of the best in Thailand. We ate there every day. The food was amazing - taste, big portions. you always get the best quality food for a really good price. They might have similar prices elsewhere, but the portions and quality is nowhere near this place. Just go there and try, you will eat there all the time!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I have been going there almost everyday for lunch when in the area, the Pad Thai there is excellent not to say the best I have had.. but what makes it even better is the quantity of food served for 50 nth... I can barely finish it... And when I do I feel too full to do anything after.. I haven't had anything else there as the Pad Thai is mistlikely the best thing on the menu and no doubt the best value around highly recommend

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Had a really tasty red thai curry with duck breast . Very nicely presented and good flavour. Staff were really friendly . Quite basic but well worth a visit .

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: this small restaurant is a gem. their food is very fresh, lots of vegetables. and also important, portions are big! very friendly owner aswell. next time we are in chiang mai we are eating there again, for sure!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: The title says it all: simply the best Thai food I've experienced in Chiang Mai! The staff is great, the cooking is done on the moment and is always quickly delivered, every dish from the small selection is amazing. So good I often found myself looking forward for lunch and dinner time! (PS. Pad Thai is absolutely a must)

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Aroy Dee (It's delicious) is as good as it's name. Located in a row of open-air restaurants on Mun Mueang, it's an unpretentious establishment next to a pizza parlor with the cook preparing the food at a front counter and all the fresh ingredients on display. I've had chicken with cashew and other dishes here, and all were top-notch. It's one of the terrific values in the Old City.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: We were hungry late night and all the restaurants near our B&B were closed except this one. Everyone greeted and welcomed us with a smile. We ordered pad see ew and morning glory. Both tasted delicious and healthy/clean. They don't use MSG and they cook the food at the front of the store instead of the kitchen in the back. Owner came to us halfway through our meal asking how they tasted with a huge smile. Price is very reasonable and food tasted amazing! We also had a really long conversation with the owner, as it was our first day in Chiang Mai, he recommended us some things to do around town and how to avoid getting jibbed by the tuk2 drivers. Will definitely be back for some other Thai food goodness before leaving town! Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: After traveling through Thailand, this restaurant served the best Thai food I have ever had. I went on a recommendation of another friend who said the same thing. The food is all cooked by someone at the front of the restaurant with ingredients brought up by an older woman from the back. At dinner they had a separate waiter but when I went back for lunch it seemed like the cook was also the only one who spoke English so we had to wait for her to finish someone else's food before she came to take our order. There are two menus, one is a much thicker book composed of full page photographs and the other one is more just text with smaller photos. However the menus vary slightly and the dish I really loved was only on the full page photo book (creamy tom yum with chicken and noodles!). Sooo delicious. My dining partner also loved what he got both time we went - a green curry shrimp stirfry and the tom kha gai. Large portions at a good price (~$2). Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I've always been told that little hole in the wall restaurants in Thailand has some of the best food and Aroy Dee is one such place. Needed a good kao soi before leaving Chiang Mai and stumbled upon this restaurant by chance. So glad that I ate here as the kao soi is delicious and the staff are really nice and friendly. Need to go back!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Had a bunch of noodles dishes, a bowl of tom yum goong and a curry paste prawn dish. All noodles dishes and especially the curry paste prawn dish were unseasoned and bland. The curry paste prawn dish was particularly disappointing for me. Tom yum soup was the only decent thing that I like. Go here if you like relatively bland food.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Great find! Big portions, delicious food and great friendly staff. We will def be going back during our time in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: Authentic quick yummy Thai food run by a very nice family with excellent service. Local street vender with super cheap prices. Ka!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I stumbled upon this place by the friendliness of the employees while walking down the moat road. They were very welcoming and the smells from the open kitchen led me inside. It is small with only 5 tables so don't go during peak dinner hours. I ordered the pad see ew and pad thai. AMAZING. The pad see ew wasnt too greasy which was great as most thai dishes overuse oil. There was a plethora of different flavors, not just sweet like American pad see ew. The pad thai was tangy and delicious. Best part about this place was the cost, 50 baht per plate which is on the same range of street cart prices but restaurant quality food. Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอร่อยดี Aroi Dee
Detail: I stopped here for Shrimp Pad Tai recently. I could not believe the care taken by the chef to achieve this dish! It was worth the wait! This is a total stall experience yet they have a permanent home. His wagon is parked across the front of his store! Behind that he's amassed a variety of ingredients he would not have fit in the wagon. Kickin it old school! Right next to the pizza joint almost all the way up to the corner on Moon Mueng (the Thae Pae Gate road) on the inside road. There's really quite an assortment down there and this hits the mark!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: Was here for the bbq. But didn’t like the pork fat thing on the bone. The pork itself was decent for BBQ. Side dishes were all similar. Noisy, way too bright lighting inside and indifferent teenage staff. Wouldn’t go back.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: We weren’t impressed by this particular Korean restaurant. Side dishes were very small and ordinary, dumplings were tasteless, the rolled cheese omelette made up for our disappointment.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: I went there to book day trip..however the lunch buffet looked so tempting and decided to have korean food as my fist meal in Chiangmai. I never regret it.. the food was very good with reasonable price and i want to come back before leaving this city.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: Menus are illustrated and thi-lingual. The dishes were average and the several Korean side dishes less than average. I was not impressed. Ordered: karae dopbab (pork curry) and fried mandu (dumplings).

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: One of the good korean food restaurant in chiangmai, thailand. Try it if you miss the tastes of korean food. BBQ is recommended.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: I went here for Gochujang many times. Excellent service. I never eat the grill buffet, so I can't comment on that. But Gochujang always taste very good!

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: I am Korean and traveling here on holiday and needed a Korean food fix. I was so happy when I found this place and then it was downhill from there. They were offering a lunch buffet and my husband opted for that and I ordered a dolsot bibimbap. My husband hit the buffet and found some of the dishes were cold and barely anything was left. He cancelled the buffet and decided to order the dolsot bibimbap also. In the meantime mine came out and it was a total disappointment! There were none of the traditional Korean vegetables in it. It was basically sautéed onions (?!), two unidentifiable vegetables and only thing right was the sautéed carrots. I will say the ban chan was passable and the daen jang jigae was good. As my husband saw my dolsot bibimbap he cancelled his. I have been to many Korean restaurants around the work and never have I had an unauthentic dish trying to pass off as Korean. I would excuse this restaurant but I saw that the owners are Korean!! Shameful! The best thing was the ice cold Chang beer 👏 ps I should have returned the dish but I was in such shock I ate it quietly Read less

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: Wanted to try Korean food, as we had only had Korean barbecue. If you love cabbage then go to this restaurant. If not forget it. I'll stick with the barbecue from now on. Enough said. Nice service.

Restaurant_Name: ร้านอาหารเกาหลีมิโซเน่เชียงใหม่
Detail: Looked a Korean restaurant in Chiang Mai since the other I use to go to is not there anymore. So far Misone is the best one. The only problem for me that's it's on the spicy side for. But on the bright side it clears my pores in my skin. Just kidding. But the food is really tasty.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: My Thai wife and I (Japanese) have visited this place over 30 times and now it is one of our most favorite restaurants in town in terms of taste, healthyness and good value for money. One easy way to find a good Japanese restaurant in Thailand is to try tempura and to see if the oil has been well drained before being served and you don't feel too oily. Despite being an inexpensive Japanese restaurant, Smiley Kitchen always meets this requirement.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: I love coming here for my Japanese fix. Lovely Jap lady owner. Great lunch deals. Very good home cook Jap food.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: You know when you walk in that you've found the right place. Most of the other diners are speaking Japanese, a good sign that you're in for an authentic dining experience. The food was delicious and the portions were generous. Both my spouse's fish and my pork were tender. Fine food in a clean setting. Avoid the middle table of the dining room, though, unless you enjoy the frigid blast of the air con. (We moved to the delightful outside dining area and watched the street traffic, mostly pedestrians, whlle we ate.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: The cooked foods are pretty good, and the daily bento is good, given that it tends to be a little heavy on rice. The daily soup often served with the daily bento is homemade and very good.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: This is a simple family run authentic Japanese restaurant. The food was great comparable to what i have eaten in Japan ! I had a generous amount of beef with my udon ! Met other Japanese guest who eats at this restaurant as well, and some are very regular at this place. Will definitely go there again when I go to Chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: We came for late lunch and was last patrons to leave. This allowed us to chat up the owner who is a lovely lady from Hokkaido. Today I had gyoza set, which was 6 gyoza with soy-chili oil dip, cabbage salad garnish with Japanese potato salad, small bowl of miso soup and bowl of white rice. I ordered eggplant in miso as a side, and I loved it, but truly too much food!! All delicious and nothing too salty. Hubby had grilled mackerel set which had miso soup, white rice and Japanese pickles. He ordered fried octopus and it was not greasy, crispy and fresh. He had one large Singha and I had iced green tea and bill was 775 baht. Took us an hour to get through it and we left sated and full. So glad we revisited this place. Only farang in the joint, so that’s always a good sign. Highly recommended!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: This family restaurant is run by Japanese - and you can tell! The food is really good and authentic and most of the customers are Japanese too. A great restaurant and we will definitely be coming back - often.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: -Feel like... Japanese mom cook for her children. Love to be like that. Please, don't change! -Some dish also difference from other place, such as egg.... can't explain, you have to try. Recommended Salmon rice.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: Excellent food, service, and atmosphere. Some of the best Japanese food Ive had and the owner is so pleasant! She speaks English which is convenient and the menu has English in it too (with pictures). My wife & I went 3 times and every time (lunch and dinner) at least 50% of the customers were Japanese and you definitely know why they come once you try the food! Highly recommended!

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: Tired of Thai cooking and so we decided to have some Japanese food for a change. Initially wanted to try the Tengaku but on the way we saw this cosy little home cooking restaurant and were attracted to it. It is a very small restaurant and most of the customers were Japanese on that day. The menu is simple but have enough varieties. There are quite a number of set menu and some A la carte. We ordered an onion salad, grilled egg plants with miso sauce, a pork sukiyaki set and a rice bowl with fried pork chops with eggs. The salad was simple but delicious. The Japanese dressing went very well with the onion The egg plants were tender and hot and yummy too. The pork chop rice was just divine. The pork was tender and the sauce has seeped into every tiny bits of rice and I could not help but finish the whole bowl of rice. The sukiyaki was also good with lots of vegetables. Overall it tasted exactly like have comfort food at home and I will definitely come back again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: Simple but authentic Japanese cuisine. The restaurant itself is as basic as its food but honest and of good quality.

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: Smiley Kitchen is a smallish Japanese restaurant that offers authentic home style Japanese cuisine. Seating either inside an air conditioned dining room or on the open-air veranda in front, is comfortable and spacious. The menu is extensive and divided into the different types of food available. I chose a grilled eggplant with pork set which was absolutely delicious as well as being a generous serving - I was fully satiated with the last bite. The pork was cut into very thin strips and was tender and juicy. I chose iced green tea, which had a pleasant and mild flavor and there are numerous other beverages to choose from including Japanese beer if you prefer. I certainly recommend Smiley Kitchen for great food, friendly service and a good place to chat with friends.Open 11:00 - 15:00, 17:00 - 21:00, Monday to Saturday. Read less

Restaurant_Name: สมาย คิชเช่น ร้านอาหารญี่ปุน
Detail: Walk down Nimmanhaemin Soi 3 and Smiley Kitchen is about 50 meters on the right. Look for the pink and yellow sign in the window (see pic) written in Japanese. This is a Japanese style cafe with traditional Japanese dishes. However there is no Sushi or Sashimi on the menu. I had the rice bowl with fried prawn which was simply amazing, as was the Japanese style fried chicken. The place is run by a Japanese lady and had many Japanese dining when I was there, ALL a good sign. Prices are very reasonable, expect 500 to 600 baht for two people. Quality and freshness I would give this place a 10. Will be going back for sure. If it is not too hot the outdoor seating is very nice too. Read less
Date_Written: in Japanese. This is a Japanese style cafe with traditional Japanese dishes. However there is no Sushi or Sashimi on the menu.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best Northern Thai food ever! I love Khao Soi so much 🥰 It’s super cheap and sooo delicious! The staff were really nice and friendly too. Definitely coming back!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Order Khao So mussels and saiuas. I looked for saiua from the menu, but I couldn't find anything that sounded like it in Thai and English, so I asked the staff and they said that was it. It is surprisingly fast from the time you order until the food comes out. The khaoso mussels were just delicious, and the saiuas were very good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Terrible food. The only pro is the atmosphere is very quaint and full of antiques. The chili paste with egg was utterly inedible, without flavor or spice. It was just raw vegetables to be dipped in a "paste" that was too watery to stick to them. The pork rib curry was acceptable. Very, very disappointed. The prices were also higher than elsewhere. Avoid this restaurant. Will not return.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good authentic local food. Not fancy but clean and staff speaks enough English to get by. Near temples so convenient for tourists. Would come again.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is basic middle-class Thai restaurant. I had the Kao Soi Gai and there were several dishes ordered by different Thai folks in the group. Everyone agreed the food was prepared properly and tasty. We were recommended to this place by a Baht Bus Driver. That is usually a good sign the food will be good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Asked a Baht Bus driver where the Best Kao Soi Gai (soft noodle soup with crispy noodles, chicken, pickle, and bean sprout or onion). He took us here without hesitation and he was right. Delicious. Our party of four was very satisfied with the food and service. This is a very Northern Thailand restaurant for the Thai People to eat at regularly. The vibe is just right if you want to sit in middle-class Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We liked the shop at the front of the restaurant. Very quirky. I wasn't that enamored with the Khao soi. I have never eaten it before and didn't know to add the egg, onion and chilli on the side plate. It may have added to the taste. Really liked the spicy chicken and the pork. The staff were helpful. I guess you try it once.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went here for lunch a few times, food was always good and cheap especially the Khai Soi, the menu is pretty big and the prices are good. It seems a favorite with locals and tourists, when they first arrive in Chiang Mai, its a great place to start you Thai food adventure and there is a great Artist's Studio behind the restaurant which is great to visit before or after your lunch, no doubt you will find better Khao Soi but you will probably want to visit at least once due to its reputation, regardless of whether it deserves it, the service is slow and you might order and then have to chase the waiter for your iced tea etc

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great local spot with delicious khao soi!!! Highly recommend for anyone visiting and looking for authentic northern thai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was great, Restaurant had a good ambience Service left something to be desired Hygiene (at least outdoor seating) was horrendous - rodents running past you on the flower beds and under the floor boards

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food is for tourists only. It's not authentic Thai food. I ordered Kang Hang Lay (แกงฮังเล) but I got Kang Kaduk Moo (Pork Rib Curry) แกงกระดูกหมู

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was basic at most. While the dishes were okay, nothing stood out. It was our first stop for Northern Thai food and unfortunately was a dismay. Skippable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is really two restaurants, one at the corner, open in the daytime, and the other about 20 meters along, open for dinner only. The lunch shop is comfortable and attractive, and pretty quiet. The dinner side is like an antique shop, definitely a dining experience. The lunch shop has very good khao soi, Thai-style salads, and other Northern Thai dishes. You'll need to announce yourself to get pointed to a seat, but otherwise service is swift but impersonable. Prices are a little higher than the standard food stalls, but not crazy, maybe 10 or 20 baht higher. It's one of my standard lunch stops. I haven't been in the dinner room for a while, so won't review it here, but past experiences have been good. The lunch side was always packed before the pandemic, but does not seem as popular since then. I'm not sure why, but the food seems the same and I can choose my table now. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Fuyez. It may have been good, but expensive and with pitiful service. When we saw the waiters clear the other tables, it cut our appetite and we couldn’t finish our dishes. The decor is original, but it does not excuse everything.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We come here when our favorite Khao Soi places are closed!! They have decent KS and we are thankful that they can serve us our favorite food, EVER. the cold beer is wonderful , as well.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a really good, independent, local Northern Thai restaurant... think light curries etc.. and by international standards very modestly priced. The interior is quirky and cool, the staff laid back and the food is tasty..

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A tourist trap to escape absolutely, especially in a city like Chiang Mai that offers an incredible variety of excellent alternatives. The portions are ridiculously small to the point that I first thought we were bringing a tray of condiments. The food is pretty much anything. The service is so cold - like the dishes served elsewhere - that it becomes unpleasant. The room we were in looked like an aquarium for white tourists with an air-conditioning thrust to the point that we were cold. And all for an unacceptable Award compared to other restaurants in the city. To flee!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Overall this is a poor place resting on its reputation. No english from waiting staff made it difficult to order. Tom yam soup was thin and sweet. Mince pork salad was decent but incredibly spicy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you didn't know where the restaurant was, you would walk right by. The only tell tail is one menu out the front. Otherwise, you would think you were entering an antique / collectables shop. The food was traditional North Thai, very fresh and yummy and probably the best value in the area. This is a must if you are in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Characteristic place, nice atmosphere, it looks like an antique shop, the positive things end here. Waiters absent although present in number, questionable cleanliness of cutlery, placemats and plates. Insufficient fans. Edible food, we still had some doubts about its freshness!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I won't be able to talk about the food since after 15 m´ of waiting, we went to find a waiter. This one, not knowing how to give us some basic information on the dishes, went looking for a colleague... who never came. So we left this restaurant without any welcome.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Hard to find, looked closed from outside, looked as an antique shop from the inside, but then we noticed the dining area in the back, in between the antiques. Interesting menu with a lot of northern, local specialties. We chose a variety of dishes to share and combine, including khao soi, laab moo, local sausage and morning glory. All very good. No wine, but good beers. Service can be busy and messy, but just ask for their attention.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Mediocre food terrible service. First place in Thailand where people weren’t friendly. Had to go and find a waiter. Obviously a huge inconvenience for him. Food came out immediately, so you know it was prepared in advance and simply kept warm. Fun ambience with all the Knick knacks around. Sorry we wasted a meal here. One more thing, I was surprised at the number of people who came in and left without eating. Pretty sure the staff rudeness turned them away.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Service very poor. 30 minutes at the entrance without acknowledgment then 40 minutes before order was taken. การบริการแย่มาก 30 นาทีที่ทางเข้าโดยไม่ตอบรับ และ 40 นาทีก่อนรับออเดอร์

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice restaurant with great Khao Soi and soooo delishous crispy pork chips! It’s really a worth visit place during a stay in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice place with interesting interior design. One of the best Khao soi we found. Nice service. Recommended! Good price.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were looking for a place to meet up with some friends and several of our initial options were closed on Saturdays. Interesting side note: when I asked a local why a restaurant would be closed on what is traditionally the biggest night of the week (at least in North America), the answer was a simple “they probably have other things they want to do”. Kinda zen, eh? So back to this restaurant. It’s not on the main road and looks a bit shabby from the outside. When you walk in, it’s got a curio shop feel to it with a narrow hall stuffed with vintage wooden Knick knacks. Even though we had a reservation, they seemed unsure about it and simply directed us to an empty table in a little alcove. We ordered beers and Khao Soi, and it was served up quickly, and was delicious. Possibly the best we had in Chiang Mai. But man, service was spotty, veering on nonexistent. We couldn’t get a second round for love nor money. And when the bill came it was more than reasonable, but you can’t pay with credit for less than 1,000 baht. Note: it would’ve topped that total had we been able to order another round. To summarize: location is not great, ambiance and food are amazing, service not so much. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Half of the dishes did not arrive and then we were billed for the whole menu. After negotiation they realised the dishes had not been delivered. No apology about the missed dishes. We were actually grateful they hadn't delivered all the food as the ones we had were not great. The chicken salad was not actual chicken, more like a strange white processed ham (maybe traditional, I am not sure) but it did not taste good. There was a chilli and mince paste which was served with vegetables which was okay but not the best thing I've eaten. The only dish which was nice was the pork soup (curry), although there was not a lot of meat, it was mostly bone. I'm aware this is a traditional northern Thailand restaurant so all the food was served at room temperature as is traditional, and perhaps the food was just not to my taste. However the service was not great overall either sadly. The environment is nice, so with some improvements to service and possibly more transparency with the menu (pictures would help), this place has potential to be better. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There was a bit of a wait (we arrived a little before 6:30pm and the line grew much bigger by 7pm) but it was worth it. The food here was incredible, we ordered the pad thai, khao soi, pork curry and chicken larb. Each dish had a ton of flavor, but the khao soi was our favorite. We love this dish and this restaurant did it justice. The staff does not really speak English and it took awhile to get our food, but the prices are reasonable and portions are as expected. The interior is nicely decorated and was probably one of the coolest in terms of ambiance that we went to in Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant is very characteristic for its Thai decor and we also ate well, Pad Thai noodles and fried vegetables (that's what it was written but they were boiled, still good). You can't pay with credit cards but the prices are very good.
Date_Written: but they were boiled, still good). You can't pay with credit cards but the prices are very good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is located behind WAT CHEDI LUANG if you exit at the back entrance. very good Northern Thai cuisine. very cheap but long queue frequented mainly by tourists. We waited 25 mins. food served very quickly upon our decisive and speedy order. I read past reviews highlighting food served (cold) not warm. Technically, Thai Northern cuisine is served at room temperature as food is already prepared. We enjoyed this home cooking Northern cuisine. It simply taste good. for those expecting hot and cook to order food, please avoid this restaurant. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was recommend by a Thai friend to come here for traditional food. I can say their Khao Soi is really good, and once I ordered my food they brought the soup pretty quickly. Sadly, I also ordered a bottle of water and a dessert, but I never got them, even when I asked for them two more times over my dinner. Since they were taking forever to bring me the rest of my order, I decided to stand up and pay just for the Khao Soi. When they gave me the check, they were charging me for the whole order even though I didn’t get half of it. Overall, I can say that since their Khao Soi is really good, if you’re willing to wait more than usual, then give it a go. Also, I’m guessing this experience happened to me because I went solo, so they probably will pay more attention to larger groups. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very disappointing. Made us wait a long time before we could order. While it wasn't busy at all. Long wait again after ordering. Apparently they had forgotten about us. Finally one bowl was brought. Before the rest arrived, the first bowl was cold. Only much later was a plate and cutlery brought. The final order took a very long time and was also wrong. The quality of the dishes was below the standard that is common here in Thailand. In short, the place looks nice but the experience was disappointing.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by our guide for authentic Khao Soi a short walk from our last stop at Wat Phra Singh temple. Food was okay but I wouldn’t go out of my way to eat here. Didn’t like the bamboo shoot salad. Prices are reasonable though. Staff don’t speak much English but menu has English translations.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Four of us went to Huen P. on the recommendation of our accommodation. The interior is more reminiscent of a Thai flea market than a restaurant. Well, the food itself didn't convince us. The selection of dishes is rather meat-heavy. My daughter's fried rice with vegetables was rather dry and one of the worse we've ever had in Thailand. The portions were unusually small and despite several sidekicks we weren't full, even though we're not big eaters. We're in Chiang Mai for another week, but we won't be eating there again.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited this restaurant with a local friend we met in Chiang Mai in December. The food/drinks are very affordable and the Khao Soi was truly an authentic dish. We enjoyed our meal here, but would recommend visiting with someone local, as in our case many of the waiters didn’t speak or understood minimal English.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: EXTREMELY DISAPPOINTED We were recommended this place and came a few days ago.. it was ok - very average and thought we would try it again Our take is the parents have worked exceptionally hard and have stepped aside and thier kids run it. We ordered Khao Soi... it was more than twice the price of other places . My chicken wasn't cooked. We asked for sticky glutinous rice didn't get it. The Morning Glory was overcooked bitter & inedible .. we ordered crispy pork rib and again awful... I think it was once amazing.. now badly run by kids who don't want to be there.. its quite dirty.. stained table cloths... and the friendliest thing thier was thier cat.. Really sad as the place has such a great ambience...but thats all.. Chiang Mai has some really amazing food almost on every corner... run by friendly passionate people.. but not here.. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: No frills diner serving good food at cheap prices. Service was quick and cheerful. like a lot of Khao soi restaurants not open after 4pm so go for lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After reading reviews and recommendations that this restaurant had delicious food (apparently the best Khao Soi) and a cute atmosphere we decided to try it. It was lunch hour and maybe 5 tables were full but a large group came in after us. We sat at the table for over 10 minutes, I tried to call over an employee and he deliberately ignored me. The restaurant was chaotic and almost had a cafeteria feel to it (not the cute antique vibes as advertised) we weren’t sure whether we were supposed to order at the table or counter. Just as we were about to leave because we still hadn’t been acknowledged (15 minutes) someone came over and took our order. The food came out quickly enough but was not hot. The Khao Soi was mostly crispy bits and very little broth, noodle or meat, flavour was mild curry, nothing special (First picture is from Huen Phen - the second is from another local spot that we thought was 100 times better). When it was time to leave no one gave us the bill or acknowledged us again, so we eventually got to a counter and gave the table number where they had our cost of bill waiting. It appears as though local patrons have a much nicer experience with quicker service and more effort. Although there may have been a language barrier I have not experienced service this poor at any other restaurant across Thailand (Bangkok, Sukhothai, Chiang Mai). As a foreigner, I wouldn’t recommend eating here unless you want to be disappointed and frustrated. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We found this place through lonely planet book. Interior looked great. However, staff very unattentive, did not speak to us at all. Compared to any other thai restaurant we have been the portions were small and the price was above average. Would not recommend.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had a lovely dinner here last night. The restaurant itself is amazing (it’s almost like you are eating in an antique shop!) Food was great - we had the menu for two which provided us with dishes we wouldn’t have chosen for ourselves so was nice to try. It’s a bit of an experience which I would recommended!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Happy bowl of Khao Soi in this no nonsense restaurant. Had many inviting dishes on the counter that I will come back for!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Worst experience ever. I was looking for a restaurant and saw there a lot of people inside so I figured it must be a good spot—wrong. The reason there were so many people is because they were all waiting for their food. Once I finally got a table I waited 10 minutes before I had to call a waiter over to order. Then after waiting 30 minutes for my food I asked how much longer it was going to be and the waiter came back with a check for my water saying they didn’t know and that there were tables in front of me. Walk down the street a few blocks to See You Soon Cafe—so many options of smoothies, coffee, and food. Staff is so polite and staff is excellent. Huen Phen should take note.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The service was OK, they did not go out of their way to make it soecial. The pork snd ginger had amazing flavors however the meat was cold and there was a lot more fat and bones than meat The sausage was vey good and very spicy

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Super raccomanded! The place is tipical and very suggestive inside! The food is very good, the wait staff is young and someone speaks english. The food is quite spicy but they prepare less spicy plates if you ask.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Heard many good reviews and decided to try. Nice atmosphere but bad service. Very slow service and staff doesn’t seem to bother serving us. Not worth the visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Khao Soi was was amazing. Broth is rich, and the chicken is not over cooked. I would highly recommend this restaurant. We had a few smaller dishes as well and they were very good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went to dinner last night with a friend... If the setting is very nice and the food correct, the service is very slow, disorganized and the staff not especially friendly. As for the presentation, it's rather ugly and we have the impression to be in a canteen! In short, we can go there for the atmosphere but certainly not for the service which is very slow and not for the beauty of the dishes either!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had dinner here on a very slow night, with three other tables occupied. The ambience is amazing in the formal dining portion of the restaurant -- lots of antiques and knickknacks in semi-private rooms. Five stars so far. Unfortunately, the food was not as good as I hoped. We got the Northern Thai dinner set with kaeng hung leh, nam prik ong, nam prik noom, Northern-style larb, sai oua sausage, a vegetable and pork soup funky with fermented fish paste, and bananas in coconut milk. The kaeng hung leh didn't have any pork belly and it was more brothy than I prefer, but the flavor was great. The nam prik chili dips were both great, as was the soup. The sai oua was too mild for my taste. The bananas and coconut milk were nice to finish with. Inexcusably, though, the sticky rice that accompanied the meal clearly was left over and re-heated. You could tell because the grains of rice had become a gummy mass of mush. Very unappetizing. Perhaps most foreigners couldn't tell the difference, but I buy 50 lb bags of sticky rice back home and I would be ashamed to serve what Huen Phen served to my family. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was recommended on various websites for Northern Thai food, and was about a 10-minute walk from my hotel, so I decided to give it a try. I went back to the restaurant 2 times after my first visit, not because the food was particularly good, but because it was convenient and appeared to offer the most authentic Northern Thai experience in the Old Town. The other restaurants in the area offered the usual Thai fare like Pad Thai, which you can find in every city and town throughout the country. The restaurant is not air-conditioned and can feel very stuffy, even with the fans on, so be warned. Food-wise, the young jackfruit salad was delicious. A chicken dish I ordered was too salty, and so was a Northern Thai vegetable soup (though I am not sure if it was meant to be that way). On my second visit, I ordered a simple omelette and vegetables, which you can't really go wrong with. On my third visit, I had a Khao Soi, which was not served piping hot, just lukewarm. Prices are very reasonable but I'm just not quite sure if Northern Thai food is meant to be so heavy on salt and sodium. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was mediocre. One of the items was a soup and it arrived cold. I returned it so the brought it back luke warm. No apologies or concern. We literally asked for our bill, payed it, and left the majority of the uneaten food there. Walked a few blocks to Kats place and had one of best meals of out trip.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Taste great, portion was large. Waiter was attentive. Service was really quick. Affordable price. The restaurant itself is located in the central of old city. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a beautiful restaurant - the rooms are decorated with a dizzying array of art, carvings and artefacts and the tables cleverly distributed so that wherever you sit it feels private. They serve Northern Thai food of a very high standard. We particularly enjoyed the Khao Soi and their sausages. The staff were friendly and attentive and knew their way around the menu. We enjoyed our meal so much that we ate there on two consecutive nights in order to try more of the dishes on the menu. Top notch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A restaurant with a lot of ambiance and atmosphere. Specializing on Northern Thai food and we left happy in regards to the quality and taste of the food. Service can be improved upon.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Worth to go for lunch for one of the Best Khao Sois in Thailand - The other food is very Thai style and also good but I would just have a second Bowl of the Khao soi if still hungry lol. Recommended for a nice lunch if you in the area

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great food and very good prices. Very pleasant staff. Basic place, mostly semi-open, but does have an aircon room, but not needed. Had a fantastic lunch here, the famous Kanom Jeen Nam Ngiaw and equally famous Khao Soi. The Khao Soi was the second best I had in Chiang Mai (number one was at the Shangri-La), but was amazing value for money. Two mains and a large Chang beer, 220 THB. I would come back here in a heartbeat.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Traditional Northern Thai food in authentic surroundings. Enjoyed both the set meal for two and looking at all the old Thai ornaments on display.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My wife and I have dined in here many times, and will be back as soon as possible for more Northern Thai food. The Chicken Khao Soi is delicious, and the Northern Pork Curry, spicy and delicious, although some time fatty.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Avoid this place - the waiter scoffed at our tip and spoiled our experience. He has obviously been spoiled by tourists who pour in. If you want good Northern Thai cuisine go to Tong in the Nimman area (soi 13) where the service is professional, quality is excellent and Thais also love the place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The back room smelled like roaches. The bathroom dirty and improper ventilation coming from the kitchen. Beautiful antiques and rooms. The fried egg 70 baht tasted like charcoal. They didnt wash their pans because they think it will add taste. The pork was decent. Only had one kind of somtum thai with no dried shrimp. The stir fried vegtables was okay but served in plain bowl found at a cheap place. The taste was mediocre and not worth it. I did enjoy the antiques and quietness. The staff are awkwardly standing around. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are used to regular Thai foods and tastes, you will be surprised by how different northern Thai foods are. Folks that give negative reviews of Huen Phen have no perception of what northern Thai foods should taste like. It is different from Thai foods in Bangkok and even the southern islands. The taste and presentation is unique. That’s what makes the foods of Thailand distinct. If you’re looking for the taste of northern cooking, Huen Phen is one of the places to come. Even the billboard in front is written in Thai not English. 70% locals/30% tourists.
Date_Written: in Thai not English. 70% locals/30% tourists.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious Northern Thai cuisine restaurant. I've tried the mince pork Northern style and the pork soup Burmese style and both were delicious.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The stir fried vegetables ordered by my wife and the chicken with ginger that I ordered shared the same taste of something wrong. We couldn’t find what was this taste coming from but we could not finish our plates. So, the food was not ok. The service was even worse. The staff seems to be freelance people, not interested in what they are paid for, and it seems that the customers are bothering them. I guess the manager was out, or drunk, or dead, or there was no manager at all. This behaviour is unusual in Thailand where the people are usually very dedicated to their job, kind and smiling. This restaurant is a shame and doesn’t deserve to exist is a so wonderful country. Should I add that the chicken with cashew nuts costs 240 thb? A small plate of cashew nuts 220 thb. First time after spending the equivalent if several years in Thailand that I am deeply disappointed by a restaurant. Fortunately, there are many other restaurants around, tasty, cheap and with warm welcome. I just recommend you to forget this one. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
