Detail: Seems this restaurant has passed its heyday. Even once touted by Anthony Bourdain, the food quality & service are clearly not what they once were. Was probably the worst meal we had in Chiang Mai. Over priced... very dirty bathrooms. If you’re looking for Khao Soi there are plenty of better options.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Upon arrival to Chiang Mai, we asked a local about a place where we could taste Northern Thai dishes, and Huen Phen was his suggestion. We decided to have lunch there and had a great meal. The food was spicy, but still enjoyable. I had a Kao Soi which was full of flavor. The staff was friendly, the service was fast. It's not a fancy restaurant, but it is perhaps above the average in the area in terms of hygiene. If you happen to visit Chiang Mai, don't think it twice, this is the place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Small portions for large prices. You can get the same anywhere else for much less. Doesn't live up to the hype. 60 baht for a tiny plate of pork rinds. Not even fresh.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A quaint and rustic restaurant that serves excellent northern Thai food. We ordered the sort of tasting tray with five different dishes and one dessert for two people. It was listed as 'Khantoke set menu for two' in the menu. It was a good introduction to a few of their popular items. It was not quite enough for two so we ordered khao soi and two other items and desserts as well. All in, a good meal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food was stale. The flavors were mediocre. Tourist trap. That being said, if you’re looking for ambiance, this is your place. The kao soi was good. Bad service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was our first dining experience in Chiang Mai. The chicken kai soy was yummy and spicy. The Thai tea was very sweet and had a bit too much ice. The servers seemed like they didn’t want us there. Overall, we wouldn’t return but the food wasn’t terrible

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Monday lunch before our flight back home. Found a queue at 12:15 and we were seated in 15 minutes but had a long wait to get a waiter to take our order. After reminding the guy giving the queue tickets at the door a few times we finally ordered. The locals ordered by filling a menu sheet themselves and got their food much faster. For us, another 20 minutes wait to get our food on the table. Of course they forgot some of the items we ordered and we didn’t chase them. We rushed back to our hotel to check out at 2pm. Not fast food at all considering all the food we ordered is ready cooked. The quality of the food that arrived was delicious. Shame about the disorganised service. If they could manage the business better, Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Had to stop here at a friend’s recommendation to have Kai soy and it was good but not amazing. Beef was full of arteries and fat which was gross so skip that but chicken was very good. Fried chicken was mostly bones and very little chicken and sticky rice was very dry so our experience was mixed. Service was very fast and friendly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Skip it. Our tour guide recommended this place for north Chang Mai cuisine...and from the service to the food, it was a major disappointment. The service is awful - the person upfront can’t tell you how long it will be until you are seated...menus are apparently optional, dishes come out randomly...and nevermind that we were charged to have ice. The food is lackluster, bland, and I have to say that if this place was really on Bourdain’s list of must try, something has clearly gone down hill since. Finally don’t focus on the thick coat of dust that has collected on what seems like flea market explosion meet coffee house vibe.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really enjoyed this place we came here through reading the lonely planet book on Thailand. The food was really cheap and the sticky rice was lovely. Would definitely come here if your looking for a local atmosphere as there was plenty in there!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommend this restaurant by the hotel staff and we found the experience very pleasant. My partner is vegetarian and the restaurant were happy to convert a meat dish into a tofu dish which was very much appreciated

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our guide, Pat told us this is where he takes his family for traditional Lanna food. It is located off a side street near the police station in Old Chiang Mai and Is a great option if you are looking for a place to sample traditional Lanna food. They do not speak much English, but the pictures on the menu work just fine. The place is filled with lots of local crafts, photographs and furniture. A great find.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went twice based on reports and reviews. On both occasions the restaurant was packed and the waiting staff seemed too busy: one had to keep reminding to get things like rice!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You dont go here to be catered to as a tourist. You go here to experience eating regional food like the locals. If you keep that in mind (you wont get treated like some rich white foreigner), this place is GREAT. We picked a chicken curry soup, pork sausage, guava salad, and dessert, and all the plates were delicious. Fast service if you go at lunch time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went here and had 2 salads.. very spicy.. and very tasty.. the Pomelo salad and baby aubergine.. they were delicious.. service was fast.. and I was very impressed..

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One of the best places we tried in Chiang Mai, went for lunch and tried about 6 dishes and drinks for about 350bht which were all delicious, service was good and the waiter suggested a few dishes to try

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had been here sooooo manytimes never have any time to write about this place When you look for local foods fast small portions fast fresh delicious low key fast healthy foods this place is my number choices You can see foods in stand and few local cooks behind the counter foods Papaya salad , northern sausage , sticky rice , hang lay curry beef or rice curry chicken .. all delicious But as I say it is nice cheap decent place no fancy decor but they got next door partly open for dinner all antique collection but the foods during lunch time far more delicious even you do not know the dish just go and look and check it before order them My lovely local canteen !! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went with my wife to eat here because from outside it looks very nice, but in reality it is a bit of a trap. There are like 6 youngster waiters playing around and not caring about the visitors. We had to ask if they wanted to take away our empty plates. The food arrived medium warm. We asked to reheat it. It meant partly that the food was not freshly cooked. Again I understand that one cannot cook all meals instant, but then serve it at least hot. The food itself was tasty but the fact that it was cold served deteriorated the experience, even more because no excuse or offer to get the beers for free for example. I wouldnt therefore recommend it if you want to have a carefree evening out.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Authentic Thai northern food, very popular among locals so prepare to cue up for dinner seats. Variety choice of Thai northern food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Someone recommended the place It was a mistake to go there at night They have two different setting one for lunch and one for dinner It is not clean It is not good Staff is not caring It is expensive Voilà Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My girlfriend and I ordered 4 entrees to split. Ginger pork, minced pork salad, eggplant salad and cabbage soup with beef. honestly the ginger pork was pretty good. The soup however smelled absolutely horrible, extremely fishy with a dirty quality to it. I couldn't eat it but my girlfriend enjoyed it saying "it's just a fish broth" I conceded and powered through some of it thinking "if this is really northern Thai flavors I'm going to have to rethink moving here". Around 2am that night it hit us. Let's just say we are both staying very close to the bathroom. Currently struggling with day two we've had to cancel plans and can still barely stomach anything, even water. I feel taken advantage of anyone could have smelled that will and known it was off. I got suckerd bc of my general unfamiliarity with Lanna flavors. Eat next door. Not this place. Stay away. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had a very tasty Burmese style pork curry and a couple of cold Chang beers in this nicely decorated restaurant. No thrills, just good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Perfect for vegetarians, vegans and meat-lovers; this restaurant can cater to all! Mostly Thai cuisine. We had the Khoa Soy which was fabulous! Full bar and lots of beer choices. Everything was very reasonably priced.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best khao soi we had. The hotel recommend this place after we asked them where to find the best local dish. The place is very basic and we could only spot local people. The smell of the big stews brewing in the open kitchen immediately let’s you know you came to the right place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My partner and I ordered a starter, and a main. The starter arrived with the main. Both were poor. I would give this one a miss unless everywhere else is closed, and you're intoxicated, and have lost a bet.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My husband and I visited. The service was terrible! We waited for everything for extremely long time. It felt like we were invisible...the waiters didn’t even acknowledge us for at least 10 minutes after we walked in. After meal order, meal came out promptly and was put on table, and waiter left, without a word. We only had one plate at table and no utensils to eat food with! They basically ignored us. So it was really stressful and unpleasant. Not what one goes out to dinner for! Horrible service!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Highly recommended by our hotel. Clearly a place catering more for non-locals. Decent tasty food well presented in a comfortable setting. Modest sized portions. Reasonable value for money. The restaurants own adjoining “cafe” area that’s open during the daytime is better overall.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our hotel recommended this place to us and it is close to the hotel. Good varieties and price. Food was ok, tried 4 different salad and they were all ok. Sweet, garlicky and watery. The Khao Soi Beef was pretty good. Staffs are nice. Good enough for a quick lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If interested in Northern Thailand food you might try this cozy place for dinner. The menu is widespread and most typical dishes are at your choice. When it comes to food quality the dishes look nice and the herbs and tastes are well balanced. But, not any dish was spicy, even when marked as such with two chillies on the menu. It would have been much better and more authentic if the waiter will ask, how spicy the guest want to be served. Unfortunate!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We are from Northern part of Malaysia, who quite familiar with Thai Food. In fact, what we knew so far is southern Thai Food, which have more seafood, and spicy-sweet-sour dishes. This trip just become our first Northern Thai Food journey, that more emphasis on poultry. Huen Phen is a must visit place,

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I should have believed the recent bad reviews. The food was the worst I was served in Thailand for years. You could almost tell by it looks when it arrived. The Northern Curry with banana blossoms came lukewarm, everything in there was cooked to nearly mash consistence, the meat hardly identifiable. And of course it tasted awfully. The pomelo salad wasn’t much better. I could not eat any of it, paid 200 THB and left.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to Huen Phen tonight on the recommendation of our tour guide today....what a beautiful tasty meal that was not too spicy. We had the crispy chicken noodle curry and pork Burmese curry and they were both amazing. If you are looking for something away from the normal Thai food give this place a go!!! It is tucked away on a quiet side street on the 3rd floor away from all of the noise of the Chaing Mai streets. Prices are great as well.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Though our hotel’s concierge recommended it to us, it was such a disappointment. We ordered about 8 dishes and none of them was good. And we’re rather easy when it comes to food. You can trust that the overall rating of Trip Advisor is not bad for no reason.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You will not be blown away, but still give it a shot. Known locally for the traditional Northern Thai dish *khao soi*, which I would not say was remarkably good, but it was still pretty good and surely worth trying even though the portion is too small for many people. I ordered 2, but I tend to eat more than many people. Very casual, local family-style seating & setting- nothing fancy here. You can also order other regional/subcultural Northern Thai dishes which is good even if for no other reason than the experience of eating food different from other places. Maybe get some as takeaway for later since you're there. Like many other reviewers, I did get a bit of a sense of the staff being perhaps slightly sub-par, but also keep in mind they don't speak English and they are dealing with a steady stream of customers who are difficult to communicate with because they also don't speak Thai. So definitely drop all unreasonable expectations of being served as if you are in a fancier restaurant that is built on catering to non-Thai-speaking foreigners. This place is not that. Humility & patience are always in good order. Regarding drinks, do keep in mind that what Thais consider "just a little sweet" will be to the Western palate more like "Instant Chronic Diabetes." So make sure you emphasize that you do not want your drinks sweet if that is an issue for you. Although still reasonable, this is not *the* cheapest eats you can find; but again, one or two meals here during your stay in Chiang Mai is warranted for the regional cuisine experience. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food is average. Perhaps unique for tourists but not family friendly due to subtle erotic carvings of man parts throughout.The restaurant it'self is not clean very dusty and cuttered. The thai carved cabinets are nice but you cannot see the walls. As a plus side the food came quickly but after eating halfway through the meal we spotted rats 1 meter from out feet. They were well feed and plump so very easy to spot from their size and seemed to enjoy the food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ordered Khao Soi and the fried stuffed bamboo dish. Both were average at best. My sister spotted a creature underneath their cabinet, and I didn't even turn to take a peek. I figured it's best if I didn't see it. This was highly recommended by a local, so I was surprised the food was not that great.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I read that this place was famous for Khao Soi. It was good, but nothing different than I had before. They have a buffet of premade food that they serve from, so the food isn’t hot or fresh. Mediocre at best.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: At lunchtime this a fantastic authentic northern Thai spot. Lots of well prepared and hard to find dishes such as yam som o (pomelo salad), tam kanun (mashed jackfruit salad). Really good. At dinner time it turns into a typical CM tourist trap with limited menu, higher prices and silly touches liked piped Indonesian music.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is a solid choice to get your fix of Northern Thai food, however, the service left something to be desired. We went on a Wednesday for dinner in mid-March, and they were under-staffed. The food was decent, but it took a long time to even get a menu, and this is after I got up to seek out a server. They were simply under-staffed, and I don't know if that's normal, if someone called in, or if they just expected a slow night. But once the food started coming out, it came out quickly. But then it took a very long time to get the bill. Food was good, but certainly not memorable. But the price was right and we got to experience several Northern Thai dishes, so it was a good experience, just not worthy of more than 3.5 stars. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went here on a recommendation because it is northern Thai food, quite specific to the region. The food was good enough, a little bit more expensive than other places, but really it is all inexpensive. But the service is really indifferent. You can get northern Thai cuisine in other places. If you are nearby, go, but I wouldn’t make a special trip.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to this restaurant based on the recommendation in my travel guidebook. We figured it must be pretty good then. The restaurant appeared to be closed because the entire front section was in the dark, but actually everyone was being seated in the smaller, much more crowded space further back. Every inch of space is filled with dusty decorations, it's quite overwhelming. We ordered the large plate with various samples of food, those were good but not great. I couldn't enjoy my food at all (pork with garlic and pepper) because the garlic wasn't peeled properly and the food was full of unpleasant bits of the garlic skin. That and some very hard bits in the meat, not sure what that was. The server seemed extremely annoyed for some reason, and barely listened to us, always looking away. Not a great experience overall, but not a disaster either. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Have visited this restaurant twice now and both lunches of Khao Soi have been amazing. The broth is rich and flavorful- we’ve had the chicken, pork and beef - outstanding .... can’t pick one over the other as all three have terrific taste... highly recommend this restaurant!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My first and only try of the dish Khao Soy, a Northern Thai staple. Loved the confluence of Asian and Arab flavors. Restaurant is full of bric-a-brac and incense. Wait staff not the best. Food is fine. A landmark establishment with a long history and slightly inflated prices.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ate at this restaurant on the advice of our Tuk Tuk driver. We asked him where would he take his family to eat, a locals restaurant with traditional Northern Thailand dishes. He mentioned Huen Phen and said it’s popular with locals and tourists that stumble upon it. When we went inside it was MUCH larger than it looked from outside. The servers don’t speak the best English (one did) but we can always get by with pointing fingers at menu items and smiling;). They are very attentive and always keeping an eye on your table. We ordered our food and it came out super fast. Everything we ordered was very good, we went mainly with Norther Thai comfort foods that most Thai people eat on a regular basis. From northern Thai sausages to curry noodle dishes and more. The flavours were terrific and very different from one another. I would recommend a visit to this restaurant if you want a taste of the North of Thailand Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place on Ratchamanka road in the old city area of Chang Mai has created a name for food and decor( pm time).They have some northern traditional dishes like baby jackfruit salad, grilled green chilli paste with steamed vegetables,Aeb pla... Of course they have the ever popular Khao Soi . Food is good and cheap and better than many others.surely a must visit place if you want to explore beyond Khao Soi,Chicken in basil leaves, Pork knuckles , Beef in oyster sauce etc... The restaurant in the evening resembles a curio shop. As I gathered from other tourists this itself attracts many of them. I found it too cluttered . There is just this one thing, we had so got used to the friendly Thai nature that we felt that the staff here was little unattentive and uncaring towards the patrons.Just to clarify - We went there for both lunch and dinner so my observation is not an one time affair. Also the board outside the restaurant claims the closing hours to be 22:00 but it closes anytime after 21:15. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We came here after reading the recommendation in lonely planet. The interior decoration of the restaurant is really cool, but the food (we had the 2 person set menu) was underwhelming for the price (which wasn’t that cheap for Thailand), a lot of the dishes were quite cold & didn’t seem like they’d been cooked to order

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went there for lunch: I was excited to try the caramelized pork that I read about, but couldn't find it... Took some sticky rice and a spicy dish instead + a cold beverage: it was nice

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ate here twice for dinner in Feb 2019. The interior is beautiful, full of antiques - a feast for the eyes. The Khan Toke dish with many little bowls of No.Thai dishes was delicious and the Khao Soy was very good as well. Don't miss this unique dining experience - and prices are very reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a great place to have lunch , fast service, good food and a great price.The best is to order a couple of plates and share over a cold glass of beer.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My first real taste of Northern style Thai food. It was good, we got the platter that has a sample of many dishes, first pic. Also two things I wanted to try, pickled pork and pork stuff fried bamboo shoots. It was all good, I only didn't eat one dish that was too spicy for me. Low tolerance! The price isn't expensive if you compared to Bangkok. I recommend the fried bamboo shoots, it tasted to me like artichoke hearts. Yummy! The platter also came with two desserts, banana in coconut milk. Very good! You can order these separate if you don't get the platter.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Go for a late lunch or early dinner to avoid a crowd. It was our first stop in Chiang Mai and favorite meal in Thailand. Must sample the khao soy! Avoid the juices, though - not fresh, tasted artificial.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came bc lonely planet recommended, but was the pits. Ordered the guava juice and koa soi. Grave was water with a grave syrup, tasted horrific! The kao soi was just str8 up oil. I couldn’t believe it. I kept skimming the top layer of oil, but in the end there was barely any broth. I do not understand why it would have a cup of oil in it. And if ur gonna make it that fattening, then it better at least taste good. Clearly, it sucked as well. I wouldn’t recommend this place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Its weird as i always believe good food come with good environment and service that creates a better taste. But seriously, the food is really not bad, its mid range food which easily beats the rest in that area. But staff were thinking we tourist are amusing to not know how to speak to order food, laugh at us for ordering individually as each of us have different taste buds? Overall i just feel like leaving immediately after the finishing the food. Oh and they forgot my order ( just one of the dish ) Overall , north thai food is great, and i realised north thai food are a notch saltier so do take some rice to go with the dishes. Food 4 Atmosphere 1 Price 3 Will i go again: nah... maybe? I dunno... its hard to go to a place where i cant remember positive things other than food. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen pen is a Famous Northern Thai food restaurant in Chiangmai. The foods are good with varieties of menu from Kao-soi, Sai-ua, Nampriknoom, and many other local dishes. But it’s really crowded during lunch time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: the huen phen is definitely uniqe. the food is of good quality but some of it is too much adapted for the tourists. the northern spicyness is missing. and be patient because it takes a while until you get your food on the table. but over all its worth to visite that place and the prices are really fair and the staff friendly

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by a local to us and so glad she did! Service was excellent, food even better, with authentic Thai taste, no disappointment here!! Khao soi was terrific. Cucumber salad and papaya salad were perfect for a hot day!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food deserves five stars. The facility and service bring down the rating. The service was very slow, but once we placed our order our food was ready within minutes. We ordered the ginger chicken and spicy chicken and they were delicious. Cons: We had to wait 5-10 minutes at the front of the restaurant before we were acknowledged; and mosquitoes.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a nicely decorated place for dinner which is known for their Khao Soi. The food is good but doesn't always come out hot when it should have. The service is super slow so don't be in a hurry. I had to hunt down the server each visit for refills and my check. 3 stars instead of 4 because of slow service and cold dish but is worth checking out at least once.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Being a contrarian I thought I'd go there despite recent pans. We left after five minutes, a zombie like atmosphere, although the tables were full none there was zero buzz. Rude staff interaction from stressed out kid who didn't greet us just pointed to a sign-up sheet. Bad vibes. It's my policy to abort mission immediately if a restaurant feels wrong, so can't tell you about he food. Place is located next to hotels catering to cheap package tours which probably explains how a place could be crowded and yet so dismal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Staff were not very friendly, food was served cold. Taste wasn't great, just edible.For the price you pay, you're better off eating elsewhere.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had Kao Soy-soup as starter - the worst I ever had in my life. It tasted indefinable and not fresh a little like eggs. As main course I took the chicken with cashew-nuts and again it tasted indefinable and not fresh. The portion was tiny. The best thing about that dinner was the lemon ice tea - I had two to enhance the experience and wash away the taste. I have never eaten so bad in Thailand and all of that for 370 Baht - just for me as single person. Absolutely not to recommend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was our worst restaurant expérience in Thaïlande. Every street vendor is better! Waiting in line, another party was called before us. Fter waiting in line 20 minutes for lunch, we waited half an jour to order, another half another hour to be served. The food was below standard. What was praised as the best Northern Thai food was horrible. So if you can, avoid this place. It will save you a lot of anger and frustration.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Been looking for a Khao Soy in my trip today. Surprising it’s pretty good. The menu are mostly northern Thai food, which is a good change if you are a traveller that been spending a lot of time in other parts of the region.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is norththern Thai cuisine which is different from the Thai cuisine. Chiang Mai was ruled by Burma for 200yrs therefore the influence in its cooking style. I tried their more popular dishes like the pork curry, veg salad with their chilly dip, a assorted veg curry & stir fried fish with sticky rice & I love all of it. This place can be crowded with both locals & foreigners situated in the old city. There are tuk tuks outside the restaurant if u need transport back but ask the price first If you game to try the taste of northern Thai cuisine, then go for it. I had no regrets. Price is reasonable. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Awesome Khao Soi noodles (it’s the first dish listed on the back of the front page, the only one without a picture). Cool restaurant, though if you follow google maps, and see the closed Heun Phen at the corner, don’t give up. Move one shop front down, and the entrance is there.).

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The decor was very traditional. It's nice, if a bit crowded. Service was slow because it was very busy. Food was below expectations. The Fried Spare Ribs in Huen Phen Style was over-fried and extremely hard. The tomyam soup was lukewarm and lacked flavour. Price was expensive for the disappointing quality

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There's a wide variet of Local food. They have an english menu now - some of the translations is a bit weird but the pictures will help alot. You can see a wide array of the local curries in their glass display as well as their chilli dips. There is the Khao Soi which is the curry egg noodles with a choice of chicken, pork or beef. Not too spicy and a good starting point for not so adventurous taste. The rice noodle nam Geo can be a tad spicy but has the tomato taste which maybe familiar. You can tell them no blood cubes if it scares you. There's also crispy pork, and sticky rice and stir fried vegetables if you want something basic. One of the local dishes is steamed sticky rice with blood. An acquired taste but definitely bragging points! Go for the grilled Naam - Fermented Pork - It's so delicious!!!! Prices range from about 50 baht - 200 baht. Can serve well for people on a budget. I would definitely recommend this place as it's got a wide range of dishes that everyone can enjoy. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: An insider tip among locals, this restaurant for Northern Thai cuisine has become increasingly popular for tourists. And rightly so: An excellent place to get taste of Northern Thai cuisine. Make sure to try the Khao Soi soup – available with fish, chicken, beef or pork – and papaya salad. It's also extremely cheap. We paid 600 THB including drinks for 4 persons for lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The menu is only in Thai and therefore impossible to understand for foreigns. We tried to ask for two "safe" dishes and we received a stew with pork liver and a beef soup containing skin as well. I would give the place another try only with the help of a local explaining me the menu.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen Northern Thai Food was the spot for our first meal as it specialized in Northern Thai cuisine which we were not familiar with. As expected, it was busy but the staff were very efficient getting people seated and getting he orders out. Northern style khaw soi curry soup was their signature dish andit lived up to the high expectations. Northern Thai style sausage and pork curry were also excellent with distinctive flavors. Typical Thai dishes like pad thai and papaya salads were also on the menu and decent.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very busy local place with a big menu. We visited for lunch on New Years Day. Most of the clientele were locals. The place is quite simple. Food was very good. Service was nice as well.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We basically stumbled into this place, usually we are used to eat at the small food stalls at the street, but the crowd who was waiting outside this restaurant made us curious to try it. We wish we wouldn’t have... the food was the worst we’ve had after two weeks of Thailand - and the prices are way to high for that kind of absolutely BAD QUALITY! The salad was almost rotten, the curry dishes without taste, cold and you need to look very close to find a piece of meat inside. Even the sticky rice was cold and I guess they prepare it already in the afternoon, as dry as it was... not fresh at all, not tasty at all, overpriced. Just really don’t go there!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Sinds I had to wait for 50 minutes to get a table on a sunday night, I was hungry. They promise free wifi but I could not find the password. And the system with writing your name down in order to get a table seems only to be working when your Thai. But finally seated I discovered only 2 vegetarian dishes on the menu. My mistake because I could have asked for the menu while waiting. The Thai omelet was nothing special; just a plain omelet. When you like pork it seems a good place to go.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our guide highly recommended this place so we decided to give it a try. There was a line but got a table quickly. We were given the first table beside the entrance. We ordered as soon as we sat since we were hungry. We were looking forward to eating and we saw trays and trays passing by. We followed up our order a couple of times. Server said coming but didn't do anything. We asked the woman who took our order and she said she will check. Maybe twenty or so minutes later, we saw the people sitting beside us, who arrived minutes after us, were all eating.... But no food for us. So we asked about our food again. Someone came and asked what did you order. OMG, they lost our order. We said we ordered from her. She was not a new server. The woman we ordered from is a senior staff who you pay to also. Then we said she took our order. They said sorry. Please order again. The woman we ordered from didn't even apologize. We ordered khao soi. This was really good. The fried chicken was so so. We preferred the one from the night bazaar. The sausage and minced pork were just okay. Nothing to rave about here. After paying the girl we originally ordered from, I said next time please don't forget an order.... And she said nothing!! Won't EVER go back here again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have been to this restaurant more than 10 years ago and it was very good. This time, i tried the branch at the hotel. Disappointing. Taste have been watered down to ‘tourist’ tastes and the portions are small. Will not come again.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were advised to go by one of our guides, and despite the mixed reviews gave it a go. The service was not friendly and a bit slow. We had the appetiser plate which at 220THB was overpriced; a small pork & tomato dip, 2 types of spicy sausage, some bean, celery & cabbage, pork crackling and a boiled egg. That came out at the same time as the main meals, we would have preferred it on it’s own, and as it was advertised as an appetiser thought this would be the case. We had a northern chicken and vegetable curry, we were battling to find chicken, it was a bit incipid & watery, probably the least enjoyable dish we have eaten all week. The curried veggies were lovely with a nice flavour and a little heat. All up with a small beer & water 500THB. The place is nice with eclectic bits and pieces all around, it wasn’t awful, just disappointing and the usual welcoming Thai feel wasn’t there. There was a que by the time we left, I was tempted to say “It’s not worth the wait”, as there are plenty of other places to try! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice restaurant right in the heart of Chiang Mai's Old City. The staff were very friendly and provided excellent service. The food was very good. This became our go to place for lunch during our visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I’ve been here every single time I visit Chiang Mai. It’s not always crowded but if you’re here during the holidays or high season, it can get very out of hand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you enjoy your food with no soul, no depth in all the flavor. This is the place! I know my food and this place is seriously overrated. Enough say.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really bad, cannot imagine anything worse. Just a touristic place where we were treated as sheep; we waited for around 1hour for our food, they didn’t even bring the drinks during that time. They didn’t understand us when we tried to complain, to the point that we were about to leave, when the food finally came. They didn’t bring the silverware a long, so we had to wait again for it to come: in the end then food was cold, and nothing really good compared to any of the other restaurants we have tried in Chiangmai. It was by far our worst experience in a restaurant in Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Overrated. Variety of local food but the chaos is real. Unfriendly atmosphere as the staffs are not smily.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We arrived at 4:55pm and sat in the front end of the restaurant and one waiter brought me a menu. I took a peak and things were very well priced about 60 baht for most dishes and then another waitstaff comes by and says we have move to the back of the restaurant for dinner. He took the menu I received away and hands me the “dinner menu”, which had fewer options and at prices two times the other menu. We decided to stay for dinner anyways because what’s 120 baht for an entree, it’s still affordable. We placed our order and the wait staff brings out some appetizers and forgets utensils for one of the people in our party. It took 15 minutes for him to come back with the set. The rest of the food comes out and I was not impressed. Mediocre for what it is, and we kept waiting for our last dish, and realized the waiter forgot it. The only fast part of this experience was us grabbing the check and paying immediately to get out! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: WE go here for Khao Soi for lunch each time we come to Chiang Mai, never fails to impress us. Very busy, sometimes chaotic, but the Khao Soi and Chang beer are always great. Cant wait to go back!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I don't get the few bad reviews, but this place has amazing Northern Thai food at very fair prices! Do not miss this place on your Chiang Mai visit!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Yesterday evening we decided to give Huen Phen a try. Many of the reviews before were very disappointing. So I did not expect such a good experience we found yesterday actually. We had to wait for 30 min because this is a very popular restaurant. The decoration is like an antiques shop. We had a nice table between all the antiques with many young people. We got the menu card quickly and we found many interesting dishes from northern Thailand. Our ordered dishes were delivered quickly and they were very tasteful. We enjoyed the stay there and I can clearly confirm that this was a great choice for the evening.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It was lukewarm, the fried noodles were not fresh and it was a tiny portion. For 90baht it is such a rip off - if you're gonna charge me that much, at least make it good! This is definitely aimed at tourists and is not worth the money. There was only chicken available apparently even though other reviews said fish and beef existed? I've had amazing khao soi for 40baht before but this is not authentic at all. When I tried complain to the staff they didn't understand and just walked off. If you know good Thai food, or even good food in general, don't go here. I only went here out of desperation with a group of other people and will definitely not be returning. Please, this is the only place I've felt is so not worth it I've posted this review on both here and Google to make sure as many other people who know good food, don't make the same mistake. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our local driver recommended this restaurant so we hopped in for a late lunch. Very surprised to see a full house at 3:30pm! The menu has plenty of photos so we picked a few of our favorite: chicken curry, morning glory veg, salad and desserts. They ran out of white rice so we were served sticky rice with curry. Well, it was good. The final surprise was the bill! We were stuffed and it hardly cost anything. We would have had to pay at least double for the same back home. Good find! Will go again for sure.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had dinner at Huen Phen on our driver's recommendation. Although we are not experts, we enjoyed a wonderful meal. Our group of 5 shared a variety of dishes. The dining space is filled with Thai antiques, making the space a fun atmosphere for enjoying a meal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A simple place with cheap prices and unsophisticated service but offering tasty dishes efficiently served. Quite successful with tourists service yo may have to wait.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You are in North Thailand and you wanna eat what the Thais call “northern food.” Where do you go? Go Huen Phen! Located in the Old City at the middle of Ratchamanka Road, at the junction where Jhaban Road cuts across, this restaurant offers a wide variety of lovingly prepared, tasty dishes. The food is so much a must-try that bus loads of tourists flock over to experience northern Thai cuisine. Thais visiting from other parts of the country also make a stop at Huen Phen. The food is that good and the staff helpful and friendly. We tried a grilled eggplant dish, vegetables with chilli tomato dip, pomelo salad and a slightly spicy soup with pork and bamboo shoots. Seems like fried chicken with sticky rice is also a popular choice, though we didn’t try that. They serve both hot and iced Thai milk tea. Thai milk tea is rarely served hot in Chiang Mai, so for those who wish to try Thai milk tea but can’t take cold stuff, try it in Huen Phen. Drawbacks: 1. The restaurant has a mosquito problem 2. There is a no wifi... not that you need wifi to eat, but this could be a minor inconvenience for some If you’re heading there for lunch you may wish to drop in just before noon. Otherwise you will be hard pressed finding a table as it gets really crowded during mealtimes. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by our hotel and it was a very good experience!!Charmingly local,very good food. We asked the waiter to decide what to eat and we got seven very good dishes. We had several good meals in Chiangmai, but this is the one we certainly will remember!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our hotel recommended this restaurant for authentic Northern Thai food, and I’m glad they did. Everything we’ve ordered has been delicious: Khao Soi, green papaya salad, bbq pork ribs, baby eggplant salad, guava salad, Tom yum gai... Food is cheap for what you get. Worth at least one visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The ambience is very interesting with lots of Thai antiques. We were placed at a table where the cloth looked very dirty, however the placemats covered the worst of it. Drinks and orders were taken and arrived promptly. The omelette, veg and rice were good, but the basil and pork dish was poor. The taste was terrible. We had to wait a long time to get our bill. Disappointing food and service overall.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food is great. A pity we didn’t have a chance to go back and try others on menu. We ordered fried chicken, grilled pork, sticky rice and Tom Yum Kung for a late lunch which was simple yet delicious except for the pork. The fried chicken was the best we had in Chiang Mai and enjoyed it better than famous SP Chicken in town. Veggies in Tom Yum Kung were fresh and there was a lot of shrimp in the soup but felt it has more coconut milk in it than our liking. The next morning, I repeated the place and ordered Khao Soi with beef but they gave me one with Chicken and it was still quite delicious so I decided not to complain. Overall, food was pretty good. Prices are reasonable. The place was clean and the atmosphere was that of a typical SE asian restaurant frequented by both locals and tourists. Would be better if staff are given more trainings for service. Will definitely go back to Huen Phen if I ever visit Chiang Mai again! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Set in a traditional ambiance, the restaurant has a variety of Nouthern delicacies. The mix platter will allow you to try the highlights. The downside.....waiters are not attentive.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is clean, beautifully clean. Environment is clear, ambient is good, food is nice in display. Food is great, service is perfect, available both air con room and open air. Sweet, drink, and ice cream also available. Don't missed this place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our host in Chiang Mai recommended Huen Phen as his #1 choice for authentic northern Thai food! He told us to get the Khao Soi (all we had been eating thus far was Pad Thai which we learned is not the most authentic Thai food, lol). And we were up for trying what Thai people actually eat. So we ventured over here to check it out! We walked in and initially, I was not sure I was going to like it, the smells were SO overwhelming and it was super hot since there's no AC// and lots of things cooking! We sat down and ordered a Chang along with Khao Soi and sticky rice. The food came out very quickly (we assume this is one of their most popular dishes so they probably had it made). This meal did NOT disappoint! We loved how flavorful and different it was! If you make the sticky rice into balls and dip into the curry it is delicious! Despite our initial hesitation, we were so glad we tried this place. 10/10 would recommend to a friend or stranger! It is also very inexpensive! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We stopped in after reading this place had one of the best Khao Soi dishes in town. We left hungry and shaking our heads. Everything we ordered was bad and obviously pre-cooked. The Khao Soi was short on noodles and tasted like chili powder. The chicken with ginger was almost entirely made of onions -- there was no ginger flavor and next to no chicken. And the pork with lemongrass had no lemongrass flavor. This was the worst meal we have had in Thailand ever.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My husband and I visited Huen Phen with our two children for dinner as we had heard it was a great destination for Northern Thai food. It didn’t disappoint. Our waitress was lovely and helpful and based on a couple of her suggestions we ordered the Northern Thai Pork Curry and the Khao Soi with chicken. We also ordered stuff fried mixed vegetables and fried chicken. Everything was delicious, beautifully balanced and fresh. We paid 380 baht for all of the food we ordered. Whilst not super cheap we thought it offered good value for money. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Visited after reading tripadvisor reviews and was not disappointed. The restaurant is busy during lunch hour, be sure to pick a table close to a fan if visiting during warmer weather. The restaurant serves northern Thai dishes. I tried the northern Thai meat platter with a tomato based dip which was not spicy and great for sharing. Also tried a sweeter thick soup egg noodle and the Huen Phen salad which was nice and light. The drink selection is awesome with local flavours as well as a great variety of coffee and smoothie. A great place to get with the local scene when in Chiangmai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First things first, only go to Huen Phen during lunch service. Dinner provides a quirky setting, but service is slow, cuisine average and prices high. So, on to lunch! Do not miss these two items. 1. Fried Papyaya Salad 80b 2. Fermented Pork Spare Ribs 40b + Stickyrice I suggest sharing these two dishes and ordering a bit of stickyrice, then moving on to sample other offerings throughout the city. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went there for lunch and the meal was honestly not up to expectation. Much of the food seems to be pre-cooked for lunch so not sure if the dinner experience would be better. We felt that the Khao Soi was on the salty side and dishes that we ordered were not memorable. Not sure if this is because we are not used to the local taste but definitely won't go there again for lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I never went there for 5 years,i just went there last week and ordered native food. Taste changed from previous ,just simple not outstanding.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was recommended this place for it's Khao Soi and unfortunately they didn't serve at dinner time when I went. The staff weren't necessarily rude but it took us a while to get their attention for ordering and paying. The atmosphere was fairly average and while the food good, it wasn't good enough for the price. Worth a try but there are definitely better places to eat in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I haven’t been to Chiangmai that often, but once our driver took us to this restaurant, which looks rather old-fashioned, I recognized that years ago some locals we knew took us here once. I wasn’t that impressed with the food then (but I blamed the food choices) but I remember that they told me this is one of the best places to eat in Chiangmai. It has been years since our last visit to Chiangmai and this visit. We asked our driver to recommend a place we should eat before heading back to BKK and he, a very local Chiangmaian, took us here. We have chosen a variety of food this time and made sure we did pick good ones. My family and I actually enjoyed it very much. What I like about this place, in comparison to others, is that besides being delicious, it also tastes local. I’d recommend this place if you want something local and ordinarily delicious. The food there may not ‘wow’ you like some food in fine restaurants do, but I still think it’s worth at least a visit. I’ll go there again next time. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There are varieties of local food served here. Reasonable price, good taste and rather delicious. Clean rest room. Open rather morning time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Having first eaten here 10 years ago I was so impressed my return journey was amazing food simple but quality has not changed since then and I recomend you go all out if group and order a few things... Cow soi still a hot hit

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I'd read many positive reviews about this restaurant in Chiang Mai and finally I decided to try its typical nothern kitchen. There were a lot of people for lunch, I waited for a long queue and when was my turn, they gave me a table close to the toilet. When first dishe arrived I couldn't smell it for toilet fragrance! I asked for an other table and they changed it to me, but new table was dirty. In the meantime everything I've ordered arrived, so I decided to eat in that table. I ate khao soi with pork, deep fried bamboo with chicken and grilled pork: they were good, but not epic or unforgettable! Many dishes were ready yet (you can see them when you enter in the restaurant). The service was hasty, inattentive! Waiters run all the time...probably because of the quantity of clients..but I didn't feel neither thai hospitality nor the delight travel of flavors in this restaurant! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We dined here 2 nights in a row as the food was very much to our liking. Could not really appreciate the truly traditional dishes like young jackfruit salad as it was not what we expected.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Firstly, we arrived around 7pm and there was a 6 group queue for tables, where you were left to sit outside the restaurant. This wasn't the end of the world as the wait wasn't too long, but it would have been nice to have been offered drinks The restaurant itself was very nicely decorated with a very authentic feel to it. It's clear a popular restaurant with tourists but still offers some authentic northern Thai food that a lot of people will not have come across before. We went for the set menu for 2, which was good although wasn't that big (we ordered an extra dish and I actually got a mcdonalds burger on my way home too). I wasn't blown away by the experience, but i would visit again. Price is reasonable Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It’s a tough call leaving relatively negative reviews for businesses but our experience was truly average and we ended up leaving food because it was so bland and truly below average. It’s cheap but then that’s no excuse. Some of the best home style or street style food in Thailand is really cheap and excellent. Service was OK, the ambience was tacky and lacking and as I’ve said already the food was below average. Lacking in attention to detail and lacking flavour. I cannot recommend it I’m afraid. So many better places to eat in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: What's wrong with Thai restaurateurs underestimating foreigner visitors and compromising Thai food, one of the world most amazing cuisine?? Money of course, and in CASH ONLY PLEASE! Again, Tripadvisor comments seemed to play in favor of this establishment. We were very much looking forward to enjoying our favorite cuisine, alas! - No Thai guests (the usual first sign of a not so much local eatery) - Soundtrack of the evening: all hits of the 80's, in jazzy version please. - Decorum? A mix and match of Dali, vintage ads, Chinese signs and stuff, a loooot of stuff. - Service: indifferent, non communicating. Our waiter spotted a dead cockroach on the floor, didn't dare picking it up and left it to us, as a dining companion. - Food? The same westernized Thai food you'll get in any Thai restaurant abroad, poor in coconut, double amount of sugar, watery tom yam goong, bland larb gai, a fish dish with nearly no fish in it, no fresh coconut water (probably due to the higher cost compared to moving cola and beer by the bottle). - Damage: not much but in cash only. No debit card, no credit card, no Alipay, no WeChat. They compromise on the food but not on the payment. Not usually carrying much cash my girlfriend had to walk 20mn away to the closest ATM and back, in submission to their ridiculous cash only rule. It's 2018, your business model consists in tourists-milking and patrons have to sweat to comply to a fishy policy?? There are hundreds better restaurants in Chiang Mai, don't trust the trend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is very nicely furnished with a lot of attention on details. It’s a lovely place that lets one feel a bit like being in a living room. Unfortunately, the roof isn’t proof, so we got some drops of rain. The food is traditional Northern Thai cuisine. One may like it or, as in my case, not so much, so tourists shouldn’t expect dishes that they are familiar with when they go to Thai restaurants in their own countries. The staff was very nice and quickly responding to our requests. They speak limited English, but since the menu is bilingual, it’s no problem to order. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: So cluttered which in a restaurant environment just makes me think, how do they keep it clean. My place mat felt sticky and the plates and cutlery needed to be rubbed with a napkin. Teenage boys gave stand offish nervous service. Food average at best. Just don't understand why anyone would go.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I stopped in for a late lunch as I was walking around town. This place has a bunch of local dishes that all looked excellent. I got the Khao Soi and a coconut smoothie. Highly recommend!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went in for a lawyer dinner and had excellent service! The food was delicious and the heat ratings are accurate

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is one of the places you find mostly local faces - a very good thing when you want to find the best food - true northern Thai cuisine is made and served here in a very picturesque setting, almost a courtyard like maze you go through to find a table. I have never food-smiled as much as during our meal here.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First place I ate after landing in Chiang Mai as it was recommended by a local. Lots of options here. I really enjoyed the spicy fish and chicken curry. I would definitely recommend this spot to anyone in the area. Be sure to also try the Thai tea with milk.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I visited Huen Phen for my first dinner in Chiang Mai and thoroughly enjoyed it. We sat in their dining room that was full of interesting items that made me feel like I was in someone's living room. Great cashew chicken, fried chicken, and pork with fried noodles. Don't miss this place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My husband and I went to Huen Pen for dinner. It was recommended online and by the concierge at our hotel as a place to try northern Thai food. We ordered the chicken wings, northern Thai curry, jackfruit salad, and fried rice with shrimp. We also had two Chang beers. Total for meal was around $18-not bad for all of the food. It took about 15-20 minutes for someone to come over and take our drink order and then several more minutes to get them. The restaurant was crowded and several of the staff were just sitting around on their phones. Food was decent but you could find better northern Thai somewhere else. Restaurant is small but does have a Thai feel. We would not visit this restaurant again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went there for Khao Soi, based on the recommendation of a local and I will say that it was the best we had during my 5 days in Chiang Mai. We tried several other places but they have the most flavoursome broth here and it was just great! We did however, try the deep fried pork ribs and were somewhat disappointed with is as it wasn't as tender as we expected. All in all, must try the Khao Soi here! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant does not a huge entrance or a very attention grabbing signage. It was a simple and humble green signange which is very easy to miss. I passed by the signage and the restaurant entrance a few times whilst touring the old city without knowing. I liked the decor of the restaurant and also the crockeries used. It makes me feel that I am in Thailand, northern Thailand specifically. Its decor and feel is congruent and flows seamlessly from the old city streets. The food here did not disappoint as well. It was a truly authentic northern thai cuisine that we were treated to (or at least it was not the usual thai fare we were used to in Bangkok). The "tom yum" soup equivalent tasted refreshingly different and I really loved the shrimp salad that was served. The fried shrimp was crunchy and flavoured with a mix of herbs that just stands out when eaten with the leafy green sides. The "tom yum" is not as powerful as the southern equivalent, and has a "calmer" taste to it. Not as much oomph but still unique in its own way (more "herbal" taste I feel). The (crunchy) red ruby was equally good too, a good sweet end to seal my palate for the night (or rather time being). Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very normal street food, can't complain with low prices, but this is overrated. However, the atmosphere in the restaurant was good, however very hot with no air con!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was a little place well located with delicious food. The ambience was spoilt by the service staff hanging out in the dining area. The place is quite small so a wait staff member practicing his kick boxing didn't help. The food was mid range in price and had dishes local to the region.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I go there for lunch with my Thai wife and the restaurant was recommended by local tour guide. This is our four trip to Chiangmai and we wanna sample again local Food. The soup was cold and appear not very fresh and the other dishes were below average as well. You can get better quality food from food stands in Yaowarat area for less price. On the top of it, waiters stuff is kind of not super friendly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Fair warning: They do not sell khao soi during dinner time! I should have left and return during lunch time but wanted to give this place a try. We order fried chicken, fried rice special, chicken ginger dish, and Thai tea. More expensive than other restaurants. The fried chicken came out first as our appetizers, no utensils given and hardly any paper napkins. I watched other tables repeatedly ask for things that should have been refilled before dinner service. I was looking forward to the fried chicken but was disappointed. It was reheated old fried chicken, what a bummer. The other dishes were not great as well. We left and found a hole in the wall to get mango sticky rice where the food smelled amazing and each dish was 50 baht. We definitely wasted our time going to this restaurant! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We loved the environment of the restaurant! The restaurant was beautifully decorated. Thai iced tea and fried chicken was awesome! Omelette was our favourite! :)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is a long established restaurant in Ratchamanka Rd in the center of the Old City of Chiang Mai. Founded by Mrs. Phen in the last century, it is still being run by the same family. It is overlooked by the Great Chedi of Wat Chedi Luang and could not be more athmospheric. If you can, go to the older part of the restaurant where there is a series of rooms decorated with interesting historical artefacts and Antiques. Huen Phen has built up its reputation for excellent Northern Thai/Lanna cookery so these are the dishes to try. Prices are reasonable and serving sizes are not too big so you can try a few different things. There is a sort of tasting menu for two people which is good value and allows you to try the signature dishes of the area. If you want an authentic dining experience in pleasant surroundings with friendly service, this is the place to go Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really amazing Kow Soi with fresh hand made egg noodles, We also had the papaya salad It was good portion with great taste Wife had the fried chicken which was crispy And only wanted more Super nice older lady took our order and made us feel So welcome there They also sell bags of the noodles if you want to take home We all hope we get to eat here again Prices are best in Chang Mai Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Had chicken Khao soi and Thai iced tea. All was very good. It's an open area restaurant, so expect flies.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This establishment is open early for breakfast, and 1 of the better khao soi (local curry noodles) that come in small servings so that you have allowances to try other dishes. Open kitchen with the noodles made/prepared openly, so if you don't speak the local language, all you have to do is point at the ingredients you want.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We asked our guide to take us to a place where we could sample local dishes, and he took us to Huen Phen. The food was outstanding! The atmosphere is very casual and bustling - often with a wait.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The service at this restaurant is simply appalling. You’re supposed to wait to be seated, but no one greets you, and then they just throw menus on a table which hasn’t been cleaned. The waiters sit with their backs to you and play on their cell phones; we had to get out of our seats and go up to the counter anytime we needed something. But the eclectic atmosphere (a wooden house filled with all sorts of knick-knacks) is enjoyable, and the Northern Thai dishes are quite good. For our money, we prefer the sister restaurant at the top of Baan Huen Phen hotel, across the street and further down the block.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The northern Thai food here at Huen Phen is slightly different from other places in Chiang Mai. The Tom Yum soup and curry were good. The only drawback was that the place was like a jungle with lots of trees inside and I was constantly stung by mosquitoes during the meal. Kindly spray some mosquito repellent on you before going there.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant's decor is worth a visit in itself - antiques and art everwhere. But one goes here for the food. It is actually 2 restaurants back to back - one serves lunch and one dinner. Both are very modestly priced - you won't go over budget at either one. The food is delicious and then dinner menu is extensive - a good place to try authentic Northern Thai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went for dinner, and the prices were very reasonable. We had about 5 dishes to share between 2 of us plus drinks and it only came to about 600 Baht! Great way to experience some Northern Thai dishes which is quite different to what you'd get in Bangkok

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food sold here is a good introduction to the region's culinary, it tastes good. The place has been provinding this kind of service for 40 years, so is quite authentic, a friendly service but not fake or over the top. The food portions are enough for europeans and asian standards (maybe not for Northern Americans) ideally you should order 2 or 3 plates per person (Asian style). We observed that they don't clear the plates until you ask to pay, so they can account your bill from seeing your plates, not looking at the previous ticket order (it has probably been their technic for years, it was not a problem for us). Good for travellers and explorers, but not for people looking for luxury or personal service. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: For some particular reason, this restaurant is visited by a lot of North Americans. Who then complain about portion sizes. Yes, portions are Thai sized. But the food is good. Not exquisite, just good. Nice way to get to know northern Thailand’s cuisine. We had the special plate for two persons, can’t remember the name, sorry. Which included a variety of northern dishes. And the restaurants famous green chilli paste. With 380 baht, it’s cheap. But not the best eating we’ve down in Chiang Mai so far. The interior is quite quirky. And for all those reasons, give this place a chance. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good food served by friendly helpful staff. Visited for a late Thai breakfast and was not disappointed. Good food (Larb Moo & northern curry with sticky rice - please excuse any spelling mistakes!). Will definitely eat there again on my next visit to Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is confusing because it's open on one side for lunch and the other side for dinner. It's really two different restaurants in one I think. We first came for lunch on a week day when it wasn't too busy and had the BEST experience. It was quiet, the service was good, the beer was very cold. The price is reasonable as well. All 4 of us got the Khao Soy and it is still after our trip is over- the best dish we had in all of Thailand. Some of us had it with fish, some with meat, we all loved it. It was our first meal in Chiang Mai. The next day we went to the dinner restaurant on the other side at night when the lunch place is shut down. We waited forever to be seated, it was cramped and we felt like we were eating with the people next to us, the food and beer were double the price, not as good and only half the selection of food options. It was our most expensive meal of the whole trip and the worst. We were so unimpressed. The day that we left we came back for another lunch because we wanted more Khao Soy and we were ignored for about 20 minutes after sitting down and had to flag a server over. I understand the reviews speaking about the service not being very good. The servers seemed to be more attentive to the locals than the tourists for sure. We just simply did not let this ruin our amazing lunch. The food really makes up for the service. And the second time we came for lunch it was packed without a dry seat in the house, so we chalked it up to the busy day. Come by when it isn't too busy and have Khao Soy for lunch with a nice cold beer. You will have traditional Northern Thai food and won't be disappointed. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food here is very good, and the Lanna dishes are the most authentic in the old city. The only thing that prevented a 5 star rating was that some of the portions can be small and the service was not as welcoming as we have become used to in southeast Asia.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Every time I am back in Chiang Mai I visit Huen Phen for nam prik, kao soi and other northern classics. I highly recommend the Kao soi gai, which has a very rich broth and topped off with crispy fried noodles.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Loved the selection of northern cuisine offered here. Pricing is reasonable (on average each dish - curry, khao soi, salads etc is around 60 baht) Their khao Soi is the best I’ve tasted (and I’ve tried several - including some well-known khao Soi institutions in Chiang Mai) Highly recommended as a place to try northern Thai food!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant looks quite nice and special inside. We ordered a salty crab salad and a mix of local hors d'oeuvres and a special rice bowl. The food was ok but way too expensive for what it was. You can get the same if not better at any night marketplace. But the worst part of it was the service. The waiters were more interested in making fun and laughing together than taking orders and welcoming people. Some clients left after having waited in a half-empty place for a waiter to come. Once we got a sit, one of the waiters came and literally threw a menu on the table without saying a word. And it just went on like this. The table was dirty and sticky. They brought the desert without cleaning the table from the main dish. I normally don't expect the service to be impeccable, I'm fine with it if the food is served with a smile and some nice words, but here it was absolutely not the case. Very poor choice for a dinner out. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I came here with my boyfriend and loved it. I had the Khao Soy, he had the laarb, sticky rice, and pork rinds, with the nam pik noom. Amazing! Okay seating, not comfortable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I found Huen Phen walking around town and checked reviews - generally thought of as good Northern Thai food. Went back later after it opened - great Chiang Mai Sausage. Had an assortment of dishes , all really good. It was a little difficult getting their attention after our food arrived to get dessert, the bill, etc.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Had lunch here one day, the restaurant is large and we were seated right away. Service was fine and Food was average.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Although we knew this restaurant had mixed reviews we thought we’d try it giving it the benefit of the doubt. I have to say I wish we’d gone elsewhere. The service wasn’t at all friendly, very jaded waiters and then serving very average food. Definitely wouldn’t recommend with so many other good restaurants to choose from.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommended this restaurant by our taxi driver, he said it was a great place to sample some true Thai food with many Thai locals frequenting the place. The first time we tried to get in was a Saturday night around 8pm, massive queue out the door so we came back the following evening. What a wonderfully wacky and eccentric atmosphere with plenty of authentic and not so authentic Asian nic nacs to look at. Massive menu with all the typical options but plenty of local delicacies. The food on the first visit was far superior and we enjoyed each dish, we had ordered a couple each so had plenty to try. We left satisfied with our visit and talked about a further visit before we left Chiang Mai. The food and service on the second visit was way below par, just got the feeling the staff couldn't be bothered and spent more time dashing back and forth trying to avoid your gaze for service. The food didn't have the same sparkle and was quite average too - this time round we left disappointed. Seems to be a bit hit and miss which is a shame, if they could guarantee quality and service with the typical Thai friendliness this would be a cracking find. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We spent a couple of days in Chiang Mai and this was our favorite Kao Soi. This is definitely a place to go for authentic local Thai food. No bells and whistles, just good food. The service was good and quick. They had clean bathrooms. We didn’t have time to go again, but would have if we could. Try the chicken kao soi. Delicious!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I can not say enough about this place. The best northern Thai food. The pork curry was my favorite with some sticky rice but everything I had was amazing. Also the kha soi is the best I tried in all of thailand. Sorry if I mispelled it. The prices are so low also each dish is around 80 bhat.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I read several recommendations on how this restaurant serves the best Kao Soi in Chiang-Mai but now I certainly can rate 10 other better Kao Soi restaurants in the Rose of the North or even outside. So yes it is cheap and unpretentious but a bit "regular" to me. Had to queue for 15mns for that... not really worth all that talk...

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Let’s start with the good: the food was incredible and it came out very timely! We had the chicken Khao Soi, pork sausage and guava salad. All hit the mark! The bad: we walked into the restaurant and no one was even there to seat us. Bottom line: there is zero effort on their part to make it a positive experience (almost as if we were bothering them). The staff was unfriendly, almost with permanent frowns on their faces (which is so counterintuitive to the rest of our entire experience in Chiang Mai). Food portions are rather small, so don’t be afraid to order multiple dishes! 4 for the food; 2 for the staff gives this a 3 Star. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen was recommended to us and we were looking forward to an enjoyable evening dining experience, but we were very disappointed the staff were unsmiling (very unlike Thai people) the food was very average,the portions small and pricey, but all food was freshly prepared. Our overall experience was one not to be repeated as there are many very welcoming restaurants in Chiang Mai

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A nice local Thai restaurant with good curry dishes. Sizes are not very big bit prices are good. If you are hungry take another dish.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Took the change and it was worth it! The food was really good. Our experience at dining was good service and good food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It was an incredibly long wait, and very slow service to get a table and order food. Once the food was ordered, it was fast to come out (comparatively). The food was ok, but we had better dishes at other restaurants. They don't serve khao soi in the evening, keep that in mind. Overall it was ok, but nothing to rave about.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by a local friend, this is the classic place you will not find on tripadvisor rating because everyone is local. Kao Soi was excellent, sausage appetisers were best I had in Thailand and more than reasonable prices. 3 of us has lunch for 500 bat.(around £12) which is a steal Place is clean and service is very fast

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Poor service! Here's why: 1. We only had to wait 3-5 minutes for a table, but then the host pointed to the end of the room where there were several uncleaned tables so.. we got to pick which dirty table we wanted to sit at. 2. The busboys went around cleaning other EMPTY tables around us while we sat at our dirty table waiting for it to be cleaned. It took about 5-10 minutes until one of them finally cleaned our table. 3. We didn't receive a menu during that 5-10 minutes so I had to grab one from the front. 4. After an additional 15 minutes of sitting around, I had to flag down a waiter to get our order taken and later had to flag someone down to pay for our meal. During the time it took us to order our food, another (local) family was seated, had their order taken, and received their food before us. If I hadn't flagged anyone down, who knows how long we would have sat around waiting. This restaurant definitely caters more to locals over anyone else. I believe the service was bad because the staff was severely lacking training and it was busy, not because they were being rude. Some staff did not seem comfortable speaking to foreigners at all... However, I gave this place 2 stars because the food came out quickly and I thought the khao soi was delicious. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Look no farther for best Khao Soi in town. It's a local spot that is typically very busy. Expect about 20 minute wait. The Khao Soi is super flavorful, curry is really tasty. Order sticky rice to help soak up extra broth. Preferring street food, but would still recommend this restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Khao Soi Gai at 50dhs = very nice if broth a bit thick for my taste. Lime soda at 40dhs = daylight robbery. Seriously not impressed

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
