Detail: We ate here on the recommendation of our hotel. Needed to wait and queue for about 10 mins for a free table. Interior resembles an antique shop and if you are unlucky you can end up stuck in a corner where its hard to get the staff's attention. Enjoyed the food, no reason to doubt it was authentic Lanna cuisine. Certainly not the standard fare you would expect in a Thai establishment. Particularly enjoyed the pork with ginger. Worth bearing in mind that portions are quite small, order more dishes and share if you are in a group. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Not sure about the rest of the menu, but I’ve had a few other khao soi soups in town and say this one was one on the top ones. For the rest of the menu goes I’m not sure... haven’t tried.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was a great introduction to northern Thailand food. Came here on the recommendation of a guide book for lunch and the place was busy. We ordered kaoh soi, beef lahb, fried pork spare ribs, and a beef in red sauce dish. Everything was so flavorful and fresh. Dishes were very inexpensive for what we got. Would recommend.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to Huen Phen for dinner. It lived up to our expectations which were the not very high. Prices are fair and obviously higher if you compare to food street. But all in all, it’s good value for money.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommended to go to this restaurant by a local but what a disappointment! Had to wait for our food for over half hour considering it’s a casual eating place. The portion is small compared to other Thai restaurants we went. The food tasted ok which is why I still gave 2 stars out of 5.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was recommended to me by a local. I went for lunch here and was really disappointed. The waiter was impolite. I ordered 3 dishes and steamed rice. Everything came in 5 min. That means it was prepared in advance. Noodles in 2 dishes were over cooked and fried chicken was ok but nothing special. I had better in Krabi and Bangkok. The side dish for pork soup was old and not fresh so I didn’t even eat it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Tried a meal following recommendations. The Khantoke set meal for two is inexpensive at 380baht and offers a variety of local tastes that carry Burmese influence. Furthermore, we were lucky to sit comfortably in the only cabana-like seating area. However, service is paltry and one gets the feeling that the food items were re-heated (as one other reviewer pointed out).

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food didn’t look so bed, but tasted terrible. Everytheng was heated, probably in microwave. Staff didn’t speak english. There are so many better places to eat in Chiang Mai!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We (sadly) decided to give it a try mainly because of the proximity from our hotel - don't make our mistake. Definetly not worth it. Not fresh, not tasty, not recommended. It gets a two because of the beer and ok staff.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: WE went last year for dinner and found that we just couldn't figure out the menu..This year, again, searching for the best Khao Soi..went for lunch. Fabulous. They make a great dish, and yes, it is next door, so what. Its the lunch crowd. And bad service, well, we had GREAT service and loved every moment of it. They dish was one of our favorites searching for the best. Flavorful, large portions and plenty of chicken. I give Huen Phens Khao Soi a 5 *...

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very nice restaurant with kind service. The food is good but not super Thai wise cuisine. But it is worth it to have a quiet diner in a ambient environment. The prices are very economical.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: For appetiser we had the mixed meats Combo with Northern chilli paste for 170 THB. This included vegetables, dip and Northern Sausage. We had the chicken and beef Kao Soys and were both delicious for 40 THB. Dessert was Truffle with coconut milk and black jelly in condensed milk, 20 THB each. We went at 3pm and was not busy. Served up within 10 minutes and the main course was steaming hot. Would recommend this restaurant and will be coming back soon! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've visited Huen Phen on 3 occasions. 2 out of those 3 visits were involved me losing my patience with the staff. Yes, it can get busy but they have a significant number of people working the floor, so there is no excuse for the truly awful service. The place is just badly managed. There are around 6/7 people whose sole purpose seems to be just to clean tables! At no point has any staff member ever approached me to actually do their job - offer a menu, take my order, bring the bill etc. I have had to flag someone down every single time and, quite frequently, the staff either ignore you or nod but then disappear again. During my last visit, one of the senior staff members just shook his head and walked off when I asked if I could order, another one ignored me as I repeatedly shouted "excuse me" at him and no-one bothered to bring the bill, despite me asking for it twice. If I was a less scrupulous person I would have left without paying; instead I just left the money on the table and walked out. No-one stopped me. Furthermore not one person is even remotely friendly or dares to crack a smile. The food is ok but definitely not as great as Huen Phen's reputation implies. They leave bins full of dirty plates and rubbish in the middle of the restaurant next to where people are eating - disgusting. Avoid. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was recommended by « le routard », my husband and I was so disappointed by the all place! My husband took a curry with pork and had only bones, practically no meat. The portion of my fried Thai curry was ridiculous and very spicy when it was not indicated on the menu. The service is poor and the price not so low. We do not recommend this restaurant, there is so many nice place in Chiang Mai, do not waste your time and money there!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by our hotel, we decided to check this place out to get a taste of all northern Thai dishes. We had Khao Soi, curry with chicken, fried chicken, papaya salad, sticky rice and more. It was a great place to get a taste of the variety of northern cuisine to see what we like/we don’t. The portions are small so and prices are great so go for a taste! We loved it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food here tasted like it had been made 3 days prior. The service was brisk bordering on rude. The decor is OK but even that is not worth your time to go into. We tried 3 different dishes and all were incredibly disappointing. There are a myriad of other places in Chiang Mai to fill your belly with delicious food - the Coconut Shell near by is incredible, friendly and less expensive.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant had a number of recommendations on the internet. We tried it for a special occasion. On the plus side, the ambience is nice and I was interested to try the different cuisine on offer. The waiter wanted to seat us at a table near the door ( where we wouldn't even be in the restaurant proper) but as we saw empty tables in the decorated room we moved there. The waiter took the order for a northern style curry and a chicken cashew, both with rice. He brought a chipped ceramic soup spoon so I used the metal cutlery. The dishes came out quickly and together. But the soup was lukewarm and both (very small) portions of rice were cold. The chicken cashew portion was also small and hadn't many cashews. The curry soup lacked both flavour and chicken. The beer turned out to be a small beer ( not mentioned on the menu) but priced the same as a large beer elsewhere. Service was not great and getting the attention of a waiter was difficult Go elsewhere! Try nun's or any restaurant on the west end of ratchedemneon road. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The minced meat that was recommended was horrible, vegetables that accompanied the dish looked like zombies. The recommended spicy chicken had a weird taste. The only saving grace was the curry but it was a tad too salty. Disappointing. Good luck if you are having your dinner here.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You really should not miss having a meal here; the Khao Soi was especially good! Great watching the chefs at the open kitchen rustle up endless local delicacies, good atmosphere and ideal place to linger at in between exploring the sights of the old city.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Pros: really cool decor, Cons: I went for dinner wanting to try Khao Soi but they didn’t have Khao Soi the night I went (I’m not sure they serve it for dinner?). So instead I tried the chicken vegetable curry. It had spicy peppers next to it on the menu but it was not spicy at all and had comically little chicken, basically just veggies. Overall, okay but wouldn’t recommend.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It will be a good choice if you want to try typical Northern Thai food, We went there for lunch and there are quite a lot local people cooking there. It is authentic Northern Thai style local food and we tried the curry noodle, fish wrap, fried chicken and thai salad which were all very tasty. The only thing is all food are luke warm which Chinese may not use to it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you ever get a chance to travel to Chiang Mai, one of Thailand's jewel cities known for it's charm, weather and people, don't miss this restaurant. Chiang Mai is known for its khao soi gai and Huen Pen restaurant is known to serve one of the best. At 50 Thai baht, it is the steal of a meal deal anywhere you will find in Thailand. The only reason I can't give it a 5 star is we stood alone at the door to be seated for almost 7 minutes with no one else waiting before we were seated... they need to work on this a little... don't let this dissuade you however as you could have a totally decent service experience and remember you are going there for the khao soi gai!! Enjoy..

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I stumbled across this place on a quiet night and scored a table on the top floor terrace. Ordered a juice (was a no alcohol day) and a delicious curry meal. The view over to Wat Chedi Luang was great too. Worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had heard so much about this restaurant for local authentic northern thai food. We were extremely disappointed. Except for interesting layout decor food was below average. Bad quality meat used in dishes, inexperienced uninterested staff. Highly overrated.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It’s famous for travelers so I went there with my friends in the evening. And the dish I want is served in the morning 😭 so I chose others. It’s ok, not good, not bad. I feel uncomfortable when the staff talked Chinese but I’m Vietnames. I think it’s quite expensive.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went to Chiang Mai looking for Northern Thai food, which is completely different from the pseudo-generic-Asian food found on Khao San Road. I was not disappointed and have gone back to Huen Phen a number of times over the years. I have read the negative reviews on this page and really don't know what people who wrote them were expecting. If "intimate and friendly service" is your idea of a good restaurant experience, this is not what you will find. The servers will suggest, based on your questions and input, but are not over-bearing and no, they are not intimate. True, portions on some dishes are on the small side, but perfectly adequate making a good-sized meal when you order several different dishes. I found the freshness and quality to be nothing less than excellent, all at a very reasonable price. It is important to say that Huen Phen is NOT a restaurant that caters to Western tourists, although those "in the know" go there knowing what to expect. The restaurant is in two separate areas of the same building. During the day, the times I went, the customers in the cafeteria-like rather sparse surroundings were mostly Thai, and the dining area was completely full at lunch-time. The food is on display at the serving area. If you don't know what you want, just point or ask for suggestions. You can order from a very eclectic menu. I found it interesting that the few Westerners who happened to wander in looked pretty bewildered and ended up ordering banal fare like fried rice, chicken and vegetables, because they just didn't know any better. The more up-market dining area opens at 5 PM for dinner, with a smaller but equally authentic menu. The ambience is magical and intimate, and the whole place is like an art gallery filled with a variety of tribal artifacts and other objects. There are more Westerners in the evenings, it gets very busy, and the prices do seem a bit higher, but value is still good. I enjoy going to Huen Phen every time I am in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ate here twice, as it was easy stroll from hotel. Food is above average to excellent. The dishes we had were only mildly spiced; we had to ask for Thai chilies. Service is inattentive at best. With better service, this might have approached a 5 rating

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was mediocre. Cheap but you get what you pay for. Hardly any meat in the curry. Not particularly tasty food. Ambience is good though. The restaurant setting is very quaint. Waiters can hardly speak english and didn't understand us and they don't smile either. Busy and unhappy workers.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Let's try the Local Northern Thai food at Huen Phen, Chiangmai. There are 2 branches in Chiangmai. I visited the branche no.2 near Royal Park Rajapruek. Khao-Soi is the Best! แวะชิมอาหารเหนือที่ร้านเฮือนเพ็ญ มี2สาขา ไปลองชิมสาขา2ใกล้พืชสวนโลก ข้าวซอยอร่อยมากๆ ขอตัดคะแนนพนักงาน หน้าตาไม่รับแขก ไม่สนใจลูกค้า ถึงอร่อยก็ไม่อยากกลับไปกินสาขานี้ค่ะ 尝一尝泰国北方菜！在清迈ั有两个地方。我去拉查帕皇家花园 附近的饭店。 泰北咖喱面条超好吃！ Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Stumbled across this place tired and hungry while exploring the Old City. Delicious spicy fish, morning glory, and jack fruit salad (so, so good and more difficult to find at other restaurants). Portions were not huge, but flavors were. Friendly staff and quick service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Close to my hotel so since it was raining heavily I thought I'd eat here rather than walking to the markets. At first it doesn't look like much but as you walk further into the building you find interesting artefacts and furniture with little nooks so although it was quite busy it seemed very private and cosy. There is a fairly wide selection of northern Thai and Burmese dishes and the menus are divided up into sections eg stirfry, salads etc with pictures of several and english descriptions to make it easier to decide. The servings are small, entree sized which I though was fantastic as it allows you to try a variety rather than just one dish and are very well priced. Service was very quick and efficient. I think I am quite likely to find my way back here soon Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Love this place) Real northern thai food, good prices. This place is very popular between locals. Si, if you want to try real Kao Soi, visit thus place)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good place to try the local food. Dishes can be hit or miss. Service is average and servers speaks limited English but they are generally very polite. Pork dish was good. We also tried a sampler dish. The restaurant is quite cluttered with decors which I think they might pose a challenge to dust off.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Visited here for lunch after being recommended to try it out by a local shopkeeper. Definitely is a good sigm when a place is bustling with locals. We had the most delicious khao soi, rice and fried pork. Paid around 240 baht for a meal plus beers for 2. Will be returning again before leaving Chiang Mai. May not look like the most amazing restaurant but well worth going off the main road to try some awesome food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you're a foodie this is a must-do in Chiang Mai. It was full of locals and had a huge, diverse menu. Most items were a bit more adventurous than the standard pad Thai and chicken cashew you'll find everywhere else (but there are still safe options). Very reasonably priced - but the portions are tiny. Every dish we ordered was delicious. The restaurant is a bit cluttered and cramped, there's no a/c and the staff are pretty dismissive - so we were a bit uncomfortable, but the food was so good it was still a great experience. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One of the best khao soi in Chiangmai, and many other options available for northern Thai food lovers. On the khao soi taste alone I would rate them 5 stars easily. But the portions are very small for 50 baht, and one of my other best khao soi places (Tee restaurant, next to Metro technological school - not on tripadvisor) sells a double portion for only 40 baht... So, 4 stars! But great busy place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Burn Phen is known among local and Thai visitors from other provinces for the varieties of northern Thai dishes. There are 2 locations one in the old city of Chiang Mai and another one is a branch in the subdistrict of Mae Hia on the outer ring road towards the southwest of the old city.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One of the best places to stop for lunch and try traditional food - khao soi was incredibly good. Papaya and mango salad as well. Prices were cheap, even less for khao soi than in the saturday night market. Must-try!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This appears to be the spot in downtown Chiang Mai that everyone recommends for authentic northern Thai food. Our guest house recommended it to us when we asked for Khao Soi, but they do not serve Khao Soi here! We ended up getting the platter for two, which was still pretty tasty, but there is a lot of food around that is probably as good or better.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very authentic. Go there for dinner. Beware of the heat and do order the Khantoke Set Menu for 2. Good stuff. It gives you a taste of the best the restaurant has to offer based on the northern region. It has a history since 1986.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The dining room is a work of art with all its gorgeous pieces reflecting this region of the country. An A for ambience. I arrived shortly after dinner service started so it wasn't crowded at all except for a large Chinese tourist group next to me. They were rather loud but I was so captivated by the room I blocked them out. Ordered a northern Thai pork curry--very tasty; I ate every bite. Service was generally lax (you have to catch their attention), but it seems to be the norm and it didn't bother me. Once I got a hold of my server, he was always polite and accommodating. FYI: The website uploaded for this restaurant is incorrect. Baan Huen Phen is a restaurant (and hotel) a short walk down the same street. I made the mistake but quickly figured out I was at the wrong place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Have you ever wondered what it'd be like to eat delicious meals amongst Grandma's most precious antiques? Well, wonder no more! This restaurant is profusely decorated with tchotchkes and knickknacks, you'd barely remember to pick up your jaw from the floor. Ornate lamps and plants hang from the ceiling. The dining tables are nestled amongst decorative fountains, benches piled with plush Thai-style cushions, and wooden display cupboards laden with collectibles of various categories. The whole atmosphere is nothing short of magical, if a tad claustrophobic. I imagine your Instagrammer friend would go bonkers (mine certainly did) - the photo opportunities here are quite literally endless. This restaurant specialises in tropical Northern Thai cuisine, and they have been feeding hungry locals and foreigners alike since 1986. Easy enough to locate, it's situated on Ratchamanka Road which is one street away from the road where the Sunday Market is held. I highly recommend the pomelo salad, the Shan-style pork rib noodles, the Thai beef curry and the basil soup with chicken. I shared dinner with three others and the whole meal costed us THB600 - talk about unbeatable prices! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good local food, reasonable price, convenient transportation, staff can communicate in Chinese. But perhaps the customer care may not be as detailed as it should be. There are many choice of foods. Overall good

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My favorite dish is mango spicy salad with crispy pork skin(kab mhoo). There are varieties of northern food. It is small portion so I can try some. Don't use the toilet just wash your hands.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I heard here is a best northern Thai food restaurant. So I came here many times and tried many kind of food. In like some food but I don't like some food. But it is good experience enjoy a real northern Thai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Stayed 3 nights in Town and had lunch twice there. Khao soi is delicious. Service is fast and efficient. Looks like an institution here given most of the patrons are locals.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Simple restaurant that serves local thai food.. Affordable price... The staffs are friendly and speak very simple english!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Note: One day after eating here, we did a northern Thai cooking course, so this review is done with a little bit more of knowledge on that. It's of course not enough, but at least we have tried both versions, good and bad, and this restaurant is unfortunately the bad one. We had dinner here yesterday and we were really disappointed as the food was bad and the staff really unfriendly. We decided to go after seeing the place on Lonely Planet and reading people's reviews. First of all, the place looks fantastic, it's cute and charming. So we were already really happy. We ordered 2 classical northern thai soups, some chicken starters, the special rice of the house and a typical northern papaya salad. The soups felt really industrial, and specially after trying other really good and tasty soups here in Chiang Mai, I can say it basically sucked. Chicken was OK but it's almost impossible to make that wrong. Papaya salad was just garlic and a few shrimps and the special rice was some sort of really dry rice inside an omelet. The final price with 2 drinks was 700 baht (totally crazy considering that the food was terrible), while in the market you can get a delicious dinner for less than 300. Last but not least, waiters were really slow and most of the times they were looking at their mobile phones while serving you. We will not come back, and I don't think I would recommend this place to anybody if it was not because of its nice sphere. Take a photo and leave. What a pity! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The papaya salad and nam prik were OK. They had run out of thai tea, sadly. Otherwise, fine place for a snack after touring wats.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Though known around the city, this is a very simple restaurant, serving simple, not so tasty food. Useful for a quick pit-stop during the tours.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Had a delicious meal with friends, recommended by our hotel manager and did not disappoint. Eclectic surrounds, friendly staff and such good value.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ordered the 2 person platter of traditional northern Thai dishes and I think it was aggressive for our first dinner. The food was good, but we definitely weren't accustomed to the tastes and I wished we had ordered something different. Our friends went 2 nights later and loved it. It's a quaint, open airy restaurant with great service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This a pretty cool place that's semi open air. The whole place is decorated with Thai antiquities which makes for a pretty cool setting. The food was good, though I did order a northern style chicken curry and it tasted pretty fishy. I'm not sure if that is just northern style, or what, but since I'm not a fan of fish, the dish wasn't my favorite. Also, if you're a fan of spicy make sure you ask for it. I asked for spicy, and it was more mild than anything "spicy" I've had during my time in Thailand. Definitely worth checking out though!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went here five years ago and ate some of the most interesting, and hard-to-find food. I got to eat ant eggs in a soup, and several different types of sausage. The person at the time had many things to recommend and I gladly ate ALLLLLLLL of it.. This year, I went...and the things I asked for, weren't there. They either stopped making it, or ran out. I went during the daytime as well as the evening. Evening is great for ambiance. They really stepped it up since the last time I came. Daytime, I noticed a very different population. Not sure what to think about it, now. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our little hotel happened to be tucked inside the old city and right down the street for this gem! I had read reviews before arriving in Chiang Mai but had no idea exactly where Huey Phen was. A simple walk around the area to explore was how I discovered its location! While I didn't eat in, I ordered "take away" twice and both times the food was extraordinary! I only wish I had taken a pic of the name of my dishes. I especially crave the dry stirfried ground pork with bamboo shoots, which was perfectly Thai spicy and full of flavors I've never tasted before! When I return to Thailand and for sure, Chiang Mai, Huen Phen will be my first meal!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Tried a variety of different dishes. Order the Khantoke dinner for two. Great way to get a small taste of a few thai dishes. Very cheap. The thai and lemon tea are to doe for.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Environment: no aircon Service: excellent Food: Small portion, decent taste but not amazing. Price: 5 pax with beer, about 1600 Bah. Not bad.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Simple but delicious meal, reasonably priced as well but portion is on the smaller side. Must try Kao Soy, Pig Skin, Sticky Rice, Thai Ice Milk Tea and O-Leang.. Would def make a trip there again when we visit Chiang Mai again in the future.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We asked our hotel for a recommendation for a restaurant serving real northern Thai food. They sent us to Huey Phen. We were not disappointed. The restaurant is in an old house full of Thai antiques and artifacts. We enjoyed a popular northern Thai pork curry, which has a taste and flavor unlike any other curry you will get anywhere else in Thailand (Mu Hunglay). We also tucked into a plate of northern Thai sausage, which again is very different from sausages from other parts of Thailand. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The setting is simple but the food here packs a punch. Go for Khao Soy which is a northern Thailand delicacy. Its deep fried noodles in a spicy curry broth and every bite sends you to heaven. Provided you enjoy spicy food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We asked our taxi driver to drop us somewhere for Khao Soy and we ended up at Huen Phen. We came for the Khao Soy and it didn't disappoint, I had the fish and my partner had the pork & chicken version, both were amazing. We also had some salads and drinks and all was amazing. The price was amazing as well :)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious khao soy, glass noodle salad...Mango salad very average...ok service, small helpings and very very hot if the fan isn't on you...Btw, food gets screwed all over Thailand due to pallid western palates - ensure to tell them to make it spicy or else why eat Thai food? G

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good location. Wide variety of northern food. I think it's too salty for northern food. High cost for quality of food. Could find better restaurants with same amount of money.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are looking for a good local restaurant when visiting Chiangmai, I totally recommend this place. The food was nice and the price was reasonable to pay.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food was mediocre along with the service, but the real kicker came when the manager in charge told me my drink cost more than the listed menu price. When I explained that the menu was not clear and I had ordered the 30baht drink, all she said was sorry and walked away without giving me any change. The customer is not always right here. Don't go.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The Huen Phen was one of those Northern Thai restaurants which were recommended in our guide book. That's why we went there for dinner; it turned out to be a mixed experience. There are in fact reasons to go there: The ambience is quaint, bordering on crazy; to call it overdecorated would be a great understatement. That makes this restaurant certainly special. On the downside, what's not so special is the food. We found it quite boring, and especially the braised pork (part of the hors d'oeuvre muang) was way below any acceptable standard, just bones and cartilage instead of meat, swimming in an undefinable gravy. The service was not really attentive. At the end, when we were the last guests, but still before closing time (10:00 p.m.), they started a tidying racket that we almost felt thrown out. Thankfully, there are better dining options in Chang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best Tum ma khua (eggplant spicy sald), best tum khanun (jack fruit spicy salad), fried chicken, Kanom Jean Nam Ngew (rice nooldes with northern Thai curry). Very cheap, the portions are small. So much fun to order almost everything they have and share. they can be very busy and the wait can be long. always go back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food is really great, tasty authentic and varied. The plates are very small (recommend three each) but very cheap. Will be going back

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I came here because i ask my room mate where i can eat a traditional local food. She told me about this place very popular here. No difficult to find it situated in the middle inside the old town. Simple restaurant with indoor terrace. I tried Kao soi, Laab moo, thai sausage with sticky rice. Very spicy but very tasty. I recommend this place if you want eat the traditional Chiangmai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I don't typically judge restaurants in Thailand by their rice, because on balance it is abundant and fresh. We ordered several Northern style dishes at Huen Phen - what they are known for - and they were fine, at best, but the sticky rice was clearly not fresh. This just shouldn't happen at a restaurant in Chiang Mai that specializes in Northern Thai cuisine. And although it wasn't terribly crowded, the service was quite slow and the staff not terribly attentive. There are plenty of places to go for amazing food at far lower prices.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went along December 2016 I have eaten here before but in the evening on the other side of the restaurant. I wanted to go along during the day in the other side for the day time menu which is slightly different. The dishes I went for was, aeb pla (แอ๊บปลา) Gaeng ho (แกงคั่วโฮ๊ะ) Khao Ngiaw All very nice and I enjoyed them. Service was good also. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: No English spoken. Really dirty and rotten food. They try to disguise the bad food with tons of spice. Stay away.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious food served fast with no frills. The menu has photos to guide you and the portions are generous and cheap. Located in a side street opposite a school, it's also a chance to see the locals going about their daily business.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Visited 6 years ago and liked it. What a disappointment this year. I fell cheated. Watery curry, small portion. Plain rice for 25 Baht? Small beer for 90 Baht? Usually these items are priced in proportion with the menu. Here it is not the case. I would never visit again. I hope the owner reads this and learns from it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We when to Huen Phen tonight What A Let Down we have eaten there before but at the Day time Restaurant. That was Fantastic Great service great food Fantastic Staff full of local People Tonight in the same Eatery that only serves after 1700 we ordered Chicken Rice sent back twice for being cold Roast Chicken as a starter served cold when asked if we could have it hot they took it back and fried the same piece of chicken ??? the appraisers came after the Main Meals Ordered Really when we advised the gentleman that took payment he Said Foreigners and turned away ?? I would say respect All your customers. Would not advise any one to eat there after 1700 but the day time eatery Fantastic Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had a wonderful meal here recently. The restaurant is quirky in appearance and looks more like an antiques shop than a restaurant but it works and creates a relaxing ambience. We tried a variety of specialist dishes indigenous to north Thailand including the northern style curry (kaeng hang lei), chilli dip (nam prik noom), northern Thai sausage (sai oua) and a northern style larb moo dish called larb kua. We also ordered some chicken wings and sticky rice. Everything tasted delicious and the northern style curry, sausages and larb kua were absolutely delectable. This was one of the best meals we'd had for ages and if it hadn't been for the fact we discovered this restaurant on our last night in Chiang Mai then we would have eaten here again. If you are in Chiang Mai this restaurant is an absolute MUST visit if you want to experience authentic north Thailand cuisine. The dishes are very reasonably priced and the service was good too. A fantastic experience! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice restaurant with great atmosphere and decor. The food was incredibly tasty full of lemongrass and chillies. We did try several dishes and all of them were really spicy but delicious. I can recommend the eggplant and eggs spicy salad and most of the northern thai and burmese style stir-fries. Will defo go back before I leave Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We came across Huen Phen when wandering round Old Chiang Mai. It specialises in Northern Thai food, which is more hot and spicey than anything we have had before. Do try it. After your tastebuds get over the shock, you will delight in the wonderful flavour of each dish. We felt that this must be genuine Thai, as we were the only westerners in the place. Wonderful food for those on a budget too. Each dish, though small, was big enough and only around 50 Baht! We didn't know what we were eating, so I won't recommend any one dish - try them all! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The worst terrible dining experience in Chiang Mai. The service is poor. The waiters hardly response your request. The food is poor. The rib bones are few and small. The meat is overcooked and hard to chew. Will never come back again!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place has a good local chaingmai food , easy and quick for lunch and they have another zone for dinner as well ... over all I think its good, location is good too

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After seeing Chedi luang temple we all went to this restaurant for lunch. My son had located this as near the temple. There was big crowd and we had to wait to get a table. Service time was high , maybe because it was a busy time. We ordered Thai food . But I did not find it very tasty.The children also found the food ordinary.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to Huen Phen for dinner. It is beautifully decorated and the service is good. We ordered minced pork with vegetables, fried rice and stir fried pork and vegetables with curry. The food is in authentic northern Thai style and tastes good. Though the minced pork is too spicy. The price is reasonable but the portion is small. There were already some people waiting outside the restaurant when we left at 6pm.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food (mixed appetizers, young jackfruit salad, green chili paste) was a little bland. Service was unattentive. Ambience a little cramped. No reason to queue up for a table.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Don't go there, not clean, nothing special, you won't miss anything in that restaurant, the food it's not only greasy it's cold too !, After that terrible experience I try another restaurant with western food to forget that greasy flavor in my mouth

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ate here due to the reviews from Trip Advisor. It is a basically a large room with tables and chairs , somewhat dark and dingy. I got the worst case of food poisoning I have ever had in my life two days after eating there. I was laid up for 3 days. It was the only time I ate away from the hotel. I had ordered a meal with juice. The waitress brought a juice with ice. I asked her for another juice without ice. She basically brought back the same drink minus the ice which she probably fished out with her hands. My husband had a beer and that was the difference. The food was also average and nothing special. So I would not take the chance of eating there again, even if not having a juice. Also the service was poor. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is an old favourite of ours in Chiang Mai, and we were very much looking forward to visiting again. We were thrilled to find that it was just as good and all our favourites were still on the menu. Khao Soi, fish curry, som tam, spicy Chiang Mai sausage all washed down with icy cold beers and Thai iced lemon tea. Simple, homely food that's just full of flavour. Everything is so fresh and tasty. On our last visit we did go along for dinner but we far prefer enjoying lunch here. We'll be back! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Had a lovely breakfast here this morning. They have an English menu although everything looks Thai. The breakfast menu was limited and they had lattes, cappuccino, etc but we couldn't get one for some reason. Not sure why?

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My family and I just rocked up off the street because we could hear and see the crowds in this restaurant! Turns out it was a great decision! We had no trouble getting a table (3pm on a Sunday).... The food was fantastic... Quick... Supremely tasty.... Staff were very helpful and managed to decipher our strange attempts at pronunciation... And it's ridiculously great value! We're only in Chiang Mai for two nights but are planning to return for dinner tomorrow night.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came here as the guidebook and the hotel recommended it but not really sure it's up to the hype. We came early but there was a big queue when we left. It's charming with a great eclectic decor bug we were disappointed by the food. 2 out our 3 dishes were bland. We have had a lot better elsewhere. But the food is well priced. Really wouldn't bother. Mr Kai's along the road is same price and MUCH tastier!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommended to try out this place for Northern Thai food. Perhaps I wasn't used to the taste. They also forgot our fried chicken. We had to remind them 2 times for them to bring it over. By the time it came, we finished all other food. We ordered the following and our review. Pomelo salad: Disappointing; does not taste fresh. Tom Yum Soup: generous portion of ingredient. But lacks punch in flavour. Fried Pork with Basil: Ok. Fried Chicken: Tasty. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Probably more a 4.5 rating! We visited for lunch and its very busy/popular but we managed to get a table. The food comes out quickly and is of a high standard. Its very reasonable so budget friendly. Worth a visit if you are exploring the city.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very reasonable and good taste. We had khao soi and northern food platter. Maybe because of year end high season, it was quite crowded and there was long queue. We needed to take ticket (yellow paper) first and wait to be called out the ticket number. We waited around 15 min.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ok... they only list one Huen Phen restaurant but there are actually 2 side by side. One is open for lunch; the other is open for dinner. They actually use the same space but have two completely different menus!? Khoa Soy is ONLY served at lunch at it was AMAZING!!! I am a major fan of Asian noodle dishes and this one may have rocketed to the top! The dinner menu is also northern Thai curry soups, meat dishes and vegetables but no Khao Soy... in fact zero overlap on any of the dishes. I only give it a 4 because of this confusion. Both lunch and dinner have long wait lists. Lunch is more worth the wait. Lunch staff seemed better organized but they were not as friendly. Night staff was friendly but appeared to be stretched to their work capacity. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Not worth your time... bad service, bad food, go somewhere else, anywhere else is probably better💩💩

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I still have dreams about the khao soi. Definitely the best one I had on my entire trip in Thailand. So delicious!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I'm reviewing this restaurant with mixed feelings. First, I believe the food could have tasted great, it's just that, since I wanted to avoid a lot of people, I went for late lunch here (maybe around past 3PM). I got what I wanted, not a lot of people around, BUT, the food served were cold. They need to do a better job at keeping their food warm. The Khao Ngiaw (steamed rice with pork blood wrapped in banana leaf) was good, but the pig blood curry that went with it was a bit on the bland side and cold. The Tam Kanun (jackfruit salad) was also quite good. The Gaeng Ho was a let down because it was already cold. Maybe, if the Pork Blood Curry and Gaeng Ho were warm, the experience would have become different. Overall, foodie tourists could try Huen Phen for authentic Northern Thai food, but just make sure to come around proper lunch or dinner time so the food will still be warm and are in their best quality. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Foods are pretty good but I don't think staffs there know the words 'service mind' and 'hospitality'. We were treated terribly, it was such a nightmare for my family vacation! Ladies and gentlemen, you should probably have another choices to go!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We come here from local recommendation and it was good food. We like the tom yam soup, grilled pork ribs, fried fermented pork, and the sticky rice. Would recommend for visitors when you are in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Can not for the life of me understand why there are so many positive reviews here - but perhaps we ordered the wrong things. We arrived at 8:15pm on a Tuesday night and still had a 40 minute wait. The menu seemed to have many delicious items on it, we were having trouble choosing. We ordered: the fried chicken (tasty, but not much meat - lots of bone and gristle), the crab dip - unexpectedly, black crab with a different flavor (this could be an acquired taste), pork fried rice WITH NO PORK IN IT, and Huan Phen curry soup with pork spare ribs ALSO WITH NO PORK IN IT. The soup was a particular disappointment as it was my main course. The "spare ribs" consisted of 3 bones with fat and no meat - they weren't even worth bothering to gnaw on. The fried morning glory was tasty, but greasy. Maybe this place is good if you're vegetarian? Or maybe we just had a massive order fail. Either way we are grateful this hot mess only cost 350 baht, which would have still been better spent at Krua Dabb Lob around the corner. Feel free to try but don't order what we did! And definitely don't wait 40 minutes to get in. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by lonely planet. Super cheap and has a wide range of local specialties. Friendly service, and fairly prompt even when busy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were in Chiangmai for three days and once we found this gem we were in each afternoon. The food is delicious, the service super and the prices really good value This is a must visit. Try the Thai sausages served with shredded ginger. They are addictive!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came for dinner as strongly recommended by my friend back in Malaysia "You must visit this place to feel the northern Thai dishes". Expectation is high when I arrived. Tripadvisor covers show khao soi and I asked for Khao Soi. The waiter said 'No no no khao soi'. I was like 'Errr.. there is picture of khao soi and the picture of place / paintings matches the one shown in TripAdvisor. I told myself 'Maybe they only serve for lunch'. We ordered few dishes including Tom Yum Goong (I know Tom Yum Goong is not original Northern Thai delicacy). Well, I have mixed feeling. It taste like the one I had in US, the Americanized version. Don't get me wrong, my wife loves me, but I kind of savor the real Tom Yum Goong. Food is good, my parents and kids kinda like them, but I'd not come back again. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came here for dinner. Apparently they have another cheaper restaurant next door but only open during the day. Food was ok, but nothing really stood out. This restaurant has the strangest collection of decor I have ever seen. Predominantly Thai but lots of modern art hanging on the wall as well. The cuisine is northern Thai, but Andrea Bocelli was singing in the background (followed later by harpsichord baroque). Bizarre...

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I almost never found this place as the entrance takes you far into the building before you're really sure it's not someone's private house. The place is full of eclectic stuff and will keep you eyes busy for a while. Table seating is close-proximity so mind what you say as others are privy to it. I guess this is what they mean by 'cozy'. I wasn't sure if the staff was being overwhelmed or what but getting seated, then getting the order in was a bit of a chore. My friend and I ordered a single item from the menu to start and I sensed a bit of confusion in the server but let it go as a language issue. When food arrived at the table, I looked at my friend and he at me. Neither one of us knew what it was on the table and I figured it was a misplaced dinner for someone nearby. We told the waiter that it wasn't what we ordered (I ordered the house special rice dish) and the head waiter (or at least someone older) came out just after our neighbour identified one of the items as the special house rice dish by the photo in the menu, so it was confusion for all base upon our own mistake. I guess that one covered up the fact that this restaurant is one where you order a few things and then take from them (family style) onto your own plate. We heathens from the great white north are not accustomed to sharing in this manner and so we ate just the item we each ordered, braving any giggles and finger pointing that might be deserved. By the way, the special house rice dish (wrapped in a thin egg omelette) was extremely good so just as well I got to eat all of it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have been in Chiang Mai for 2 weeks and this is the best meal I have had! I went for lunch and had an eggplant dish with a hard boiled egg and a pomelo salad. Both were incredibly delicious! The eggplant is a strange gray color and a bit spicy, but the flavor is wonderful! The pomelo salad is crazy good. It has some tiny dried shrimp in it which give amazing flavor and texture. I will go back tomorrow, but it will be difficult to order new dishes because I liked the first 2 so well that I want to have them again!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was invited by a business colleague who explained that this place is known for its local northern Thai cuisine. It was quite busy despite the fact that it is kind of hard to find. Which means it is probably good for many local people. For my taste there was not something exciting. That is why I it give it average. However , some people people might give it a better grade. Service is good. Food is fresh.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am not sure why our hotel recommended this place. It was disgusting. We both got very sick within two days of eating here. I cannot be 100 percent sure this was the source but I believe we got food poisoning from this restaurant. Avoid at all costs.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The fried pork taste very good but the tomyam is too spicy for me. Unfortunately the restaurant do have fry vegetables, hence I ordered mix fruit salad which is also spicy. No much choices for those who can't take spicy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We been there by mistake yesterday, the soup was uneatable, the staff very lazy, No service at all. ........................................................................................

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went o both of these restaurants- the one that serves in the afternoon and the one in the evening. Both were tasty and authentic. The evening one is a prettier restaurant and feels a bit more upscale. Service was not great due to language but overall, cheap and tasty!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is so overhyped. The food is decent but kind of overpriced (by Thailand standards) and very small portions. The dish curry vegetables with pork came in a tiny plate and had only one piece of pork in it. They also added a dish that we didn't order which turned out to be the most expensive dish on our bill and it's not even a meat dish, we only realised after we touched it so we couldn't return it. We thought maybe it was miscommunications but when the bill came we realised it was added after the rice which doesn't make sense so we don't know what they were trying to do. Also I thought this place was known for it's Khao Soi but we couldn't find it on the menu and the waiter said they don't have it when we asked. I can only recommend the pork curry (not the one with curry vegetables) and tom yum soup. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Chatting to a local person, we asked where she would recommend we eat. She immediately suggested Huen Phen, and we are very glad she did. It is quite a popular eatery with tourists from an array of countries, as well as Thais (possibly also tourists). We had four dishes - one actually from northeast Thailand, one a Burmese curry, and two from Lanna. The food (and iced tea) was very nice, but the best surprise was the bill, which was less than 500 Baht for a very satisfying meal. We'll try to go back again before we leave Chiang Mai - there are still plenty of appealing dishes on the menu. Unfortunately, we couldn't have Khao Soi - I'm sure it would have been delicious and authentic here - as it is only served at lunchtime, apparently. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: a tasty local thai restaurant, lot of local have lunch or dinner there, you can taste real northern thai food

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Tasty food, friendly service. Two of us ate well, each had a drink and all for under 8 pounds sterling!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Classic cheap Thai restaurant serving traditional Lanni cuisine. We had 3 drinks, 4 dishes and rice for same price as one starter in tourist restaurant. (£5) Lots of locals, quick service, thoroughly recommend trying a selection of different things.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice "Chiangmai" style restaurant. Very busy with a small air conditioned area in the middle. We sat on the outside. The food was good and the service was quick and easy. There were three of us and we had a lot to eat. Baht400 in total. (Around GBP10. USD12. EUR11) A more than reasonable price. That was Baht400 for all three of us. For what it was this was a great outcome. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We walked down from our hotel and were very happy with our choices. The wait staff was not very helpful, but this is very local, so we just winged it. Hubby does not like eggplant and was surprised that he ordered eggplant and chicken. So good, I could hardly get to try a bite . I had chicken curry soup. Was surprised that it was not the green that I usually see, very go,but would have the chicken/ eggplant dish next tie, the decor is wonderful. All the old carved pieces, lamps,doorways I wanted to take it all home with me!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want to try northern Thai cuisine this a a good place. We tried different dishes and all were very good. Service was good and staff friendly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited this restaurant for lunch, mainly because of the traditional decor we've seen from other reviewers' pics. However, that area is only open until 17:00! So bad that we can't sit there during our lunch... The restaurant is quite fully occupied when we reached there. We've ordered pamelo salad, khao soi with pork, fried chicken, fried pork, and mango juice and iced coffee. All dishes are already on my table within 2 minutes! Obviously the food has been prepared for some time and wait for customers to order. So the food is only warm but not hot. Having said that, the khao soi is really super tasty! One of the best in Chiang Mai. The other dishes are excellent too. Coffee is very good also (even this is not thai drink!). One thing to mention is the pamelo salad is super spicy. It has many slices of green pepper in it and super super hot. Better avoid this if you can't take spicy food. Overall, our lunch in Huen Phen is excellent!!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our Bangkok hotel owner recommended we try the Khao Soi for lunch and I'm happy we took his advice. I tried the fish and my husband had the more traditional pork and both were delicious. It's nothing fancy and it's dirt cheap.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: On my recent visit this year to Thailand we spent a few days in Chiang Mai. I'll always remember Huen Phen as one of my favorite Thai (Lanna style food) restaurants - just plain excellent food here! Close to Chedi Luang - only a couple of blocks away. I have had many meals here and always the food is consistently great! Service is also attentive while not being intrusive. A must visit if in Chiang Mai!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Saw this place on the Groovy Map, then also recommended in another couple of books/maps so thought we'd try it out. Really extensive menu with lots of Northern Thai menu options. We had Northern Thai Pork Curry, and both Chicken and Beef Khao Soi, sticky rice and papaya and apple salads. The place was really busy with a good atmosphere, English menu helped us making our choices. Every dish had lots of flavour and it was all served really quickly. Service pretty good although had to ask for things a couple of times - think it was more of a language barrier than anything else. Not a huge amount on the menu for a five year old but we got by ordering fried chicken with sweet chilli sauce which he loved. Good introduction to Northern Thai food in a friendly, busy environment. Definitely worth a try. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had a Khao soi for around 60 baht and it was delicious! This is a very local place the food comes in small portions but is cheap and delightful. The laab moo is also very good, it is mixed pork with many herbs which goes with rice. If I ever go back to Chiang Mai, I would definitely come back here.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Average on food. Service was a bit slow and haphazard. Environment is gaudy with irritable bugs. Toilet was not clean..

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is very close to the famous temple Wat Chedi Luang and Wat Pra Singh. Do drop by if you visit the temples. It wasn't crowded in the afternoon and the food was served quickly. I highly recommend their khao Soi. It's very rich in flavor and if you order chicken khao Soi, there's a drumstick in the bowl :)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I visited this restaurant once for lunch by recommendation of articles, nevertheless, it's not as good as I expected.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very simple basic place, popular with locals and tourists alike. Very efficient service, the food arrives within 10 minutes. Good Northern Thai dishes like Khao Soy, really delicious. Also other dishes like Tom Kha Gai and fried chicken were good. Extremely reasonable/cheap prices. Be aware it is simple, but if you like to try the real local dishes this might be a good option. (I would rather recommend for lunch). Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really loved the northern Thai sausage, beef khao soi and kanom jin here! Great place for a quick lunch or dinner. Seems pretty popular with locals and tourists.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to this restaurant for lunch and had a selection of Northern sausage, pickled pork ( a cured pork sausage) and fried pork with chilli dip (nam prik) put together with sticky rice. It was very good and 190 baht all in. I read some of the other reviews and couldn't work out why people were saying it was pricey until we went back in the evening to find that the area for dinner was different to the lunch area and there was a 100 baht charge for all items - twice the lunch price. To be fair it seemed more upmarket as an environment but I expect the food would have been near identical. There was also a queue so we moved on. This is the beauty of Chiang Mai and and Thailand as a whole - there is always a good restaurant round the next corner. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had dinner here the first night we arrived. The food was excellent and very affordable. We were actually surprised at the amount we paid! We came to this restaurant because it was walking distance from our hotel and there were a lot of people hanging out outside.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Despite numerous visits to Chiang Mai, this is the first time that my wife and I have eaten here. A 5 minute walk from where we were staying, it was worth the effort with the restaurant decor being quite eclectic, producing tasty Northern dishes. The food was good and very reasonably priced, with a quite a varied menu. Serving sizes were good and everything was brought to the table in a speedy manner.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was our first meal in Chiang Mai and I thought it was great. The place was very busy at lunchtime which seemed a good sign. The menu was varied, the service was super fast and they managed my son's food allergies. The setting is pretty basic, no frills with plastic plates etc. The price was just under 1000 bht for our family of 5 which is a fraction of the price we have paid in Bangkok. Can't complain.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was by far the worst we've had in Thailand in a week. When we left I looked around my seat and saw two dead cockroaches, which is disgusting. Service is not great. Definitely worth a pass.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best Khao Soi and Tom Sam dishes. Very affordable, efficient staff very close to to major tourist sites.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I saw a lot of reviews which mainly focus on the waiters. I counted and there were actually 5 waiters serving almost 20 tables. If you are blaming them...well...I don't know...you need to get in their shoes first. The food was OK. A bit salty and sweet. I know there are a lot of places better than this one but this one is decent. Its decoration is unique and filled with antique stuffs. Good for couples and families. Should try at least once.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Just fantastic Northern Thai food, very interesting tastes and many different dishes, we loved eating at Huen Phen. The ladies preparing the food were just lovely!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Staff were unfriendly and the food was salty and overpriced! Even though the interior was decorated, the food, which a restaurant should focus on, was significantly worse.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Portion sizes were small and they took our "not too spicy" request to heart but the food was still quite tasty. Based on information from my local friend, the price was slightly pricey considering we are in the north of Thailand. The decor was quite nice and the music contributed well to the overall ambience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was recommended by our tour guide and also by travellers in tripadvisor, but after my visit, I think I disagree with most of the reviews. The price is affordable, portion is quite generous, but I do not think that this restaurant deserve high ratings, sorry. From last experience, I think the owner should start thinking about better management on how to accept reservation, escourting the long awaited customers, taking orders and also maintaining food taste and quality, especially during peak hours.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Lovely restaurant, one not to miss. The food is very authentic and the chef comes round after to ask about your meal. The spice scale works well. It gets very busy so best to book ahead. I did via email and they got back to me very promptly. Would certainly go back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had lunch here and we really enjoyed it. A lunch for 2 with drinks was 300 baht. The portions are small which I really liked because it enabled us to try a few different dishes. The khao soi was amazing. Absolutely amazing.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is chaotic and hot, but honestly has the best Khao Soi I've had in Thailand (and it's cheap, I think our bill was under 200 baht for two people for noodles and drinks). If you're looking for a romantic dinner experience, or even a particularly clean one, this is not it. The portions were kind of small, but the price reflects that. However, the depth of flavor of the khao soi made it worthwhile.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was the worst food in Chiang Mai. The only saving grace was fried/sautéed mixed veggies which tasted very American. Soup tastes like dish water. Morning glory had the strangest gross flavor. Fried rice was bad. Norther chicken and other suggested options were really bad. The eggplant salad said hot but was not cooked or spicy. Everything was premade we think because nothing could be altered. No one spoke a lick of English. Don't go!!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Evrything is nice........u have to try the kao soi. Fermented pork ribs superb with its sauce. Pork skin👍. Tom yum a bit blant.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited this restaurant based on recommendations and decided to go for the Khantoke dinner set. This is a traditional way of eating Thai food - with many small portions of curries, dips, rice and dessert served in a wooden platter. The sampling was quite tasty and overall portions very good for two people. The restaurant decor is quite busy and the staff were pleasant.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to dinner here on the recommendation of the guidebook and a friend. the atmosphere was very nice; it was a nice break from the sidewalk eateries one finds all over Chiang Mai. But the wait staff was inattentive and the food disappointing. I had the pork fried rice (nearly cold and tasteless) and my wife had an eggplant dish not labeled as spicy on the menu that she said set her mouth on fire and she couldn't eat.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: As recommendation for Tripadvisor, I came here for dinner. The food was good, price was reasonable however I couldn't stand for the mosquitoes here. I asked for the prevention liquid but it only helped for 15 minutes.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great Northern Thai food, I was their with a Thai companion who enjoyed it. Small portions but you can try more variety. Good value for money, even in rainy season had to queue

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is just for her popularity on tourist guide books. The place is quite romantic with setting of old fashion look. However the food is nothing special. According to the tourist guide this place is specialised on Kao Soi. As this restaurant is more of tourism, the price was set at a relative expensive area. This is our first stop for food at Chiang Mai and we shall explore more better choices at Chiang Mai for sure. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is quite a famous local Northern Thai restaurant. Its quite basic but authentic and good value- we managed to eat a 3 course meal and drinks for two for 1250B. Not everybody will appreciate the setting but in the evening the restaurant expands back from the street into the ambience of a quaint junk shop.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We are here because we arrived at our hotel across the road late and we wanted somewhere near. The food was really good and tasty and really reasonably priced. Service was really good too, one of the best restaurants we at in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came to this place because of Trip Advisor. Food was too small a portion and establishment is full of flies. Mind u, having flies in an enclosed air/cond area is super disgusting. The quality of food is not only low but also pricey as opposed to the ones you can get from Sunday Walking Market. Their Kao Soi is just average, you should just go to Kao Soi Nimman, it's awesome. The Lemongrass drink I have ordered were mere sugar water. Just plain horrible.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Place came highly recommended. Decor was good. Food was good. Drink prices were outrageous. B110 for a small Singha. Service was not good. Worth a look for the decor, but dont have a drink unless you dont mind waiting.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nothing special about this restaurant, not many locals mostly were tourists brought over by tour agents or taxi drivers. We did not order much food, only the fried chickens. We were turn off by the flies.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have many dishes of local foods. They are quite spicy, hot but good. Many customer here, I thing because of their famous.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ordered 10 dishes. They were out of #41 which was very disheartening but the food generally was just bland and not spicy enough.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: traditional food of northern Thai, tasted special, friendly staff, but the price is a little expensive,

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Victim of its own success, the restaurant was full when we arrived around 7pm. We waited for 10min and entered the dining room, which is superbly decorated with typical sculptures etc. This one of this places in Chiang Mai where you can have a delicious overview of the local northern Thai food. Try the gaang haang lai or the northern sausage for instance ! Definitely tasty, just know that the quantities are quite small

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Here is a traditional nothern Thai food restaurant. Lunch time I had a really tasty food with cheat price. Don't miss Kao Soi and Som Tam Mango.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Pretty good bang for your buck. I felt like I was eating at a restaurant locals would come to for special occasions (no idea if that is true. Decent prices(150_400 baht for most dishea)good food and very cool/ unique dining atmosphere. Also very close to all the temples in the old city. The area we sat in had a bunch of fans but no air conditioning

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our hotel, the Rim Resort, gave us a recommendation to this restaurant and are we ever glad we ate there. Such character with all the collections displayed throughout the restaurant. Our meal was fabulous. Go and eat there!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Not so big restaurant but huge choices of local northern food, and the taste "Aroi Mak". Must try when you are in chiang mai, do not look for expensive restaurant smaller are better if you like to have the taste of real local foods.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I love everything about Huen Phen, it is a real Northern Thai Cuisine with the price and quality for a local. The variety of dishes are great. I could have lunch there every day for a week and never need to eat the same thing twice. I visit Huen Phen every time I visit Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ordered the burmese pork curry and also young jackfruit salad but found the food quite salty. We wenr abt 5+ and although the interiors were nicely decorated, it was too dark for my preference too.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The flavor was good but portions are much smaller than street food. Beware they charge for extras such as Ive and rice...... Free everywhere else

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I orderd the local dish "Khao Soi" with chicken and it so tasty but the other dish not much interesting. The location is very close to the center so after went to see famous temples, this place good to relax and have dinner.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is getting more crowded these days with the influx of China Tourists ..loud and noisy! But we will brave them to satisfy our craving for the very authentic local dishes! Love the BBQ Chicken! The Som Tam! The Variety of Curries! YUMMY!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Tasty and cheap typical chiang mai food, we found this place by my friend who is thai and said that we have to try this place. No regret we will go back there for sure =)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Been here overtime I visited Chiangmai and never disappointed, fast service, authentic taste and reasonable price. Conclusion: Can't Miss if you like to try authentic food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Restaurant specializes in northern Thai dishes that can be hard to find even in northern Thailand. We tried the set menu for two, a little bit of everything. It was fantastic! And the decor was lovely.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Dropped in for lunch, probably about 100 locals and 4 farangs, a good start! Located in an old wooden building, which apparently has more fine dining at night. At lunch, just a bustling casual restaurant. I am not as familiar with Northern Thai cuisine compared to other parts, so I was keen to sample the food. Couple of us enjoyed fried chicken, pomelo salad (excellent), egg noodle with chicken curry, Northern Thai sausage and some rice, all washed down with a couple of beers. Food was very enjoyable in a great atmosphere. No problem with the service, the whole thing came to B480 for two (about $10AUD) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really really tasty food. Great northern flavour and not too spicy. Excellent starters and great ribs too. Maybe don't look at the floor or visit the bathroom as these will put you off ur food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We decided to spend our last evening in Chiang Mai having dinner here having heard that the restaurant is very attractively furnished and you can sit outside on the balcony overlooking Wat Chedi Luang. The food is traditional Thai and delicious. The evening we went was very quiet so the place was a bit lacking in atmosphere but service was friendly and quick. The food was beautufully cooked and presented but, since I don't eat meat my choice was very limited. I do eat fish but for a real vegetarian, there was only one possible dish on the menu. Nevertheless, the dishes I did select were probably the best Thai food I've eaten in Chiang Mai. I was a bit surprised that the only alcohol on offer was beer - no wine or spirits. In spite of the fact, that this felt much more of a 'special' dining experience than in most of the CM restaurants we've visited, the prices were very reasonable - only a couple of pounds more than our normal 'cheap and cheerful' choice.I would visit again Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: With a number of friends we visited Huen Phen twice early in March. A well-known Northern Thai restaurant, it enjoys a clearly well-deserved local reputation. The décor is genuinely interesting, with a number of spaces featuring a large umber of objects and artifacts, which create an excellent vibe. This is a very popular restaurant so bookings are recommended. We were greeted warmly and the friendliness of the staff was undoubted. Service is a little patchy, but not an issue. The food was excellent. Plenty of choices with dishes well prepared and attractively presented. We tried most of the usual suspects and found everything to be flavoursome and nourishing. Reasonable priced wine is available (ie reasonable in the context of Thailand!) with beer being well priced but perhaps not cold enough on the evidence of our two visits. Reasonable prices, great décor, good food - this is a restaurant that is certainly worth a visit if you are in the old city in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were walking around Chiangmai sightseeing and decided it was time for lunch. We were about to go to a cafe when we got chatting to a local man. He recommended walking a little further along the road for some local food...we were glad we did! We found this place which was very busy with locals! We had to wait 5 minutes for a table but we didn't mind....people watching was a highlight here! The food was of course delicious and so cheap. A great atmosphere made this place a highlight!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We've been in Chiang Mai for a week and had lunch in Huen Phen three times. It's a large casual cafe with a great range of Thai dishes at great prices. Service is quick and efficient. Well worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant is heavy on figurines and small rooms for dining. Intimate and authentic. Great food and excellent prices

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is one of my favourite restaurants in Chiang Mai! The food is delicious. Recommended!!!!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went on a Monday night after a small meal at the north gate. Thank goodness we ate before because we had almost an hour wait. There were about 20 people waiting which I thought was odd for a Monday night so I thought this place was gonna be amazing. After we were seated it was another ten minutes before the waitress came and took our order. It took just as long to get two beers delivered to the table. When the food finally came out it was warm, nearly cold. The food was ok, not super flavorful except the sausage, which was the best thing we ordered. By the time we got our food the place was basically closing so the check came out super quick. Even though the food was tasty and local I would not come back or recommend this to anyone else with so many other option. Better to grab something at a food stand at the north gate and fill up there. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our party of seven enjoyed a very good meal at the restaurant. As has been mentioned by other reviewers, dishes aren't de-spiced for western palates. As a consequence you get the true test of northern Thai dishes. Our particular favourites were the local sausages and the aubergine dishes. We had as much food as we needed and with drinks we calculated that the cost per head was UK £9.45. Such great value for the money. Very good food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant's quite easy to find and the songthaew driver instantly recognized where we wanted to go. An unremarkable exterior, the restaurant has a signature pork and local sausages dish. The eggplant dish was prepared in such a texture that it melts in your mouth! Never tasted it that way before. Local "otah" is highly recommended too. The roast pork is really nice with just right amount of fats. The fried rice is nice but really small portion and big eater like me (or most men) will find it just too little. At night you may think the restaurant is closed. The usual entrance is closed but a side entrance just 10 steps further down is the entrance for dinner. This part of the restaurant is normally not used in the day and is artfully decorated with wood carvings. And it is packed. Come early! One thing is that the area is rather quiet at night and not easy to find songthaews or Tuk tuks. Prices are higher for the return trip. Also, sunday lunch time is packed with locals after their temple prayers or even lunch receptions for weddings (so we were told by the staff). Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
