Detail: We ate here twice - once in the gloriously busy lunchtime canteen with lightning fast service, cheap prices and plenty of choice on the menu - and once at the evening restaurant, with its quirky decor and slightly more expensive dishes. In both the food was excellent - the sausages were especially good - and no concessions were made to western taste,something which I found disappointing elsewhere: leaving out the spices often means bland food. Not here. Service was ok. I don't think that Thai restaurants in general have caught on to the western obsession with an entree, main course and dessert in that order- it might be called an entree but it will probably arrive along with all the rest of the meal. I don't have a problem with this. In the evening, if you do not book you will probably have to wait - but it is worth it. Portions can be a little small so order more than one dish. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Local people recommended. Friday 6pm 2 persons waited for 10m. Attractive interior. Food was ok. Closed to old city

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had to queue to get a table and it was well worthwhile. The setting is quite eclectic with interesting ephemera scattered around the creative setting. The food is authentic but certainly not fancy. We really enjoyed our dining experience at Huen Phen.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a great place to go for local Northern Thai food, as can been seen by the local crowds that fill it. Staff are friendly and efficient, food is authentic and atmosphere is zero. We were in a room with 4 tables, no windows and an air-conditioner on full blast, but it didn't matter because the food was so good. No Pad Thai or green chicken curry here - just great local dishes, serving a mostly local crowd. Chicken curry, pork sausage and fried pawpaw salad are all highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you travel to Chiangmai and didn't sampling the food from this out standing restaurant that mean you never come to Chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Of four meals in Chiang Mai, this was by far the worse. We tried five different dishes. The fried chicken appetizer was so overcooked there was no chicken flavor left. The ginger pork stir fry had so much ginger it overwhelmed any other flavor; I could only eat it when mixed with something else. The green chili fried rice was bland and had no distinct flavor--the most unsatisfying fried rice we have ever had. Two different so-called Northern Thai curry soup dishes lacked any curry flavor and not one of the dishes had any spicy heat at all. There was a short wait to be seated, a very long wait for our order to be taken, and not any attention after we were served. The decòr was tired and cluttered. We were so disappointed as this was recommended when we wanted to try Northern Thai cuisine. Don't bother with this place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our first meal in Chiang Mai because we were told that it offers very authentic northern Tha food. Maybe we are too used to bangkok food, this was not as we expected. You can get better tomyam somewhere else. Kao Soi was not bad though, the serving is not big so you can have a few bowls. The larb was good. The fried chicken and pork could be better. Price was reasonable but I think the food was average.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Although when we travel we prefer not to eat in the same restaurant twice, we enjoyed the food and ambiance of this restaurant sufficiently to break our rule. And as the restaurant seems to be very popular, I would advise booking ahead or you may have to wait in line for a table.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My first meal in Thailand was at Huen Phen and it was such a great introduction to Thai food! My friend and I both ordered the Koh Soi and it was amazing! We are both vegetarians who eat fish and we were able to find something to eat. The noodles tasted homemade. I found the dish quite spicy but I do not have spicy food often. Also, the Thai tea was delicious.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: we went here as the hotel staff recommended. when we arrive there were several groups waiting. so we were excited to have dinner here. but the taste was very very bad for bamboo shoot soup. and others also just so-so level. will not visit again.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ate here last night and ordered the sampler of local dishes for 2. The flavors were pretty good but the fresh herbs were tired. At 6pm on a Wednesday there was a 30 minute wait to get a table. The food wasn't up to the quality I expected so we won't be going back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The mixed reviews are due to some people going to another place with the same name. This place is pretty good. The Khao Soi is terrific, but the other food was just good for me.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The northern egg noodle and chicken (#2) was good as were the fermented ribs, and the pork cracklins & greens. Other dishes we tried were okay, nothing to write home about. Friendly service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went again last year to the Huen Phen. The service was slow which we expect in Thai restaurants, but the food was lovely

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Don't be put off by how it looks from the outside as the food inside is really good. There is enough choice on the menu for most people, price is cheap and the flavour of the foods is some of the best I've had in Thailand. When myself and my wife ate there it was very busy as people where queuing to get in so service was a little slow but the food makes up for it. The only down side is the rudeness of some of the other people eating.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My companion and I shared a khantoke set menu. The food was adequate, but the spice level was mild compared to other Lanna-style restaurants I've tried in Chiang Mai. The meat dishes were better done than the vegetarian stuff. I suspect that the restaurant purposefully tones down the spiciness and exoticness of their food to suit the tourist palette. The staff are very busy, but attentive and courteous. The interior is a bit whimsical, as the restaurant has grown into several structures over the years; every dining room is different. I was seated in an area that was clearly a converted house. If you're staying in the city center and want easy access to traditional Lanna cuisine, you could do worse -- but with a bit of travel, you could also do better. Be aware that the wait can be VERY long due to the popularity of this place with tourists. I arrived at 7pm on a Tuesday and had to wait about 45 minutes for a table for two. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had about a 15 / 20 minute wait and well worth it! Went for dinner and had Papaya and crab salad to start - nice and spicy! Followed by chicken and mixed green veg curry with steamed rice. Recommended dish by my waiter and it was rich and deep in flavour and just a background hint of some spice! All in all a delicious northern thai meal! 240 baht for salad, curry, rice and beer

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great decor and location, but this place is packed for dinner (from 5 to 10 pm) so advise reservations or get there early before or upon opening. Also have a separate lunch location next door, same good food but with shorter menu and less atmosphere. Chili dip was only average, pommello salad was EXCELLENT but spicy. Chicken basil dish good but mostly cut-up beans.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was there for dinner with a friend and we had a great time there even though the service is quite slow but it perfect. We got annoyed but some tourists who doesn't no how to wait and just be very rude toward the waiters,that is what you shouldn't do when you want your food tobe serve on time. Note to reserve the table before if you will be there after 5.30

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We have eaten here over the past 5 years but it has changed and no forseeable improvement as their business exploded with more tourist mktg. This will be last and there are more nicer eateries around chiangmai to experience. Not just dump food but ambience service and taste.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you have come to Chiang Mai you should be eat thai or local food at huen phen. Because Good taste, middle price but sometimes they have many customer that I waiting my order long time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We've been to Huen Phen a few times for lunch in the past but this was a first for dinner - what a disappointment! The food was terrible, portions tiny, and quite expensive for such small poor quality food. While well decorated in lanna style, this cannot make-up for bad food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant, on the 3rd floor of a hotel, is located on a noisy street in a quieter part of the old city district of Chiang Mai. It was beautiful evening, so we chose to sit on the balcony. No view, but certainly atmospheric. It was a quiet Tuesday evening and the restaurant was almost empty with exception of two young Spanish backpackers looking for some respite from their dorm room, and an older Australian gentleman with a twenty something Thai female friend. So the room had no buzz about it, but a peaceful quietness in contrast with the cacophonous street. The food was simple but tasty, although to be truthful I hankered to taste the more intense and singular flavours from the Chang Phueak night market located just outside the northern gate of the old city district. But the wife and kids were happy, so for one night I too was happy to be a little more civilised and sit to a table and eat off a China plate. To be truthful that was the joy we all spoke most about for it was nice to eat of ceramic plates for a change, with a table cloth and soft muzak wafting through the door onto the balcony. So, as a moment of respite from the noise and bustle of Chiang Mai, its smells and hustle, this was well worth it. Now, n back to the streets.... Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Have returned to this restaurant three times in three years and I'm still impressed with the range and quality of food. The dinner experience is always enjoyable amongst the quirky surroundings of the restaurant. It can get quite busy and when full you can put you name on a list and wait for table to become available. Worth the wait.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food was very tasty and a nice change from all the "regular" Thai food we ate most other nights. Portions were a little small - we ordered 13 dishes for 8 people. Decor was really interesting, almost like we were eating in an antique shop. Worst part of the evening was the long wait to get a table and the service once we sat down. We had a reservation but still had to wait 1 hour to be seated. There seemed to be many servers running around but they were not attentive. We were starving after waiting for one hour and had to constantly try to get their attention to order food and to remove dirty dishes and bottles from our table. Suggest you only try this restaurant if you are prepared to wait and are willing to be patient. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If it was for the food alone I'd give it five stars. Comparing the same dishes (or similar) with all the Northern Thailandese restaurants I visited in Chiang Mai (where I still am), Huen Phen offers consistently the best ones. The plates are small. So order several. Five or six should do it for a two people lunch (Huen Phen is closed in the evening). The bill will still be under 15 $! It is located in Chiang Mai Old Town. I am told that there is another restaurant of the same name, on the same street (this may explain bad reviews). This Huen Phen, as you walk on Rachamankha Rd. from West to East, is on your left, a shack- like one story building with a small, darkish entrance that opens up on several sitting areas. You can also recognize it by the clusters of people on the street waiting for their number to be called. The wait is where Huen Phen drops the fifth star. But once you are at your table, the service is relatively fast and always with a good attitude. I'll be back there tomorrow. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My favorite place for lunch in Chiang Mai. Tied for best khao soi I've ever had, amazing laarb also. Everything I've tried is great. I've heard that at dinner they make it fancier and its not as good, but haven't tried dinner there myself.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The service was a bit off and aloof, I'll blame a holiday week but still.. Anyways, The Burmese style pork stew became my new favorite dish while in Chiang Mai area and Huen Phen delivers a very tasty version of this magical Thai stew! Order it! We were very Happy with food just not impressed with service, since everywhere else we we usually made to feel so welcome.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Get ready for mostly meat....Not for strict vegetarians as whether or not there is meat on the plate the food is most definitely cooked in meat fats or alongside meat. Tons of traditional foods on menu including sausages, pickled meat and other meat things. All very good and the place was packed to the gills with locals.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommended this restaurant as we stayed in a hotel nearby. Apparently popular as we had to wait some 20 mins and sweat it out before being given a table. There are plenty of antique items on display which added an ethnic look to the place. But can't understand the hype as the food they serve is nothing fantastic tasting. Maybe it's THE place to be seen eating out. Service was not impressive. And we got the feeling we should eat and go as there were others still waiting for a table. All in all - won't be back. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to the main restaurant at 6pm on a weekday and had to wait for 15 mins for a table. We ordered the sampler set for 2 and morning glory. it is super greasy food in tiny portions. Not sure why people are waiting outside for this place at all.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This review is for the Huen Phen located in the hotel. Our friends recommended this restaurant to us and we found it on Google maps. We ordered the 390 baht set for 2 and found it to be a fantastic introduction to Lanna cuisine. Portions were a little small but we added an extra bowl of rice and it was sufficient. Service was attentive and polite. We opted to sit outside in the balcony with its lovely view of Chiang Mai outside. Highly recommended. Note: We realised later that this was the branch and that there original Huen Phen is located a short 10min walk away. We visited the main branch but saw many bored patrons waiting to be seated or waiting for their food. Alarm bells went off and we immediately returned to the branch for a second time. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We know it's quite popular but waiting for like an hour I don't like it's worth it to do that though. Food was great as we ordered the set of 380 baht net and it completely make us full for 2 people.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had seen this resturant on youtube so when in town I came along one night for a meal. I already knew what I wanted so ordered, Am Kanun Larb khua moo Gaeng ho All very tasty and I enjoyed them all. Word of warning, you get a lot of Chinese visit this restaurant so get there early or you will have to wait over an hour sometimes for a table. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: a small restaurant serving Lanna style food.it is incorporated in the Baan Huenphen hotel.there are two or three tables on the outside deck that offers good views.food is good,prices are very reasonable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My fam and I came at around 6. So the line was not too long. The food was served quick and they tasted very delicious. But the portion was ˊtiny`.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Clearly anyone who complains about this place has expectations that are unrealistic! This restaurant captures true authentic Northern Thai flavors. With that said nothing is perfect! I highly recommend a trip here to eat. It's worth the wait. As a foreigner myself I don't believe in altering the taste to suit my palate...I want to eat the way the locals eat! So if you want to have it your way stay home and cook! Everyone else, Coke and enjoy!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Stayjng nearby on our first night in Chang Mai so popped in for dinner I had a green curry with beef my wife had tofu along with a couple of beers it was a good value tasty meal

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: When you see plenty of local families coming here on a weekend for a nice meal, you know you just have to check it out. Menu had plenty of photos and also in English and Chinese. Limited air-conditioned seating, but rest of the place was well ventilated. Food-wise, it is simply good value for money, especially compared to other similar restaurants along Ratchadamnoen which may be easier to find, but lacking in taste and authenticity to cater to foreign tastebuds. As Singaporean foodie, I can totally vouch for this place and paid them the compliment of patronising them twice in the space of 3 days. I will definitely come back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This basic eatery has a great range of northern Thai food. For 100 baht I had the jackfruit curry, sticky rice, a tea and dessert of mixed jelly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Double check your location. The Heun Phen restaurant and the eatery at the nearby Baan Huen Phen hotel are close to each other on opposite sides of the road This review describes the low grade restaurant. The ambience and decor are excellent but the food and service are lacking. After a 20 minute wait for a table we ordered a mixed starter that consisted of soggy pork crackling, cold beans and morning glory and a variety of greasy sausages and tofu. The dishes were barely adequate and definitely not the delicious Thai food one can get elsewhere in Chiang Mai. The soup we were served tasted of bland sweetened cabbage-water the Tum Yum soup was disgusting. No delicate herb flavours just brown chilli flavoured salty water. Around us the majority of customers left most of their meals. The photo shows an example of a table where two young girls grimaced at the taste and left. Yet out of presumed sympathy for the staff they still smiled and left a small tip. My soup consisted of an unappealing brown tasteless liquor with an appropriately brown-stained melamine spoon ( photo shown) This restaurant needs a re-think. Is it an authentic Thai restaurant or a disappointing holiday attraction? A decent Thai restaurant it definitely is not. Avoid the place unless you like antiques Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: As I say, you have such choice and quality in Chiangmai so this doesn't hit the mark. It's querky, yes, like sitting in an antique market but the food doesn't measure up. We ordered starters and mains and the mains came first, followed shortly by the starters. Nothing was hot, I'm guessing it sat for a while before serving, forgotten about likely. The flavours were dull and not quality. Service was okay but nothing special, just sums up the place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First roubd the pork was raw inside, needed to send it back, so what did they do? Re-fried the same thinh in smaller pieces!!! Food in general had nothing special, the restaurant is too hot! Do waste time and money here.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There are two restaurants called Huen Phen in Chiang Mai and they are on opposite sides of the road about 200m apart. This Huen Phen is NOT the one with a top floor restaurant, it is ground floor with no views at all. The one with the views is in the Baan Huen Pen hotel. Consequently the reviews here are all muddled up. Both have decent food!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Unexpecting that the price is quite cheap for a restaurant, they also provide authentic thai food here, overall still a good restaurant. The owner speak good english and provide great services

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was amazing and great value for money. The setting was beautiful. The drinks also great value. If you're in chiang mai you definitely have to visit this place

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: this old local eatery used to be a very delicious one. but now it's reputation brings travellers to fill it every meals time and this make the taste of it's foods change a lot to fulfil foreigners but the best things of this restaurant is the cheap price and the vast varieties of menu to try.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We dined at the Huen Phen at the top floor of Hotel Baan Huenphen. We were early and there was a couple waiting on the sidewalk outside the hotel. When we mentioned we wanted to have a couple of beers before dinner, the receptionist led us to a nice pool and we washed up (we came from a day of rock climbing) at the attached washrooms and enjoyed our beers. Thereafter we went to the top floor and chose to sit at the roof terrace with a great view of Wat Chedi Luang. We ordered the set dinner menu for 2 persons (a great bargain at 390THB for 2) and also added a less spicy Tom Yam soup. The presentation was great and we love the dishes. Service was ok and they did no have chilled glasses for beers. However, the view more then made up for that. Enjoyable and recommended. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: heaps of Thai-northern style dishes available here in a very cheap price such as: Kanom Jeen Nam Ngiaw (rice noodles with simmering pork in a mixture that includes a spice paste, dried herbs and halved cherry tomatoes) Guay-Tiaw-Kao-Soi (egg noodles with curry in coconut milk) This restaurant is quite popular ones in town, sometime you might have to wait too long for the table or maybe you can just call them to book for the table. the restaurant serves from 8AM - 3PM for breakfast and lunch and 5PM-10PM for dinner. It is very easy to get to this place because it's located near Chiang Mai town, you can grab a red took-took bus to the restaurant cost about 20 Bhat per person, or you might search "Huen Phen restaurant" from GPS if you wish to drive to the restaurant (car park are also available here - free of charge.) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've been here twice and service was always good and staff was very friendly. Average meal price is around 50 baht. Really like the kao soi (spelling?) and sausage. I did try to order the bananas in coconut milk twice and they were out both times but overall have been really happy with the food, service, and prices!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very poor service, food very salty and spicy. Good ambiance and decor though, and clearly a favourite on the tourist trail as was very busy even early evening. The waiters did not seem interested and we were disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: KHAO SOI!!!!! Delicious, spicy, Northern style soup you have to get in Chang Mai! This restaurant is very different lunch versus dinner (we went during both times). Go during the lunch timing, it's much more informal and crowded and the menu is also slightly different (read: cheaper). Would mostly recommend eating Khao Soi because it is soooooooooo good, but I also had a cashew spicy stir fry that while very spicy was excellent too.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is the place that my Thai friends took us to introduce us to Northern Thai food. It was amazing and if you haven't had Northern Thai food before you have to try it. Even if you have had it before this is a great place to get your fix.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious food and very cheap prices. If want to try Nortern Thai Food, this could be one of best place in town. Recommended Food : Mango Salad/Khao Soi (Beef preferable)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Enjoyed a great meal here, the combo starter was delicious, clean surroundings and good service. The price was good for what you get, we left having stuffed ourselves on the generous portions.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came here for lunch and were surprised to see a nice quiet place with good food and great service. Some food was hard to handle, it was spicy. But either way, great place to eat at!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is far from satisfaction - it is for tourists who do not mind waiting and eating overpriced small-sized food. Flies and mosquitos are everywhere in there. The waiters and waitresses are all amateurs who are not friendly to the customers. There are so many other places in Chiang Mai that are better than it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A great local restaurant, located in the old city of Chiang Mai that serves the local Northern Thai cuisine. I recommend to reserve a place since it's very full all the time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I came here one lunchtime in April and had an amazing Khao Soi for about 60baht. After returning to chiang mai with a friend this week and being on the tightest backpacker budget we decided we would go for a cheap dinner one evening. After walking for half an hour in the rain we realised only the posher restaurant part was open. We waited 20 minutes for a table which was fine but then we realised it was a different menu and way more pricey. And also to both of our disappointments.. they didn't even do Khao Soi! The main traditional northern dish and they dont even serve it! We decided to just embrace it as we were both soaked and couldn't be bothered to roam the streets looking for elsewhere. We both ordered pork curries.. pork was on the bone and my friend had 1 piece in her 'pork curry' and i had only a tiny bit more. Our meal was twice the price and twice as disappointing! We had to go straight to 7 11 afterwards as we were both still starving! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A tradditional decoration and northern songs while dining is awesome and and also the local food was fantastic! I'm Thai but never tried this before and found that it's a must for all who came here i Recommened it to you for sure

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Not very good. Can find easily better for cheap in Town. Rice And banana soup disgusted.Only the decoration is nice. I wont go Back again.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Excellent spot for cheap northern thai food. Quick, friendly service. Definitely get the Khao Soi, a specialty northern thai curry dish with some sticky rice (curry is only available at lunch). We even tried going back to this place a few times in our short time in Chiang Mai. Highly recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My husband and I were recommend this restaurant by our driver as we were looking for local Northern Thai cuisine. We were not disappointed! The decor in here is beautiful and it has a lovely atmosphere. The food was good value ranging from 80-150bhat and was very tasty. More than the standard phad Thai that you can get everywhere. Recommend the Burmese curry! Was so good we came here twice where the second time we had to queue (always a sign of good food!). Excellent fast service too!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Pretty yummy. This is where I fell in love with Khao Soi. Clean, nice, friendly and great chiang mai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went there attracted by the great interior decoration and the numerous waiting customers. Finally we made a booking to avoid waiting. The atmosphere inside the restaurant is stunning, the place is crammed with antique objects. However we were really disappointed by the food that was really do so so. The overall cleanliness is also to be improved. At the end of dinner we saw a rat climbing through decorative artefacts.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a great spot to try out some Northern Thai dish. It's a little pricey thou, you could get the same thing at the local market for more than half the price. I went on a Sunday evening just before the walking street, it's near by as well; that's a plus. It was a bit busy during that day, we didn't have to wait long. I think the restaurant in the evening is different to the one during the day. I like the atmosphere, and how the food was serve. Nice Lanna looking decoration all around. This would be a nice dinner date place. I would take my foreign friends here:0)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Just visited the place this evening, needed to wait for a while as there was a queue, asked the waiter about how long needed to wait, which was a fair question in my opinion but met with a glare from him and he put up his hand and told me that he could not tell. One star off. Waited about 10 minute and the other waiter put us at a place where someone else was sitting but just stepped away. Moved us to a cushion place where you'll have to sit crossed legged to eat, after we complained, then were moved to a proper place. Food was slightly above average but the service really killed the whole thing. Those who think this is a fine dining or up market restaurant be warned, they don't even have air-conditioning. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The draw of this restaurant is that you can sample northern Thai food, which is why I went there. It didn't disappoint. There are plenty of things on the menu that aren't available at other restaurants. The menu is also in English, and my waiter spoke decent English, so it's great for tourists who want to go beyond standard Thai dishes. The portions are rather small, which is great because it allows you to try multiple things, but not so great because you're still paying the same amount as you'd pay for a full entree at some other restaurants. The waitress was very nice and helpful in explaining how to eat the food, too. Make sure not to confuse this place with Baan Huen Phen down the road. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Amazing authentic northern Thai food! I don't think I had better Thai food at any other place my whole trip. Worth your time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I read about Huen Phen and its authentic Lanna fare on some travel blogs. It is not very difficult to locate, it is very close to Wat Chedi Luang. I reached bit late for lunch, but was lucky they hadn't closed. Thumb rule, went with whatever was recommended by the bloggers. To being with Kanom Jim, the supposed main dish of Northern Thai cuisine (pork cooked in mildly spicy tomato curry with noodles), Kao Ngew (rice cooked in pork blood with fried garlic/shallots), Young jackfruit in spicy paste (thinly cut jackfruit in lemongrass based curry paste, dry preparation) and Lemon-grass juice (must try). The best part is their portions are not very big, so you can try more the 2 options. Kanon Jim was nice, but adding some red chilli flakes worked better for me. Pork was nicely cooked. Kao Ngew was impressive, no odour of pig blood if you're worried about that. The jackfruit preparation was very close to how vegetables are cooked in India; absolutely loved it. Service was quick, wish I wasn't alone, else would've tried some more dishes. I've read that their fish wrapped in leaf is supposed to be good as well. Do check their timings before heading. A one time visit for sure. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: we stopped by this place on the way to the Sunday walking market; it was just a street away. it may have just been the dishes we ordered (and their combination) but each was just ok—we've had much better food at other restaurants in Chiang Mai. the egg dish (in the middle) involved a wonderful omelette (the perfect amount of subtle sweetness) but the fried rice it enveloped was very bland. the fried dish (can't remember if it was pork or chicken) on the left was a bit too salty for our taste and not particularly meat. etc. the place has multiple sections. the one were in was heavily decorated with interesting Thai art and icons, only fans running, so a bit warm after quite a bit of a walk on a hot summer day. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I'm not sure what other reviewers are saying but I loved this meal! It was our first meal in Chiang Mai right when we left the airport we went straight here. We ordered a bunch of different things! Kasoy, the infamous CM sausage, papaya salad, fried chicken wings, another stewed dish, sticky rice, and a good dessert wrapped in banana leaf with some sort of sticky rice and taro and toasted coconut! The service was fast and the food was great! No complaints on my end!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is packed during lunch time, we ordered khao soi, papaya salad, tom yum and fermented ribs. Taste was average, food (except khao soi) was prepared in advance so it was not hot when served. Papaya salad was very spicy and portion for khao soi was very small, I had to asked for 2nd helping.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Didn't enjoy the food here at all. The sausage was greasy n tasted horrible. We ordered the omelette (only dish that was decent), crispy pork salad, some fish dish n a sour soup. All the dishes were too salty n service was appalling. We waited for a long time until we decided to walk over to pass them our order. Won't be going back for sure!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Used to be one of the best northern Thai cuisine places in Chiang mai. Now with the explosion of Chinese tourism over the past few years and their virtual nightly booking out of this place, quality has suffered terribly. Attention to detail is gone. Prep' quality is almost non existent by Thai standards. The food is being thrown together and it is simply ordinary. Unfortunately this scenario is likely to replicated around town at many other quality eateries. Its time has passed. Leave it for the Chinese, look elsewhere

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant is in a prime location in the city and is easy to find. The place has a low key but nice, open atmosphere. The kitchen is visible from the sidewalk. Air conditioned seating is available. There is a wide variety of food and drink and everything we tried there was delicious. You certainly can't beat the prices here either. Each of us paid less than $3 for our food. Especially try the papaya salad. Definitely would recommend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food was some of the best I had in Thailand and the entire mean cost me less than $3 USD. I got the kow soy (my favorite Thai dish, specific to the north) and it exceeded my expectations. Don't miss it!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went here 3 times with my girlfriend (we enjoyed it so much the 1st time around). We went at lunch time all 3 times and had no problem getting as table right away - maybe it's different for dinner? We sat in the A/C interior room and once in the covered area outside the A/C'd room. We both really enjoyed the cooking here - one of my best meals in Chiang Mai for the 4 weeks total I've stayed here. Service was quick and we liked the carved lacquer work in the tables and on the walls in the A/C'd room. All in all a great place to eat if your in Chiang Mai!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is the only restaurant we have tried in Thailand where we had to wait before sitting down, there is a list outside the restaurant to manage the waiting people. This was a promising point and we were expecting something special. The ambient is nice, service is quite good and prices are not far from less pretending restaurants. They have more dishes typical of northern Thailand and sometimes with Burma influence that we only found there. Finally the taste and aspect of those dishes was not that good and that is decreasing the score I give to the restaurant. Maybe it was just a wrong choice from the menu.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Northern Thai food is very different from the coconut milk based curries i was normally used to. We ate at two Lanna restos, both excellent. one was Tong Tem Toh which was chicer than Huen Phen, and in a super cool neighbourhood. but Huen Phen, the second resto may have had the edge with the food. We ate a pickled pork dish, a red pork curry, a special rice dish made with curdled blood ( delicious, cant remember what its called and it just tastes slightly meaty) two salads and a stuffed grilled fish. really worth going. it made us realise how distinct northern thai food is

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I'd read great reviews so called in for lunch. The staff had no interest whatsoever in seating me, bringing me a menu, taking my order or bringing me any food. People who came in long after me had almost finished eating and I still hadn't received my meal. Who can explain it as other people seemed to do fine.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Fodor's recommended this place, so I went. The restaurant in the back that opens after 5pm was really neat. It had wonderful decorations. I had the Jackfruit salad that was delicious - I have no idea what was in it, but it was wonderful! Good prices. Staff did not speak English, so it was a bit difficult to communicate, but that was okay.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This renown lunch place is always packed at lunch time with both locals and tourists. Food is great and clean. I love their Nam Ngieow (spicy rice noodle soup with pork blood) and their dessert Tab Tim Krob (water chestnut in ice). Other dishes are also delicious and authentic.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went for lunch, so did not get to see the restaurant that is shown in the photographs. The lunch venue is pretty basic and unpreposessing and seems more like a canteen. However, the food is extremely cheap and tasty , but it is not a place you want to linger in. It was very busy though with both tourists and locals.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very traditional and very cheap, with amazing food. one of the best restaurants, very close to the temple

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: this rest is too famous but what I got is not as good as what I read from the website. fried rice too sticky, fried chicken is delicious but you can found same or better quality from other rest. Spicy soup with fish is special and taste good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is realy one of my favorit norther Thai restsurant, lunch time it is outside very simple seatted and bussy. In th evening it is in the back side, lovely decorated woth many Thai Lanna handicrafts and decore items, cosy ! Make sure you make a reservation as it can be bussy as well in the dinner time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First of all it's important to realise that Lanna or northern Thai cuisine is quite distinctive from the regular red, green, yellow curries & the padthais & fish cakes of Bangkok & the southern beach towns. if you go expecting that then prepare to be disappointed. But if you want to try authentic Lanna food this is the place to go. So delicious we ended up there for lunch 3 days out of 6. It was also encouraging to see a lot of locals dining there, indicating this is not a restaurant that caters to tourists. As some other reviewers pointed out there are 2 quite distinct menus for lunch & dinner & while both are good we far preferred the options available at lunch time. Our favourites in the lunch time menu --- 1) Khao Suai - delicious noodles in a light (coconut free) curry with your choice of meat. 2)Grilled fish in Basil leaves 3) Young jackfruit salad (unusual but scrumptious) 4) Northern thai sausage (full of flavours of lemongrass & coriander) 5) Eggplant salad with boiled egg (lovely smokey flavour to the eggplant). Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The Thai food here is great, reasonably priced, and very flavorful. Despite the fact that it is recommended by many guidebooks, it was an amazing meal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Despite being warned by my local Thai friend that this place was overrated, I still decided to try it since it is "the" place to go in Chiang Mai. Food was just ok. They had an extensive menu but it seemed that the price does not justify the taste. I ordered papaya salad and fish curry with rice. Maybe it was not their specialty. I had tried Lanna food with my friend somewhere else and it taste much better and cheaper than Huen Phen's. The interior decors were nice though. The tables and chairs were unique and the beer was served cold. Service was pretty quick - although it was already 9pm when I got a seat. Had to wait for almost 30 or so mins. Go to the Sunday night market instead. Lots of Lanna food to try and honestly there's nothing to miss out if you haven't had the chance to try Huan Phen. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: People in line often get frustrated that there are empty tables. They know their kitchen capacity and take a list of names. That list is how many people they'll be able to serve until closing. So if you're on the bottom of a full list then you'll have a long wait. The food is very good but we had nothing that was amazing.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am just back from the northgate night market because I had to eat something decent to forget about the food I was served at Huen Phen. We ordered 4 dishes and all of them were awful, we didn't do more than take a taste, paid 300 and left the place without eating. Not sure if the deliberately served us a farang version of there food, but it was not spiced at all, the crispy pork for 50Bath was cold, the dishes had nothing to do with the pictures and the service was uninspired. There are some many VERY GOOD restaurant in chiang mai, don´t bother to go here if you want to eat thai food and not some farang version with no garlic, no spice, no chilli, no taste at all. We ordered crispy pork (got served the pork skin you get in big packages everywhere, no sauce and cold), Tomato minced pork relish (advertised as spicy again no taste at all, like a convenient store bolognese), Curry with banana flowers and pork (the taste is best described as rancid, uneatable. Again no flavour, no herbs, no garlic, nothing) and a eggplant egg Northern style salad (tasteless, the eggplant from a can) All in really one of the worst thai food exp. in my live. Every street stall in chiang serves better food than this place. Ok, they have tablecloth(cleaned once a month?) and a few buddhas placed on the wall, but I take any plastic stoled TASTY place for this. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First you need to know there is a differnent scene at lunch vrs dinner. The lunch is served at the front and very busy at night they close this and you move to a more intimate local that has loads of character and I think very good service. Either way the foods are well prepared and tasty, lower cost but a wee bit small. I think of it as "Thai Dim Sum" you can order many dishes and taste them amongst friends for a varried meal that in the ned cost about the same as others, you just need multiple dishes to satisfy a hunger. I really enjoyed the Egg Plant and Chicken dish, The curried Pork and morning glory. Enjoy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: In a week I think I ate there like 4 times. There is a huge choice of Thai food. Northen Thai food. If you like to try different meal that'S the place to be cause it's cheap and all food is good.. I mean all food I have tried is good. You should try the Northen sausage, fried papaya salad, nam tok moo, khao sow, fried porc rib etc.... You won't be decieved. If I can give you a tip, don't wait for the waiter to come to take your order or bring you the bill, you have to raise your hand or something when you are ready. And by the way, go there in the afternoon, cause at night, it'S closed, but they operate another restaurant just beside, with same menu, but it's really smaller and a long line-up. Bon appetit !!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went here because a local shopkeeper recommended it. Cafeteria style. Packed with locals and tourists. So it must be good right? Wrong. I ordered the Chicken with Vegetable stirfry and steamed rice. Got my food in 2 minutes which means it was obviously not cooked fresh. My chicken stirfry had 2 tiny peices of bones (with little chicken on it). It tasted awful. I actually couldn't eat it and went somewhere else to eat.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is recommended to all westerners by hotel, tuk-tuk drivers etc., which basically is a very bad sign. On our first evening we followed the recommendation of the Le Meridien concierge and walked all the way to Huen Phen. At arrival we saw a lot of westerners, but no asian people, waiting in-line. You've to write yourself into a reservation-book and wait. We did so but then decided to go inside to have a look at the food. The first thing we recognized is the interior design. It's made as first timers expect a Thai restaurant to be. The menu was pretty basic and they seem to cater the westerner taste, not very authentic. We decided to leave and look for another, more authentic restaurant with real Thai food. My tip: in case you're looking for a restaurant, go to MR. KAI, on the same street, not far away. In case you're looking for just fantastic food go to the north gate, turn left until you'll see a place with many, many food stalls. We had the best food (SUKI) there. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Busy place....you can't book so arrive early for lunch or dinner. Set up at lunch uses whole restaurant. At night just the bavk bit which is beautiful, but missing some of the bustle of the full deal. Highlight was pickled pork and egg, ate one, ordered another. Good food, bit dumbed down for tourists so not enough spice maybe....but top notch cooking nonetheless. Will be back!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Not much information regarding their queueing system, there should be a sign up that explains or someone at the door to help as people wandering inside trying to find out whats going on. Waited around 30 mins for a table which isn't bad, got seated on a lovely table place looks really good however the service really lets this place down. We waited a while for our food which we wouldn't mind as they were busy but our food was stone cold when it came and they only brought us the curry And no rice we had to sit and wait a further 10 minutes until we asked someone for our rice. Food was nice. Just a shame about the service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were a group of six. Heard about this place from other reviewers. and it was very near the Rachamankha where we stayed. When we arrived there was a long queue, a sure sign that the food was good. We put our name on their q system and waited for about 40 mins. After getting a table we ordered something like 12 dishes off the menu. Most of the dishes were quite different from the southern Thai food we know so well. This was northern Thai. We have no complaints about the service as our food came shortly after we placed our order. What was best was the price. Everything was so cheap. Dinner costs us only THB 1400 for so many dishes. Definitely worth a visit if you are in the area. Its just on Ratchamnkha road.. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: changed a lot over the past few years. evening service is now in an area seperated from the canteen, more expensive and not as nice. lunch still good and worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: When we arrived there were around 15-16 couples in the waiting list. We decided to wait but after 45 min. we went out for a short walk... apparently during those 5 min. the waiter called us out but we didn't hear anything!! When we went inside only one couple was called after us, we tried to explain the situation and were told to go to the end of the queue!?!! They asked us to wait but after 15 min. nobody came to help us. Never again, big dissapointment. Go at your own risk.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We take several portions per persons in order to try different local dishes, and it was tasty, do not expect to meet the european standart (most of all regarding the service)!!! In term of quality and price it was a good diner.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After a late morning Sunday Mass at the Cathedral, my wife & I took a long walk to Huen Phen. All the locals we asked directions for knew of this restaurant and enthusiastically pointed us in the right direction. On arrival it was crowded & we put our names on a piece a paper and eventually got called up for our table. An extensive menu and good to see so many locals enjoying lunch. Loved the Khao Soi and the Papaya Salad with Salted Egg. We came back for dinner with our sister-in-law from the UK and were disappointed to find the Restaurant closed where we had lunch earlier on. Then discovered that Dinner was at another venue off a side lane. What a delightful restaurant with homely old world Thai decorations. And again worth the wait for a table. A lovely way to spend our last night in Chiang Mai savouring a memorable meal. Typically Northern Thai and so YUMMY and reasonably priced! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great authentic northern/Burmese food. The spicy Burmese chicken was really good. The staff were really lovely & the surroundings were very interesting lots of artefacts. Worth a visit but be sure to book a table.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was there for dinner with a group of friends. We were 4th on the waiting list, but had to wait about 2hours to get a table. The restaurant seems to serve all tables at a go, and only receive another group when all the tables in the first round have finished their meal, instead of receiving customers as and when a table is cleared. The food was just average and definitely not worth the wait and price. Looking at the decor and menu, this restaurant seems to be more catered to the tourist crowd.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice Burmese style food in an older wooden house. The food was not your typical Thai so it was a nice break. Note: it's a little spicy. Get there early as it doesn't seat many people.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Extremely popular authentic Thai restaurant. We had to wait 10 minutes for a table but it was worth it. I had the Khao Soi & my wife had the Pappaya Salad with a side dish of eggplant, all of which were delicious. The food plus 2 bottles of water came to 160THB. A really good place to go for lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our hotel highly recommend this for Northern Thai food. We went for dinner. It is popular and there is no reservation system. When you get there you put your name on a list and wait in a somewhat tatty front area - not even offered to purchase a drink. We were told 1 hour wait but as they seem to take people through in whatever order they like it was only 30 mins. .Didn't understand this as we could see in the restaurant there were empty tables. The issue seemed to be that the staff were hopeless - not clearing /resetting tables. We were put at a table at the back right next to the loos, kitchen and admin desk. Lots of waiters hanging around this area you would think would give attentive service - but not. The guys just didn't give any attention to their jobs - chatting to each other etc. We had to call them over for everything and then again to remind them of what we had asked for (drink, order food, cutlery, bill etc). The food itself was poor. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great northern Thai food at this slight rundown looking popular and crowded restaurant. They have a huge menu, which comes in English, thou the staff might not be able to speak a word of it. Just point to what you want and you’ll be ok. Do try the hors d'oeuves variety plate so you can try a range of northern foods. Its only 85 bath and enough for 2 to 4 people.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One of the local girls working at my hotel recommended Huen Phen for authentic Northern Thai Cuisine and it was spot on. All of the locals seem to know Huen Phen ( I found out as I was lost a few times). It is conveniently located within the old walls. I had the Khao Soi, which was delicious and cold lemongrass herbal "tea"/drink ( highly recommend this). My meal was inexpensive. I actually almost went back for dinner, but was running late for a tour. Service was quick and courteous, both to take my order and to receive my food. Highly recommend for authentic Northern Thai cuisine. Will be back again!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was the first meal we ate after landing in Chiang Mai. Finally, the delicious (and cheap!) food we had been expecting from Thailand! Our Groovy Map pointed us here, and we walked over from our hotel for lunch. We had read that they had amazing northern-style food, so we ordered northern Thai style spicy minced pork salad and the sausage. It was our first time ever eating northern style food, and it was amazing. Those are two dishes you must try! The quality of the food was excellent, and we were very happy with the prices (I seem to remember the lunch dishes being under $5 each, and they were large). Our table was in a back corner so it was a bit tough to get the server's attention at times. But this tended to happen in most places- you just have to be a bit more obvious in trying to get the server's attention. It was low season when we visited (in September), so while the place was fairly busy, we did not have to wait for a table. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Location: fantastic Food: awesome Service: ok Portions: small Price: great Basically what everyone else said. The food is awesome, everything else is bit off. To be fair, it was super busy for lunch... We got a table right away though. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were decidedly underwhelmed by Huen Phen - not that it wasn't GOOD, it was perfectly fine - but the reviews were so positive that we had high hopes. We actually had a fairly difficult time finding good restaurant food in Chiang Mai, and had our best meals on the street and at our hotel breakfast. But sometimes you want a nicely drawn out dinner in a nice space, and Huen Phen offers that. We went for dinner and sat inside in a room filled with great Thai antiques. I'd recommend it, but don't go in with huge expectations.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: When we got to Chiang mai, we immediately asked for a local place to have Khao Soi, we were led to this place. It was almost 2pm and the place was still packed. We signed for the waiting list and were accomodated after an hour. We were given a shared table with strangers because the place was still packed. We sat around for 10 minutes and finally our orders were taken. Then we waited again for 30 minutes because they forgot our orders. I was beginning to get pissed, I was hungry and I cant wait anymore. When our orders were finally served, the moment my lips met the khao soy broth, i forgot all the waiting we did. It was soooo good. Probably the best khao soi i had ever. I wouldnt mind getting back to this place again and again, but next time I think I have to be wise and go there when its not really packed. And they dont serve khao soi for dinner. I dont know with other restaurants in Chiang Mai Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Make a booking! But it is still worth the wait which was about an hour for dinner. The restaurant is very interesting, like being in a very eclectic lounge. The food is very different to most food in Thailand, and great. It seems like the restaurant is run by 3 people so service is slow but still worth it for the food. If you stay at Baan Huen Phen you get a discount.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The Hotel concierge recommended this restaurant, although we did not have to wait long for a table we were sat right outside the toilet and out of sight out of mind. Our order was taken quickly and not delivered - we had to ask for our drinks and only one dish out of five was delivered and after 30 minutes we had to ask where our food was? I was very ill after lunch running to the toilet twice in the restaurant before leaving. So hopefully dinner would have been a better experience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our dinner was preceded by a 45 min wait to be seated. We assumed it must be something special because of so many people waiting to eat there. There was a mix of Thai and foreigners, so we thought it must be something special in the neighborhood. The food was pretty good and some unique flavors of northern Thailand. The larb was great and quite spicy. The curry was average and the rice was overcooked. The fried chicken was disappointing. The glass noodle salad was okay. Overall I wouldn't come back here. There are so many good choices around, this was just by the best. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had lunch here on our first day in Chiang Mai at the recommendation of the receptionist at our hotel. It was quite busy and we had to wait for a table, but not for too long (approximately 20 minutes). Once we were seated, it took ages to get someone over to the table to take our order, but once we had placed our order, the food arrived fairly quickly. We tried two types of khao soi as well as Chiang Mai sausage. Both were quite good and a great introduction to the local cuisine, although the khao soi wasn’t quite as good as some we tried elsewhere in the city later during our visit. It also took a while to get the attention of a waiter to pay our bill at the end of the meal. Overall, the food was quite good, but it took a lot of effort to get any service here despite the number of staff members on the floor. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: this restaurant serves northern Thailands food which you have to taste at least one time if you in Chiangmai. Warning: the food is very spicy. There are another restaurants which changed food taste to not spicy with hope to attract foreigners but in Huen Phen they cook traditional recipes. You can see many locals here which is good sign. Actually there are two restaurants with one kitchen. During the day there is more simple designed huge restaurant open after 5pm opens beautiful small restaurant hidden inside the big one. Not five stars because it got very touristy. Portions are getting smaller and prices higher with the popularity. Anyway recommended. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited Huen Phen for our first meal in Chiang Mai and enjoyed everything we tried. We asked the waitress for recommendations and soon found ourselves with a table full of new tastes (all washed down with local beers of course). The standouts were the Khao Soi and the local sausage with sticky rice. We arrived at 2:30pm and were seated right away. The crowd looked to be a mix of locals and tourists. Pricing was extremely reasonable and service was efficient if not exactly friendly. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Definitely a must do place, but a reservation is probably key. We went one night and it looked like utter chaos, so we left. We had our hotel make a reservation the next time and then we had no problem. Great food and cool ambiance.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Due to rave reviews for this restaurant, we decided to try it out. The line was long but the wait was not bad. We waited about 15-20mins. This place is very local so don't expect a nice ambience. The food was decent but not so amazing that I would come back a second time. We tried the kao soi which was different from what we usually have. The hot food were all served warm to cold so I didn't really like that. Also, do not expect good service as the servers are really just serving you food and cleaning up so they can get the next batch of customers in. There are so many other great restaurants to try in Chiangmai so unless you have an ample amount of time, I would suggest to go elsewhere. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Liked the grilled pork very much. Had fish, chicken, pork and kow soi everything was ok nothing jaw dropping but it was a solid meal. I'd recommend Huen Phen for a northern Thai meal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am not Thai, so would not have a clue of what "authentic" northern thai food is...but I did enjoy the beef curry with egg noodles and sticky rice along with the pork sausage..washed down with a nearly cold Singha or two. Service was slow...it seemed until business slowed down a little was when us out of towners got some attention. Overall..an average meal.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was highly recommended for northern style Thai food and it seemed a good choice when we arrived judging by the extensive queue. It was NOT. A victim of it's own success, it mostly caters for Chinese tourists with some westerners and BKK tourists. The food was very bland and had an atmosphere of fast food, quantity over quality. Don't believe the hype, this place is bad.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Looks and acts like a local place. Food was excellent and very authentic. It was incredibly cheap to eat there as well.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: excellent authentic Thai food northern style. Different menus during lunch than dinner. Lunch is sort of quick Thai street food mentality set in a restaurant whereas dinner is set in a different part of the restaurant where you can take a nice date to dinner. Check for hours open as they close for a few hours each day between lunch and dinner. Things to try at lunch is a must...the best khao soi in town! During dinner tho there is no khao soi on the menu, order the chicken, pork, nham prick ong, nham prick noong, and of course some sticky rice (kao mui) amoung other things. You won't be disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: the best meal i have had in a long time...yet strangely when i went back the next day the Khao Soy was still fantastic, (melt off the bone chicken) yet the guava salad was not as fabulous. I still think this is amazing and I have been traveling and trying street food. and the price is one of the lowest in town, authentic feeling, old ladies preparing the food, lemongrass tea is a bit sweet as is all Thai drinks.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We dined here on recommendation from local people and would recommend booking at night, the place is very busy and seems slightly chaotic. The food is of a good standard and they have plenty of choice and menu's in English. Prices are slightly higher than other places in the area and drink prices are quite expensive, but friendly and enjoyable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The cool interior is nice, yet I travel solo and they didn't seem to serve me as quickly as larger parties. Anyway, I went there with Gabe from Elie Bum B&B and had a wonderful meal. The service is simple, the food was pretty amazing, and having a Thai tell me about the food was fantastic. My favorite was trying all of the herbs, bettle leaf, acacia, etc. http://www.thereluctantexpat.com/2012_09_23_archive.html

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I do not understand the sheeple mentality that keeps this place among the top restaurants reviewed in Chiang Mai for authentic Northern Thai cuisine. If you like being surrounded by dusty old quasi-antiques, then maybe it seems real. It's not. There are much better places for the real food of Chiang Mai: in town, Hong Tauw on Nimmanhaemin has been serving a consistent menu that includes Northern Thai and Central Thai food carefully prepared, small, but larger that Huen Phen portions, and well-priced. A short trip out of town (near San Kamphaeng) on Rt.1317 to Huen Jai Yong will take you to a real Northern Thai restaurant- the menu is only in Thai and most of the customers will be Thai. To arrive after noon is to miss some favorite dishes as they sell out fast to this crowd. Sticky rice is the vehicle for wonderful 'gaeng hung lay' or other flavorful dishes. Their sausage (sai oua) is world-class. Take a Thai friend for a truly local experience. The art gallery in back is an added bonus. This is a family affair: wife runs the restaurant and husband is an artist/musician. A very special, magical combination. Lunch only. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ordered a northern starter platter, a "northern thai" pork rib soup, and fried pickled pork. The only things edible on the table are the steamed vegetables in the starter platter and fried pork which is just shredded pork made into meatballs then fried, nothing special. Being extremely unsatisfied with the meal, we ordered the "signature" fried chicken, which is just a discount version of fast food fried chicken in sweet chilli sauce. I really don't understand the hype about this place. Whatever they are serving, its definitely not northern thai food. In fact, the pork rib soup cannot even be called food. It was absolutely disgusting. There are lots of wonderful choices in Chiang Mai, why waste your time with this one? Even Burger King in old city could have offered me a better food experience than this. You may read the review thinking I'm too harsh and "authentic" northern thai food is "some likes it, some hates it", and you are going to try anyway. Simply, don't, save yourself, please. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My wife and I came here for dinner on the back of the reviews and left completely confused and disappointed. The ambience was great ... that is all. The food is ordinary and tastes nothing like how Northern Thai food was described to us. It was relatively cheap but the servings were small. The service was above average but not great. All in all, we left wondering why so many travellers rated it so highly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Nice cosy restaurant. Not some fancy restaurant touting traditional fair. This is a family restaurant that has been around for years I guess. Come here for a taste of authentic northern thai food without having to worry about hygiene as compared to street food. Very reasonably priced as well. Try their sets for a little of everything.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Lovely restaurant...beautifully set up, lovely decor, nice ambiance, good local northern Thai food....Superb. BUT.... Service need to improved!!! Very poor service probably short of staff as this place tends to be very crowded during lunch and dinner period. I had to take the menu personally from the counter and had to call for the staff for ordering... But things took a turn when the crowd fizzles away... it's like Dr Jekyll & Mr Hyde.... Well.....still 2 thumbs up but it will be 2 thumbs and 2 toes up if their service is improved. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The place was bustling when we arrived. There were people waiting to be seated as the place was full. We did not mind waiting a while, so we wrote our name in the waiting list. 5 minutes later we got to a table that was being cleaned. Luckily we had grabbed a menu from the pile while waiting, as it seemed that it took quite a while before the menus were brought to the people sitting. Ordered pomelo salad, some thai aubergine with meat, kao soi and fried spare ribs. The kao soi and the spare ribs arrived in a reasonable time considering the chaos that took place around us. Unfortunately they were average and did not offer any culinary experiences. After we had finished eating these two dishes the pomelo salad arrived, which was extremely spicy - though that was to be expected. The aubergine dish never arrived. The service was impersonal, we were not greeted or pretty much told anything else during the stay. We got more help from other customers in the place. Prices are low, the whole lunch for two cost 200 baht - and they did not even try to charge the aubergine dish, so at least that went ok. Definitely no reason to return. You will find similar prices and decor in Aroon Rai, but the food is many times better and even the service more personal. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Skipping one tourist attractions, we decided to go for lunch. We ask the driver to introduce and here he brought us. Really good northern Thai dishes. There are spicy and non spicy type. Must try the fried chicken and fried fish..yummy. Their English is really bad. Only one or two can understand when you take order. Quite crowded during lunch time and mainly local people visit their restaurant.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant belongs to the same people who own Baan Huen Phen, a new boutique hotel just 200m down the road. The restaurant has a fine dining look and feel but with affordable prices! We had dinner for 3 here one night - rice, 3 dishes and water came up to RM28. And since we stayed at Baan Huen Phen, we got a discount too and our bill for 3 came to RM25! Generally though food in Chiangmai is cheaper than Bangkok. Don't expect lots of spice though. I think Northern Thai food is milder on the chili scale. Most dishes in Chiangmai are pork-based. I would come back again if only to try out more dishes. They didn't allow us to make reservations though. That was the only weird policy. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Service is not hospitable. And the food is just so-so, it is traditional, and cheap, but with small amout. not recommended.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Been here over the past 30 years. The food quality and taste has been the same. They offer authentic tradtional Northern Thai food, with absolutely good tastes. This is always my favorite stop whenever I visit Chiangmai. The price is also very reasonable. :)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am mystified that anyone gave this a better than poor rating. This is ordinary at best and poor at worst. A fast food Thai place is better. I travel in Thailand a lot and have had cheap good food but this was a zero. The best aspect of the meal was the music they were playing (jazz) and the decor of the restaurant. Service was fine. The food was poor, and I'm being kind. It is cheap and perhaps that is the selling point, but that is all. In fact the Singha beers were higher priced than at my hotel. Pass, there are so many better places in Chiangmai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We came to this restaurant twice. One for lunch and one for dinner. In the lunch time, as mentioned in Lonely Planet, it is just a restaurant with many tables and chairs. The meals is pleasant for us although we had waited for a long time. When we went to ChingMai for the second time, we had dinner here. To our surprise, the arrangement inside is much different to our first time. Many thailand-style antiques were put around the tables . You may appreciate them when you are eating. The fired chicken was enjoyed by my little son. It is not difficult to find this place .It locates behind the wat chedi luang. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: the food here taste good and cheap. i like tom yam kung. taste just nice. and the environment was unique.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had lunch at this very busy restaurant and were not disappointed. The food was hot delicious and came to the table quickly and with a smile. The khao soy was very tasty and although the portions were not overly large it was perfect for lunch. Will definitely go back!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ordered 4 items. First, the room temperature northern style curry, it's different from the normal curries with lots of chillies and coconut milk. Nice, just didn't appeal to my palette. Followed by the northern soup with pickled pork has a certain tartness to it, that will whet your appetite. The other dish looks like Chinese kaikan stir fried with BBQ pork. Okay but nothing special. The last one bring fried rice with shrimp was at room temperature which was not appetizing at all. Service was super fast, it's almost like cafeteria fast. Doesn't gel with the decor and my thought of having slow food in Chiangmai... my bad. Would I go there again? Probably not. Oh, and the queue. 1 hour at least. Happy waiting. But I'll go there for the lunch! And that's another story. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Lunch here has always been a culinary delightful experience. The ambience is nothing much to shout about but the level of service and consistently good flavour of its offerings continue to satisfy the repeat customer. We had delicious khao soy, fried chicken, pork meat loaf wrapped in banana leaf and vermicelli in spicy curry sauce (without coconut milk).

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We asked the concierge at the hotel to recommend a great restaurants where the locals go, this was it. It's always busy so be prepared for a line to get in, but it was delicious and really great to try what the Chiang Mai locals eat. The food comes out quickly and is cheap. I'd recommend huan phen to anyone who wants a local restaurant experience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is a bit of a hole in the wall but all the locals seem to know it. Go there for khao soi - a tasty dish with two kinds of noodles and your choice of meat. The prices are so low you might ask yourself whether you really can have quality food for less than $2 a bowl. You can. I also had the green papaya salad, a favorite dish of mine. Its a bit different than in Bangkok, but still tasty with tamarind and nam pla. A word for the American palate: the juices here (and all over Thailand) are very sweet. I tried the chrysanthemum juice, it was so sweet my teeth hurt!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After trying and failing to find a TA reviewed place, called Lemongrass, on my GPS, my wife asked a tuk tuk driver for a Northern Thai restaurant that locals go to and tourists are few. We followed him and settled in for a great lunch of northern specialities. All in all, we had 8 dishes including Khao Soy (the signature coconut based noodle curry dish), ground chilly w/ vegetables and pork skins, grilled chicken, sliced pork w/spicy sauce, esarn sausage w/spicy sauce, etc. There are very few decent northern Thai restaurants where we live so this was a real treat. The only complaint I have is the sliced pork was cut too thick for my taste and the esarn sausage was a little dry. The other dishes were excellent. Any complaint you may read about the food will usually be from one whose palate is not attuned to this northern style cuisine. The only tourists we saw were those brought by an individual tour guide as this place is not located where the busses can roll up. Not easy to find so do as we did and have a tuk tuk driver take you---it is well known by local people. The service was excellent as we had a very energetic young lady whose aim was to please. Best of all is the price---the dishes are modest size and the 8 we had was only an extremely modest 450 Baht. If it were double, I would gladly pay it! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Came to Huen Phen with a friend who lives in Chiang Mai. A little difficult to find. Sat inside in the AC room where there was a sink to wash hands. We ordered too much food including kowsoy, a meat and pickle sampler, meat wrapped in bamboo leaf, sticky rice, young papaya salad, and drinks. We split the bill to about 150 baht each. It was all tasty, well-spiced, each dish tasted distinct, and would have been cheaper if we didn't have so many dishes.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want to taste the real Northern/Lanna kitchen ,this is the place to be in Chiang mai....the decoration itself is allready worth visit.Because the restaurant is used as a location in the famous movie 'Lost in Thailand'...its very famous for Chinese tours\ists ,so reservation is a must do.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to Huen Phen at the suggestion of some friends and a cooking class instructor! The food was very tasty and the service was attentive to our needs. While you can find cheaper in Chiang Mai, Huen Phen definitely doesn't break the bank. Tip: Sit inside the air conditioned section of the restaurant to beat the heat!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Enjoyed a nice lunch here during a recent visit to Chiang Mai, stayed in the hotel down the street with the same name, also a good experience, Try the Northern Thai sausage plate, four types of sausage and compliments, excellent and a good value for a sit down restaurant, Closed Sundays

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: the food looks horrible, looks like just from the other table, taste bad, for this restaurant, it may be famous by its decoration.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to thus place twice. The first time for diner in the restaurant part. The staff was uninterested as the waiter was yawning while taking our order. We ordered a second round but they didn't bother to clear away the dishes of the first round. Food tasted ok not really special. We came back the next day for some lunch because we heard some good reviews about the lunch. I must say we enjoyed the lunch much better then diner. Don't know why maybe because of the staff...

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is one of my favorate restsurants to eat northen food, especially at lunch time, the restaurant is busy every day, the food and service is just great?
