I enjoy to go there since over 23 years and the food is every time just " arrrroy" which means in Thai just great! I love the green mango salad nampric ong as well, the northen "sayhua" is just great. Kao Soy is nice as well or pomelo salade with eglant and lemongrass to trink the green cress " bay bua bok" Enjoy it to!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Really authentic dishes, all very cheap. Wait staff also spoke english effectively. Great if you're looking for local food!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is well-known in Chiangmai's old city. This is not only a local favorite, but a popular place with tourists as well. Our tuktuk driver highly recommended this place and informed us that this place has the best Northern cooking. We tried the Northern speciality of beef curry with pieces of solidified pig's blood and a fish dish cooked in banana leaf. We also had ordered papaya and noodle salads. The food is ok and not that remarkable although their fresh lemongrass juice was out of this world while their coconut dessert was so refreshing. But, I can't really complain. The food is so cheap and very hearty. Each dish cost us about 45 baht ($1.40). So you can't beat that. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Read some reviews and we decided to check this place out. We ordered the fried chicken, ribs, sausage and a curry noodle, two iced teas all come as 300 bhat, very cheap!! If you want something cheap and okay food, this might be the place, but if you are looking for somewhere slightly tastefully decorated or friendly service, I'd go somewhere else.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant is located in the downtown. If you stay in the downtown, you can go there easily by walk or the red bus( Songtaew). The food is really delicious and they are exactly northern Thai menu. I went there at night, I totally like their restaurant decoration, it's very antique and unique. such a romantic place, They also provide some private space if you wanna have a romantic night with ya lover.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great food for cheap prices. Strongly recommend the set menu for 2, absolutely exquisite, and provides you with a knowledge of Thai food you may like for the rest of your stay.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good food, Try the Koy Soy and the flavoury and spicy sausage. Good prices. Busy at lunchtime. Difficult to get a table for dinner.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Fancied some genuine local cuisine so tried this place. Was seated at the back of the restaurant in fantastic surroundings. However had to wait an age to attract the attention of the of the waiter to get the table cleared and ask for a menu three times. Ordering food suffered the same ordeal. It's as if they had blinkers on. Food was delicious but very few vegetarian options available. The very obvious request for a tip was unashamedly ignored by myself. If the service improved this place would be brilliant.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great restaurant. Head inside and eat in the well decorated and ornate room. Must try the fried chicken appetizer, best chicken I think I have ever had. We also had the sausage which wasn't overly exciting and the spare ribs which were delicious. Also had the special fried rice which was essentially an omelette with rice, not the best. The pork curry was great. Try a few things with the star next to it (most popular). Service was lacking in some members of staff but that would be down to the language barrier. Would recommend. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We tried this restaurant and it was a good experience. Customers were nice and fast You can try good thai local food Atmosphere was fine, some local thai people and kids because infront there is a school

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is one of the most popular local restaurants for trying northern delicacies. It's often crowded, but the good taste never compromise. Price is very reasonable and service is friendly and fast. Highly recommended for trying unique northern style Thai food, as it is not easily available in Bangkok.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A favorite haunt for those seeking authentic northern style cuisine with unique flavors and takes on local dishes. The collection of rooms and layers of antiques, artifacts and decorations add to the dining experience. The set dinners offer a great way to try out a variety of tastes.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is our 6th visit to Chiangmai Mai & till tonight Huen Phen has always been our favourite. We arrived early evening, maybe 10 other pax there. Taken to a table promptly & given a menu. A good 5 minutes later another couple were seated at the table next to us. We're still trying to catch one of the many waiters eye. Table next to us received drinks, still can't get a waiter to look at us, table next to us then gets their meal.... So we got up & left after sitting there 20 min, VERY DISAPPOINTED when we'd waited so long to return here with such anticipation.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: this place is awfully crowded with noisy Chinese tourists... khao soy wasn't good at all... they let customer smoke inside the restaurant as well! horrible!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went for our first dinner in Chiang Mai here but our experience wasn't great. The food is average and tiny portioned (too spicy and heavy for us) and location difficult to find in the dark. The ambience seemed authentic but full of tourists.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Note that the Dinner outlet is different from the Lunch outlet. Also, the menus are different for Lunch vs Dinner. e.g. khao soi is not available for dinner as it is deemed a lunch item (ok, am I the only one who didn't know that!) While a little "touristy", this is a local-food place that serves northern thai food in a local style (no Tom Yum here. That's not northern. Know your North vs South, else you might start a war!). The curries are also not as think and creamy because that just isn't the northern style. It is more like a spicy soup. No coconut milk because there are no coconut tress in the northern thai highlands! The absolute price of stuff here isn't expensive. If you're comparing to street stalls, certainly there's a premium. Compared to the restaurant at Le Meridian down the road, this is cheap. 2 of us ordered 6 items total Plus 2 local beers and the bill was less than US$20. In summary, knowing what to expect when you dine here will go a long way towards enjoying your meal - Northern Thai styled food. Not quite the same as the stuff you get in Bangkok. I would definitely go back there on my next trip to Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If we spent a few more nights in Chiang Mai I probably would have returned here a few more times! Recommended to us by a Thai couple we met during our afternoon travels, this place did not disappoint. The restaurant itself is interesting a cute. The food was fantastic. We ate Burmese style curry, some cured meats, their special spicy salad, and a veggie dish-- all were amazing. We did have to wait awhile before we oredered, and then again to pay, but didn't care. Want to go back!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Enjoyed my meal (dinner) at Huen Phen. It felt authentic and carefully prepared. While the service is a tad slow, I didn't mind as there's a lot to look at. Really enjoyed the breeze coming in the window and the relaxed quirky antique atmosphere.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by our favorite ... And really disappointed guide! The food arrived cold, especially in small amounts and frankly poor quality! While almost everything is good in Thailand, it's a shame to dine in this restaurant. The curry was tasteless. The crispy pork is downright bad (this is especially the fried fat) and the risk is a cold sticky mass ... The service is slow and not really pro. Avoid!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I can't rave enough about this place. Went here for lunch and had the Khao Soi, Sai Krok (Thai Sausage) and Mango Sticky Rice. Best flavor I had on my entire vacation in Thailand. You could tell that it was good by how many locals were eating there as well. This is my most memorable eating experience in Thailand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Guide books and Trip Adviser rave about this place but my wife and I found the food to be less than stellar. More of a tourist oriented place, prices were high for what you get and the food was not as good as we have had in orher places of less renown that are hakf the price. All in all a disapointing experience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My wife, who is from Bangkok, and I went there for dinner during a recent visit to Chiang Mai as it was recommended by one of our tour guides. It seems like the place caters mostly to tourists as it was filled with mostly chinese tourists and a few caucasians. We didn't see many Thais. The service was excruciatingly slow - we waited for a good ten to fifteen minutes just to get menus. The food was just OK. My wife wasn't crazy about it at all. We did experience a bit of a language barrier with the wait staff as they spoke neither english nor Thai. I don't think we'd return as we had experienced better meals elsewhere in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited this restaurant based on good views but was totally disappointed. It is such set up for tourists, service was slow and rude. The decoration inside was very exotic but tables are really close to each other so they can fit more tables inside. The food came in such a small portion and I can’t believe we paid that price for it. Tastes was also just average. We had so many better meals in Chiang Mai than at Huen Phen. Don’t go there if you want authentic local food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: All I can say is don't go at dinnertime as long as the Chinese tourist invasion is going on...they are reliving a popular Chinese film set in Chiang Mai and and are not behaving in the way that fits in thailand ...pushy, loud, etc. Go at lunchtime...it's local and delicious. Great northern food. Fair prices. We had lunch and dinner there.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Tried the evening for a meal for 3 in their lovely room ... very ornate and pleasing to sit there. However, this place is full of other asain tourists and I think that the taste of the food has been geared towards this clientele. The dishes are very small given the price you pay ... as for authenticity of said dishes very poor IMO. I ordered "Hung Le" Curry and the sauce was so so and the meat conent tough and overly fatty and also small. The "Laap Khrua", a northern style mince pork dish was totally tastless given the amount of notrthen chilli they are 'supposed' to put in it. "Nam Prik Num", "Nam Prik Ong" and the grilled chicken were good but the "Sai Ua" was not ...you can find much better Chaing Mai sausage at pretty much any local market here. Food for 3 and I left feeling hungry was 1400 Baht. I'm no expert when it comes to Thai food but I've eaten Thai food for the last 13 years in Thailand pretty much everyday and I can tell you that the dishes served at this restaurant are not all the "authentic Thai food" label that many attach to this establishment. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Love the set up of the restaurant and just like you entering into a small museum. everything is full of story and taste were very delicious too. good to try this traditional north thai style food and I am sure everyone can have a great time despite of the waiting time a little bit long.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is lucky to get one star. The lunchtime has apparently a decent reputation - but avoid at all costs in the evening. Prices increase and quality nosedives. Tonight, I ordered a chicken and veg curry. It was the first time I've ever had one without meat. A small Chang was 80Bh to add to the misery. Do not visit this place - there are far better places on the street.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are looking for authentic Thai cuisine this is the place. They have a English menu for those of you who don't speak Thai and the staff are helpful with food descriptions. The food is simply delicious! We prefer their out of town location.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have worked in Chiang Mai and visited several times, eating at this restaurant on three occasions but only at lunch time. The food and service was consistently good and the price very reasonable. On 13 March, my travelling companion and I went there for dinner for the first time. I was frankly very embarrassed to take him there on the strength of my lunch time visits, as the evening experience was very different indeed: it is effectively two very different restaurants according to the time of day.The evening menu was shorter and the prices substantially higher, and unsurprisingly (by contrast with lunch time) there were no local customers. We ordered nam prik ong (pork and tomato dip with vegetables) for starters, and it was fine. The main courses, however, were utterly appalling. My companion ordered a chicken and vegetable curry which was a bland soup containing absolutely no chicken. I ordered hanglay (pork) curry which ought to be a signature dish at a restaurant purporting to specialise in Lanna food. The "meat" content was 95% bone and gristle and the sauce poorer than I had previously experienced at similar northern Thai restaurants in Chiang Mai such as Rattana's Kitchen and Aroon Rai. The service was slow and inattentive, despite there being several waiting staff on duty and only a dozen or so customers (all tourists). We decided not to make a fuss but to put the visit down to experience and to warn others about our evening experience. By all means go there for lunch - as many locals do every day - but for heaven's sake avoid the evening and go elsewhere. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited twice the 1st time we had the set meal for 2 which was amazing really great value , the building is full of antiques. The next time we ordered off the menu and chose dishes we hadn't tried before , some were great we did have a few dishes we didn't enjoy as much but dishes cost so little it didn't matter. Great north Thai food !

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Hands down the best food I have had in Thailand. We went for my daughter's birthday dinner, I booked in advance which I recommend as there were many people waiting outside for a table. The staff let us bring a cake & happily stored it & served it with lit candles & ice cream after our meal. We were concerned at the start as it took a long time for the staff to take our food & drinks order but once they were taken, our meals didn't take long to arrive. Can't wait to eat here again!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is my "go-to-place" in Chiang Mai. I prefer the outdoor cafe for lunch instead of the dinner building next door (open evenings). There's another cafe on the outskirts of town, serving the same delicious Lanna (Northern Thai) dishes at reasonable prices. My favorites are the fried chicken, pork ribs, Papaya salad, Pork in Red Curry and Sticky Rice, washed down with a Thai beer. HP is informal, frequented by locals and tourists alike, so it can be crowded. Find a table, sit down and flag down a waitress or waiter and order. As you enter, on your left, you can see the various dishes, so that's a good starting point. Then, ask your server, "What's good?" You'll see what's on other peoples' tables and they know what they like. Parking is scarce, so ride a tuktuk. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My favorite dishes are fried pork ribs, fried chicken and hung-le curry served with sticky rice. This place gets very busy during lunch time, so, plan to be there early. However, the foods are worth the waiting. There are only 3 tables in the air-conditioning area.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We are real foodies and walked in quite excited about this place. The atmosphere and decor are really fun and their menu full of promise. That's where the good part ends. From that point on It was all slow uninspired and bland... both the food and the staff. In the 45 minutes it took for a waiter to come and reluctantly take our order (only after I finally asked) they had the biggest blowout I have ever seen with a customer anywhere. A group that had been sitting at the table next to us had apparently been charged for things they had not ordered and the customer was not having it. A solid 10 minutes of screaming from both customer and staff ensued and finally customer left. It struck me as odd that a business would prefer to let something so silly get so out of hand rather then try to diffuse the situation, but I figured cultural norms around customer service are different everywhere. We were remarking to each other that there may have been some lost-in-translation issues at that table. Most of the staff don't speak any English which did not put us off at all, but we decided we would be very clear with our order an have them read it all back to us. Long story short: our order was clear yet they apparently just bring whatever they want. We ordered a jackfruit salad and were given a pork salad. In my mind this was not a mistake as the guy even said "jackfruit" salad as he put it down on our table (which is why it took us a long time to be certain that this was not what we had ordered). By the time we got through the debate with eachother ("maybe jackfruit salad also has pork in it?", "maybe young jackfruit has a completely different taste"?) and concluded that the interesting pickled taste meant we were served the pickled pork salad, we had eaten most of it. At that stage we did not want to cause another scene like the previous group, but felt quite cheated and like the staff just take their customers (most of whom are tourists who are not necessarily very familiar with authentic northern Thai food) for fools. We made sure the bill was accurate before we left (the price of our salad had been scribbled out and changed to reflect what we had ordered, but it was obvious they were initially going to charge us for the pork). After reading a few reviews here and replaying last night's events, it's clear that the drama we witnessed was not a one-off event and the staff really make the customers feel unwanted (getting any attention at all is like pulling teeth). Mostly they are dishonest. First time I don't leave a tip. As for the food? Well it was really nothing to right home about. Pretty bland, luke warm, and boring. Save your cash and head out for some street food. Every quick street meal has been 100x better and friendlier (even with the language barrier) than this completely hyped up and overrated place. Maybe if you go with a local (our reference came from two different locals) you may have a better experience. Otherwise, tourist beware! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am still at the restaurant. Ordered 5 dishes including a soup for 3 people. The portion is very small. The banana flower and pork rub soup is lukewarm. The worst is the Huen Phen fried rice. The rice was cold and not fully cooked and worst, tasted sour. Obviously the rice went bad under the got whether. We called the waiter and tried to explain it. The waiter said if you said so then I would take it, was there anything else you would like me to take? It made me feel that we are just trouble makers!!! I asked for a replacement, the waiter didn't listen and left. When he came back, he just gave us a bowl of simple rice. The rest of the food is so so. This place, in my opinion, is overrated. Btw, this is the first time I wrote a review on Tripadvisor. I guess I am just too disappointed this time. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went here on first night in Chiang Mai - that was Friday, 7th March. Service was a bit slow, but the food was tasty and the setting amongst the eclectic collection of ornaments was great, so all forgiven. We returned on Sunday 9th, for a very different experience. It was not as busy, yet service was disorganised and poor - it took ages to get even the menu. We ordered quickly (just 2 of us) but only part of the meal was delivered. We waited and waited. Finally got someone's attention and they brought the rice, but my meal was supposed to have pork in it, and there was none. We got this across and he left, came back and picked up one on the dishes and said 'I change for you.' 1 min later he returned with the same dish, with 2 tiny pieces of pork on it - one was mostly bone and gristle. The whole dish was barely warm. We were really hungry and ate something of all the dishes, but were really disappointed. We asked to speak to the manager, but either the waiter didn't understand, or didn't want to, or there was no manager - perhaps part of the problem? We paid and left. I think the restaurant is so popular that it may be getting complacent. Or it was just an off night, but we won't be returning, there are so many other good options in town. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've been here twice, both times after massage time at the Thai Massage Conservation Club at 99 Ratchamanka. My favourite items on the menu are the Lemon grass drink (which is a lemon grass infused syrup, so refreshingly delish on a hot day) and the Lod Chang (similar to Chendol, a Singaporean dessert but without Kacang!) I liked the mango salad and the Som Tam. The Khao Soy was good, the Nam Tok Moo had large pieces of pork with a fair bit of fat on it, which was different from the excellent one sold at the Chang Puak Gate food stalls. I tried cutting the pork with my spoon and bent the metal quite badly!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was on our street when we stayed in Chiang Mai and I have to say it was really lovely. It felt good to eat at a place that the locals eat at too. We really enjoyed all the chili dips and a little platter of various bites, pork scratching, sausages etc. All the food was cooked freshly and it was really tasty. I also got a takeaway one day and it was 60 Baht for sticky rice, chiang mai curry (which came with some soft and crispy noodles) and some little shallot and lime sides. This was excellent value! It can get quite busy in the evening time, so for a lunch spot, this place is perfect.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Ate here twice on a 3 night trip to CM. We stayed 50m away at Elliebum but would have travelled to eat here. We had 6 to 8 dishes each visit and found little to dislike in any. The service was ok, the beer was cold and the staff were happy to explain what was on offer - displayed SE Asian style in large stainless steel pans. They even suggested a couple of non-spicy dishes for our kids. Try any dish and feel confident to ask for recommendations - the place is well regarded by Thai's for a reason. We would return and are happy to recommend without hesitation. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: For 200 baht (about USD 6.13), I had the following: + The Khao Soi was very good with a crispy egg noodle topping (sometimes it's stale or not crunchy) with a chicken drumstick; small portion though + Fried Fermented Pork Spare Rib with shredded fresh ginger, chili peppers, toasted garlic slices, and peanuts - not bad + Fried Chicken with sweet chili sauce - very crispy outside encasing tender succulent meat inside + Northern Style Thai Sausage with chili paste and shredded ginger - It's very busy at lunch time, so go early or be prepared to wait to be seated, wait for your order to be taken, wait for your check, etc. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: dishes are not good taste and as well the service is bad. Waiters almost ignored us everytime.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Khao Soi pretty good. The ribs had too much fat but tasty. http://theeyesofnick.tumblr.com/post/76934261590/chiangmaithailand

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went here for both lunch and dinner. Both times i loved the food. I think i tried a total of about 7 dishes. None of them were labelled hot, however about 6 of the dishes were so spicy i saw stars. So much so i needed 5 minutes between bites or i wouldnt have been able to taste it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We found the place on a hot afternoon with empty stomachs. So nice to find a place open and serving food at 3 pm -- we learned our lessons that many restaurants in Chiangmai are either closed between 2:30 and 5:00 pm or not open at all during the day. The decors are full of character. The portion is not big, which is an advantage for we wanted to try more things. The presentation of the dishes was so so but we did not expected to be excellent either.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We've driven by this place a few times, but this time we decided to give it a try and we were not disappointed. The ambience was great, wooden walls and roof, really relaxed, a little tiny (manmade) stream running around the restaurant. Just perfectly tranquil. The food was absolutely excellent. We had the dish pictured on the trip advisor, it's chicken in an amazing curry. We also had the sticky rice, so unbelievably yummy. My husband ordered the fried beef which as amazing. But the best dish of all was the chicken fried rice, not sure what made it so special but it was just perfect. My daughter loved it. The chicken fried rice should not be confused with the fried chicken on rice, we didn't have that dish. I would definitely come here again, but unfortunately we are leaving Chiang Mai tomorrow. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had a fantastic meal here which allowed us to sample a wide range of Northern Thai cuisine. It was incredibly good value. We had 5 dishes with sticky rice and juice for 280B. Jackfruit salad curry, fried papaya salad, pork skin, dry dried pork (like jerky) and grilled pork curry were all absolutely delicious. They do Northern cuisine particularly well with no pretense. Staff were friendly and attentive but it still felt very Thai, rather than a tourist joint. It had a nice atmosphere even though it was pretty quiet at that time of day (we went at about 4pm) Just writing this review makes me want to jump on a plane just for another meal here! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: For those who loves to explore traditional asian dishes. Strictly not for people who wants 5 star services and cleanliness.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our guide recommended this restaurant to us. The food was fantastic. So many wonderful dishes. Chicken with egg noodles, papaya salad and Thai sausage were standouts. I wish I could have tried more! We got there right at 12 but the restaurant filled quickly with students, locals and tourists. A real treat...highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I researched and found this restaurant in my Lonely Planet guide and it took some finding so it was a surprise when we arrived to find Huen Phen fully booked with a queue of waiting diners. We decided to wait, which was only 15 minutes, because of the quaint and quirky décor of this traditional Thai house. It was certainly worth waiting for as the Northern Thai cuisine was a real treat and we ordered more. The staff were attentive and we were very surprised and the low cost and excellent value. It's definitely worth finding and I will return when next in Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited Chiang Mai for three weeks over Christmas 2013 and into 2014 and we visited Huen Pen a number of times. We were told about Huen Pen by a tuk tuk driver who recommended it for good, authentic Chiang Mai food and we were not disappointed. The service was excellent and the food amazing (and so cheap!). Arguably the best Khao Soi in the city - and I can tell you that I ate a lot of Khao Soi during this trip as I just love it! :) We mostly visited for lunch, but decided to go for dinner one evening as the staff had told us that it was very different from the lunch service. They were right! During the day it's a busy place that you just walk in right off the street. You'll most likely share a table, and it's noisy and very busy. But at night you enter from the other side of the restaurant into a different area that is more formal. It's actually like having dinner in an antiques store as there is just so much stuff in there! Wouldn't mind pinching some bits for my house ;) Dinner was OK, but it was nowhere near as good as lunch and as we much preferred having lunch there we stuck with that. I can only assume that some of the other poor reviews on here were made about the dinner service as it is very different. If you go, go for lunch. Be sure to try the Khao Soi Gai (chicken curry) and the fish with curry paste in banana leaves - they are really delicious. To me, a restaurant is only good if I really want to return. Do I want to return to Huen Pen? Absolutely! Wish I was there now .... Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Lunch time is fast food thai style, over crowded with group tours, dinner is really a great experience, very reasonably priced

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious local food, great service and a great price. We were recommended to go there and were watching the clock to go back for more lunch!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was recommended to us by the concierge at the Holiday Inn where we stayed. The restaurant was packed but we didn't have more than a 5 minute wait to be seated. I was disgusted by the clearing of the table in front of our eyes from the diners before us by the staff. They just used old, gross towels to wipe down the tables and they didn't use any glass cleaner which would have been sanitary since the table covering was made of glass. The food was slow to get to us, the service was bad in terms of aloof/apathetic staff, and the fact that all the food was cold really ticked me off. If you can't serve hot food, please close your restaurant because it means you simply don't care. Just inexcusable! We sent one dish back to be reheated and it took 20 minutes and we had to ask the staff twice before it came back hot. This place does so much business due to it's proximity to the famous temples and I assume it was quite good at one time but it appears they now rest on their laurels and they have gotten lazy. Do not give them your money when there are lots of nice places in the area who would give you hot, quality food and good service. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One thing I've learned in Thailand so far is that crowded places with long lines doesn't necessarily mean good food. In fact most of the time it's opposite. This is one of those places. Super crowded with long lines with rude service. All the good served is cold and not fresh. Also, everything we ordered was just plain nasty.. Overcooked chicken, old pomelo salad that had gone bad, dry crusty sausage, dry old rice, and overly oily khao soy. Avoid this place. Even mcdonalds would be a better option which is sad.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We didn't book and so we waited for about forty minutes to get inside. The restaurant is nicely decorated in Thai based nick backs and kitsch and has small cabinets and furniture that are used to create a series of small discreet spaces into which tables are squeezed to enable intimate chats and illicit rendezvous. The music which was played throughout the evening was unexpected and rather nice and eclectic. It started off with sixties and seventies pop music in a ballad style and then became a little more jazzy and took on that late night early hours of the morning slightly boozy quality that is so nice. When we sat down to eat the waiter was rather aloof, silent and seemed unfriendly. When I asked about a dish from him it became clear that he had no English and was probably anxious. He was clearly not what I had initially assumed and was actually very jolly. The food itself was interesting. We didn't really order enough because the amounts, at least of the dishes we ordered, were pretty small and in addition two of the dishes weren't to my taste particularly. The chicken red curry soup and the selection of starters were very good. My qualms about the food were that some of the dishes were clearly made using preserved pickles, vegetables etc and they have a slightly odd flavour. It was also very difficult to tell what some dishes were from the look and the flavour. The baby jack fruit salad I mistook for crispy minced pork and the crispy minced pork I thought was a dish we hadn't ordered. The minced pork was dish that looked like mashed off green vegetable and was a little heavy on the chili for my taste. I am not trying to suggest there was anything wrong with the food as it all seemed good, it is simply that it is not all going to appeal to Western tastes so choosing carefully is key. I was a bit dismayed to see diners sitting here on their mobiles throughout the meal and seeing that echoed by one of the waiters at the same time. I do think it is rude and if I were paying the salary of the waiter I'd have a few things to say about it to him. The wine was reasonably price and though not special was perfectly reasonable. I was not impressed that the glasses and bottles of beer were straight out of the fridge and had that lovely frosted look while my wine glasses were room temperature as my white Chardonnay nearly was until after about five minutes in the ice bucket. So odd that's they treat bottled beer with that sort of reverence but not the wine. The meal was very inexpensive and the service was quick and pleasant. I had to go and find something to eat afterwards from a 7/11 just to take the edge of my appetite as I had not chosen well and in addition was not tempted by the dessert menu. The desert menus in Thailand are usually not particularlyb exciting it seems. I would go back to this restaurant again because it offered something different. After a week in Thailand I had been finding the diet a bit samey and although the dishes in this restaurant weren't all to my taste they were all different to food we had eaten elsewhere. Some of it delicious. If I had more time in Chiangmai I would have returned to try some of the other dishes. My partner, who is Chinese, enjoyed all the dishes and hoovered up the ones I did not like with a smile and a smacking of gums. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It was a good restaurant, but its getting worse, with many tourists visiting that making the food bad and CS worse.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is an unpretentious place...authentic northern Thailand food. I look for places where locals eat when I travel, not where the 'Club Med' crowd goes. Even folks in Bangkok are envious of such cooking in the north which they cannot get in the south. THE DISH TO ORDER IS 'KHAO SOI'. A curry based soup/stew with noodles which you can order with chicken (a drumstick) or pork. Was in Chiang Mai for 7 days and I ate there 3 times. My order was always khao soi, a side of sausage, jasmine rice, and one of their delicious desserts. You want the real thing, come here. Remember...unpretentious but authentic.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: In the evening time, good atmosphere, good food.Good decoration and friendly staff. Try a 'Orderve Muang'. Don't miss.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is definitely for the locals. The service was good and the food was tasty and plentiful. Although is was a bit on the grainy side and was no substitute of a good brat wurst, the Sai Qua (Northern Thai Sausage) was very flavorful and unique.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It quite different feeling of lunch and dinner personally. We love to go for dinner even place is so small but not to much noisy. Don't miss traditional nothern dish.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: 15 minutes. 50 baht. One Khao soy, one sticky rice. I was in a hurry but last year I had been to this place and I couldn't miss it. I was in and out in 15min. The khao soy was amazing, not too spicy although it did make me sweat, but it was great. The service was good and they were very nice. All for 50 baht. Need I say more?

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My favorites: Sai Oua (spicy northern thai sausage), nam prik ong (spicy pork and tomato chili paste), gang ho (spicy vermicelli noodles with veggies and meat) tham tua makhua (spicy roasted long beans and eggplants served with boiled egg) khanom jeen nam ngeaw (thin white noodles served in a clear spicy red curry broth with congealed blood, ground pork, pork ribs, dok ngeaw, bean sprouts, preserved cabbage, tomatoes, scallions, and some fried garlic on top. you can order khan thoke during dinner time which comes with a variety of northern thai dishes, but in smaller portions, as well as sticky rice, and dessert. I come here every single time I'm in Chiangmai. There are other cheaper restaurants that serve the same fare, but there's something about this place that keeps me coming back. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen they are open in the morning and close at 16.00 hrs. we love the food outhere, the day we reach Chiang Mai we always visit this resturant even before we leave for the Airport, anywhere we are we will back to this resturant before they closed. Its funny right but for us this is the real test of Lanna food (last trip from Pai we direct to have lunch here !, this time from Mae-Rim direct to Huen Phen) 5555

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great lunch for five people--five courses plus lots of great sticky rice = $10 total. Really great food, great Thai iced tea, mostly Thai locals. The service was not so great but the food made up for the. Spotty service w here we sometimes felt ignored. The atmosphere was authentically local.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went there at 1100am, and was absolutely ignored by all the waiters, even though the restaurant wasn't even that busy. i decided to take the menu to the cook, and she helped me out with the order right away. I ordered the jack fruit salad, and a grilled curried fish, which, both were very good. however, is it much better than other restaurants? i doubt it. would i come back, probably not, as i don't enjoy rudeness.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best food in Chiang Mai. Very fantastic restaurant with delicious local dishes. Fresh vegetables and Thai herbs prepared with combo of sweet, sour, salty and spicy. I will go back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: They have good food and the place has a great charm about it. It' s tough to give a place like this a 5 star review - the food is good and authentic, the waits are short, if at all, and the food arrives as it should, but there are so many great restaurants in Chiang Mai. The staff is great, the food is good and you will definitely not be disappointed.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had previously enjoyed lunch here a few years a go but never visited in the evening, having been put off by descriptions of the interior and some of the reviews criticising the food and service. This time we decided to give it a go and after a 10 minute wait at 6:30pm we were in. The restaurant does have a slightly junk shop feel to it but I quite liked it. Not authentically Thai or sophisticated but still more interesting than most places. The dining experience was also a little quirky with strangely random portion sizes (plates of rice are tiny so order extra) and the staff are friendly but sometimes absent for too long. There is a decent menu of northern Thai food but a lot of the dishes include pork (as is the way in northern Thailand) so this does limit the options slightly if you don't eat it. Having said that there should be enough to choose from to satisfy most people and we enjoyed three or four dishes between the two of us. As I mentioned some of the portions are large and some small so I would advise a degree of over ordering, just in case. It's reasonably priced after all, so why not. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Eleven years ago my wife and I discovered a small restaurant, eclectically decorated serving delicious and spicy Northern Thai food. Unfortunately it looks like Huen Phen has been added to every tourist's itinerary. Day 1, we went at 8.30 pm and there were nine groups ahead of us. These days since everyone has to photograph their food before eating, things are slower anyway. Day 2 we went at 5.30 pm and got a table right away. But within 15 mins the place had filled up. And no, they won't take reservations, at least not from foreigners. A note about the food, spicy means really spicy. So be careful. The papaya salad was excellent. And the northern pork sausage so good we ordered it twice. Have dinner at 5 pm and you can't go wrong with this place. A must do on a CHIANG Mai trip and sadly on everyone's list as well. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great selection of local foods, great prices. Not a tourist trap... and you should be fond of chilli! Delicious.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went to this restaurant because of my friend invite me to go there. we just order 4 dishes. they all was delicious. we love mix food stir fried. we will go again next time.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is cheap, but the items we ordered werent to our liking. The papaya salad tasted too much of shrimp and was spicey like it said it would be and our noodle soup lacked flavor.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Was recommended by my hostel and loved the Khao Soy! Got it with iced Chrysanthemum tea, which was nice and refreshing. Service was a bit slow, but we went at a really busy time and there was only one English speaking waitress attending most of the tables. Don't let that stop you from visiting though.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Stay for the food, not the service. We had lunch here our first day in Chiang Mai, after it was recommended by our hotel. We had Tom Yum soup, spicy salad and green curry. All were fantastic! As everyone has stated, the service leaves something to be desired - but don't let that keep you away.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: In the old town and a bit out of the way but worth a visit. We made our way here for dinner and the restaurant was very busy but we were still able to get a table without delay. The atmosphere is interesting - cluttered with curios. Food was not quite the "standard" Thai menu - a bit different and interesting, portions are small...so just order and try more dishes! Service was attentive but the food was just luke warm... Would return and give it another try...

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There are 2 Huen Phen restaurants, one open during day and one open in the evening. The one during the day is FAR BETTER. Cheaper, and awesome food. Try the Khao Soi and pickled sausage.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We absolutely loved this place. There are tons of dishes to choose from and the servings are small and inexpensive enough that you can try a lot of different things (great for us as we wanted to explore the world of northern Thai cuisine). It always seemed to be very crowded, but the service was good. Most all of the clientele were Thai when we went, which is always a good sign.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Amusing decor, tasty northern Thai specialties. Useful menu for non-Thai speakers. We ate dinner there twice, a different range of dishes each time. Try the jackfruit salad!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant is located in what looks like an antique store with lots of eclectic decorations about the place. The food is great and I ate here a number of times and each time was equally as good. Most nights there was a short waiting list to get in, but it seems to move fairly quickly. Would definitely go back again if in Chiang Mai....one of my favourite places.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have heard this restaurant about local food that should visit when being in Chiang Mai. Tasting may be match who doesn't like more tasty.I like rice noodles in spicy tomatoes and minced pork soup Atmosphere was local restaurant I like it. Very busy in Lunch time. Good to recommend for friend

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We stayed at Elliebum next door from Huen Phen and ate at the restaurant a few times during our stay. Delicious food, great prices and very fast service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We read about this place from many review sources and decide that we must try it. Once we landed in CNX and pick up our rental car, we programmed our GPS to take us directly to this place! It is not far from the Airport at all. The place offers a small parking lot (takes about 5 cars) nearby but it was full when we got there. So we parked along the street which seemed to be full of customers of this place! We ordered the all the popular local Northern food i.e. Local appetizers (consist of Nam Prik Ong (chili & tomatoes paste with ground pork + Sour pork sausage, Pork Rhine, steam veggies), Kao Soi (Northern Curry Noodle), Hung Lay Curry (Northern Pork Curry), BBQ Chicken, etc. All the dishes were so tasty, we decided to order second helping for some of them! When we saw the bill, it was a pleasant surprise to see such affordable prize!! It was about half of the cost we would be paying if we had the same dishes in Bangkok!!!! And none of them are as tasty as this place! We highly recommend this place to anyone who is more adventurous to try different type of Thai food other than Pad Thai or Tom Yum, etc. You will realize that there is much more to offer in Thai food!!! Cheers! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: a magical place full of magical foods. we went back the next day for lunch it was so good. a definite highlight of our one month in thailand.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great local food, reasonable priced. It's always packed so do expect to wait a little, but the flow of people are relatively quick and their service is efficient. They don't speak much English so have to rely on the photo to order.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious and delicate Thai food, with a unique offering of northeastern Thai food, such as Hung Ley curry and fermented rice noodle sets that you cannot find easily in Bangkok.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We loved this restaurant! 100% recommended. The ribs were amazing!!!!! Great value, great atmosphere!!!!!!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I am a resident of Chiang Mai for over 8 months now and will continue for many months. Never have I visited a restaurant with so many facades and little substance.....Khao soi promised all over the place, yet they dont serve it at night...Go figure..Portions were assuming a small child would eat it and the flavor of the 2 plates we had were shall I say non-existent....And the portions we got were warm, not close to freshly prepared......Waiters has zero personality and it showed...The place inside resembled an antique shop.....Not much having to do with Thailand by the way....Place had a few foreigners and no Thais that I saw which tells you who they were catering to. All in all, there are many, many REAL markets around the city where the service is quick, the food is made before your eyes and you will interact with interesting Thai people. Plus the price is very reasonable and you will return often to the same place like we do... Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After scanning the mixed reviews of this place, I saw one poster who had deftly analysed the problem: huen Phen is great for lunch, but bad for evening meals. We went for lunch and ordered like 7 dishes, all were great and at good value too! The most surprising was the northern thai sausage with chili dip, who knew sausages in asia could be this good? we're considering going back and trying a few more dishes, Huen Phen is a great place to try a lot of different dishes and I'd recommend a lunch there to everyone.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A friend swore to me this is the best Thai food restaurant in Chiang Mai. I also checked the TA reviews and decided to give it a shot. Granted, I've only been there once, so maybe it was an off day for them or something, but I was not impressed. I had lunch there with my girlfriend today, and we ordered about 5 dishes between us to share. The noodle soop was good, but had a NASTY piece of 'chicken' (cartilage, gristle, and skin) floating in it. Ordered a half of a chicken that from the menu appeared to be 'rotisserie' style, but when we got it, it was deep fried, about 5 hours ago. It was cold and hardened. We ordered some pork rib appetizers too, same thing. Pretty sure they had been sitting all day and no one even bothered to microwave them. Cold, hard, and chewy to the point where it hurt my teeth. The venue is nice, the people were friendly, service was great. The food was just sub par. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The dishes, north Thai style, were delicious. The atmosphere is very nice and quiet. The furnitures and all decorative stuffs look typical. As for me, the service may be improve as the waiters don't pay much attention.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: In Central Changmai, worth seeking out (we'd hired bikes for the day). Tradional Northern Thai food which is tough for vegetarians, luckily we eat fish and enjoyed a spicy fish soup, greens and rice. An interesting setting stuffed full of temple relics, good service.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I used to live around the corner from Huen Phen and ate here at least 5 times a month. The Larb salads are AWESOME as is everything I've ever tried. That said, portions are bit small thus really one dish is best suited per person. I don't eat pork but everyone who've I've taken here has raved about the Northern saugages.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is just great for me; perfect northern Thai food. Pity is on the tourist trail trap now ( not that that seems to bother the staff ! )

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant serves authentic Northern Thai, which is actually quite different to the typical Thai. The restaurant seems like an antique shop in an old house. The service was OK, the food very tasty and extremely cheap. Worth seeking out for some good and cheap food. Don't get discouraged by limited signs of life after 9pm, walk all the way through to the "antique shop like room". Enjoy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Established in 1986. this restaurant serves Authentic northern Thai cuisine. We tried the fried chicken and other recommended dishes. Great! It's walking distance to the Sunday night market too.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: One of the best meals we had in Chiang Mai, this place offers a wide variety of northern Thai food including the famous Khao Soi. They are quite busy and service can be a bit slow. They forgot a few of our dishes, but eventually we got everything we ordered. I recommend walking to the front and seeing what looks good. You can order from the menu also, but it helps to see what they have already prepared. Recommended for sure, just be patient.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was recommended to dine here by a Bangkok lady who manages Doi Chang Cafe in Rachadamnoen Rd. The place is packed right after 6pm. We had rice with grill eggplant in shredded pork, fried pork,tomyam and 1coke, all at 460B, we left with a huge smile and content.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen has some great and some rather disappointed reviews. The pattern that emerges is that the people who loved it came for lunch, the people who were disappointed came for dinner. Why? Huen Phen tries to go upscale for dinner and uses a completely different, fancier dining room, charges more, and provides smaller servings. What makes this place great is the quality of the food, the variety, and the very reasonable prices (at lunch!). Go at lunch, skip the Kao Soi (which is great, but one of the few things that is overpriced even at lunch), order some fried meats, a curry, a grilled curry ("aeb"; the fish is particularly good) and some salads (do sample the bamboo salad. Amazing.) I go about once a week, and have been going for years. Oh, and try to get there before 12:00 if you want the speediest service. It's generally not slow even when full, but it doesn't hurt when the hordes of Bangkok foodies descend to have gotten there first. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food here was nice but not amazing, the servings were all very small which didn't really work for a large group. The service was fairly average having to ask for things a few times and language barriers started creating issues. We asked for things to be really hot and spicy but came out mostly medium. You will need to book if you want to get in.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Here's very good Thai food. They have good seating big space. but very busy on lunch time. you better to go early. many kind of Thai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was recommended to us by a knowledgeable local. It was a feast for our eyes as well as our stomach. Delicious food everywhere. It always seems packed with people, but service was smooth, efficient and friendly. The air-conditioned section was very welcome a couple of afternoons, and sharing a huge table with other people was not a problem. It was an opportunity to see some of the endless selection of dishes on the menu. My perfect meal is ordering a half dozen dishes, some of which we don't have a clue about, and digging in. Jackfruit curry? Yum! And the best khao soi I had in Chiang Mai. Just about everything was a treat. Including the bill. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Read lots of good reviews about this restaurant and decided to give it a try. Atmosphere is okay, the owner piled way too much stuff in that tiny space though. This is absolutely not art. Service is slow and the waiters barely speak any English. The food is overpriced, taste is plain. Overall not worth the trip.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you go during lunch hours, it will be jam packed with tourists. Service crews are impatient and rude. Make sure you know what you want to order for everyone (if you are travelling in a group of people), otherwise the waitress will just walk away. Even if you are not sure what food to order, do not bother to ask cos' you will get the wrong answer.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place is better enjoyed after the tourist has already adapted to the Thai timing, since it may take a while until anyone tells you that you should find yourself a table and brings you the menu. That said, it was perhaps the best food we had in Chiang Mai, and for very little money. This place was once reviewed by a New York Times critic, reason why we heard about it from other tourists in Phuket. Portions are indeed small, but at their individual price there is no reason not to order 2 or 3 per person and sample as many dishes as you can from their long menu. Just make sure you include the local sausage, grilled to perfection, and the spicy pork salad. Also a great place to try Khao Soy, a northern specialty. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I really regret going here. I arrived at opening, 5 on the dot. Took a seat and eventually was given a menu. I ordered two dishes, one of which was meant to come with rice. Around 50 mins later something arrived which I presumed was the salad I ordered. The portion size was terible, as it turned out this was meant to be TWO meals, I was sat there for another 20 minutes thinking my other dish was going to arrive at some point! I ordered an eggplant and pork salad and minced chilli with crispy pork, which should have come with rice. The crispy pork was in a bowl not much bigger than an egg cup!!! And this cost 80thb! No rice ever arrived with it. I reckon they figure they can have crap service and tiny portions and people will still come! Give this place a miss! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The spot was recommended by a man working at a temple not too far away as authentic Thai We ate in the simpler part of the restaurant since it was for a late lunch. She offered what she had cooked for the day and the food was amazing. I still salivate for her pork curry sauce. We didn’t have to wait, we got seated in a ventilated area and the food came right away. Only later did we realize there was a very cute restaurant at the back that was beautifully decorated. There the menu looked more sophisticated. Ultimately, I am glad we tried the simpler side of the restaurant; it seemed more genuine.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had read about this reaturant in Rough Guide and were a bit disappointed. It was crowded and we had to wait for a table - we were told to put our name on a list in the back and to check frequently about our wait status. We took a couple of beers and sat out front, but no one checked on us at anytime. We finally got in and then waited a long time before a server approached our table. We contemplated leaving, but after investing nearly an hour waiting we decided to stay. We did finally get some food and it was quite satisfactory.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My group of 3 friends loved this restaurant! The food is different from standard Thai fare, and is much spicier. The restaurant itself is very unique - it has lots of interesting art, and "doo-dads" around so lots of things to look at. We tried many different dishes (like more than 1 per person) and the bill was still $7 per person. Highly recommended to explore a new cuisine!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited Huen Phen for lunch after reading reviews online and after receiving a recommendation from our guest house owner. The food truly lived up to our expectations as being delicious northern thai food at an extremely reasonable price. In fact, it was so inexpensive, we ordered multiple meals just to sample from their large menu. Thumbs up! Unfortunately, the atmosphere left much to be desired. It was extremely crowded -as any great restaurant should be, but it felt disorganized which left us feeling confused and unsettled. If great food is your highest priority, go to Huen Phen. If atmosphere is of equal importance, go to Dash! (a lovely restaurant located a short walk away)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Admittedly, the place is popular for both tourists and locals with money. Lots of traditional northern Thai items on the menu but for what you pay, the portions could be a little larger. For 50 baht, the Khao Soy was a rip off, maybe five noodles at the most. There are plenty of places to get better Khao Soy nearby. The service is the usual rushed Thai. You can usually flag someone down to get your order in and the food will come quickly. All in all, its slipped a notch but the food is still decent.....but pricy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A great choice of Northern Thailand / Laos /Burmese dishes downtown Chiang Mai. Very reasonably priced and in an interesting place. Very crowded; go early in the evening to avoid queuing

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We just enjoyed a late lunch at Huen Phen. We walked right in, with no wait, and sat in the air conditioned section, alongside the locals. We sat at a communal table and admired the authentic wood carvings on the wall. Relative to our other CM restaurant experiences ("Top 10 TA"), this was the best "sit-down" experience we've had this trip, the food was the best we've had, the most Northern Thai authentic - though served luke warm, our only complaint. Service was good/attentive, and our server did speak good enough English for it all to come out as ordered - and quickly. Four dishes (khao soi, minced park, local sausage, curry w/vegetables) for the two of us, with rice and a large beer for $11US, an excellent value - and we walked away full. As a general comment, we find the restaurant food in CM to be of the same quality as many of the market or stand stalls, but sometimes you just want to sit down, so it costs a little more. As good as it gets for Chiang Mai, unless you want to step up to a hi-end restaurant. Our experience was good, the food was very good, as was the value. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Busy restaurant, interesting menu reasonably priced and nice decor. I would highly recommend the Burmese Curry it was to die for!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Was recommended by hotel to go there and was surprised how nice it was. The old character and close seating gives a special atmosphere and the food is very nice if you like Thai food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great place to experience local northern Thai food. We had a feast of 7 dishes and 6 different juices feeding 3 people, all for 500 baht. Highly recommend.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A beautiful, interesting restaurant with pleasant staff and interesting menu. We did the Khantoke tasting menu which was perfect. While wait of about an hour wasn't fun, they did provide internet access and beverages in a nice outdoor area to make the wait tolerable. One word of caution: while we don't expect world class bathrooms in Northern Thailand, my gf turned around about reaching the ladies room... (I found the men's room acceptable, so she may have had bad timing).

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After days of excellent and amazing street food, we decided to taste a "real" restaurant. What a mess. Initially it was overcrowed so we decided to come around the end of service (21h30). Decoration is nice and interesting but it don't feed you ;-) The food wants to be authentic, and probably is: but it means served cold, and all toghether (we ordered the testing menu (tourist menu!) with a little bit of everything), even the desert... The price was quite high compared to other restaurant, service poor (no explaination on what is what and which order if there is any. Toilets disgusting... And what is said, when toilets are bad, better not see the kitchen... And we got ill.... So better trust in ... street food Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've heard it's less affordable for dinner so I tried lunch. I promised myself not to repeat any restaurants during my stay there but I went back to this one for the second time. It could be super busy and you might have to wait in queue but it's well worth a try. I'd also go again if I visit Chiangmai :)

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It's like a little treasure that is tucked away and when you find it, you don't want to tell too many people :) But obviously many people know about it as the 1st time we tried to get a table there was a long queue. The food is good although the service is pretty basic. The great thing about the place is that if you get tired of your company, you can just look around and soak in the decor.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place can be chaotic. Not sure if they take reservations but we turned up without one. There was a long queue and we were told it would be at least an hour wait. They do have an outdoor seating area where you can wait your turn with drinks service. Our name went on a list and we went for a walk. Came back an hour later and had missed our turn. Luckily the line was shorter and we only had to wait 10 minutes. Service was only just above average. Decor is dusty antiques, very quaint if you like that sort of thing. The horsduerves (appetiser) plate was good in my opinion, average in my wife's opinion. Similar story for our main courses. I had the northern red curry pork which I found very good. It was good value for money. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: GREAT food, wonderful, attentive service and totally inexpensive. What more could you ask for? We arrived at lunch time and were lucky to get a table immediately. Our order was taken quickly and the food arrived in record time. My wife is Thai and I had her order for us. Wow! the food was unbelievable. Our lunch cost less than $7 US for two. We returned the next day and ate different food this time. Same result. Open air but no outdoor seating. The view is of the school across the street. The food and value make up for any other shortcomings. This place should be included on any trip to Chiang Mai.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Different from your typical run of the mill Thai food, prepare to be pleasantly surprised! Yes, waiting time is long, and they don't take advance booking by phone, would suggest you to come here to make a reservation and then go to the nearby Wat for a walk and take some photos and then come back. You can also wait at the waiting area to have a drink and use their free WIFI. Remember this is a small local restaurant so they don't have many waiters and English level is not good. If you are looking for a place to fine dine, this is not the place. But if you are looking for good authentic Thai cuisine, THIS IS THE PLACE!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food at the riverside restaurants are fine, but if you are looking for authentic Northern Thai food, Huen Phen is a must visit. The place is always crowded with both locals and tourists. Order the set course menu or a la carte (as we did) to enjoy more variety of food. Whatever and how much you order, the price will be half or less than the restaurants along the riverside. The place is hard to find, so ask your hotel concierge for direction. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We visited this popular eaterie as part of a cooking excursion from our hotel. The place was packed - always a good sign - but we found a table near door. Large restaurant of 200+ covers. Popular with locals/Thai travellers. Menus were fairly basic, not very foreigner friendly with few descriptions. Tables were sticky. Generally the place was grubby. Northern Thai food is the main fare. We picked some classic dishes and also some unusual ones. Food was OK. We've had much better in other restaurants in Thailand. Northern Thai Sausage was good. Cheap but not so cheerful Service was fast but impersonal. No smiles. Recommend? Ummmm Return visit? Probably not... Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: What a delightful culinary experience if you enjoy Northern Thai cuisine. The ambiance and atmosphere was stunning - like a little Thai treasure cove. The food was exceptional - the baby eggplant was salad was delicious! Highly recommended to anyone who loves the local cuisine and wants to get away from the hustle and bustle of the city, this restaurant is a little oasis. NOTE: By default- so many dishes in Thailand have pork in them. ( even the salads!) If you do not eat pork - Be sure to make this well known when you make your order. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Authentic northern Thai food, fast service, reasonable price! We came back here several time during our stay in Chiang Mai. We loved the curried fished wrapped in banana leaves, khao soi curry noodles, and the sai uwa (northern sausages!) with sticky rice.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Locals and tourists flock to this unpretentious restaurant. They have an extensive selection of Thai dishes, primarily Northern Thai. And very good value for money. Highly recommended.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: As someone who just moved to Chiang Mai a few months ago, I'm eager to meet others and try out different spots. Normally I don't write reviews unless they're amazing or, as in this case, something to avoid. Huen Phen is "fine" all around. The food (we ordered about 6 dishes) had some that was undercooked (eggs) and others that were bland (soup) and all of the portions were small. The food wasn't horrible at all, but it just wasn't anything to write home about. Now maybe I'm stingy, but I spend 30 baht per night on my meals from street vendors. The vendors give me large portions and fantastic hot food. Had we wanted to eat on tables and chairs on the sidewalk, we could have had 15 meals for the price we had. But I'm not really picky about prices, it's just something to note. So even though I tend to prefer street vendors, I'm also happy to spend money when it comes to a great atmosphere or good location. I wasn't going to Huen Phen for myself--I was going to have food with many other people. But when it comes to that, it still doesn't win any points. There are so many better places for atmosphere that it isn't even worth a visit to Huen Phen. So skip your trip here and find another one. Street vendors are the best when they make sense, otherwise look at somewhere like Dash's if you need a sit down place. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The service is bad as too many people, you have to wait for a long time to get the response from the waiter. The food is just average. That's it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want to eat local food.Such as,I like Khanom jeen nam ngiaw delicious.(popular northern Thai-style noodle soup), Khao Soi (Northern Style Curried Noodle Soup with chicken,pork,beef,meatball.),Khao Kan Jin or Jim Som Ngiao (rice steamed with blood and bits of meat),Kaeng Kradang (is a northern Thai pork curry which has been set in to a jelly),Sai Ooah (Thai northern sausage),Nam Prik Ong (that consists of ground pork, tomatoes, garlic, and chillies.),Nam Prik Num (mixture of roasted young chillies, shallots, garlics, tomatoes, and other ingredients.),Kaeng hang lay is a rich curry based around pork belly. (hang lay is a corruption of the Burmese word for pork curry) The dish is found all over northern Thailand,with the ginger, tamarind and garlic. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: First time to visit here with my friend - easy to reach - nice location - reasonable price - varieties of menu - good taste But during the lunch time will crowded but no worry there are so many seats available. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You need to go there with a crowd so you can get to sample more of their dishes on offer. Packed out for lunch with mainly locals which says alot about it's authenticity. A little on the grubby side, but I guess that all adds to the flavor. Very cheap lunch.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was like a place of my dreams. The food was pretty good, but for anything that the food didn't wow us with the ambience did. The food was definitely good, this place is just slightly more expensive than other places you could go, but the location made it way worth while. Nestled into a little garden you feel like you're walking into someone who collects everything's beautiful little home. You can sit in quiet corners with windows and knick knacks or at larger family sized tables that feel like you're in a home dining room. All in all it was a very unique experience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is one of those places where food, not ambience, or service, will have to be your priority. I tried this on two occasions, both for lunch, and notice a good mix of Thais and foreigners. Go early, or you will not have a table without making any reservations. Food is simple, no frills northern Thai fare, served with little attention to details or thought for presentation. Here, food is everything. Expect to have a hard time getting the waiting staff's attention, and sometimes to have a dish or two forgotten. And expect some difficulty if your communication requirements include perfect command of English. But if you can put all that aside, and just evaluate this place on the merit of its food alone, you will find this to be one of the better places to eat in Chiang Mai's old city. I also found the prices to be very reasonable ~ 100 Baht per person on average for 3 dishes and mineral water. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place gets extremely busy for lunch, so before the midday or after 1 pm are the best times to come here. Find a table and try to get an attention of one of the usually extremely busy waiters. After that, khao soy (noodles in creamy sauce) is the obvious choice and the menu opens with few options of this iconic Chiang Mai dish. Go for one with pork and chicken unless you certainly prefer a particular kind of meat (no vegetarian options are available). The dish is not spicy hot as served, but feel free to add chillies provided. Sticky rice is just perfect here - translucent, chewy. If you feel hungry, go for another local speciality, a spicy Chiang Mai sausage, as well. And then a salad - they can be spicy, but come with undressed leaves and herbs in addition to the main plate. A meal will probably cost between 100 - 200 Baht per person incl. drinks. As always with a Thai meal - order more different dishes and share them all! The evening dinner is served in the back premises with fantastic interior filled with wood carved, metal and paper artefacts. The place is not buzzing exactly and the service is amateurish. If the outstanding local food, rather than the wholeness of the experience, is your main priority, go for it: the food is second to none and prices are very agreeable - expect to pay from 300 Baht per person including beer. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I think the scoring here would depend on which was more important to you. We came for lunch, which is served outside in the general seating area. The ambience was nothing special - but if you regularly eat at hawker's stalls in the East, at least it was generally clean. Service was not great - generally get ignored for a while, before anyone deals with you. Forget about service with smile. The menu we were given had English translations, which helped a great deal. Food portions were average, but the taste was good. We tried a variety of dishes, some "Northern Thai" style. For lunch, this is a convenient spot in the Old Town for a quick bite of Thai fare, before heading back to cool off in the heat of the afternoon. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Truly could not comprehend the hype for this restaurant. Food quality was passable. We had a platter of sausages and pork skin crackers, the khao soy noodle, and fried pork ribs. My dining companion,a local Thai had the desserts- red ruby, besides what we had already ordered. We felt the food is average, the platter was ordinary mish mash of entrees that could be assembled easily, noodle gravy was one-dimensional, pork was a tad dry, dessert was forgettable.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We dined at Huen Phen on a Friday night. The restaurant is situated in an old house, with tables set up in dusty and poorly lit nooks and crannies in the garden. Service was poor. We had to wave frantically to get some attention and when the food was served, the rice came long after the dishes which needed to be eaten with rice. Many of the dishes were a mystery as we could not discern what the origin of the minced and mashed meat and vegetables was. And to top it off, the waiter had forgotten a pork dish that we had ordered. We decided not to have dessert as we did not want to face another long wait. This is one place I will not re-visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ate twice at Huen Phen and enjoyed the experience and the food tremendously. We went both times at around 1.30 p.m and it was buzzing, noisy, full of locals and tourists who had worked out where it was. You can eat at single tables or at longer communal tables. Your choice depending on the demand! Yes, the service is a bit hit and miss (they are busy and at the beck and call of lots of diners) but that is part of the experience. There is a large menu with English translations. The food is great (especially the khao soi and the various papaya salads), very reasonably priced and of reasonable quantity. We loved the atmosphere and the energy of this place. If you go at night the main dining area is closed and a more intimate area with interesting Thai decor and soft lighting is opened. Prices are a little higher. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We arrived at 7:00 last night and waited 25 minutes for a table, but we were nicely given beers and a table to wait outside. The restaurant is pretty interesting inside, with unique decor. The linens and general area were not scrubbed clean however, and it is extremely hot and stuffy in the small room in which we were seated. Every single patron was a tourist. We ordered after 10 minutes, then waited 50 minutes for our food to be served. Appetizers and main courses came at once, with an incorrect rice order. The food was bland and boring, not different from any other Thai restaurant, perhaps worse in terms of quality and freshness. The beers were cold and inexpensive. Our replacement rice never served but we were charged for it. Very disappointed as we only have two dinners in chiang mai. My impression is that the restaurant receives business because of its accessibility to foreigners, but has allowed the food and management to decline. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is supposed to be a local restaurant but it seems that it was discovered by many tourists (thai and not). I was there at rush lunch time and service was terrible. I hope they can use this (and many others) review to either reduce the amount of tables served at the same time or find a better way to control the orders. Food seems to be very good yet.
