If you have any food allergies and want to go there at rush hours forget about any special attention.. You would need to know what to order without any help. For gluten allergies, I know that papaya salad is most of the times safe (unless they put oyster sauce what is not really common for papaya salad but depending on our sensitivity levels we always need to check), and there are probably some other options (which we may not find out).. . I would like to believe that out of rush hour this would be a better experience, but maybe I am being too optimistic.. However, in high season I would just try this restaurant again in unconventional times, but I need to recover from this bad experience first... Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Try the northern chicken stew. also excellent vegetarian options. A small 2-3 star place that locals flock too.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I wrote a review of this small hidden away restaurant in the old city of Chiang Mai some years ago and I gave it glowing reports. However, I guess others did the same. So now, rather than being an hidden away Thai local's restaurant, it's obviously now on a lot of tourist's itinerary. We have been back this Summer on two occasions and again last week and I am afraid that our visits have confirmed the worst. This is a restaurant full of tourists, not a Thai in sight (contrast when e first started to go there some yeas back when we were the only foreigners to be seen); service is poor, dishes are average, and pricy. If you are looking for the charm of a hidden away gem, look somewhere else and give this place a miss. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A little hard to find, but worth it. Go for lunch, as they serve the Khao Soy the traditional Northern Thai noodles. The best thing I ate in Chiang Mai and very unique. Would be a bummer to come to Chiang Mai and not have it, and this supposedly is the best place. And super cheap.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I been here several times, especially as many freinds like this restaurant, personally I find it a tourist trap in the evening, daytime is fine. The difference is so huge, daytime cilentel are mainly Thai, large portions no aircondition really fine and accepatable if it local food you are looking for. Evening things changes to a place where decoration is abundence, to mcu of everything and if one look carefully dust is all over the height of the clearners heads. Food is minimal, it is teasers and my personal estimation is that the food cost is aroung 15-20% of the charge, this makes it an expensive restaurant, what further was out of wack was the varried quality of the dishes, some dishes are good others are just passable. I will visit again when guest from out of town ask specificly to go here. Otherwise I chose with my feet and eat elsewhere. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ate here several times. During the day it's a pretty humble little place, but the food is delicious and very affordable. A good mix on the menu. At night we had to wait for a table in the other part of the restaurant which is quite atmospheric amongst all the antiques. A good crowd, not rowdy, but clearly everyone was enjoying themselves. The food really was delicious and especially on the first visit when we scored the slightly more private table set up near the front, kind of in an alcove, it was great. It was my favourite place in Chiang Mai for food. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Recommended by the hotel staff and our driver that this is a great place to sample the Northern Thai dishes at moderate price. Although they have a menu, all food are cooked and displayed at the end of the shop. Once you order, it will arrive at your table in 5 minutes at room temperature. Taste wise it is ok. They have the famous Chiang Mai Kaw Soi. and other Northern Thai dishes. Strangely they do not serve any Tom Yum. If you want something fast and simple and sample some Northern Thai dishes at reasonably cheap price, can try here. I feel there are way better food elsewhere.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are walking in town visiting the Temples, try and visit Wat Chedi Luang just before lunch so you can enjoy a lunch here as all the locals seem to frequent it and they can't be wrong. The food was wonderfully local and prices that are so low that they are hard to believe. Don't expect anything fancy but enjoy the experience of fitting in and chose from what others are eating or from the English menus. It was recommended by our Hotel Mgr and we were pleased we went.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Based on recommendations from several people (Chiang Mai residents), we came here for our last dinner in Thailand. The food was awesome, and we loved the ambiance -- Asian antiques everywhere. The only problem was the service. No one greeted us when we arrived, and it took about 10 minutes to get a menu once we were seated. But mediocre service aside, the food was delicious and reasonably priced, and we weren't in a hurry. Advice: get a reservation for dinner before you show up if you want to avoid a long wait.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is not very easy to find, and it was packed, even on a low season, but this place is definitely recommended! One of the best restaurants we experienced in Chiang Mai. But you have to sit inside, not on the terrace, but inside - it's situated in the old Thai house, and interior is full of porcelain, statues, old family photos and portraits, etc - a very special and atmospheric place. Food is very decent, and there's a good selection of Northern Thai dishes, and prices are very affordable. Recommended, definitely. They also have breakfasts/lunches (08.00 - 15.00) and evening opening hours are 17.00 - 22.00.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Very Impressive Restaurant. The most special is their environment, all kinds of local style, the furniture, the Ornament. That's very worthy experience. But about the food... I have to say maybe a little strange, and some food maybe too dry or too spicy.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Being a Chiang Mai born-and-raised, though now living overseas, I had fond memories of Huen Phen as a place where we always took our guests out for authentic Northern Thai (Chiang Mai) cuisine. This time, two of us went out for dinner at Huen Phen and unfortunately had what could only be described as awful experience. Waiters not attentive, food overpriced, messy decor (not our taste, certainly) and worst of all was the awful food served! Food purchased from a 'fresh market' would have been better than what we had. The Chiang mai sausages were grilled WELL past well-cooked point. Kua Ho', which is a classic dish, was very very poorly done. Jackfruit dish was not up to standard at all. It was a truly disappointing and forgettable experience. Thank you but I think we have had our final visit. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I love to eat here whenever I am in Chiang Mai. They serve many unique Northern dishes which can be hard to find elsewhere at very reasonable prices. Eating in this restaurant is like sitting in an antique shop, a great atmosphere. The only problem can be the rather slow service and it does get very busy (but the service is slow even when they are not busy). Also be aware that it is in a very dark street where nothing else is open around it at night, so it can be hard to find (also the main sign is in Thai script). Also take some insect spray as the restaurant is not enclosed.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I know lots of people would hate this place but we loved it. It's a fascinating place, it's completely chock full of antiques, all a bit dusty with beautiful but mismatched crockery. The dishes are on the small side, not a problem just order more, it's very cheap. It is a little hard to find and in fact looks closed in the evening , that's because you have to go through an ordinary looking cafe to get to the main restaurant. We hadn't made a reservation and it was busy but don't be put off if this happens to you just ask if you can come back in an hour or less, we did and there were a few tables to choose from. It's not modern but has bags of charm!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Went here a couple of weeks for lunch twice. Food was really tasty fresh and delicious. Service was fast and staff helpful! Prices were amazingly low. Thought the serve sizes were good as you were then able to try more dishes! Would definately recommend !

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It's a nice local restaurant which serves great foods. They serve both lunch and dinners, cheap prices and has some outdoor plus indoor seating. We went for a lunch and a dinner, dinner was indoor and was quite busy with lots of tourists. The foods were tasted great and we enjoyed the place. Though it was a little busy not if you are wishing to give a try for local foods then try here!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: During our 6th month in Bangkok, we paid a visit to Chiang Mai and dined at Huen Phen on the recommendation of a trusted local there. We were not disappointed. Our party of four included a vegetarian, a picky Western eater, and a spicy food wimp. Everyone was happy with dinner here. The menu was huge and full of interesting selections in many categories. The food was well prepared and delicious. The prices were not cheap, but still reasonable. The indoor atmosphere was quite unique, funky, and alluring. I read some of the negative reviews here and would offer a few things to note if you're not a Thai native: - The service here is what many outsiders would call "slow". (So is much of the pace of Thailand, but that's certainly beside the point.) - The portions here are what many non-Thais would call "not very big". If your build resembles that of a refrigerator, you may want to order at least two dishes, a 'Bia Singha' and maybe one of the excellent desserts. The prices are reasonable, and this lets you try more different things. - Northern Thai cuisine is different from most of the Thai dishes typically found in a Western Thai restaurant (or even a Bangkok Thai restaurant). I'd call it a bit more 'gamey' and pungent. Even the curries are different. All is definitely and unavoidably spicy (unless you state that you want otherwise, preferably in Thai). - The staff's English is very limited, so communicating things in English like, "It's not too spicy, is it?" and then expecting them to understand you is not realistic here. If you have strong culinary feelings about spiciness, or if you have food allergies, etc., then I would suggest learning the few short key phrases in Thai that you'll need - it's not that hard, even if you're only in the country for a couple weeks. - In keeping with Thai restaurant tradition, dishes arrive at the table in the order that they are done being prepared in the kitchen. This can easily result in the scenario where the "first eater" is finishing up before the "last eater" has even seen their plate. Another reason to order many things and just share them among the table. The beef noodles, the khao soi, the steamed fish, and the spicy minced pork salad were *great*. The sticky rice was right on, too. It turns out that I can't eat som-tam with that salty blue crab, either, but I'm certainly not going to fault the restaurant for serving me a classic Thai dish that I ordered, just because I didn't happen to like it. That's part of the adventure, I think, and this restaurant has plenty of culinary adventure on its broad menu. Good luck! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen has two faces. For lunch the famous Kao Soi eateries packed with people coming from everywhere to have the opportunity to visit one of the Kao Soi "sanctuaries" in Chiang Mai. At night, just next door, the son of the owner reinvented the daily restaurant in a cozy atmosphere of "his own home" full of antiques and memorabilia. The quality of the food is the same and much to appreciate!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Like almost all restaurants in Chiang Mai, really good value for money. It's distinguishing featured north thai cuisine, so some ppl may not really like it. But it's well worth to try, as long as you don't really want to eat what you always eat in your own country. Go here for dinner, the environment is good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Saw this recommended on a blog and when we got here and saw the array of local food on display (it reminded me of an Indonesian rice table) and that the place was full of locals we were hooked. The fried chicken and the dry curried fish in banana leaf were my favourites. The price was excellent (I've attached a copy of the menu so you can see that each dish cost little more than a $1 each!) and we just wanted to order everything. We nearly did and felt very piggish, especially when we couldn't physically finish it all! We loved it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I would not recommend this restaurant. When we got there it was all full of people with their copies of Lonely Planet under their arms. We had to wait but it is not why we were disappointed. We were disappointed because: 1) service was very long; 2) the two dishes out of three we ordered were not available; 3) the portions were very small (in the "chicken with cashew nuts" there were only two small pieces of chicken, the rest was boiled onions). And last but not least, when we ordered Papaya salad with Salty crab we expressly asked if it was not too spicy and the reply was "not spicy". But when we got it, it was impossible to eat. Overall, the food is too spicy there and I, who loves spicy food, almost did not touch my plate. It is also located in the place where it is difficult to find taxi or tuk-tuk so you will have to walk to find it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The sai-uah (pork sausage) is wonderful here, as are many of the dishes. Worth a visit..we preferred the funky dining room at night versus the somewhat typical lunchtime restaurant (they're separate places).

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A honest old tuktuk driver took us here when we asked him to take us someplace where "Thai people go to eat" and was he spot on! Large menu, good service, superlative food - go here if you want to eat authentic food at very reasonable charges. Its simple ambiance is welcoming and comfortable. You can feel that this place is all about feeding the guests. The service is quick and cheerful even though the service staff speak very little English. Go for an early lunch on Sunday and then walk the Sunday market, which is a stone's throw from this place, to digest it. Enjoy!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen was recommended to us by a number of different people and since it was just down the street from where we were staying, decided to give it a try. The restaurant was almost full when we got there so we felt that was a good sign, but overall the experience was a letdown. The food was good but the portions were much smaller than we would get at other Thai restaurants we have been to, and coupled with the higher prices the value was just not there. We had 3 dishes (we were 2 people) and a drink each and our bill was 350 baht - we had a similar meal the night before and also got dessert for only 260 baht. With the smaller portions at Huen Phen we wandered off to the street food market after dinner to supplement the meal. The fried chicken dish was at best appetizer size for example. The service was also very lacking for what I would consider to ba a "dining room" type place, and we often had to watch for a server and wave them down to get things. Given all the things we had heard about the place it was a letdown and we have had equal quality meals for better price/value at other places. Some people will want to go to be able to say they have been, but I would recommend people to look elsewhere, there are better options available. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A good restaurant. They use heart to make the food. I like the spareribs and the Kao soi even it's my first time to taste it. The waiter is very helpful that he tried his best of his English. But I still not very sure about the difference between khao Ngew and sticky rice. The mixed dessert is good but a bit too sweet. Overall, it a good place for a fast and simple meal in a north Thailand cuisine.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Yes, Huen Phen is the real thing. Go for lunch, and enjoy; this is one of the very best local joints in Chiang Mai. An encyclopedic menu; it's all wonderful. And if in doubt, order what everyone else is ordering.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The Chicken Kou Soi (Northern Thai/Burmese Curry) is where it is at. I also ordered the crispy papaya salad, which was also very good. Located very close to Wat Chedi Luang

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This amazing restaurant aethetically appears similar to a garage cluttered with unrelated objects scattered all over the place in each room! But this gives the restaurant an overall warm charm about the place. The local food is very cheap and most importantly is to a very good quality!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If the service matched the food, this place would be highly recommended and the some. Unfortunately, it didn't. After we were seated, we waited 10 min. To get our menus and another 15 min to place our order - and another 10 minutes before our Singha arrived. Patience might be a virtue; but, neglect is a sin. A wash at best. Once the food finally arrived, it was excellent. Deep fried short ribs - ridiculously delicious. Everything was tasty. Unfortunately, the neglect of the staff continued throughout the meal. Cons: frustrating service/attentiveness. Pros: Excellent food. Bad service can ruin a good meal. Would we go back? Not sure.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want to eat some delicious and inexpensive food and go where the locals go, this is your best bet. Thai Dishes are about 30-50 THB - approx $1-$2 per dish. We went here for lunch - I got the Khao soi noodle dish, which was a great introduction to northern Thai curry. It is a must. Staff was pleasant - our food arrived within 5 minutes of ordering, which is great bc we were starving. Location is convenient. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our guidebook recommended this restaurant and it was a terrific experience to eat here. The entrance is filled with plants and you might think you are entering a store (and you are if it isn't lunch or evening) however once inside the tables are easily spotted along with a array of interesting Thai antiques.Each room held a number of fascinating items to look at and ponder - lovely serving dishes, porcelain painted elephants (my favourite) and wonderful lanterns. The menu is substantial and has the classic Thai dishes and they constituted some of the most delicious food we ate in Thailand. The wait staff were attentive to the seafood allergy note we showed them and drew attention to menu items not including shrimp and fish sauce. The soft shelled crab and Tom Yeum along with a green curry soup were the highlights.If you are in Chiang Mai make time to visit this restaurant. Very good value. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We want to huen phen for lunch after they were closed last night when we wanted to have dinner. The gentleman who served us was friendly enough, but the place looked nothing like the pictures up on TA. More like a dirty diner. Also,the wooden sign out the front was in fact a neon Pepsi sign, but the school across e road was definitely there, so perhaps they've changed? I don't care a jot about decor, but the food was dreadful. We ordered two curries and an eggplant dish, all of which came out suspiciously fast, and tepid at best. The pork curry was alright, but the chicken curry was described by my truly unfussy husband as "bits of stringy weeds suspended in some vaguely chicken flavoured oil". Very disappointing, as we were really looking forward to this meal. Yuk. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is a good restaurant with a fair taste and good atmosphere.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen specialises in northern Thai food, and boy do they do it well. I remember the portions being pretty cheap (~50-90 B), but deceptively small though. We ordered 5 dishes for 4 people and it wasn't quite enough. I recommend their sampling platter where they give you a selection of northern Thai snacks. Also the Burmese pork curry was the melt-in-your-mouth deliciousness (I wanted to go back and get it again the whole time I was in Chiang Mai and still regret not doing it). Also the decor inside is pretty neat. Antiques and stuff.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was quite good. Dessert was best, Thai red curry was really horrible

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: What an amazing place! For starters, a bit difficult to find, this restaurant is beautifully decorated with old Asian antiques.It is hidden from the street, cozy and private and has good service. But this is not the only nice thing about this place. The food rocks! This is the place to go and try Norther Thai food, so rare in any European and American thai restaurants. Try green chilli paste with steamed vegetables (!!!) for starters and then one of their recommended dishes. It is a true unique experience for the senses. Lonely Planet gives is a lousy review for no good reason. The food is great and experience likewise. Prices shockingly low (everything 40-100 BHT). This restaurant is as good as in Lotus of Siam in Las Vegas, voted as one of the best Thai places in USA. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food is just great although portions are rather small and the amount of chili might be too much for some people. For a truly unique experience, order the set menu (USD 12 for two) and try to reserve the cushioned bed right next to the entrance. It's so much fun sitting in there and taste those Northern Thai delights.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This review based on two of us ordering the set banquet for two. We were expecting more. Crispy pork was just fried pork crackling and the assortment of other items of very small portions. Don't order this option. Perhaps if we had tried individual orders our impressions would have been more favourable. We had dessert at a place not far from where we were staying.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I have been going here for years and I always enjoy it. The food is good, the service is good. We like to experiment with the different dishes we cannot eat elsewhere in Thailand. Prices are a good value. The only problem is the location as upon finishing, it's difficult to find transportation.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: we visited Huen Phen based on reviews and a recommendation from someone at our hotel, and we weren't disappointed. We went in the evening, and it wasn't full - there were no locals in there though - which is what we had been expecting, The service was excellent, and the food was fabulous, hot and spicy, fresh and authentic, and the prices were fabulous. We loved sampling the northern Thai specialities - would love to go back

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was the #1 recommendation by the guy at the front desk who worked at 3 Sis for Chiang Mai style and northern Thai food. Some friends of ours also recommended this place as well. We stopped by in the daytime, and it was packed--but we didn't wait long. We really enjoyed the khao soy here!! In the daytime, it is not a fancy place, but I think it gets fancier at night.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: There are many options of dishes at Huen Phen but if you are food lover yes of course! You would know how real nothern thai food taste like! Nothing outstanding.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We enjoyed our lunch here and felt Ike we got off the tourist path a bit. They have family style tables and quick service. Go for good northern style food and great people watching.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The best Khao soi i ever had. Good food, inexpensive price. There are 2 dinning zones at Huen Phen. Day time eating area is easy and simple, while the evening time dining place is unique and rich of culture. I recommended anyone who is interested to local food and art to have a 'dinner' in this place. Apart from great taste, you will enjoy sitting in the museum atmosphere and all the details on the plates.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was the first resturant we were sent to by the hotel once they realised that we were serious about trying local food. It is mostly frequented by locals, but the staff are sufficiently reassuring and the food is sufficiently well illustrated so as not to be daunting to the less than adventurous.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was recommended this restaurant by our Guest House's owner. After half and hour of walking from our guest house, we finally found the restaurant. It was 230pm, thus not crowded. Waiter quickly gave us the menu and we were surprised that price of food listed is very reasonable and consider cheap. Just within few minutes, our food is ready and we had one of our best lunch in Thailand. Food is very nice! We're love it. Understand that many people complained about their bad services, but come on, this is just a local restaurant. This is like many coffee house + basic restaurant at the corner road. The staff has not been trained nor study in hotel management degree. They are probably just a part timer. So, please don't expect they come to serve you like you had it in 3 or 4 stars Michelin restaurant. Don't ask for recommendation as they simply don't know what you guys like/dislike to eat. Just simply choose it from the menu, you will be surprise how good/bad is the food. Be more adventures. : ) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I read about this resto in Singapore Airline's inflight magazine and wanted to give it a try. I stepped into this restaurant last night (25 Feb) and was ignored by the staff though I've been standing by the entrance for minutes! Tried catching the eye of a waiter who finally asked me if I need a table. But that's all he did, he just asked. However, I still walked my way in to see if some thoughtful staff could offer me a seat and still I was ignored. I simply left disgusted with the whole experience. There are other good restaurants in the city but if you want to be insulted and humiliated, then go to Huen Phen.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Got there at 630pm. The Resturant was full. They told us it would be 45 minutes for a table to open up. Within 5 minutes we got a table, only because my wife grabbed a table that a couple just left. So we sat at the table for a while before they cleaned it. They finally took our order after 10 minutes. It took over 30 minutes to get our food. The appetizers were served with the main course. Which defeats the purpose of ordering appetizers. Plus they forgot to give us one of our main dishes. By the time we could get the staff attention we were finished with our dinner. The food is ok, we've had better Thai food. But the huge turn off for us was the attitude of the staff. They could careless if we never ate or even stopped by. We saw numerous western people sitting there for long time just waiting for a menu. Then waiting for their drinks. We will never go back there. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were introduced to this restaurant after visiting Wat Jedi Lerng. Hard to find at first as maps were confusing but finally we got there, we were greeted by the lady wearing a Thai costume. It was crowded with customers. Food was tasty not too spicy. Pricing on the menu was reasonable and friendly service. No wonder they are very popular.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to this restaurant at the recommendation of our hotel staff. Very close to the main attractions in the old city area. Very local decor and nothing much to speak. We order the usual local food and was very impressed with the tastes. Many dishes are quite dry in comparison to same dishes in other restaurants, but I must say, the tastes are much better. I would gladly go back for more in my next visit to Chiang mai. This is one restaurant not to missed!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Our hotel recommended it, but on our first try we couldn't find it. It looked like it was closed, but we had to walk about 10 meters farther down the street and the entrance was set way back from the street. There isn't anything else around it it, and at night the street is poorly lit and not very inviting (but perfectly safe). We didn't have a reservation, so we took the last table, which was a couch hidden behind stacks of antiques. A cozy spot for a romantic dinner, but you have to sit crosslegged on the sofa, so not for those with arthritic knees! We had to wave for attention a few times, but given our seating location and busy night, I can understand. We definitely would recommend it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This was recommended by a friend from Chiang Mai as a place to try "authentic" northern Thai food. The food was really good (most anyway). We started with the mixed starter platter as it had a lot of what we wanted to eat anyway. The sausages and nam prik nun (sp?) was so delicious we wanted to order them again. The curries we ordered were really good. The soup was also good but not great. The morning glory is a PASS - I would not order it again. It wasn't very good. Overall, it was nice. The interior is filled with artefacts (likely replicas, but I don't know) and we were put in a nice little nook so we were not out in the open. We had a great time and it is worth a trip for the starters alone! Not the typical cheap food you get on the streets, but it's worth a stop. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are claustrophobic this may not be place to dine peacefully. The restaurant is like an antique shop with all sorts of collections from different parts of the world. BUT the place is overfilled with them. I felt if I leaned the wrong way or stretch my arms out, I might take down some stuff from the shelf. The stuff are dusty too. It was fun to look around while waiting for our food. This take us back to the point of visiting the restaurant-- to get dinner. It was recommended by our hotel. When we got there about 6:30, we were seated almost immediately. Though the service did not come right away. I guess the 3 staff was not sufficient or efficient in doing their waiting. They would bring one thing out, instead of taking things bottles or cans from our table or the next, they would just walked back empty handed. The great thing about this place is the authenticity of the food. The flavor was pungent with the use of pickled crab, and pork skin. We ordered various samplers including homemade sausage and fish patties. We also had the papaya salad, local soup, and young jackfruit. Hard to describe now...but worthy of a trip out. By the time we left about 8:00 pm, there was a line of about 20 people waiting for seats. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My wife and I had dinner at Huen Phen since it was strongly recommended to us. It was a negative experience from start to finish. In my opinion, there are far better options nearby. We arrived shortly after dinner service started, so there was no waiting line, which was good. What was not so good was to be ignored instead of being shown a table, although this could have been attributed to lack of staff, and the fact that the restaurant was already more than half-full. We had to approach the staff and ask for a table ourselves. Crammed into a small room, within elbow distance of 2 other tables, and surrounded by what seemed to be hundreds of small statuettes/idols. I suppose some would have found the setting "exotic"- I just found it claustrophobic. My wife found it eerie and uncomfortable to eat surrounded by heaps of dusty deities- somewhat like dining in a tomb. The food was decent enough, but not terribly good value. I liked the presentation, and the fried chicken was tasty, though not any more so than what one can get along the street. Service was efficient but terribly harried and brusque-- this was probably the only meal we had in our 1 week in Chiang Mai, where the staff didn't smile, and instead simply rushed here and there with vacant, stressed out expressions on their faces. As I mentioned, the place seemed understaffed, which is the fault of the owners, but in my opinion, dining is as much about ambience as food, and one without the other renders the entire experience a failure. By the time we left, about an hour later, there was a queue at the door waiting to enter. We won't be returning to Huen Phen until they spruce up the place, and hire more staff. With the turnover they are getting, they should be able to afford to. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went to Huen Phen during peak lunch time, so we were surrounded with locals, which is a good sign that the place is going to be authentic. Having 2 young kids with us, we ordered the fried chicken and pork ribs with sticky rice - and they had no problems finishing them to the bone. The curry noodles is decent and like all the reviews, the sausages were the best we have tried after a week in CM. Do try their ice lemon tea, its a refreshing treat and remember to sit inside with a quaint courtyard. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Good taste of Northern food but the restaurant decoration gives uncomfortable feeling.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This food is very traditional northern Thai. We were all about trying something different but this may have been a bit too much for our palates, and I'm normally a pretty adventurous eater. Prices were low but portions are small. Our experience was a "2" but I'm giving it a "3" because we just didn't like the food.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Must have been the most crowded place in Chiang Mai. The first evening we did not wait 45 minutes to get seated so the next day we booked a table, recommended! Good local and Thai food and also wine (imported) at fair prices. The service was alert and very friendly.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Food was excellent but very small portions and very very slow service. Roll the dice sounds like order of the day

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Yes the service is tardy but then the place is busy and the staff are young so you can see it coming. We found the food very cheap and tasty although admittedly you will eat better on your travels. If you are prepared to sit back and relax you'll enjoy your evening here. Combine this with a very nice setting and it's definitely worth a visit.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My mom and I visited Huen Phen after a wonderful day of elephant touring and and thai massages. The owner of our hostel recommended this place mentioning that it was highly regarded by lonely planet. I checked our 2012 Lonely Planet guide and although it was listed, Lonely Planet had not reviewed it or recommended it in the book. Regardless, we decided to give it a shot. Upon arriving at about 6pm (before the crowd got too crazy), we were greeted by no one and left standing in a very narrow hallway trying to make eye contact with anyone who looked like they worked there. We were not the only ones this way. After about 15 minutes of hoping for the best, we were escorted to our table by one of the waiters. After sitting down and reviewing the menu for a little bit of time, the waiter came over, clearly rushed to take our order and get on with things. When we asked about vegetarian options, he hurriedly pointed at a few items and simply said "no" when we asked him if they could make other dishes vegetarian. He was pretty unhelpful in this sense and it would have been nice if he could have taken the time to tell us how many dishes we should have ordered in order to be full. But again, he seemed frantic to simply burn and turn. After ordering, it literally took 20 minutes for the waiter to bring out my Singha beer. Another 10 minutes went by before my very basic vegetarian dish came out (which I could have made in my home blindfolded in about 5 minutes by the way). After noticing how little we ordered and how long things were taking, we went ahead and ordered another dish and our banana in coconut milk desserts. Another 10 minutes and my mom's northern fish soup came out. Another 10 minutes and the final dish came out along with the desserts. After being completely underwhelmed by the food and annoyed by the lack of service, I figured that the staff would at least help us get out in a timely manner....but as I watched the minutes on my watch go by and began stacking our dishes as high as they could go as a smoke signal that we wanted to leave (and this was after verbally asking for our check twice), it was another 20 minutes before we finally received our check. This was strange to me since there was an extremely long line of people waiting to be sat by this time, around 7:45 (forgive me if my math doesn't add up). I'm normally a pretty low maintenance person when it comes to restaurants. I waited tables in the U.S. for 2 years and have traveled extensively around Asia, so I know what kind of service to expect. In my opinion though, the staff at this restaurant did the very bare minimum--take your order, serve you your food, and get you your check...all very slowly. The food was not great. I will say that the atmosphere is very unique and quaint. The location of the restaurant is in some hold in the wall in a relatively obscure street so I can see how it appeals to tourists. If you're willing to sacrifice everything else for this ambience though, then have at it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: we waited for 1hr (we were obliged to wandering in Sunday walking street) before we finally got table around 830pm at the restaurant. Food was most disappointed, very small portion for all dishes. Flavor was ordinary but without surprice. Decoration and environment was really highlight of the restaurant. service is lousy, the waiter brought dishes to our table but even not put it on our table rather wait us to take dishes ourselves. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The decoration is as the other reviews say so if that's what matters to you, go ahead. But the food is not so great. The dishes are a bit unusual, which is fine, but there is no great feeling that they're of good quality or particularly well made. The pork rib curry soup with banana flowers (which some reviewers said was very good) was particularly disappointing, it smelled so awful I couldn't eat it. The service is as bad as people say. I had to go and ask for a menu after sitting there for five minutes, and I felt like I was being treated a lot worse than the locals. The waiters never came to us, we had to get everything - e.g. ordering, napkins, bill - by grabbing them as they went to serve the locals. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We go to Chiang Mai every year and every year we eat here at least once. The ambience is a mash of old meets new art shrines, with Thai, Lao, Indian & Burmese influences. I always bring my camera for photo ops. The food has never disappointed and because the dishes are small plates, the menu allows you to sample a lot of different tastes. I always wish we had a couple more people in tow to sample even more. The price is very reasonable. The service, tho friendly is often lacking in attention, mostly due to the fact that the place is packed out all the time. Don't expect to get in and out of here quickly. And don't be surprised if you have to ask for your bill while you still have plates uncleared. It's all part of the experience :) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went twice on the recommendation of a friend. The first time we ordered our usual amount that we would in another restaurant (one entree a piece with rice and drinks). We had to order more because we were still hungry. We are two petite girls and don't eat much. The second order took so long, we canceled it and left. Came back a second time. The place is very cooly decorated and a feast for the eyes so we thought we would try it again. Second time ordered the special large appetizer and entrees, rice and water. We were the second table to arrive so they were not busy yet. We got our app. and one ent. fairly quickly but the second ent. was came much after my friends and the rice came about 45 minutes later and the water never got there. We asked twice for the bill and waited forever for that. When we were leaving there was a couple who mentioned to us that they had the same experience that we did but they were giving it a second chance. I told them good luck, paid the bill and will not go back. We get better food much quicker at the small walk in street restaurants. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is located in the Old City. I recommend eating there for dinner (5-10 PM) because the meals are served in a gorgeous dining room that resembles a Northern Thai antique shop. The service is up to Thai standards and there is a wide variety of Northern Thai offerings, including the special sausage which is made with the distinct Northern Thai spices. The noodle dishes were very well-prepared as were the fish and chicken dishes. All-in-all, a good value for the money.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went for lunch and thoroughly enjoyed it! Great atmosphere. We had fried pork rinds, sticky rice, thai sausage, a mango or papaya salad, a chicken dish and a couple of beers. The chicken dish didn't have a lot of chicken in it, but everything was cheap and delicious. Would definitely go back.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you go to Chiangmai,you shoud try northern style food of Thai. And sure,should Huen Phen.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The food was pretty tasty, but the price was great. Our entire meal was less than $5.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My girlfriend and I went here for lunch after searching for an authentic northern Thailand meal. We were led to Huen Pen by a couple of locals. At 1pm the lunch crowd was in full force and waiters were whipping around the restaurant taking orders from the fully Thai dominated crowd. We ordered chicken curry, stuffed pork sausage, & chicken/pork khao soi. Great flavor & spice. Recommended if anything else, just to watch the locals eat.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Have eaten here twice in the past 3 weeks. The first time we stumbled into the place by accident in the middle of the afternoon. We were happy with the place and the food (delicious traditional dessert!). The second time, after eating several mediocre meals in places claiming to have authentic Northern Thai food, we made our way back intentionally. Food was even tastier the second time. Huen Phen has a split operation. The main restaurant opens from 8:30 a.m. and closes at 4 p.m. then they open just a section of the restaurant, with limited offerings (no Khao Soi, Kanom Chin, and several of the Northern curries) and substantially higher prices, at 5 p.m. until 10 p.m. During the day they are very busy and, following one of my rules to knowing if a restaurant is good and a good deal or not, the place is packed with more Thais than farangs. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: we ate at huen phen on our first night in chiang mai, and athough it was about a 30 min walk from our hotel it was well worth it, lovely thai meals in a nice relaxing setting either inside or outside, and was rather busy.. fast service and always with a smile. another beautiful hidden secrets in chiang mai

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Let's just be crystal clear: this is a street restaurant. So, although it has an indoors dining room and plenty of staff, you'll sit in plastic chairs, the bowls and plates will be basic melamine, the food won't be fancy and the menu will be grubby bits of paper in a plastic folder. With me? Good. So what you *will* get is a whole lot of authentic northern Thai food, including the delicious Khao Soi curry, and you'll pay peanuts for it. I've had sandwiches back in England that cost more than an entire meal for two with fresh coconut milk to drink. We love food, and wanted to try every single restaurant in Chiang Mai (impossible!), but we still managed to find time to come here twice. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen is tucked away from the main road and as you keep walking along the dark alley and the beautiful aroma of Thai cooking will reassure any first time visitors that this is the right place. Make no mistake, this is not an up-market, chi-chi restaurant but it definitely serves authentic northern thai cuisine at some very very reasonable prices.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We had a dinner in this restaurant after reading very good reviews in a travel guide book. I have to say we were very disappointed. If the decoration is nice, the food and the service were average. You can find a lot of other good restaurants in Chiang Mai with much better service, better food and lower prices.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: our tour guide took us to Huen Phen during our day with him and it was very good. It is incredibly inexpensive and the food was very good. All full of locals with a small amount of tourists which was nice.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: It is located quite a walking distance away from where I stayed which is near to Tha Pae gate. I went there in the evening and I didn't see many people on the street while on the way and I felt unsafe. However this restaurant is right in the hidden quiet street. They were lack of staffs, I could only see 2 staffs serving 6-7 tables of customers! Surprisingly the food were served faster than we thought. The dishes are delicious, suitable for those who like strong flavor. For those first timers, I recommend to order the Khantoke dinner set if you can't make up your mind. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Inspired a little by the positive reviews, we checked out this cafe-restaurant. It is well located and looks the (Northern Thai) part. We both selected the noodle soup, a Thai staple. Disappointment describes the experience. At 35 baht the meal was certainly affordable, but while the stock for the soup was strong and vivid, and even the thin little chicken's leg was tasty$ but luke warm, the presentation was lacking. A small plate with a pile of onions, lime and picked cabbage, unsorted and looking tired accompanied the standard plate of greens. Here we were faced with browned edged mint leaves (how old were they?) and green beans that were so limp they could be bent backwards without snapping. Something that should have been fresh, vibrant and rich instead came across as old, rushed together and, frankly, very poor. There is much much better available in Chiang Mai, where we have been for the past eight weeks. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: When you see a place where 95 percent of customers are local then it s a good spot to try! They have fixed menu but cheap and great tasting local food. Some of the items which are on the menu you will not find in normal "Farang" approved places! I have been eating lunch every day and love it!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want to taste the real northern Thai cuisine the way the local eat then look no where else. This is the place. I had lunch there for 3 consecutive days. Simply wonderful.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The restaurant was filled with wooden relics. The food was moderately priced for thai standards. We found locals and tourist at the location. My husband had the tasty crispy pork which he raved about and I would recommend. My only observation is that they really, really need to dust off some of those relics.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This is a great place to stop in while touring old city. Popular dishes there seem to be the som tam, grilled pork, fried chicken, and the beef noodles. Everything we had was fantastic. The beer was cheap. The service was great. The restaurant was comfortable. Definitely stop into Huen Phen when in Chiang Mai! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You might miss the narrow entry to Huen Phen's unless you look up for the sign, but keep looking and follow the tile path into what looks like a cosy antique shop with small tables scattered throughout its three rooms. Burmese carvings, Buddhas, screens, cabinets stuffed with Asian curios make dining here a delight for the eyes as well as the palate. The food is from the Isaan, or northeast area of Thailand, and features red and green curries, tangy sausages, spicy salads, and rice that comes wrapped in a cone-shaped banana leaf. The menu indicates what's hot, as in spicy, and what's not, so you can choose just what your palate will tolerate. Service is pleasant and unobtrusive, and the cold beer is very welcome after a hot day seeing the sights. Foreigners and locals alike eat here; we heard Thai, Mandarin, French, and English on a recent visit. Can't make it for dinner? Try Huen Phen's noon-time khao-soi noodle buffet. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Learned of this restaurant on Trip Advisor, and sought it out on a dark, rainy night when most of the neighborhood seemed shut down, in spite of being 2-3 blocks off the Sunday night market. Dinner was in what seemed like a homey antique & art shop. The dishes were fresh and bursting with flavor, and there were many local and Northern Thai influences influenced. We loved it so much we went back for lunch, and were seated in a patio up front, on the street. Different ambiance, many local people crowding the many tables, but the same great food, and the same incredible prices. We particularly loved their fried chicken, a bit of comfort food but still uniquely done and probably the best ever, in my opinion. One of the best dining experiences in our 3.5 week trip through S.E. Asia. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I love the decor of the restaurant. I went there for dinner with family and friends. They serve northern food - tapas style. You should order many dishes to share. It seems to have a lot more foreigners than Thai eating there. I enjoy their norther sausage and grilled chicken.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My daughter and I ate at Huen Phen three times during our week in Chiang Mai, twice for lunch and once for dinner. We especially enjoyed the Khao Soi which is only served at lunch. Our concierge recommended Huen Phen from a list of restaurants I had compiled from tripadvisor and travel guide recommendations, so we took a tuk tuk from our hotel to the restaurant for lunch. Upon arrival at the restaurant, we were promptly greeted and seated. We were the only non-Thai diners in the restaurant. The server presented us with an English/Thai picture menu and we ordered five dishes, all of which were inexpensive and delicious! Our server asked if we wanted our som tam (papaya salad) spicy and chuckled when we replied, "yes, Thai spicy"! The som tam was just as we ordered. The service was excellent and the five dishes plus three honey lemon iced teas came to $8.00. My daughter had to order an adittional iced tea to help quench the spiciness of the som tam! Our second visit was for dinner and admittedly we were a little disappointed, different menu, different ambience. Dinner is located in an enclosed area of the restaurant with a lovely ecclectic decor. The restaurant was filled with diners and even though we did not have a reservation we were seated at what appeared to be the only available table, within minutes. The menu was extensive (no pictures) and the prices were substantially higher. Service was attentive even though the limited number of waiters were kept quite busy serving the full house. We ordered several dishes and were disappointed with the very small portions but everything we ordered was delicious. We weren't asked if we wanted our som tam spicy and the server must have assumed we were typical tourist because the som tam was totally spice-less, no chilis were used in the preparation, so we had to pile on the heat with the table condiments. Dinner was not a total disappointment but we vowed we would limit our visits to lunch. We enjoyed a delicious array of dishes at Huen Phen in the street side dining area, the following day around noon. Once again, the restaurant was packed with diners and all the servers were quite busy but the same server who greeted us two days previously appeared and found us a small table for two. Again, we were totally delighted with the lunch service and the food! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Arrived midday on a religious festival day (all the nearby temples were busy). It was busy but we got a table. Very fast service. The eggplant salad was out of this world. Have a cha yen (iced milk tea). As it was a special day the place was full of families including little old grannies... Perhaps that's the best recommendation possible.. If you would take your granny there it must be good! Also temples are within walking distance.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've eaten here several times and as a resident of Chiang Mai find the food a poor example of northern Thai cuisine, plus the portions are small and the prices high. Tourists may not know the difference, but one can eat extremely well in Chiang Mai for much less. I will admit that at night the ambiance in the antique-filled back of the restaurant is fun, but it's a distraction from the mediocre food. Do a little homework and you will find much tastier northern Thai food for better prices. ( I would recommend some, but don't know if that is allowed here) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Delicious northern Thai cuisine. Recommend the curries and sausage. Very affordable prices.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: North Thai Style Food - Authentic Huen Phen is not hole-in the wall as I read in one of the review. It is relative large restaurant with several separate sections. I ate there twice, both times for lunch. Lots of “chachkes” around, so if they are busy, you have something to look at while you wait for your order. They were not open in the evening, but that was possibly because New Year Celebration (Songkran). The sign indicated that they are normally open 17:00 to 22:00. Food was very good, good selection, not the "Same, Same" as touristy town are know for. I think the dishes were about 65-90 baht. ($2.20 to $3.00) . Portions could have been bigger. Even rice we had to order one extra. It was in very small bamboo container. The reason I came twice was, that I was a bit disappointed with food offered elsewhere. This restaurant is better then average budget place. It is in relatively quiet area of center Chiang Mai, but not hard to find. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: not that hard to get to. great atmosphere inside, very ancient and ethnic... lots of ornaments to look at like pots, wooden things and bowls etc. wish i had a picture to show! i had pork in leaves (can't remember what it was called on the menu!) tasted amazing like nothing i have ever had before. the rice came in a leaf shaped in a cone.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We went for dinner which appears to be somewhat distinct in atmosphere from lunch when the front of the restaurant is used, closer to the street, and may be both very busy and somewhat noisier by report. In the evening one walks through the empty, open air front half to reach the inner dining area which is choc full of wonderful antiques. These include wood and stone carvings, weavings, wooden vessels, figurines and more from floor to ceiling, creating unique seating areas with plenty to gaze at. Service was fast and food was fresh with an extensive menu. Prices were incredibly reasonable such that although we didn't order more than one dish each we easily could have. Portions were delightfully appropriate (i.e. non-gargantuan American). Casual dress is fine and groups of 2 to 6 or even 8 can be accommodated in the several uniquely cozy seating areas. Would return here with friends and family many times were we to be in town longer. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Big menu, authentic taste and very very cheap. It's always packed so dont go when you're too hungry. Love their Khao Soi!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Excellent spot for dinner. Tasting menu is great value and gives you lots of excellent small dishes of authentic Thai cuisine. Really cool interior. Great value - 1000bht for dinner and drinks for 2 adults and 2 kids.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Since Khantoke (i.e. low table) dinner is one of the must-try dining in Chiang Mai, we've decided to visit Huen Phen for that, instead of other the touristy site like Old Chiang Mai Cultural Center et. Although there's no dancing show, the food is great: let it be spicy, that's local taste, I love their fried rice in folded egg too. Price is reasonable: the set for 2 person is about 300B, cant really remember though. And atmosphere is great too. Each tables has a different setting, so choosing a table is where the fun starts! I really think this is a great alternative for Khantoke Dinner, if you wish not to look too much like a tourist, and would like to blend in. There are so many interesting things on the menu that I didnt get to try. However, like many places in Chiang Mai, expect for the whole family of mosquitoes to dine with you! Remember to ask the waiters for help. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Pen remains the best local restaurant - which also caters to westerners (by having an English language menu). After many years in the business - I've eaten here for the past 5-8 year - they have maintained their level of quality and service. This is a Thai restaurant - folks go there for food, not pretentious or western style atmosphere. We ate there a couple of times this visit and all of the dishes were well executed with no noticeable mistakes. We'd recommend the salads of pomelo or green egg plant. All of the laabs are (really) exceptional as are the curries. Also try their selection of homemade Thai sausages and for an adventure into "texture" try the fried green bean with pork cracklings ....and well, shoot, have to say it, just have to...some of the best fried chicken in SE asia. highly recommend - please be extra polite to these folks and please dress respectively (AKA not like a bar hopping tourist). I'd hate for them to get jaded by rude or clueless tourists and lose their compassion. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Read the reviews and decided to try this restaurant over one recommended by our hotel's reception that didn't have good reviews. We weren't disappointed. The food is good, the service was satisfactory. The portions are small so it was perfect for ordering many dishes to sample for a party of 4. We especially enjoyed the Sausage Huen Phen style.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: After reading the reviews on Tripadvisor we decided to give this place a try. We tracked it down for dinner only to find it closed for the night for some unexplained reason. Undeterred we went again the next day for lunch. Very unhelpful service got us off to a bad start. But then the food was really disappointing. My dish was cold and overcooked to the extent that it was difficult to tell what you were eating. The only positive was that it wasn't that expensive. The next night we took pot luck at one of the stalls in the market near our hotel and had a sensational meal for about a quarter of the price. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Amazing food and atmosphere... really feels like you're eating in someone's living room. You might not notice it at first because it's a bit set back from the street, but once you're in you'll be happy you found the place.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I can't say enough about the quality and variety of Northern Thai dishes served here. I usually go for lunch where seating is open air. Dinner in the small dining room is more of an upscale experience. The Naem fried ribs are not to be missed!!! Assorted steamed fresh vegetables with nam phrik, a serving of sticky rice and a large Singha beer and that is heaven!!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant was very special. It feels like sitting in a northen Thai living room. Everywhere are cubboards with books, figures, boardgames, drawings and and and The food was delicious but don't expect the central Thai food. Here northern cuisine is served.... very authentic. This place is worth a visit. Recommend !!! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Cheap, tasty northern Thai dishes with quick service. I go there often and usually get my food within minutes of ordering it. There are few places with such simple but delicious tastes that I enjoy more. It is like eating dim sum or tapas and getting Thai food. Few restaurants I have been too provide more bang for the buck and have such a pleasant local/foreigner ambience.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We ate at this restaurant a couple of nights ago after reading good reviews on here. I have to say it was not all that good. Service was extremely slow, when our food eventually came out we were very disappointed. Our chicken dish had two small pieces of chicken in it and came out luke warm, the mixed starter we ordered was tasteless and cold. It was cheap but no more so that other restaurants we have eaten in this week. Do yourself a favour and don't waste your money, go to Hinlay Curry House or Lemongrass which are both as cheap but SO much nicer!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This restaurant offers a good variety of Northern Thai specialties. Their sauces are really good. The prices are extremely reasonable. Although we were not blown away by the food, and understand from the locals that the food quality has gone down in the past year or so, each of the dishes were solid, except for a barely-warm soup. I wouldn't steer people away, but there are better choices in town.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: We were recommended to try this place by the concierge at our hotel (ping nakara) and it was one of the only things they did which we were pleased by (service at the hotel is disappointing). The restaurant is a simple, local eatery but it knocksout excellent Thai food. We popped in for a quick lunch and couldn't help bu order more than we needed because it was all so good. The staff are well informed, understand English well and are helpful, if a little slow a times. Ask them for the typical northern Thai dishes and you wot go wrong! Enjoy! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: The best place to have a original northern thai (Lanna) food in a very resonable price .Nice decoration full of antique pieces , food original lanna taste .

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Filled with Thais & farang, this little gem serves tasty, authentic food at very reasonable prices. Drinks, sticky rice for 3, & 4 medium size meals filled up 2 people for only 175 baht - less than US$6.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: can't fault this place. the food was amazing, the portions for main course may appear small but trust me they are very filling! the restaurant itself is beautiful inside with very friendly staff and it is extremely cheap. well worth the visit!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I've been to Huen Pen several times, both for lunch and dinner. If you go during lunch time, you will see lots of local people. You can select the prepared dishes that you want at the front of the restaurant. No need for menus, just point at whatever looks good. Very cheap and authentic Northern Thai food. My favorite is Kanomjeen Nam-ngeo (White noodle with northern style pork soup) and the northern sausage. If you go during dinner, it is a different dining room. More beautiful decorations and atmostphere, but also more pricey. You can order from the menu. There will be more foreigners. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place rated so highly on TripAdvisor so thought we'd check it out. Firstly, the atmosphere is great. Really nice interior and lovely setting for dinner. Service was OK, nothing special, but good enough. Food was small portions. Negative: The prawns were horrible. Not sure if they were off or something, but they just tasted wrong. I took one bite and had to spit it out into my napkin. The baby octopus also didn't taste very good. Personally I won't be recommending this place to friends. Had a much better time all-round at Ginger and Riverside. One other small thing was the bill. The menu said prices were ''plus taxes and service charges'' - which was usually 5% tax and 10% service charge elsewhere. Our bill came to 360THB, then they added 140THB in taxes and service charges, making it 500THB total. Still cheap and we didn't complain, but the maths didn't add up for me. The total should have been 400THB, not 500THB. Seemed they just added an extra 100THB for the sake of it which just left a bad taste in my mouth. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Visited the place twice in 3 days , good place to sample local food and readily amend the recipe to cater for kids ( remove the spice) .Their fried chicken is good and you can have a good meal without breaking your budget.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I love the way they present Thai food especially when they wrap the rice with banana cone. All dishes are prepared with supreme Thai style and taste good.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: You will not missed this ! Its situated right outside of Wat Chedi Luang. A very nice and cosy atmosphere. It has a fusion of Thai and other culture traditional. My partner and I chose to sit in a small hut, sat in a folding leg position and surrounded by nice comfortable pillows. If you have no idea of what to call, try their "Khantoke Menu for Two". It comes with 6 dishes(portion for 2), 1 dessert each, and sticky rice as well. Their crispy pork and curry chicken is fantastic. And if you deep your sticky rice with sauces, its really delicious ! You might end up wanting to just go with sticky rice and sauce alone. All in only 300 baht ! (about US$8 each person). Its really good to sit in and relax even after the meal. Staff are really friendly and nice ! You really got to try this one ! :) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was our favorite meal in the two weeks we spent in Thailand. They have lots of options. It's all good and all very cheap (great value). They have options to eat in air conditioning or open air. Yum, I miss it.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: i always take my friends who are visiting CM to Huen PHen -- the secret is to know that you can go in the day time (the day time cafe is next to the restaurant) and is brilliant and in the evening the restaurant itself is a hidden gem.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I ate at Huen Phen for lunch and dinner both. I preferred my lunch, but both were excellent. It was the best food I had in Thailand...in a restaurant. The cool thing about Chiang Mai, and Huen Phen, is this in Northern Thai. It's different than the rest of Thailalnd, you only get this stuff here... I can't see why anyone could call this place expensive. By American standards it is budget dining.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: A nearby travel agent recommended this to us - we would never have found it otherwise as its not on one of the main drags. The selection of Northern Thai cuisine was one of the most comprehensive we had encountered but the vast choice did not mean a long wait. The service staff were friendly and helped guide us with our meal selection - highlighting if anything was particularly spicy and you could see some of the dishes prepared in the open kitchen alongside the restaurant. The restaurant appears popular with locals and also has an outlet at the night bazaaqr food court which offers a limited selection but is also of a high standard.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This popular hole-in-the-wall serves great khao soi noodles, a classic Chiang Mai noodle dish. The crispy noodles are served in a spicy broth with cilantro and your choice of meat, and the place is usually packed for lunch. We asked a taxi driver to take us to a lunch spot where locals eat, and we eventually found out that the place has also been written up by the NY Times. I tried khao soi noodles at several other restaurants during my stay in Chiang Mai and Huen Phen definitely had the best.
Date_Written: up by the NY Times.  I tried khao soi noodles at several other restaurants during my stay in Chiang Mai and Huen Phen definitely had the best.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I went there for dinner with my partner.... we ordered two dishes, a fried rice, and a bamboo soup with sticky rice. Thinking that would be enough.... they were I think 70 baht for each dish. The portions are TINY. Had to order another dish, a curry next.... but still didn't fill him up (he's got a big appetite). At least the food was pretty tasty, though!! There were a few groups of locals there, which is always a good sign. It's really quirky too - got lots of knick knacks in the restaurant. Good character! Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: This place was very cute and you felt like you were in an antique shop almost! The dishes were ok but not really as amazing as I was expecting considering good reviews that we had read.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I had dinner there on the evening of 6 Dec. There were 6 of us in the party, and it's a good thing we arrived early (before 7 pm) as the place was packed as the evening wore on. Food was good, the bill for us came to slightly less than B1000. Cheap by my standards, but I'm not sure whether it's cheap by Thai standards. Oh, be aware - we had to pay cash as the establishment doesn't accept credit cards.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: On a trip to Chiangmai, we had a list of restaurants we wanted to try out. This was the first on the list because I was wanting to try Chiangmai Khao Soi so badly. Along with the Khao Soi, we ordered Larb Kua, Hang Lay Curry, Kanom Jean Nam Ngeow, Namprik Nhum and Som Tum Tord. The food was very quick and the Khao Soi was superb! Hang Lay Curry was also very delicious with very tender meat. The Deep Fried Som Tum was also new for me and I loved it - the papaya was very thinly sliced and deep fried until we could barely taste the papaya it was like some sort of chip. We loved the place so much that on the last day of the trip we had to go back for a huge meal and take away (to Bangkok) before getting on the plane. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I love Huen Phen. A small, atmospheric Thai house becomes a restaurant and serves excellent northern staples. The Nam Priks are terrific, as is the pork curry in the Myanmar style. I love the ook gai, chicken with lemon grass curry as well, all served with either sicky or steamed rice. It is perhaps not the place to take your vegetarian friends as there are not many choices on the menu for them and not so much fish, but for a meaty treat it's the tops

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you want authentic food that has not had the spiciness taken out of it for tourists this could be your place. We had the chili paste, the eggplant salad, and a chicken curry dish that were all fantastic. The menu was in English which made it easy and the service was good. The decor is a combination of kitschy northern Thailand items that is slightly over whelming but adds a certain character to the place. The entrance of the restaurant is hard to find but tucked away behind some jungle plants. I would highly recommend going here, despite the spice and the difficulty of finding the place. Also beware despite the fact that you are indoors there is no air con and there can be mosquitoes but the staff provided some organic repellent and we had no problems. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I don't really know what Northern Thai food is because it's not common in Hong Kong (my hometown). But I think it's not bad as there were so many local customers. It's a very local restaurant, no decent decoration, most of the seats are in open area, no air conditioning. It's famous in its N.Thai Noddle. Price is low, food is good. it's really hard to find, but most of the locals know where it is.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Having been in Chiang Mai for nearly a week, we look back at our dinner at Huen Phen as one of our highlight meals here. The interior is charming and beautifully furnished, with friendly service and an authentic Northern Thai-oriented menu. I had delicious pork ginger curry and papaya salad. Surprisingly, it was the fiery salad that had me looking around the table for something to cool the heat with (tip: beer doesn't work - try the sticky rice instead =-). As the poster below mentioned, you may wish to ask for the heat to be toned down as the food is 'Thai spicy'. Huen Phen also does Khao Soy (noodles with coconut milk and meat or seafood) and other simple dishes at lunch, served in the covered outdoor seating area. Dinner is inside the lovely restaurant. We thought Huen Phen was very inexpensive and delicious. Foodwise, we enjoyed it much more than our meal at Tha Nam restaurant on the river. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: If you are looking for great authentic northern thai cuisine, Heuan Phen is the place to go. It has an absolutely charming atmosphere, wonderful wait staff and tasty food. A warning for those who like no spice stars in their thai food, Heuan Phen serves some spicy dishes. If you need a more mild spice level you need to ask for 'no chillies' or you might be in for a surprise. If you can handle the spice, Heuan Phen has some of the best food in Chiang Mai. It is really a hidden gem. If they didn't have a sign on the sidewalk pointing you in the right direction you'd never find it. For those looking for a romantic dining experience, try to sit in the antique chinese tea house.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: I was a bit disappointed when we entered the restaurant to find a room full of non-asians, as we thought this was a local place. Wish I had read the previous review and gone at lunchtime, There were dusty tchotchkes everywhere, which I don't find all that charming. The food was very good-- and just a little different from the Thai food we had been having. Not sure how to describe how... but maybe more herbal than spicy. Complex. Great flavors. In the meat dishes, I thought that the meat was a bit scant. The service was good, not great. I would recommend the restaurant... but not if you have only one dinner in Chiang Mai. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: My friend and I received a suggestion to visit this restaurant on our second evening in Chiang Mai. It turned out to be one of the highlights of our dining experience. She enjoyed spicy, delicious larb, and I enjoyed a northern thai soup, with pork and delicious flavor. A cold beer topped off the meal. I'd definitely recommend this restaurant to others in Chiang Mai. The price was right and the food was tasty!

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Situated in the middle of the old town, Huen Phen actually provides two distinct settings in one location. For lunchtime, when locals who work in the neighbourhood are the main clients, seating is open air (but undercover) with the favourite dish being 'Khao Soy' - a Northern Thai style egg noodle with curry and coconut-based soup with either chicken or beef. Other local fare is also on offer and made to the local taste in terms of spiciness and flavour. However in the evening the open air section is closed and guests dine in an old house decorated with a beautiful selection of antiques and local artifacts. This is definitely aimed more at the tourist market and the food is prepared accordingly. We went with a party of locals who had to ask for the food to be spiced up and flavoured more to the local palate. Portions are not overly big, but the menu has a nice selection of Northern Thai dishes - both the everyday favourites and some dishes less often seen. The 'Sai Oua' (local sausage), 'Nam Prik Noum' (chilli dip made with large green chillies - hot!) and 'Nam Prik Ong' (chilli dip made with minced pork and tomatoes and only mildly hot) are good. The 'Gang Hinlay' (Burmese Pork Curry) was very disappointing compared with elsewhere. If you are only in Chiang Mai for a short visit and have just one chance to try out Northern Thai food, Huen Phen is not the worst place to try, but I would recommend going at lunchtime when the place is nicely busy with locals enjoying their midday break. What is on offer in the evening just feels a little too contrived. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Great northern Thai food (which means they don't use coconut milk even in their curries). Very nice setting, looks more like someone's house than a typical restaurant. The only problem is the mozzies, I got bitten at least 10 times so after finding out the first few mozzy bites, I spent the rest of the time gulping down my food and fighting the mozzies from myself and my 3-yr-old. Not fun. Bear in mind however that I am especially prompt to mozzy bites, my wife got only (!) 2-3 bites, my son maybe 5 bites. And yes, after the first few bites, I sprayed insect repellent spray. (Actually before that we already had insect repellent sticky patches on us) Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Excellent Northern Thai food. Try the morning glory, it's hot but delicious. The soups, glorious. The use of herbs and seasonings in Northern cooking is so different from what I believe most people are used to for Thai food. The restaurant is eclectic with antiques and such as decorations. We ate three meals here, two dinner and one lunch. All different dishes each time and not one disappointing dish.

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Huen Phen does not offer good Thai cuisine.....it does not offer great Thai cuisine....Huen Phen offers simply the BEST and most EXCEPTIONAL Thai cuisine you may ever have! With a fairly discriminating palate for Asian food, we were excited to find this gem in the heart of Chiang Mai. We were lucky enough to dine at Huen Phen 3 times for dinner and once for lunch. Both the evening restaurant menu (and lunch menu mentioned below) are extensive and highlight northern cuisine. Everything we tried for dinner was out of this world. The dishes, from the most delicate of soups and Thai style omelets to more pungent dishes of morning glory, meat dishes and Thai chili, drenched our palates with a multitude of flavors. The aromas and tastes of the many layers of seasonings were excellently married together to bring out best in each dish. If this review sounds almost poetic, it's because the food we ate at Huen Phen was exactly that! The ambience of Huen Phen is no less poetic. The small restaurant is like walking through the tropics to reach quaint, beautifully-decorated reclusive seating areas. Your eyes are treated as much as your palate is. During the day, the main restaurant is closed, but their open patio up front is open for lunch. Not the high-end cuisine of evening, it is still a good quality place for a delicious and substantial lunch, with local favorites such as khao soy, spareribs and dozens of other selections. Although the evening meals are of exceptional quality, your wallet will also be happy. We enjoyed full meals for approximately 500B for 3 people. Maybe a little more with a beer and dessert. Lunch was an even better value. You must treat yourself to Huen Phen - Your trip to Chiang Mai will not be complete without it. Read less

Restaurant_Name: เฮือนเพ็ญ นิมมานเหมินทร์
Detail: Best meal in Chiang Mai. Very casual restaurant with delicious, fresh, local dishes. Fresh herbs and vegetables prepared with perfect combo of sweet, salt, sour, and spicy heat. Mostly locals. Ask your waitress for recommendations if you're not sure. Delicious!!
